stage: deployed
last-stage-change: 2026-05-23

# Upstream upgrade path — `dlr-shepard/shepard 5.2.0` → `noheton/shepard main`

**Snapshot date.** 2026-05-16.
**Upstream baseline.** `gitlab.com/dlr-shepard/shepard` v5.2.0 (the
container tags pinned in `infrastructure/docker-compose.yml`:
`registry.gitlab.com/dlr-shepard/shepard/backend:5.2.0`,
`.../frontend:5.2.0`).

This is the **live ledger** of every merged change on `main` that an
admin upgrading from upstream 5.2.0 needs to know about. New rows
land in the same PR as the change they describe — see `CLAUDE.md` at
the repo root for the standing rule.

The intended audience is an **operator** who ran `docker compose up`
on upstream 5.2.0, has data in their Neo4j / Mongo / Timescale, and
wants to upgrade to a build of this repo's `main` without losing it.

**v1 byte-stability framing (V1COMPAT.0, Phase 1).** Rows in the
table below claim byte-stability of `/shepard/api/...` against
upstream shepard 5.2.0. From the V1COMPAT.0 row onward, that
stability is **conditional on `shepard-plugin-v1-compat` being
installed** (the default — the plugin ships in the `with-plugins`
profile and is in every standard image) **AND**
`:LegacyV1Config.enabled` being `true` (the default — seeded as
true by `V63__Bootstrap_legacy_v1_config.cypher` on first start,
and never auto-flipped by any future migration). Operators who
flip the singleton's `enabled` field to `false` are opting in to
HTTP 410 Gone responses for every `/shepard/api/...` request —
the singleton is a deliberate operator gesture, not a fork-imposed
sunset. See `aidocs/platform/103a` for the design and
`docs/reference/v1-deprecation.md` for the runbook.

## TL;DR

As of the snapshot date, **upgrading is essentially `pull-and-restart`**.
No data-mutating migration has shipped yet; every change is either
additive (new endpoints, new optional config) or migration-safe
(idempotent Neo4j constraint adds with null-tolerance). The biggest
operator-visible change is a single config-key namespace deprecation
(A3c); with the v6.0 version bump now shipped, **operators should rename
`shepard.spatial-data.*` keys to `shepard.infrastructure.spatial.*`
at their earliest convenience** — the old keys are still accepted but
the alias will be removed in a future release.

## API-version policy (the big rule)

The standing rule in `CLAUDE.md` (repo root) is reproduced here for
admin convenience:

- **`/shepard/api/...` is frozen.** Byte-compatible with upstream
  `dlr-shepard/shepard 5.2.0`. Existing clients (Python / TS / Java
  generated against upstream OpenAPI) keep working unchanged.
- **`/v2/...` is this fork's development surface.** Every new
  endpoint we add lands here. Opt-in for clients that want our
  additions; ignored by clients that don't.
- L2d formalises the split (`aidocs/25 §4 Phase 4`); L2e eventually
  drops the upstream long-id paths after a deprecation window.

Each row below indicates whether the endpoint touches `/shepard/api/`
(compat surface, additive only) or `/v2/` (this fork). Rows that
touch neither (config keys, internal behaviour) leave the column
blank.

The L2 chain (`L2b` … `L2e`) will introduce data-mutating migrations
and an API deprecation window — see "What's coming" at the bottom.

## Operator quick-start

```bash
# Stop upstream stack.
cd /opt/shepard && docker compose down

# Update images. Build locally, or pull this repo's CI-built tags
# once they're published.
git pull origin main
docker compose build      # if building locally
# or: docker compose pull  # if using published tags

# Optional one-time prep: rotate any default credentials per
# aidocs/07 H8 (this repo's main flags these as already-public).
$EDITOR .env

# Bring up. V11 (Neo4j unique constraints) applies on first start.
docker compose up -d
docker compose logs -f backend  # confirm 'neo4j migrations' lines log V11 applied
```

The frontend container does not need a separate migration step;
the backend is the only service with schema state.

## Change ledger

Status legend:

- **ZERO** — additive only; nothing for an admin to do.
- **CONFIG** — admin should change config (with backward-compatible
  alias / fallback in place).
- **AWARE** — admin should know about a new behaviour even though
  it's not breaking.
- **BREAKING** — a real breaking change with required admin action;
  spelled out in the row.

Migration column: filename of the Neo4j / SQL / app-side migration,
or `—` if no migration ships.

| ID | Change | Status | Config / endpoint impact | Migration | Operator action |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **A1**  | DB connection wait: bounded timeout + exponential backoff | ZERO | New optional config `shepard.migrations.connection-wait-timeout` (default `PT60S`) | — | None. Default is safe. Consider raising the timeout if your Neo4j boots slowly. |
| **A1b** | Per-DB health check separation (startup vs runtime) | ZERO | `/healthz` body now reports per-DB `state` and `kind` | — | None. Useful for monitoring — point your liveness probe at `/healthz` (was already there) and your readiness probe at the same endpoint. |
| **A1c** | Graceful degradation when optional DBs (PostGIS) are down | ZERO | Spatial endpoints return 503 with RFC 7807 body + `Retry-After` when PostGIS is DOWN, 404 when feature toggle is OFF | — | None. Replaces a previously-broken behaviour where the whole API hung. |
| **A1e** | `MigrationsRunner` fails fast on migration errors | **AWARE** | A failed Neo4j-Migrations run now **aborts startup** (was: log + continue) | — | If you were dodging a known-broken migration by relying on the old swallow, that no longer works. Fix the migration or pin to upstream 5.2.0 until you can. |
| **A1f** | Automated DB recovery scheduler | ZERO | New `quarkus-scheduler` dependency. Config `shepard.health.recovery.interval` default `PT15S` | — | None. |
| **P1** | Parallel DB startup warm + parallel recovery scheduler | ZERO | `DbConnectivityWarmer` fires one virtual thread per required DB at `StartupEvent`; `DbRecoveryScheduler` parallelises per-tick recovery probes. No new config keys — reuses `shepard.migrations.connection-wait-timeout`. No wire-contract change; no schema change. Log lines may appear interleaved across DBs at startup (was sequential Neo4j → Mongo → … order). | — | None. Startup is faster when any DB has latency; behaviour is identical when all DBs are up. Log aggregators that parse startup timing from sequential ping lines should switch to reading the individual per-DB `DbHealthState` lines. |
| **A1d** | Flyway startup retry ceiling aligned with Neo4j 60s gate | **CONFIG** | New `quarkus.flyway.connect-retries=10` + `quarkus.flyway.connect-retries-interval=PT5S` (and `quarkus.flyway.spatial.*` equivalents). MongoDB and Agroal JDBC pools already fail fast within the ceiling — no change there. See `aidocs/17`. | — | None required. Defaults already match if you haven't changed Flyway retry config. |
| **A3**  | Runtime feature toggles via `@ConditionalOnFeature` | ZERO | Mechanism only; `versioning` toggle migrated to it | — | None. |
| **A3b** | Runtime feature-toggle admin API: ~~`GET /v2/admin/features`~~, ~~`PATCH /v2/admin/features/{name}`~~ → **`GET /v2/admin/runtime-toggles`**, **`PATCH /v2/admin/runtime-toggles/{name}`** (renamed fire-356, APISIMP-FEATURES-ADMIN-BESPOKE) | **PATH CHANGE** | Path renamed from `/v2/admin/features` to `/v2/admin/runtime-toggles` to distinguish transient (JVM-lifetime) toggles from persisted config at `/v2/admin/config/{feature}`. If you have tooling or scripts that call `/v2/admin/features` directly, update them to `/v2/admin/runtime-toggles`. The fork's own frontend + backend-client are already updated. `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`-gated; override is in-process only (does not survive restart). | — | Update any direct callers of `/v2/admin/features` (the upstream surface doesn't have this endpoint; only this fork's admin scripts/UIs need updating). |
| **A3c** | Config namespace `shepard.spatial-data.*` → `shepard.infrastructure.spatial.*` | **CONFIG** | Both keys resolve. Old keys log a one-shot deprecation warning. Removal planned in a post-v6.0 release — operators should migrate now that v6.0 has shipped. | — | Rename `shepard.spatial-data.*` keys in your `.env` / `application.properties` at your leisure. See `aidocs/platform/A3c-namespace-migration.md` for the full mapping. |
| **A4**  | Permission cache TTL / max-size made tunable | ZERO | New optional `shepard.permissions.cache.ttl` (default `PT5M`), `.max-size` (default `10000`) | — | None. Defaults match upstream's effective behaviour. |
| **L5**  | Semi-permanent API keys with expiry | **AWARE** | `ApiKeyIO` now carries `validUntil`. JWTs minted on this branch carry an `exp` claim. | — | **Existing API keys keep working** — they were minted without `exp`, so the validator treats them as non-expiring. Newly-minted keys honour the new field. Distinguishable 401 on expiry. |
| **P3**  | Migration progress monitoring endpoint | ZERO | New `GET /migrations/progress` (read-only) | — | None. Wire up to your monitoring stack if useful. |
| **P3c** | `/temp/migrations/*` now requires `instance-admin` role (security fix; migration state is operator information) | **AWARE** | `GET /shepard/api/temp/migrations/{state,*containerId*}` returns 403 for non-admin callers (was 200 for any authenticated user) | — | If you had a monitoring scraper polling `/temp/migrations/state` with a regular user's JWT/API-key, switch it to an instance-admin API-key, or rotate the API-key to one with `roles: ["instance-admin"]` per the A0 role-claim mechanism. |
| **U1a** | `User.orcid` field + `PATCH /v2/users/me` (merge-patch, orcid only) | ZERO | New nullable `User.orcid` property (auto-persists via OGM; no migration). `UserIO` carries `orcid` additively — existing `GET /shepard/api/users` clients ignore unknown fields. New `/v2/users/me` PATCH endpoint accepts `application/merge-patch+json` (also `application/json` for compatibility). | — | None. Users can self-set their ORCID via the new endpoint; the field surfaces automatically in `GET /shepard/api/users` and the RO-Crate export (`aidocs/31`). |
| **G1a** | `:GitReference` (loose-link mode-(a)) + `/v2/data-objects/{appId}/git-references` CRUD | ZERO | New `:GitReference` node label with `appId` unique constraint (`V19`, idempotent). New `/v2/data-objects/{appId}/git-references` endpoints (GET list, POST create, GET single, DELETE). Permission piggybacks on DataObject Read/Write. | `V19__Add_appId_constraint_GitReference.cypher` (idempotent, additive) | None. Greenfield label, no existing rows to migrate. |
| **U1b** | `User.displayName` override field + `effectiveDisplayName` derivation; `PATCH /v2/users/me` extended | ZERO | New nullable `User.displayName` property (auto-persists; no migration). `UserIO` adds `displayName` (raw user override) and a computed read-only `effectiveDisplayName` that falls back through `displayName → firstName lastName → redacted-username` per `aidocs/16 U1b`. `PATCH /v2/users/me` accepts the new field alongside `orcid`. | — | None. Users with cryptic Keycloak-UUID usernames can now set a friendlier rendered name; audit-trail render switch wired in U1b2 (see below). |
| **G1-cred** | Per-user git credentials (host + username + AES-GCM encrypted PAT) + `/v2/me/git-credentials` CRUD | **AWARE** | New `:GitCredential` node label with `appId` unique constraint (`V20`, idempotent). New `OWNS_CREDENTIAL` outgoing relationship on `:User`. New `/v2/me/git-credentials` endpoints (GET list, POST create, GET single, PATCH, DELETE). PAT encrypted at rest with AES-GCM-256; **requires new config key `shepard.secrets.encryption-key`** (base64-encoded 32-byte key). If key is absent, the GET/PATCH credential endpoints return 501. | `V20__Add_appId_constraint_GitCredential.cypher` (idempotent, additive) | **Config action required**: generate a 32-byte key and set `shepard.secrets.encryption-key` in `application.properties` or as env var `SHEPARD_SECRETS_ENCRYPTION__KEY`. Without it, PAT storage is disabled and endpoints 501. Generate: `openssl rand -base64 32`. The encryption key must be kept secret — treat it like a database password. |
| **U1b2** | Audit-trail display-name render switch — `createdBy` / `updatedBy` fields in all IO classes and RO-Crate export now surface resolved display names instead of raw usernames | **AWARE** | `BasicEntityIO`, `VersionIO`, `SubscriptionIO`, `LabJournalEntryIO` all call `DisplayNameResolver.effectiveDisplayName()` for `createdBy`/`updatedBy`. RO-Crate `addPersonEntity()` uses ORCID as `@id` when set, otherwise falls back to username; `name` property uses `effectiveDisplayName()`. JSON field **keys** are unchanged (`createdBy`, `updatedBy`) — values change from raw username to human-readable display name. | — | **For users with `firstName`/`lastName` set or a `displayName` override:** the `createdBy` field in API responses and RO-Crate exports will now contain the rendered name rather than the raw username. Clients that hard-code username comparison against `createdBy` (e.g. scripted exports or test fixtures) should switch to comparing against `displayName`/`effectiveDisplayName` from `GET /v2/users/me`. For users whose names are not set, the fallback redacts cryptic Keycloak UUIDs to an 8-char prefix — no information loss for readable usernames. |
| **U1d — user preferences API** | `aidocs/16` U1d. New `GET /v2/users/me/preferences` and `PATCH /v2/users/me/preferences` endpoints. `User` node gains an additive nullable `preferencesJson` property (JSON-serialised `Map<String,String>`). RFC 7396 merge-patch: null removes a key; non-null string sets it; absent keys are preserved. Open-world keys — known UI keys are `theme`, `language`, `timeZone`, `dateFormat`, `defaultPageSize`, `defaultLandingPage`. Auth: authenticated user; no role gate. 11 unit tests in `MePreferencesRestTest`. | **ZERO** | **Additive `/v2/` endpoint only.** New nullable `preferencesJson: STRING` property on `:User` nodes — Neo4j-OGM persists it on first explicit preference write; existing users see it as `null` (GET returns `{}`). No new constraints, no migrations. No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. | — (no migration needed — additive nullable property; Neo4j accepts it lazily on first write) | **No action required for upgrade.** The endpoints are immediately available after restart; users who have not set preferences receive an empty map `{}`. |
| **P14** | NDJSON streaming ingest for timeseries | ZERO | `POST /timeseriesContainers/{id}/payload` accepts `application/x-ndjson` in addition to existing JSON | — | None. Optional. New caps `shepard.timeseries.ingest.ndjson.batch-size` (default 5000), `…max-duration` (default `PT5M`). |
| **R2**  | Body-form selective RO-Crate export (Phase 1) | ZERO | New sibling `POST /collections/{id}/export` with `ExportSelection` body. Existing GET unchanged. | — | None. Existing exports byte-identical. |
| **R2b/c/d/d2** | Selective-export refinements (per-payload picks, redaction, metadata bundling, subscription emission) | ZERO | Additive fields on the body-form endpoint. Defaults preserve current behaviour. | — | None. |
| **L2a** | Additive `appId` (UUID v7) on every Neo4j entity write | **AWARE** | New nullable `appId: STRING` property on 28 node labels. New per-label unique constraint (Neo4j ignores nulls until backfill). | `V11__Add_appId_unique_constraints.cypher` (idempotent, additive) | None at upgrade time. **Be aware**: subsequent writes mint `appId`; existing rows keep `appId = null` until L2b backfills them. The L2 chain is documented in `aidocs/25`. |
| **L2b** | Backfill `appId` on pre-L2a rows | ZERO | No endpoint or config impact | `V12__Backfill_appId.cypher` (chunked 10k rows per batch, idempotent via `WHERE n.appId IS NULL`) + operator-run rollback at `V12_R__Rollback_Backfill_appId.cypher` | None — runs automatically on next startup. After completion, every node in the 28 labels has a non-null `appId`. The V11 unique constraint (which previously tolerated nulls) becomes meaningfully unique. Backfill is bounded by graph size; chunked at 10k rows so it doesn't lock the entire graph. On a `> 1M` node graph, expect minutes-to-low-tens-of-minutes startup delay the first time. Subsequent starts are no-ops (`WHERE n.appId IS NULL` returns zero rows). |
| **C5** | Cypher injection fix — parameter-binding + property-name allowlist in `Neo4jQueryBuilder`, `PermissionsDAO`, `DataObjectDAO` | ZERO | No endpoint or config impact (internal Cypher construction only) | — | None. Search and DAO query shapes change internally — the wire contract (request body, response shape) is unchanged. Closes the injection vector against user-controlled property names + IRI values; subsumes M9. |
| **C5b** | Second-wave parameter-binding fix — `GenericDAO.getSearchForReachableReferences*`, `VersionDAO`, `ShepardFileDAO`, `StructuredDataDAO`, the `*ReferenceDAO` family, `SemanticAnnotationDAO` | ZERO | No endpoint or config impact | — | None. Same shape as C5; closes the remaining `id()=` concat sites. After this lands, the L2c read-path swap from `long` to `String appId` can ship without re-opening injection sites. |
| **H5** | `PublicEndpointRegistry` exact-match (was `startsWith`) — closes the path-traversal vector + the future-entry foot-gun | ZERO | No endpoint or config impact | — | None. Internal filter behaviour change only. Today's single registered entry `/versionz` keeps matching; `/versionzanything` and `/versionz/../containers/1` now correctly do not match. |
| **P4** | `/v2/` JAX-RS routing scaffolding — `quarkus.http.root-path` flipped to `/`; existing resources carry explicit `@Path("/shepard/api/...")` prefix; `de.dlr.shepard.v2` package reserved with ArchUnit fence; `@Sunset` annotation skeleton ready for L2e | ZERO | User-facing URLs unchanged (`/shepard/api/...` paths still resolve identically). Internal config + package convention only. | — | None. Confirms after upgrade that `https://<host>/shepard/api/healthz` still returns 200. The `/v2/` shelf is now reachable for future L2d endpoints; today no `/v2/...` paths exist. |
| **L2c** | Read-path swap — internal Cypher reads switch from `WHERE ID(e) = $entityId` to `WHERE e.appId = $appId` across `PermissionsDAO`, `DataObjectDAO`, `GenericDAO.getSearchForReachableReferences*`, the `*ReferenceDAO` family, `VersionDAO.findVersionLightByNeo4jId`/`createLink`, `ShepardFileDAO.find`, `StructuredDataDAO.find`, `SemanticAnnotationDAO.findAllSemanticAnnotationsByNeo4jId`. New request-scoped `EntityIdResolver` translates the OGM Long to its `appId` at the DAO boundary so call signatures stay `long`. | ZERO | No endpoint, config, or wire-contract impact. The `/shepard/api/...` (`/v1/`) surface is byte-frozen; only the internal Cypher changes. The permissions cache key stays `long` (per `aidocs/25 §3.3`). The OGM `Long id` stays in place. `Neo4jQueryBuilder`'s search-JSON-derived numeric-id predicates also stay (changing them would be a public-API-shape change — that's L2d). `id(c_pointer) = id(c_pointed_to)` in `VersionDAO.copyInternalDataObjectReferences` flipped to `c_pointer.appId = c_pointed_to.appId` (semantically identical given the V11 unique constraint). | — | None. Internal-only change; no migration needed (constraint and backfill already shipped in L2a/L2b). Read paths now require `appId` to be populated — already guaranteed by L2b's V12 backfill. The resolver bootstraps via `MATCH (e) WHERE id(e) = $ogmId RETURN e.appId LIMIT 1` (still uses `id()` once at the boundary; full `id()` retirement is L2e's job, gated on Neo4j-OGM removal `#274`). |
| **L2d Phase A.1** | `/v2/collections` core CRUD with `appId` path params — first `/v2/` resource to expose a top-level entity by its application identifier. New endpoints: `GET /v2/collections` (paginated, `?page` `?size` `?name`), `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}`, `POST /v2/collections`, `PATCH /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}` (RFC 7396 merge-patch), `DELETE /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}`. Auth: per-resource `EntityIdResolver.resolveLong(appId)` → `PermissionsService.isAccessTypeAllowedForUser` (the same pattern `CollectionPropertiesRest` / `CollectionSnapshotRest` / `CollectionWatchesRest` already use — the `aidocs/25 §4` claim that `PermissionsService.isAllowed` needs a parser update applies only to the v2 path of `SubscriptionFilter`, which is a separate observability concern tracked under L2d follow-ups). DTO: reuses existing `CollectionIO` — it already carries both `id: long` (legacy) and `appId: string` (canonical) fields on `BasicEntityIO`, so v2 clients never need to read the long. Related-entity references (`dataObjectIds`, `incomingIds`, `defaultFileContainerId`) stay `long`-shaped for now; a parallel `CollectionV2IO` exposing them as `String[]` lands in **L2d Phase B** alongside the same v2 surface for `DataObject` and the three container kinds. | **ZERO** | **Five new `/v2/` endpoints** (additive, opt-in). No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path — upstream clients keep using the long-id CRUD at `/shepard/api/collections`. No new config keys. No new Neo4j label or migration. The `:Collection` shape is unchanged. `:Subscription` notifications do not currently fire on `/v2/` writes (`SubscriptionFilter` reads the path-segment parser in `PermissionsService.isAllowed`, which still bails on non-numeric segments — tracked as the L2d follow-up). Mixed-shelf clients can use the `/v2/` endpoints for new code while keeping their existing `/shepard/api/...` callers working byte-identically. | — (no migration needed — reuses the `appId` already present on every `:Collection` since L2a/L2b). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the new endpoints appear in the OpenAPI spec at `/shepard/doc/openapi/v2.json` and the combined `/shepard/doc/openapi.json` (each operation tagged `[v2]` in its summary by the OAS2 `ShelfTagFilter`). To migrate a script from v1 → v2: change the path from `/shepard/api/collections/<long-id>` to `/v2/collections/<appId>` (the `appId` is already present on every v1 response). The PATCH surface accepts both `application/merge-patch+json` (RFC 7396 preferred) and `application/json`. **Subscription delivery on `/v2/` writes is not yet wired** — if you rely on `:Subscription` notifications for Collection mutations, keep using `/shepard/api/collections/...` until the L2d follow-up lands; the v2 endpoints themselves succeed and persist correctly, only the subscription fan-out is silent. |
| **L2d Phase A.2** | `/v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects` core CRUD with `appId` path params — second `/v2/` resource pattern-application, same shape as Phase A.1 applied to the `DataObject` entity. New endpoints: `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects` (paginated, `?page` `?size` `?name`; lists DOs under a Collection — requires Read on the parent), `GET .../data-objects/{dataObjectAppId}`, `POST .../data-objects`, `PATCH .../data-objects/{dataObjectAppId}` (RFC 7396), `DELETE .../data-objects/{dataObjectAppId}`. Auth: read on the parent Collection for list / create; read on the DataObject for get; write on the DataObject for patch / delete. DTO: reuses existing `DataObjectIO`. Deferred Phase B knobs: the `parentId` / `predecessorId` / `successorId` filters (v1 takes longs; v2 will take appIds) and the `versionUID` query param land alongside the v2 versioning surface. | **ZERO** | **Five new `/v2/` endpoints** under `/v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects`. No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path — `/shepard/api/collections/{id}/dataObjects/{id}` stays byte-frozen. No new Neo4j label or migration. Same `SubscriptionFilter` caveat as Phase A.1 — subscription delivery on `/v2/` writes is not yet wired. | — (no migration needed — reuses the `appId` already present on every `:DataObject` since L2a/L2b). | **No action required for upgrade.** Endpoints appear in OpenAPI under `/v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects` with `[v2]` summary badges. Migration from v1: change `/shepard/api/collections/<long>/dataObjects/<long>` to `/v2/collections/<appId>/data-objects/<appId>` — both `appId`s come from the v1 response. |
| **CI quality gates** | JaCoCo coverage gate (60% line / 60% branch); SpotBugs + findsecbugs on `verify`; OWASP Dependency-Check (weekly, fail at CVSS≥7); gitleaks secret scan | ZERO | New `backend/pom.xml` properties `jacoco.haltOnFailure` + `spotbugs.failOnError` (default `false` locally; CI flips to `true`) | — | None for an admin running shepard. Affects developers contributing to this fork: `mvn verify` now invokes JaCoCo `check` and SpotBugs `check` (informational locally; gating in CI). New `dependency-check-suppressions.xml` (empty stub, template-documented) lives at `backend/`. |
| **CI image build** | This fork now publishes its own GHCR images (`ghcr.io/noheton/shepard-{backend,frontend}:{latest,sha-<7>,vX.Y.Z}`) via `.github/workflows/build-images.yml` on push to `main` and on `v*.*.*` tags. | ZERO | An admin running their own shepard deployment can opt into the fork's GHCR images via `infrastructure/docker-compose.override.yml.example` (rename to `docker-compose.override.yml`). Default compose still references upstream gitlab.com images. | — | **Optional**: copy the override file template to use the fork's images. Without the override, `docker compose up` fetches the upstream 5.2.0 images and you don't get this fork's changes. With the override, `docker compose pull` fetches `ghcr.io/noheton/shepard-{backend,frontend}:latest`. Pin to `:sha-<7>` for stability. |
| **CI security extension** | Six security gates now in CI: SpotBugs+findsecbugs (SAST/Java) + **CodeQL** (SAST/Java+JS, security-extended queries) + OWASP Dependency-Check (SCA, weekly) + **Trivy** (container CVE scan on GHCR images) + gitleaks (secrets) + **dependency-review** (PR-time licence + new-CVE check). **SBOM** (CycloneDX) generated for every published image via `anchore/sbom-action` and attached to GitHub releases. New `.github/dependency-review-config.yml` carries the GPL/AGPL/SSPL deny-list. | ZERO | An admin running their own shepard deployment is unaffected — these run only in this repo's CI. Affects developers contributing to the fork: more gates per PR, more findings to fix or suppress (with justification). | — | None. Findings flow to the Security tab → Code Scanning. |
| **v5 wire-fidelity regression corpus** | New `backend/src/test/resources/fixtures/v5/` corpus + `V5WireFidelityTest` superclass + 6 concrete `*V5WireFidelityIT` subclasses (Collections, DataObjects, Containers ×3, StructuredDataReferences, Users, Permissions — 10 fixtures total across 9 entity kinds). Each fixture pins the canonical JSON shape (key-set strict, dynamic-mint fields normalised to sentinels via `V5JsonNormalizer`). A non-additive change to any covered `/shepard/api/...` shape now fails `mvn verify`. Re-record approved changes with `-Dshepard.fixtures.record=true`. Drift catalogued in `docs/reference/v5-cross-instance-quirks.md` (existing additive fields: `appId`, `revision`, `heroImageUrl`, `license`, `accessRights`, `status`, plus `UserIO` additions and `TimeseriesReferenceIO` AI1c/TM1 fields — all cross-referenced to their original ledger rows). Coverage extension to FileReference / TimeseriesReference / `/collections/{id}/export` deliberately deferred (1-test, 1-fixture pattern per the superclass javadoc recipe). | ZERO | Test infrastructure only. No new endpoints, no config keys, no schema migration, no wire-shape change. Affects developers contributing to this fork: any future PR that touches a `/shepard/api/...` shape covered by the corpus must regenerate the fixture and document the wire change in the same PR (refuses to merge silently). | — | **No action required for upgrade.** This row is contributor-facing — it formalises the byte-for-byte compat policy from `CLAUDE.md §"API-version policy"` as a CI gate instead of a code-review eyeball check. |
| **M4 / M5 / M2** | JWT-filter Bearer-prefix mangling fix; redaction of token values in warn-level logs; `0600` perms on the generated PKI private key | ZERO | No config / endpoint impact | — | None. Behaviour change observable only in (a) tokens that contain the literal substring `"Bearer "` mid-payload now parse correctly, (b) `Log.warn` of `"Invalid/missing authorization header"` now reports `present`/`absent` instead of full header values, (c) `~/.shepard/keys/private.key` is created with `rw-------` rather than the umask default. Pre-existing key files keep their old perms — re-run `chmod 600 ~/.shepard/keys/private.key` once on upgrade if your audit story requires it. |
| **H4** | RFC 7807 (`application/problem+json`) error responses + sanitised exception logging — subsumes M7 | **AWARE** | Every error response (`/shepard/api/...` and `/v2/...`) now defaults to `application/problem+json` with the RFC 7807 fields (`type`, `title`, `status`, `detail`, `instance`). For `Accept: application/json` (with no `application/problem+json` and no wildcard), the legacy `ApiError` shape is still returned — preserves upstream-client wire compatibility. The `instance` is `X-Request-Id` if present, otherwise a request-scoped UUID. `type` URIs are `https://noheton.github.io/shepard/errors/<code>` (docs page can be authored later — URI is a stable identifier per RFC 7807 §3.1 even before the page exists). | — | **Be aware**: 5xx response bodies are now generic — `detail` reads `"An internal server error occurred. Reference: <traceId>"` instead of leaking the underlying Hibernate / Neo4j / Mongo exception message. Clients that scraped the legacy leaky message stop seeing it. Known shepard 4xx exceptions (`InvalidAuthException`, `InvalidBodyException`, `InvalidPathException`, …) keep surfacing their (controlled) message as `detail`. Server-side: full stacktrace logs at `debug`; the `error` line carries only `traceId + exception class + http method + request path` (no request body, no headers, no exception message). To get the legacy `ApiError` shape on a one-off basis, send `Accept: application/json` (must not also accept `*/*` or `application/problem+json`). |
| **A0 + F8** | Instance-Admin role mechanism (backend slice). New `instance-admin` role tier; configurable OIDC roles-claim path (`shepard.oidc.roles-claim-path`, default `realm_access.roles`); `shepard.instance-admin.role` names the IdP-claim role-string that maps to `instance-admin`; new `Role` Neo4j entity + `:HAS_ROLE` relationship; bootstrap-token mechanism (writes `/opt/shepard/.bootstrap-token` mode 0600 on first start when no instance-admin exists); `POST /v2/admin/bootstrap`, `GET/POST/DELETE /v2/admin/instance-admins`, `GET /v2/admin/permission-audit`; API-key `roles` field with allowlist + caller-must-have-each-role validation. | **AWARE** | New optional config keys (all default safe): `shepard.oidc.roles-claim-path`, `shepard.instance-admin.role`, `shepard.apikey.role-allowlist`, `shepard.bootstrap.token-path`, `shepard.permissions.default-owner` (see C3 row). New endpoints under `/v2/admin/*` — additive surface, all `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`-gated except the bootstrap one. The IdP-side admin grant is **opt-in** — leave `shepard.instance-admin.role` empty (default) to disable IdP-side grants entirely; bootstrap-token + Neo4j edges remain the only path. | `V13__Add_appId_constraint_Role.cypher` (single-statement constraint add, idempotent) | **First-start operator runbook**: (1) `docker compose up -d`. (2) Watch the logs: a single-line `WARN` reports the bootstrap token path. (3) `sudo cat /opt/shepard/.bootstrap-token` and feed it to `POST /v2/admin/bootstrap` with body `{"token": "<token>", "username": "<your-IdP-username>"}` — the user must have logged in at least once via OIDC. (4) Confirm via `GET /v2/admin/instance-admins`. (5) Optional: configure `shepard.instance-admin.role=<keycloak-role>` to also grant via the IdP claim. The `shepard-admin` CLI commands that wrap these endpoints land in L1 Phase 1 (post-this-slice). |
| **C3** | Permissions fallback fail-closed — `PermissionsService.getRoles` now returns `Roles(false, false, false, false)` for entities lacking a `:has_permissions` edge (was: full read+write+manage for every authenticated user). Pre-migration hook `OrphanPermissionsBackfillContext` aborts startup if orphans exist AND `shepard.permissions.default-owner` is unset. | **AWARE** | Bundled with **A0**. New required-when-orphans-exist config `shepard.permissions.default-owner=<existing-username>`. Greenfield deployments (no pre-permission entities) can leave it unset. | `V14__Backfill_orphan_permissions.cypher` (idempotent — only attaches Permissions to nodes lacking them; the Java pre-hook aborts if config is unset and orphans exist) + `V14_R__Rollback_Backfill_orphan_permissions.cypher` (operator-run; deletes the V14-marker Permissions nodes via the `legacyBackfill = 'A0-V14'` property tag) | **Operators upgrading from a really old shepard with pre-permission entities MUST set `shepard.permissions.default-owner` before the next start** — otherwise the Java pre-migration hook aborts startup with a clear error pointing here. Pick a username that already exists as a `:User` node in your Neo4j (the user must have logged in at least once via OIDC, or be pre-created). After the backfill applies, the C3 fail-closed default takes effect — every entity now has a Permissions node, no fallback needed. Verify via `GET /v2/admin/permission-audit` (post-A0): list should be empty. **Existing healthy deployments** (no orphans) can leave the config unset; the hook is a no-op. |
| **A0 — API-key roles claim** | `POST /apikeys` body grows an optional `roles: [...]` field; minted JWTs carry a `roles` claim cross-checked against the Neo4j-stored `roles` Set on every (uncached) request. | **AWARE** | Existing API keys keep working with their stored `roles` (validator cross-check unchanged). Clients that want to call admin-gated `/v2/admin/...` paths via an API key must mint **new** keys — and the minting user must already hold the role (no privilege escalation). The role must be in `shepard.apikey.role-allowlist` (default now `["instance-admin","quality-engineer"]`; shrink to `[]` to forbid role-bearing keys entirely). Mismatch between the JWT's `roles` claim and the stored Set on read returns 401 (treats as forged). | — | **No action required for upgrade.** Existing keys keep their stored role set and stay valid. Mint new role-bearing keys when adopting admin tooling that needs them (e.g. the future L1 `shepard-admin` CLI in Phase 1). |
| **RESEED-FIND-APIKEY-ROLES — API keys inherit effective realm roles** | A minted API key now defaults to the owner's **effective** realm roles (the Neo4j `:HAS_ROLE` grants — the same source `JwtTokenAuthService.resolveDualSourceRoles` reads for an interactive OIDC token) intersected with `shepard.apikey.role-allowlist`, instead of minting role-less. Explicitly-supplied `roles: [...]` (the A0 path) is honoured unchanged when present. Fixes role-gated endpoints (NCR `status` needs `quality-engineer`; `@RolesAllowed(...)` surfaces) returning 403 for an otherwise-authorised user's API key. The allowlist default widens to `instance-admin,quality-engineer` so the NCR flow works out of the box. | **AWARE** | **No new endpoints, no schema change.** One default changed: `shepard.apikey.role-allowlist` now `instance-admin,quality-engineer` (was `instance-admin`). Behaviour change: keys minted from this release forward carry the owner's effective roles (∩ allowlist) — so an instance-admin's key can now reach admin surfaces and a quality-engineer's key can set NCR statuses, matching their interactive-token capability. **Existing keys are untouched** (their stored `roles` Set and JWT are immutable; mint a fresh key to pick up the new behaviour). To forbid role-bearing keys entirely, set `shepard.apikey.role-allowlist=` (empty) → all new keys mint role-less regardless of owner roles. | — (no migration — read-path behaviour + config default only). | **No action required for upgrade.** Existing API keys keep their stored roles and stay valid. After upgrade, re-mint any key whose owner needs role-gated access (e.g. an admin's automation key, a QE's NCR-disposition key) so it picks up the owner's effective roles. To verify: decode the new key's JWT (`jwt.io` or `cut -d. -f2 | base64 -d`) and confirm the `roles` claim lists the owner's realm roles. To lock down: set `shepard.apikey.role-allowlist=` to mint only role-less keys. |
| **Removed: deploy-target + deploy guides** | Dropped `.github/workflows/deploy-test-instance.yml`, `docs/deploy-self-hosted-zoraxy.md`, `docs/deploy-oracle-free.md`; `docs/deploy.md` rewritten without those two paths. GHCR image-build workflow is retained — operators can still pull `ghcr.io/noheton/shepard-{backend,frontend}` via the override template. | ZERO | An admin running their own shepard deployment is not affected (no shipped endpoint, config, or schema change). The two deleted guides were maintainer-host-specific recipes; the upstream production deploy guidance (`gitlab.com/dlr-shepard/shepard` wiki) remains authoritative. | — | **None.** The deletions only remove maintainer-side infra. If you were following one of the deleted guides verbatim, fall back to the upstream wiki or to `docs/deploy.md`'s comparison matrix. |
| **PROV1a — provenance / activity capture** | New `:Activity` Neo4j entity (HasAppId; modelled on W3C PROV-O `prov:Activity`); JAX-RS request+response filter captures POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE on 2xx responses by default. New `GET /v2/provenance/{activities,count}` query endpoints (casual users see only their own rows; instance-admin sees all). Designed in `aidocs/55`. | **AWARE** | Three new optional config keys (all default-safe): `shepard.provenance.enabled=true` (master toggle), `shepard.provenance.capture-reads=false` (opt-in read capture), `shepard.instance.id=local` (per-deployment origin stamp; used by `aidocs/60` Edge sync). The capture filter is a JAX-RS Provider — applies to every authenticated endpoint. Captures NOT persisted on failure paths (provenance is observability, never blocks the request). | `V15__Add_appId_constraint_Activity.cypher` (single-statement constraint add, idempotent; mirrors V13's shape for `:Role`). | **No action required for upgrade.** Activity rows begin landing from the first 2xx mutation post-deploy. Operators wanting read capture flip `shepard.provenance.capture-reads=true` (cost: row-volume grows ~10×). To verify capture is working: `GET /v2/provenance/activities` after the first POST returns at least one row. |
| **AAS1-well-known — AAS self-description** | New unauthenticated `GET /v2/aas/.well-known/aas-server` endpoint (`aidocs/52 §4a.5`). Returns JSON: `enabled`, `aasApiProfile`, `endpoints`, `supportedSubmodelTemplates`, `shellCount`, `registryRegistrations`. Capability flags + counts only — never per-Shell identifiers — so reachable pre-auth is the design (mirrors `/versionz` / `/v2/admin/bootstrap`). | ZERO | Two new optional config keys (both default-safe): `shepard.aas.enabled=false` (master toggle; when `false`, the endpoint still answers — `enabled: false` is the signal an AAS-aware client uses to skip), `shepard.aas.api-profile=Submodel-Repository-Read-3.1` (advertised profile). | — | **None.** Operators wanting to advertise AAS capability flip `shepard.aas.enabled=true`. `supportedSubmodelTemplates` auto-populates from `ShepardTemplate` rows with `templateKind=AAS_SUBMODEL_TEMPLATE`. The `endpoints` map now advertises `{shells: "/v2/aas/shells"}` following AAS1a. |
| **AAS1a — Shell listing** | New `GET /v2/aas/shells` endpoint. Returns one IDTA AAS v3 AssetAdministrationShell per Collection the authenticated caller may read (`id`=`urn:shepard:collection:{appId}`, `idShort`=sanitised name, `assetInformation.globalAssetId`=`urn:shepard:asset:{appId}`, optional `description`, empty `submodels`). Permission-aware via `CollectionDAO.findAllCollectionsByShepardId`. `AasServerSelfDescriptionService` updated to advertise `/v2/aas/shells` in the `endpoints` map. | ZERO | No new config keys. No migration. | — | **None for upgrade.** Purely additive endpoint. Any AAS-aware client that reads `/.well-known/aas-server.endpoints.shells` will now get `/v2/aas/shells` and can enumerate Shells. |
| **AAS1-reg Commit 1 — outbox entity + well-known wiring** | New `:AasRegistration` Neo4j entity tracking per-shell registration state at an external IDTA AAS Registry. `AasRegistrationDAO` with `findByRegistryUrl`, `listAll`, `distinctSyncedRegistryUrls`. New optional config key `shepard.aas.registry.url` (deploy-time URL of the target IDTA AAS Registry; empty disables outbound registration). Well-known doc's `registryRegistrations` now populated: advertises the configured registry URL when the outbox is empty (pre-sync), switches to synced URLs once registration succeeds (AAS1-reg Commit 2). `V46` migration adds uniqueness constraint on `:AasRegistration.appId`. | ZERO | One new optional deploy-time config key: `shepard.aas.registry.url` (default empty). Empty = outbound registration disabled; well-known `registryRegistrations` will remain `[]`. | `V46__Add_appId_constraint_AasRegistration.cypher` (idempotent `IF NOT EXISTS` constraint; runs on startup via `MigrationsRunner`) | **None for upgrade.** V46 runs automatically on restart. Set `shepard.aas.registry.url` when ready to enable outbound IDTA registry registration (AAS1-reg Commit 2 is the client that actually calls the registry). |
| **AAS1-reg Commit 2 — registry HTTP client + outbox sync service** | New `AasRegistryClient` (JDK `HttpClient`, `POST {registryUrl}/shell-descriptors`). New `AasRegistryOutboxService.syncAll()`: seeds a PENDING `:AasRegistration` row for every non-deleted Collection that has no outbox row, then pushes all PENDING/FAILED rows. Startup hook (`@Observes StartupEvent`) triggers `syncAll()` on a virtual thread (best-effort; failures log WARN, never block readiness). New `AasShellDescriptorIO` wire shape for the IDTA ShellDescriptor payload. Two additional optional config keys. | ZERO | Two new optional deploy-time config keys: `shepard.aas.registry.api-key` (Bearer token for the registry; omit when the registry is open) and `shepard.aas.base-url` (public URL of this shepard instance; used in the ShellDescriptor endpoint href — omit when the registry is not configured). | — (no new migration) | **None for upgrade.** Set the three `shepard.aas.registry.*` + `shepard.aas.base-url` keys when ready to enable outbound IDTA registry registration. Restart triggers the startup sync. |
| **AAS1-reg Commit 3 — admin REST endpoints** | New `GET /v2/admin/aas/registrations` (lists all `:AasRegistration` outbox rows; returns `AasRegistrationIO[]`) and `POST /v2/admin/aas/registrations/sync` (triggers `AasRegistryOutboxService.syncAll()` on demand; returns `{ "synced": N }`). Both gated on `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`. No new config keys. No migration. | ZERO | No new config keys. No new migration. | — | **None for upgrade.** Purely additive endpoints. Operators can use `POST /v2/admin/aas/registrations/sync` to retry FAILED registrations without restarting the pod (e.g. after a registry was temporarily down during startup). |
| **AAS1b Commit 1 — single-Shell lookup** | New `GET /v2/aas/shells/{aasId}` endpoint. Accepts `{aasId}` as (a) base64url-encoded Shell IRI (`urn:shepard:collection:{appId}`) per IDTA-01002-3-2 §4.3, or (b) bare shepard Collection appId. Returns 404 (not 403) when the Shell does not exist or the caller lacks read access (404-on-no-read discipline, `aidocs/52 §7`). New `CollectionDAO.findByAppId(appId, username)` method embeds the permission filter in the Cypher query (same pattern as `findAllCollectionsByShepardId`). `AasShellMappingService.COLLECTION_URN_PREFIX` widened to `public` (was package-private) to allow cross-package use by the ID resolver. | ZERO | No new config keys. No migration. | — | **None for upgrade.** Purely additive endpoint. IDTA AAS-conformant clients sending base64url-encoded Shell IRIs and native shepard clients sending bare appIds are both supported. |
| **AAS1b Commit 2 — submodels endpoint + AasReferenceIO type** | New `GET /v2/aas/shells/{aasId}/submodels` endpoint. Returns one IDTA AAS v3 `ExternalReference` per top-level DataObject of the Collection (`type="ExternalReference"`, `keys[0].type="Submodel"`, `keys[0].value="urn:shepard:dataobject:{appId}"`). 404-on-no-read same as Commit 1. New `DataObjectDAO.findTopLevelByCollectionAppId(appId)` Cypher query. New `AasReferenceIO` + nested `AasKeyIO` Java records. `AasShellMappingService.toSubmodelRefs(List<DataObject>)` + `toShell(Collection, List<DataObject>)` overload. `GET /v2/aas/shells/{aasId}` now returns a populated `submodels` list. **Breaking on `/v2/` surface**: `AasShellIO.submodels` field type changed from `List<String>` to `List<AasReferenceIO>`. Permitted by CLAUDE.md API-version policy (was always `[]` in AAS1a so no deployed client was reading a non-empty list). | **AWARE** | No new config keys. No migration. | — | **None for upgrade.** Additive new endpoint. The `AasShellIO.submodels` type change is a `/v2/` surface change — any client built against AAS1a's `List<String>` shape (always empty) will need to re-generate against the new schema. The JSON wire shape changes: submodels entries are now `{"type":"ExternalReference","keys":[{"type":"Submodel","value":"..."}]}` objects rather than bare strings. |
| **AAS1d — bundled IDTA Submodel Templates + `shepard-admin aas import-idta-templates`** | Three IDTA Submodel Templates imported as `ShepardTemplate` entities with `templateKind = AAS_SUBMODEL_TEMPLATE`: Digital Nameplate v3.0 (IDTA-02006), Technical Data v2.0 (IDTA-02003), Time Series Data v1.1 (IDTA-02008). Bundled in `backend/src/main/resources/aas-idta-templates.yaml`. New `AasIdtaTemplateImportService` (idempotent upsert — skip when body + description + tags are identical, copy-on-write when they differ). New `POST /v2/admin/aas/import-idta-templates` (`@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`). Returns `{ created: [...], skipped: N }`. New `AasAdminRest` resource. New CLI command `shepard-admin aas import-idta-templates` (human table: NAME / VERSION / APP-ID; `--output json`). `AasServerSelfDescriptionService.AAS_SUBMODEL_TEMPLATE_KIND` widened from package-private to `public static final`. Templates auto-surface in `/.well-known/aas-server.supportedSubmodelTemplates` after import. | **AWARE** | No new config keys. No migration. | — | **None for upgrade.** Purely additive. To populate the template registry run: `shepard-admin aas import-idta-templates` (or call `POST /v2/admin/aas/import-idta-templates`). Safe to run on every restart — idempotent. The three templates will then appear in `GET /v2/templates` and `/.well-known/aas-server.supportedSubmodelTemplates`. |
| **AAS1-plugin — AAS extracted to `shepard-plugin-aas`** | All 18 AAS main source files + 8 tests moved from `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/{aas,v2/aas,v2/admin/aas}/` to `plugins/aas/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/plugins/aas/`. New `AasPluginManifest` + `AasPayloadKind` SPIs. `aas-idta-templates.yaml` moved to plugin resources (classloader-accessible). V46 migration stays in backend (Docker runner reads `/deployments/neo4j/migrations/` which only contains backend resources). `NeoConnector.connect()` no longer directly references `AasRegistration`; the package is registered via `AasPayloadKind` through `ServiceLoader<PayloadKind>`. Backend `pom.xml` adds `shepard-plugin-aas` to the `with-plugins` profile; `application.properties` adds `quarkus.index-dependency.shepard-plugin-aas.*`. All existing AAS endpoints (`/v2/aas/...`, `/v2/admin/aas/...`) continue to respond identically — zero wire-shape change. Latent test bug fixed: `AasIdtaTemplateImportServiceTest.createsNewVersionWhenBodyDiffers` expected `times(5)` but correct count is `times(4)` (2 for Nameplate: retire + create-new; 1 each for the 2 other templates). Three plugin docs shipped: `plugins/aas/docs/{reference,quickstart,install}.md`. | **AWARE** | One new deploy-time config key: `shepard.plugins.aas.enabled=true` (default `true`, mirrors pre-extraction posture). No new endpoints, no new migrations, no wire-shape changes. | — (V46 stays in backend, applied as before) | **Default upgrade: no action required.** The AAS plugin is bundled in the default image (built with the `with-plugins` profile); restart and `GET /v2/admin/plugins` shows `"id":"aas","state":"ENABLED"`. **Custom-image operators who build with `-DnoPlugins`** must add `shepard-plugin-aas-${revision}.jar` to `/deployments/plugins/` before restarting — otherwise AAS endpoints 404. Set `shepard.plugins.aas.enabled=false` to disable without removing the JAR. |
| **L10 — `shepard.oidc.username-claim` configurable identity-claim** | 2026-05-27. New optional backend config key `shepard.oidc.username-claim` (default empty). When empty, the existing behaviour is preserved: the JWT `sub` claim is split on `:` and the last segment is used as the Shepard username (the Keycloak-UUID-as-username behaviour documented in `aidocs/99-api-annoyances.md §A-01`). When set (e.g. `preferred_username`, `email`, `upn`): `JwtTokenAuthService.parsePrincipalFromAccessToken` reads the named claim from the JWT body; if present and textual, it wins; if absent, a WARN is logged and the method falls back to sub-split. `UserFilter.resolveUsernameFromUserinfo` mirrors the same logic (from the userinfo response) so the username-match check in `UserFilter` stays satisfied. New `extractUsername(Claims, String)` method on `JwtTokenAuthService` (package-visible for tests). Documented in `backend/src/main/resources/application.properties` with a migration warning. 4 new JUnit tests (`JwtTokenAuthServiceTest`): default-empty uses sub-split, `preferred_username` wins when present, fallback to sub-split when claim absent, arbitrary claim (`upn`) works. | **CONFIG** | One new optional config key `shepard.oidc.username-claim` (default empty — zero behaviour change). Companions `SHEPARD_OIDC_USERNAME_CLAIM` env var (Quarkus naming convention). **Identity-migration footgun**: setting this key on an existing deployment renames all users — Neo4j `:User` nodes keyed by the old value (Keycloak UUID) will no longer match incoming JWT usernames; users see 401/404 until their node is migrated. Operators must (a) take a Neo4j backup, (b) run `MATCH (u:User) SET u.username = <new-value-from-idp>` for every user, (c) then flip the config key. No automated migration script shipped — the field values differ per deployment and per IdP. | — (no schema migration — the `username` property already exists on `:User`; this change only affects which claim value is stored). | **No action required for upgrade.** Default behaviour is unchanged (empty claim → sub-split). Operators who want human-readable usernames (e.g. `preferred_username` from Pocket ID, Authentik, Azure AD, Auth0) can set `shepard.oidc.username-claim=preferred_username` **on a fresh install with no existing `:User` nodes** at zero cost. On an **existing deployment**, the rename must be preceded by a Neo4j username-migration pass. See `aidocs/ops/22-admin-cli-draft.md §4.11a.5` for per-IdP claim names. **⚠ DEFAULT FLIPPED 2026-06-17 → `preferred_username`** (was empty/sub-split). Rationale: every user was materialising with a Keycloak-UUID username — unreadable in the UI and forcing UUID-keyed group/role ops (surfaced building the MFFD demonstrator org). **BREAKING for operators upgrading with existing `:User` nodes keyed under sub-split**: their users duplicate/401 on next login (`createOrUpdateUser` matches on the `username` @Id). Operator action before deploying this version: `MATCH (u:User) SET u.username = <preferred_username-from-IdP>` for every user, OR set `shepard.oidc.username-claim=` back to empty to retain legacy behaviour. Fresh installs unaffected. Marked **BREAKING**. |
| **L1 Phase 1 — `shepard-admin` read-only CLI** | New optional admin CLI shipped at the top-level `cli/` Maven module (`de.dlr.shepard:shepard-admin`, Java 21 + Picocli 4.7, standalone — no aggregator pom). Three read-only verbs in this phase: `features list` (calls `/v2/admin/features`), `health` (calls `/shepard/api/healthz/{ready,live}` and maps overall UP → exit 0, anything DOWN → exit 1), `migrations status [containerId]` (calls `/shepard/api/temp/migrations/state` or `/shepard/api/temp/migrations/{containerId}`). Designed in `aidocs/22 §4.4-§4.6` + `§7.1`. | **AWARE** | **No backend wire-protocol change** — every CLI call hits an endpoint that already shipped. Auth via `X-API-KEY` header (per `JWTFilter.parseApiKey`); the API key must carry the `instance-admin` role for `/v2/admin/features` and post-P3c `/shepard/api/temp/migrations/*` to return 200 instead of 403. Config sources (in priority order): CLI flags `--url` / `--api-key`, env vars `SHEPARD_ADMIN_URL` / `SHEPARD_ADMIN_API_KEY`, file `~/.shepard/admin.toml`, defaults (URL falls back to `http://localhost:8080`). Two output modes: `--output human` (default; plain-text table that pipes cleanly into `awk`/`grep`) and `--output json` (pretty-printed). | — | **Install.** Until a JBang catalogue entry + GHCR container image ship in a follow-up phase, the install path is: clone this repo, `cd cli && mvn package -DskipTests`, ship `cli/target/shepard-admin-*.jar` to any host with Java 21. **Configure.** Mint an instance-admin-roled API key per A0, then `export SHEPARD_ADMIN_URL=https://<your-shepard>` and `export SHEPARD_ADMIN_API_KEY=<key>`. **Use.** `java -jar shepard-admin-*.jar features list` — additionally `health` and `migrations status [<id>]`. **Skip.** Doing nothing is fine; the CLI is purely additive and adds no new endpoints, no schema change, no config keys to set. See `docs/reference/admin-cli.md` for the full quick-start with sample output for each command. |
| **FR1a — `FileReference` → `FileBundleReference` rename + `FileGroup` sub-node** | `aidocs/53 §1`. Internal Java rename + new sub-Reference grouping primitive. The legacy `:FileReference` Neo4j label is preserved (every bundle now carries BOTH `:FileReference` and `:FileBundleReference` labels, the latter via `@Labels`). The upstream `/shepard/api/...fileReferences/...` REST surface — including the `BasicReferenceIO.type = "FileReference"` wire field, achieved by overriding `FileBundleReference#getType()` — keeps its byte-for-byte shape. New `/v2/bundles/{appId}/...` shelf surfaces the new shape with `groups: [...]` populated. Newly-created bundles get a default `FileGroup` (`name="default"`, `index=0`) auto-attached on save; the V21 migration backfills the same default group on every existing bundle. | **AWARE** | New `/v2/bundles/{appId}/{groups,groups/{groupAppId},groups/{groupAppId}/files}` endpoints; permission-checked against the parent DataObject via `PermissionsService`. Operators who never call `/v2/` see no change. No new config keys. | `V21__Rename_FileReference_to_FileBundleReference_and_introduce_FileGroup.cypher` (idempotent + fail-fast: adds `:FileBundleReference` label to every `:FileReference` node, attaches a default `:FileGroup` named `"default"` with `index=0` and a fresh `appId`, re-parents existing `(bundle)-[:HAS_PAYLOAD]->(file)` edges under the default group as `(group)-[:HAS_PAYLOAD]->(file)`, **leaves the bundle's direct `HAS_PAYLOAD` edges in place** as a compatibility shadow for the upstream-API read path); rollback `V21_R__Split_FileBundleReference_into_FileReference.cypher` (refuses to run if any user-created groups exist — guard via division-by-zero in Cypher); `V22__Add_appId_constraint_FileGroup.cypher` (V11-shape uniqueness constraint on the new `:FileGroup` label). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V21 + V22 apply on first start. Verify via `cypher-shell`: `MATCH (b:FileBundleReference) RETURN count(b) AS bundles, sum(CASE WHEN (b)-[:HAS_GROUP]->(:FileGroup) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS bundles_with_group;` — both numbers should match. The follow-on FR1b will introduce a true singleton `FileReference` primitive, a parallel `/v2/files/...` shelf, and the V16b opt-in carve-out migration (`shepard.migration.split-singletons.enabled` — see `aidocs/53 §1.8`); FR1b ships separately. |
| **AI1c — `TimeseriesReference.qualityScore` background job** | `aidocs/16` AI1c (pure-heuristic channel-quality scorer; no LLM). New scheduled `TimeseriesQualityScoringJob` (Quarkus `@Scheduled`) computes a `[0.0, 1.0]` score from completeness (`1 − missing_value_ratio`) + coverage (`observed/expected_points`) + stability (`1 − stddev/|mean|`), averaged, on every non-deleted `:TimeseriesReference` with a container. Per-ref rescoring window 24 h. Score + `lastScoredAt` surface read-only on `TimeseriesReferenceIO` (existing JSON shape extended with two optional fields — additive, upstream clients ignore unknown fields). Searchable via the existing search shape (e.g. `{property: "qualityScore", operator: "lt", value: "0.5"}`) since `qualityScore` is a primitive numeric Neo4j property. | **CONFIG** | Three new optional config keys, all default-safe (off): `shepard.timeseries.quality-scoring.enabled=false`, `shepard.timeseries.quality-scoring.interval=PT6H`, `shepard.timeseries.quality-scoring.batch-size=100` (clamped `[1, 10000]` in the job). When disabled (default), the scheduled tick logs at debug and does no DB I/O. Two new nullable properties on the `:TimeseriesReference` Neo4j node (`qualityScore: float`, `lastScoredAt: long`) — Neo4j-OGM persists them automatically on next write, no schema constraint needed. | — (no migration — additive nullable properties; Neo4j accepts them lazily on first write) | **None for upgrade.** Default-off — operators opt in by setting `shepard.timeseries.quality-scoring.enabled=true` and (optionally) tuning the interval / batch size. Once enabled, the job starts emitting `qualityScore` on the next scheduled tick; existing rows score `null` until first visit. Per-ref rescoring is 24 h — refs scored within the window are skipped. Reference page at `docs/reference/timeseries-reference.md` is deferred (file doesn't exist yet — will land when the broader timeseries reference docs do); the field semantics are documented in the `TimeseriesQualityScorer` Javadoc and the `TimeseriesReferenceIO` schema annotations. |
| **FR1b — singleton `FileReference` + `/v2/files/...` shelf + V23 opt-in split-singletons** | `aidocs/53 §1.8`. New singleton `FileReference` primitive (one `ShepardFile` per Reference, no `FileContainer`; bytes in a shared `_shepard_files` Mongo namespace). Distinct Neo4j label `:SingletonFileReference` so the FR1a bundle DAO (`:FileReference` label, kept for upstream-API byte-compat) and the new singleton DAO query disjoint row sets. New `/v2/files/{appId}` shelf with `POST /v2/files` (multipart upload, requires `?parentDataObjectAppId=`), `GET /v2/files/{appId}`, `GET /v2/files/{appId}/content` (HTTP-range capable), `PATCH /v2/files/{appId}` (RFC 7396 merge-patch on `name`), `DELETE /v2/files/{appId}`. The upstream `/shepard/api/...fileReferences/...` surface still only ever creates bundles — singletons are `/v2/`-only by definition. | **AWARE + CONFIG** | New `/v2/files/{appId}/{,content}` endpoints; permission-checked against the parent DataObject via `PermissionsService`. Operators who never call `/v2/files` see no change to their byte storage layout. New optional config key `shepard.migration.split-singletons.enabled` (default `false`) gates the V23 migration — see Migration column. Operators may also set this `true` ahead of an upgrade so V23 runs on the next restart. | `V23__Split_singleton_bundles_to_FileReferences.java` (JavaBasedMigration, opt-in via `shepard.migration.split-singletons.enabled=true`; default off). When enabled, for each `:FileBundleReference` with exactly one `ShepardFile` reachable through its single default `:FileGroup`: moves the Mongo metadata doc from the per-bundle collection into the shared `_shepard_files` namespace; drops the `:FileBundleReference` + `:FileReference` labels and adds `:SingletonFileReference`; drops the synthetic default `:FileGroup` node; stamps `legacyV23Singleton: true` + `legacyV23BundleMongoId: <oid>` on the row for rollback. GridFS chunks stay in place (one shared bucket across the database). Rollback `V23_R__Rejoin_singletons_into_FileBundleReferences.cypher` (Cypher-only; refuses if any user-created singleton exists OR any V23-minted singleton has been patched — timestamp guard). `V24__Add_appId_constraint_SingletonFileReference.cypher` (V11-shape uniqueness constraint on the new label; always-on). | **Default upgrade is no-op.** Restart shepard; V24 (constraint) applies; V23 (carve-out) skips with an info-log unless the toggle is on. To opt in: set `shepard.migration.split-singletons.enabled=true`, take a Neo4j + MongoDB backup, restart. Migration logs every 1 000 rows; budget ~5–10 min per 10 000 singleton-shaped bundles. Verify via `cypher-shell`: `MATCH (s:SingletonFileReference) RETURN count(s) AS singletons, sum(CASE WHEN (s)-[:has_payload]->(:ShepardFile) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS singletons_with_file;` — both numbers should match. The toggle is opt-in for upgrades because moving Mongo metadata between collections is the kind of operation an admin wants to schedule. Greenfield installs may flip the toggle on at install time (no-op until bundles exist). Sibling **FR1b-ui** ships frontend drag-count dispatch separately. |
| **P4c — OpenAPI per-shelf split** | Two new public endpoints `GET /shepard/doc/openapi/{v1,v2}.json` serve per-shelf OpenAPI documents derived from the live combined doc via `OASFilter` (`V1OpenApiFilter` / `V2OpenApiFilter`). v1 keeps every non-`/v2/` non-platform path (matching both the raw `/shepard/api/...` shape and the post-`ApiPathFilter`-strip shape); v2 keeps only `/v2/...` paths; platform paths (`/healthz`, `/openapi`, `/openapi.json`, `/swagger-ui`, `/metrics`) belong to neither. Both endpoints honour `?format=yaml` (default JSON). The combined `/shepard/doc/openapi.json` smallrye-openapi already serves keeps working unchanged. Implementation: `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/common/openapi/OpenApiPerShelfRest.java` clones the live `OpenApiDocument.INSTANCE` via `MergeUtil` before filtering so the singleton is never mutated. `PublicEndpointRegistry` updated so the JWT filter lets the two new paths through pre-auth (OpenAPI specs are public — same posture as the combined doc). | ZERO | Two new public endpoints, no config keys, no schema. Combined `/shepard/doc/openapi.json` is unchanged — operators are not required to switch to the split docs. | — | **None for upgrade.** Tooling that wants a tree-shaken client can now generate against `/shepard/doc/openapi/v1.json` (upstream-compatible surface only) or `/shepard/doc/openapi/v2.json` (this fork's `/v2/` surface only) instead of the full combined doc. Generator-side adoption is tracked under P4b / P17. |
| **N1a — internal semantic repository (neosemantics)** | `aidocs/16` N1a + `aidocs/48`. New `SemanticRepositoryType.INTERNAL` enum value + `InternalSemanticConnector` routed through the in-process Neo4j-OGM session. New `N10sBootstrapHook` runs from `ShepardMain.init` **after** `MigrationsRunner.apply()` (post-A1e), invoking `CALL n10s.graphconfig.init` idempotently and ensuring the `n10s_unique_uri` constraint. The `infrastructure/docker-compose.yml` Neo4j service now declares `NEO4J_PLUGINS=["n10s"]` and adds `n10s.*` to `dbms.security.procedures.{allowlist,unrestricted}`. Lint-only `WHERE NOT n:Resource` audit script at `backend/src/test/resources/n10s-resource-label-audit.sh` flags shepard's Cypher writes that traverse without a label predicate (informational; not wired into CI in this slice). | **AWARE** | Two new optional config keys, both default-safe (the bootstrap hook gracefully degrades on a missing plugin): `shepard.semantic.internal.enabled=true` (master toggle — flip to `false` to skip the hook entirely), `shepard.semantic.internal.handle-vocab-uris=IGNORE` (forwarded verbatim to `n10s.graphconfig.init({handleVocabUris})`). The Neo4j compose service grows three env vars (`NEO4J_PLUGINS`, `NEO4J_dbms_security_procedures_unrestricted`, `NEO4J_dbms_security_procedures_allowlist`). Operators who haven't pulled the new compose file see `INTERNAL`-typed repositories report `healthCheck() == false` — the bootstrap hook logs a clear warning at startup, and every other repository type (`SPARQL` / `JSKOS` / `SKOSMOS`) continues to function unchanged. | — (no Cypher migration needed — the `n10s_unique_uri` constraint is created by the bootstrap hook itself, idempotently; n10s ships its own write paths under the `:Resource` label and doesn't touch shepard's domain graph). | **No action required for upgrade.** On a fresh `docker compose up -d neo4j` against the new compose file, the n10s plugin fetches automatically (`NEO4J_PLUGINS=["n10s"]`); shepard's startup hook then runs `n10s.graphconfig.init({handleVocabUris: 'IGNORE', applyNeo4jNaming: true, keepLangTag: true, handleMultival: 'ARRAY', handleRDFTypes: 'LABELS_AND_NODES'})` and ensures the unique-uri constraint. Operators who run their own Neo4j (i.e. didn't pull the new compose) keep their existing setup — the connector returns the empty map / `false` from `getTerm` / `healthCheck`. To opt in manually on an existing Neo4j: drop the n10s 5.x JAR into `/plugins`, add `n10s.*` to both `dbms.security.procedures.allowlist` and `dbms.security.procedures.unrestricted`, restart Neo4j, restart shepard. Test obligation: the testcontainer-based integration regression (n10s plugin actually loaded, real `graphconfig.init`, real `:Resource` write) is **deferred** — tracked in this row and `aidocs/44` N1a notes; pre-merge coverage is unit-level only (mocked OGM session). |
| **A5a — HDF5 / HSDS sidecar Phase 1** | `aidocs/35` A5a row. New `HdfContainer` Neo4j entity + `/v2/hdf-containers/{appId}` create / read / delete endpoints. New optional `shepard-hsds` compose service (`hdfgroup/hsds:v0.9.5`) under the `hdf` profile (off by default; mirrors the `spatial` profile shape). Phase 1 ships create / read / delete only — the per-DataObject `HdfReference` (A5c), the byte-identical download fallback (A5d), the shared-Keycloak token relay (A5e), and the permission bridge (A5b) all arrive in later phases. Phase 1 uses HTTP Basic between shepard and the HSDS sidecar; per-user OIDC token relay is A5e. | **AWARE** + **CONFIG** | New `/v2/hdf-containers/{appId}` endpoints (`POST`, `GET /{appId}`, `DELETE /{appId}`) — `/v2/` shelf, additive on the development surface; opt-in. Operators who never touch the `hdf` profile see no change. Five new config keys, all default-safe: `shepard.hdf.enabled=false` (master toggle), `shepard.hdf.hsds.endpoint=http://shepard-hsds:5101`, `shepard.hdf.hsds.username=` (empty default), `shepard.hdf.hsds.password=` (empty default), `shepard.hdf.hsds.timeout=PT10S`. When the toggle is on, **the operator MUST set `username` + `password`** — startup fails fast otherwise. New compose profile `hdf` enables the sidecar with a `./hsds-storage:/data` POSIX volume mount; capacity-planning rule of thumb is ~1.2× raw HDF5 size on disk. | `V25__Add_appId_constraint_HdfContainer.cypher` (V11-shape uniqueness constraint on the new `:HdfContainer` label, idempotent + fail-fast). No rollback file — the constraint is additive on a fresh label and `DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS` is one cypher line. **Migration regression test deferred** — testcontainer fixture for the constraint will land alongside the broader Neo4j migration testcontainer once that scaffold is in place; this row is the tracker anchor. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V25 applies on first start (zero rows in `:HdfContainer` until someone calls `POST /v2/hdf-containers`). To enable the feature: (1) put `HSDS_USERNAME` / `HSDS_PASSWORD` in `.env`, (2) mirror them onto the backend as `SHEPARD_HDF_HSDS_USERNAME` / `SHEPARD_HDF_HSDS_PASSWORD` + set `SHEPARD_HDF_ENABLED=true`, (3) `docker compose --profile hdf up -d`, (4) restart the backend. Full operator runbook in `docs/admin.md` §"HDF5 (HSDS) — opt-in sidecar". |
| **N1b — pre-seeded common ontologies (PROV-O / DC / schema.org / FOAF / QUDT / OM-2 / Time / GeoSPARQL)** | `aidocs/16` N1b + `aidocs/48 §4`. Eight ontology Turtle bundles ship at `backend/src/main/resources/ontologies/` (`prov-o.ttl`, `dublin-core.ttl`, `schema-org.ttl`, `foaf.ttl`, `qudt.ttl`, `om-2.ttl`, `time.ttl`, `geosparql.ttl`), each pinned by SHA-256 in `ontologies-manifest.json`. New `OntologySeedService` reads the manifest from classpath at startup, verifies SHA-256 per file, and calls `CALL n10s.rdf.import.inline($rdf, 'Turtle')` per bundle on the same OGM session as `N10sBootstrapHook` (n10s `import.fetch` doesn't accept `classpath:` URIs, hence inline). The hook invokes the seed service after the `n10s_unique_uri` constraint step — n10s's constraint absorbs duplicates so re-runs are idempotent (status `OK`, `triplesLoaded=0` on the second pass). The currently-bundled `.ttl` files are minimum-viable Turtle stubs carrying each ontology's canonical IRI prefix plus a handful of representative classes/properties (per-bundle 1-2 KB; ~16 KB total today) — enough for the casual annotation flow on a fresh install. Operators who want the full vocabularies replace the relevant `.ttl` and regenerate the manifest entry (or run the forthcoming N1c CLI). The design target of ~13 MB lands when the full canonical Turtle replaces today's stubs. ADR-0019 in `aidocs/63` records the default-on rationale. | **AWARE + CONFIG** | Two new optional config keys, both default-safe: `shepard.semantic.internal.preseed-ontologies.enabled=true` (default ON — casual users get a useful annotation vocabulary on day one; flip to `false` for a bare-n10s install), `shepard.semantic.internal.preseed-ontologies.skip-bundles=` (CSV of bundle ids to skip, e.g. `qudt,om-2`; default empty). The seed service is fail-soft per-bundle — a missing classpath file, SHA mismatch, or n10s call error is logged at WARN and the next bundle is attempted. Operators who haven't pulled the n10s plugin (per N1a) see the seed skip entirely; everything else (external `SPARQL` / `JSKOS` / `SKOSMOS`) continues unchanged. Bundled-ontology licences (PROV-O / Time: W3C Document License; DC / QUDT / OM-2: CC BY 4.0; schema.org: CC BY-SA 3.0; FOAF: CC BY 1.0; GeoSPARQL: OGC Open Data) are catalogued in `backend/src/main/resources/ontologies/LICENSING.md`; none fall in the banned licence families. | — (no Cypher migration — ontology import is content-level + idempotent; n10s dedups by IRI via the `n10s_unique_uri` constraint added by N1a). | **No action required for upgrade.** On the first restart post-N1b the seed service walks the eight bundled `.ttl` files and imports each via `n10s.rdf.import.inline`. Operators see one INFO log per bundle (`OntologySeedService: bundle '<id>' imported`) plus a final cumulative line (`pre-seed complete — seeded=N skipped=M failed=K; :Resource count A -> B`). To verify: `cypher-shell` → `MATCH (r:Resource) WHERE r.uri STARTS WITH 'http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#' RETURN count(r);` returns ≥ 1 once seeding has run. To opt out: set `shepard.semantic.internal.preseed-ontologies.enabled=false`. To skip a subset (e.g. don't want the units ontologies): set `shepard.semantic.internal.preseed-ontologies.skip-bundles=qudt,om-2`. To refresh a bundle to the upstream canonical Turtle (replacing the shepard-bundled stub): drop the canonical TTL in place at `backend/src/main/resources/ontologies/<file>.ttl`, recompute its SHA-256 + byte size, update the matching entry in `ontologies-manifest.json`, and restart shepard — the manifest mismatch alarm is the safety rail that prevents stale-pinned imports. Image-size impact: ~16 KB today (stubs), with a target of ~13 MB once N1c lands the canonical-Turtle refresh path. Test obligation: the testcontainer-level integration regression (real n10s plugin, real `import.inline`, assert `:Resource` rows post-import) is **deferred** alongside the N1a obligation; pre-merge coverage is unit-level (mocked OGM session + in-memory classloader). |
| **G1b — GitLab tracked-artifact adapter** | `aidocs/16` G1b + `aidocs/38` + `aidocs/63` ADR-0021. New `GitReferenceMode` enum on `:GitReference` (`LOOSE_LINK` / `TRACKED_ARTIFACT` / `PINNED_SNAPSHOT`); `V26__GitReference_mode_default.cypher` backfills `LOOSE_LINK` on pre-G1b rows (renumbered from V25 since A5a's V25 landed first). New `GitLabRestClient` (Java 21 HttpClient) + `GitArtifactCache` (Quarkus Caffeine, keyed by `userSub × repoUrl × ref × path`, PT5M TTL). New `GitAdapter` interface + `GitAdapterRegistry` seam — host-substring match on `gitlab`, with `shepard.git.adapter.gitlab.hosts` CSV override for self-hosted GitLab on non-obvious DNS. New `GET /v2/data-objects/{appId}/git-references/{refAppId}/preview` returns `{available, sha, mimeType, byteSize, content, contentTruncated, reason}` — `available=false` with `reason=no-credential` when the user hasn't stored a PAT. | **AWARE + CONFIG** | One new endpoint (`GET .../preview`); permission-checked on the parent DataObject. Six new optional config keys, all default-safe: `shepard.git.adapter.timeout=PT15S`, `shepard.git.adapter.gitlab.hosts=` (CSV), `shepard.git.cache.ttl=PT5M`, `shepard.git.cache.max-size=1000`, `shepard.git.preview.max-bytes=1048576`. Non-GitLab hosts return RFC 7807 `git.adapter.unsupported-host` 501; GitHub + Gitea adapters ship in G1d. | `V26__GitReference_mode_default.cypher` (idempotent backfill `mode=LOOSE_LINK` where null; additive nullable property otherwise). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V26 applies on first start. Existing `:GitReference` rows are backfilled to `LOOSE_LINK`. To use tracked-artifact mode: PATCH a reference with `{"mode": "TRACKED_ARTIFACT"}`, then GET `…/preview` (needs the user's `git.pat` stored via G1-cred for the matching host). Test obligation: integration-level (real GitLab via testcontainer) deferred; pre-merge coverage is unit-level (in-process `HttpServer` stub). |
| **G1c — PINNED_SNAPSHOT mode + RO-Crate SoftwareSourceCode export** | `aidocs/16` G1c + `aidocs/38`. Activates the third `GitReferenceMode` (`PINNED_SNAPSHOT`). Three changes: (1) `POST /v2/data-objects/{appId}/git-references` and `PATCH .../…` now resolve `ref` → commit SHA via the registered `GitAdapter` + caller's PAT at write time, then freeze the result onto `GitReference.sha` + `resolvedSha` + `resolvedAtMillis` (server-managed; the `sha` field is rejected with 400 if the client tries to set it directly). (2) New `ExportContributor` SPI in `backend-core` (already wired in `ExportService`); git plugin ships `GitReferenceExportContributor` — the first `@ApplicationScoped` contributor — which emits a `schema:SoftwareSourceCode` contextual entity in every RO-Crate export for a DataObject containing a `GitReference`. The entity's `@id` is a commit-SHA blob permalink (when sha + path are available), a commit URL (sha only), or the bare `repoUrl` as fallback. (3) SHA resolution order at export time: `sha` (pinned) → `resolvedSha` (last tracked fetch) → best-effort live resolution (silent on failure). | **ZERO** | No new endpoints. No new config keys. No schema migration (the `:GitReference` node label already has `sha`, `resolvedSha`, `resolvedAtMillis` properties since G1b — they were scaffolded nullable and this slice activates them). The `ExportContributor` SPI is purely additive to the `ExportService` dispatch loop and backward-compatible with all existing export consumers. Existing `GitReference` rows with mode `LOOSE_LINK` or `TRACKED_ARTIFACT` are unaffected. The only observable change for an operator is that RO-Crate ZIPs for DataObjects containing a `GitReference` will now include a `schema:SoftwareSourceCode` contextual entity in `ro-crate-metadata.json` — a JSON-LD additive extension that compliant RO-Crate consumers ignore if they don't understand it. | — (no migration — properties were scaffolded by G1b; this slice writes to them for the first time). | **No action required for upgrade.** To use PINNED_SNAPSHOT: `POST /v2/data-objects/{appId}/git-references` with `{"mode": "PINNED_SNAPSHOT", "repoUrl": "...", "ref": "v1.0.0"}` — shepard resolves `v1.0.0` to a commit SHA and returns it in the response `sha` field. The snapshot is thereafter immutable: PATCHing the reference does not re-resolve unless `mode` changes. Prerequisites: the caller must have a git credential stored via `/v2/me/git-credentials` for the matching host, and the host must have a registered adapter (GitLab / GitHub / Gitea). Missing adapter → 400. Missing credential → 400. Adapter failure → 502. Test obligation: unit-level (10 new tests); testcontainer-level integration deferred alongside G1b. |
| **G1d — check-update endpoint for tracked refs** | `aidocs/16` G1d + `aidocs/38`. New `POST /v2/data-objects/{appId}/git-references/{appId}/check-update` endpoint: resolves the GitReference's `ref` via the matching `GitAdapter` (using the caller's stored PAT *if present* — works without a PAT against public repos), compares the new SHA to the persisted `resolvedSha`, and returns `{currentSha, previousSha, updated, checkedAtMillis}`. Side-effect: refreshes `resolvedSha` + `resolvedAtMillis` on the entity even when nothing changed, so the timestamp tracks the last successful check. Unlike `pinSnapshot` (G1c, write-time only), this endpoint is read-mostly and the caller doesn't have to flip mode — it works for `LOOSE_LINK` / `TRACKED_ARTIFACT` / `PINNED_SNAPSHOT` alike (for `PINNED_SNAPSHOT` the result tells the user "your pin is N commits behind", though the pin itself isn't moved — that's a separate mode flip). Permission: Write on the parent DataObject (the call mutates `resolvedSha` + timestamp). 5 new unit tests in `GitReferenceServiceTest` covering blank-ref / no-adapter / public-repo-no-PAT / unchanged / moved / adapter-failure cases. Frontend "updated" badge that consumes this endpoint is a follow-up. | **ZERO** | **One new `/v2/` endpoint** (additive). No new config keys. No schema migration — reuses the `resolvedSha` / `resolvedAtMillis` properties scaffolded by G1b. No new dependencies. | — (no migration). | **No action required for upgrade.** Call `POST /v2/data-objects/<doAppId>/git-references/<refAppId>/check-update` to see whether the upstream `ref` has moved. The endpoint is safe to poll on a schedule (the underlying adapter calls are cached for PT5M per the G1b cache); pair it with the matching frontend "updated" badge once that lands. Public repos work without a stored PAT. |
| **APISIMP-BASICENTITY-DROP-ID (slice 1) — suppress Neo4j `id` from `/v2/containers` wire shape** | `aidocs/16` APISIMP-BASICENTITY-DROP-ID slice 1. Introduces `BasicEntityV2IO` (root foundation, abstract, `@JsonIgnoreProperties("id")`) and `BasicContainerV2IO extends BasicContainerIO @JsonIgnoreProperties("id")`; migrates `ContainerV2IO` to extend `BasicContainerV2IO`. The Neo4j internal node-id (`id: Long`) is no longer serialised in `/v2/containers/{appId}` or `GET /v2/containers?kind=…` responses. `appId` (UUID v7) is the sole stable identifier for callers. `getUniqueId()` on `ContainerV2IO` now returns `appId` rather than the numeric id string. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is not touched. | **AWARE — PRE-PROD WIRE BREAK on `/v2/containers`** | The JSON response shape changes for `GET /v2/containers/{appId}` and `GET /v2/containers?kind=…`: field `id` (number) is removed from the response body. Any client reading `.id` from container responses must switch to `.appId`.
