The Neo4j node-id is still used internally — no data is lost. | — (no Postgres or Neo4j schema migration; additive class hierarchy change only). | **No action required for upgrade.** The frontend already uses `appId` for all container navigation (per the APISIMP-TSCONT-APPID-KEY chain); no frontend changes needed. If you have scripts that read `container.id` from `/v2/containers`, update them to use `container.appId` instead. |
| **APISIMP-BASICENTITY-DROP-ID-2 — suppress Neo4j `id` from `/v2/collections` and DataObject wire shapes** | `aidocs/16` APISIMP-BASICENTITY-DROP-ID-2. Extends the `id`-suppression from slice 1 to the Collection and DataObject surfaces. New `CollectionV2IO extends CollectionIO @JsonIgnoreProperties({"id"})` in `v2/collection/io/`. `DataObjectDetailV2IO` and `DataObjectListItemV2IO` gain `@JsonIgnoreProperties({"id"})` directly (both are already v2-only classes). `CollectionV2Rest` returns `CollectionV2IO` for list / get / create / patch responses; the "carries both `id` and `appId`" description comment is removed. 10 new tests. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is not touched — `CollectionIO` and `DataObjectIO` are unchanged. | **AWARE — PRE-PROD WIRE BREAK on `GET|POST|PATCH /v2/collections[/{appId}]` and `GET /v2/collections/{appId}/data-objects[/{appId}]`** | The JSON response shape changes: field `id` (number) is removed from the response body of all Collection and DataObject `/v2/` endpoints. Any client reading `.id` from these responses must switch to `.appId`. Note: `dataObjectIds[]` and `incomingIds[]` in `CollectionV2IO` are still long arrays (pre-L2e numeric ids) — these are separate from the entity's own `id` and will be addressed in a later slice. | — (no Postgres or Neo4j schema migration; additive class hierarchy change only). | **No action required for upgrade.** The frontend uses `appId` for all DataObject and Collection navigation; no frontend changes needed. If you have scripts that read `.id` from `/v2/collections` or `/v2/collections/{appId}/data-objects` responses, update them to use `.appId` instead. |
| **APISIMP-PERM-AUDIT-LOG-APPID — drop BIGSERIAL `id` from audit-log wire shape; add `app_id` UUID column** | `aidocs/16` APISIMP-PERM-AUDIT-LOG-APPID. Eliminates the last numeric-id leak from `GET /v2/admin/permission-audit/log`. `PermissionAuditLogEntryIO` drops the `long id` (BIGSERIAL primary key) and adds `String appId` (UUID v7, stable row identifier). `permission_audit_log` table gets a new `app_id UUID NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()` column with a unique index (backfills existing rows atomically via Postgres default). `PermissionAuditLogQueryService` updates its SELECT clause from `id` to `app_id`. `backend-client/src/models/PermissionAuditLogEntry.ts` updated: `id: number` → `appId: string`. Unit tests updated. | **AWARE — PRE-PROD WIRE BREAK on `/v2/admin/permission-audit/log`** | The JSON response entries change shape: field `id` (number) is removed; field `appId` (string UUID) is added. Any script reading `.id` from audit-log entries must be updated to read `.appId`. The Postgres `BIGSERIAL` column (`id`) stays as the physical primary key — no data loss. | `V1.19.0__add_appid_to_permission_audit_log.sql` (idempotent `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS app_id UUID NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()` + unique index — backfills existing rows atomically). | **Operator action required if calling `GET /v2/admin/permission-audit/log` programmatically.** After upgrade, `V1.19.0` applies on first start; existing rows get a stable UUID. Update scripts that read `.id` (number) to read `.appId` (string). The frontend admin panel requires no change. |
| **F3 — permission audit log UI** | Frontend viewer for the F3 audit log endpoint. `PermissionAuditLogPane.vue` (filterable, paginated data table with actor / entityAppId / ISO-8601 from/to filters; action chips colour-coded GRANT/REVOKE/UPDATE); `useFetchPermissionAuditLog` composable; `AdminPermissionAuditApi` backend-client class; wired into the admin page under a new "Permission Audit Log" sidebar entry (`AdminFragments.PERMISSION_AUDIT_LOG`). 0 new backend changes. | **ZERO** | Pure frontend addition — no new endpoints, no new config keys, no schema changes. The backend endpoint (`GET /v2/admin/permission-audit/log`) was already shipped in the F3 backend slice. | — | **No action required for upgrade.** Refresh the admin UI; the "Permission Audit Log" entry appears in the sidebar and the viewer is immediately usable. |
| **Nuke endpoint** (`POST /v2/admin/instance/nuke`) | Nuclear instance-reset endpoint added to `InstanceAdminRest`. Wipes all research data (Collections, DataObjects, References, Containers, LabJournalEntries, SemanticAnnotations, Versions, Templates, Activities; secondary stores: MongoDB collections + Postgres timeseries records). Preserves identity/config nodes: `User`, `UserGroup`, `ApiKey`, `InstanceAdminGrant`, `InstanceRorConfig`, `SemanticRepository`, `OntologyConfig`, `SemanticConfig`, `FeatureToggle`, `SqlTimeseriesConfig`. Requires `instance-admin` role + exact confirmation phrase `"yes drop everything"` in the request body. Returns `NukeResultIO` with deletion counts. 10 new unit tests (`NukeServiceTest` + `InstanceAdminNukeRestTest`). | **AWARE** | **Additive `/v2/` endpoint.** No new config keys. No new Neo4j schema. No new Postgres migration. No `/shepard/api/` surface touched. **Intended for dev/demo instances only.** If you're running a shared production instance, add a network-level access rule restricting the `/v2/admin/*` path group from public access. | — | **No action required for upgrade.** The endpoint is inert unless invoked. To invoke: `POST /v2/admin/instance/nuke` with `Authorization: Bearer <instance-admin-token>` and body `{"confirmPhrase": "yes drop everything"}`. Response `200` with deletion counts on success; `400` if the phrase is wrong; `403` if not an instance-admin. **WARNING: this is irreversible.** There is no undo. Back up Neo4j before using it on any data you care about. |
| **APISIMP-USER-SEARCH-NO-PAGINATION — `GET /v2/users?q=` response wrapped in `PagedResponseIO`; `page`/`pageSize` added** | `aidocs/16` APISIMP-USER-SEARCH-NO-PAGINATION. `UserSearchV2Rest.searchUsers()` now returns `PagedResponseIO<UserIO>` (`{items, total, page, pageSize}`) instead of a bare `List<UserIO>`. New `@QueryParam("page") @DefaultValue("0") @PositiveOrZero int page` and `@QueryParam("pageSize") @DefaultValue("20") @Min(1) @Max(100) int pageSize` params. New `UserSearchService.searchPaged(body, page, pageSize)` delegates to new `SearchDAO.getUserTotalCount` + `findUsersPaged` (ORDER BY username ASC SKIP/LIMIT); existing `SearchDAO.findUsers()` (used by v1 `SearchRest`) is untouched. 5 unit tests in `UserSearchV2RestTest`. | **AWARE — PRE-PROD WIRE BREAK on `GET /v2/users?q=`** | The JSON response body changes from a bare array `[{...}, ...]` to `{"items": [...], "total": N, "page": 0, "pageSize": 20}`. Any client or script directly destructuring the response as an array must be updated to read `.items`. Default page size is 20; max is 100. The v1 `POST /shepard/api/search/users` surface is untouched. | — (no migration; code-only change). | **Action required only for programmatic callers of `GET /v2/users?q=`.** Update array destructuring to `.items`. The frontend user-search composable reads `PagedResponseIO` already — no frontend change needed. |
| **APISIMP-PERMISSION-AUDIT-LOG-PAGINATION — `GET /v2/admin/permission-audit/log` response wrapped in `PagedResponseIO`; `page`/`pageSize` bean-validated** | `aidocs/16` APISIMP-PERMISSION-AUDIT-LOG-PAGINATION. `InstanceAdminRest.permissionAuditLog()` now returns a `PagedResponseIO<PermissionAuditLogEntryIO>` envelope (`{items, total, page, pageSize}`) instead of a bare `List<>`. `X-Total-Count` header added matching the envelope `total`. New `PermissionAuditLogQueryService.count()` method (same WHERE filters as `query()`, `SELECT COUNT(*)`) feeds the total. `page` now carries `@PositiveOrZero`; `pageSize` carries `@Min(1) @Max(500)`. `@APIResponse` schema annotation updated from `SchemaType.ARRAY` to `PagedResponseIO.class`. 19 new/updated unit tests in `PermissionAuditLogRestTest`. | **AWARE — PRE-PROD WIRE BREAK on `GET /v2/admin/permission-audit/log`** | The JSON response body changes from a bare array `[{...}, ...]` to `{"items": [...], "total": N, "page": 0, "pageSize": 50}`. The `X-Total-Count: N` header is now set. Any client or script directly destructuring the response as an array must be updated to read `.items`. Plain `pageSize=0` or `page=-1` params now return 400 (bean validation) instead of silently clamping. The endpoint remains `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`; no new config keys; no schema change. | — (no Postgres or Neo4j migration; code-only change). | **Action required only for programmatic callers of `GET /v2/admin/permission-audit/log`.** Update array destructuring to `.items`; read `.total` for the count. The admin UI's `PermissionAuditLogPane.vue` already reads `body.items` and `X-Total-Count` per the F3 frontend spec — no frontend change needed. |
| **F3 — permission audit log** | `aidocs/16` F3 + `aidocs/24` F3. New `permission_audit_log` Postgres table (auto-migrated via Flyway `V1.10.0`). Every `PermissionsService` write path records one row: `createPermissions` → action `GRANT`; `updatePermissionsByNeo4jId` → action `UPDATE` (before/after JSON in `detail_json`); `deletePermissions` → action `REVOKE`. New `PermissionAuditLogService` (`@ApplicationScoped`, `AgroalDataSource`, fire-and-forget — a log failure never blocks the permissions write). New `GET /v2/admin/permission-audit/log` endpoint (`@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`, query params: `entityAppId`, `actor`, `from`/`to` ISO-8601, `page`/`size` default 0/50, max page-size capped at 500). Returns `PermissionAuditLogEntryIO` records sorted `occurred_at DESC`. 13 unit tests (`PermissionAuditLogServiceTest` + `PermissionAuditLogRestTest`). | **ZERO** | **Additive only.** New Postgres table (append-only, never mutated). One new `/v2/` endpoint. No new config keys. No Neo4j schema change. No `/shepard/api/` surface touched. | `V1.10.0__add_permission_audit_log_table.sql` (idempotent `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` + two `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS` — the only side-effect is a new Postgres table). No rollback file needed — `DROP TABLE IF EXISTS permission_audit_log` from `psql` is the one-liner. Migration regression test deferred — testcontainer fixture for the new table and index will land alongside the broader Postgres migration testcontainer scaffold. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; `V1.10.0` applies automatically at startup. Every subsequent permission write (grant/revoke/update) lands a row in `permission_audit_log`. Query via `GET /v2/admin/permission-audit/log` (instance-admin auth). To filter: add `?entityAppId=<uuid>&from=2026-01-01T00:00:00Z`. Retention is the operator's responsibility — the table is append-only; prune via `DELETE FROM permission_audit_log WHERE occurred_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '365 days'` or a pg_partman lifecycle rule. |
| **A5b — HDF5 / HSDS permission bridge** | `aidocs/35` A5b row + `aidocs/63` ADR-0020. Three pieces: (1) new `PermissionsChangedEvent` CDI event, fired from `PermissionsService.createPermissions` / `updatePermissionsByNeo4jId` / `deletePermissions`; (2) new `HdfPermissionBridge` observer (`@LookupIfProperty(shepard.hdf.enabled=true)`) that listens on `AFTER_SUCCESS` and rewrites the HSDS domain ACL via `HsdsClient.setDomainAcl` — shepard Reader → HSDS read, Writer → read+update+create+delete, Manager → all + ACL-mod; (3) new admin endpoint `POST /v2/admin/hdf/rebuild-acls` (`@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`) that re-derives every HSDS ACL from shepard's permission graph from scratch, returning a `{containersProcessed, containersSynced, errors[]}` shape. Bridge failures never block the user's shepard write — they're logged + queued in an in-memory retry list (size-capped). RFC 7807 ProblemJson on the admin endpoint's error paths (`hdf.disabled` for feature-off, `hdf.bridge.partial-failure` for enumeration failures). | **AWARE** | One new endpoint: `POST /v2/admin/hdf/rebuild-acls` (instance-admin only; returns 404 when `shepard.hdf.enabled=false`). No new config keys — the bridge reuses the existing `shepard.hdf.enabled` toggle. **Caveat for operators:** shepard is now the source of truth for HSDS ACLs. **Direct mutations on the HSDS side via `h5pyd` / `hsadmin` will get clobbered** on the next shepard permission change for the affected container. If an operator has been editing HSDS ACLs out of band, run `POST /v2/admin/hdf/rebuild-acls` once after upgrade to re-anchor the HSDS view on shepard's graph (idempotent, safe to re-run). | — (no migration — additive event + endpoint; no schema change) | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the event firing + observer wiring become active on first start. With the HDF feature off (the default), the observer is never instantiated and no HSDS round-trip is ever attempted. With the feature on, every existing permission write begins flowing into the HSDS sidecar best-effort. **If you've been editing HSDS ACLs directly:** invoke `POST /v2/admin/hdf/rebuild-acls` (instance-admin auth) once after upgrade to re-anchor. Test obligation: integration-level coverage (real HSDS testcontainer, full event → bridge → ACL round-trip) is **deferred** — pre-merge coverage is unit-level only (Mockito on `HsdsClient`; loopback HTTP server stub for the ACL JSON shapes). |
| **ONT1a — RO (Relation Ontology) added to the pre-seed bundle** | `aidocs/16` ONT1 + `aidocs/58 §6`. Follow-up to N1b that adds a ninth bundle, `backend/src/main/resources/ontologies/obo-relations.ttl`, SHA-256-pinned in `ontologies-manifest.json` (`id=obo-relations`, IRI prefix `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_`, canonical source `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ro.owl`, ~2 KB stub today carrying the headline relations the LUMEN-inspired showcase wants: `part_of` / `has_part` / `derives_from` / `participates_in` / `has_input` / `has_output` / `precedes`-family). RO ships under **CC0 1.0**, the most permissive of the bundled-ontology licences. The seed service is unchanged — N1b's manifest-driven loop picks the new entry up automatically; the only code touch is the class-Javadoc edit reflecting nine bundles instead of eight. LUMEN-seed integration that actually cites RO terms is **ONT1b**, deferred. | **AWARE** | One additional ontology in the pre-seed pass; same two config keys as N1b (`shepard.semantic.internal.preseed-ontologies.{enabled,skip-bundles}`). To exclude just RO: `skip-bundles=obo-relations`. No new config keys, no new endpoints, no schema delta. | — (no migration — same content-level idempotent path as N1b; the `n10s_unique_uri` constraint absorbs re-runs). | **No action required for upgrade.** On first restart post-ONT1a the seed service reports `seeded=9` instead of `seeded=8`; one extra INFO line `OntologySeedService: bundle 'obo-relations' imported`. To verify: `cypher-shell` → `MATCH (r:Resource) WHERE r.uri STARTS WITH 'http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_' RETURN count(r);` returns ≥ 1. To skip: set `shepard.semantic.internal.preseed-ontologies.skip-bundles=obo-relations`. Image-size impact: +~2 KB (stub); +~5 MB once N1c refreshes the bundle to the canonical OWL/RDF-XML. Test obligation: covered by the same testcontainer-level deferral as N1a / N1b; pre-merge coverage adds three unit tests asserting the 9-bundle manifest, RO IRI prefix + CC0 licence, and `skip-bundles=obo-relations` semantics. |
| **N1c — `shepard-admin semantic refresh-ontologies` CLI** | `aidocs/16` N1c + `aidocs/48 §4` + `aidocs/22 §7.1`. Operator-triggered refresh of the bundled ontologies against their pinned canonical URLs — the runtime complement to N1b's startup pre-seed (per ADR-0019). New backend endpoint `POST /v2/admin/semantic/refresh-ontologies` at `de.dlr.shepard.v2.admin.semantic.SemanticAdminRest` (instance-admin gated; JSON body `{ "bundles": [...], "force": false }`, both optional). New `OntologyRefreshService` walks the manifest, fetches each bundle's `canonicalUrl`, recomputes SHA-256, and re-imports via `n10s.rdf.import.inline` when the hash differs (or when `force=true`). Best-effort per bundle — partial failures land in the `errors[]` array with 200 OK overall, mirroring the seed service's fail-soft pattern. The CLI side ships `shepard-admin semantic refresh-ontologies [--bundles=prov-o,qudt] [--force]` as the third top-level subcommand alongside `features` / `health` / `migrations` (post-L1 mutation slice). | **AWARE** | One new endpoint on the `/v2/` development surface (`POST /v2/admin/semantic/refresh-ontologies`); operator-only by design — there is no frontend exposure. No new config keys (reuses the `shepard.semantic.internal.*` namespace). The endpoint depends on the n10s plugin being present (N1a); when it isn't, every per-bundle attempt errors with the n10s call-failure message and the operator sees a clean `errors[]` array rather than a 5xx. Refresh imports use the same `n10s_unique_uri` constraint as N1b, so re-runs against a hash-matched canonical (no diff) skip the import entirely and report `alreadyCurrent`. | — (no migration — content-level idempotent path, same as N1b). | **No action required for upgrade.** The endpoint and CLI subcommand are inert until an operator invokes them. Typical invocation: `shepard-admin semantic refresh-ontologies` (defaults to all nine bundles, no force) — output prints a one-line summary (`Refresh complete — requested=9 refreshed=7 alreadyCurrent=2 errors=0`) plus a per-bundle errors table when something failed; exit code 0 on full success, 1 on any per-bundle error. Useful after a major ontology release: `--bundles=prov-o,qudt` scopes to specific bundles, `--force` re-imports even when the hash matches (useful after an n10s data reset). Image-size impact: zero (no new bundled assets — refresh pulls from the canonical URL at runtime). Test obligation: testcontainer-level integration regression (real n10s, real canonical fetch) is **deferred** alongside N1a / N1b; pre-merge coverage is unit-level — `OntologyRefreshService` 87% line / 74% branch, `SemanticAdminRest` 100% line / 92% branch (against a JDK `HttpServer` loopback for the canonical-fetch path). |
| **UH1a — Helmholtz Unhide publish plugin baseline** | `aidocs/16` UH1a + `aidocs/67`. First `shepard-plugin-*` module ever. New Maven module `plugins/unhide/` whose sources are stitched into the backend uber-jar at build time via the `build-helper-maven-plugin` `add-source` / `add-test-source` hooks in `backend/pom.xml` (Phase 1 / ADR-0023 path — Phase 2 flips to packaged-JAR drop-in once the `PluginManifest` SPI lands; per ADR-0023's "UH1a is exempt from the full ServiceLoader hookup as long as the PluginManifest SPI hasn't landed"). Five new files in the plugin namespace: (1) `:UnhideConfig` Neo4j entity (`HasAppId`, singleton — runtime-mutable per `aidocs/67 §5`); (2) `UnhideConfigDAO` + `UnhideConfigService` (seed-on-first-start, RFC 7396 merge-patch, mint/verify/revoke harvest API key with SHA-256 + constant-time compare); (3) `UnhideAdminRest` exposing `GET / PATCH /v2/admin/unhide/config` + `POST /v2/admin/unhide/harvest-key/{rotate,revoke}` + `DELETE /v2/admin/unhide/harvest-key`, all `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`; (4) `UnhideFeedRest` exposing `GET /v2/unhide/feed.jsonld` (schema.org + metadata4ing JSON-LD) with the runtime auth model — 503 RFC 7807 `unhide.feed.disabled` when `:UnhideConfig.enabled=false`, 401 `unhide.harvest-key.absent` when `feedPublic=false` + missing/wrong `X-API-KEY`, 200 otherwise; (5) `UnhideFeedService` that projects every non-deleted Collection onto a `schema:Dataset` + `m4i:Dataset` entry with creator built from User firstName/lastName/displayName/username + ORCID `@id`. CLI parity: new `shepard-admin unhide {status,enable,disable,set-feed-public,set-contact-email,rotate-harvest-key,revoke-harvest-key}` subcommand group. Harvest-key plaintext is returned exactly once at mint, never logged (only the first-8 hex chars of the SHA-256 fingerprint), never stored, and never enters `:Activity` (PROV1a's `ProvenanceCaptureFilter` captures request method/path/status only). One small backend touch: `/v2/unhide/feed.jsonld` added to `PublicEndpointRegistry` because its access model is runtime-mutable on `:UnhideConfig` (a static path registry can't express it); the resource is `@PermitAll` and the per-call enabled/feedPublic/X-API-KEY check runs inside. Auth-fallback simplification (call-out): `aidocs/67 §5.1`'s "OR caller has instance-admin role" private-feed fallback is omitted in Phase 1 — the harvest key is the sole non-public auth path (operationally cleaner; the instance-admin inspection flow is "mint key, curl"). | **AWARE** | Four new config keys (all default-safe): `shepard.unhide.enabled=false` (master toggle install-default; seeds `:UnhideConfig.enabled` on first start), `shepard.unhide.feed.public=false` (install-default for the feed-public flag), `shepard.unhide.contact-email=` (install-default for the contact email surfaced in feed metadata), `shepard.unhide.feed.page-size=100` (cursor page size; **not** runtime-mutable per the CLAUDE.md "Buffer sizes / page sizes" exception). Three new RFC 7807 problem types: `unhide.feed.disabled` (503), `unhide.harvest-key.absent` (401), `unhide.config.read-only-field` (400). Two new public endpoints: `GET /v2/unhide/feed.jsonld` (auth predicate as above) + the four admin endpoints under `/v2/admin/unhide/...` (all instance-admin gated). `/shepard/api/...` surface unchanged. | `V30__Add_appId_constraint_UnhideConfig.cypher` — idempotent `CREATE CONSTRAINT IF NOT EXISTS FOR (n:UnhideConfig) REQUIRE n.appId IS UNIQUE` (mirrors the V11 / V22 / V25 / V26 idiom; the service-layer seed in `UnhideConfigService#seedIfNeeded()` holds the singleton invariant at the application layer; this constraint is the database-side belt-and-braces). V29 was taken to avoid V29 already used by KIP1a on the parallel slice (`SemanticConfig` + `UserOntologyBundle`); constraint-only migrations are independently rebasable. | **No action required for upgrade.** A fresh restart post-UH1a sees one INFO line on startup (`UH1a: seeded :UnhideConfig singleton (appId=…, enabled=false, feedPublic=false)`). The plugin is inert (enabled=false default); the feed endpoint returns 503 `unhide.feed.disabled` until an operator opts in. To opt in: `shepard-admin unhide enable && shepard-admin unhide set-contact-email <ops-email> && shepard-admin unhide rotate-harvest-key` (the rotate prints the plaintext exactly once — pipe it into a secret manager immediately). To verify: `cypher-shell` → `MATCH (n:UnhideConfig) RETURN n.enabled, n.feedPublic, n.contactEmail;`. To inspect the feed: `curl -H "X-API-KEY: <plaintext>" https://shepard.example.dlr.de/v2/unhide/feed.jsonld`. Image-size impact: ~12 KB (plugin classes + IO records). Test obligation: 53 plugin-unit + 12 CLI + 14 PublicEndpointRegistry tests landed in-tree; testcontainer-level integration regression (boot Quarkus + real Neo4j, mint a key end-to-end, hit feed, assert 200/401/503 paths) is **deferred** alongside the N1a / N1b / ONT1b precedent. |
| **UH1b + UH1c — Unhide feed entries grow m4i provenance + KIP citation** | `aidocs/16` UH1b/UH1c + `aidocs/67 §9` + `aidocs/64 §3.2` (PROV1h m4i shape) + `aidocs/66 §3.2` (KIP record shape). Two sibling additive extensions to the UH1a `GET /v2/unhide/feed.jsonld` feed-entry shape — no new endpoints, no new admin surface, no new config keys other than one buffer-sizing knob. **UH1b**: each entry's body grows a `m4i:hasProcessingStep` array inlining the most-recent N `:Activity` rows targeting the Collection. Each step is a self-contained metadata4ing `ProcessingStep` node (dual-typed `["m4i:ProcessingStep", "prov:Activity"]`, with `prov:startedAtTime` / `prov:endedAtTime` typed `xsd:dateTime`, plus `m4i:hasMethod` / `m4i:hasInput` (READ-flavour) / `m4i:hasOutput` (write-flavour) / `prov:wasAssociatedWith` agent-id stub / `prov:type` + `shepard:summary` + `shepard:originInstance`) rendered via a new `ProvJsonLdRenderer.renderActivityAsM4iNode(Activity)` entry point that emits the per-activity body without the standalone `@context` / `@graph` wrapper — the feed's own top-level `@context` (from UH1a) already declares the `m4i:` / `prov:` / `xsd:` / `shepard:` namespaces, so the inner nodes carry compact prefixed terms. The renderer reuses PROV1h's existing m4i logic (no duplication; same `activityTypes(true)` / `typedDateTime` / `isReadAction` / `isWriteAction` helpers). Window size is the deploy-time-only `shepard.unhide.feed.provenance-window` property (default 5, hard cap 100; "buffer-sizing" CLAUDE.md exception — no operator demand for per-window runtime tuning). Collections without activities omit the field entirely (absent-key JSON-LD semantics; not `[]`, not `null`). DAO failures fail-soft (logged at WARN + field omitted; one slow Cypher hop never fails the whole feed page — same posture as the UnhideConfigService startup-seed). **UH1c**: each entry's body grows three KIP citation fields when the Collection has been published via KIP1a — `schema:identifier` (schema.org `PropertyValue` carrying the PID under `propertyID="pid"`), `schema:url` (the public `/v2/.well-known/kip/{pid-suffix}` resolver URL), and `m4i:hasIdentifier` (the same PID, m4i-flavoured for NFDI4Ing consumers). Collections without a Publication omit all three fields entirely (schema.org "no identifier" semantics). The plugin never reads `:Publication` directly — it goes through `PublicationDAO.findByEntityAppId` from KIP1a. Multi-publication Collections (idempotent re-publish via `?force=true` on `POST /v2/{kind}/{appId}/publish`) cite the row with the latest `mintedAt` via explicit `max()` (hardened against future DAO sort-order changes). | **AWARE** | **Zero new endpoints, zero new schema constraints, zero new admin REST.** One new deploy-time-only config key: `shepard.unhide.feed.provenance-window=5` (buffer-sizing exception per CLAUDE.md; hard cap 100). The feed-entry wire shape grows two namespaces already declared at the top-level `@context` of the feed in UH1a (`m4i:` and `prov:`, plus the inner nodes also use `xsd:` for typed literals — already in scope from UH1a) plus three optional `schema:` citation fields per published Collection. Existing feed consumers that don't parse the new keys ignore the extra fields per JSON-LD's open-world semantics — wire shape grows but plain readers stay happy. `/shepard/api/...` surface unchanged. Plugin-first per CLAUDE.md: all feed-side changes are inside `plugins/unhide/`; the renderer's new `renderActivityAsM4iNode(Activity)` entry point lives in core because the m4i shape itself is a cross-cutting SPI every plugin compiles against per `aidocs/47 §2`. | — (no migration — content-shape changes on the existing feed; the V30 `:UnhideConfig` constraint from UH1a is the only schema artefact in play). | **No action required for upgrade.** UH1b/UH1c shipping does not flip any default; the feed-entry shape grows whether or not an operator opts the plugin on. Operators who've enabled Unhide via UH1a will see the new fields appear in `GET /v2/unhide/feed.jsonld` on the next restart with no further action. To verify post-upgrade: `curl -H "X-API-KEY: <plaintext>" https://shepard.example.dlr.de/v2/unhide/feed.jsonld | jq '.entries[] | select(."m4i:hasProcessingStep")'` shows per-entry provenance, and `... | jq '.entries[] | select(."schema:identifier")'` shows KIP-cited Collections. To tune the provenance-window per page entry (e.g. for an instance with very chatty audit trails): set `shepard.unhide.feed.provenance-window=3` in `application.properties` and restart; hard cap is 100. Image-size impact: ~3 KB additional plugin classes + the renderer's `renderActivityAsM4iNode` method (~1.5 KB in core). Test obligation: 12 new `ProvJsonLdRendererTest` cases on the per-activity m4i path (null-input safety, dual-typing, agent-id stub collapse, READ-vs-write mapping, summary/origin propagation, no-target-appId omission, fresh-map allocation per call) plus the expanded `UnhideFeedServiceTest` covering `buildProcessingSteps` (window clamping, null/blank appId, DAO fail-soft, sort-order preservation) and `buildKipCitation` (multi-publication max-mintedAt selection, blank-PID skip, DAO fail-soft, absent-publication omission). Testcontainer-level integration regression (boot Quarkus + real Neo4j + activities + publication, hit feed, assert m4i + schema fields appear) is **deferred** alongside the UH1a precedent. |
| **ONT1b — metadata4ing (NFDI4Ing v1.4.0) added to the pre-seed bundle** | `aidocs/16` ONT1b. Sibling follow-up to ONT1a that adds a tenth bundle, `backend/src/main/resources/ontologies/metadata4ing.ttl`, SHA-256-pinned in `ontologies-manifest.json` (`id=metadata4ing`, IRI prefix `http://w3id.org/nfdi4ing/metadata4ing/`, canonical source `https://w3id.org/nfdi4ing/metadata4ing/1.4.0/`, ~2.7 KB stub today carrying the headline classes/properties: `m4i:ProcessingStep` subtype of `prov:Activity`, `m4i:Method`, `m4i:Tool`, `m4i:InvestigatedObject` subtype of `prov:Entity`, `m4i:NumericalVariable`, `m4i:hasUnit` / `hasValue` / `hasParticipant`, `m4i:Person` / `m4i:Organization` subtypes of the `prov:` equivalents). metadata4ing ships under **CC BY 4.0** and is the NFDI4Ing engineering-research extension of W3C PROV-O — composes cleanly with this fork's PROV1-series baseline (`aidocs/55`) and RO-Crate export. The seed service is unchanged; manifest-driven loop picks the new entry up automatically. Unblocks the future PROV1h slice that will lift PROV-O rendering on `/v2/provenance/*` to `m4i:`-flavoured shapes via an `Accept: application/ld+json; profile=metadata4ing` content-type (not yet scheduled — separate dispatch). | **AWARE** | One additional ontology in the pre-seed pass; same two config keys as N1b / ONT1a (`shepard.semantic.internal.preseed-ontologies.{enabled,skip-bundles}`). To exclude just metadata4ing: `skip-bundles=metadata4ing`. No new config keys, no new endpoints, no schema delta. | — (no migration — same content-level idempotent path as N1b / ONT1a; the `n10s_unique_uri` constraint absorbs re-runs). | **No action required for upgrade.** On first restart post-ONT1b the seed service reports `seeded=10` instead of `seeded=9`; one extra INFO line `OntologySeedService: bundle 'metadata4ing' imported`. To verify: `cypher-shell` → `MATCH (r:Resource) WHERE r.uri STARTS WITH 'http://w3id.org/nfdi4ing/metadata4ing/' RETURN count(r);` returns ≥ 1. To skip: set `shepard.semantic.internal.preseed-ontologies.skip-bundles=metadata4ing`. Image-size impact: +~2.7 KB (stub); +~600 KB once N1c refreshes the bundle to the canonical w3id.org Turtle. Test obligation: covered by the same testcontainer-level deferral as N1a / N1b / ONT1a; pre-merge coverage adds three unit tests asserting the 10-bundle manifest + m4i row, IRI prefix + CC BY 4.0 licence + canonical URL pin, and `skip-bundles=metadata4ing` semantics. |
| **AI1r — `shepard-experiment` domain ontology added to the pre-seed bundle** | `aidocs/16` AI1r. Adds the eleventh bundle, `backend/src/main/resources/ontologies/shepard-experiment.ttl` (~18.8 KB shepard-authored stub), SHA-256-pinned in `ontologies-manifest.json` (`id=shepard-experiment`, IRI prefix `https://shepard.dlr.de/ontologies/experiment#`, version `1.0.0`, CC BY 4.0). Seven SKOS ConceptSchemes: **ExperimentPhase** (6 concepts — Preparation / Conditioning / TestRun / Cooldown / PostProcessing / Analysis; the ConceptScheme also carries `rdfs:subClassOf prov:Activity` so individual phase instances can be materialised as PROV activities with `prov:startedAtTime` / `prov:endedAtTime` provenance stamps — composing directly with the PROV1-series and PROV1h m4i rendering); **MeasurementRole** (5 concepts — Reference / Actual / Deviation / Characterization / Calibration; CalibrationMeasurement carries a `qudt:hasQuantityKind qudt:QuantityKind` hook); **QualityFlag** (5 concepts — Pass / Warning / Fail / Suspect / Pending); **DefectType** (8 concepts — Delamination / Crack / Porosity / Disbond / ImpactDamage / FiberMisalignment / VoidContent / ForeignObject); **InspectionMethod** (7 concepts — UltrasonicCScan / UltrasonicAScan / Thermography / DIC / XRayInspection / EddyCurrentTesting / VisualInspection); **ManufacturingProcess** (8 concepts — AFP / ATL / HandLayup / Welding / Machining / Curing / Bonding / HeatTreatment); **SensorRole** (4 concepts — Primary / Reference / Redundant / CalibrationTarget; CalibrationTarget also carries `qudt:hasQuantityKind`). Unlike the other pre-seed bundles (which stub upstream-published vocabularies), this bundle is **shepard-authored** — there is no canonical upstream URL to refresh from; it is the authoritative source. The seed service is unchanged; manifest-driven loop picks the new entry up automatically. `required=false` — operators can exclude via `skip-bundles=shepard-experiment`. | **ADDITIVE** | One additional ontology in the pre-seed pass (`seeded=11` on first restart post-AI1r); same two config keys as N1b / ONT1a / ONT1b (`shepard.semantic.internal.preseed-ontologies.{enabled,skip-bundles}`). To exclude: `skip-bundles=shepard-experiment`. No new config keys, no new endpoints, no schema delta. `/shepard/api/...` surface unchanged. | — (no migration — same content-level idempotent path as N1b / ONT1a / ONT1b; the `n10s_unique_uri` constraint absorbs re-runs). | **No action required for upgrade.** On first restart post-AI1r the seed service reports `seeded=11` instead of `seeded=10`; one extra INFO line `OntologySeedService: bundle 'shepard-experiment' imported`. To verify: `cypher-shell` → `MATCH (r:Resource) WHERE r.uri STARTS WITH 'https://shepard.dlr.de/ontologies/experiment#' RETURN count(r);` returns ≥ 7 (one per ConceptScheme). To skip: set `shepard.semantic.internal.preseed-ontologies.skip-bundles=shepard-experiment`. Image-size impact: +~18.8 KB (the entire vocab is bundled; no refresh-to-canonical needed since this is the canonical). Test obligation: covered by the same testcontainer-level deferral as N1a / N1b / ONT1a / ONT1b; no additional unit tests required (domain artefact, not logic under test; seed service is already tested). |
| **PROV1h — metadata4ing (m4i) content-negotiation on `/v2/provenance/*`** | `aidocs/16` PROV1h + `aidocs/64 §3.2` + `aidocs/55 §6`. Additive content-type negotiation on the three existing `/v2/provenance/` read endpoints (`/activities`, `/entity/{appId}`, `/count`) — no new endpoints, no new config keys, no migration. `Accept: application/json` (the default) returns today's plain JSON shape **unchanged** (byte-compatible regression). `Accept: application/ld+json` returns plain PROV-O JSON-LD with the `prov:` / `shepard:` / `xsd:` namespaces embedded inline in `@context` and activities typed as `prov:Activity` with `prov:startedAtTime` / `prov:endedAtTime` / `prov:wasAssociatedWith` / `prov:used` / `prov:generated` per the W3C PROV-O vocabulary. `Accept: application/ld+json; profile="https://w3id.org/nfdi4ing/metadata4ing/"` (or short `profile=metadata4ing`) returns the m4i flavour — adds the `m4i:` namespace, dual-types activities as `["m4i:ProcessingStep", "prov:Activity"]` (keeping the PROV-O parent in the array so PROV-O-only readers still parse), agents as `["m4i:Person", "prov:Agent", "prov:Person"]`, entities as `["m4i:InvestigatedObject", "prov:Entity"]`, and surfaces `m4i:hasInput` (READ) / `m4i:hasOutput` (CREATE/UPDATE/DELETE/EXECUTE) / `m4i:hasMethod` (pragmatic mapping from action-kind) on top of the parallel PROV-O fields. `/count` JSON-LD wraps the integer as `shepard:numberOfActivities` typed `xsd:nonNegativeInteger` under the same context family. Unknown `profile=` parameter → RFC 7807 406 `provenance.unsupported-profile`. Designed in `aidocs/64 §3.2`; gated on ONT1b's m4i preseed (already shipped). | ZERO | No new endpoints (the negotiation is on existing JAX-RS resource methods via `@Produces` dispatch). No new config keys. No new dependencies (no JSON-LD parsing library — emission only). No schema delta. The plain-JSON callers — every existing client, including the frontend's sparkline composable — see no wire change. The two new JSON-LD media types are opt-in per request via `Accept`. | — (no migration — additive response shape on existing endpoints; the n10s `n10s_unique_uri` constraint that scopes the m4i ontology terms was already created by N1a + filled by ONT1b). | **No action required for upgrade.** The negotiation is inert until a client sends `Accept: application/ld+json` (with or without the `profile=` parameter). Test the new surface from the CLI: `curl -H 'Accept: application/ld+json' https://shepard.example/v2/provenance/activities` → PROV-O JSON-LD; add `; profile=metadata4ing` to the Accept header → m4i shape. Clients that want to ingest the activity log into a SPARQL store (Apache Jena, RDF4J, Stardog) can now pipe the response through any JSON-LD-aware loader; the `@context` is embedded so the loader needs no network round-trip to resolve namespaces. **Plugin-first note**: PROV1h ships in-tree per CLAUDE.md's plugin-first rule — it extends the existing in-tree provenance feature (the renderer is one more output shape on top of `:Activity`), not a new external integration. The m4i terms themselves come from N1b/ONT1b's pre-seeded `metadata4ing.ttl` bundle; PROV1h adds the rendering, not the vocabulary. Test obligation: 56 new unit tests in this slice cover the renderer (`ProvJsonLdRendererTest`, 36 cases; 97% line / 85% branch) + the resource dispatch (`ProvenanceRestJsonLdTest`, 20 cases; new content-neg methods 93-100% line). |
| **M4I-b — canonical m4i predicates in `ProvJsonLdRenderer` (BREAKING for m4i consumers)** | `aidocs/16` M4I-b + `aidocs/semantics/94 §4.2`. Bug fix: `ProvJsonLdRenderer` was emitting three non-canonical predicates that do **not exist** in the published m4i 1.4.0 OWL (`m4i:hasMethod`, `m4i:hasInput`, `m4i:hasOutput`). Rewritten to canonical predicates: `m4i:hasMethod` → `m4i:realizesMethod`; `m4i:hasInput` → `obo:RO_0002233`; `m4i:hasOutput` → `obo:RO_0002234`. Added `"obo": "http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/"` to the `@context` when `profile=m4i`. Added `m4i:investigates` from activity to entity on READ actions. Affects both code paths: `render(activities, m4i)` (the full `@graph` renderer backing `/v2/provenance/activities?Accept: …;profile=metadata4ing`) and `renderActivityAsM4iNode(Activity)` (the UH1b per-entry embed path). Verified against canonical OWL at `https://nfdi4ing.pages.rwth-aachen.de/metadata4ing/metadata4ing/`. | **BREAKING** | **Zero new endpoints, zero new config keys, zero migration.** The wire shape change is on the m4i-flavoured `Accept: application/ld+json; profile=metadata4ing` path only; plain `Accept: application/json` callers see no change; PROV-O callers (`Accept: application/ld+json` without a `profile=` param) see no change. | — (no migration — wire-shape bug fix on an opt-in content-type). | **Action required only for m4i consumers.** If you have a client explicitly reading `m4i:hasMethod` / `m4i:hasInput` / `m4i:hasOutput` from the `profile=metadata4ing` response shape, update it to `m4i:realizesMethod` / `obo:RO_0002233` / `obo:RO_0002234`. Real-world risk is low (no known external consumer; the old predicates were undefined in canonical m4i and any standards-conformant m4i parser would have ignored or rejected them already). Also update any SPARQL queries targeting these predicates. The UH1b Unhide feed `m4i:hasProcessingStep` embeds use `renderActivityAsM4iNode` — the same canonicalisation applies there; consumers of the Unhide feed's embedded provenance steps must also update. To verify post-upgrade: `curl -H 'Accept: application/ld+json; profile=metadata4ing' /v2/provenance/activities \| jq '.["@graph"][] \| select(."m4i:realizesMethod")` — should return activity nodes. Old `m4i:hasMethod` must return empty. |
| **M4I-c + M4I-d — m4i DataObject SHACL shape + renderer + content-neg** | `aidocs/16` M4I-c + M4I-d + `aidocs/semantics/94 §4.3 / §4.4 / §12`. Three additive pieces: (1) new SHACL shape `backend/src/main/resources/shapes/m4i-dataobject-shape.ttl` declaring the m4i:DataObjectShape contract (mandatory: `m4i:InvestigatedObject` type, `dcterms:identifier`, `dcterms:title`, `schema:dateCreated`; optional: `m4i:hasIdentifier`, `obo:RO_0002233/4`, `prov:wasGeneratedBy`, `m4i:realizesMethod`, `m4i:hasEmployedTool`, `m4i:hasNumericalVariable`, `schema:keywords`); (2) new `M4iDataObjectRenderer` (in `de.dlr.shepard.v2.m4i`) with three lazy resolvers — `MethodResolver` mints `shepard:method/<actionKind>` on the fly, `ToolResolver` mints `shepard:tool/<targetKind>`, `NumericalVariableResolver` promotes `SemanticAnnotation` rows with non-null `numericValue` to typed `m4i:NumericalVariable` blank nodes carrying `qudt:unit` (consumes the QUDT unit IRIs written by the 2026-05-28 channel-axes-and-units recovery script); (3) content-negotiation on the existing nested resource `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects/{dataObjectAppId}` — `Accept: application/ld+json; profile="https://w3id.org/nfdi4ing/metadata4ing/"` (or short `profile=metadata4ing`) returns the m4i-flavoured projection, unknown profile → 406 RFC 7807 `dataobject.unsupported-profile`, plain JSON callers see no change. Renderer architecture is an **overlay** over the existing DataObjectService read path (one Cypher fetch + one Activity lookup), not a parallel renderer with its own DAO chain (board consultation in §12). pyShacl acceptance script at `examples/mffd-showcase/scripts/validate_m4i_shape.py` validates the live LUMEN + MFFD seeds. **Note on the design-doc path mismatch**: §4.3 names `/v2/data-objects/{appId}` as the target; the actual L2d Phase A.2 resource is the nested form. Content-neg shipped on the nested path; a flat `/v2/data-objects/{appId}` shelf is an L2 follow-up. **Cut**: channel-level numeric variables (95 `:AnnotatableTimeseries` unit annotations addressable only via a multi-hop DO → ref → container → channel walk) are deferred to `M4I-d-3-followup` in `aidocs/16`. | ZERO | **No new endpoints** (content-neg on existing resource). **No new config keys.** **No schema delta.** Plain-JSON callers see no wire change. The new JSON-LD media-type opt-in is per-request. The renderer is CDI-injected — no startup-side effects until the first m4i-flavoured request arrives. | — (no migration — additive response shape on existing endpoint + new classpath resource file `shapes/m4i-dataobject-shape.ttl` that is read once at first render via classpath; no n10s ingest). | **No action required for upgrade.** The negotiation is inert until a client sends `Accept: application/ld+json; profile="https://w3id.org/nfdi4ing/metadata4ing/"`. Verify post-upgrade: `curl -H 'Accept: application/ld+json; profile=metadata4ing' "https://shepard.example.dlr.de/v2/collections/<coll-appid>/data-objects/<do-appid>"` returns a JSON-LD body with `@context` declaring `m4i`/`obo`/`prov`/`dcterms`/`schema`/`qudt`/`shepard`/`xsd` and the DO node carries `dcterms:identifier`, `dcterms:title`, `schema:dateCreated` minimum. Run the acceptance script: `pip install pyshacl rdflib requests && python3 examples/mffd-showcase/scripts/validate_m4i_shape.py --shepard-url https://shepard.example.dlr.de --api-key $KEY --collection-id <collection-appid>`. Expected on LUMEN + MFFD seeds: 100% pass on mandatory triples; optional-triple violations (no Activity, no predecessors) are normal and don't fail the script. Image-size impact: ~6 KB (renderer + shape file + test classes). Test obligation: 26 new `M4iDataObjectRendererTest` cases shipped in-tree; pyShacl integration is the Python sidecar acceptance script (Java testcontainer pyShacl integration deferred). |
| **PROV1i — SnapshotService auto-emits `prov:Entity` typing on creation** | `aidocs/16` PROV1i + `aidocs/55`. After `SnapshotService.createSnapshot` persists the `:Snapshot` node and its `:SnapshotEntry` rows, it now automatically creates a `:SemanticAnnotation` with `propertyIRI = rdf:type`, `valueIRI = prov:Entity`, `subjectAppId = snapshot.appId`, `subjectKind = "Snapshot"`, `source = "system"`, `sourceMode = "ai"`. This annotation is picked up by `GET /v2/annotations?subjectKind=Snapshot` and by any SPARQL query over `SemanticAnnotation` nodes. The typing is best-effort: if the DAO write fails, the failure is logged at WARN and the snapshot is still returned to the caller. No new endpoints, no new config keys, no migration (annotations are already `:SemanticAnnotation` nodes; the Snapshot entity already has `appId`). | **AWARE** | **No new endpoints. No new config keys. No migration.** The `SemanticAnnotationV2DAO` is injected into `SnapshotService` — a new CDI dependency. Existing `SnapshotService` callers see no change; all three REST resources (`CollectionSnapshotRest`, `SnapshotRest`, `SnapshotDiffRest`) are unaffected. Import scripts that previously emitted the typing manually (e.g. `mffd-import-v15.py:1438,1556`) will now emit a duplicate annotation if they still fire — harmless (both annotations have distinct `appId`s), but the import script's manual path should be removed in the next importer slice. | — (no migration — `SemanticAnnotation` nodes exist; the new typing is just an additional node written at snapshot creation time). | **No action required for upgrade.** Post-restart, every new snapshot automatically carries a `prov:Entity` annotation queryable via `GET /v2/annotations?subjectKind=Snapshot`. Pre-existing snapshots keep their existing annotation state; to back-fill, run: `GET /v2/collections/{appId}/snapshots` → for each snapshot, `POST /v2/annotations` with `{subjectAppId: snap.appId, subjectKind: "Snapshot", predicateIri: "http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type", objectIri: "http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#Entity", sourceMode: "ai"}`. **Verifying post-upgrade**: create a snapshot; `GET /v2/annotations?subjectKind=Snapshot&subjectAppId=<snap.appId>` → returns one annotation with `propertyIRI = rdf:type`. |
| **KIP1a — HMC Kernel Information Profile baseline** | `aidocs/16` KIP1a + `aidocs/66`. New in-tree `Minter` SPI (`de.dlr.shepard.publish.minter.Minter`) — Handle/DOI minting interface every plugin compiles against — plus `MockMinter` (the default in-core impl, PID format `mock:shepard:<kind>:<appId>:<epoch-millis>`) and `MinterRegistry` (CDI discovery + fail-fast resolution against the configured id at startup). New `:Publication` Neo4j entity (`HasAppId`, append-only by KIP convention) attached to a published entity via `(entity)-[:HAS_PUBLICATION]->(publication)`; properties carry `pid`, `mintedAt`, `minterId`, `publishedBy`, `entityKind`, `entityAppId`. Two new endpoints: `POST /v2/{kind}/{appId}/publish` mints + attaches (auth: Write/Manage on the entity; idempotent — re-POST returns existing row; `?force=true` mints fresh), and `GET /v2/.well-known/kip/{pid-suffix}` returns the public HMC KIP record (unauthenticated; `landingPage` URL it points at may still require auth — that's correct per `aidocs/66 §4.2`). Supported `{kind}` segments in KIP1a: `data-objects`, `collections`; future slices (bundles, files, lab-journal-entries) add a row to the `PublishableKind` enum without changing the URL shape. ePIC (KIP1c) + DataCite (KIP1d) ship as `shepard-plugin-minter-{epic,datacite}` drop-in JARs per ADR-0023 — the in-tree `Minter` interface is the SPI seam. | **AWARE + CONFIG** | One new config key (deploy-time-only): `shepard.publish.minter=mock` selects the active minter at startup — switching the active PID provider is a re-bootstrap decision, so the "cluster identity / topology" CLAUDE.md exception applies and there is no runtime `:*Config` for this knob (per-minter knobs like the ePIC handle prefix land with the plugin in KIP1c/d using the standard admin-runtime `:*Config` pattern). Two new endpoints on the `/v2/` development surface; the `.well-known/kip/*` family is registered in `PublicEndpointRegistry` via a new `PUBLIC_PATH_PREFIXES` set + `matchesPrefix` helper (the prefix's next-char-must-be-`/` rule closes the same foot-gun H5 closed for the exact-match list — `/v2/.well-known/kip-foo` correctly doesn't match `/v2/.well-known/kip`). RFC 7807 problem+json on every non-200 path: `publish.kind.unsupported` (404 for unknown URL segments), `publish.entity.wrong-kind` (404 for appId-of-wrong-label), `publish.minter.failed` (500 for adapter throws), `kip.pid.not-found` (404 for unknown PID suffix). Frozen upstream classes (`BasicEntityIO`, `DataObjectRest`, `CollectionRest`) untouched per CLAUDE.md. | `V29__Add_appId_constraint_Publication.cypher` (V11-shape uniqueness constraint on the new `:Publication` label; idempotent + fail-fast). No rollback file — additive constraint on a fresh label; one-line `DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS` reverses it. **Migration regression test deferred** — testcontainer fixture for the constraint will land alongside the broader Neo4j migration testcontainer once that scaffold is in place; this row is the tracker anchor. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V29 applies on first start (zero rows in `:Publication` until someone calls `POST .../publish`). To use the publish surface: hit `POST /v2/data-objects/{appId}/publish` (or `/v2/collections/{appId}/publish`) with Writer or Manager on the entity; the response carries the freshly-minted PID + the `resolverUrl` (the public `/v2/.well-known/kip/{pid-suffix}` URL clients can dereference without auth). To switch the active minter (KIP1c/d once shipped): set `shepard.publish.minter=epic` or `shepard.publish.minter=datacite` in `application.properties` / env (`SHEPARD_PUBLISH_MINTER`); a startup-time fail-fast confirms the matching plugin JAR is in `backend/plugins/`. KIP1b folded into KIP1a (the resolver shipped in the same slice as the publish endpoint); KIP1e (Vue Publish button) + KIP1f (unpublish/retire) queued. |
| **N1c2 — Admin-configurable ontology preseed (runtime enable/disable + custom-bundle upload)** | `aidocs/65` + `aidocs/16` N1c2. Closes the two pre-N1c2 gaps: (1) the only per-bundle opt-out was the deploy-time `skip-bundles` CSV (restart-required), and (2) there was no way to add a custom TTL without editing the classpath. The slice ships: a manifest `required: boolean` field with `prov-o` + `obo-relations` flipped on (required bundles always seed — admin attempting disable returns 409 with RFC 7807 `semantic.bundle.required`); a `:SemanticConfig` Neo4j singleton (`HasAppId`, V27 uniqueness constraint) carrying `preseedEnabled` + `disabledBundles` — first-start seeded from the deploy-time `shepard.semantic.internal.preseed-ontologies.{enabled,skip-bundles}` defaults, runtime row wins forever after (per CLAUDE.md "Always: surface operator knobs"); a `:UserOntologyBundle` Neo4j catalogue (V28 uniqueness constraint) for operator-uploaded bundles, with bytes on disk at `shepard.semantic.internal.user-bundles-dir` (default `/var/lib/shepard/ontologies/`, deploy-time only per the filesystem-topology exception in `aidocs/65 §2.3`); five new REST endpoints on `/v2/admin/semantic/ontologies` (`GET` merged-list, `POST {id}/enable`, `POST {id}/disable`, `POST` multipart upload with 10 MB cap, `DELETE {id}`) all `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`; five new CLI subcommands `shepard-admin semantic ontologies {list,enable,disable,upload,remove}` with shared `--output={human,json}` + `--url` + `--api-key`. The N1c bulk-refresh endpoint stays valid — operators who want immediate import of a newly-uploaded bundle run `shepard-admin semantic refresh-ontologies --bundles=<id>`. | **AWARE + CONFIG** | One new deploy-time-only config key `shepard.semantic.internal.user-bundles-dir` (default `/var/lib/shepard/ontologies/`) — the directory operator-uploaded TTLs live in. Container deployments should mount this path persistently. The existing `shepard.semantic.internal.preseed-ontologies.{enabled,skip-bundles}` deploy-time keys keep working — they're now the **install defaults** that seed the `:SemanticConfig` singleton on first start; runtime mutations via the admin REST/CLI take precedence forever after. Five new endpoints on the `/v2/` development surface. New RFC 7807 problem codes: `semantic.bundle.required` (409 on disable of prov-o / obo-relations), `semantic.bundle.not-found` (404), `semantic.bundle.duplicate-id` (409 on upload id collision with built-in or existing user bundle), `semantic.bundle.builtin-not-removable` (409 on delete of a built-in), `semantic.bundle.invalid-ttl` (400, fails the parse-shape heuristic), `semantic.bundle.too-large` (400, > 10 MB), `semantic.bundle.bad-metadata` (400, missing id/iriPrefix/license). | `V27__Add_appId_constraint_SemanticConfig.cypher` + `V28__Add_appId_constraint_UserOntologyBundle.cypher` — idempotent appId uniqueness adds (V11 / V22 / V25 idiom). No data-mutating migration; the rollout is structurally additive. | **No action required for upgrade.** First restart post-N1c2 seeds the `:SemanticConfig` singleton from your existing `shepard.semantic.internal.preseed-ontologies.{enabled,skip-bundles}` deploy-time values. Operators on the existing skip-bundles workflow see zero change. To flip a bundle at runtime: `shepard-admin semantic ontologies disable qudt` (or `POST /v2/admin/semantic/ontologies/qudt/disable`). To upload a lab-specific TTL: `shepard-admin semantic ontologies upload --file=lab-vocab.ttl --id=lab-vocab --iri-prefix=https://lab.example.org/vocab/ --license="CC BY 4.0"`. To verify required-bundle protection: `shepard-admin semantic ontologies disable prov-o` → exits 1 with stderr `409`. To make the upload directory persistent in a container deployment: bind-mount `/var/lib/shepard/ontologies/` (or set `shepard.semantic.internal.user-bundles-dir` to a different path). Image-size impact: zero (no new bundled assets). Test obligation: backend coverage `OntologyConfigService` 90.8% line / 78.4% branch; `SemanticAdminRest` 97.9% line / 85.7% branch; `RuntimeConfig` 100/100. Testcontainer-level integration (real Neo4j + V27/V28 migration apply + real multipart upload through the JAX-RS chain) is **deferred** alongside the N1a-series obligations. |
| **KIP1e — Vue Publish button (frontend)** | `aidocs/16` KIP1e + `aidocs/66 §7`. Frontend-only slice: adds a primary "Publish" button to the Collection detail pane (`frontend/pages/collections/[collectionId]/index.vue`) and the DataObject detail pane (`frontend/pages/collections/[collectionId]/dataobjects/[dataObjectId]/index.vue`), gated on the same `isAllowedToEditCollection` predicate as the existing Edit/Add affordances. Three new files under `frontend/`: `components/context/publish/PublishButton.vue` (primary action + post-publish snackbar with "Copy resolver URL" + "Copy PID" actions), `components/context/publish/PublishModal.vue` (SPDX licence drop-down — `CC-BY-4.0`, `CC-BY-SA-4.0`, `CC0-1.0`, `MIT`, `Apache-2.0`, `LGPL-3.0`, `GPL-3.0` — plus a confirmation copy explaining the append-only KIP convention), and `composables/context/usePublishEntity.ts` (raw `fetch` wrapper around `POST /v2/{kind}/{appId}/publish` because the `@dlr-shepard/backend-client` regeneration for KIP1a hasn't landed yet; trivial to swap once it does). Scope-downs documented in `aidocs/16` KIP1e row: existing-publication-state display deferred (no KIP1a side-channel "list publications" endpoint; `BasicEntityIO` is frozen per CLAUDE.md), licence wire-shape deferred (KIP1a's POST takes no body; the drop-down is informational today and the modal already emits the SPDX id on submit for when the backend grows a body), i18n not wired in this Nuxt app (no `vue-i18n`), no frontend test suite exists. | **ZERO** | No new backend endpoints, no new schema, no new config keys. Frontend bundle grows by ~5 KB (the two Vue components + the composable). Calls existing KIP1a endpoints (`POST /v2/{kind}/{appId}/publish`); backend behaviour unchanged. | — | **No action required for upgrade.** A researcher with Writer / Manager permission on a Collection or DataObject now sees a "Publish" button at the top of the detail pane; clicking opens the licence modal, confirming fires the publish, the snackbar shows the freshly-minted PID with copy actions. To re-mint (force a fresh PID), users still need to hit the REST endpoint directly with `?force=true` until the UI follow-up slice lands the popover affordance. To verify the slice rendered after upgrade: navigate to any Collection where you have Writer / Manager — the "Publish" button shows up between the title block and the Description expansion panel. |
| **PM1b2 — plugin runtime hardening: `JarSignatureVerifier` + semver-range enforcement of `shepardCompatibility()` + `DEGRADED` state** | `aidocs/16` PM1b2 + `aidocs/63 ADR-0023` forward-work checklist (JAR signature verifier + semver-range enforcement items). Two orthogonal hardening gates land in the same slice: (1) **`JarSignatureVerifier`** verifies plugin JARs against operator-configured trust anchors (JKS / PKCS12 truststore via `KeyStore.getInstance` — same JDK API `jarsigner` uses). The verifier runs **before** the URLClassLoader is built so an untrusted JAR's classes are never loaded; the entry is registered FAILED for operator visibility with `failureMessage=plugin.signature.unsigned` or `plugin.signature.untrusted`. Default `shepard.plugins.signing.required=false` (backward-compatible — UH1a / KIP / minter-local plugins ship unsigned today; making this gate active by default would break the upgrade path). Operator opts in by setting four `shepard.plugins.signing.*` keys + signing each plugin JAR with `jarsigner` before drop-in. (2) **Semver-range enforcement** of `PluginManifest.shepardCompatibility()` against the running `shepard.version`. Pre-PM1b2 the field was informational; post-PM1b2 a plugin whose declared range doesn't accept the running version lands FAILED with `failureMessage=plugin.compatibility.failed: requires shepard <range>, running <version>` (or `plugin.compatibility.unparseable: <details>` for a malformed range string). Enforcement runs **between** discovery and dependency-resolution so compat failures pre-empt the dependent's `plugin.dependency.missing` (more accurate root-cause). Default `shepard.plugins.compatibility.strict=true` (operator override-valve: set false to register incompatible plugins with a loud `THIS IS A DANGER ZONE` WARN — escape valve for the "we forked shepard 5.2 → 5.3 and vendor X hasn't refreshed their manifest" case). (3) **`VersionRange` parser** grew the npm/Composer-style operator-comma syntax (`>=5.2.0,<6`) every existing plugin manifest already uses (UH1a, KIP, minter-local) — pre-PM1b2 the parser only accepted Maven-bracket syntax (`[5.2.0,6)`) which no plugin actually wrote. (4) **`DEGRADED` lifecycle state** + `PluginEntry.markDegraded(String)` lifecycle hook (forward placeholder for PM1b3 — runtime-only JAR detection ships in PM1b3 per `aidocs/69`). New `PluginEntryIO.state` enum descriptor mentions `DEGRADED`. Tests: 9 `JarSignatureVerifierTest` (unsigned / self-signed-no-truststore / signed-by-anchor → TRUSTED / tampered → JarSignatureException / empty-truststore → UNTRUSTED / default-not-required / invalid-truststore-path fail-soft / isSigningRequired / multi-anchor-any-match — `keytool`-minted keys + JDK's `jdk.security.jarsigner.JarSigner` API, no internal-class access) + 11 `PluginRegistryCompatibilityTest` (compatible / too-old / too-new / empty / null / unparseable / strict=false override × 2 / compat-precedes-dependency / multi-plugin / already-failed-not-reprocessed) + 8 new `VersionRangeTest` cases on operator-comma syntax (>=5.2.0,<6 / single-clause ge / single-clause lt / =-operator / empty-clause IAE / malformed-clause IAE / semver-numeric compare / bare-clause-as-exact) + 4 `PluginRegistryDegradedStateTest` (markDegraded transitions / list visibility / enum stability / markEnabled clears DEGRADED message). Child-classloader CDI integration (the third PM1b2 dispatch item) deferred to PM1b3 with the full design rationale in new `aidocs/platform/69-runtime-plugin-cdi.md` — Quarkus's build-time CDI scanner doesn't expose a runtime bean-addition API; the structurally correct fix is to give plugins a non-CDI Vert.x HTTP-handler shape, which deserves its own slice. PM1b2 ships the prerequisites; PM1b3 closes the gap. | **AWARE + CONFIG** | **Five new deploy-time-only config keys** (all default-safe): `shepard.plugins.signing.required=false`, `shepard.plugins.signing.trust-anchors=`, `shepard.plugins.signing.truststore.path=`, `shepard.plugins.signing.truststore.password=`, `shepard.plugins.compatibility.strict=true`. **Zero new endpoints** — the new `DEGRADED` / `FAILED` states flow through the existing `GET /v2/admin/plugins`. **Zero new schema constraints, zero new migrations** (the registry's compat + signature gates are runtime state, not graph data). **One new lifecycle state** `DEGRADED` on `PluginState` enum (additive — pre-PM1b2 clients that script against the enum's name() see a new value they should treat as an "unknown / inert" state; same convention as adding to any enum surfaced over JSON). **Four new RFC 7807 problem keys in `plugin.*` family** (logged + surfaced in `failureMessage` on the IO): `plugin.signature.unsigned`, `plugin.signature.untrusted`, `plugin.compatibility.failed`, `plugin.compatibility.unparseable`. `/shepard/api/...` surface unchanged. **No new dependencies** — JarSignatureVerifier uses JDK's `java.util.jar.JarFile` + `java.security.KeyStore` exclusively; the semver-range parser is the in-tree `VersionRange` PM1c shipped (extended with operator-comma syntax). | — (no migration — runtime hardening only; no graph data touched). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the PluginRegistry now logs `PM1a: plugin discovery complete — N discovered, N enabled, 0 disabled, 0 failed, 0 degraded` (notice the new `degraded` count column in the startup log line). All three bundled plugins (`unhide`, `kip`, `minter-local`) keep registering — their unsigned status is fine under the default `signing.required=false`, and their `shepardCompatibility=">=5.2.0,<6"` declarations are now actually enforced and pass cleanly against the running 5.2.x version. **To opt in to signed-plugin enforcement** (recommended for production): mint a publisher cert with `keytool -genkeypair -alias my-publisher -keystore /etc/shepard/truststore.jks ...`, sign every plugin JAR with `jarsigner -keystore my-keystore.jks <plugin>.jar my-publisher`, set `shepard.plugins.signing.required=true` + `shepard.plugins.signing.truststore.path=/etc/shepard/truststore.jks` + `shepard.plugins.signing.truststore.password=…` in `application.properties`, restart. Bundled plugins will land FAILED until you sign them (or until the build pipeline emits signed JARs — PM1b3 design parking). **To diagnose a failed plugin post-upgrade**: `shepard-admin plugins list --output=json | jq '.plugins[] | select(.state == "FAILED") | {id, failureMessage}'` — the `failureMessage` prefix tells you whether it's a `plugin.signature.*` issue, a `plugin.compatibility.*` issue, or a `plugin.dependency.*` issue from PM1c. **To override the compat gate** (rare — operator escape valve for a fork-bump where a vendor hasn't refreshed their manifest): set `shepard.plugins.compatibility.strict=false`. Full operator runbook at `docs/reference/plugins.md` §"Signing + compatibility enforcement" (added in this PR). Test obligation: 32 new unit tests (9 verifier + 11 compat + 8 VersionRange + 4 DEGRADED) landed in this PR; testcontainer-level integration regression (real Quarkus + a tampered JAR dropped into `/deployments/plugins/`, assert `GET /v2/admin/plugins` returns FAILED with the signature failureMessage) is **deferred** alongside the broader QuarkusTest scaffolding. **PM1b3 (queued)**: true runtime CDI integration via Vert.x router so vendors can drop in a JAR without declaring it in the `with-plugins` Maven profile — full design at `aidocs/platform/69-runtime-plugin-cdi.md`. |
| **PM1e — persistent runtime overrides for `PluginRegistry`** | `aidocs/16` PM1e + `aidocs/63 ADR-0023` forward-work checklist ("Persist runtime overrides"). Closes the final operator gap of the PM1 chain: a `shepard-admin plugins disable <id>` now survives a backend restart without editing `application.properties`. New Neo4j label `:PluginRuntimeOverride` (one row per plugin id, V32 uniqueness constraint mirroring the V11/V22/V25/V27/V30 idiom) carrying `pluginId` + `enabled` + `updatedAt` + `updatedBy`. `PluginRegistry.seedOverridesFromDao()` runs before lifecycle invocation at startup, so the in-memory cache reflects the persisted state before any `onRegister` sees it; `PluginRegistry.setEnabled(id, enabled, actorSub)` writes through to the DAO synchronously within the PATCH request (sparse-table semantics — when an admin flips back to the deploy-time default, the row is DELETEd rather than upserted to match, so the table only ever carries rows that actually differ from `shepard.plugins.<id>.enabled`). `PluginsAdminRest.patch` now takes a `@Context SecurityContext` and propagates the caller's `sub` to the registry → `:PluginRuntimeOverride.updatedBy` (redundant with the `:Activity` audit row PROV1a captures automatically, but kept for fast "who flipped this most recently" lookups). CLI success messages updated: `enable` / `disable` now print `"override persisted; survives restart"` instead of the pre-PM1e `"runtime override survives until restart"` caveat. Fail-soft on DAO outage at startup (registry falls back to deploy-time defaults — same as pre-PM1e) and on PATCH write (in-memory cache is current; the override is lost on restart and the WARN is loud so the operator can re-PATCH after the DB recovers). 13 new `PluginRuntimeOverrideDAOTest` (Mockito-on-Session — entityType / findByPluginId hit+miss+blank / findAllOverrides / save mints appId / save rejects null+blank / deleteByPluginId hit+miss+blank), 11 new `PluginRegistryPersistenceTest` (startup-seed populates cache / empty / idempotent / fail-soft on DAO exception / write-through persists / reset-to-default deletes / unknown plugin throws / null actor → anonymous / DAO write failure preserves in-memory state / simulated restart preserves disable / 2-arg setEnabled delegates), 4 new `PluginsAdminRestTest` (caller-sub from SecurityContext, null principal, blank-name principal, 2-arg overload), 3 new `PluginsCommandsTest` (CLI success messages mention "persisted"+"survives restart" / no longer mention "in-memory only"+"until restart" / two-PATCH simulated restart). | **AWARE** | **One new Neo4j label** (`:PluginRuntimeOverride`) + **one new schema constraint** (V32 `pluginId IS UNIQUE`). **Zero new endpoints** — uses the existing PATCH path. **Zero new config keys** — uses the existing `shepard.plugins.<id>.enabled` runtime-toggle pattern PM1a established (deploy-time install default; the new persistent row wins forever after, matching the A3b / N1c2 / UH1a "admin-configurable" idiom with the persistent-override variant). **PATCH wire shape unchanged** — the `:PluginRuntimeOverride` row's `updatedAt` / `updatedBy` fields are server-internal and not surfaced on the PATCH response. **Zero new RFC 7807 problem types.** `/shepard/api/...` surface unchanged. The deploy-time `shepard.plugins.<id>.enabled` key in `application.properties` continues to work — it's the install default that seeds new plugins; an existing persistent override row supersedes it after first runtime flip. | `V32__Add_pluginId_constraint_PluginRuntimeOverride.cypher` (V11-shape uniqueness constraint on the new `:PluginRuntimeOverride` label; idempotent + fail-fast). No rollback file — additive constraint on a fresh label; one-line `DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS pluginId_unique_PluginRuntimeOverride` reverses it. **Migration regression test deferred** — testcontainer fixture for the V32 constraint will land alongside the broader Neo4j migration testcontainer (same posture as PM1a / N1c2 / UH1a's V27–V30 constraint migrations). | **No action required for upgrade.** First restart post-PM1e finds no `:PluginRuntimeOverride` rows; V32 applies on first start. Existing `shepard.plugins.<id>.enabled` keys in `application.properties` keep working as the install default. To verify persistence works: `shepard-admin plugins disable unhide` → restart shepard → `shepard-admin plugins list` shows `unhide \| 1.0.0 \| ... \| false`. To reset a plugin to its deploy-time default: `shepard-admin plugins enable unhide` (when default is enabled) — the persisted row is DELETEd so the table stays sparse. To audit who flipped which plugin: `MATCH (a:Activity {targetKind:"PluginEntry"}) RETURN a.actor, a.targetAppId, a.method, a.timestamp ORDER BY a.timestamp DESC LIMIT 20` (PROV1a path; same as PM1b) OR `MATCH (o:PluginRuntimeOverride) RETURN o.pluginId, o.enabled, o.updatedAt, o.updatedBy ORDER BY o.updatedAt DESC` (fast cypher-shell path on the persisted row). The deploy-time `shepard.plugins.<id>.enabled` value still drives the install default for newly-installed plugins; existing override rows continue to win. Test obligation: 31 new unit tests (13 DAO + 11 persistence + 4 admin-REST + 3 CLI) landed in this PR; testcontainer-level integration regression (real Quarkus + real Neo4j + V32 applied + PATCH a plugin off + restart + observe persisted state) is **deferred** alongside the broader migration-test scaffold. |
| **PM1c — `PluginManifest` SPI metadata enrichment + plugin-dependency resolution** | `aidocs/16` PM1c + `aidocs/63 ADR-0023` forward-work checklist. Two additive extensions on top of the PM1a SPI: (1) six new default methods on `PluginManifest` — `title()` (defaults to `id()`), `description()` (default `""`), `homepageUrl()` (default `Optional.empty()`), `repositoryUrl()` (default `Optional.empty()`), `licence()` (SPDX id, default `""`), `dependencies()` (default `List.of()`). All are `default` methods so existing plugin implementations keep compiling and the SPI surface stays additive. (2) new `de.dlr.shepard.plugin.PluginDependency` record + in-tree `VersionRange` parser (Maven-style: exact `1.0.0`, bounded `[1.0,2.0)`, half-open `[1.5,)` / `(,3.0]`, empty `→` accept-any; semver-aware numeric compare so `1.10 > 1.2`). Registry topologically sorts the dependency graph via Kahn's algorithm between discovery and lifecycle (`PluginRegistry.validateAndOrderDependencies()`) — failures mark the offending plugin `FAILED` fail-soft (`plugin.dependency.missing` / `plugin.dependency.version-mismatch` / `plugin.dependency.cycle`); the surviving subgraph is reordered so each plugin's `onRegister` is guaranteed to run after every declared dependency's `onRegister`. Wire shape of `GET /v2/admin/plugins` grows six fields: `title` / `description` / `homepageUrl` / `repositoryUrl` / `licence` (all nullable strings; empty strings collapse to `null` so clients only see what the plugin declared) + `dependencies` (a JSON array of `{pluginId, versionConstraint}` objects, never null — empty list when none declared). `shepard-admin plugins list` table grows three columns: TITLE (width-capped 30 chars, truncate with `…`), LICENCE (12 chars), REPOSITORY (40 chars). JSON-mode output unchanged shape-wise (just emits the richer `PluginInfo` shape). `UnhidePluginManifest` filled in — `title="Helmholtz Unhide Publish"`, `description=…`, `homepageUrl=https://unhide.helmholtz-metadaten.de/`, `repositoryUrl=https://github.com/noheton/shepard`, `licence="Apache-2.0"` (matches the fork's `LICENSE`), dependencies empty (UH1a depends on in-tree backend SPIs only, not on any other plugin). | **AWARE** | **Zero new endpoints.** Wire shape of the existing `GET /v2/admin/plugins` grows six fields (all nullable / arrays — backwards-compatible additive per JSON's open-world semantics; clients that don't know about the new keys ignore them). CLI `plugins list` table grows three new columns — visible-but-script-safe because operators scripting against the CLI should be using `--output=json` for stability (call-out in the §"shepard-admin plugins" docs section). **Zero new config keys.** Three new RFC 7807 problem types in the `plugin.*` family (logged + recorded in `:Activity` via PROV1a; surfaced in `failureMessage` on the IO): `plugin.dependency.missing`, `plugin.dependency.version-mismatch`, `plugin.dependency.cycle`. `/shepard/api/...` surface unchanged. **Pure SPI surface area — no new dependencies added** (the `VersionRange` parser is hand-rolled in-tree to avoid pulling `org.apache.maven:maven-artifact` for one method; same posture as N1c2's ontology-bundle slug parser). | — (no migration — SPI / wire-shape additive change). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the PluginRegistry logs `PM1c: plugin discovery complete — N discovered, N enabled, 0 disabled, 0 failed` on startup (provided no plugin declares an unsatisfiable dependency). `GET /v2/admin/plugins` now carries the six richer fields; `shepard-admin plugins list` shows three new columns (`TITLE | LICENCE | REPOSITORY`). The UH1a row now reads `unhide | Helmholtz Unhide Publish | 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT | ENABLED | true | Apache-2.0 | https://github.com/noheton/shepard | …`. Vendors shipping a `shepard-plugin-*` JAR with dependency declarations should ensure their declared deps are themselves on the classpath / in `/deployments/plugins/` — a missing dep marks the dependent plugin FAILED, the cause visible in the admin REST's `failureMessage` field. To inspect a plugin's dependency graph from JSON output: `shepard-admin plugins list --output=json \| jq '.plugins[].dependencies'`. Test obligation: 8 `PluginManifestTest` + 12 `VersionRangeTest` + 12 `PluginRegistryDependencyTest` + 2 new `PluginsAdminRestTest` + 3+3 new CLI tests landed in this PR; testcontainer-level integration regression (boot Quarkus + two plugin JARs with declared dependencies, assert correct registration order via `:Activity` ordering) is **deferred** alongside the PM1a / PM1b precedent. |
| **PM1d — CLI extensibility SPI + Unhide CLI migrates to `plugins/unhide/`** | `aidocs/16` PM1d + `aidocs/63 ADR-0023` forward-work checklist. Mirrors PM1a's backend-side `PluginManifest` SPI on the CLI side: plugin JARs ship their own `shepard-admin` Picocli subcommands via a new `de.dlr.shepard.cli.plugin.AdminCliCommandProvider` SPI, discovered at CLI startup by a `CliPluginBootstrap` ServiceLoader-scan of the same plugin directory the backend uses. Two new files in core (`AdminCliCommandProvider`, `CliPluginBootstrap`); the `ShepardAdmin` root command no longer hard-codes the `unhide` subcommand — it ships an `AdminCliCommandProvider` plugin alongside the backend bits in `plugins/unhide/` and the bootstrap discovers it. The full `unhide` command tree (UnhideCommand + 7 verb classes + UnhideAdminPaths + UnhideConfig + HarvestKeyMinted IO records + UnhideCommandsTest) moves from `cli/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/cli/...` to `plugins/unhide/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/plugins/unhide/cli/...`. The plugin's pom.xml grows two `provided`-scoped deps (CLI + Picocli), the CLI's pom.xml grows a `with-plugins` profile (copies every `de.dlr.shepard.plugins:*` dep into `target/plugins/` at packaging time, mirroring backend's profile shape) plus a `test-jar` execution so the plugin module can pull the CLI's `StubBackend`/`CliRunner` test fixtures via `<type>test-jar</type>`. **Discovery shape**: env `SHEPARD_PLUGINS_DIR` or sys-prop `shepard.plugins.dir` overrides the plugin dir; defaults are `/deployments/plugins` (container) then `cli/plugins` (local dev); each JAR's real path must lie inside the configured dir's real path (same defence-in-depth posture as PM1a). Fail-soft per-provider: a plugin JAR with a broken provider class logs a WARN to stderr but doesn't crash the CLI — operators still get `shepard-admin features list` working when one plugin's CLI is broken. **End-user UX byte-identical**: `shepard-admin unhide …` works exactly as before; same verb names, same flags, same JSON shapes, same exit codes — the 12 `UnhideCommandsTest` cases all pass unmodified after the move (only their package + the parent-wiring test were updated). Tests: 5 new `AdminCliCommandProviderTest` cases on the JAR-walk path (smoke discovery from a synthetic JAR built inline with the Java compiler API, bare-JAR-no-service-file skip, non-existent-dir warning, no-default-dir silent no-op, duplicate-subcommand-name warn-and-skip); 4 new `ShepardAdminBootstrapTest` cases on the `ShepardAdmin.commandLine()` wiring (core subcommands intact, idempotent across calls, --help, --version). Pre-existing CLI bug fix continuity: the duplicate `delete(String)` overload PM1b already removed stays removed. **Backend untouched**: PM1d is a `cli/`-side + `plugins/`-side refactor only. | **AWARE** | **Zero new endpoints.** **Zero new config keys** on the backend; the CLI's plugin-dir lookup reuses the same env-var name (`SHEPARD_PLUGINS_DIR`) the backend image already pins via PM1a. One new SPI interface published on the CLI surface (`de.dlr.shepard.cli.plugin.AdminCliCommandProvider`) — third-party plugin authors compile against `de.dlr.shepard:shepard-admin` with `<scope>provided</scope>` (same shape as the backend dependency from PM1a). The `shepard-admin unhide …` subcommand path is unchanged: operators see no difference between pre-PM1d and post-PM1d invocations. One image-shape consequence: the CLI's plugins-dir bootstrap means operators with custom plugins must drop CLI-side JARs alongside backend-side JARs in `/deployments/plugins/` — the same plugin JAR typically carries both (a `PluginManifest` for the backend AND an `AdminCliCommandProvider` for the CLI), so the operator workflow is unchanged. `/shepard/api/...` surface unchanged. | — (no migration — code reorganisation only; the V30 `:UnhideConfig` constraint from UH1a is the only schema artefact in play). | **No action required for upgrade.** The unhide CLI subcommand keeps working at the same path (`shepard-admin unhide {status,enable,disable,set-feed-public,set-contact-email,rotate-harvest-key,revoke-harvest-key}`) — only the source code's home moved. The `shepard-plugin-unhide-<version>.jar` baked into the image now also carries the CLI subcommand classes; an operator using a custom plugin directory mount needs to drop the same JAR (backend bits + CLI bits) into the directory. To verify post-upgrade: `shepard-admin --help` should still list `unhide` alongside `features` / `health` / `migrations` / `plugins` / `semantic`; `shepard-admin unhide --help` should still show the seven verbs. Third-party plugin authors writing their own `shepard-admin <feature>` subcommands: depend on `de.dlr.shepard:shepard-admin` with `<scope>provided</scope>` plus Picocli (also `provided`), ship a `META-INF/services/de.dlr.shepard.cli.plugin.AdminCliCommandProvider` file pointing at your implementation; the bootstrap picks it up on `shepard-admin` startup. Full reference docs in `docs/reference/plugins.md` §"CLI extensibility". Test obligation: 5 + 4 new CLI SPI tests landed in this PR; the 12 UnhideCommandsTest cases run unmodified in their new location. Testcontainer-level integration regression (run `java -jar shepard-admin.jar unhide status --url http://backend` end-to-end against a live container) is **deferred** alongside the PM1a / PM1b precedent. |
| **PM1b — admin REST + CLI parity for `PluginRegistry`** | `aidocs/16` PM1b + `aidocs/63 ADR-0023` forward-work checklist.
