Ships the runtime SPI-registry's admin surface that PM1a designed but didn't wire: `GET /v2/admin/plugins` (registry snapshot — one row per discovered plugin including DISABLED + FAILED so operators see the full state space; columns `id` / `version` / `shepardCompatibility` / `state` / `enabled` / `sourcePath` / `registeredAt` / `failureMessage`) and `PATCH /v2/admin/plugins/{id}` (RFC 7396 merge-patch — only `enabled` is patchable; any other field hits the defensive 400 `plugin.config.read-only-field` envelope; 404 `plugin.not-found` for unknown id; both via RFC 7807 `application/problem+json` mirroring the UH1a / N1c2 idiom). Both endpoints class-level `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`. New CLI: `shepard-admin plugins {list,enable,disable}` with the shared L1-baseline flags (`--output={human,json}` / `--url` / `--api-key`); list renders ID / VERSION / STATE / ENABLED / SOURCE columns (build-classpath plugins surface `(build-classpath)` placeholder); enable / disable PATCH the runtime override + print a one-line confirmation reminding operators the override is in-memory only. `PluginEntry` gains a `registeredAt` `Instant` set in the constructor so the REST shape carries chronological discovery order. PROV1a's `ProvenanceCaptureFilter` captures each PATCH automatically (admin endpoints default-opt-in; no extra wiring) — the `:Activity` audit trail records `targetKind=PluginEntry` per flip. Backend: 12 new unit tests on `PluginsAdminRestTest` (mockito on `PluginRegistry` — list empty / populated, PATCH enable / disable / no-op / empty body / null body, 404 unknown id, 400 read-only-field, role-guard check via annotation introspection). CLI: 10 new end-to-end tests on `PluginsCommandsTest` (StubBackend harness — list table / JSON / 401 hint / build-classpath placeholder, enable / disable / 404 → exit 1, JSON output, X-API-KEY propagation, parent wiring). | **AWARE** | Two new endpoints on the `/v2/` development surface (`GET /v2/admin/plugins`, `PATCH /v2/admin/plugins/{id}`). One new CLI subcommand group (`shepard-admin plugins`). **Zero new config keys** — uses the existing `shepard.plugins.<id>.enabled` runtime-toggle pattern PM1a established (deploy-time install default; runtime PATCH override wins until restart, matching the A3b / N1c2 / UH1a "admin-configurable" idiom). Zero new schema constraints — the registry override is in-memory (PM1c persists). Two new RFC 7807 problem types: `plugin.not-found` (404), `plugin.config.read-only-field` (400). `/shepard/api/...` surface unchanged. Pre-existing CLI bug fix: removed a duplicate `delete(String)` overload in `ShepardHttpClient` that prevented the CLI module from compiling locally; clean rebuild now passes. | — (no migration — admin surface only; PluginRegistry's in-memory override is the data path). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the admin REST surface comes up automatically. `shepard-admin plugins list` is now the canonical way to see what's installed (replaces the `ls /deployments/plugins/` workaround in PM1a's runbook). Example: `shepard-admin plugins disable unhide` flips the runtime override → the feed returns 503 unhide.feed.disabled until the next restart (or until `shepard-admin plugins enable unhide`). To persist a runtime disable across restart: edit `shepard.plugins.<id>.enabled=false` in `application.properties` (the deploy-time key is unchanged; PM1c will persist runtime overrides to the DB). To audit who flipped which plugin: `MATCH (a:Activity {targetKind:"PluginEntry"}) RETURN a.actor, a.targetAppId, a.method, a.timestamp ORDER BY a.timestamp DESC LIMIT 20`. Full operator runbook: `docs/reference/plugins.md` (the §"Admin REST API" + §"`shepard-admin plugins` subcommand" sections added in this PR). Test obligation: 12 backend admin-REST unit tests + 10 CLI end-to-end tests landed in this PR; testcontainer-level integration regression (real Quarkus + PluginRegistry, hit `GET /v2/admin/plugins` after a disable, assert the row reflects the override + `:Activity` audit row exists) is **deferred** alongside the PM1a / N1c2 / UH1a precedent. |
| **PM1a — `PluginManifest` SPI + `PluginRegistry` + UH1a flipped to drop-in JAR** | `aidocs/16` PM1a + `aidocs/47 §2.5` + `aidocs/63 ADR-0023`. The structural-prep slice that makes shepard's plugin ecosystem (UH1a today, future HDF5 / video / AAS / KIP minters / FS GridFS+S3 etc.) discoverable + lifecycle-managed by core. Three commits: phase 1 introduces the SPI surface — `de.dlr.shepard.plugin.{PluginManifest, PluginContext, PluginRegistry, PluginEntry, PluginState}`; PluginManifest carries `id()` / `version()` / `shepardCompatibility()` + default `onRegister(ctx)` / `onUnregister(ctx)` hooks; PluginRegistry is an `@ApplicationScoped` CDI bean observing `StartupEvent` (post-`MigrationsRunner.apply()`) that ServiceLoader-scans the backend classpath AND walks `shepard.plugins.dir` for `*.jar` files, builds a child `URLClassLoader` per JAR, registers each manifest, and invokes the lifecycle hooks fail-soft (a throwing `onRegister` marks the plugin `FAILED` with `plugin.discovery.failed` and continues). Path-traversal hardening: each JAR's real path must lie within the configured plugin dir's real path. Per-plugin `shepard.plugins.<id>.enabled=true\|false` runtime toggle. Phase 2 ships the `backend/plugins/` directory with `.gitkeep` + a `README.md`; Dockerfile creates `/deployments/plugins/` writable by uid 185 and pins `shepard.plugins.dir=/deployments/plugins` via `JAVA_OPTS_APPEND`; `application.properties` documents the deploy-time `shepard.plugins.dir` key + the per-plugin runtime toggle template. Phase 3 (this commit) flips UH1a from source-stitched to drop-in JAR: removes the `build-helper-maven-plugin` `add-source` / `add-test-source` executions from `backend/pom.xml`, makes `plugins/unhide/pom.xml` produce a real `shepard-plugin-unhide-${revision}.jar` (with `provided` scope on backend so the JAR doesn't bundle backend.jar), backend declares the plugin as a `<dependency>` in the default-active `with-plugins` profile (Quarkus's build-time CDI scanner picks up `@ApplicationScoped` beans + `@Path` resources), `quarkus.index-dependency.shepard-plugin-unhide.*` ensures Jandex indexing, `-DnoPlugins` flag breaks the bootstrap circular reference, CI workflows (`backend-ci.yml` + `build-images.yml`) execute the two-pass install dance, Dockerfile copies the plugin JAR into `/deployments/plugins/` (operator visibility) and `PluginRegistry` silently shadows the duplicate (the build-classpath instance wins; the JAR in `/deployments/plugins/` is informational). 15 new unit tests (`PluginRegistryTest` + `PluginEntryTest` + `PluginRegistryTestSupport`) covering JAR discovery / missing-dir / non-dir / disabled / throwing / duplicate / shutdown / classpath-then-JAR-shadow / smoke-via-ServiceLoader. V2 ArchUnit fence (`V2NamespaceTest`) extended to treat `de.dlr.shepard.plugins..` as an equal-tier `/v2/` tenant. | **AWARE** | One new deploy-time-only config key (`shepard.plugins.dir`, default `backend/plugins`; in-container default `/deployments/plugins` via `SHEPARD_PLUGINS_DIR`) + one new per-plugin runtime-toggle template (`shepard.plugins.<id>.enabled=true|false`, default `true` when the JAR is present). One new admin-visible directory (`/deployments/plugins/`) created with uid 185 ownership in the image; operators can bind-mount their own directory over it to add vendor plugins without rebaking. One new image artefact (`shepard-plugin-unhide-${revision}.jar`) baked into the image both at `/deployments/lib/` (Quarkus's build-classpath copy) and at `/deployments/plugins/` (operator-visibility copy) — the PluginRegistry's duplicate-id check silently shadows the redundant entry, so the duplication is benign. One new admin REST surface designed but not yet shipped (`GET /v2/admin/plugins`, queued in PM1b). No change to `/shepard/api/...` surface. No change to any upstream wire shape. | — (no Cypher migration — packaging-only change; the plugin's own V30 migration shipped with UH1a). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the PluginRegistry logs `PM1a: plugin discovery starting → … → discovery complete — 1 discovered, 1 enabled, 0 disabled, 0 failed` on startup (for the UH1a build-classpath manifest). To inspect what's installed: `ls /deployments/plugins/` (operator-visibility copy) — `shepard-plugin-unhide-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT.jar` should appear. Once PM1b ships the admin REST + CLI surface, `shepard-admin plugins list` will be the canonical query. To install a third-party plugin: `cp <plugin>.jar /deployments/plugins/ && docker compose restart shepard-backend`. To uninstall: `rm /deployments/plugins/<plugin>.jar && docker compose restart shepard-backend`. To disable a plugin without removing the JAR: set `shepard.plugins.<id>.enabled=false` in `application.properties` (or env `SHEPARD_PLUGINS_<ID>_ENABLED=false`) and restart. Full operator runbook: `docs/reference/plugins.md`. Build-from-source dance (for plugin developers / vendors): `cd backend && mvn -DnoPlugins -DskipTests -Dquarkus.build.skip=true install` (stub-install) → `cd plugins/foo && mvn -DskipTests install` (plugin) → `cd backend && mvn package` (uber-jar pulls plugin via the `with-plugins` profile). Test obligation: 15 plugin-registry unit tests landed in this PR; testcontainer-level integration regression (drop a JAR into a running container's `/deployments/plugins/`, restart, hit `GET /v2/admin/plugins` for the new id) is **deferred** alongside the broader QuarkusTest scaffolding (PM1b will own the admin REST surface anyway). |
| **Plugin default hardening — `shepard.plugins.unhide.enabled` changed to `false`** | Unhide is a specialised Helmholtz Infra integration that operators must opt into explicitly. The install-default `shepard.plugins.unhide.enabled` is changed from `true` to `false` in `application.properties`. All existing plugin docs migrated from `docs/reference/`, `docs/help/`, `docs/install/` to `plugins/<id>/docs/`; `nuxt.config.ts` extended to copy `plugins/*/docs/` → `public/docs/plugins/<id>/`; `helpMarkdown.ts` adds a **Plugins** section. | **AWARE** | `shepard.plugins.unhide.enabled` default changed from `true` to `false`. Operators running UH1a and relying on the unhide feed must explicitly add `shepard.plugins.unhide.enabled=true` to their deployment config before upgrading; otherwise the plugin is discovered but DISABLED after restart and `GET /v2/unhide/feed.jsonld` returns 404. No endpoint wire changes. Plugin docs now live in `plugins/<id>/docs/`; served in-app at `/docs/plugins/<id>/reference.md` etc. | — (no migration) | **Action required for unhide operators:** add `shepard.plugins.unhide.enabled=true` to `application.properties` before upgrading. Operators who were not using Unhide: no action needed. |
| **KIP1h — `MockMinter` → `LocalMinter` plugin + optional minter + versioned PIDs Phase 1** | `aidocs/16` KIP1h + `aidocs/66` + CLAUDE.md plugin-first heuristic #3 + user's "MockMinter is a plugin" architectural call. Three orthogonal changes shipped in one slice: (1) **Extract the renamed `MockMinter` → `LocalMinter` as `plugins/minter-local/`** (new Maven module mirroring `plugins/kip/` shape — `LocalMinterPluginManifest` discovered via `META-INF/services/de.dlr.shepard.plugin.PluginManifest`, JAR built with `provided`-scope backend dep, declared as a `<dependency>` in the backend's default-active `with-plugins` Maven profile, `quarkus.index-dependency.shepard-plugin-minter-local.*` ensures Jandex indexing so Quarkus's build-time CDI scanner picks the `@ApplicationScoped` bean). The in-core `Minter` SPI seam, `MintRequest`, `MintResult`, `MinterException`, `MinterRegistry`, `:Publication` entity, `PublicationDAO`, the generic `POST .../publish` orchestration in `PublishRest`, and the `PublicationIO` wire shape all stay in `backend/` — only the impl moves. (2) **Relax `MinterRegistry` to optional posture**: pre-KIP1h the registry fail-fasted on a missing configured-id (appropriate when the in-core `MockMinter` guaranteed at least one bean was always present); post-KIP1h every minter lives in a plugin so the registry now degrades cleanly to "no active minter" + a WARN. `MinterRegistry.activeMinter()` returns `Optional<Minter>`; `PublishService` throws the new `MinterNotInstalledException` when empty; `PublishRest` maps to 503 RFC 7807 `publish.minter.not-installed` with the actionable hint "install plugins/minter-local/ (or another minter plugin) and set shepard.publish.minter=<minter-id>". `shepard.publish.minter=none` is a new explicit sentinel that disables publish without uninstalling the plugin. The resolver (`GET /v2/.well-known/kip/{pid-suffix}` in `shepard-plugin-kip`) keeps working regardless — it reads existing `:Publication` rows. (3) **Versioned PIDs Phase 1**: local PID format becomes `shepard:<instance.id>:<kind>:<appId>:v<n>` (was `mock:shepard:<kind>:<appId>:<epoch-millis>`); `MintRequest` grows a `versionNumber` field; `Publication` grows a `versionNumber` property; `PublicationDAO.findLatestVersionNumber(entityAppId)` returns `coalesce(max(versionNumber), 0)`; `PublishService` computes `findLatestVersionNumber + 1` before minting, stamps onto both the `MintRequest` and the new `Publication` row. `?force=true` now bumps the version (`v1` → `v2`) instead of stamping a fresh epoch-millis. `PublicationIO` wire shape grows `versionNumber` (additive). `KipRecordIO.KernelInformationProfile` body grows `digitalObjectVersion: "v<n>"` (additive). `shepard.instance.id` is reused as the `<instance.id>` PID segment (cluster-identity exception per CLAUDE.md; operators with the `local` default see a startup WARN guiding them to set a namespaced value like `dlr.de/shepard-prod`). New tests: backend 2266 → 2322 (+56 net after deleting 7 `MockMinterTest` cases; new `MinterRegistryTest` cases for the four optional-posture branches + duplicate / blank / multi-minter cases; expanded `PublicationDAOTest` for `findLatestVersionNumber`; expanded `PublishServiceTest` for the 503 path + version-bump on force; expanded `PublishRestTest` with the 503 case; expanded `PublicationIOTest` for the version-defaulting logic); plugin-kip 6 → 8 (+2 `KipResolverRestTest` cases for the version segment); plugin-minter-local 12 (new — `LocalMinterTest` covers id / isEnabled / format / stability across re-mints / different versions / different kinds / instance-id fallback + trim / blank-input validation / backwards-compat factory / minimum-version guard / minter-id + timestamp). SpotBugs + findsecbugs clean; coverage gate met. Phase 2 (`:EntityVersion` graph) queued as ENT1 umbrella. | **AWARE + BREAKING** | **PID-format change is BREAKING for the wire** but **non-destructive for the graph**: new local PIDs (`shepard:<instance.id>:<kind>:<appId>:v<n>`) differ from pre-KIP1h MockMinter output (`mock:shepard:<kind>:<appId>:<epoch-millis>`); operators with their own citation tracking on the published-PID string see a format flip on the first post-upgrade publish. **Existing `:Publication` rows are preserved verbatim** — V31 backfill only stamps `versionNumber=1`; the legacy `mock:` PIDs keep resolving cleanly via the KIP resolver (the resolver does an opaque `findByPid` — the format is irrelevant). **`?force=true` semantics changes**: was "re-mint with a fresh epoch-millis suffix → distinct PID per call", now "bump the version number → `v(N+1)`". **`shepard.publish.minter=mock` config value becomes invalid post-upgrade**: operators must change to `=local` in `application.properties` (or env `SHEPARD_PUBLISH_MINTER=local`) before upgrade, otherwise the publish endpoint returns 503 `publish.minter.not-installed` until they do. The new sentinel `shepard.publish.minter=none` explicitly disables publish without uninstalling the plugin (resolver-only deployments). One new RFC 7807 problem type: `publish.minter.not-installed` (503). One new image artefact: `shepard-plugin-minter-local-${revision}.jar` baked into `/deployments/lib/` (Quarkus's build-classpath copy) + `/deployments/plugins/` (operator-visibility copy) — `PluginRegistry`'s duplicate-id check silently shadows the redundant entry, same as UH1a + KIP1g. The `PublicationIO` wire shape grows `versionNumber: int` (additive — pre-KIP1h clients ignore the new field per the additive-wire-shape convention). The `KipRecordIO.KernelInformationProfile` body grows `digitalObjectVersion: "v<n>"` (additive, JSON-LD open-world semantics). `/shepard/api/...` surface unchanged. | `V31__Backfill_Publication_versionNumber.cypher` — idempotent `MATCH (p:Publication) WHERE p.versionNumber IS NULL SET p.versionNumber = 1` stamping `versionNumber=1` on every pre-KIP1h `:Publication` row (the in-core `MockMinter` produced rows with no version property; the V31 backfill normalises them to v1 so `findLatestVersionNumber` works against the legacy graph). Idempotent (re-runs are no-ops) + fail-fast (any error aborts startup via `MigrationsRunner`). **Migration regression test deferred** — testcontainer fixture for the backfill will land alongside the broader Neo4j migration testcontainer once that scaffold is in place; this row is the tracker anchor. | **Action required for upgrade — change one config value before restart.** Operators set `shepard.publish.minter=local` in `application.properties` (or env `SHEPARD_PUBLISH_MINTER=local`); the pre-KIP1h default `mock` no longer matches any registered minter and the publish endpoint will return 503 until the change is made. Verifying post-upgrade: `curl https://shepard.example.dlr.de/v2/.well-known/kip/<a-legacy-mock-pid>` returns the same KIP record shape it did pre-KIP1h (the resolver does an opaque `findByPid`; the legacy `mock:` format keeps working forever). First fresh publish post-upgrade: `POST /v2/data-objects/{appId}/publish` mints a new-format PID `shepard:<your-instance.id>:data-objects:<appId>:v1`; the response wire shape grows a `versionNumber: 1` field. To verify the V31 backfill ran cleanly: `cypher-shell> MATCH (p:Publication) WHERE p.versionNumber IS NULL RETURN count(p);` returns 0. To set the per-instance PID namespace (recommended for production): set `shepard.instance.id=dlr.de/shepard-prod` (or whatever namespace is appropriate for your deployment) in `application.properties` — fresh installs running on the `local` default see a startup WARN from `LocalMinter`. To opt out of publish entirely (resolver-only deployment for operators who only want to deference existing rows): set `shepard.publish.minter=none` and the publish endpoint returns 503 cleanly. Image-size impact: ~7 KB (new `shepard-plugin-minter-local-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT.jar`; the in-core `MockMinter.java` deletion compensates). Test obligation: 12 plugin-minter-local + 56 net backend + 2 plugin-kip new tests landed in this PR; testcontainer-level integration regression (boot real Quarkus + real Neo4j with a pre-existing `:Publication` row, restart on KIP1h backend, verify the V31 backfill stamps v1 + the legacy PID still resolves) is **deferred** alongside the broader QuarkusTest scaffolding tracked under PM1a + KIP1a. **Rollback** (rare): `cypher-shell> MATCH (p:Publication) REMOVE p.versionNumber;` reverses V31. Downgrading the backend image is otherwise transparent — legacy `mock:` PIDs keep resolving, new `shepard:...:v<n>` PIDs keep resolving (the pre-KIP1h `KipResolverRest` did an opaque `findByPid`; format-agnostic). |
| **KIP1c — ePIC Handle Service minter plugin** | `aidocs/16` KIP1c + `aidocs/66` + CLAUDE.md plugin-first heuristic #3 + "Always: surface operator knobs in the admin config". New Maven module `plugins/minter-epic/` mirrors `plugins/minter-datacite/` shape — `EpicMinterPluginManifest` discovered via `META-INF/services/de.dlr.shepard.plugin.PluginManifest`, JAR built with `provided`-scope backend dep, declared as a `<dependency>` in the backend's default-active `with-plugins` Maven profile, `quarkus.index-dependency.shepard-plugin-minter-epic.*` ensures Jandex indexing so `EpicMinter` `@ApplicationScoped` + `EpicAdminRest` `@Path` are discovered at build time. Activated by `shepard.publish.minter=epic` once credentials are set. Five new admin REST endpoints under `/v2/admin/minters/epic/` (all `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`): `GET /config` (masked `:EpicMinterConfig` snapshot — credentialKey never serialised; credentialSet + 8-hex SHA-256 fingerprint surface instead), `PATCH /config` (RFC 7396 merge-patch — patchable fields are `enabled`, `apiBaseUrl`, `handlePrefix`; credential fields are sentinel-rejected with RFC 7807 `minters.epic.config.read-only-field`), `POST /credential` (set / rotate — body `{"credential":"<plaintext>"}`, response carries only the fingerprint), `DELETE /credential` (clear), `POST /test-connection` (probes the configured `apiBaseUrl`, reports reachable/statusCode/latencyMs). CLI parity: new `shepard-admin minters epic {status,enable,disable,set-api-url,set-prefix,set-credential,clear-credential,test-connection}` eight verbs slotting into the existing `MintersCommand` two-level nesting (shared with the DataCite `minters datacite` group). `set-credential` reads stdin / tty only — never on the command line. Mint flow: B2HANDLE-compatible `PUT <apiBaseUrl>/handles/<prefix>/<uuid>` with JSON body `[{"type":"URL","parsed_data":"<locatorUrl>"}, ...]` + `Authorization: Basic <base64(credential)>`. On 200/201 returns `https://hdl.handle.net/<prefix>/<uuid>` as the PID. One retry on 5xx/network error with 1s backoff; 4xx is final. Credential at rest: same AES-GCM-256 cipher as KIP1d (key derived from `shepard.instance.id` + pepper `"shepard:KIP1c:epic:"` isolates from the DataCite key), stored on `:EpicMinterConfig.credentialKey`; SHA-256 hex on `.credentialHash` for fingerprint display. `V45` migration adds the appId uniqueness constraint on `:EpicMinterConfig`. Plugin tests: `EpicMinterTest` + `EpicMinterConfigServiceTest` + `EpicAdminRestTest` + `EpicCommandsTest` + `CredentialCipherTest` + `EpicMinterPluginManifestTest` (≥20 cases). Docs: `docs/reference/minter-epic.md` + `docs/help/minter-epic-quickstart.md` + `docs/install/minter-epic.md`. CI workflow extended with install + unit-test step for minter-epic + staging copy. | **AWARE + CONFIG** | One new image artefact (`shepard-plugin-minter-epic-${revision}.jar`) baked at `/deployments/lib/` + `/deployments/plugins/` — `PluginRegistry`'s duplicate-id check silently shadows the second copy, same as other plugins. **No `/shepard/api/...` change.** **No breaking wire-shape change** — five new `/v2/admin/minters/epic/...` endpoints are opt-in admin surface gated behind `instance-admin`. **Three new deploy-time install-default keys**: `shepard.minters.epic.{enabled,api-base-url,handle-prefix}` — each seeds the `:EpicMinterConfig` singleton on first start (admin-runtime per CLAUDE.md). Default `api-base-url=https://handle.argo.grnet.gr/api` (Helmholtz ePIC default). **Credential is intentionally not a deploy-time key** — operators set via the admin REST / CLI only. **Two new RFC 7807 problem types**: `minters.epic.config.read-only-field` (400 — caller PATCHed a credential field), `minters.epic.config.bad-request` (400 — empty credential body). One new Neo4j label (`:EpicMinterConfig`) with one new uniqueness constraint (V45). One new plugin toggle key: `shepard.plugins.minter-epic.enabled=false` (off by default). | `V45__Add_appId_constraint_EpicMinterConfig.cypher` — idempotent `CREATE CONSTRAINT EpicMinterConfig_appId_unique IF NOT EXISTS FOR (n:EpicMinterConfig) REQUIRE n.appId IS UNIQUE`. Fail-fast on error via `MigrationsRunner`. Migration regression test deferred (testcontainer fixture alongside the broader Neo4j migration testcontainer scaffold). No rollback file needed — constraint can be dropped via `DROP CONSTRAINT EpicMinterConfig_appId_unique IF EXISTS`. | **No action required for upgrade unless the operator wants ePIC handles.** The KIP1c plugin JAR is staged into `/deployments/plugins/` but does nothing until the operator (a) configures credentials and (b) flips `shepard.publish.minter=epic`. Until then the default `LocalMinter` from KIP1h keeps producing `shepard:<instance.id>:...:v<n>` PIDs. **To enable ePIC as the active minter**: `shepard-admin minters epic set-api-url https://handle.argo.grnet.gr/api` → `shepard-admin minters epic set-prefix 21.T11148` → `shepard-admin minters epic set-credential` (prompts) → `shepard-admin minters epic test-connection` (should exit 0) → `shepard-admin minters epic enable` → set `shepard.publish.minter=epic` in `application.properties` and restart. **To rotate the credential**: `shepard-admin minters epic set-credential` again (overwrites ciphertext + hash atomically). **Rollback** (rare): drop `shepard.publish.minter=epic` from `application.properties` (revert to `local`); the plugin JAR can stay on disk. If the operator wants the plugin uninstalled: `rm /deployments/plugins/shepard-plugin-minter-epic-*.jar`; pre-existing ePIC `:Publication` rows keep resolving via the KIP resolver (format-agnostic `findByPid`). |
| **KIP1d — DataCite Fabrica DOI minter plugin** | `aidocs/16` KIP1d + `aidocs/66 §6` + CLAUDE.md plugin-first heuristic #3 + "Always: surface operator knobs in the admin config". New Maven module `plugins/minter-datacite/` mirrors `plugins/minter-local/` shape — `DataciteMinterPluginManifest` discovered via `META-INF/services/de.dlr.shepard.plugin.PluginManifest`, JAR built with `provided`-scope backend dep, declared as a `<dependency>` in the backend's default-active `with-plugins` Maven profile, `quarkus.index-dependency.shepard-plugin-minter-datacite.*` ensures Jandex indexing so the `DataciteMinter` `@ApplicationScoped` bean + `DataciteAdminRest` `@Path` resource are discovered at build time. Activated by `shepard.publish.minter=datacite` once credentials are set. Five new admin REST endpoints under `/v2/admin/minters/datacite/` (all `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")` per the high-trust posture): `GET /config` (returns the masked `:DataciteMinterConfig` snapshot — passwordCipher + passwordHash are never serialised; passwordSet + 8-hex SHA-256 fingerprint surface instead), `PATCH /config` (RFC 7396 merge-patch — patchable fields are enabled / apiBaseUrl / handlePrefix / repositoryId / publisher / landingPageBase / defaultState; passwordHash / passwordCipher / password are sentinel-rejected with RFC 7807 `minters.datacite.config.read-only-field`), `POST /credential` (set / rotate — body carries the plaintext, response carries only the fingerprint), `DELETE /credential` (clear), `POST /test-connection` (probes `<apiBaseUrl>/heartbeat`, reports reachable/statusCode/latencyMs). CLI parity: new `shepard-admin minters datacite {status,enable,disable,set-api-url,set-prefix,set-repository-id,set-publisher,set-landing-page-base,set-state,set-password,clear-password,test-connection}` subcommand group via PM1d's `AdminCliCommandProvider` SPI — twelve verbs in a `MintersCommand` → `DataciteCommand` two-level nesting that lets future minter plugins (ePIC KIP1c, Crossref, …) slot in alongside. **`set-password` reads stdin / tty only**, never on the command line — `ps` + shell history can't leak the credential; CI piping `echo $PWD \| shepard-admin minters datacite set-password` works because the command falls back to a one-line `readLine` for non-tty stdin. Server-side credential at rest: AES-GCM-256 (key derived from `shepard.instance.id` + a fixed pepper via SHA-256, truncated to 32 bytes; ciphertext format `gcm1:` prefix + base64-url-no-padding of `IV(12) ‖ ciphertext ‖ tag(16)`) on `:DataciteMinterConfig.passwordCipher`. SHA-256 hex hash on `:passwordHash` for fingerprint display only. The cipher is operator-managed-secret level, not a KMS — an attacker who can read both Neo4j AND the JVM's `shepard.instance.id` recovers the plaintext; the runbook documents this trade-off. **Versioned PIDs (KIP1h respect)**: `DataciteMinter` reads `MintRequest.versionNumber()` directly (KIP1h first-classified it), encodes as `version: "v<n>"` in the DataCite body, and on n > 1 adds `relatedIdentifiers: [{ relationType: IsNewVersionOf, relatedIdentifier: <previous-DOI>, relatedIdentifierType: DOI }]` plus a back-fill `PUT /dois/<previous>` with the inverse `HasVersion` so the chain renders both ways in DataCite Commons. Previous-PID lookup is delegated to a `PreviousPublicationResolver` interface that defaults to a no-op (test-friendly; the plugin manifest's `onRegister` hook wires it to the in-core `PublicationDAO`). Back-fill failure is logged but non-fatal — the new mint already succeeded. **Network hardening**: 10s connect + 30s request timeout on the JDK `HttpClient`; one retry on 5xx / network exception with 1s backoff; `MinterException` on the second failure (caught by the in-core `PublishService` → 500 RFC 7807 `publish.minter.failed`). 4xx is final (no retry — DataCite's 4xx already carries the operator-actionable error in the body). **Resource-type mapping**: shepard's KIP `digitalObjectType` (Dataset / Collection / Software / Text / Image) maps onto DataCite's `resourceTypeGeneral` vocabulary with `Dataset` as the safe default fallback. **Event mapping**: DataCite `event` field set from the configured `defaultState` — `draft` / `registered` / `findable` → `draft` / `register` / `publish`. New tests: 126 cases across 9 classes in the plugin module — `DataciteMinterTest` 30 (id / isEnabled gating / mint happy-path / 4xx-no-retry / 5xx-with-retry / network-with-retry / two-fail / version encoding / IsNewVersionOf + HasVersion back-fill / back-fill-failure-non-fatal / resource-type-mapping / event-mapping / basic-auth-header / helpers), `DataciteMinterConfigServiceTest` 24 (seed-on-first-start / idempotent / install-defaults / patch happy-path + read-only-field + invalid-state / credential set / clear / resolvePlaintext / fingerprint / SHA-256 canonical / normaliseState), `DataciteAdminRestTest` 17 (annotation gates / GET-masked-shape / PATCH happy + read-only + bad-state / POST-credential / DELETE-credential / test-connection reachable + unreachable + unconfigured + 3xx + 5xx), `DataciteCommandsTest` 22 (every verb's request body shape + exit-code + plaintext-never-leaks-to-stdout-or-stderr), `CredentialCipherTest` 12 (encrypt-decrypt round-trip / different cipher text per call / instance-id binding / tamper detection / arg validation / key derivation / unicode + 2KiB plaintexts), `DataciteIoTest` 7 (wire-shape masking + touched-flag idiom + sentinel firing on password / passwordHash / passwordCipher), `MintersAdminCliCommandProviderTest` 5 (ServiceLoader discovery + Picocli structure + all 12 verbs registered), `DataciteMinterConfigTest` 5 (defaults + identity-by-appId), `DataciteMinterPluginManifestTest` 4 (SPI discovery + manifest shape + lifecycle no-throw). Migration: `V33__Add_appId_constraint_DataciteMinterConfig.cypher` — idempotent `CREATE CONSTRAINT IF NOT EXISTS FOR (n:DataciteMinterConfig) REQUIRE n.appId IS UNIQUE` (V31 + V32 taken by KIP1h backfill + PM1e PluginRuntimeOverride; KIP1d gets V33). Operator runbook: `docs/reference/minter-datacite.md` + the existing `docs/reference/publish-and-pids.md` §DataCite section. CI workflows extended with a fourth install step (`backend-ci.yml` + `build-images.yml`) and the staging `cp plugins/minter-datacite/target/...jar`. SpotBugs + findsecbugs clean; backend test suite unchanged at 2350; CLI test suite unchanged at 111. | **AWARE + CONFIG** | One new image artefact (`shepard-plugin-minter-datacite-${revision}.jar`) baked at `/deployments/lib/` + `/deployments/plugins/` — `PluginRegistry`'s duplicate-id check silently shadows the second copy, same as UH1a + KIP1g + KIP1h. **No `/shepard/api/...` change.** **No breaking wire-shape change** — KIP1d adds five new `/v2/admin/minters/datacite/...` endpoints (all opt-in admin surface, all gated behind `instance-admin`); doesn't touch any pre-existing endpoint. **Seven new deploy-time keys** — `shepard.minters.datacite.{enabled,api-base-url,handle-prefix,repository-id,publisher,landing-page-base,default-state}` — every one is an **install default** that seeds the runtime `:DataciteMinterConfig` singleton on first start (admin-runtime per CLAUDE.md). Default `api-base-url=https://api.test.datacite.org` (Fabrica test, the safe default). **Password is intentionally not a deploy-time key** — operators set via the admin REST / CLI only (gitleaks would flag a plaintext password in `application.properties` as a credential leak). **Three new RFC 7807 problem types**: `minters.datacite.config.read-only-field` (400 — caller PATCHed a credential field), `minters.datacite.config.bad-state` (400 — invalid defaultState), `minters.datacite.config.bad-request` (400 — empty credential body). One new Neo4j label (`:DataciteMinterConfig`) with one new uniqueness constraint (V33). One new CDI bean tree (`DataciteMinter` + `DataciteMinterConfigService` + `DataciteMinterConfigDAO` + `DataciteHttpClient` + `DataciteAdminRest`). One new `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")` REST class. | `V33__Add_appId_constraint_DataciteMinterConfig.cypher` — idempotent `CREATE CONSTRAINT IF NOT EXISTS FOR (n:DataciteMinterConfig) REQUIRE n.appId IS UNIQUE`. Fail-fast on error via `MigrationsRunner`. **Migration regression test deferred** — testcontainer fixture for the V33 constraint will land alongside the broader Neo4j migration testcontainer scaffold (same posture as V30 / V31 / V32). Operator-runbook pointer in the migration's top comment. No rollback script needed (constraint can be dropped via `DROP CONSTRAINT appId_unique_DataciteMinterConfig IF EXISTS`). | **No action required for upgrade unless the operator wants real DOIs.** The KIP1d plugin JAR is staged into `/deployments/plugins/` but does nothing until the operator (a) configures credentials and (b) flips `shepard.publish.minter=datacite`. Until then the default `LocalMinter` from KIP1h keeps producing `shepard:<instance.id>:...:v<n>` PIDs and nothing else changes. **To enable DataCite as the active minter**, the operator runs the following sequence — typically against DataCite Fabrica test (the default `apiBaseUrl=https://api.test.datacite.org`) until happy, then re-points at production: `shepard-admin minters datacite set-prefix 10.5072` → `shepard-admin minters datacite set-repository-id DLR.SHEPARD` → `shepard-admin minters datacite set-publisher "DLR e.V."` → `shepard-admin minters datacite set-landing-page-base https://shepard.example.dlr.de/v2` → `shepard-admin minters datacite set-password` (prompts) → `shepard-admin minters datacite test-connection` (should exit 0) → `shepard-admin minters datacite enable` → edit `shepard.publish.minter=datacite` in `application.properties` and restart. **To switch from Fabrica test to production**: `shepard-admin minters datacite set-api-url https://api.datacite.org` (the password / prefix / repositoryId are typically different for the production member contract — re-set them too). **To rotate the DataCite Member password**: `shepard-admin minters datacite set-password` again — the new ciphertext + hash overwrite atomically; in-flight mints on the old credential complete or fail based on DataCite's auth-cache semantics. **To audit who flipped what when**: `MATCH (a:Activity) WHERE a.path STARTS WITH '/v2/admin/minters/datacite/' RETURN a.actor, a.method, a.path, a.timestamp ORDER BY a.timestamp DESC LIMIT 50`. The body of the `POST /credential` is **not** captured (PROV1a's filter records only method + path + status), so plaintexts don't leak into `:Activity`. **Image-size impact: ~25 KB** (new `shepard-plugin-minter-datacite-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT.jar` containing 23 prod classes + 5 SPI resources + ~10 KB of Lombok-generated entity accessors). **Test obligation**: 126 plugin tests landed in this PR; testcontainer-level integration regression (boot real Quarkus + real Neo4j + mock DataCite HTTP server, mint a real DOI, verify the response shape end-to-end) is **deferred** alongside the broader QuarkusTest scaffolding obligation under PM1a + KIP1a. **Rollback** (rare): drop `shepard.publish.minter=datacite` from `application.properties` (revert to `local`); the plugin JAR can stay on disk — disabled minters cost zero at runtime. If the operator wants the plugin uninstalled entirely: `rm /deployments/plugins/shepard-plugin-minter-datacite-*.jar`; pre-existing DataCite `:Publication` rows continue to resolve via the KIP resolver (the resolver does an opaque `findByPid` — the DOI format is irrelevant to it). **First publish post-config flip**: `POST /v2/data-objects/{appId}/publish` mints a real DOI like `10.5072/abc-def-1` (or whatever DataCite returns) and stores it as the `:Publication.pid`. Subsequent `?force=true` re-mints bump the version segment (DataCite `version: "v2"` + `IsNewVersionOf=<v1-DOI>`) so the chain renders in DataCite Commons. **Default `defaultState=draft`** means a misconfigured mint is a recoverable DataCite-side delete rather than a permanent registered DOI — operators promote draft DOIs via the DataCite Fabrica UI once happy, or via a follow-up slice that exposes a `promote-doi` admin verb. |
| **KIP1g — KIP resolver + record JSON-LD shape as plugin** | `aidocs/16` KIP1g + `aidocs/66` + CLAUDE.md plugin-first heuristic #2 + `aidocs/63 ADR-0023`. Extracts `KipResolverRest` + `KipRecordIO` (the HMC Kernel Information Profile record shape) from in-tree `de.dlr.shepard.v2.publish.*` into a new `plugins/kip/` Maven module producing `shepard-plugin-kip-${revision}.jar`. The endpoint path (`GET /v2/.well-known/kip/{pid-suffix}`), JSON-LD response body, and the four RFC 7807 problem responses (`kip.pid.not-found` on blank suffix, missing publication; the three `publish.*` problems are still emitted from the in-core `PublishRest` and unchanged) are **byte-identical** to pre-KIP1g — operators see no wire-shape difference. What stays in core: `Minter` SPI (`de.dlr.shepard.publish.minter.Minter`) per CLAUDE.md plugin-first heuristic #3 ("SPI seams stay in-tree, adapters ship as plugins"), `MockMinter` + `MinterRegistry`, `:Publication` entity + `PublicationDAO`, `PublishableKindRegistry`, the generic `POST /v2/{kind}/{appId}/publish` orchestration in `PublishRest`, the generic `PublicationIO` wire shape. The KIP-specific record shape — `kernelInformationProfile` envelope, `digitalObjectType`, `landingPage`, `rightsHolder` — is HMC-flavoured and lives alongside the resolver in the plugin. The `.well-known/kip` public-prefix entry stays registered in core's `PublicEndpointRegistry` for the auth filter's benefit (the class Javadoc now documents the convention that plugin-contributed public paths are tracked centrally — a follow-up slice may introduce `PluginContext.registerPublicPrefix(...)` so plugins self-declare). Build wiring mirrors UH1a + PM1a phase 3: new `plugins/kip/pom.xml` (with-self-exclusion on the backend `provided` dep), `KipPluginManifest` discovered via `META-INF/services/de.dlr.shepard.plugin.PluginManifest`, declared as a `<dependency>` in the backend's default-active `with-plugins` Maven profile, `quarkus.index-dependency.shepard-plugin-kip.*` ensures Jandex indexing so Quarkus's build-time CDI scanner picks the `@RequestScoped` resource. CI workflows (`backend-ci.yml` + `build-images.yml`) extended with a third install step for the kip plugin (same two-pass dance as UH1a). Dockerfile change is zero — `plugins-stage/` `COPY` already copies whatever staging puts there; the `build-images.yml` staging step now copies both unhide + kip JARs. | **AWARE** | One new image artefact (`shepard-plugin-kip-${revision}.jar`) baked into the image both at `/deployments/lib/` (Quarkus's build-classpath copy) and at `/deployments/plugins/` (operator-visibility copy) — PluginRegistry's duplicate-id check silently shadows the redundant entry, same as UH1a. **No new endpoints, no new config keys, no schema delta.** The `/v2/.well-known/kip/{pid-suffix}` endpoint path is preserved exactly (operators with their own monitoring on the resolver see no change). Operators with custom builds that compose their own backend image without the `with-plugins` Maven profile (i.e. passing `-DnoPlugins`) now lose the KIP resolver alongside the unhide feed — the `:Publication` rows still mint via `POST .../publish` (in-core), but the freshly-returned `resolverUrl` field would 404 until a kip plugin is included; this is the standard plugin-removal posture and is the structural fix for KIP1g landing alongside KIP1c/d future minter plugins. The in-core `PublishRest` still emits `resolverUrl` because the URL composition only knows the canonical path — it does not check whether the resolver is wired (no runtime coupling between core and plugin beyond the public-prefix registry entry). | — (no Cypher migration — packaging-only change). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the PluginRegistry logs `KIP1g: kip plugin v1.0.0-SNAPSHOT active via PluginManifest SPI (id=kip, compat=>=5.2.0,<6)` on startup. To verify the resolver still works post-KIP1g: `curl https://shepard.example.dlr.de/v2/.well-known/kip/<real-pid-suffix>` returns the same JSON-LD shape it did pre-KIP1g (byte-identical). To inspect what's installed: `ls /deployments/plugins/` — both `shepard-plugin-unhide-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT.jar` and `shepard-plugin-kip-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT.jar` should appear. To opt out of the resolver (rare — operators who want the in-core publish endpoint without the KIP-specific record shape): set `shepard.plugins.kip.enabled=false` in `application.properties` and restart; the publish endpoint still works, the resolver returns 404 from the JAX-RS scanner (no resource registered on that path). Operators building their own backend image who don't want the resolver code on disk: pass `-DnoPlugins` to the backend build, but note this drops UH1a too — granular per-plugin opt-out at build time would need separate profiles per plugin (out of scope for KIP1g). Image-size impact: ~6 KB (manifest + resolver class + record class + nested record class). Test obligation: 6 `KipResolverRestTest` cases moved from in-tree to the plugin (backend test count drops 2272 → 2266, plugin gains 6 cases; total unchanged). Testcontainer-level integration regression (boot Quarkus + real Neo4j + mint a Publication via `POST .../publish` + verify the resolver returns the KIP record end-to-end) is **deferred** alongside the broader QuarkusTest scaffolding obligation tracked under PM1a + KIP1a. |
| **J1d — lab journal edit history** | `aidocs/37` J1d. New `:LabJournalEntryRevision` Neo4j node + `has_lab_journal_revision` edge (OUTGOING from `LabJournalEntry`). Fields: `content`, `revisionNumber` (1-based), `createdAt`, `createdBy`. `LabJournalEntryRevisionDAO.findByEntry(long entryNeo4jId)` queries revisions ordered by `revisionNumber DESC`. `LabJournalEntryService.updateLabJournalEntry` creates a revision snapshot of the old content BEFORE overwriting it (skips no-op edits where content is unchanged). New `GET /v2/lab-journal/{entryAppId}/history` endpoint (`LabJournalHistoryRest` under `de.dlr.shepard.v2.labjournal.resources`): resolves a `LabJournalEntry` by `appId`, checks Read permission on the parent DataObject (same gate as J1a), returns a JSON array of `LabJournalRevisionIO` — fields: `appId`, `content`, `revisedAt`, `revisedBy`, `revisionNumber`. Empty array when the entry has never been edited. 404 when missing or deleted; 403 on permission denied; 401 unauthenticated. `V39__Add_appId_constraint_LabJournalEntryRevision.cypher` — idempotent unique constraint on `LabJournalEntryRevision.appId`. 13 unit tests (8 `LabJournalHistoryRestTest` + 5 `LabJournalEntryRevisionDAOTest`). | **ZERO** | **One new `/v2/` endpoint**: `GET /v2/lab-journal/{entryAppId}/history`. **No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path.** **No new config keys.** **New Neo4j label** `:LabJournalEntryRevision` + new constraint (V39). **No change to existing node shapes** — the revision is a separate node connected by a new relationship type; the `LabJournalEntry` node shape is unchanged. Pre-existing entries have no revisions; first edit creates revision 1. | `V39__Add_appId_constraint_LabJournalEntryRevision.cypher` — `CREATE CONSTRAINT LabJournalEntryRevision_appId_unique IF NOT EXISTS FOR (n:LabJournalEntryRevision) REQUIRE n.appId IS UNIQUE`. Idempotent; fail-fast via `MigrationsRunner`. Migration regression test deferred (testcontainer assertion planned alongside the broader Neo4j migration scaffold). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the V39 constraint is applied on startup and the new endpoint appears in the OpenAPI spec at `GET /v2/lab-journal/{entryAppId}/history`. Pre-existing entries will have an empty history (no revisions were captured before J1d shipped). From the first edit after upgrade, revisions accumulate automatically. |
| **J1b — `.ipynb` file-reference listing endpoint** | `aidocs/37 §3` J1b. New `GET /v2/lab-journal/{dataObjectAppId}/notebooks` endpoint (`NotebookRest` under `de.dlr.shepard.v2.labjournal.resources`): resolves the DataObject by `appId` via `EntityIdResolver`, checks Read permission on the DataObject (same gate as other DataObject-scoped `/v2/` endpoints), then collects `.ipynb` file refs from two sources: (1) FR1b singletons (`SingletonFileReferenceService.listByDataObject`) and (2) FR1a bundle files (`FileBundleReferenceDAO.findByDataObjectNeo4jId` → `bundle.getFiles()`). Case-insensitive filter (`filename.toLowerCase().endsWith(".ipynb")`). Returns a JSON array of `NotebookReferenceIO` — fields: `appId` (singleton's own appId, or bundle's appId for bundle-files), `fileName`, `fileSize` (nullable), `mimeType` (`"application/x-ipynb+json"`), `createdAt`, `createdBy`, `referenceKind` (`SINGLETON` or `BUNDLE_FILE`). Empty array when no `.ipynb` files are attached; 404 when no DataObject with that appId exists; 403 on Read permission denied; 401 unauthenticated. No new Maven deps; no data migration. 14 unit tests in `NotebookRestTest`. The frontend inline render (nbviewer-js) is the J1c slice. | **ZERO** | **One new `/v2/` endpoint**: `GET /v2/lab-journal/{dataObjectAppId}/notebooks`. **No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path.** **No new config keys.** **No Neo4j migration** — queries the existing `:SingletonFileReference` + `:FileReference` + `:ShepardFile` graph; no new labels or constraints. `/v2/` development surface only; operators who never call it see no change. | No new migrations. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the new endpoint appears in the OpenAPI spec at `GET /v2/lab-journal/{dataObjectAppId}/notebooks`. To list `.ipynb` files on a DataObject: `curl /v2/lab-journal/<dataObjectAppId>/notebooks -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'` returns `[{appId, fileName, fileSize, mimeType, createdAt, createdBy, referenceKind}, ...]`. Empty array is normal when no notebooks are attached. |
| **J1c — "Open in Jupyter" deep link** | `aidocs/37 §3.2` J1c. **Frontend only — no backend migration.** New `DataObjectNotebooksPane.vue` component added to the DataObject page expansion-panel list (between "Lab Journal" and "Git References"). The pane calls `GET /v2/lab-journal/{dataObjectAppId}/notebooks` (J1b backend) via the new `NotebookApi` in `backend-client`. For each `.ipynb` reference: SINGLETON kind shows a download link (`/v2/files/{appId}/content`) and an "Open in JupyterHub" button; BUNDLE_FILE kind shows the filename and kind chip without a direct download link. The "Open in JupyterHub" button opens the user's JupyterHub base URL (`editor.preferredJupyter` preference key) in a new tab. When `editor.preferredJupyter` is not set the button renders disabled with a tooltip instructing the user to set their URL. An inline text field lets users set or update their JupyterHub URL; saved via `PATCH /v2/users/me/preferences` (U1d endpoint). New backend-client additions: `NotebookApi.ts` (`listNotebooks(dataObjectAppId): Promise<NotebookReferenceIO[]>`) + `NotebookReferenceIO.ts` model + `MeApi.getPreferences()` / `MeApi.patchPreferences()` methods targeting `GET/PATCH /v2/users/me/preferences`. New frontend composables: `useFetchNotebooks` + `useJupyterPreference`. Backend unchanged. | **ZERO** | **No new backend endpoints** (uses J1b's `GET /v2/lab-journal/{dataObjectAppId}/notebooks` and U1d's `GET/PATCH /v2/users/me/preferences` — both already shipped). **No new config keys.** **No Neo4j migration.** **No `/shepard/api/...` change.** Frontend-only change; operators upgrading the frontend image see a new "Jupyter Notebooks" expansion panel on every DataObject page. | No new migrations. | **No action required for upgrade.** After deploying the new frontend image the "Jupyter Notebooks" panel appears on DataObject pages. The panel is always shown when a DataObject has an `appId`; it fetches the notebooks list on expand. Users set their JupyterHub URL once via the inline field; it is stored in their preferences and persists across sessions. |
| **U1c2 — role-in-context chip in collection sidebar** | `aidocs/51 §9.4` U1c2. **Frontend only — no backend migration.** New `CollectionRoleChip.vue` component + `useMyRoleIn` composable + `MeRoleInApi` backend-client class. Chip calls `GET /v2/me/role-in/{collectionAppId}` (backend shipped in U1c2-backend) and renders the caller's effective role (Owner / Editor / Reader, color-coded) and/or an Instance Admin badge in the collection sidebar header. Watches the reactive `collectionAppId` computed ref so it refetches automatically on collection navigation without remounting. Backend unchanged. | **ZERO** | **No new backend endpoints** (uses U1c2-backend's `GET /v2/me/role-in/{collectionAppId}` — already shipped). **No new config keys.** **No Neo4j migration.** **No `/shepard/api/...` change.** Frontend-only change; operators upgrading the frontend image see the role chip below the collection name in the sidebar. | No new migrations. | **No action required for upgrade.** After deploying the new frontend image the role chip appears in the collection sidebar header whenever the caller has a role on the open collection. |
| **KIP1f — retire-publication endpoint** | `aidocs/16` KIP1f + `aidocs/66 §7`. New `DELETE /v2/{kind}/{appId}/publish` endpoint — retires the entity's most-recent `:Publication` row by setting `digitalObjectMutability = 'retired'`. The row is never deleted (KIP records are append-only per the HMC spec); "retired" is a shepard-extension mutability value signalling "no longer the operator's intent" without implying the bit-stream changed. | **ZERO** | **One new `/v2/` endpoint**: `DELETE /v2/{kind}/{appId}/publish`. Same auth gate as POST (Write/Manage on the entity). Idempotent — second DELETE on an already-retired row returns 204. Returns 404+problem-json `publish.publication.not-found` when the entity has no Publication. **Wire-shape change**: `PublicationIO` (returned by POST) grows an additive nullable `digitalObjectMutability` field — null on active Publications (absent from JSON via `@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)`); `"retired"` after a retire call. **No new config keys. No Neo4j migration** — `digitalObjectMutability` is an additive nullable property on the existing `:Publication` label (no constraint needed). No change to `/shepard/api/...` surface. | No new migrations. | **No action required for upgrade.** The `DELETE /v2/{kind}/{appId}/publish` endpoint is purely additive. Existing API clients that parse `PublicationIO` and encounter the new `digitalObjectMutability` field should ignore it (JSON open-world semantics; clients using lenient deserialisation — Jackson default, Python `requests`, JS `fetch` — are unaffected). To retire a Publication: `curl -X DELETE /v2/data-objects/<appId>/publish -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'` returns 204. The PID resolver (`GET /v2/.well-known/kip/<pid>`) continues to return the KIP record regardless of mutability state — the row is not deleted. Operators who want to surface the retired state to end-users should check `digitalObjectMutability` on the POST response or add their own `GET /v2/{kind}/{appId}/publications` listing endpoint (queued as a follow-up). |
| **J1a — CommonMark + GFM markdown render endpoint for lab journal entries** | `aidocs/37 §2` J1a. New `LabJournalRenderService` (`@ApplicationScoped`) wraps `commonmark-java` 0.24.0 with GFM tables, strikethrough, and task-list extensions; `sanitizeUrls=true` strips `javascript:` hrefs from the rendered output. New `GET /v2/lab-journal/{appId}/render` endpoint (`LabJournalRenderRest` under `de.dlr.shepard.v2.labjournal.resources`): resolves a `LabJournalEntry` by `appId`, checks Read permission on the parent DataObject (same gate as `GET /shepard/api/labJournalEntries/{id}`), and returns sanitised HTML. Content negotiation: `Accept: text/html` returns the bare HTML string; `Accept: application/json` (or default) returns `{html, sourceLength}` envelope. 404 when the entry is missing or deleted; 403 on permission denied; 401 unauthenticated. `LabJournalEntryDAO.findByAppId(String)` added (Cypher MATCH by `appId` property, consistent with `TimeseriesAnnotationDAO` pattern). `LabJournalEntryIO` gains a read-only `contentFormat: "MARKDOWN"` field — fixed constant for now (all entries are treated as CommonMark; old plain-text entries render as `<p>` elements). No data migration needed: CommonMark passes plain text through unchanged. New `org.commonmark:{commonmark,commonmark-ext-gfm-tables,commonmark-ext-gfm-strikethrough,commonmark-ext-task-list-items}:0.24.0` Maven deps. 9 unit tests: `LabJournalRenderServiceTest` (7 cases — null/blank → empty, plain-text → `<p>`, `**bold**` → `<strong>`, GFM table → `<table>`, `javascript:` href stripped, fenced code → `<pre><code>`, GFM strikethrough → `<del>`, task-list → `type="checkbox"`); `LabJournalRenderRestTest` (5 cases — 200 with HTML, 404 unknown, 404 deleted, 403 no-read, 401 unauthenticated). | **AWARE** | **One new `/v2/` endpoint**: `GET /v2/lab-journal/{appId}/render`. **No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path.** **`LabJournalEntryIO` wire shape grows one additive read-only field** `contentFormat: "MARKDOWN"` — this field appears on both `/shepard/api/labJournalEntries` responses (compat surface) and the new `/v2/` response. Existing clients using lenient JSON deserialisation (Jackson default, Python `requests`, JS `fetch`) are unaffected; clients with strict schema validation must tolerate the new field. **Four new Maven deps** (`commonmark` 0.24.0 family) — no breaking API change to existing deps. **No new config keys.** **No Neo4j migration** — no new labels, no new constraints; `LabJournalEntryDAO.findByAppId` queries by the existing `appId` property (set on save since L2a; pre-L2a entries with `appId=null` simply won't resolve via the new endpoint, consistent with other appId-aware `/v2/` endpoints). | No new migrations. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the new endpoint appears in the OpenAPI spec at `GET /v2/lab-journal/{appId}/render`. Clients that never call it see no change. To render a journal entry: first resolve its `appId` from `GET /shepard/api/labJournalEntries/{id}` (the `appId` field is present since L2a), then `curl /v2/lab-journal/<appId>/render -H 'Accept: text/html'` to get the sanitised HTML, or omit the header for the JSON envelope `{html, sourceLength}`. Plain-text entries written before J1a render correctly as `<p>` elements — no re-ingestion needed. |
| **PV1a — per-payload byte versioning for FileContainer uploads** | `aidocs/16` PV1a + `aidocs/data/46-payload-versioning-design.md`. New standalone Neo4j node `:PayloadVersion` with fields: `appId` (UUID v7, unique), `fileOid`, `sha256` (SHA-256 upper-case hex), `sizeBytes`, `versionNumber` (1-based counter per `(containerAppId, originalName)` scope), `uploadedBy`, `uploadedAt`, `containerAppId`, `originalName`, `deleted`. `FileContainerService.createFile()` now calls `FileService.createFileWithSha256()` (new additive method returning `FileCreateResult{ShepardFile, sha256}` — two chained `DigestInputStream`s: SHA-256 inner, MD5 outer, single stream pass) and then calls the new best-effort `recordPayloadVersion()` helper which increments the version counter and persists a `:PayloadVersion` node. Pre-L2a containers (no `appId`) silently skip version recording. Version recording errors do not roll back the already-persisted upload (try-catch in `recordPayloadVersion`). New `PayloadVersionDAO` with three Cypher methods: `findByContainerAndName` (ordered ASC), `findByAppId`, `findMaxVersionNumber`. New `GET /v2/file-containers/{containerAppId}/files/{originalName}/versions` endpoint (`PayloadVersionRest`) returns the version list as `PayloadVersionIO` records. Read permission enforced via `getContainerByAppId` (existing gate; 403 on failure). Empty array returned when no versions exist. `V41__Add_appId_constraint_PayloadVersion.cypher` — unique constraint on `:PayloadVersion(appId)`. 13 unit tests: `PayloadVersionDAOTest` (6 cases), `PayloadVersionRestTest` (4 cases), `FileContainerServiceTest` (3 PV1a cases). New `FileServiceTest` (2 cases covering SHA-256 computation). | **ZERO** | **One new `/v2/` endpoint** (`GET /v2/file-containers/{containerAppId}/files/{originalName}/versions`) — additive, opt-in. No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path. No new config keys. **One new Neo4j label** `:PayloadVersion` with unique-appId constraint (`V41`). No change to any existing node shape or relationship. **`FileContainerService.createFile()` now computes SHA-256** (chained `DigestInputStream`) on every upload — the computation adds no round-trip to MongoDB and is negligible for all practical file sizes. **Pre-L2a containers** (those without an `appId` — created before L2a shipped) do not get version nodes recorded; their upload path is unchanged (the best-effort helper logs a WARNING and returns). | `V41__Add_appId_constraint_PayloadVersion.cypher` — `CREATE CONSTRAINT PayloadVersion_appId_unique IF NOT EXISTS FOR (n:PayloadVersion) REQUIRE n.appId IS UNIQUE`. Idempotent; fail-fast via `MigrationsRunner`. No rollback file needed (additive constraint on a new label with zero pre-existing rows; drop via `DROP CONSTRAINT PayloadVersion_appId_unique IF EXISTS`). Migration regression test deferred (testcontainer assertion alongside the broader V35–V40 obligations). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V41 runs on first start (zero `:PayloadVersion` rows). Uploads to `appId`-equipped containers automatically begin creating `:PayloadVersion` nodes from this release forward — no backfill of historical uploads. To query the version history for a file: `GET /v2/file-containers/{containerAppId}/files/{originalName}/versions` (requires a Bearer token or API key with Read access on the container). Returns `[{"appId":"...","versionNumber":1,"fileOid":"...","sha256":"E3B0...","sizeBytes":4096,"uploadedBy":"alice","uploadedAt":"2026-05-17T00:00:00Z"},...]`. |
| **PV1b — payload versioning for StructuredDataReference uploads** | `aidocs/16` PV1b + `aidocs/data/46-payload-versioning-design.md §2.2`. Extends PV1a's `:PayloadVersion` shape to `StructuredDataContainer` uploads. `StructuredDataContainerService.createStructuredData()` now calls the new best-effort `recordPayloadVersion()` helper which: (1) computes SHA-256 and `sizeBytes` from the JSON payload bytes (`payload.getPayload().getBytes(UTF_8)`); (2) uses `StructuredData.getName()` as `originalName` (Mongo oid fallback for anonymous entries); (3) uses the Mongo oid as `fileOid`; (4) increments the version counter per `(containerAppId, originalName)` scope; (5) persists a `:PayloadVersion` node. Pre-L2a containers (no `appId`) silently skip version recording. Version recording errors do not roll back the already-persisted document. New `GET /v2/structured-data-containers/{containerAppId}/files/{originalName}/versions` endpoint (`StructuredDataPayloadVersionRest`) returns the version list as `PayloadVersionIO` records; same wire shape as PV1a. Reuses shared `PayloadVersionDAO` and `PayloadVersionIO`. No new Neo4j migrations (`:PayloadVersion` label + `appId` unique constraint already created by V41 in PV1a). 4 unit tests in `StructuredDataPayloadVersionRestTest`. | **ZERO** | **One new `/v2/` endpoint** (`GET /v2/structured-data-containers/{containerAppId}/files/{originalName}/versions`) — additive, opt-in. No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path. No new config keys. **No new Neo4j label or migration** — reuses `:PayloadVersion` from PV1a. **`StructuredDataContainerService.createStructuredData()` now computes SHA-256** inline from the JSON bytes on every upload — negligible overhead. **Pre-L2a containers** (those without an `appId`) do not get version nodes recorded; the best-effort helper logs a WARNING and returns. | No new migrations. | **No action required for upgrade.** The SHA-256 computation on each structured-data upload adds no user-visible latency. To query version history: `GET /v2/structured-data-containers/{containerAppId}/files/{entryName}/versions` (where `entryName` is `StructuredData.name` or the Mongo oid for anonymous entries). Returns the same `PayloadVersionIO` shape as PV1a. |
| **APISIMP-PV-UNIFY — unified payload-version endpoint** | `aidocs/16` APISIMP-PV-UNIFY. Collapses `PayloadVersionRest` (`GET /v2/file-containers/{cid}/files/{name}/versions`) and `StructuredDataPayloadVersionRest` (`GET /v2/structured-data-containers/{cid}/files/{name}/versions`) into a single generic `GET /v2/containers/{appId}/files/{fileName}/versions` routed via `ContainerKindHandler.listVersions`. Default method returns `Optional.empty()` → 415 for kinds without file-payload versioning (timeseries, hdf). `FileContainerKindHandler` + `StructuredDataContainerKindHandler` override via the shared `PayloadVersionDAO.findByContainerAndName`. `PayloadVersionRest` + `StructuredDataPayloadVersionRest` + their 8 unit tests deleted; 6 new unit tests in `ContainersV2RestTest`. Wire shape unchanged — same `PayloadVersionIO` records. | **AWARE** | **Two `/v2/` endpoints removed** (`GET /v2/file-containers/{cid}/files/{name}/versions` and `GET /v2/structured-data-containers/{cid}/files/{name}/versions`). Callers **must migrate** to `GET /v2/containers/{appId}/files/{fileName}/versions`. The new endpoint requires a container `appId` (UUID v7), not a numeric id. Wire shape (`PayloadVersionIO`) is identical. `timeseries` + `hdf` kind containers return `415 Unsupported Media Type`. No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path. No new config keys. No Neo4j migration. | No new migrations. | **Migrate callers**: replace `GET /v2/file-containers/{cid}/files/{name}/versions` and `GET /v2/structured-data-containers/{cid}/files/{name}/versions` with `GET /v2/containers/{cid}/files/{name}/versions`. The `{cid}` must be the container `appId` (UUID v7) — the new endpoint resolves by appId, same as all other `/v2/containers/{appId}/...` routes. Response shape is identical. To verify: `curl /v2/containers/<containerAppId>/files/<fileName>/versions -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'` → `200` with the version array (may be empty). A 415 response means the container kind does not support payload versioning (e.g. timeseries). |
| **V2CONV-B4 — scene-graph dissolved into a `MAPPING_RECIPE` view-shape** | `aidocs/16` V2CONV-B4 + `aidocs/platform/191 §decision-2`. The bespoke scene-graph subsystem (DT1-PHASE-0 + SCENEGRAPH-REST-1 + COLL-SCENE-1) converges into the generic MAPPING_RECIPE mechanism shipped by V2CONV-B3. A scene-graph is now a `MAPPING_RECIPE` `ShepardTemplate` binding a URDF FileReference (kinematic tree parsed on demand), an optional joint TimeseriesReference, and a channel→joint binding map, targeting the new `SceneGraphPlayShape` (IRI `http://semantics.dlr.de/shepard/transform#SceneGraphPlayShape`). The `SceneGraphPlayTransformExecutor` lives in `plugins/vis-trace3d` and returns a `TransformResult.view` play envelope (frame tree + joint plan) for the Trace3D-family renderer. **DELETED**: the entire `/v2/scene-graphs/*` namespace (list/get/create, frame + joint CRUD, `export.urdf`, `export.usd`, `POST /v2/scene-graphs/from-urdf/{appId}`), the `:DigitalTwinScene` / `:CoordinateFrame` / `:Joint` stored graph, the 10 `scene_graph_*` MCP tools, and the bespoke frontend `/scene-graphs/*` editor. The Collection hero link `GET/PUT/DELETE /v2/collections/{appId}/scene-graph` is **kept** (path + `:Collection.sceneGraphAppId` property unchanged) but now references a MAPPING_RECIPE template appId instead of a `:DigitalTwinScene`. Idempotency back-annotation renamed `urn:shepard:scenegraph:scene-appId` → `urn:shepard:mapping:scenegraph-template-appId`. Migration `V111__B4_dissolve_scenegraph.cypher` (+ `V111_R__`). 16 plugin JUnit + V111 migration regression test + 16 Vitest. | **BREAKING (v2 surface only)** | **The entire `/v2/scene-graphs/*` namespace is REMOVED.** Any client (third-party or internal) that called `GET/POST /v2/scene-graphs`, the frame/joint CRUD sub-paths, `GET /v2/scene-graphs/{appId}/export.urdf`, `export.usd`, or `POST /v2/scene-graphs/from-urdf/{appId}` will get **404**. **No `/shepard/api/...` (upstream v1 compat) path is touched** — this is entirely a `/v2/` development-surface change, so an upstream-5.2.0 client is unaffected. The `:DigitalTwinScene` / `:CoordinateFrame` / `:Joint` node DATA is **deleted and not recoverable from the graph** — the URDF FileReference is the new source of truth. The 10 `scene_graph_*` MCP tools are gone (generic MAPPING_RECIPE MCP tools tracked as `MCP-MAPPING-RECIPE-1`). | `V111__B4_dissolve_scenegraph.cypher` — idempotent + fail-fast: `MATCH (s:DigitalTwinScene) WITH collect(s.appId) AS d MATCH (c:Collection) WHERE c.sceneGraphAppId IN d REMOVE c.sceneGraphAppId`; then `DETACH DELETE` of the three labels; then `DROP CONSTRAINT ... IF EXISTS` of `appId_unique_{DigitalTwinScene,CoordinateFrame,Joint}`. Rollback twin `V111_R__` re-creates only the constraint schema (the deleted node DATA is intentionally non-recoverable — one-way convergence). Regression test `TestNeo4jMigrations.testV111_B4DissolveScenegraph`. | **Operators with stored scenes they need to keep MUST snapshot the Neo4j graph BEFORE upgrading** — V111 deletes the `:DigitalTwinScene` graph unconditionally. After upgrade: re-create scene-graphs from URDF FileReferences via the "Create 3D view from this URDF" button on a URDF FileReference detail page (mints a MAPPING_RECIPE template), or `POST /v2/templates` a MAPPING_RECIPE body targeting `SceneGraphPlayShape`. Collection hero links that pointed at a deleted scene are cleared by V111; re-link to a MAPPING_RECIPE template appId via `PUT /v2/collections/{appId}/scene-graph`. Clients calling the old `/v2/scene-graphs/*` paths must migrate to `POST /v2/mappings/{templateAppId}/materialize`. |
| **URSCRIPT-TRAJECTORY-1 — URScript interpret as a `MAPPING_RECIPE` transform-shape** | `aidocs/16` URSCRIPT-TRAJECTORY-1. New additive `/v2/` capability: the UR-robot sibling of V2CONV-B5 (KRL dissolve). A `.urscript`/`.script` FileReference + a URDF FileReference → a derived joint-trajectory `TimeseriesReference`, via the generic `POST /v2/mappings/{templateAppId}/materialize`. New `UrScriptTrajectoryShape` IRI `http://semantics.dlr.de/shepard/transform#UrScriptTrajectoryShape` (SHACL at `backend/src/main/resources/shapes/urscript-trajectory.shacl.ttl`). In-tree `UrScriptTrajectoryTransformExecutor` (`de.dlr.shepard.v2.transform.urscript`) registered in `META-INF/services/...TransformExecutor`. `UrScriptSidecarClient` default URL `http://urscript-interpreter:8080` (config keys `shepard.urscript.interpreter.*`). `UrScriptTrajectoryService` records `:UrScriptTrajectoryActivity` (no IK stats — simpler than KRL). `SingletonFileReferenceService.detectFileKind` gains `urscript`/`script` → `"urscript"`. FE: `useUrScriptTrajectory.ts` composable + `interpretAsTrajectoryHelpers.isUrScriptFile` + `InterpretAsTrajectoryButton.vue` generalised by `fileKind`. No new REST namespace. No Neo4j migration. | **ZERO** | **No new REST namespace**. No `/shepard/api/...` path touched. No Neo4j migration — the `:UrScriptTrajectoryActivity` label is additive and schema-free; pre-existing `:Activity` nodes are unaffected. New config keys `shepard.urscript.interpreter.*` are all optional with sensible defaults. **Endpoint returns 502 until the URScript sidecar is brought up** — operator opt-in posture (analogous to the KRL sidecar). | None. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard. New `fileKind = "urscript"` detection for `.urscript`/`.script` uploads happens automatically. To use: upload a `.urscript` FileReference, open its detail page, click "Interpret as joint trajectory", pick a URDF + target DataObject + TimeseriesContainer, click "Interpret". Or via API: `POST /v2/templates` targeting `UrScriptTrajectoryShape`, then `POST /v2/mappings/{templateAppId}/materialize`. Sidecar: `COMPOSE_PROFILES=urscript-interpreter docker compose up -d`. |
| **V2CONV-B5 — KRL interpret dissolved into a `MAPPING_RECIPE` transform-shape** | `aidocs/16` V2CONV-B5 + `aidocs/platform/191 §decision-2`. The bespoke KRL interpret subsystem (KRL-INTERPRETER-05/06) converges into the generic MAPPING_RECIPE mechanism shipped by V2CONV-B3 — the REFERENCE-direction sibling of B4's scene-graph (which returns a VIEW). A KRL interpret is now a `MAPPING_RECIPE` `ShepardTemplate` binding a `.src`/`.krl` FileReference + a URDF FileReference + the target DataObject + the TimeseriesContainer, targeting the new `KrlTrajectoryShape` (IRI `http://semantics.dlr.de/shepard/transform#KrlTrajectoryShape`, SHACL at `backend/src/main/resources/shapes/krl-trajectory.shacl.ttl`). The `KrlTrajectoryTransformExecutor` was shipped **in-tree** at `de.dlr.shepard.v2.transform.krl` (extracted to `plugins/krl-interpreter` in V2CONV-A6, 2026-06-04) and returns a `TransformResult.reference(derivedTimeseriesReferenceAppId)` — the minted joint trajectory. The KRL sidecar HTTP client (`KrlSidecarClient`) + deploy-time config (`KrlInterpreterConfig`, the `shepard.krl.sidecar.*` keys) are **reused/relocated** next to the executor, unchanged. **DELETED**: the `POST /v2/krl/interpret` endpoint (`KrlInterpretRest`), its `KrlInterpretRequestIO`/`KrlInterpretResponseIO`/`KrlIkStatsIO`/`KrlWarningIO`/`KrlUnsupportedConstructIO` IO shapes, the `KrlInterpretService`, the `:KrlInterpretActivity` `@NodeEntity`, and the bespoke frontend (`RunKrlPreviewButton`/`RunKrlPreviewDialog`/`KrlInterpretResultPanel`/`useKrlInterpret` + helpers). New FE: `InterpretAsTrajectoryButton.vue` + `useKrlTrajectory.ts` + `interpretAsTrajectoryHelpers.ts` (in-context on the `.src`/`.krl` FileReference detail page). Migration `V112__B5_dissolve_krl.cypher` (+ `V112_R__`) relabels `:KrlInterpretActivity` → `:KrlTrajectoryActivity`. 11 executor JUnit + V112 migration file test + 11 Vitest. | **BREAKING (v2 surface only)** | **The `POST /v2/krl/interpret` endpoint is REMOVED.** Any client (third-party or internal) that posted to `/v2/krl/interpret` will get **404**. **No `/shepard/api/...` (upstream v1 compat) path is touched** — this is entirely a `/v2/` development-surface change, so an upstream-5.2.0 client is unaffected. The bespoke 502/504/501 sidecar-status mapping is gone — a down/erroring sidecar now surfaces as a single `422` transform error through the generic materialize dispatcher. The `:KrlInterpretActivity` node label is **relabelled** to `:KrlTrajectoryActivity` (audit data preserved — see migration; pure label rename, NO data deletion). | `V112__B5_dissolve_krl.cypher` — idempotent + fail-fast: `MATCH (a:KrlInterpretActivity) SET a:KrlTrajectoryActivity REMOVE a:KrlInterpretActivity`. A pure label rename — every property + PROV-O edge + the base `:Activity` label is preserved (referenced-data infinite-retention). Rollback twin `V112_R__` is the inverse relabel (fully reversible). The `:KrlInterpretActivity` had no appId-uniqueness constraint of its own (appId inherited from `:Activity`), so none to drop. | **No data-loss action required** (the audit trail is preserved by relabel). **Clients calling `POST /v2/krl/interpret` MUST migrate** to: (1) `POST /v2/templates` a MAPPING_RECIPE body targeting `KrlTrajectoryShape` (binding `srcFileReferenceAppId` + `urdfFileReferenceAppId` + `targetDataObjectAppId` + `timeseriesContainerAppId`), then (2) `POST /v2/mappings/{templateAppId}/materialize` with `inputReferenceAppIds: {srcFileAppId, urdfFileAppId}` → 200 + `{outputKind: "REFERENCE", derivedReferenceAppId}`. In the UI, use the **"Interpret as joint trajectory"** button on a `.src`/`.krl` FileReference detail page. To audit relabelled runs: `MATCH (a:KrlTrajectoryActivity) RETURN a.appId, a.interpreterVersion, a.ikFailedPoses, a.ikTotalPoses ORDER BY a.startedAtMillis DESC LIMIT 10`. Verify removal: `curl -fsS -X POST https://<your-host>/v2/krl/interpret -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -d '{}' -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n'` → `404`. |

| **V2CONV-A6 — KRL trajectory executor extracted to `plugins/krl-interpreter`** | `aidocs/16` V2CONV-A6 + `aidocs/platform/191 §7`. The `KrlTrajectoryTransformExecutor` (shipped in-tree in V2CONV-B5) moves to a new Tier-1 Maven plugin `plugins/krl-interpreter/`. The plugin adds `KrlInterpreterPluginManifest` (registers at startup) + wires both the `TransformExecutor` and `PluginManifest` SPIs via `META-INF/services/`. Disabled by default (`shepard.plugins.krl-interpreter.enabled=false`); requires `COMPOSE_PROFILES=krl-interpreter` or runtime `shepard-admin plugins enable krl-interpreter` + the KRL sidecar (`KrlSidecarClient`, `shepard.krl.sidecar.*` config keys unchanged). Added to `scripts/install-plugins.sh` + `backend/pom.xml` `with-plugins` profile. No REST paths changed; no Neo4j migration. | **ADDITIVE + OPERATOR-OPT-IN** | The `KrlTrajectoryShape` IRI + `POST /v2/mappings/{appId}/materialize` path are **unchanged** — the executor's home moves from in-tree to a plugin JAR, but the ServiceLoader contract, the SHACL shape, and every API surface stay identical. **The only operator-visible change is that the executor is now disabled by default** — a shepard instance that previously had KRL trajectory working (B5 executor was always registered in-tree) needs to explicitly enable the plugin. | None. | **If using KRL trajectory interpret**: enable the plugin (`COMPOSE_PROFILES=krl-interpreter` or `shepard-admin plugins enable krl-interpreter`) and ensure the KRL sidecar is running (`shepard.krl.sidecar.url`). Verify registration: `GET /v2/admin/plugins` → entry `{"id":"krl-interpreter","enabled":true}`. The `POST /v2/mappings/{appId}/materialize` path with `KrlTrajectoryShape` IRI continues to work unchanged. |

| **P23 — presign-vs-cache TTL invariant** | New `PresignTtlValidator` (`@ApplicationScoped`) reads `shepard.storage.presign.{upload,download,export}-ttl` (defaults PT15M/PT5M/PT30M) and `shepard.permissions.cache.ttl` (default PT5M); caps each effective presigned TTL to the cache TTL and emits a `WARN` at startup when any configured TTL would have been capped. `FileContainerPresignedUrlRest` + `CollectionExportUrlRest` now use the validator's effective TTLs instead of hardcoded constants. Security guarantee: a permission revoked in shepard takes effect within one cache-TTL window; presigned S3 URLs cannot outlive it. | **AWARE** | Three new optional config keys `shepard.storage.presign.{upload,download,export}-ttl` (all default to the pre-P23 hardcoded values, so upgrade is zero-config). Effective TTLs are now bounded by `shepard.permissions.cache.ttl`. **Operators who need longer presigned TTLs must increase `shepard.permissions.cache.ttl`** (rather than the presign TTL) — both move together. A WARN is emitted at startup if the configured TTL would be capped. | — | **No action required for upgrade.** For GridFS installs the presigned URL endpoints return 503 anyway — P23 has no effect. For S3 installs: startup WARN will appear if any configured presigned TTL exceeds the permissions cache TTL (default PT5M); the effective TTL is silently capped. To suppress: set `shepard.storage.presign.upload-ttl=PT5M` (or smaller) + matching `shepard.permissions.cache.ttl`. |
| **FS1g — RO-Crate export-URL endpoint (sync variant of aidocs/31 §O3)** | `aidocs/45 §9 FS1g` + `aidocs/31 §O3`. New `POST /v2/collections/{appId}/export-url` endpoint: builds the collection's RO-Crate ZIP synchronously (same `ExportService.exportCollectionByShepardId()` call as the existing `GET /collections/{id}/export`), uploads the resulting bytes to S3 under `exports/<uuid>.zip` (new `FileStorage.presignedExportUrl()` SPI default method; `S3FileStorage` implements it; `GridFsFileStorage` inherits the default empty return), and returns a presigned GET URL valid for 30 minutes as `{downloadUrl, fileName, expiresAt}`. The JVM is no longer in the download path — clients receive the ZIP directly from S3. **Actual win**: offloads download bandwidth from the JVM (not in-memory cost — `ExportBuilder` already buffers the ZIP in a `ByteArrayOutputStream`). Returns 503 when the active storage provider does not support presigned export (GridFS); callers should fall back to `GET /collections/{id}/export`. Optional `ExportSelection` body supported (mirrors existing `POST /collections/{id}/export`). New SPI method `FileStorage.presignedExportUrl(String key, byte[] zipBytes, String fileName, Duration ttl) → Optional<URI>` (default returns empty). New `S3FileStorage.presignedExportUrl()`: uploads to `exports/<key>` in the same bucket + returns presigned GET. New classes: `CollectionExportUrlRest` + `ExportUrlIO` under `de.dlr.shepard.v2.collection.*`. Pre-existing compile fixes: missing `import org.eclipse.microprofile.config.ConfigProvider` in `N10sBootstrapHook.java` (Java 21 incompatibility); illegal multi-catch `NoSuchBucketException | S3Exception` in `S3FileStorage.ensureBucketExists()` (subtype relationship — simplified to `S3Exception`). S3FileStorageTest: 3/3 (including new disabled-adapter guard). CollectionExportUrlRestTest: 8 cases covering 401/404/403/503-no-provider/503-gridfs/200/500-export-fail/500-storage-fail (parallel build env issue prevented local run; verified by code inspection against identical pattern in `CollectionPropertiesRestTest`). | **AWARE** | **One new `/v2/` endpoint**: `POST /v2/collections/{appId}/export-url`. **No new config keys.** **No schema migration.** **Export objects accumulate in S3** under the `exports/` prefix until cleaned up by a bucket lifecycle rule — see `docs/reference/file-storage.md §Export URL lifecycle`. **`FileStorage` SPI grows one new default method** (`presignedExportUrl`) — plugins that implement the SPI (if any) compile without change (the default returns `Optional.empty()`; non-S3 adapters get 503 transparently). The O3 async variant (ZIP built off-request; 202 + jobId + downloadUrl response) is deferred until O2 (async export jobs) lands. | No new migrations. | **No action required for upgrade** for GridFS deployments — the endpoint returns 503 and clients can fall back to `GET /collections/{id}/export`. **For S3 deployments**: the endpoint works automatically. **Recommended**: configure a bucket lifecycle rule to expire objects under the `exports/` prefix after 24 hours so stale ZIPs are cleaned up automatically (see `docs/reference/file-storage.md §Export URL lifecycle`). No CORS changes required for this endpoint — the presigned download URL is returned as JSON; the browser hits S3 directly via the provided URL, not via a cross-origin fetch. |
| **EXPORT-V2-STREAM — `GET /v2/collections/{appId}/export` streaming sibling** | `aidocs/16` EXPORT-V2-STREAM.
