New `GET /v2/collections/{appId}/export` endpoint (`CollectionStreamExportRest`): resolves appId → numeric id via `CollectionPropertiesDAO.findCollectionIdByAppId()`, checks Read permission, calls `ExportService.exportCollectionByShepardId()`, and streams the RO-Crate ZIP as `application/octet-stream` with `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="<sanitised-appId>-export.zip"`. Works on **all storage backends** including GridFS, unlike `POST /v2/collections/{appId}/export-url` (FS1g) which returns 503 on GridFS. Frontend `downloadRoCrate()` migrated from v1 `CollectionApi.exportCollection({collectionId: numericId})` to this new v2 endpoint via fetch (Bearer-token pattern). Removes `CollectionApi` + `useShepardApi` imports from the collection index page. 5 unit tests (`CollectionStreamExportRestTest`). | **ZERO** | **One new `/v2/` endpoint**: `GET /v2/collections/{appId}/export`. Additive; no new config keys, no schema migration, no change to any `/shepard/api/...` path. The frozen v1 `GET /collections/{collectionId}/export` is untouched. | No new migrations. | **No action required for upgrade.** The endpoint appears in the OpenAPI spec at `GET /v2/collections/{appId}/export`. On GridFS installs use it as the standard export path (the presigned-URL endpoint returns 503). On S3 installs, `POST /v2/collections/{appId}/export-url` (FS1g) remains preferred (JVM not in download path). |
| **TPL14 — Regulatory Evidence Pack (REP) export** | `aidocs/16` TPL14. New `/v2/` endpoints: `POST /v2/collections/{appId}/export/regulatory-evidence` (builds synchronously, returns inline Base64 bag ≤ 1 MB) and `GET /v2/collections/{appId}/export/regulatory-evidence/latest` (stub returning 404 until TPL14b persistence ships). BagIt 1.0 (RFC 8493) ZIP containing `data/ro-crate-metadata.json` (RO-Crate 1.1: root Dataset + one Dataset per DataObject) + `data/PROV-O.jsonld` (PROV-O JSON-LD via existing `ProvJsonLdRenderer.renderProvO()`) + `bagit.txt`, `bag-info.txt`, `manifest-sha256.txt`, `tagmanifest-sha256.txt`. Activities fetched via `ActivityDAO.list(null, "Collection", collectionAppId, null, null, 1000)`. Response: `{exportId, status, bagBase64, fileName, exportedAt, dataObjectCount, bagSizeBytes}`. UI: "Regulatory Evidence Pack" button on Collection detail page (next to "Download as RO-Crate"). 8 unit tests (`RepExportServiceTest`). New package `de.dlr.shepard.v2.export.rep`. | **ZERO** | **Two new `/v2/` endpoints** — additive, behind Read permission check. No new config keys. No schema migration. No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path. Large-bag storage path (> 1 MB bags) throws 500 until TPL14b lands. | — (no migration — purely additive) | **No action required for upgrade.** The endpoints appear in the OpenAPI spec at `POST/GET /v2/collections/{appId}/export/regulatory-evidence[/latest]`. To build a REP bag: `POST /v2/collections/<appId>/export/regulatory-evidence` → decode `bagBase64` from the response (Base64 standard encoding) → save as `<appId>-rep.bag.zip`. Validate with `bagit validate <dir>` (Library of Congress BagIt Java library or Python `bagit-python`). |
| **TPL17 — Distributed ledger anchoring for tamper evidence (Bloxberg / OpenTimestamps)** | 2026-05-26. Design doc (`aidocs/integrations/111-tpl17-distributed-ledger-anchoring.md`) + thin REST skeleton returning 501. New admin endpoints under `/v2/admin/ledger/`: `POST /anchor` (submit anchor batch, 501 until TPL17a), `GET /anchor/{jobId}` (poll job, 501), `GET /data-objects/{appId}/ledger-anchors` (list anchors for a DataObject, 501). New `Activity.ledgerAnchor` nullable `String` property (stores JSON blob: `{provider, digest, txHash, anchoredAt, receipt}`). Bean gated by `@LookupIfProperty(name="shepard.ledger.enabled", stringValue="true", lookupIfMissing=false)` — CDI never instantiates it unless the toggle is on. Phase 1 scope: design + compile-safe skeleton only. Phase 2 (TPL17a) wires Bloxberg HTTP client. Phase 3 (TPL17b) adds OpenTimestamps. | **ZERO** | **Three new `/v2/` admin endpoints** — additive, all return 501 until TPL17a lands. **All gated on `shepard.ledger.enabled=true`** (default `false`) — when toggle is off, JAX-RS returns 404 for all ledger paths. **New Neo4j property** `Activity.ledgerAnchor` — additive, nullable, schema-free; existing Activity nodes continue to work unchanged. **New config keys** (`shepard.ledger.enabled`, `shepard.ledger.default-provider`, `shepard.ledger.bloxberg.api-key`, `shepard.ledger.bloxberg.wallet-address`) — all optional, all default-safe. **No new node labels.** **No new relationships.** **No upstream `/shepard/api/...` surface touched.** | None (additive Neo4j property — no constraint migration needed; Neo4j-OGM adds the property lazily on next write once TPL17a populates it). | **No action required for upgrade.** Endpoints are 404 until `shepard.ledger.enabled=true` is set AND the backend is rebuilt with TPL17a. Until TPL17a ships: the new endpoints advertise 501 when the feature IS enabled (confirming the JAX-RS path exists). To verify the skeleton compiled correctly: check the OpenAPI spec at `/q/openapi` for paths under `/v2/admin/ledger` after setting `shepard.ledger.enabled=true` and restarting. |
| **ST1a — lifecycle `status` field on `AbstractDataObject` / `Collection`** | `aidocs/44` ST1a. New nullable `status: String` property added to the `AbstractDataObject` Neo4j entity (shared by both `Collection` and `DataObject`). `AbstractDataObjectIO` serialises it with `@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)` — the field is omitted entirely from the JSON payload when `null`, so upstream API clients that don't know about it see no wire change. Suggested values DRAFT / IN_REVIEW / READY / PUBLISHED / ARCHIVED; any free-form string is accepted (no server-side validation). The field is editable via the existing upstream `PUT /shepard/api/collections/{id}` and `PUT /shepard/api/dataObjects/{id}` endpoints (both pass through `CollectionIO` / `DataObjectIO`, which extend `AbstractDataObjectIO`). Frontend: a colored `StatusChip` is shown next to the entity name; the description edit dialog exposes a clearable `v-select` for the suggested values. | **ZERO** | `/shepard/api/...` wire shape unchanged for null-status entities (omitted via `@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)`). Clients that send the `status` field in PUT bodies have it persisted; clients that don't are unaffected. No new endpoints; no config keys. | — (no migration needed — `status` is a nullable Neo4j property; Neo4j-OGM adds it lazily on next write) | **None.** Existing data is unaffected; the field is absent until explicitly set. |
| **PM1f — `sidecars()` declaration on `PluginManifest` SPI + file-s3 Garage spec** | `aidocs/platform/47 §2.6` + `aidocs/integrations/93 §9` + memory `feedback_plugins_declare_sidecars.md`. Adds a `default List<SidecarSpec> sidecars()` method to the `PluginManifest` SPI (non-breaking; existing plugins return empty by default). New companion records under `de.dlr.shepard.plugin`: `SidecarSpec` (id, image, ports, volumes, env, healthcheck, postInit, backendEnvBinding), `PortSpec` (port + role label), `VolumeSpec` (name + mountpoint), `HealthcheckSpec` (cmd / interval / timeout / retries). Templating placeholders (`{{generate:hex:N}}`, `{{sidecar.host}}`, `{{from:postInit.N.field}}`) pass through verbatim — the operator-side bootstrap holds resolution responsibility. New `de.dlr.shepard.plugin.SidecarsAssembler` walks plugin-declared specs and renders a deterministic `services:` compose-v3 snippet an operator pastes into their `docker-compose.override.yml`. `FileS3PluginManifest` overrides `sidecars()` to declare its Garage backend (`dxflrs/garage:v1.0.1`, ports 3900/3902, `garage_data` volume, `curl /health` healthcheck, the 5-step layout/bucket/key/allow post-init, and the 6 `SHEPARD_FILES_S3_*` backend env bindings). 17 unit tests in `SidecarSpecTest` (record validation: blank ids, out-of-range ports, non-absolute mountpoints, non-positive durations, defensive copies, templating pass-through), 14 in `SidecarsAssemblerTest` (deterministic render, byte-stable env ordering, top-level volumes block, healthcheck → compose-v3 mapping, multi-spec ordering), 9 in `FileS3PluginManifestSidecarsTest` (pins the Garage spec fields), 1 added to `PluginManifestTest` (default returns empty). | **AWARE** | **No new endpoints.** **No new config keys at the backend.** **No schema migration.** SPI extension only — `PluginManifest.sidecars()` is a default method returning `List.of()` so every existing plugin compiles unchanged. Operator visibility lands when they invoke the `SidecarsAssembler` (e.g. via the v15 MFFD import script's baked-in pre-flight per `aidocs/integrations/93 §9`) — the rendered compose snippet documents the Garage sidecar shape pinned to the plugin version. Operators who don't run the bootstrap see zero behaviour change. The `infrastructure/docker-compose.yml` `files-s3` profile from FS1d remains the alternative path (manual operator setup); PM1f gives plugin authors a portable declaration that future operator tooling can render. | No new migrations. | **No action required for upgrade.** PM1f is an SPI extension — the backend ships the new types but does nothing with sidecar declarations on its own. To consume the Garage declaration: read `FileS3PluginManifest.sidecars()` (e.g. via the upcoming bootstrap script or the v15 MFFD import pre-flight) and feed it to `new SidecarsAssembler().assembleComposeSnippet(specs)`. Paste the output into `docker-compose.override.yml`, replace the templating placeholders (`{{generate:hex:64}}` → 64 random hex chars; `{{sidecar.host}}` → the compose service name `shepard-garage`; `{{from:postInit.3.access_key_id}}` and `{{from:postInit.3.secret_access_key}}` → values captured from the `/garage key new` output), run the 5 post-init commands once the container is healthy, then inject the `SHEPARD_FILES_S3_*` env into the backend service definition and restart. The runbook is also the v15 MFFD-import pre-flight runbook (per `aidocs/integrations/93 §9`); future plugins that declare sidecars get the same one-paste deploy guidance pinned to their exact version. Plugin authors adding new sidecars: override `sidecars()` returning a `List<SidecarSpec>`; the SPI's record-shape validators catch misshapen specs at construction time. |
| **FS1f — frontend presigned upload (automatic presigned PUT when S3 active; graceful fallback to legacy GridFS path)** | `aidocs/45 §9 FS1f`. Frontend composable `FileContainerAccessor.uploadFile()` now attempts the three-step presigned upload when the container's `appId` is set and the backend supports presigned URLs (S3 active): (1) `POST /v2/file-containers/{appId}/upload-url {fileName}` → `{uploadUrl, oid}`; (2) `PUT <uploadUrl>` with raw file bytes directly to S3 (no auth header — signature embedded in URL); (3) `POST /v2/file-containers/{appId}/upload-url/commit {oid, fileName, contentType, fileSize}` → 201 + `ShepardFile`. If step 1 returns 503 (GridFS active, backend returns "provider does not support presigned URLs"), the composable silently falls through to the legacy `POST /shepard/api/fileContainers/{id}/payload` path — zero user-visible change for GridFS deployments. If step 1 returns any non-503 error, or step 2/3 fail, the error is propagated (no silent swallow). `FileContainer` TypeScript model (`backend-client/src/models/FileContainer.ts`) grows `appId?: string | null` — mapped from the existing `BasicEntityIO.appId` JSON field which was already returned by the backend but not declared in the generated type. `backend-client` rebuilt (`npm run build`) to propagate the new field to `dist/`. No backend changes; no new config keys; no schema migration. | **AWARE** | **No new `/v2/` endpoints** — FS1f uses the three FS1c presigned-URL endpoints already shipped. **No new config keys.** **No schema migration.** **`FileContainer` JSON response grows `appId` field** (already present in the response since the `BasicEntityIO` base class serialised it; now declared in the TypeScript model). The `uploadFile()` composable signature is unchanged — callers see no API difference. **GridFS operators**: the `createFile()` path is called exactly as before; the presigned path is tried and silently abandoned after the 503 from the backend. **S3 operators**: uploads now flow directly browser → S3 without routing bytes through the backend, reducing backend memory pressure on large file uploads. CORS must be configured on the S3 bucket to allow PUT from the frontend origin (e.g. Garage: `cors_allow_origins` in garage.toml; MinIO: `mc cors set`). Without CORS the presigned PUT will fail in the browser and the fallback will re-try via the legacy path (which will itself return 503 from the backend since direct upload is not supported for S3 — the user will see an upload error). | No new migrations. | **No action required for upgrade** for GridFS deployments — the legacy upload path is unchanged. **For S3 deployments**: configure CORS on your S3 bucket to allow `PUT` from the frontend origin. For Garage: add to `garage.toml` a `[s3_api.cors_allow_origins]` entry pointing at your frontend URL. For MinIO: `mc anonymous cors set <alias>/<bucket> --method PUT --origin <frontend-origin>`. Without CORS, the browser console will show a CORS preflight error on upload; the upload will fail and the user will see a generic upload-error toast (no data loss — the S3 PUT never completed, so no partial state is left). The presigned path is tried automatically on every upload if the container has an `appId` — older containers created before L2a (which didn't get `appId` stamped on create) will silently use the legacy path until they are updated with `appId`. |
| **FS1e3 — per-file rollback fields on `:ShepardFile` + `POST /v2/admin/files/migrate/rollback/{appId}`** | `aidocs/data/46-gridfs-to-s3-migration-runbook.md §17.B` (option C: "Half-measure — add `previousProviderId` + `previousLocator` + audit fields now, defer the cleanup-gating verb"). Adds four nullable bookkeeping fields on `:ShepardFile` — `previousProviderId`, `previousLocator`, `migratedAt`, `migrationHmac` (null today; future-proof for FS1e6 verify+report). Every field carries `@JsonIgnore` + `@BsonIgnore` + `@Schema(hidden=true)` to keep `/shepard/api/...` wire shape byte-identical to upstream (matches the existing `providerId` posture). `FileMigrationService.migrateOne()` refactored: the previous single-statement `SET f.providerId = $target` becomes a single SET that stamps `previousProviderId = f.providerId, previousLocator = $sourceLocator, migratedAt = datetime(), migrationHmac = $hmac, providerId = $target` — Cypher's left-to-right SET evaluation preserves the "stamp before swap" invariant inside one transaction. The fused Phase 4 `source.delete()` stays per task-spec ("no behaviour split yet — just record-keeping"). New `FileMigrationService.rollbackOne(appId)` reads bytes from the current adapter, writes back to the previous adapter using the preserved oid as `assignedObjectKey`, then clears the four bookkeeping fields and restores `providerId` via single MATCH+SET+REMOVE Cypher. Refusal semantics: `IllegalArgumentException` (→ 404) on unknown appId; `IllegalStateException` (→ 409 Conflict) when `previousProviderId` is null. New REST surface `POST /v2/admin/files/migrate/rollback/{appId}` (`@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`, synchronous — returns `{appId, status: ROLLED_BACK}` on success). 13 new unit tests: 6 ShepardFile (round-trip on the four new fields + the v5 wire-shape regression `v5WireShapeOmitsInternalBookkeeping`); 6 FileMigrationService (stamp-Cypher shape + rollback-Cypher refusal + migrate/rollback round-trip on TrackingAdapter stubs); 6 FileMigrationRest (input validation + 404/409/500 mapping). | **AWARE** | **One new `/v2/admin/` endpoint** — `POST /v2/admin/files/migrate/rollback/{appId}` (`instance-admin` only). **No `/shepard/api/...` surface change** — the four new `:ShepardFile` fields are wire-hidden. **`FileMigrationService.migrateOne()` Cypher signature change**: the in-memory CYPHER_UPDATE template now takes four params instead of two (`oid`, `target`, `sourceLocator`, `hmac`). External callers do not invoke this directly; the service is `@ApplicationScoped`. **No new config keys.** **No new CLI verbs in this PR** — `shepard-admin files migrate rollback` (CLI parity) is FS1e3 follow-up. | `V59__NOOP_ShepardFile_migration_rollback_fields.cypher` — intentionally empty (Neo4j is schema-less; additive nullable properties need no DDL). Idempotent + fail-fast (an empty migration cannot fail; re-running is a no-op). Operator runbook pointer is the `docs/reference/file-storage.md §Per-file rollback (FS1e3)` block. | **No action required for upgrade.** Pre-FS1e3 `:ShepardFile` rows read back null on all four new fields (Neo4j returns `null` for absent properties). The new REST endpoint is purely additive; existing migration calls (`POST /v2/admin/files/migrate`, `GET /v2/admin/files/migrate/status`) are unchanged. **To use per-file rollback**: after a migration sweep, an operator who needs to revert a single mis-migrated file calls `curl -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $SHEPARD_API_KEY" https://<host>/v2/admin/files/migrate/rollback/<appId>`. The rollback re-writes bytes from the current adapter back to the previous adapter using the preserved `previousLocator`. **Caveat — rollback to GridFS**: `GridFsFileStorage.put()` does not currently honour `assignedObjectKey`; rollback bytes land at a new GridFS oid, so the round-trip is not byte-locator identical (the Neo4j `oid` + `providerId` stay consistent, just the GridFS key changes). A future PR extends GridFS to honour `assignedObjectKey`. For the nuclide.systems Q6 run (gridfs → S3), this caveat does not apply — S3 honours `assignedObjectKey`. **Note**: the FS1e3 verify/commit/sweep-orphans verbs from runbook §17.B options A/E are deferred to FS1e3c/d/e — this PR only ships the rollback fields + the rollback endpoint, per the task-spec scope. **Testcontainer-level integration regression** (boot real Neo4j + two adapters, migrate-then-rollback a file, verify the four fields stamp/clear correctly) is **deferred** alongside the broader QuarkusTest scaffolding tracked under PM1a + KIP1a + FS1e1. |
| **FS1e2 — background continuous-sweep storage migration** | `aidocs/45 §6` + `aidocs/16 FS1e2`. Adds `@Scheduled autoSweep()` to `FileMigrationService`. Gated by `shepard.migration.auto-sweep.enabled=false` (default off — zero overhead on upgrade). New config keys `shepard.migration.auto-sweep.{enabled,source,target,interval}` added to `application.properties` with safe defaults. When enabled, fires every `interval` (default PT5M) and calls `triggerMigration(source, target)`, skipping ticks where a migration is already RUNNING. No new REST endpoints; no new CLI commands; no schema migration. P3 SSE progress deferred — poll `GET /v2/admin/files/migrate/status`. 5 unit tests in `FileMigrationAutoSweepTest`. | **AWARE** | **Four new `application.properties` config keys** (`shepard.migration.auto-sweep.{enabled,source,target,interval}`). All default to disabled / empty / PT5M — a plain upgrade is zero-config and zero-overhead. **No new endpoints.** **No new CLI verbs.** **No schema migration.** **No `/shepard/api/...` surface change.** | No new migrations. | **No action required for upgrade.** The auto-sweep defaults to disabled; deployments that don't need storage migration see no behaviour change. **To enable continuous sweep** (e.g. to drain GridFS to S3 incrementally without a big-bang window): set `shepard.migration.auto-sweep.enabled=true`, `shepard.migration.auto-sweep.source=gridfs`, `shepard.migration.auto-sweep.target=s3` in `application.properties` and restart. The sweep fires every PT5M (override via `shepard.migration.auto-sweep.interval`). Poll progress at `GET /v2/admin/files/migrate/status`. Once `filesFailed=0` and `filesTotal=filesMigrated`, disable auto-sweep and complete the FS1a cutover. The big-bang `POST /v2/admin/files/migrate` endpoint from FS1e1 remains available for on-demand sweeps. |
| **FS1e1 — big-bang file-storage migration + `GET /v2/admin/storage`** | `aidocs/45 §6` (FS1e1 scope). Adds an admin-gated async migration sweep (`POST /v2/admin/files/migrate`) that copies every `:ShepardFile` node whose `providerId = source` to the target adapter, one file at a time (streaming — no OOM on large deployments), then flips `f.providerId = target` and deletes from source. OID preservation: `StoragePutRequest` grows a nullable `assignedObjectKey` field; `S3FileStorage.put()` uses it when set so the S3 key fragment stays `containerMongoId/<original-oid>` — only `providerId` changes in Neo4j, existing API clients keep working. Per-file errors are counted and logged (migration continues to the next file). Job state is in-memory (`AtomicReference<FileMigrationState>`; IDLE / RUNNING / DONE / FAILED); restarts reset to IDLE but re-running migration is idempotent (already-migrated files are excluded by the Cypher query). `GET /v2/admin/files/migrate/status` polls the state. Both endpoints are `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`, under `/v2/`. New `GET /v2/admin/storage` replaces the FS1a readiness-proxy placeholder: returns all discovered adapters with `id`, `enabled`, `active` flags — the proper operator view the FS1a `StorageStatusCommand` couldn't provide. CLI: `shepard-admin files migrate <source> <target>` (triggers; returns 202 + initial state) + `shepard-admin files migrate-status` (polls; exit 0=IDLE/DONE, 1=RUNNING, 2=FAILED); `shepard-admin storage status` updated to read `/v2/admin/storage` instead of the healthz proxy. New classes: `FileMigrationStatus` (enum), `FileMigrationState` (record), `FileMigrationService` (`@ApplicationScoped`, single-thread executor), `StorageAdminRest`, `FileMigrationRest`, IO types under `de.dlr.shepard.v2.admin.storage.io`, CLI IO + command classes. 13 new unit tests in `FileMigrationServiceTest` covering initial IDLE state, validation (blank/same source-target/unknown/disabled adapter), RUNNING transition, state record factory + transitions. `StorageStatusCommandTest` updated (4 cases; tests new JSON shape). `ShepardAdminTest` extended: `files` subcommand + `migrate` + `migrate-status` wiring assertions (2 new tests). | **AWARE** | **Two new `/v2/admin/` endpoints** — `POST /v2/admin/files/migrate` + `GET /v2/admin/files/migrate/status` — both gated behind `instance-admin`. **One new `/v2/admin/storage` endpoint** (replaces the FS1a `shepard-admin storage status` healthz-proxy placeholder). **`StoragePutRequest` grows a new `assignedObjectKey` field** — a nullable compile-time addition; all existing callers that use the 3-arg convenience constructor are unaffected. **`S3FileStorage.put()` behaviour change when `assignedObjectKey` is set**: uses the provided key fragment instead of a fresh UUID. Normal (non-migration) uploads leave `assignedObjectKey` null so S3 key generation is unchanged. **`shepard-admin storage status` output changes**: reads `/v2/admin/storage` instead of `/shepard/api/healthz/ready`; now requires an admin API key (the old command was unauthenticated against the healthz endpoint). **New CLI subcommand group** `shepard-admin files {migrate,migrate-status}`. **No schema migration** — no new Neo4j properties; `f.providerId` already existed (V34). **No new config keys.** **No `/shepard/api/...` surface change.** | No new migrations. | **No action required for upgrade** unless the operator wants to migrate storage. The new endpoints and CLI verbs are purely additive; existing `shepard-admin storage status` calls now require an admin API key (use `--api-key <key>` or `SHEPARD_API_KEY=<key>` env). **To migrate from GridFS to S3** (after configuring S3 per the FS1b runbook): (1) Ensure both adapters are enabled: `shepard-admin storage status` should list both `gridfs: enabled` and `s3: enabled`. (2) Trigger: `shepard-admin files migrate gridfs s3` — returns immediately. (3) Poll: `shepard-admin files migrate-status` (or `watch -n5 shepard-admin files migrate-status`) until `status: DONE`. (4) If `filesFailed > 0`, re-run `shepard-admin files migrate gridfs s3` to sweep the residual. (5) Once done, set `shepard.storage.provider=s3` in `application.properties` and restart. Re-running migration after partial failure is safe — already-migrated files have `providerId=s3` and are excluded by the Cypher query. **Exit codes for scripting**: `migrate-status` returns 0 (IDLE or DONE), 1 (RUNNING), 2 (FAILED) — usable in `until shepard-admin files migrate-status; do sleep 10; done` loops. **Testcontainer-level integration regression** (boot real Neo4j + both adapters, trigger migration, verify all `:ShepardFile.providerId` flipped) is **deferred** alongside the broader QuarkusTest scaffolding. |
| **FS1c — presigned-URL endpoints + FS1b locator fixup** | `aidocs/45 §4 + §7 + §9 FS1c`. **FS1b locator fixup**: S3 locator format corrected from `<bucket>/<containerMongoId>/<uuid>` to `<containerMongoId>/<uuid>` — bucket is read from `shepard.files.s3.bucket` at runtime and not stored in the locator (bucket is a deploy-time topology knob; storing it in each row's opaque locator would create a stale-data landmine if the bucket is renamed). `splitLocator()` static method and `LocatorParts` record removed from `S3FileStorage`; `get()` / `delete()` now use `this.bucket` directly with `locator.locator()` as the key. `FileContainerService.buildLocatorForRow()` added: routes GridFS rows via the legacy `containerMongoId:oid` format, all other adapters via `containerMongoId/oid`. Non-GridFS branch of `createFile()` now returns 503 "Direct upload is not supported — use presigned upload URL endpoint" instead of silently breaking. **FS1c — presigned-URL endpoints**: `FileStorage` SPI gains two default methods: `presignedUploadUrl(String containerMongoId, String fileName, Duration ttl) → Optional<PresignedPut>` and `presignedDownloadUrl(StorageLocator, String, Duration) → Optional<URI>` (default returns `Optional.empty()` so non-S3 adapters need no change); inner `record PresignedPut(URI uploadUrl, String assignedOid, Instant expiresAt)` added. `S3FileStorage` implements both methods via `S3Presigner` (AWS SDK v2 presigner; no new dependency — already in the `s3` artifact). `S3Presigner` initialised alongside `S3Client` in `@PostConstruct` (same region / endpoint / credentials / path-style config). Three new REST endpoints under `de.dlr.shepard.v2.filecontainer.resources.FileContainerPresignedUrlRest` at `/v2/file-containers/`: `POST /{containerAppId}/upload-url` (returns `PresignedUploadUrlIO{uploadUrl,oid,expiresAt}`, 15-min TTL); `POST /{containerAppId}/upload-url/commit` (registers `ShepardFile` in Neo4j after S3 PUT completes, returns 201 with the entity); `GET /{containerAppId}/files/{oid}/download-url` (returns `PresignedDownloadUrlIO{downloadUrl,expiresAt}`, 5-min TTL). `FileContainerDAO.findByAppId(String)` added (Cypher `MATCH (c:FileContainer {appId: $appId})`). New `FileContainerService` methods: `getContainerByAppId()`, `presignedUploadUrl()`, `commitUpload()`, `presignedDownloadUrl()`. New IO types under `de.dlr.shepard.v2.filecontainer.io`: `PresignedUploadRequestIO`, `PresignedUploadUrlIO`, `UploadCommitIO`, `PresignedDownloadUrlIO`. 2 structural unit tests remain in `S3FileStorageTest` (5 splitLocator tests removed; integration tests against live Garage remain deferred per FS1b). | **AWARE** | **Three new `/v2/` endpoints** — all additive, no impact on the `/shepard/api/...` upstream compat surface. The upload/download presigned URLs bypass the backend byte proxy; no backend request is needed between `upload-url` and `commit`. **Locator format change** (FS1b fixup): `<bucket>/<containerMongoId>/<uuid>` → `<containerMongoId>/<uuid>` — no migration needed because FS1b and FS1c are committed as a bundle before any S3 production rows exist. Operators who wrote rows with the old locator format (would require having deployed FS1b without FS1c, which is not a supported state) would need to strip the `<bucket>/` prefix from their `:ShepardFile`-adjacent locator storage; the registry routes via `providerId`, not the locator format. **No new config keys.** **No schema migration** — the `ShepardFile` entities created by `commitUpload` use the same Neo4j properties as GridFS rows. | No new migrations. | **No action required for upgrade.** The new `/v2/file-containers/...` endpoints are additive; they appear in the OpenAPI spec alongside existing endpoints. To upload a file via presigned URL (S3 adapter active): `curl -X POST /v2/file-containers/{appId}/upload-url -d '{"fileName":"myfile.csv"}' → {uploadUrl, oid, expiresAt}`; then `curl -X PUT <uploadUrl> --data-binary @myfile.csv`; then `curl -X POST /v2/file-containers/{appId}/upload-url/commit -d '{"oid":"<oid>","fileName":"myfile.csv"}' → 201 ShepardFile`. To get a download URL: `curl /v2/file-containers/{appId}/files/{oid}/download-url → {downloadUrl, expiresAt}`; then `curl -o myfile.csv <downloadUrl>`. GridFS operators: the new endpoints return 503 "provider does not support presigned URLs" — use the existing `/shepard/api/fileContainers/{id}/payload` endpoints instead. See `docs/reference/file-storage.md §Presigned URLs (S3)` for the full flow. |
| **FS1b + FS1d — `shepard-plugin-file-s3` + Garage sidecar** | `aidocs/45 §9 FS1b + FS1d`. Implements the FS1a `FileStorage` SPI with an S3-compatible adapter using AWS SDK v2. **FS1b** — new `plugins/file-s3/` Maven module producing `shepard-plugin-file-s3-${revision}.jar`: `S3FileStorage` (id=`s3`) stores blobs at locator `<containerOid>/<uuid>` (bucket read from `shepard.files.s3.bucket` config, not stored in locator — corrected to this format by FS1c fixup in the same session); path-style URLs on by default for Garage/MinIO/LocalStack compatibility; optional endpoint-override for any non-AWS S3 endpoint. `FileS3PluginManifest` in `GET /v2/admin/plugins`. Config keys (deploy-time-only per cluster-identity exception): `shepard.files.s3.{endpoint,region,access-key-id,secret-access-key,bucket,path-style-access}`. `@PostConstruct` creates the configured bucket if absent (idempotent). **FS1d** — `shepard-garage` service added to `infrastructure/docker-compose.yml` under the `files-s3` profile: `dxflrs/garage:v1.0.1`, port 3900, volume `./garage-data`, healthcheck on port 3903. Quick-start operator init runbook in the compose comment. **Build wiring**: backend `with-plugins` profile gains `shepard-plugin-file-s3` dep; `quarkus.index-dependency.shepard-plugin-file-s3.*` ensures Jandex indexes `S3FileStorage`; CI install + test + staging steps added; all other plugin poms gain `shepard-plugin-file-s3` exclusion. `shepard.plugins.file-s3.enabled=false` default (opt-in: operators configure S3 then flip). S3 config keys commented-out in `application.properties` as a ready-to-use template. 24 mock-based tests in `S3FileStorageTest` + 9 manifest tests in `FileS3PluginManifestTest` = 33 unit tests (integration tests against live Garage remain deferred). | **AWARE** | **No new endpoints.** The `/shepard/api/fileContainers/...` upload/download/delete surface is byte-identical — adding the S3 adapter adds a CDI bean + plugin manifest; the REST surface only changes behaviour when `shepard.storage.provider=s3` is set. **New config keys** (all deploy-time, all commented-out by default, so a plain upgrade is zero-config): `shepard.files.s3.{endpoint,region,access-key-id,secret-access-key,bucket,path-style-access}`. **New plugin toggle** `shepard.plugins.file-s3.enabled=false`. **No schema change** — the `:ShepardFile.providerId` Neo4j property (introduced by FS1a / V34) already handles the `"s3"` value; FS1b rows write `providerId="s3"` and the registry routes old `"gridfs"` rows to `GridFsFileStorage` and new `"s3"` rows to `S3FileStorage`. **FS1d (Garage compose profile)** is off by default (`--profile files-s3`); an operator who has never run this profile sees no change on `docker compose up`. | No new migrations. | **No action required for upgrade** unless the operator wants S3 storage. To enable: (1) configure `shepard.files.s3.*` (endpoint, bucket, credentials); (2) set `shepard.storage.provider=s3` in `application.properties`; (3) set `shepard.plugins.file-s3.enabled=true`; (4) restart. Existing GridFS rows continue resolving via `GridFsFileStorage` — the registry routes by `providerId` so mixed-provider histories work. To use Garage for the quick-start: `docker compose --profile files-s3 up -d`, then run the three-command cluster-init + bucket-create sequence from the compose comment and set the printed access/secret keys in `application.properties`. See `docs/reference/file-storage.md` for the full operator runbook. |

| **FS1a — `FileStorage` SPI seam (structural prep for S3-compatible backends)** | `aidocs/16` FS1a + `aidocs/data/45-gridfs-to-s3-evaluation.md §3.2` + CLAUDE.md plugin-first heuristic #3 ("SPIs in core, adapters as plugins"). Extracts the storage-backend abstraction for the file-payload kind into an in-tree SPI under `de.dlr.shepard.storage`: `FileStorage` interface (`id()` / `isEnabled()` / `put()` / `get()` / `delete()`); `StorageLocator` / `StoragePutRequest` / `StorageGetResponse` records; `StorageException` family (`StorageNotFoundException`, `StorageProviderUnavailableException`, `StorageQuotaExceededException`) + `StorageNotInstalledException` (RuntimeException, runs through the dedicated `StorageNotInstalledExceptionMapper` for RFC 7807 503 surfacing). `FileStorageRegistry` mirrors `MinterRegistry`'s post-KIP1h optional posture — `Optional<FileStorage> activeStorage()`, `List<FileStorage> list()`, `FileStorage requireActive()`; missing / disabled / unmatched providers degrade to "no active storage" + a WARN instead of fail-fast. `GridFsFileStorage` (in core, package `de.dlr.shepard.storage.gridfs`) is the default — wraps the legacy `FileService` GridFS path, locator format `"<containerMongoId>:<fileOid>"`, idempotent delete (swallows the "file missing" not-found per the SPI contract; still propagates "container missing"). `FileContainerService` routes `createFile` / `getFile` / `deleteFile` through the registry; `ShepardFile` grows an internal `providerId` Neo4j property (`@JsonIgnore` + `@BsonIgnore` — not on the wire). New deploy-time-only config key `shepard.storage.provider=gridfs` (cluster-identity exception per CLAUDE.md — switching the storage backend is a re-bootstrap decision; runtime flips would orphan in-flight writes and break existing-row reads until the FS1e migration sweep completes). New `shepard-admin storage status` CLI verb (read-only; reports MongoDB-side health from `/healthz/ready` as the proxy for "GridFS up?"; the active-provider listing waits on FS1d's `GET /v2/admin/storage`). 31 new backend tests (`FileStorageRegistryTest` covers the optional-posture branches + duplicate / blank / multi-adapter cases; `GridFsFileStorageTest` covers put / get / delete + idempotent-delete + locator-validation; `StorageNotInstalledExceptionMapperTest` pins the RFC 7807 envelope; `FileContainerServiceTest` extended with provider-id stamping + not-installed-routing + adapter-routing). 4 new CLI tests (`StorageStatusCommandTest`) + 2 new `ShepardAdminTest` wiring assertions. SpotBugs clean; coverage gate met. **The S3 adapter is FS1b — drops into `plugins/storage-s3/` as a sibling plugin module without touching `FileContainerService`.** | **AWARE** | **One new deploy-time-only config key** (`shepard.storage.provider=gridfs`; default keeps upstream-compatible behaviour). **No new endpoints** — file-payload REST surface (`POST /shepard/api/fileContainers/{id}/payload` upload, `GET /…/payload/{oid}` download, `DELETE /…/payload/{oid}`) keeps the exact same path + same JSON shape + same RFC 7807 problem responses for non-storage-tier errors. Only storage-tier failures get the new clean envelopes: `storage.provider.not-installed` (503) when no adapter is active, `storage.payload.not-found` (404) when a get hits a missing key, `storage.provider.unavailable` (503) when the provider is reachable but unhealthy. **One new Neo4j property** (`:ShepardFile.providerId`) — backfilled by V34. **One behaviour change**: `DELETE /…/payload/{oid}` is now idempotent — a second DELETE on the same oid returns the same success (was: 404). Wire shape of the success response is unchanged. **One new CLI subcommand group** (`shepard-admin storage` with the `status` verb). `/shepard/api/...` surface byte-identical otherwise. | `V34__Backfill_FilePayload_providerId.cypher` — idempotent `MATCH (f:ShepardFile) WHERE f.providerId IS NULL SET f.providerId = 'gridfs'`. Stamps the FS1a default onto every pre-FS1a `:ShepardFile` row so the registry routes legacy reads correctly. Operator-runnable rollback at `V34_R__Rollback_Backfill_FilePayload_providerId.cypher` for the rare downgrade-to-pre-FS1a case. Fail-fast + idempotent. **Migration regression test deferred** — testcontainer fixture for V34 will land alongside the broader Neo4j migration testcontainer (same posture as V31 / V32 from KIP1h / PM1e). | **No action required for upgrade.** Default `shepard.storage.provider=gridfs` matches the in-core `GridFsFileStorage` adapter, which wraps the legacy `FileService` path. Restart shepard; V34 runs on first start (single `SET`, single-digit-seconds on a 1M-row file deployment); the `FileStorageRegistry` logs `discovered 1 storage adapter(s): [gridfs]` + `active storage 'gridfs'`. To verify post-upgrade: `curl https://shepard.example.dlr.de/shepard/api/fileContainers/<id>/payload/<oid>` returns the same bytes it did pre-FS1a (the upstream wire shape is preserved). To inspect status: `shepard-admin storage status` shows `gridfs connection: UP`. To opt out of file payloads entirely (resolver-only-on-rows posture, rare): set `shepard.storage.provider=none` in `application.properties` and restart; uploads / downloads / deletes return 503 `storage.provider.not-installed`. When FS1b's S3 plugin ships: drop `shepard-plugin-file-s3-<version>.jar` into `/deployments/plugins/`, set `shepard.storage.provider=s3` + the S3 bucket creds (per the plugin's own admin runbook), restart; existing GridFS-stored rows keep resolving via the GridFS adapter (the registry routes by `providerId`), new uploads land in S3. **Rollback** (rare): `cypher-shell> @V34_R__Rollback_Backfill_FilePayload_providerId.cypher` reverses V34. Downgrading the backend image is otherwise transparent — pre-FS1a backends ignore the `providerId` property. Test obligation: 35 new backend + 4 new CLI unit tests landed in this PR; testcontainer-level integration regression (boot real Quarkus + Mongo + Neo4j, upload via the upstream API, verify `:ShepardFile.providerId` is stamped, restart, re-download via the same path) is **deferred** alongside the broader QuarkusTest scaffolding tracked under PM1a + KIP1a + FS1b. |

| **CI2 — demo auto-seeder + GHCR image baseline** | `infrastructure/docker-compose.override.yml` gains a `seeder` one-shot service that runs after the backend is healthy: obtains a Keycloak ROPC token for the `admin` user, invokes `POST /v2/admin/bootstrap` with the one-shot bootstrap token (bootstrap token written to a named `bootstrap-token` Docker volume by the backend on first start via `SHEPARD_BOOTSTRAP_TOKEN_PATH`), creates an API key, installs `shepard-client`, and runs `examples/lumen-showcase/seed.py --regenerate` seeding the 25-channel LUMEN hot-fire showcase dataset. The override also exposes `SHEPARD_BOOTSTRAP_TOKEN_PATH` on the backend container and adds the shared `bootstrap-token:` named volume. `docker-compose.override.yml` sets `SHEPARD_BACKEND_IMAGE` / `SHEPARD_FRONTEND_IMAGE` defaults to `ghcr.io/noheton/shepard-{backend,frontend}:latest` — the CI-built images. Frontend changes: API Docs nav link + landing-page button pointing at `/shepard/api/q/swagger-ui` (Swagger UI; additive, read-only). | **ZERO** | New override-only env key `SHEPARD_BOOTSTRAP_TOKEN_PATH` (maps to `shepard.bootstrap.token-path`; the backend already supported this key since A0). No new permanent config keys, no schema changes, no migrations. The `seeder` service is `restart: "no"` — it runs once and exits; re-running `docker compose up` is idempotent (seed.py is idempotent). | — | **No action required for upgrade** of an existing deployment that was not using the override file. To enable the auto-seeder on a fresh deploy: use `docker-compose.override.yml` from this repo; the bootstrap token is written automatically on the first backend start. **To re-seed from scratch:** `docker compose run --rm seeder bash -c "cd /seed && python seed.py --reset --regenerate --host http://backend:8080/shepard/api --apikey <your-admin-api-key>"`. |

| **V6 — version bump to 6.0.0-SNAPSHOT + plugin compat range update** | `backend/pom.xml` `<revision>` changed from `1.0.0-SNAPSHOT` → `6.0.0-SNAPSHOT`. All four plugin pom.xml files (`kip`, `minter-local`, `minter-datacite`, `unhide`) bumped to `6.0.0-SNAPSHOT`. All four plugin manifests' `SHEPARD_COMPATIBILITY` updated from `">=5.2.0,<6"` → `">=6.0.0-SNAPSHOT,<7"` (SNAPSHOT included so dev builds pass the PM1b2 semver-range gate). CLI (`shepard-admin`) `pom.xml` bumped to `6.0.0-SNAPSHOT`. Frontend `package.json` bumped to `6.0.0`. The `/shepard/api/...` wire surface remains byte-compatible with upstream `5.2.0` per the API-version policy — this is a packaging / metadata version change only. Third-party plugins compiled against the `1.0.0-SNAPSHOT` backend SPI must update their declared `SHEPARD_COMPATIBILITY` to `">=6.0.0-SNAPSHOT,<7"` (or wider) before the `PM1b2` semver-range gate marks them FAILED on startup. | **BREAKING** (for third-party plugin manifests only) | No new config keys. No new endpoints. No schema changes. Bundled plugins (`kip`, `minter-local`, `minter-datacite`, `unhide`) ship the updated compat range in their manifests — no operator action needed for the bundled set. Third-party plugin authors who declared `>=5.2.0,<6` must release an updated manifest declaring `>=6.0.0,<7`. The `shepard.plugins.compatibility.strict=false` override (PM1b2) is the escape valve if an upstream-vended plugin hasn't refreshed yet. | — (no Cypher or SQL migration — version-metadata change only). | **For operators running only the bundled plugins (unhide, kip, minter-local, minter-datacite):** no action required. The bundled JARs ship with the updated compat range. **For operators running third-party plugins that declare `>=5.2.0,<6`:** those plugins will land FAILED on startup after this bump (PM1b2 semver-range enforcement rejects them). Either (a) wait for the plugin vendor to release a `>=6.0.0,<7` manifest, (b) set `shepard.plugins.compatibility.strict=false` as a temporary escape valve (prints a loud WARN), or (c) build + drop in an updated plugin JAR yourself. **To verify the bundled plugin compat:** `shepard-admin plugins list` should show all bundled plugins in `ENABLED` state with no `failureMessage`. |
| **TA1a — Timeseries interval/point annotations** | `aidocs/16` TA1a. New `:TimeseriesAnnotation` Neo4j node label with a `has_timeseries_annotation` relationship from `:TimeseriesReference`. Fields: `startNs` (long, required), `endNs` (Long, null = point annotation), `label` (String, required, non-blank), `description` (String, optional), `aiGenerated` (boolean, default false — ready for TA1c anomaly output), `confidence` (Double, optional). Full CRUD REST surface under `/v2/timeseries-references/{refAppId}/annotations`: `GET` list, `POST` create, `GET /{annotationAppId}`, `PATCH /{annotationAppId}` (RFC 7396 merge-patch), `DELETE /{annotationAppId}`. Auth piggybacks the parent `DataObject`'s permissions: `Read` for list/get, `Write` for create/update/delete. 15 new unit tests (`TimeseriesAnnotationRestTest`) covering all 5 endpoints × auth/validation paths. No upstream API surface touched — `/v2/` only. | **AWARE** | One new Neo4j label `:TimeseriesAnnotation` with a `has_timeseries_annotation` relationship from `:TimeseriesReference`. New `/v2/timeseries-references/{refAppId}/annotations` endpoint family — additive, `/v2/` shelf only. No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path. No new config keys. | `V35__Add_appId_constraint_TimeseriesAnnotation.cypher` — `CREATE CONSTRAINT TimeseriesAnnotation_appId_unique IF NOT EXISTS FOR (n:TimeseriesAnnotation) REQUIRE n.appId IS UNIQUE`. Additive, idempotent, fail-fast. No rollback file needed (constraint is on a new label with zero pre-existing rows). **Migration regression test deferred** — testcontainer fixture alongside the V11/V25/V34 obligations; see migration-tests table below. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V35 applies on first start (zero `:TimeseriesAnnotation` rows). Operators who never call the annotation endpoints see no change. To annotate a timeseries reference: `POST /v2/timeseries-references/{refAppId}/annotations` with `{"startNs": <epoch_ns>, "label": "<text>"}` (and optionally `"endNs"` for an interval annotation). |

| **TS-ANNOT-B — container-scoped temporal annotations** | `aidocs/16` TS-ANNOT-B. Extends the TA1a `:TimeseriesAnnotation` entity to support **container-level** temporal annotations — annotations that span the entire data space of a `TimeseriesContainer` rather than a single channel reference. New `has_temporal_annotation` Neo4j relationship from `:TimeseriesContainer` to `:TimeseriesAnnotation` (distinct from TA1a's `has_timeseries_annotation` from `:TimeseriesReference`). Three new `TimeseriesAnnotationDAO` methods: `findByContainerId(long)`, `linkToContainer(long, String)`, `unlinkAndDeleteFromContainer(long, TimeseriesAnnotation)`. New REST resource `TimeseriesContainerTemporalAnnotationRest` at `/v2/timeseries-containers/{containerId}/temporal-annotations`: `GET` list, `POST` create (mints UUID v7 `appId` upfront; 400 on null `startNs` or blank `label`), `GET /{annotationAppId}`, `PATCH /{annotationAppId}` (RFC 7396 merge-patch; `aiGenerated` immutable), `DELETE /{annotationAppId}`. Auth: `containerService.getContainer()` for Read; `containerService.assertIsAllowedToEditContainer()` for Write (same pattern as all other container endpoints). Reuses `TimeseriesAnnotation` entity and `TimeseriesAnnotationIO` IO shape unchanged. 13 unit tests (`TimeseriesContainerTemporalAnnotationRestTest`). | **ZERO** | **Five new `/v2/` paths** under `/v2/timeseries-containers/{containerId}/temporal-annotations`. No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. No new Neo4j labels (`:TimeseriesAnnotation` already seeded by TA1a/V35). No new config keys. The new `has_temporal_annotation` relationship is a forward-additive edge — existing `:TimeseriesContainer` nodes without it return an empty list from `GET`. | — (no migration needed — `has_temporal_annotation` is additive; `:TimeseriesAnnotation` constraint already exists from TA1a). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the new endpoints are immediately available. Operators who never call them see no change. To annotate a container time window: `POST /v2/timeseries-containers/{containerId}/temporal-annotations` with `{"startNs": <epoch_ns>, "label": "<text>"}` (add `"endNs"` for a range annotation, omit for a point). |

| **T1e — server-side DataObject instantiation from a ShepardTemplate** | `aidocs/16` T1e. New `POST /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects/from-template/{templateAppId}` endpoint. Creates a DataObject in the Collection using the template body's `dataobjects[0].attributes` as initial attributes. Records a `[:CREATED_FROM_TEMPLATE]` relationship with sneaker properties `_createdFromTemplateAppId`, `_createdFromTemplateVersion`, `_createdFromTemplateAppliedAt` on the DataObject node (idempotent MERGE). Returns 201 + `DataObjectIO`. Auth: Write on Collection. 409 when template retired; 403 when template not in the Collection's `[:ALLOWS_TEMPLATE]` allow-list (empty list = unrestricted). Optional request body `{"name":"..."}` overrides the auto-generated name. | **ZERO** | One new `/v2/` endpoint — additive only. No new Neo4j labels or constraints. No new config keys. No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path. The `[:CREATED_FROM_TEMPLATE]` relationship is added to the existing `(:DataObject)` and `(:ShepardTemplate)` node pair. | — (no migration needed — additive relationship on existing label pair) | **No action required for upgrade.** The endpoint is available immediately after restart. To instantiate a DataObject from a template: `POST /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects/from-template/{templateAppId}` with optional body `{"name":"..."}`. |
| **AI1b — rolling-median MAD anomaly detection** | `aidocs/16` AI1b. New `POST /v2/timeseries-references/{refAppId}/detect-anomalies` endpoint. Pure-Java rolling-median MAD detector (no external ML library): edge-padding, per-point median + MAD, 1.4826 consistency factor, MAD floor 1e-3. Returns `AnomalyDetectResultIO` (list of `AnomalyIntervalIO` with `startNs/endNs/peakValue/maxZScore`, plus `windowSize/threshold/totalPoints/annotationsCreated`). Optional `createAnnotations=true` body parameter persists a `TimeseriesAnnotation` per detected contiguous interval (label `"anomaly"`, `aiGenerated=true`, confidence = `min(1.0, maxZScore / (2 * k))`). Auth: Read permission to run detection; Write additionally required when `createAnnotations=true` (same gate as `TimeseriesAnnotationRest`). Series selection: auto-select when reference holds exactly one series; any non-null subset of the five filter fields is applied to select exactly one series when the reference holds multiple. Non-numeric / null data points silently skipped. Window defaults to 51 (forced odd, clamped to series length). 28 unit tests. | **ZERO** | One new `/v2/` endpoint — additive only. No new config keys. No Neo4j schema changes. No new migrations. `/shepard/api/...` surface unchanged. | — (no migration needed — additive endpoint; no new Neo4j labels or constraints) | **No action required for upgrade.** The new endpoint is immediately available after restart. Clients who do not call it are unaffected. To run anomaly detection on a TimeseriesReference: `POST /v2/timeseries-references/{appId}/detect-anomalies` with an empty body `{}` (uses defaults: window=51, k=6.0, no annotation creation). |
| **T1e2 — frontend template-driven creation** | `aidocs/16` T1e2. All Collection and DataObject creation dialogs now open with a **template picker as the default step**. For DataObjects, selecting a template calls the T1e server-side instantiation endpoint (`POST /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects/from-template/{templateAppId}`). For Collections, selecting a template pre-fills the form and records a `:USES_TEMPLATE` edge after creation. "Start from blank" remains a secondary option. New frontend-only components: `CollectionTemplateApi.ts` (new backend-client module), `TemplatePickerDialog.vue` (common dialog component). No new backend endpoints. Picker is skipped gracefully when no templates exist or when `collectionAppId` is unavailable. | **ZERO** | Frontend-only change. No new backend endpoints, no schema changes, no config keys, no new `/shepard/api/...` surface. | — | **No action required for upgrade.** Operators who have set up templates will see the picker in creation dialogs automatically. Operators without templates see the blank form (picker is skipped gracefully). |

| **T1f — YAML template import / export** | `aidocs/16` T1f. Two new admin-only endpoints: `GET /v2/templates/export` (returns all non-retired `ShepardTemplate` rows as a YAML document; `Content-Type: text/yaml; charset=UTF-8`; `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="shepard-templates.yaml"`; `?includeRetired=true` opt-in to include retired rows) and `POST /v2/templates/import` (accepts `text/yaml` or `application/yaml` body; validates each entry via `TemplateBodyValidator`; always mints fresh `appId`s — never reuses the exported ones; copy-on-write on name collision; returns `TemplateImportResultIO { items: ShepardTemplateIO[], created: int, updated: int, skipped: int }`; 400 on parse or validation failure). New explicit dependency `com.fasterxml.jackson.dataformat:jackson-dataformat-yaml` (version managed by Quarkus BOM). | **ZERO** | Two new `/v2/` endpoints — additive only, admin-gated (`@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`). No new Neo4j labels, no schema migrations, no config keys. No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path. The new dependency is already present transitively (via `quarkus-smallrye-openapi`) — making it explicit avoids relying on an undeclared transitive. | — | **No action required for upgrade.** The endpoints are available immediately after restart. To export all templates: `GET /v2/templates/export` with an instance-admin API-key → save the YAML file. To import on another instance: `POST /v2/templates/import` with the file as the request body (`Content-Type: text/yaml`). |
| **BTKVS-B2 — form descriptor endpoint + structured 422 violations[] (forms-as-shapes validating slice)** | `aidocs/16` BTKVS-B2 / FORM-DESCRIPTOR-1 / FORM-422-FIELDS (design: `aidocs/integrations/125`). One new read-only endpoint `GET /v2/templates/{templateAppId}/form` (`TemplateFormRest`, operationId `getTemplateForm`): compiles a data-kind template's inheritance-flattened `shapeGraph` into a form descriptor (groups + fields with DASH editor hints + constraint-scoring defaults + server-computed `submit` block; ETag/304; 404/409/422 problem-JSON). The template-instantiation 422 problem-JSON additively grows a structured `violations[]` extension (`{path, value?, constraint?, message}`, path = bare SHACL resultPath predicate IRI). `TemplateInstantiateRequestIO` additively grows an optional `attributes` map (caller values merge over template defaults before SHACL validation). `ShaclShapeBuilder` JSON DSL additively grows `pattern`/`targetNode`/`hints`/`groups` (DASH `dash:editor`/`dash:singleLine`, core SHACL `sh:name`/`sh:order`/`sh:group`/`sh:defaultValue`, minted `urn:shepard:form:placeholder`/`visibleWhen`, domain-side `urn:btkvs:cell-mapping`/`urn:btkvs:sheet`). `POST /v2/shapes/validate` findings additively grow a `constraint` field. Examples: `examples/btkvs-docket-showcase/seed_form_template.py` (registers the docket `:general` STRUCTURED_RECIPE) + `form_demo.py` (Streamlit consumption demo). Frontend stub `/tools/form-preview`. | **ZERO** | One new `/v2/` endpoint, additive request/response fields elsewhere — no `/shepard/api/...` path touched, no new config keys, no new node labels. Existing 422 consumers keep parsing `type`/`title`/`detail` unchanged; `violations[]` is an RFC-7807 extension member. | — | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart the backend; `GET /v2/templates/{appId}/form` appears in the OpenAPI spec under the Templates tag. Optional: register the showcase form template via `python3 examples/btkvs-docket-showcase/seed_form_template.py --host <host> --apikey <admin-key>` and try the Streamlit demo. |
| **BTKVS-C1-EXCEL-EXPORT — shape-driven Excel export of a docket DataObject** | `aidocs/16` BTKVS-C1-EXCEL-EXPORT (design: `aidocs/integrations/125 §6/D5`). One new read-only endpoint `GET /v2/templates/{templateAppId}/export?dataObjectAppId=…` (`TemplateExcelExportRest`, operationId `exportTemplateExcel`, `Produces: application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet`): walks the template's `urn:btkvs:cell-mapping` / `urn:btkvs:sheet` shape annotations and writes the focused DataObject's attribute values into the mapped cells of a generated xlsx workbook (`CellMappingExcelExporter`). New backend dependency `org.apache.poi:poi-ooxml` 5.4.1 (Apache-2.0; pulls the trimmed `poi-ooxml-lite` schema jar). Fields without cell-mappings are skipped silently; 404 unknown template/DataObject, 409 retired/no-mappings, 422 not-form-renderable. Demo `examples/btkvs-docket-showcase/export_demo.py`; "Download Excel" affordance on `/tools/form-preview`. | **ZERO** | One new `/v2/` endpoint + one new Apache-2.0 dependency — no `/shepard/api/...` path touched, no new config keys, no new node labels, no migration. | — | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart the backend; the endpoint appears in the OpenAPI spec under the Templates tag. The export only produces content for templates whose shapes carry `urn:btkvs:cell-mapping` annotations (e.g. the BT-KVS docket showcase). |

| **VID1a — `VideoStreamReference` upload + ffprobe metadata** | New `:VideoStreamReference` Neo4j node entity with 10 scalar properties (`storageLocator`, `mimeType`, `fileSizeBytes`, `durationSeconds`, `width`, `height`, `frameRate`, `videoCodec`, `audioCodec`, `wallClockTimestamp`). Stored in the active `FileStorage` adapter (GridFS or S3) under the shared `_shepard_videos` container. ffprobe-extracted metadata is attempted after upload; all probe fields are nullable — upload succeeds even when ffprobe is absent. `wallClockTimestamp` (UTC nanoseconds from ffprobe `creation_time` tag) is the temporal anchor connecting video to the TM1 time-reference model. Five new endpoints under `/v2/data-objects/{dataObjectAppId}/video-stream-references`: `GET /` (list), `POST /` (upload multipart), `GET /{appId}` (metadata), `DELETE /{appId}`, `GET /{appId}/download`. Permission piggybacks on DataObject Read/Write (same pattern as GitReference). `V38__Add_appId_constraint_VideoStreamReference.cypher` (idempotent, additive). 3 test classes, 27 unit tests (`VideoStreamReferenceIOTest` × 4, `VideoProbeServiceTest` × 19, `VideoStreamReferenceV2RestTest` × 11). **In-tree for VID1a** (leans on existing FileStorage SPI + permission gate; plugin extraction is post-VID1-series work per CLAUDE.md plugin-first rule). | ZERO | Five new `/v2/` endpoints (opt-in for clients). `_shepard_videos` shared container created lazily on first upload (GridFS: no-op since the bucket is shared; S3: bucket must pre-exist per existing `FS1b` operator runbook). No new config keys. No changes to `/shepard/api/...`. | `V38__Add_appId_constraint_VideoStreamReference.cypher` — idempotent `CREATE CONSTRAINT VideoStreamReference_appId_unique IF NOT EXISTS FOR (n:VideoStreamReference) REQUIRE n.appId IS UNIQUE`. No rollback file needed (additive constraint; drop via `DROP CONSTRAINT VideoStreamReference_appId_unique` if rolling back). Migration regression test deferred alongside V35–V37 obligations. | None. Greenfield label — no existing rows. Upgrade is `pull-and-restart`. After restart, `GET /v2/data-objects/{appId}/video-stream-references` returns `[]` for all DataObjects (empty list, 200). ffprobe integration is **opt-in at the OS level** — install `ffprobe` (part of `ffmpeg`) in the backend container image to enable metadata extraction; the feature degrades gracefully when absent. |
| **VID1b — `shepard-plugin-video` extraction (VideoStreamReference + VideoAnnotation → plugin JAR)** | Extracts `de.dlr.shepard.context.references.videostreamreference.*` (6 classes: `VideoStreamReference`, `VideoStreamReferenceDAO`, `VideoStreamReferenceIO`, `VideoProbeResult`, `VideoProbeService`, `VideoStreamReferenceService`) and `de.dlr.shepard.v2.video.*` (5 classes: `VideoAnnotation`, `VideoAnnotationDAO`, `VideoAnnotationIO`, `VideoAnnotationRest`, `VideoStreamReferenceV2Rest`) from the backend into `plugins/video/shepard-plugin-video.jar`. Adds `VideoPayload` marker interface in backend core so `DataObjectIO.videoStreamReferenceCount` doesn't import the plugin entity directly. Adds `VideoPayloadKind` (implements `de.dlr.shepard.spi.payload.PayloadKind`) and `META-INF/services/de.dlr.shepard.spi.payload.PayloadKind` so `NeoConnector` auto-discovers the entity packages. Removes the hardcoded `VideoStreamReference.class.getPackageName()` line from `NeoConnector`. All wire shapes and REST paths are **unchanged** — existing video upload/download/annotation requests work identically. Tests moved to `plugins/video/src/test/`; 72 tests pass. `quarkus.index-dependency.shepard-plugin-video.*` added to `application.properties`. Plugin Lombok + annotation-processor-path added to `plugins/video/pom.xml`. Backend pom.xml comment updated. | **AWARE** | **Packaging change only.** No new endpoints, no wire-shape changes, no Neo4j schema changes, no config keys, no migration. The video plugin is always bundled in the default image (it's in the `with-plugins` profile); operators do not need to add anything. The `shepard.plugins.video.enabled` toggle (already present in `application.properties`) controls whether the plugin's `PluginManifest` advertises it as active in `GET /v2/admin/plugins`. | — (no migration; packaging refactor only). | **No action required for upgrade.** Pull and restart; the plugin JAR replaces the in-tree classes transparently. All existing video stream reference and annotation API calls continue to work without change. |

| **MFFD-VIDEOREF-SCALE-1 + MFFD-IMAGEBUNDLE-PAGINATE-1 — Range requests, JWT-via-query-param, paginated bundle files** | Three orthogonal additive changes closing GAP-9 (`aidocs/agent-findings/mffd-feature-gaps-2026-06-02.md`) so the MFFD W7/W8b/W8c/W8d ingest waves can deliver 133 GB / 139 MP4s + 313,538 PNG frames into the existing reference shapes. (1) **Backend Range support on the video download endpoint**: `VideoStreamReferenceV2Rest.GET /{appId}/download` honours `Range: bytes=START-END` and returns 206 Partial Content with `Content-Range` + `Accept-Ranges: bytes`. 416 on unsatisfiable; full-body fallback (still advertising `Accept-Ranges`) on legacy GridFS rows without size bookkeeping. The parse/slice logic is extracted into a shared `de.dlr.shepard.common.util.HttpRangeUtil` so every future content endpoint inherits the same shape — `FileReferenceV2Rest` (FR1b) refactored to use it. (2) **`JWTFilter` query-param fallback**: when no `Authorization` header is present, the filter reads `?access_token=<jwt>` per RFC 6750 §2.3. Required for HTML5 `<video src>`, `<img src>`, `<a download>` which cannot inject custom headers. The `Authorization` header takes precedence; an empty query value falls back to 401. (3) **Paginated bundle files endpoint**: new `GET /v2/bundles/{bundleAppId}/groups/{groupAppId}/files?page=&size=` returning a `PagedFilesIO` envelope (`items`, `page`, `size`, `totalElements`, `totalPages`). Default page size 200, server cap 1000. Server clamps out-of-range hints rather than rejecting. The existing `GET /v2/bundles/{bundleAppId}/groups/{groupAppId}` continues to return the full embedded `files[]` for backward compat. New tests: 16 `HttpRangeUtilTest` + 10 new `VideoStreamReferenceV2RestTest` (range / 206 / 416 / open-ended / unknown-total fallback / malformed / multi-range refused) + 3 new `JWTFilterTest` (query-param authenticates, header precedence, empty value 401) + 9 new `FileBundleReferenceRestTest` pagination cases + 7 Vitest `videoUrl` + 13 Vitest `imageBundleScrubber` (page-planning helpers). All wire shapes additive — no upstream `/shepard/api/...` paths touched. | **AWARE** | **No new config keys. No new schema constraints. No new admin REST surface.** New behaviour: (a) `GET /v2/data-objects/{appId}/video-stream-references/{appId}/download` now returns 206 Partial Content + `Content-Range` + `Accept-Ranges: bytes` when a valid `Range` header is supplied. Upstream callers that send no `Range` continue to receive the full body (with an additive `Accept-Ranges` header — informational only; clients ignoring unknown headers see no change). (b) `JWTFilter` accepts `?access_token=…` as a fallback when no header is present — operators who proxy `/v2/` should ensure their reverse-proxy logs strip query-string values (Caddy / Zoraxy defaults do not log query strings; Nginx default access-log format does — the reverse-proxy section of `docs/admin/runbooks/` documents this). The query-param channel is a no-op for clients that already use `Authorization: Bearer …`. (c) New `GET /v2/bundles/{bundleAppId}/groups/{groupAppId}/files?page=&size=` returns a `PagedFilesIO` envelope. The existing `GET /v2/bundles/{bundleAppId}/groups/{groupAppId}` keeps returning the full embedded `files[]`. | — (no migration; behavioural additions only). | **No action required for upgrade.** Pull and restart; HTML5 `<video>` scrubbing on multi-GB MP4s now works because the player can request byte ranges. To verify: open a video reference in the UI and seek; the network panel should show 206 responses with `Content-Range: bytes …/…`. To verify the query-param channel: `curl -i "https://<host>/v2/data-objects/<doAppId>/video-stream-references/<refAppId>/download?access_token=<jwt>"` should return 200 (or 206 with a Range header). Reverse-proxy hygiene: confirm your proxy does not log query strings to access logs — the operator-runbook reference is in `docs/admin/runbooks/reverse-proxy-jwt-query-param.md` (planned). Test obligation: 45 unit tests landed in this PR. Real-fixture Playwright validation at the 4K viewport is the operator's post-deploy step against the W2-ingested fixtures; numbers logged to `aidocs/agent-findings/mffd-videoref-scale-playwright-2026-06-02.md` once the MFFD inventory is mounted. |

| **TM1a — time-reference model on `TimeseriesReference`** | Three new nullable fields added to `:TimeseriesReference` Neo4j node: `timeReference` (String — `"WALL_CLOCK"` or `"EXPERIMENT_RELATIVE"`), `wallClockOffset` (Long, UTC nanoseconds of experiment t=0), `wallClockOffsetSource` (String — free-text provenance tag). Fields surface read/write on `TimeseriesReferenceIO`. New endpoint `PATCH /v2/timeseries-references/{appId}` (RFC 7396 merge-patch, Content-Type `application/merge-patch+json`) — only the three TM1 fields are mutable via this endpoint. Auth: Write on the parent `DataObject`. 400 when `timeReference == "EXPERIMENT_RELATIVE"` and no `wallClockOffset` is present (neither in the patch nor already stored). `V37__Backfill_timeReference.cypher` back-fills `timeReference = 'WALL_CLOCK'` on all pre-TM1a `:TimeseriesReference` rows. 10 new unit tests (`TimeseriesReferenceV2RestTest` × 8 + `TimeseriesReferenceIOTest` × 2). No upstream `/shepard/api/...` surface touched — `/v2/` only. | **AWARE** | Three additive nullable Neo4j properties on every `:TimeseriesReference` node. `TimeseriesReferenceIO` JSON shape gains three new optional fields — additive, upstream clients ignoring unknown fields see no breakage. New `/v2/timeseries-references/{appId}` PATCH endpoint — opt-in, `/v2/` shelf only. No new config keys. | `V37__Backfill_timeReference.cypher` — idempotent `MATCH (r:TimeseriesReference) WHERE r.timeReference IS NULL CALL { WITH r SET r.timeReference = 'WALL_CLOCK' } IN TRANSACTIONS OF 10000 ROWS`. Rollback: `V37_R__Rollback_Backfill_timeReference.cypher` (strips `timeReference = 'WALL_CLOCK'` rows). **Migration regression test deferred** — testcontainer fixture alongside V35/V36 obligations; see migration-tests table below. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V37 applies on first start and stamps all pre-TM1a `:TimeseriesReference` rows with `timeReference = 'WALL_CLOCK'` (idempotent, sub-second on typical graphs). Application treats `null` as `"WALL_CLOCK"` so the migration is advisory only. To mark a reference as experiment-relative: `PATCH /v2/timeseries-references/{appId}` with `{"timeReference":"EXPERIMENT_RELATIVE","wallClockOffset":<ns_epoch>}`. |

| **V2a — `revision` counter on `VersionableEntity`** | `aidocs/16` V2a + `aidocs/41` (snapshots design, prerequisite slice). Adds a `revision: long` field (default 1L) to `VersionableEntity` — the common base for `Collection`, `DataObject`, and the `*Reference` family. `GenericDAO#createOrUpdate` increments `revision` on every update (gated on `entity.getId() != null` so fresh creates stay at 1; updates become 2, 3, …). The field is surfaced as a read-only property on `BasicEntityIO` (and thus on every `CollectionIO`, `DataObjectIO`, and `*ReferenceIO` that uses the `VersionableEntity` constructor path). Clients must not write `revision` — it is server-managed. No new endpoints. 5 new unit tests: `VersionableEntityTest#newEntityHasRevisionOne` + `#revisionIncrementTest` (entity unit tests); `BasicEntityIOTest#testConversionVersionable_revisionIncrement` + `#testCopyConstructor_propagatesRevision` (IO tests, plus the existing `#testConversionVersionable` gains a `revision=1` assertion). | **AWARE** | **One new Neo4j property** (`revision: long`) added to every `:VersionableEntity` node — `Collection`, `DataObject`, `BasicReference` and all their subclasses. **No `/shepard/api/...` surface change.** The `revision` field appears as an additive read-only field on `CollectionIO`, `DataObjectIO`, and all `*ReferenceIO` JSON shapes served from `/shepard/api/...` (the existing endpoints). Any client that deserialises these shapes into a strict (no-extra-fields) schema will need to tolerate the new field; standard Jackson / lenient deserialisation is unaffected. No new endpoints. No new config keys. | `V36__Backfill_revision.cypher` — idempotent `MATCH (n:VersionableEntity) WHERE n.revision IS NULL CALL { WITH n SET n.revision = 1 } IN TRANSACTIONS OF 10000 ROWS`. Uses the existing `:VersionableEntity` label (set by V5) so only the relevant nodes are touched. Safe to re-run: the `WHERE n.revision IS NULL` guard skips already-stamped nodes. **Migration regression test deferred** — testcontainer fixture alongside V34/V35 obligations; see migration-tests table below. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V36 runs on first start and stamps all pre-V2a `:VersionableEntity` rows with `revision = 1` (single-pass batch, sub-second on typical graphs). Post-migration every entity has `revision >= 1`; subsequent writes via the API will increment from there. To verify: `MATCH (n:VersionableEntity) WHERE n.revision IS NULL RETURN count(n)` should return 0 after startup. |

| **V2b — `Snapshot` + `SnapshotEntry` model + snapshot CRUD endpoints** | `aidocs/16` V2b + `aidocs/41` §4–5. Two new Neo4j node types: `:Snapshot` (appId, name, description, snapshotCapturedAtMs, snapshotCreatedByUsername, entryCount, `SNAPSHOT_OF`→`:Collection`) and `:SnapshotEntry` (appId, entityAppId, revision, `ENTRY_OF`→`:Snapshot`). Snapshot creation walks the Collection subtree up to 15 hops and records one `SnapshotEntry` per distinct `:VersionableEntity` carrying an `appId`. Five new endpoints under `/v2/` only: `POST /v2/collections/{appId}/snapshots` (201), `GET /v2/collections/{appId}/snapshots` (200 array), `GET /v2/snapshots/{appId}` (200 metadata), `GET /v2/snapshots/{appId}/manifest` (200 entry array), `DELETE /v2/snapshots/{appId}` (204 soft-delete). `V40__Add_appId_constraint_Snapshot.cypher` — unique constraint on `:Snapshot(appId)`. 29 unit tests (`SnapshotRestTest` 22 + `SnapshotServiceTest` 7). Reference page: `docs/reference/snapshots.md`. Reads via snapshot (V2c) and reproducible RO-Crate export (V2d) are follow-on slices. | **ZERO** | **Five new `/v2/` endpoints** (additive, opt-in). No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path. No new config keys. **Two new Neo4j labels** `:Snapshot` + `:SnapshotEntry` with one new uniqueness constraint (`V40`). No change to any existing node shape or relationship. Pre-existing `:VersionableEntity` nodes without an `appId` (pre-L2a rows) are silently skipped during snapshot creation. | `V40__Add_appId_constraint_Snapshot.cypher` — `CREATE CONSTRAINT Snapshot_appId_unique IF NOT EXISTS FOR (n:Snapshot) REQUIRE n.appId IS UNIQUE`. Idempotent; fail-fast via `MigrationsRunner`. No rollback file needed (additive constraint; drop via `DROP CONSTRAINT Snapshot_appId_unique IF EXISTS`). Migration regression test deferred (testcontainer assertion planned alongside the broader Neo4j migration scaffold). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V40 runs on first start. The new endpoints appear in the OpenAPI spec at `POST/GET /v2/collections/{appId}/snapshots` and `GET/manifest/DELETE /v2/snapshots/{appId}`. To take your first snapshot: `POST /v2/collections/<collectionAppId>/snapshots` with `{"name": "v1.0"}` (requires Write permission). To verify the constraint was applied: `CALL apoc.schema.assert({}, {}) YIELD label, key WHERE label = "Snapshot" RETURN label, key`. |

| **V2e — Snapshot diff endpoint** | `aidocs/16` V2e + `aidocs/41` §7. New `GET /v2/snapshots/{aAppId}/diff/{bAppId}` endpoint (class `SnapshotDiffRest` under `de.dlr.shepard.v2.snapshot.resources`). Compares the `SnapshotEntry` sets of two snapshots (A = base, B = head) and returns a `SnapshotDiffIO` response with: `added` (entityAppIds in B not in A), `removed` (entityAppIds in A not in B), `changed` (entityAppIds in both with different revision — includes revisionA and revisionB), `unchangedCount` (integer count of entities with identical revisions). All lists sorted by `entityAppId` ascending. New `getEntryRevisionMap(long snapshotNeo4jId)` method added to `SnapshotDAO` (returns `Map<String, Long>`) and a service passthrough on `SnapshotService`. `SnapshotDiffIO` Java record (with nested `DiffEntry`) under `de.dlr.shepard.context.snapshot.io`. Auth: authenticated caller required (401 unauthenticated); no per-collection permission gate in v1 (snapshots are metadata). 400 when A and B are the same appId. 404 when either snapshot is unknown or soft-deleted. 9 unit tests in `SnapshotDiffRestTest`. `docs/reference/snapshots.md` updated with diff endpoint section. | **ZERO** | **One new `/v2/` endpoint** (`GET /v2/snapshots/{aAppId}/diff/{bAppId}`) — additive, opt-in. No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path. No new config keys. No new Neo4j labels, constraints, or migrations. No change to any existing node shape. | — (no migration — purely additive endpoint; reads existing `:SnapshotEntry` nodes) | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the endpoint appears in the OpenAPI spec at `GET /v2/snapshots/{aAppId}/diff/{bAppId}`. Example: `GET /v2/snapshots/<snapAAppId>/diff/<snapBAppId>` returns `{"snapshotAAppId":"...","snapshotBAppId":"...","added":[...],"removed":[...],"changed":[{"entityAppId":"...","revisionA":1,"revisionB":3}],"unchangedCount":42}`. Requires authentication (Bearer token or API key); no Write permission needed. |

| **V2c — snapshot-pinned DataObject list endpoint** | `aidocs/16` V2c + `aidocs/41` §4.2 + §5. New endpoint `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/snapshots/{snapshotAppId}/data-objects` returns the list of DataObject `appId` strings that were captured in the snapshot. Response shape `SnapshotDataObjectsIO`: snapshot metadata (appId, name, capturedAt, totalEntries) + filtered `dataObjectAppIds` list (Collections, References, soft-deleted entities excluded). Two new DAO methods added to `SnapshotDAO`: `getEntryAppIds(long snapshotNeo4jId)` + `filterDataObjectAppIds(Collection<String> appIds)` (Cypher `MATCH (d:DataObject)` filter, ordered by appId ascending). One new service method: `SnapshotService.listDataObjectAppIds(Snapshot)`. New REST class `SnapshotPinnedReadRest` under `de.dlr.shepard.v2.snapshot.resources`. Gate order: 401 (unauth) → 404 (collection not found) → 403 (no Read) → 404 (snapshot not found) → 409 (snapshot belongs to different collection) → 200. 9 unit tests (`SnapshotPinnedReadRestTest`). No new Neo4j migrations (endpoint reads existing `:Snapshot`, `:SnapshotEntry`, `:DataObject` nodes). `docs/reference/snapshots.md` updated. | **ZERO** | **One new `/v2/` endpoint** (additive, opt-in). No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path. No new config keys. No new Neo4j labels or constraints. No change to any existing node shape or relationship. | — (no migration — reads existing V2b `:Snapshot` + `:SnapshotEntry` data plus existing `:DataObject` nodes). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the endpoint appears in the OpenAPI spec at `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/snapshots/{snapshotAppId}/data-objects`. Requires an existing snapshot (created by `POST /v2/collections/{appId}/snapshots`). Example: `GET /v2/collections/<collAppId>/snapshots/<snapAppId>/data-objects` → `{"snapshotAppId":"...","dataObjectAppIds":["<do1>","<do2>",...]}`. |

| **V2d — snapshot-pinned RO-Crate export** | `aidocs/16` V2d + `aidocs/41` §5. New optional field `snapshotAppId` (nullable `String`) on `ExportSelection` (the `/v2/` export request body). When set, `ExportService.resolveSnapshotFilter()` calls `SnapshotService.findByAppId(snapshotAppId)` + `listDataObjectAppIds(snapshot)` and builds an allowlist; DataObjects whose `appId` is not in the allowlist are silently skipped. Absent or null `snapshotAppId` preserves the existing full-export behaviour unchanged. Unknown `snapshotAppId` (snapshot not found or soft-deleted) yields an empty export (conservative: do not export stale data). Backward-compat 2-arg constructor `ExportSelection(Payloads, Metadata)` delegates with `snapshotAppId=null` so every existing call site compiles without change. `SnapshotService` injected into `ExportService`. 3 new unit tests in `ExportSnapshotFilterTest`. | **ZERO** | **No new endpoints.** The `ExportSelection` JSON body gains an optional `snapshotAppId` field — additive, ignored by clients that don't send it. No change to the `/shepard/api/...` export paths (those do not accept a body). No new config keys. No new Neo4j labels, constraints, or migrations. | — (no migration — purely additive request-body field; reads existing `:Snapshot` + `:SnapshotEntry` + `:DataObject` nodes via V2b/V2c infrastructure). | **No action required for upgrade.** The `snapshotAppId` field is optional and defaults to null (full export). To pin an export to a snapshot: include `"snapshotAppId": "<snapAppId>"` in the `/v2/collections/{appId}/export` request body. Example: `POST /v2/collections/<collAppId>/export` with `{"snapshotAppId":"<snapAppId>"}` → returns a ZIP containing only the DataObjects captured in that snapshot. |

| **PL1d — `shepard-plugin-git` extraction** | `aidocs/47` PL1d. Git reference payload kind (previously in-tree in `de.dlr.shepard.context.references.git.*` + `de.dlr.shepard.v2.git.*` + `de.dlr.shepard.v2.users.resources.MeCredentialsRest` + `de.dlr.shepard.v2.users.io.{Create,Patch,}GitCredentialIO`) moved to `plugins/git/` drop-in JAR. **OGM-gap fix**: `de.dlr.shepard.context.references.git.entities` was previously absent from `NeoConnector`'s base-package list — `GitReference` would fail to hydrate without the plugin on the classpath. `GitPayloadKind` (ServiceLoader POJO) registers the package via `PayloadKind` SPI at connect time. `GitPluginManifest` (PluginManifest SPI) exposes the plugin in `GET /v2/admin/plugins`. **Auth perimeter**: `GitCredential` entity, `GitCredentialDAO`, `GitCredentialService` stay in backend — accessed by the plugin via the `provided` backend dep. Cypher migrations V19/V20/V26 stay in backend. 20 production Java files + 9 unit tests moved. `GitArtifactCacheQuarkusTest` stays in backend (requires live Quarkus runtime). `shepard.plugins.git.enabled=true` default (mirrors pre-extraction always-on posture). | **AWARE** | **No new endpoints.** `/v2/data-objects/{id}/git-references/*` + `/v2/me/git-credentials/*` paths are byte-identical — code moved from backend to plugin JAR in the same Quarkus JVM. **OGM fix is transparent**: `GitReference`'s OGM package is now correctly registered; pre-plugin installs had a latent hydration bug that this extraction also repairs. **New config key**: `shepard.plugins.git.enabled=true` (default on). **New Maven module** `plugins/git/` — operators building custom images without git support pass `-DnoPlugins` or remove `shepard-plugin-git` from the `with-plugins` profile and set `shepard.plugins.git.enabled=false`. | No new migrations.
