V19/V20/V26 stay in backend — migration state tracking would re-run them on existing installs if moved. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the plugin activates via the `with-plugins` classpath exactly as before. Git references remain enabled (default `true`). Run `GET /v2/admin/plugins` after restart to confirm the `git` plugin entry appears. |

| **PL1c — `shepard-plugin-hdf5` extraction** | `aidocs/47` PL1c. HDF5/HSDS payload kind (previously in-tree in `de.dlr.shepard.data.hdf.*` + `de.dlr.shepard.v2.hdf.*` + `de.dlr.shepard.v2.admin.hdf.*`) moved to `plugins/hdf5/` drop-in JAR. **OGM-gap fix**: `de.dlr.shepard.data.hdf.entities` was previously absent from `NeoConnector`'s base-package list — `HdfContainer` would fail to hydrate in any install without the plugin on the classpath. `HdfPayloadKind` (ServiceLoader POJO) registers the package via `PayloadKind` SPI at connect time, making it impossible to have an HDF-enabled deploy without OGM registration. `HdfPluginManifest` (PluginManifest SPI) exposes the plugin in `GET /v2/admin/plugins`. `HdfFeatureToggle` stays in backend (in `de.dlr.shepard.common.configuration.feature.toggles`, accessed by plugin via `provided` dep). Cypher migrations V25 (appId constraint) stay in backend. 9 production Java files + 10 unit tests moved. `shepard.plugins.hdf5.enabled=false` default (matches pre-extraction posture where `shepard.hdf.enabled=false`). | **AWARE** | **No new endpoints.** `/v2/hdf-containers/*` + `/v2/admin/hdf/*` paths are byte-identical — code moved from backend to plugin JAR in the same Quarkus JVM. **OGM fix is transparent**: on upgrade the HDF plugin registers `HdfContainer`'s OGM package correctly; pre-plugin installs with `shepard.hdf.enabled=true` had a latent hydration bug that this extraction also repairs. **New config key**: `shepard.plugins.hdf5.enabled=false` — mirrors the existing `shepard.hdf.enabled` toggle. Both must be set to `true` to enable HDF. **New Maven module** `plugins/hdf5/` — operators building custom images without HDF support pass `-DnoPlugins` or remove `shepard-plugin-hdf5` from the `with-plugins` profile. | No new migrations. V25 (`V25__Add_appId_constraint_HdfContainer.cypher`) stays in backend — migration state tracking would re-run it on existing installs if moved. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the plugin activates via the `with-plugins` classpath exactly as before. HDF feature remains off (`shepard.hdf.enabled=false`) unless the operator explicitly enables it. If upgrading an install that already had `shepard.hdf.enabled=true`: restart normally — the OGM-gap fix applies silently on startup. Run `GET /v2/admin/plugins` after restart to confirm the `hdf5` plugin entry appears. |

| **A5c — HdfReference per-DataObject anchor at dataset path** | `aidocs/16` A5c. New `:HdfReference extends :BasicReference` Neo4j entity in `plugins/hdf5/`. Inherits the `(DataObject)-[:has_reference]->(HdfReference)` graph edge from `BasicReference` for free. Additional `(HdfReference)-[:IS_HDF_REFERENCE_OF]->(HdfContainer)` outgoing edge. Fields: `appId` (UUID v7, server-minted), `datasetPath` (required, e.g. `/sensor_data/channel_A`), `description` (optional). Three endpoints, all gated by `@EndpointDisabled(name="shepard.hdf.enabled", stringValue="false")`: `GET /v2/data-objects/{dataObjectAppId}/hdf-references` (list, READ perm), `POST /v2/data-objects/{dataObjectAppId}/hdf-references` (create, WRITE perm), `DELETE /v2/data-objects/{dataObjectAppId}/hdf-references/{referenceAppId}` (delete, WRITE perm). Permissions checked via `PermissionsService.isAccessAllowedForDataObjectAppId`. Frontend: `HdfReferencesPane.vue` wired into DataObject detail page; handles 404 gracefully when feature is off. `V84__HdfReference_indexes.cypher` + rollback `V84_R__`. `HdfPayloadKind.entityPackages()` updated. 18 Mockito tests + 10 Vitest tests. Annotation hookup (E6/`AnnotatableHdfDataset`) is explicitly deferred as row A5c-annotation in `aidocs/16`. | **ZERO** | Additive `/v2/` endpoints only. No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path. Returns 404 when `shepard.hdf.enabled=false` (default). No new config keys. | `V84__HdfReference_indexes.cypher` — idempotent (`IF NOT EXISTS`). Creates: (1) unique constraint on `:HdfReference.appId`, (2) index on `:HdfReference.datasetPath`. Rollback: `V84_R__HdfReference_indexes.cypher`. Migration regression test deferred (tracker anchor: `aidocs/16` row A5c). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V84 applies on first start (zero `:HdfReference` nodes on a clean upgrade — purely additive constraint + index). With `shepard.hdf.enabled=false` (the default), the REST endpoints return 404 and the frontend panel renders its empty state silently. To start creating references: enable the HDF feature per the A5a runbook (`docs/admin.md §"HDF5 (HSDS) — opt-in sidecar"`), then `POST /v2/data-objects/{appId}/hdf-references` with `{"hdfContainerAppId": "<containerAppId>", "datasetPath": "/sensor/ch_a"}`. |
| **A5c-annotation — HdfReference semantic annotation affordance** | `aidocs/16` A5c-annotation. Adds the annotation layer to existing `HdfReference` nodes — no new entity, no new REST endpoint. Three artefacts: (1) `V87__hdf_vocabulary.cypher` seeds a `:Vocabulary {uri:"https://shepard.dlr.de/ontology/hdf#", prefix:"hdf", label:"HDF5 Vocabulary", enabled:true}` node (V72 MERGE-on-uri pattern; idempotent; rollback `V87_R__hdf_vocabulary.cypher`). (2) `backend/src/main/resources/shapes/hdf-shapes.ttl` declares `hdf:HdfReferenceShape` with `hdf:hasDatasetPath` + `hdf:hasContainerAppId` property shapes; auto-loaded from `classpath:/shapes/` by `SemanticShapeLoader` — no code change. (3) `HdfReferencesPane.vue` gains a `mdi-tag-outline` Annotate button per reference row (always visible, not gated by `canEdit`) that opens `AnnotationDialog` with `subject-app-id="ref.appId"` and `subject-kind="HdfReference"`. Annotation writes go through the existing polymorphic `POST /v2/annotations` endpoint (`SemanticAnnotationV2Rest`) — no new backend code. 5 new Vitest tests in `hdfReferences.test.ts`. | **ZERO** | **No new endpoints.** `POST /v2/annotations` with `subjectKind=HdfReference` was already accepted by `SemanticAnnotationV2Rest` (polymorphic over any `subjectKind`). No new config keys. No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path. Purely additive: a vocabulary node in Neo4j, a SHACL shape in the classpath, and a UI button. | `V87__hdf_vocabulary.cypher` — idempotent MERGE on `uri`. Rollback: `V87_R__hdf_vocabulary.cypher` (`MATCH (v:Vocabulary {uri:"https://shepard.dlr.de/ontology/hdf#"}) DETACH DELETE v`). Migration regression test deferred. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V87 seeds the vocabulary node on first start. The `mdi-tag-outline` button appears in `HdfReferencesPane` immediately for all DataObjects in HDF-enabled installs. The button is read-through: clicking it opens the same `AnnotationDialog` used everywhere else; no HDF-specific permissions check is added (annotation permissions are governed by the annotation subsystem). |
| **A5d — `GET /v2/hdf-containers/{appId}/file` download endpoint** | `aidocs/16` A5d. New `GET /v2/hdf-containers/{appId}/file` endpoint in `plugins/hdf5/`. Calls `GET /?domain=<hsdsDomain>` on the HSDS sidecar with `Accept: application/x-hdf5`, streams the response body to the client as a byte stream. Supports HTTP Range requests: inbound `Range` header passed through verbatim; HSDS 206 Partial Content response propagated with `Content-Range` + `Accept-Ranges` headers. `Content-Type: application/x-hdf5`; `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="<name>.h5"` (RFC 5987 encoded for non-ASCII names). Auth: Read permission on the container (same as `GET /v2/hdf-containers/{appId}`). Feature-gated: returns 404 when `shepard.hdf.enabled=false` (consistent with other HDF endpoints per `@EndpointDisabled`). New methods: `HsdsClient.exportFile(domain, rangeHeader)` returning an `ExportResponse` record (status, streaming `InputStream`, `contentLength`, `contentRange`, `acceptRanges`); `HdfContainerService.downloadFile(container, rangeHeader)`. 7 new `HsdsClientExportTest` tests + 4 new `HdfContainerRestTest` tests + 4 new `HdfContainerServiceTest` tests. Reference page `docs/reference/hdf-container.md` updated. | **ZERO** | Additive `/v2/` endpoint only. No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path. No new Neo4j labels, constraints, or migrations. No new config keys. Requires `shepard.hdf.enabled=true` and the HSDS sidecar running — same pre-condition as all other HDF endpoints. | — (no migration needed — additive endpoint) | **No action required for upgrade.** The endpoint is available after restart when `shepard.hdf.enabled=true`. Offline fallback: `curl -H "X-API-KEY: <key>" https://shepard.example.dlr.de/v2/hdf-containers/<appId>/file -o container.h5` then open with `h5py.File("container.h5")`. |
| **V2CONV-A7-HDF — `/v2/hdf-containers/*` folded into the unified `/v2/containers` surface (bespoke base CRUD removed; `/file` → generic resolver)** | `aidocs/16` V2CONV-A7-HDF-UNIFY + `aidocs/platform/191 §7a`. The bespoke `HdfContainerRest` (`/v2/hdf-containers/*`) is **deleted**. HDF container get/create/delete/list now flow through the already-shipped unified `/v2/containers` surface (`GET\|DELETE /v2/containers/{appId}`, `POST\|GET /v2/containers?kind=hdf`) via the `HdfContainerKindHandler` (`ContainerKindHandler` SPI). The raw HDF5 download (formerly `GET /v2/hdf-containers/{appId}/file`) moves to a **generic** `GET /v2/containers/{appId}/file` resolver: new core value type `ContainerFileDownload` + an optional `ContainerKindHandler.downloadFile(appId, range)` default that returns empty → `415` for kinds with no single-file payload; `HdfContainerKindHandler` overrides it, reusing `HdfContainerService.downloadFile` (HSDS reading unchanged — `application/x-hdf5`, `<name>.h5` Content-Disposition, 206/Content-Range relay all preserved). FE page `containers/hdf/[containerId]` repointed. `HdfContainerRest` + `HdfContainerRestTest` deleted (234 LoC); `ContainersV2RestTest` +6, `HdfContainerKindHandlerTest` +4 tests. | **BREAKING (dev surface only)** | **The bespoke `/v2/hdf-containers/*` paths now return 404.** This is a `/v2/` development-surface change — the frozen `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is untouched (upstream has no HDF support). Any third-party client built against this fork's `/v2/hdf-containers/{appId}` must repoint: get/delete → `GET\|DELETE /v2/containers/{appId}`; create → `POST /v2/containers?kind=hdf` with body `{name, description?}`; list → `GET /v2/containers?kind=hdf`; raw download → `GET /v2/containers/{appId}/file`. The unified read shape is `ContainerV2IO` — HDF-specific `hsdsDomain`/`description` move from top-level fields into the `payload` map. No new config keys; no new Neo4j labels/constraints/migrations. | — (no migration — REST surface change only; `:HdfContainer` nodes + appIds unchanged). | **No action required for an operator who only uses the Shepard frontend** (it was repointed in the same change). **A third-party `/v2/hdf-containers/*` client must update its base paths** (see "byte-compat" column). Verify the cutover after restart: `curl -so /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' -H "X-API-KEY: <key>" https://<host>/v2/hdf-containers/<appId>` → `404`; `curl -so /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' -H "X-API-KEY: <key>" "https://<host>/v2/containers?kind=hdf"` → `200`; raw download `curl -H "X-API-KEY: <key>" https://<host>/v2/containers/<appId>/file -o container.h5`. |
| **V2CONV-A7-SVDX — bespoke `/v2/svdx/ingest` dissolved onto the generic MAPPING_RECIPE / `TransformExecutor` seam** | `aidocs/16` V2CONV-A7-SVDX-REST-DISSOLVE + `aidocs/platform/191 §7/§7a`. The bespoke top-level `POST /v2/svdx/ingest` endpoint (`SvdxIngestRest`) and its `SvdxIngestRequestIO`/`SvdxIngestResponseIO` shapes are **deleted**. The real CSV→TimescaleDB ingest (parse a TwinCAT-Scope `.csv` sibling, build per-channel 5-tuple identities, write rows via `TimeseriesService.saveDataPoints`, mint a derived `TimeseriesReference`) is **rehomed** behind `SvdxCsvTransformExecutor` — a ServiceLoader `TransformExecutor` (in `plugins/fileformat-svdx`) that claims the `SvdxCsvIngestShape` IRI (`http://semantics.dlr.de/shepard/transform#SvdxCsvIngestShape`) and reuses `SvdxCsvIngestionService` verbatim via `CDI.current()`. It runs through the generic `POST /v2/mappings/{templateAppId}/materialize` dispatch, exactly mirroring the KRL (V2CONV-B5) and URScript dissolutions. The deterministic `svdx-ingest:<svdxId>+<csvId>` idempotency contract is preserved. New SHACL shape `backend/src/main/resources/shapes/svdx-csv-ingest.shacl.ttl`; META-INF registration in the plugin; `SvdxCsvTransformExecutorTest` (10 cases). The `SvdxManifestParser` annotate-on-upload path is untouched. | **BREAKING (dev surface only — pre-prod)** | **The bespoke `/v2/svdx/ingest` path now returns 404.** This is a `/v2/` development-surface change — the frozen `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is untouched (upstream has no SVDX support). This v2 path is pre-prod (no third-party client built against it; only the welding showcase seed referenced it, in prose) → **deleted outright, no shim**. A caller that did `POST /v2/svdx/ingest {svdxFileAppId, csvFileAppId, dataObjectAppId}` migrates to: (1) create a `MAPPING_RECIPE` `ShepardTemplate` whose body declares `mappingRecipeShape = SvdxCsvIngestShape` + `svdxFileReferenceAppId`/`csvFileReferenceAppId`/`targetDataObjectAppId` (optional `timeseriesContainerAppId`/`referenceName`); (2) `POST /v2/mappings/{templateAppId}/materialize` with `{inputReferenceAppIds:{svdxFileAppId, csvFileAppId}}` → `{outputKind:"REFERENCE", derivedReferenceAppId}`. No new config keys; no new Neo4j labels/constraints/migrations. | — (no migration — REST surface change only; svdx ingest never had a typed `:*Activity`, it relies on `ProvenanceCaptureFilter`, so no relabel is needed). | **No action required for an operator who only uses the Shepard frontend** (no FE caller existed). **A third-party `/v2/svdx/ingest` client must repoint to the MAPPING_RECIPE materialize flow** (see "byte-compat" column). Verify after restart: `curl -so /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' -X POST -H "X-API-KEY: <key>" https://<host>/v2/svdx/ingest` → `404`; the executor is discoverable when `shepard.plugins.fileformat-svdx.enabled=true` (default) — a materialize against `SvdxCsvIngestShape` returns the derived TimeseriesReference appId, or a clean `404 transform.executor.not-registered` when the plugin is disabled (fail-soft registry). |
| **UI-011e — `POST /search/collections` now returns populated `dataObjectIds[]` (bug fix)** | `aidocs/16` UI-011e. `SearchDAO.findCollections` previously projected the Collection neighborhood with `Neighborhood.ESSENTIAL` (`path=(c)-[*0..1]->(n) WHERE n:Permission OR n:User`), which only hydrates `:User` and `:Permission` nodes. As a result, the `:Collection`-[:has_dataobject]->(:DataObject)` edge was never walked and `CollectionIO.dataObjectIds[]` came back as `[]` on the wire for every collection — including MFFD-Dropbox (8514 DOs) and LUMEN (17 DOs). The frontend `/collections` listing reads `(item.dataObjectIds || []).length` for its `# DOs` column, so every collection showed `0`. The non-search list path (`GET /v2/collections` → `CollectionDAO.findAllCollectionsByShepardId`) had always returned correct counts because it uses the default `Neighborhood.EVERYTHING` depth-1 projection. The fix aligns the search projection with that precedent: `emitCollectionReturnPart` now uses `Neighborhood.EVERYTHING` (depth 1). Container / User / UserGroup `emit*ReturnPart` helpers stay on ESSENTIAL (their list cells don't expose nested-entity counts). The wire field `dataObjectIds` was always advertised in `CollectionSearchResult` — its contents are now correct. | **ZERO** | **No new endpoints, no new fields.** Pure bug fix: `POST /shepard/api/search/collections` and `POST /search/collections` both now populate `dataObjectIds[]` correctly. Clients that already read `dataObjectIds.length` (frontend `CollectionList.vue`) start seeing the real count after redeploy. Any client that relied on `dataObjectIds` being empty on search results (would be a latent bug) gets the correct value. No new config keys. No new Neo4j label, constraint, or migration. | — (no migration — pure Cypher projection change). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the `# DOs` column on the `/collections` page populates correctly on the next page load. Verify on the wire: `curl -fsS -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -X POST -d '{"searchParams":{"query":"{\"OR\":[{\"property\":\"name\",\"value\":\"\",\"operator\":\"contains\"}]}"}}' "https://<host>/shepard/api/search/collections?page=0&size=20" \| jq '.results[] \| {name, doCount: (.dataObjectIds\|length)}'` should now show non-zero counts for populated collections (was 0 for all rows pre-fix). |
| **SPI1a — `PayloadKind` SPI + `shepard-plugin-spatial` extraction** | `aidocs/47` PL1a + PL1b. Two coupled changes land together: (1) **`PayloadKind` SPI interface** (`de.dlr.shepard.spi.payload.PayloadKind`) — two-method interface (`name()` + `entityPackages()`) discovered by `NeoConnector.connect()` via `ServiceLoader.load(PayloadKind.class)` to register Neo4j-OGM entity packages contributed by plugins. This replaces the two hardcoded package references to `de.dlr.shepard.data.spatialdata.model` + `de.dlr.shepard.context.references.spatialdata.entities` that lived in `NeoConnector`. (2) **`shepard-plugin-spatial`** — new Maven module `plugins/spatial/` extracts the PostGIS spatial-data payload kind from the backend into a drop-in plugin JAR. `SpatialPayloadKind` (ServiceLoader POJO) contributes the two OGM entity packages; `SpatialPluginManifest` (PluginManifest SPI) registers the plugin in `GET /v2/admin/plugins`. All spatial production code moves: `de.dlr.shepard.data.spatialdata.*`, `de.dlr.shepard.context.references.spatialdata.*`, the SQL Flyway migration `V1.0.0__setup_spatial_data_tables.sql`, and nine unit-test classes. Two integration tests (`SpatialDataIT`, `SpatialDataReferenceIT`) stay in the backend (they need the full Quarkus image). `ContainerType.SPATIALDATA.getTypeName()` now returns the string literal `"SpatialDataContainer"` instead of `SpatialDataContainer.class.getSimpleName()` — identical at runtime. `hibernate-spatial` remains in the backend pom (needed by the plugin at Quarkus-augmentation time and at runtime). The backend `with-plugins` Maven profile adds `shepard-plugin-spatial` as a compile-scope dep; `quarkus.index-dependency.shepard-plugin-spatial.*` ensures Jandex indexes it. `shepard.plugins.spatial.enabled=true` by default (mirrors the pre-extraction always-on posture). | **AWARE** | **No new endpoints.** All existing `/shepard/api/spatialdata/...` + context-reference paths are byte-identical — the code moved from the backend to a plugin JAR but runs in the same Quarkus JVM on the same classpath. **No new config keys.** `shepard.infrastructure.spatial.enabled` (A3c) is unchanged. `shepard.plugins.spatial.enabled` is a new PluginManifest toggle (defaults `true`); an operator who sets it to `false` disables the plugin manifest lifecycle hook but does NOT disable the spatial REST surface (that's the existing `shepard.infrastructure.spatial.enabled` gate). Both toggles must be considered. **New Maven module** `plugins/spatial/` — operators building their own image who want to exclude spatial can either (a) pass `-DnoPlugins` + remove from `with-plugins`, or (b) set `shepard.plugins.spatial.enabled=false` + `shepard.infrastructure.spatial.enabled=false`. | `V1.0.0__setup_spatial_data_tables.sql` (unchanged; moved from `backend/src/main/resources/db/spatial/migration/` to `plugins/spatial/src/main/resources/db/spatial/migration/`; resolved by Flyway via classpath, which finds it in the plugin JAR — no operator action needed). No new Neo4j migrations. | **No action required for upgrade.** The spatial payload kind is present and enabled by default (same posture as pre-SPI1a). If you build a custom image without the spatial plugin, add `-DnoPlugins` or remove `shepard-plugin-spatial` from the `with-plugins` profile in your fork of `backend/pom.xml`, then set `shepard.infrastructure.spatial.enabled=false` so Flyway doesn't try to run spatial migrations and the PostGIS datasource isn't required. |

| **TS-CHUNK1 — TimescaleDB hypertable chunk / partition optimisation** | `aidocs/16` TS-CHUNK1. New Flyway SQL migration `V1.13.0__Optimize_timeseries_chunk_config.sql` (idempotent). Two changes to the `timeseries_data_points` hypertable: (1) **chunk time interval reduced from 7 days to 1 hour** — the default 7-day window is wrong for AFP tapelaying sensor data at 180 Hz (~1 080 rows per 6-second track); 1-hour chunks give ~6 000 track runs per chunk and match the "one test session" query pattern. (2) **4 space partitions on `timeseries_id`** — allows 4 importer workers to write to distinct physical partitions without row-level lock contention. Both changes are fully idempotent: each block reads current state first and emits a `NOTICE` log line reporting what it did or skipped. The chunk interval change only affects **new** chunks; existing data is not repacked. | **AWARE** | **Performance change only — no API, schema, or config-key change.** Existing data in 7-day chunks is unaffected until those chunks are explicitly repacked (operator's choice, documented in the migration comment). The 4 space partitions are additive and cannot be removed via SQL — they are a one-way change. Operators who are already using `add_space_partitions` on this hypertable (non-standard setup) will see the idempotency guard emit a `NOTICE` and skip. | `V1.13.0__Optimize_timeseries_chunk_config.sql` — idempotent (reads `timescaledb_information.hypertables` and `.dimensions` before acting). No rollback file needed for the space-partition step (additive; cannot be removed, but safe to leave). Chunk interval rollback if needed: `SELECT set_chunk_time_interval('timeseries_data_points', INTERVAL '7 days')`. Migration regression test deferred. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V1.13.0 applies on first start and logs a `NOTICE` per block. Verify the interval: `SELECT integer_interval FROM timescaledb_information.dimensions WHERE hypertable_name = 'timeseries_data_points' AND dimension_type = 'Time'` → should return `3600000000000` (1 hour in microseconds). Verify the space dimension: `SELECT dimension_type FROM timescaledb_information.dimensions WHERE hypertable_name = 'timeseries_data_points'` → should return rows including `Space`. Operators wanting to retroactively repack existing chunks for full locality benefit: `SELECT recompress_chunk(c) FROM show_chunks('timeseries_data_points') c;` (expensive — run outside peak hours). |

| **TS-CORE-SCHEMA-01 — 5-tuple moved to `channel_metadata` side table** | `aidocs/16` TS-CORE-SCHEMA-01. New Flyway SQL migration `V1.14.0__drop_5tuple_from_core_timeseries.sql`. The five legacy channel-identity columns (`measurement`, `field`, `device`, `location`, `symbolic_name`) are removed from the `timeseries` table and moved to a new `channel_metadata` side table joined 1:1 via `channel_metadata.timeseries_id`. The `timeseries` table now contains only: `id`, `container_id`, `value_type`, `shepard_id`. JPA mapping uses `@SecondaryTable` — all existing callers are transparent (same constructors, getters, JPQL filters). The 5-tuple uniqueness constraint moves to `channel_metadata`. This is a stepping stone; the TS-SEMANTIC-01 migration (row below) adds the Neo4j dual-write. | **BREAKING (schema)** | The `timeseries` table no longer has `measurement`, `field`, `device`, `location`, `symbolic_name` columns after migration. Any direct SQL or external tool querying these columns on `timeseries` will break. Read them from `channel_metadata` joined by `timeseries_id`. The v1 and v2 API surfaces are unaffected (reconstructed from the JOIN automatically). | `V1.14.0__drop_5tuple_from_core_timeseries.sql` — idempotent (Step 2 is conditional on column existence). Rollback: `V1.14.0_R__restore_5tuple_to_timeseries.sql`. Migration regression test deferred (note in migration-tests table). | **Action required:** restart shepard; V1.14.0 applies on first start. Migration takes ~seconds on typical row counts (one bulk INSERT into `channel_metadata`, then ALTER TABLE to drop 5 columns). Verify: `SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name='timeseries'` → should NOT include `measurement`, `field`, etc. Verify channel_metadata: `SELECT count(*) FROM channel_metadata` → should match `SELECT count(*) FROM timeseries`. If you have external tools (dashboards, custom SQL) reading `timeseries.measurement`, update them to `JOIN channel_metadata cm ON cm.timeseries_id = t.id` and read `cm.measurement`. |

| **TS-SEMANTIC-01 — dual-write `channel_metadata` to Neo4j `SemanticAnnotation` nodes** | `aidocs/16` TS-SEMANTIC-01. New `@ApplicationScoped TimeseriesSemanticDualWriteService` called from `TimeseriesRepository.upsert()` immediately after a successful `channel_metadata` INSERT (the `cmRows > 0` branch). Writes one `SemanticAnnotation` node per non-blank 5-tuple field, linked via `AnnotatableTimeseries` in Neo4j. `AnnotatableTimeseries.appId` is set to `TimeseriesEntity.shepardId` (the Postgres channel UUID) to bridge the two substrates — Phase 3 (TS-SEMANTIC-02) can then flip reads to Neo4j and drop `channel_metadata` using that same UUID. Five new predicate IRI constants: `urn:shepard:channel:{measurement,field,device,location,symbolicName}` (defined in `Constants.java`). Source tag `ts-channel-metadata`. Subject kind `AnnotatableTimeseries`. `sourceMode="ai"` (technical debt: should be `"system"` when that enum value is introduced in TS-SEMANTIC-02). Best-effort: any Neo4j failure is caught inside the service and logged at WARN; the Postgres write completes normally. TODO(TS-SEMANTIC-02): async queue for bulk-ingest scenarios (MFFD creates thousands of channels). | **ADDITIVE** | **No new REST endpoints.** No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. No new Postgres migration — `channel_metadata` rows are unchanged. The only Neo4j change is additive: new `AnnotatableTimeseries` + `SemanticAnnotation` nodes for newly-created channels. Pre-TS-SEMANTIC-01 channels that already exist in `channel_metadata` do NOT get annotation nodes retroactively — that's Phase 3. An upgrader with pre-existing channels keeps them in `channel_metadata` only (no loss of data, no broken reads). New channels created after restart will have both `channel_metadata` rows AND `SemanticAnnotation` nodes. | No migration file (pure application-layer dual-write; no Flyway or Neo4j migration needed). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; all new channel creation will dual-write to Neo4j automatically. To verify: after creating a new timeseries channel via any path, run `MATCH (at:AnnotatableTimeseries)-[:has_annotation]->(a:SemanticAnnotation) WHERE a.source = 'ts-channel-metadata' RETURN at.appId, a.propertyIRI, a.valueName LIMIT 10` in Neo4j Browser → should return rows. To see that legacy channels are unaffected: `SELECT count(*) FROM channel_metadata` should still equal `SELECT count(*) FROM timeseries`. |

| **AI1v-Phase-1 — channel unit auto-inference (deterministic suffix matcher)** | `aidocs/16` AI1v-Phase-1. New `ChannelUnitInferenceService` (@ApplicationScoped CDI bean) ports the Python recovery script's `SUFFIX_TO_QUDT`, `PREFIX_TO_QUDT`, and `WELDING_CAP_TAIL` tables into Java. Wired into `TimeseriesSemanticDualWriteService.dualWriteChannelMetadata(...)`: every channel created after this lands a `:SemanticAnnotation{propertyIRI:'urn:shepard:unit'}` with a QUDT canonical `valueIRI`, `sourceMode='ai'`, `source='ts-channel-unit-suffix'`, and a tier-derived `confidence` ∈ {1.0, 0.9, 0.85}. Two new `Constants` entries: `TS_UNIT_PREDICATE`, `ANNOTATION_SOURCE_TS_CHANNEL_UNIT_SUFFIX`. Ambiguous fields are logged at WARN and skipped (deferred to AI1v-Phase-2 / AI plugin). | **ADDITIVE** | **No new REST endpoints**, no `/shepard/api/...` surface touched, no schema change, no migration. The only on-disk change is one additional `:SemanticAnnotation` per new channel whose `field` name carries a recognisable unit token (≈84% of MFFD+LUMEN channels). Pre-existing channels are NOT retroactively annotated by the backend (intentional — the Python recovery script handles backfill for the live instance). | No migration file (application-layer only). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; channels created after restart get the unit annotation automatically. To verify: create a new channel with `field='tcp_x_mm'` via the timeseries upload path, then run `MATCH (at:AnnotatableTimeseries)-[:has_annotation]->(a:SemanticAnnotation) WHERE a.propertyIRI='urn:shepard:unit' AND a.source='ts-channel-unit-suffix' RETURN at.appId, a.valueIRI, a.confidence LIMIT 5` → should return rows with `valueIRI='http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/MilliM'`. To **backfill existing un-annotated channels** on an instance that pre-dates this row, run `examples/mffd-showcase/scripts/recovery/annotate-channel-axes-and-units.py --all-containers`. |

| **TS-SEMANTIC-REST — v2 REST + MCP surface for channel semantic annotations** | `aidocs/16` TS-SEMANTIC-REST. Three new endpoints under `/v2/timeseries-containers/{containerId}/channels/{channelShepardId}/annotations`: `GET` (list), `POST` (create, requires Write), `DELETE /{annotationId}` (delete, requires Write). Channel addressed by UUID v7 `channelShepardId` (Postgres `shepard_id`). New `AnnotatableTimeseriesDAO.findByAppId(String)`. Three new methods on `AnnotatableTimeseriesService`. Two new MCP tools: `list_channel_annotations` + `create_channel_annotation`. Frontend stub: `PlaceholderFragmentPane slug="ts-channel-annotations"` in the timeseries container page. | **ADDITIVE** | **Three new `/v2/` endpoints** only. No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. No schema change, no Neo4j migration. Channels that pre-date TS-SEMANTIC-01 (no `AnnotatableTimeseries` node) return empty on GET and 404 on POST. | No migration file. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the new endpoints become available. To verify: `GET /v2/timeseries-containers/{id}/channels/{shepardId}/annotations` → 200 `[]` on a known channel. |

| **TS-OPT1 — SQL pre-aggregation guard for LTTB** | `aidocs/16` TS-OPT1. New `TimeseriesService.getDataPointsLttbOptimised()` method + `TimeseriesDataPointRepository.queryPreAggregated()` + `TimeseriesDataPointRepository.choosePreaggBucketNs()`. For large time windows (bucket size ≥ 1 ms after dividing window by `target×5`), routes to a TimescaleDB `time_bucket()` pre-aggregation query returning only `target×5` rows before LTTB reduces them further to `max_points`. For small windows or non-numeric value types, falls back transparently to the raw row fetch. Exposed via `GET /v2/timeseries-containers/{containerId}/channels/{shepardId}/data?downsample=lttb&max_points=N`. No new schema, no new config keys. | **ZERO** | **Additive read-path optimisation** — same endpoint, same response shape, faster for large windows. No schema change, no config key, no `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. Zero blast radius for existing callers. | — (no migration — pure service-layer change; no Flyway script required). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the optimised code path activates automatically when `?downsample=lttb` is requested on a large window. Verify: request a wide time window with `?downsample=lttb&max_points=200` and observe ≤200 data points returned faster than the non-optimised path. |

| **TS-OPT2 — Multi-channel bulk raw data endpoint + TS-OPT2-POOL-FIX** | `aidocs/16` TS-OPT2. New `POST /v2/timeseries-containers/{containerId}/channels/data/bulk` endpoint. Accepts `BulkChannelDataRequestIO` (list of up to 200 `shepardIds`, `start`/`end` nanosecond window). A single bulk `shepardId IN ?1` JPQL query resolves all channels, then data is fetched sequentially (avoids connection-pool storm). Returns `List<TimeseriesWithDataPoints>`. Also ships `TsChannelResolver.bulkFindByShepardIds()` and `TimeseriesService.getManyDataPointsByEntities()`. **TS-OPT2-POOL-FIX (commit a76bfa4e):** `TimeseriesService.getManyTimeseriesWithDataPoints()` previously used `parallelStream()` which fired O(N) concurrent DB connections, exhausting PgBouncer `pool_size=20` under concurrent chart requests. Fixed to sequential `stream().map().collect()`. | **ZERO** | **One new `/v2/` POST endpoint** — no `/shepard/api/...` surface touched, no migration, no config keys. The sequential-stream fix is a correctness/stability improvement under concurrent load that takes effect immediately on restart. | — (no migration — additive endpoint + sequential-stream fix). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the bulk endpoint becomes available at `POST /v2/timeseries-containers/{containerId}/channels/data/bulk`. Smoke-test: POST `{"shepardIds": ["<uuid1>", "<uuid2>"], "start": 0, "end": 9999999999999999999}` → 200 with array of `{timeseries, points}` objects. The pool-fix takes effect on all concurrent chart requests immediately. |

| **TS-OPT3-COPY — COPY-protocol bulk ingest endpoint** | `aidocs/16` TS-OPT3-COPY (commit `69f26cc2`). New `POST /v2/timeseries-containers/{containerId}/channels/{shepardId}/data/ingest` endpoint. Uses the PostgreSQL COPY protocol via a direct TimescaleDB connection (bypassing PgBouncer so COPY is unobstructed). Accepts a NDJSON body of `{ts_ns, value}` records. 3–5× faster than `VALUES` INSERT for bulk historical loads. Channel must pre-exist (404 if unknown shepardId). No `ON CONFLICT` handling — timestamps must be unique. Designed for import scripts doing historical bulk loads. | **ZERO** | **One new `/v2/` POST endpoint** — no `/shepard/api/...` surface touched, no migration, no config keys. Operator note: the endpoint connects directly to TimescaleDB (not PgBouncer); ensure the backend's direct TimescaleDB connection is reachable. | — (no migration — additive endpoint). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the endpoint becomes available. Channel must be created before ingesting. Smoke-test: `POST /v2/timeseries-containers/{containerId}/channels/{shepardId}/data/ingest` with `Content-Type: application/x-ndjson` body `{"ts_ns":1000,"value":1.5}\n{"ts_ns":2000,"value":2.5}` → 204 No Content on success. |

| **#75 — `scripts/upgrade-from-v5.sh` operator upgrade wizard** | New `scripts/upgrade-from-v5.sh` — a pure-bash interactive upgrade wizard for operators upgrading from upstream v5.2.0 to this fork. Features: dry-run by default (safe; prints every action it would take), `--apply` flag to execute steps, `--no-prompt` for unattended runs, tee'd log to `upgrade-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.log`, Docker / compose availability pre-flight, version endpoint probe, env-var audit (required + fork-introduced), backup recommendation with interactive gate in `--apply` mode, compose-diff summary (new Garage / HSDS / Prometheus / Grafana sidecars), new config-key table (G1-cred, A3c, KIP1h, plugin defaults, HDF, S3, AI), runtime enumeration of Neo4j `.cypher` and SQL `.sql` forward migrations with rollback hints, interactive per-breaking-change prompts (G1-cred / C3 / A0 / A3c / KIP1h / Unhide default / V6 plugin compat), `docker compose pull && up -d` apply phase, post-upgrade checklist (health probe, migration verification, bootstrap-token flow). | **ZERO** | No API or config change — ships a new script at `scripts/upgrade-from-v5.sh`. | — | Recommended usage: `./scripts/upgrade-from-v5.sh --log-dir /opt/shepard/logs` (dry-run, safe to run any time); then re-run with `--apply` when ready. |

| **DB-OPT5 — DataObject list payload diet (`?fields=` + default-trim)** | `aidocs/16` DB-OPT5. The v2 list endpoint `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects` gained two payload-diet knobs: (a) a default-trim that drops `description`, `attributes`, and the three deprecated `int` sibling counts (`timeseriesReferenceCount`, `fileBundleCount`, `structuredDataReferenceCount`) on the wire; and (b) an explicit `?fields=foo,bar` flat-CSV allow-list (GitHub REST convention) for fine-grained shape control. Identity fields (`id`, `appId`, `name`) are always included; unknown field names short-circuit the request with HTTP 400 before any DB hit. Pass `?include=full` to opt back into the pre-diet shape (transitional safety valve). New `X-Shepard-Payload-Diet` response header surfaces the active mode (`default-trim` / `full` / `fields`). Implementation: per-request `ObjectMapper.copy()` + `SimpleBeanPropertyFilter` keyed off a new `@JsonFilter("doListV2")` annotation on `DataObjectListItemV2IO`; reflection over the IO class hierarchy keeps the allow-list in lock-step with the wire shape (no hand-maintained allow-list to forget). Frontend `usePagedDataObjects` composable now sends the panel-shaped `?fields=` allow-list (13 fields covering everything `CollectionDataObjectsPanel.vue` renders). Measured savings on a single-DO synthetic payload (600-char description + 12-attr map): default-trim 75.8% smaller than `?include=full`, panel `?fields=` 86.6% smaller, minimal-fields (`?fields=appId,name,createdAt`) 93.8% smaller. End-to-end on a 5-DO page (800-char description + 12-attr per DO): default-trim 77.8% smaller. Unit tests: 10 (`DataObjectListFieldFilterTest`) + 8 new (`DataObjectV2RestTest`). | **AWARE** | **Wire-shape change on `/v2/collections/{appId}/data-objects`** — fields visible on the wire shrink by default for callers that don't pass `?include=full`. The three dropped deprecated `int` sibling counts were already deprecated in `DataObjectIO` (commit `6774744f3` — API2); this drop removes them from the v2 list-endpoint response unless `?include=full` is set. `/shepard/api/...` paths are **untouched** — upstream v5.2.0 clients hitting v1 paths see no change. Existing v2 callers that read `description` or `attributes` from the list response must either opt back in with `?include=full` or switch to the detail endpoint (`GET /v2/collections/{appId}/data-objects/{appId}`). Frontend already updated to opt back in via `?fields=` listing the exact 13 fields it renders. | — (no migration — pure serialisation-layer change). | **No action required for upgrade** unless you have an external v2 caller that reads `description`, `attributes`, or the deprecated `int` counts from the list response. Three options for that caller: (1) prefer the detail endpoint (cheap, one round-trip per DO), (2) request `?fields=` explicitly listing what's needed (best — narrowest wire), or (3) pass `?include=full` as a stop-gap. Verify: `curl -H "X-API-KEY: …" "$BASE/v2/collections/$APPID/data-objects?size=2"` returns rows without `description`/`attributes`; same URL with `&include=full` returns the pre-diet shape. Diagnostic script: `examples/mffd-showcase/scripts/diagnostics/measure-payload-diet.sh`. |

| PERF4c | Grafana k6 dashboard + perf README | additive | none | — | `infrastructure/grafana/dashboards/shepard-perf4-endpoints.json`, `scripts/perf/README.md` |

## Out-of-band changes (not yet visible to admins)

Several entries are **design done** in `aidocs/` but not yet on `main`.
They appear here only to flag the upgrade-path implications for when
they land:

| ID | What it'll do | Operator impact when it lands |
|---|---|---|
| **L2b** | Backfill `appId` on all pre-L2a rows via `V12__Backfill_appId.cypher` (chunked, idempotent) | Auto-migrates on next startup. Logs progress. Aborts startup on collision (post-A1e fail-fast). Rollback: `V12_R`. |
| **C5** | Cypher injection fix (parameter binding + property-name whitelist) | Internal only. No admin action. |
| **L2d** | `/v2/` API exposes `appId` natively | **AWARE** — additive new versioned API surface. `/v1/` paths keep `long` for ≥ 2 minor releases. |
| **L2e** | Drops `/v1/` long-id paths; flips permission-cache key shape; drops legacy TimescaleDB `containerId: long` column | **BREAKING** — clients on `/v1/` numeric IDs must migrate to `/v2/` `appId` strings. Generated clients (Python / TS / Java) will be regenerated as part of the deprecation window; pin to the previous client version if you need time. The TimescaleDB column drop will ship with a one-shot cross-DB backfill job; the operator runbook will land in this doc when the change does. |

## Image-build notes

The published `registry.gitlab.com/dlr-shepard/shepard/backend:5.2.0`
and `.../frontend:5.2.0` images on GitLab are the **upstream** tags;
this repo does not currently publish images. Two paths to deploy
from this repo:

- **Build locally.** `docker compose build` from `infrastructure/`
  builds both backend and frontend against your local maven cache.
  Reproducible per `Dockerfile`.
- **CI-built tags.** Published to GHCR via `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
  (the `build-images` job, runs on every push to `main` once both
  test gates pass):
  - `ghcr.io/noheton/shepard-backend:latest` / `…:sha-<7>` / `…:v<x.y.z>`
  - `ghcr.io/noheton/shepard-frontend:latest` / `…:sha-<7>` / `…:v<x.y.z>`

  To deploy from CI-built images: ensure the override file's
  `SHEPARD_BACKEND_IMAGE` / `SHEPARD_FRONTEND_IMAGE` default values
  (both `:latest`) are in effect, then `docker compose pull && docker
  compose up -d` from `infrastructure/`.

For the **ARM64** caveat (Apple Silicon, ARM-based cloud VMs), the
upstream images are amd64-only; either rebuild via
`docker buildx --platform linux/arm64` or run with QEMU emulation.

## Migration tests — deferred

Per the standing rule in `CLAUDE.md`, migration tests are tracked
but not blocking. The matrix below records what regression tests
should land alongside each migration:

| Migration | Required test | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `V11__Add_appId_unique_constraints.cypher` | Apply against a pre-L2a dump; assert all 28 constraints created; assert pre-existing nulls don't conflict | **deferred** — covered indirectly by `AppIdGeneratorTest` + DAO seam tests. Add explicit constraint-application IT after L2b ships. |
| `V12__Backfill_appId.cypher` (forthcoming, L2b) | Apply against a pre-L2a dump with rows of every label; assert post-backfill `MATCH (n) WHERE n.appId IS NULL RETURN count(n) = 0` | will ship with L2b |
| `V13__Add_appId_constraint_Role.cypher` | Apply against a pre-A0 dump; assert constraint created on `Role` label | **deferred** — covered indirectly by `RoleTest` + the `OrphanPermissionsBackfillContextTest` covering the V14 pre-flight checks. Add explicit constraint-application IT alongside V11/V12 once they land. |
| `V14__Backfill_orphan_permissions.cypher` | Apply against a pre-A0 dump with one orphan; assert post-backfill `(orphan)-[:has_permissions]->(p:Permissions)-[:owned_by]->(:User {username: $defaultOwner})`; assert idempotency on second apply (zero new edges) | **partial** — `OrphanPermissionsBackfillContextTest` (4 tests) covers the Java pre-hook's refusal logic + happy path with mocked driver. Apply-the-Cypher-against-a-live-Neo4j IT deferred to the migration-test profile alongside V12. |
| `V30__Add_appId_constraint_UnhideConfig.cypher` | Apply against a pre-UH1a dump; assert the constraint is created; assert `UnhideConfigService#seedIfNeeded()` minting a node post-migration succeeds (the unique-appId constraint tolerates pre-existing nulls so the migration is safe on graphs that already have other `:UnhideConfig`-labelled nodes from earlier experiments) | **deferred** — covered indirectly by `UnhideConfigServiceTest`'s seed-idempotent paths; live-Cypher IT deferred alongside the V11 / V22 / V25 obligations. |
| `V35__Add_appId_constraint_TimeseriesAnnotation.cypher` | Apply against a pre-TA1a dump (zero `:TimeseriesAnnotation` rows); assert constraint created; assert that `POST /v2/timeseries-references/{appId}/annotations` minting a node post-migration succeeds and that a duplicate `appId` on insert is rejected with a unique-constraint violation | **deferred** — covered indirectly by `TimeseriesAnnotationRestTest` (15 unit tests); live-Cypher IT deferred alongside the V11/V25/V34 obligations. |
| `V36__Backfill_revision.cypher` | Apply against a pre-V2a dump with `:VersionableEntity` rows missing `revision`; assert `MATCH (n:VersionableEntity) WHERE n.revision IS NULL RETURN count(n) = 0` post-migration; assert idempotency on second run (zero additional writes) | **deferred** — covered indirectly by `VersionableEntityTest#revisionIncrementTest` and the `BasicEntityIOTest#testConversionVersionable_revisionIncrement` unit tests; live-Cypher IT deferred alongside the V34/V35 obligations. |
| `V37__Backfill_timeReference.cypher` | Apply against a pre-TM1a dump with `:TimeseriesReference` rows; assert `MATCH (r:TimeseriesReference) WHERE r.timeReference IS NULL RETURN count(r) = 0` post-migration; assert idempotency on second run; assert `V37_R` rollback removes only `WALL_CLOCK` values and leaves any `EXPERIMENT_RELATIVE` values intact | **deferred** — covered indirectly by `TimeseriesReferenceV2RestTest` (8 unit tests) and `TimeseriesReferenceIOTest` (2 IO tests); live-Cypher IT deferred alongside V35/V36 obligations. |
| `V40__Add_appId_constraint_Snapshot.cypher` | Apply against a pre-V2b dump (zero `:Snapshot` rows); assert constraint created; assert that `POST /v2/collections/{appId}/snapshots` minting a node post-migration succeeds and that a duplicate `appId` on insert is rejected with a unique-constraint violation | **deferred** — covered indirectly by `SnapshotRestTest` (22 unit tests) and `SnapshotServiceTest` (7 unit tests); live-Cypher IT deferred alongside the V35–V39 obligations. |
| `V41__Add_appId_constraint_PayloadVersion.cypher` | Apply against a pre-PV1a dump (zero `:PayloadVersion` rows); assert constraint created; assert that uploading a file via `FileContainerService.createFile()` post-migration mints a `:PayloadVersion` node and that a duplicate `appId` is rejected with a unique-constraint violation | **deferred** — covered indirectly by `PayloadVersionDAOTest` (6 unit tests), `PayloadVersionRestTest` (4 unit tests), and `FileContainerServiceTest` PV1a cases (3 unit tests); live-Cypher IT deferred alongside the V35–V40 obligations. |

| **DX7 — toggle `source` field on `GET /v2/admin/features`** | `aidocs/16` DX7. `FeatureToggleIO` gains a new `source` field (`"default"` / `"config"` / `"runtime"`). Additive: existing clients ignoring the field see no breakage. No new endpoints, no schema change, no config keys. `FeatureToggleEntry` gains internal `propertyKey` and `volatile boolean overriddenAtRuntime` fields — not visible on the wire. | **AWARE** | **Additive change to `/v2/admin/features` response shape**: the `source` string field is new. Clients that parse the response into a typed DTO with strict-mode deserialisation must add the field to their DTO. Clients that deserialise loosely (default Jackson / JSON-B behaviour) are unaffected. No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. | — (no migration) | **No action required for upgrade.** |
| **F4 — JWT `iat`-keyed permission cache** | `aidocs/16` F4. `isAccessTypeAllowedForUser` changed from 3-arg to 4-arg `(entityId, accessType, username, jwtIat)`. The 4th element (`jwtIat` = JWT `iat` claim, seconds-since-epoch) is extracted by `JWTFilter` from the JWT's `issuedAt` field; defaults to `0L` when no JWT is present (API key auth, cache warmer, REST resources without `AuthenticationContext`). The `CompositeCacheKey` is now a 4-tuple. No new config keys, no schema change, no new endpoints. | **ZERO** | **Transparent to operators and clients.** The cache key is internal to `PermissionsService` — the wire shape of any endpoint is unchanged. Existing JWTs without an `iat` claim continue to work (they get `jwtIat=0L`, which is the previous behaviour). The change prevents stale permission cache hits across JWT rotation events (e.g. role change → new token with later `iat`). | — (no migration) | **No action required for upgrade.** |
| **F5 — Fail-closed Neo4j guard in `PermissionsService.isAllowed()`** | `aidocs/16` F5. When `DbHealthRegistry.isCurrentlyDown(DatabaseKind.NEO4J)` returns `true`, `isAllowed()` immediately returns `false` without calling the DAO. Before this change, a degraded Neo4j could yield a 500 or an exception-propagated denial; now the denial is consistent and explicit. Requires the A1b `DbHealthRegistry` (already shipped). | **ZERO** | **Transparent to operators.** The change is a safety rail: in normal operation Neo4j is UP and the guard is never triggered. During a Neo4j outage the effective behaviour changes from "500 / exception-propagated" to "clean 403 Forbidden". No wire-shape change, no new config keys, no schema change. | — (no migration) | **No action required for upgrade.** |
| **QW3 + QW6 + UI8 — frontend-only UI quick wins** | `aidocs/16` QW3 / QW6 / UI8. Three purely frontend changes: (1) **QW3** — JupyterHub URL field added to `ProfilePane.vue`; reads/writes `editor.preferredJupyter` via the existing `GET/PATCH /v2/users/me/preferences` endpoints (no new backend surface). (2) **QW6** — "Instance Health" pane added to the admin page; reads the existing `GET /v2/admin/metrics-summary` endpoint (A3b1, already shipped) via a new `AdminMetricsApi.ts` backend-client wrapper; silently hidden for non-admins (403 guard). (3) **UI8** — "Download as RO-Crate" button added to the collection detail page; triggers an authenticated blob download via the existing upstream-compatible `GET /shepard/api/collections/{id}/export` endpoint (no new backend surface). | **ZERO** | **Frontend-only. No new backend endpoints, no new config keys, no new Neo4j labels or migrations.** All three changes consume already-shipped backend surfaces. An operator upgrading from upstream sees the new UI elements; clients not using the frontend are unaffected. | — (no migration) | **No action required for upgrade.** Pull-and-restart; the new UI elements appear immediately. |
| **CC1b — "Referenced by" panel wired with real DataObject data** | `aidocs/16` CC1b. Three new `/v2/` endpoints: `GET /v2/file-containers/{id}/linked-data-objects`, `GET /v2/timeseries-containers/{id}/linked-data-objects`, `GET /v2/structured-data-containers/{id}/linked-data-objects`. Each accepts the numeric OGM container id, gates on Read permission via the existing `getContainer(id)` path, then queries Neo4j for `DataObject -[:has_reference]->()-[:is_in_container]-> Container` and returns `List<DataObjectIO>`. Frontend "Referenced by" expansion panels on the three container detail pages replaced with a real DataObject list (loading spinner, empty state, and v-list with links to the dataset pages). No new config keys, no schema migration, no `/shepard/api/` surface touched. Three new DAO methods + 6 unit tests. **Bug fix 2026-05-19** (commit `9bd0200b`): all three container DAOs were using `[:has_payload]` instead of `[:is_in_container]` for this traversal. `[:has_payload]` resolves to measurement/file data nodes, not to the container — so the "Referenced by" panel always showed zero results. Corrected in `TimeseriesContainerDAO`, `StructuredDataContainerDAO`, and `FileContainerDAO`. No schema change; pure query correction. | **ZERO** | **Three new `/v2/` endpoints only — no upstream surface touched.** An admin upgrading from upstream will see the "Referenced by" panel on container detail pages now populated with real data instead of the static placeholder. No operator action required. | — (no migration) | **No action required for upgrade.** |
| **U1e — user avatar upload/delete/render** | `aidocs/16` U1e. Three new `/v2/` endpoints: `PUT /v2/users/me/avatar` (multipart, 2 MB cap, JPEG/PNG/GIF/WebP), `DELETE /v2/users/me/avatar`, `GET /v2/users/{appId}/avatar` (public, no auth required). Bytes stored in MongoDB collection `userAvatars` keyed by `userAppId` — no Neo4j entity changes, no migrations, no `/shepard/api/` surface touched. `ProfilePane.vue` gains avatar display with initials fallback + upload/delete controls. | **ZERO** | **Three new `/v2/` endpoints only.** No new config keys, no schema migration, no upstream surface touched. | — (no migration) | **No action required for upgrade.** |
| **P10a — `POST /v2/sql/timeseries` JSON DSL bulk-read endpoint** | `aidocs/16` P10a. One new `/v2/` endpoint: `POST /v2/sql/timeseries`. Accepts a `SqlQuerySpec` JSON body (select items, table, where clause with mandatory time range + container-id filter, optional group-by / order-by / limit). Compiles to a `PreparedStatement` with all user values bound as `?` parameters; column/table names from Java enums only; alias validated against `[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]{0,62}`. Permission-gated via `PermissionsService.filterAllowedForUser`. Returns streaming JSON `{"rows":[…],"truncated":bool}`. `SqlTimeseriesRest` has package-private `executeQuery()` method so permission tests bypass the feature-toggle gate — all 14 unit tests pass in standalone JUnit runner. Two new config keys: `shepard.timeseries.sql.enabled=false` (feature flag, default OFF — returns 404 when off), `shepard.timeseries.sql.max-rows=1000000`. No schema migration, no Neo4j labels, no `/shepard/api/` surface touched. | **ZERO** | **One new `/v2/` endpoint, gated off by default.** No operator action required for upgrade; the endpoint returns 404 until `shepard.timeseries.sql.enabled=true` is set. Two new config keys are additive (safe to ignore). | — (no migration) | **No action required for upgrade.** Set `shepard.timeseries.sql.enabled=true` to opt into the endpoint after reviewing the permission model in `aidocs/platform/29-p10-implementation-design.md §4`. |
| **P10b — `POST /v2/sql/timeseries` multi-format content negotiation + streaming caps** | `aidocs/16` P10b. Same `/v2/` endpoint as P10a; no new path added. Extends the endpoint with: (1) `text/csv` (RFC 4180, CRLF, UTF-8 no BOM; `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="timeseries.csv"`); (2) `application/x-ndjson` (one JSON object per line, `\n` delimiter); (3) default for `Accept: */*` changed to `text/csv`. New config key: `shepard.timeseries.sql.max-duration=PT60S` (PostgreSQL `statement_timeout`; HTTP 504 with JSON error body on timeout via `QueryTimeoutExceptionMapper`). Row cap enforcement in writer loop. HTTP trailer `x-shepard-truncated: true` emitted via VertX `HttpServerResponse.putTrailer()` when row cap hits. `Statement.cancel()` called on client-disconnect `IOException`. `WriteResult` record + `QueryTimeoutExceptionMapper` added. `docs/reference/timeseries-reference.md` created. **Admin-config deferral:** `max-rows` and `max-duration` are deploy-time config only in P10b; a runtime-mutable `:SqlTimeseriesConfig` Neo4j singleton + `GET/PATCH /v2/admin/sql-timeseries/config` is deferred to P10c (they warrant runtime flip without restart, and P10c already involves the admin surface). | **ZERO** | **No new endpoints; no new required config keys; no schema migration.** One new optional config key (`shepard.timeseries.sql.max-duration=PT60S`) is additive. The endpoint still returns 404 when disabled. Operators who already set `shepard.timeseries.sql.enabled=true` get CSV and NDJSON support automatically; existing JSON clients continue to work. | — (no migration) | **No action required for upgrade.** If `max-duration` should differ from the 60-second default, add `shepard.timeseries.sql.max-duration=<ISO-8601 duration>` to your deployment config. Runtime-configurable admin endpoint for these caps ships in P10c. |
| **ROR1 — instance-level Research Organization Registry config** | `aidocs/16` ROR1. New `:InstanceRorConfig` Neo4j singleton (seeded blank on first start, idempotent). `GET /v2/admin/instance/ror` returns the current config; `PATCH /v2/admin/instance/ror` (RFC 7396 merge-patch, `instance-admin` gated) updates `rorId` and `organizationName`. `rorUrl` is computed as `https://ror.org/<rorId>` in the IO layer — never stored. `rorId` validated as 1-9 alphanumeric chars (lenient; canonicality enforcement deferred). Frontend: `InstanceRorPane.vue` admin pane (`mdi-office-building` icon, `AdminFragments.INSTANCE_ROR` menu entry). No deploy-time config keys. Migration: `V42__Add_appId_constraint_InstanceRorConfig.cypher` (additive, idempotent). 6 unit tests. CLI (`shepard-admin instance ror {status,set}`) queued. | **ZERO** | **Additive. Two new `/v2/admin/instance/ror` endpoints, one new Neo4j label, one additive migration.** No `/shepard/api/` surface touched. No new required config keys. The migration creates a uniqueness constraint on the new `:InstanceRorConfig` label — zero rows exist on any pre-ROR1 graph, safe on all upgrade paths. | `V42__Add_appId_constraint_InstanceRorConfig.cypher` — single-statement, idempotent. Test obligation: live-Cypher IT deferred alongside V35–V41 obligations. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V42 applies on first start. The singleton is seeded blank. To configure: `PATCH /v2/admin/instance/ror` with `{"rorId":"<suffix>","organizationName":"<name>"}` (instance-admin), or use the admin UI "Research Organization" pane. |
| **P10c — SQL timeseries runtime admin config + feature flag default-on** | `aidocs/16` P10c. New `:SqlTimeseriesConfig` Neo4j singleton (seeded from deploy-time defaults on first start). `GET /v2/admin/sql-timeseries/config` returns `{maxRows, maxDuration}`; `PATCH /v2/admin/sql-timeseries/config` (RFC 7396 merge-patch, `instance-admin` gated) tunes `maxRows` (must be `> 0`) and `maxDuration` (ISO-8601, e.g. `"PT2M"`) at runtime without restart. `SqlTimeseriesRest` reads effective caps from `SqlTimeseriesConfigService.effectiveMaxRows()` / `.effectiveMaxDurationIso()` — null singleton fields fall back to the deploy-time defaults in `application.properties`. Migration: `V43__Add_appId_constraint_SqlTimeseriesConfig.cypher` (additive, idempotent). Feature flag `shepard.timeseries.sql.enabled` flipped from `false` to `true` in `application.properties` — the `POST /v2/sql/timeseries` endpoint is **now active by default**. 10 unit tests. | **AWARE** | **The `POST /v2/sql/timeseries` endpoint is now enabled by default.** Operators upgrading from P10a/P10b who want the endpoint disabled must set `shepard.timeseries.sql.enabled=false` explicitly in their deployment config. One new `/v2/admin/sql-timeseries/config` endpoint family (2 paths). One new Neo4j label `:SqlTimeseriesConfig` + `V43` migration (additive, idempotent, zero rows to migrate). No `/shepard/api/` surface touched. | `V43__Add_appId_constraint_SqlTimeseriesConfig.cypher` — single-statement, idempotent. Test obligation: live-Cypher IT deferred alongside V35–V42 obligations. | **Action required: review the feature-flag flip.** If you are upgrading from upstream (no prior P10a/P10b deployment) or from a P10a/P10b deployment that had `shepard.timeseries.sql.enabled=false`, the `POST /v2/sql/timeseries` endpoint will be **active after this upgrade**. Set `shepard.timeseries.sql.enabled=false` to keep it disabled. Runtime caps are configurable via `PATCH /v2/admin/sql-timeseries/config` (instance-admin); the new admin endpoint is documented in `docs/reference/timeseries-reference.md §Admin config`. |

| **P16 Phase 1 — `shepard` Python convenience client** | `aidocs/16` P16. New package `clients/python/shepard/` — hand-written thin wrapper around the generated `shepard-client`. Purely additive client-side library: `Client` + 13 domain proxy attributes + `ShepardError` hierarchy + `iter_pages` helper + `to_pandas` / `to_excel` / `ro_crate` workflow helpers gated behind PEP 508 extras. 70 unit tests; `ro_crate` auth fixed. No backend changes, no new endpoints, no new config keys, no schema migration, no `/shepard/api/` surface touched. | **ZERO** | **Client library only — no operator action required.** Existing clients (upstream-generated or hand-written) are completely unaffected.
