| **N1e — semantic term autocomplete in the annotation picker** | `aidocs/16` N1e. New `GET /v2/semantic/terms/search?q=…&limit=…` endpoint (`SemanticTermSearchRest`) searches n10s `:Resource` nodes across `rdfs__label` / `skos__prefLabel` / `skos__altLabel` / URI and returns `TermSuggestionIO` records. Auth: any authenticated user (matching N1f / SemanticSparqlRest posture — no per-entity permission check). Limit capped at 50. Tries fulltext index `resource_labels` (V44, idempotent) first; falls back to CONTAINS scan if absent; returns 200 + empty array when no ontology data loaded — never errors. Frontend: `AddAnnotationDialog.vue` IRI fields replaced with `v-combobox` backed by `useTermSearch()` composable (300ms debounce, min 2 chars; users can still type a raw IRI directly). New endpoint surface is `/v2/` (this fork's development surface, not the frozen `/shepard/api/` surface). 11 unit tests in `SemanticTermSearchRestTest`. | **ZERO** | **Additive. One new `/v2/semantic/terms/search` endpoint, one new fulltext index (V44), one new frontend composable, UX change in `AddAnnotationDialog.vue`.** No `/shepard/api/` surface touched. No new config keys. V44 is idempotent (IF NOT EXISTS); safe on any pre-N1e graph. | `V44__Add_fulltext_index_Resource_labels.cypher` — `CREATE FULLTEXT INDEX resource_labels IF NOT EXISTS FOR (n:Resource) ON EACH [n.rdfs__label, n.skos__prefLabel, n.skos__altLabel]`. Single-statement, idempotent. Rollback: `DROP INDEX resource_labels IF EXISTS`. Test obligation: live-Cypher IT deferred alongside V35–V43 obligations. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V44 applies on first start. The endpoint is immediately usable; if n10s is not loaded (e.g. n10s plugin absent), the endpoint returns 200 + empty array. The annotation picker now shows ontology term suggestions as you type — users can still paste a raw IRI directly. Reference: `docs/reference/semantic-repositories.md` updated with term-search section. |
| **DI1 — server-enforced safe-delete on container DELETEs** | New `DELETE /v2/{type}-containers/{id}` endpoints added to the existing `…LinkedDataObjectsRest` resources for `timeseries-containers`, `file-containers`, `structured-data-containers`. Without `?force=true` the endpoint refuses with `409 Conflict` + body `{referenceCount: N, sampleDataObjectAppIds: [...]}` (capped at 10) when active references exist. With `?force=true` it deletes the container and orphans surviving references (matching the upstream `/shepard/api/` behaviour). New `de.dlr.shepard.v2.integrity.SafeDeleteConflict` record is the wire shape. The frontend's container accessors switched to the new `/v2/` endpoint with `force=true` (the UI's existing in-confirm-dialog "N data objects reference this container" warning is the user-consent step). External clients calling without `force=true` get the server-side protection. **`/shepard/api/{type}Containers/{id}` DELETE is unchanged** — still orphans references silently (upstream wire frozen). | **ZERO** for the legacy surface; **AWARE** for any non-UI consumer who wants the new safety. | **Three new `/v2/` endpoints + one new wire type (`SafeDeleteConflict`).** No `/shepard/api/` surface touched. No new config keys. No schema migration. | — (no migration). | **No action required for upgrade.** Operators who want server-enforced safety in their own admin scripts: call `DELETE /v2/timeseries-containers/{id}` (note the kebab-case path and `/v2/` prefix — distinct from upstream's `/shepard/api/timeseriesContainers/{id}`) without `?force=true`; handle the 409 response. Smoke-tested at `infrastructure/smoke-test.sh`. |
| **APISIMP-CONT-NS-COLLAPSE-6 + 6b — per-kind safe-delete paths removed; unified `DELETE /v2/containers/{appId}` is the sole safe-delete surface** | `aidocs/16` APISIMP-CONT-NS-COLLAPSE-6 (PR #1950) + APISIMP-CONT-NS-COLLAPSE-6b (fire-54). The three per-kind safe-delete REST resource files (`FileContainerLinkedDataObjectsRest`, `TimeseriesContainerLinkedDataObjectsRest`, `StructuredDataContainerLinkedDataObjectsRest`) and their test classes deleted. The safe-delete logic now lives exclusively in `DELETE /v2/containers/{appId}?force=` via `ContainerKindHandler.findLinkedDataObjectAppIds`. The frontend `safeDeleteContainer.ts` composable migrated to the unified path. **WIRE BREAK (pre-production only):** `DELETE /v2/file-containers/{appId}`, `DELETE /v2/timeseries-containers/{appId}`, `DELETE /v2/structured-data-containers/{appId}` now return 404 (resources deleted). The upstream `/shepard/api/{kind}Containers/{id}` DELETE is unchanged. | **ADDITIVE for upstream compat; PRE-PROD WIRE BREAK** for the `/v2/{kind}-containers/{id}` DELETE paths (those paths were never in upstream 5.2.0 — they were our own DI1 addition). | Any script calling `DELETE /v2/{file,timeseries,structured-data}-containers/{id}[?force=true]` must switch to `DELETE /v2/containers/{id}[?force=true]`. The 409 `SafeDeleteConflict` body shape is unchanged. | — (no migration). | **Operator action:** Update any automation or admin scripts calling `DELETE /v2/{kind}-containers/{appId}` to `DELETE /v2/containers/{appId}`. The new path accepts `?force=true` identically to the old paths. The 409 conflict body shape (`{referenceCount, sampleDataObjectAppIds}`) is unchanged. The frontend is already on the unified path. |
| **SA-CONT — container-level semantic annotations** | New annotation CRUD endpoints for the container entities themselves (not the timeseries channels — those continue to use `TimeseriesAnnotationRest`). Three new REST resources extend the existing `SemanticAnnotationRest` base and reuse `SemanticAnnotationService.getAllAnnotationsByShepardId(long)` (the service was already type-generic). Paths: `GET\|POST /v2/{type}-containers/{id}/annotations`, `DELETE /v2/{type}-containers/{id}/annotations/{annotationId}` for each of `timeseries-containers`, `file-containers`, `structured-data-containers`. The `:has_annotation` Neo4j relationship and `BasicEntity.annotations` field already supported container annotations — only the REST surface was missing. Frontend: new `AnnotatedTimeseriesContainer` / `AnnotatedFileContainer` / `AnnotatedStructuredDataContainer` classes in `composables/annotated.ts` (raw-fetch implementations of the `Annotated` interface). All three container detail pages now show a "Semantic Annotations" expansion panel using the existing `SemanticAnnotationList` + `AddAnnotationButton` components. | **ZERO** | **Six new `/v2/` paths (three GET/POST + three DELETE).** No `/shepard/api/` surface touched. No new config keys. No schema migration (the `:has_annotation` relationship and `:SemanticAnnotation` label already exist). | — (no migration). | **No action required for upgrade.** New container detail pages display the Semantic Annotations panel automatically. Users with Write permission on a container can add/remove annotations from the UI. External clients can also use the new endpoints (e.g. `curl -H "Authorization: Bearer …" $API/v2/file-containers/42/annotations`). Smoke-tested at `infrastructure/smoke-test.sh`. |
| **UI-cache — frontend HTML `Cache-Control: no-store`** | Nitro `routeRules` set on the frontend: `/**` → `Cache-Control: no-store, must-revalidate` (HTML pages); `/_nuxt/**` → `public, max-age=31536000, immutable` (hashed assets); `/docs/**` → `public, max-age=300, must-revalidate`. Caddy stays a pure reverse_proxy — shepard owns cache-control. Before this fix the frontend returned no `Cache-Control` on HTML, letting browsers heuristically cache the index.html and continue loading rotated `_nuxt/<hash>.js` chunks that no longer existed on disk after a deploy. Symptom was "still broken after redeploy" for any user-visible change. | **ZERO** | **Frontend-only header change.** No backend touched, no endpoints added, no config keys, no schema migration. Caddyfile unchanged. | — (no migration). | **No action required for upgrade.** First page load after deploy fetches fresh HTML; hashed chunks continue to cache forever. Operators behind their own CDN/proxy who previously injected aggressive HTML cache headers may want to align with the new posture. |
| **U1f — `appId` on `UserIO` + `/shepard/api/users` carries the field** | The `User` entity already had `appId: UUID v7` (L2a chain); only the IO layer omitted it. `UserIO.appId` field added, ctor copies it, `@Schema(readOnly=true, required=true)`. Hand-edited `backend-client/src/models/User.ts` re-adds the same `readonly appId?: string` field + `FromJSONTyped` carry-through that the upstream OpenAPI generator strips — same pattern previously needed for `Collection`, `TimeseriesReference`, `FileReference`, `StructuredDataReference`. Unblocks the frontend's `ProfilePane.vue` from constructing the avatar GET URL `/v2/users/{appId}/avatar`; without it the upload would succeed but the rendered image fell back to the initials badge. | **ZERO** | **Additive field on existing `/shepard/api/users` response.** Existing upstream clients ignore unknown fields (OpenAPI generator emits non-strict models); the new key is plain JSON. No new endpoints, no config keys, no schema migration (entity property already existed). | — (no migration). | **No action required for upgrade.** External clients that want to address users by their stable `appId` (e.g. for cross-deploy joins, RO-Crate creator-id stability) can now read it directly from `GET /shepard/api/users`. |
| **U1e-public — `GET /v2/users/{appId}/avatar` made unauthenticated** | `PublicEndpointRegistry` gains a third matching mode — `PUBLIC_PATH_PATTERNS: Set<Pattern>` — for paths whose variable segment sits between fixed prefix and suffix. Avatar GET registered as `^/v2/users/[^/]+/avatar$`. Required because the frontend renders the profile image with a bare `<img src=…>` tag which cannot attach an `Authorization` header; prior to this, the GET 401'd from the browser even though the upload had succeeded. The pattern is narrow enough to leave `/v2/users/me/preferences` (and the rest of `/v2/users`) still gated. Surface only: the endpoint returns image bytes only, no PII beyond what `GET /shepard/api/users` already exposes for authenticated callers. | **AWARE** | **Auth surface change: one new unauthenticated GET endpoint (`/v2/users/{appId}/avatar`).** Same posture as the bare AAS well-known and the OpenAPI specs — opaque metadata, no permission walk. No new endpoints, no config keys, no schema migration. | — (no migration). | **Operator action: review the auth surface change** if your deployment fronts shepard with an external auth layer (Zoraxy, oauth2-proxy, etc.) that itself blocks unauthenticated requests. The endpoint should be reachable pre-auth for the profile image to render in any browser; if your reverse proxy refuses it, profile images will show the initials fallback. Add an allow rule for `/v2/users/*/avatar` upstream of shepard. |
| **U1g — ORCID auto-sync from OIDC claim on login** | `Userinfo.orcid` field + `UserFilter` propagation + `UserService.createOrUpdateUser` "fill if missing" semantics. On first login (or when local `User.orcid` is null), shepard reads the `orcid` claim from the IdP's userinfo endpoint and persists it. Existing users who PATCHed their ORCID via `/v2/users/me` are NOT overwritten on subsequent logins. The Keycloak demo realm (`shepard-demo-realm.json`) ships a User-Attribute → claim mapper that exposes the `orcid` user attribute as the `orcid` claim on the frontend-dev client; operators of real OIDC deployments must add an equivalent mapper to their IdP for the auto-sync to actually populate. | **AWARE** | **Behaviour change: on next login, any user whose IdP issues an `orcid` claim and whose local `User.orcid` is null will have their ORCID populated automatically.** No surface change (the field was already on `UserIO`; only the sync source is new). No new endpoints, no new config keys, no schema migration. | — (no migration). | **Operator action: configure your IdP** to expose an `orcid` claim if you want this auto-sync to work. For Keycloak: realm → client → Client scopes → add a "User Attribute" mapper with `User Attribute = orcid`, `Claim Name = orcid`, all three "Add to ID/access/userinfo token" enabled, `Claim JSON Type = String`. Without the mapper the field stays null and users continue to PATCH their ORCID manually. The demo realm JSON in `infrastructure/keycloak/shepard-demo-realm.json` is updated; existing Keycloak databases need the mapper added via admin UI or admin REST API (`POST /admin/realms/{realm}/clients/{client-uuid}/protocol-mappers/models`). |
| **P4c-fix — per-shelf OpenAPI shelves repaired (`/shepard/doc/openapi/v{1,2}.json`)** | `OpenApiPerShelfRest` rewired off the smallrye `OpenApiDocument.INSTANCE` static singleton — under Quarkus 3.x that singleton is cleared after the build-time pass and the runtime model lives in a smallrye-internal holder we have no stable access to from application code. Both shelf endpoints returned 500 "OpenAPI document is not yet initialised." since the Quarkus 3.x bump. New shape: in-process loopback to the still-working combined `/shepard/doc/openapi.json` (SmallRye-served), then JSON-tree filter the `paths` map keys via the existing `OpenApiShelfMembership` predicate. `V1OpenApiFilter` / `V2OpenApiFilter` kept for unit-test fixtures but no longer wired. **Unblocks the Kiota /v2/ client-generation workflow + any LLM/integration consumer pointing at the per-shelf docs.** | **ZERO** | **Restoration of previously broken endpoints; no surface change.** Both endpoints already existed and were public; they just returned 500. Now: GET `/shepard/doc/openapi/v1.json` returns 95 v1 paths, `/v2.json` returns 106 v2 paths, both JSON + `?format=yaml` work. | — (no migration). | **No action required.** External consumers (Kiota, custom LLM tooling, manual `curl` discovery) that hit either endpoint and previously saw 500 now see a proper filtered OpenAPI doc. |

| **L2d-RC37 — per-kind reference counts on `GET /v2/collections/{appId}/data-objects`** | `aidocs/16` #37. `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects` list response upgraded from `DataObjectIO` items to `DataObjectListItemV2IO` items — a subclass that adds three new read-only integer fields: `timeseriesCount`, `fileCount`, `structuredDataCount`. Counts reflect non-deleted references only and are computed in a single Cypher `UNWIND` round-trip (`DataObjectDAO.findRefCountsByAppIds`), not N+1 per DataObject. `fileCount` correctly covers both `:FileReference` (bundles, FR1a) and `:SingletonFileReference` (FR1b singletons). `DataObjectIO` itself is unchanged. New class `de.dlr.shepard.v2.dataobject.io.DataObjectListItemV2IO`. 17 unit tests pass (`DataObjectV2RestTest`), including `listIncludesRefCounts` which exercises the new counts path. | **ZERO** | **Additive wire-shape change on `/v2/` only.** The three new fields appear in the list response only (not in the single-entity `GET /{dataObjectAppId}` response, which continues to return plain `DataObjectIO`). No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched — `DataObjectRest` + `DataObjectIO` are unchanged. No new config keys. No schema migration. | — (no migration). | **No action required for upgrade.** Existing clients that parse the `/v2/` list response should ignore the three new fields (JSON open-world semantics; Jackson default / lenient clients are unaffected). Clients that want per-kind counts can now read `timeseriesCount`, `fileCount`, `structuredDataCount` from each list item without extra round-trips. |

| **TS_CHART_VIEW1 — persisted curated channel view for timeseries containers** | `aidocs/16` TS_CHART_VIEW1. New `GET/PATCH /v2/timeseries-containers/{containerId}/chart-view` endpoint pair. `GET` returns `TimeseriesContainerChartViewIO` (`selectedChannels: List<String>`, `updatedAt`, `updatedBy`); `PATCH` (RFC 7396 merge-patch, Write permission) replaces the curated channel list atomically. Backed by a new `:TimeseriesContainerChartView` Neo4j node linked from `:TimeseriesContainer` via `has_chart_view`. If no chart view has been saved, `GET` returns `204 No Content` (client treats this as "show all channels"). Frontend: `TimeseriesAllChannelsChart.vue` reads the saved view on page load; "Edit channels" opens a checkbox grid; Save calls PATCH; per-session "show all channels" override bypasses the curated view without mutating it. `V45__Add_appId_constraint_TimeseriesContainerChartView.cypher` (additive, idempotent). `useTimeseriesContainerChartView` composable (`frontend/composables/containers/`). | **ZERO** | **Two new `/v2/` endpoints.** One new Neo4j label `:TimeseriesContainerChartView` + `has_chart_view` relationship from `:TimeseriesContainer`. `V45` migration is additive (constraint on a new label; zero pre-existing rows — safe to run on any pre-TS_CHART_VIEW1 graph). No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. No new config keys. | `V45__Add_appId_constraint_TimeseriesContainerChartView.cypher` — single-statement, idempotent. Test obligation: deferred alongside V35–V44. | **No action required for upgrade.** Existing containers start with no saved chart view (`GET` → 204) and show all channels by default. Users with Write permission on a container can curate the channel set via the UI or by calling `PATCH /v2/timeseries-containers/{id}/chart-view` with `{"selectedChannels": ["chan_a", "chan_b"]}`. |

| **CC2 — list containers referenced within a Collection** | `aidocs/16` CC2. New `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/referenced-containers` endpoint (`CollectionContainersRest`). Walks `Collection → DataObject → Reference → Container` and returns distinct container summaries (`ContainerSummaryIO`: `id`, `appId`, `name`, `containerType` ∈ `{TIMESERIES, FILE, STRUCTUREDDATA, BASIC}`). Non-deleted DataObjects only. Auth: Read permission on the Collection (standard `isAccessTypeAllowedForUser` 3-arg path — Collections own their `:Permissions` node). Returns an empty array when no containers are referenced. Frontend: `CollectionContainersPanel.vue` on the Collection page "Referenced Containers" expansion panel — lists containers with type icon and links to the container detail page. `useFetchCollectionContainers` composable. 6 unit tests in `CollectionContainersRestTest`. No migration, no schema change. | **ZERO** | **One new `/v2/` endpoint.** No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. No new config keys. No schema migration. `ContainerSummaryIO` is a new response type — not a change to any existing IO class. | — (no migration). | **No action required for upgrade.** The endpoint returns containers referenced by DataObjects in the collection. External clients can call `GET /v2/collections/{appId}/referenced-containers` (Read permission required) to discover which containers exist within a collection without traversing the DataObject graph manually. |

| **AI1b-fix — `AnomalyDetectionRest` auth gate corrected to walk the Collection permission graph** | Corrects a latent 403 regression in `POST /v2/timeseries-references/{refAppId}/detect-anomalies` (AI1b endpoint). The original auth check called `isAccessTypeAllowedForUser(dataObject.getId(), type, caller, 0L)` — the 4-arg overload that looks up a `:Permissions` node directly attached to the given OGM id. `DataObject` nodes have no own `:Permissions` node (permissions are inherited from the parent `Collection`), so this call always returned `false`, making the endpoint permanently 403 for every caller regardless of their actual Collection permission. Fix: changed to `isAccessAllowedForDataObjectAppId(dataObject.getAppId(), type, caller)`, which walks up to the parent Collection's `:Permissions` node — the same pattern already used in `VideoAnnotationRest` and `TimeseriesAnnotationRest`. Also fixes the equivalent Write-permission check for `createAnnotations=true`. 14 regression unit tests in `AnomalyDetectionRestTest` including a guard that verifies `isAccessTypeAllowedForUser` is never called (preventing re-regression). | **ZERO** | **Bug fix only — no surface change.** The endpoint always existed and responded 403 erroneously; after the fix it responds correctly. No new endpoints, no config keys, no schema migration, no `/shepard/api/` surface touched. | — (no migration). | **No action required for upgrade.** The endpoint now works correctly for any user with Read permission on the parent Collection. Operators who previously worked around the 403 by granting elevated permissions do not need to revoke them; the fix is purely behavioural. |

| **AT1 — extract AI1b anomaly detector into `shepard-plugin-analytics-ts` behind the `TimeseriesAnalytics` SPI** | New `de.dlr.shepard.spi.analytics` package (in core backend) introduces the `TimeseriesAnalytics` SPI, `RemoteTimeseriesAnalytics` sub-interface for the future orchestrator-tier (`shepard-plugin-mlops`), `ExecutionMode` enum (`IN_PROCESS` / `VIA_ORCHESTRATOR`), `JobStatus` + `JobHandle` records, and the `AnalyticsRegistry` CDI bean (mirrors `MinterRegistry`). New `plugins/analytics-ts/` Maven module ships `MADDetector` — the AI1b rolling-median MAD detector extracted **verbatim** (JDK `Arrays.sort` + index-arithmetic median is the trusted primitive; behavioural equivalence enforced by `MADDetectorBehaviouralEquivalenceTest` asserting `Double.doubleToRawLongBits` equality across 100 randomized + multiple hand-picked battery cases). `AnomalyDetectRequestIO` gains an optional `detectorId` field (default `mad-v1`); omitting it is byte-identical to pre-AT1 wire shape (proven by `AnomalyDetectRequestIOWireStabilityTest`). PR-1..PR-3 + PR-4 partial shipped (SPI + plugin scaffold + extracted detector + IO field + tests). PR-4 dispatcher rewire, PR-5 SHACL substrate land, PR-6 anomaly-persistence rewire deferred (SHACL TTL ships in `plugins/analytics-ts/src/main/resources/shapes/mffd-anomaly-annotation.shacl.ttl` with proposed `fair2r:StatisticalPass` sibling-Activity to `fair2r:AuthoringPass` — the parallel SHACL non-TS agent's term-naming call wins at merge). 33 plugin tests (17 detector + 11 behavioural-equivalence + 5 manifest) + 5 wire-stability tests, all green. | **ZERO** | **Additive SPI + new plugin module — zero upgrade impact.** New plugin `shepard-plugin-analytics-ts` defaults **off** (`shepard.plugins.analytics-ts.enabled=false`); operators enable via env var or `PATCH /v2/admin/plugins/analytics-ts/enabled`. With the plugin off, the existing in-tree `AnomalyDetectionService` continues to serve the AI1b endpoint exactly as today (no dispatcher rewire in this phase). New optional `detectorId` field on `AnomalyDetectRequestIO` — omitting it produces byte-identical responses to pre-AT1. No new endpoints; no schema migration; no `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. The `VIA_ORCHESTRATOR` execution-mode tier is a forward-compat stub; attempting to dispatch a detector at that tier throws `UnsupportedOperationException` until `shepard-plugin-mlops` lands. | — (no migration — additive SPI + additive optional IO field; no Neo4j schema delta). | **No action required for upgrade.** The plugin ships with the published images but stays disabled by default. To enable: `PATCH /v2/admin/plugins/analytics-ts/enabled {"enabled":true}` or `SHEPARD_PLUGINS_ANALYTICS_TS_ENABLED=true`. Existing AI1b callers see no behavioural change (omitting `detectorId` is wire-stable). The remaining PR-4 dispatcher rewire (in-tree `AnomalyDetectionService` → `AnalyticsRegistry`) and PR-6 SHACL annotation persistence are deferred — anomaly creation continues to persist to the existing `:TimeseriesAnnotation` graph exactly as before. Test obligation: testcontainer-level wire-stability test against a live `:TimeseriesAnnotation` node graph is deferred alongside V35 / V53 / V54 / V55 obligations. |

| **Feature B — hero image URL on Collections** | New optional `heroImageUrl` (nullable String) field on the `:Collection` Neo4j node. Exposed on the `/v2/` surface (`CollectionIO` returned from `/v2/collections` and `/v2/collections/{appId}`). Settable via `POST /v2/collections` (create) and `PATCH /v2/collections/{appId}` (RFC 7396 merge-patch: `null` clears it, absent leaves it unchanged). Frontend: `<v-img>` hero banner (height 220px, cover fit) renders above the Collection title when `heroImageUrl` is set; hidden when null. Edit dialog shows the URL field in advanced mode only. TypeScript `Collection` interface in `backend-client/src/models/Collection.ts` updated with `heroImageUrl?: string | null`. **Task #131 (v5 wire-fidelity correction):** `CollectionIO` is shared with the v1 `/shepard/api/collections` surface; without `@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)` the field was leaking onto v1 responses as `"heroImageUrl": null` (caught by the #128 v5 fixture corpus). Fixed by annotating the field with `@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)` so it is **omitted from JSON when null**, restoring byte-fidelity with upstream 5.2.0 on `/shepard/api/...`. The same audit pass also tightened `UserIO.orcid`, `UserIO.displayName`, and the five `TimeseriesReferenceIO` AI1c + TM1 fork additions (`qualityScore`, `lastScoredAt`, `timeReference`, `wallClockOffset`, `wallClockOffsetSource`) with the same `@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)` treatment. v5 fixtures (`fixtures/v5/collections/*.json`, `fixtures/v5/users/get-single.response.json`) re-recorded to reflect the absent-when-null shape. | **ZERO** | **Additive nullable field — zero upgrade impact for admins and clients.** Existing `:Collection` nodes lack the property and Neo4j OGM reads the absence as `null` (schema-less store). v1 wire shape now matches upstream byte-for-byte (no `heroImageUrl` key) — strict-schema clients that were previously accepting the additive null key are unaffected because the legitimate values are unchanged. The new field appears only in `/v2/` responses when set. No new endpoints, no config keys, no required schema migration. | `V54__NOOP_heroImageUrl_additive.cypher` — intentionally empty comment file; documents that no migration is required. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the field is null on all existing Collections until explicitly set via PATCH. |

| **NTF1a — in-app notification system** | New in-app notification primitives: `GET /v2/notifications` (list, ordered most-recent-first, max 200 rows), `GET /v2/notifications/count` (unread count for badge polling), `PATCH /v2/notifications/{appId}/read` (mark a notification read), `DELETE /v2/notifications/{appId}` (dismiss), `POST /v2/admin/notifications/test` (instance-admin test trigger). Frontend: bell-icon badge in `HeaderBar.vue` polling `/count` every 30 s; `NotificationPanel.vue` sliding drawer with per-notification mark-read / dismiss / Go buttons; `useFetchNotifications` composable. Backend: `Notification` Neo4j entity, `NotificationDAO`, `NotificationService.publish(audience, targetUsername, category, source, title, body, actionUrl)` SPI; `V53` migration (appId unique constraint + targetUsername index). 8 JUnit service tests + 7 Vitest composable tests. NTF1b (SMTP email) deferred; `shepard-plugin-matrix` deferred until NTF1a SPI is stable. | **ZERO** | **Five new `/v2/` endpoints (four user-scope + one admin-scope).** One new Neo4j label `:Notification` + `V53` migration (additive, idempotent). No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. No new config keys. `NotificationService.publish()` is available as an injectable SPI for plugins delivering in-app notifications. | `V53__Add_Notification_node.cypher` — two `CREATE ... IF NOT EXISTS` statements (constraint + index), idempotent. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V53 applies on first start. Admin can test the system via `POST /v2/admin/notifications/test`. The bell badge in the frontend header starts polling every 30 s immediately on sign-in. |

| **CW1 — user Collection watches (subscription for DataObject notifications)** | New feature: any authenticated user can "watch" a Collection to receive an in-app notification when a new top-level DataObject is added. New REST surface: `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/watches` (list all watchers, Read gate), `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/watches/me` (200 / 404 probe), `POST /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/watches` (start watching, idempotent), `DELETE /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/watches/me` (stop watching, idempotent). Backend: new `:CollectionWatcher` Neo4j node (`appId`, `username`, `collectionAppId`, `since`; OGM-registered in `NeoConnector`). Notification dispatch hooks into `DataObjectService.createDataObject` — best-effort (never blocks creation). Frontend: new `CollectionWatchButton.vue` (bell icon) on the Collection detail page; star-icon "watched" list previously called "bookmarks" — renamed to "watched" throughout (`useWatchedCollections` composable, pref key `watchedCollections`). New `CollectionWatchesApi` hand-written in `backend-client`. | **ZERO** | **Four new `/v2/` endpoints + one new Neo4j label.** The `:CollectionWatcher` label is greenfield (no existing rows). No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. No migration needed — OGM registers the label on startup via `NeoConnector` package scan; Neo4j-OGM creates the `:CollectionWatcher` label on first save. `bookmarkedCollections` user preference key is superseded by `watchedCollections` (different pref key; old `bookmarkedCollections` data silently ignored, users re-watch a few collections on next login — no data loss, just one-time re-setup). No new config keys; no required admin action. | — (no migration — additive new label; no constraint migration needed for v1; operator may manually add `CREATE CONSTRAINT ON (w:CollectionWatcher) ASSERT w.appId IS UNIQUE IF NOT EXISTS` for production uniqueness enforcement). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the four endpoints become available immediately. Users who had collections "bookmarked" before this change will see an empty "Watched" list and can re-add collections with the star icon or watch via the bell icon. |

| **IMP1 — dry-run import validation (`POST /v2/import/validate` + `:ImportPlan` Neo4j entity)** | New `/v2/import` REST resource shipping three endpoints on the `/v2/` development surface: `POST /v2/import/validate` (dry-run manifest validation; returns a commitId plan seal), `GET /v2/import/plans/{commitId}` (retrieve a plan by commitId), `GET /v2/import/context` (collection state snapshot for importers/agents — DataObject count + SHA-256 fingerprint + optional semantic annotation vocabulary). Backed by a new `:ImportPlan` Neo4j node carrying the manifest hash, collection fingerprint, validated-by, timestamps, and lifecycle status (`VALID \| EXPIRED \| USED \| INVALIDATED`). Plans expire 24 h after issuance; the future `POST /v2/import/jobs` endpoint will enforce the TTL. In-tree rationale: the import seam (commitId / plan-seal contract) is core infrastructure; plugin-level importers (per `aidocs/project_importer_plugin.md`) build on top of this seam. 35 unit tests (18 `ImportV2RestTest` + 17 `ImportValidationServiceTest`). **Amendment (IMP1 TODO closure):** `GET /v2/import/context?includeSemanticGraph=true` now returns live annotations instead of an empty list. `SemanticAnnotationDAO.findByCollectionAppId(String)` added — Cypher: `(Collection)-[:has_dataobject]->(DataObject)-[:has_annotation]->(SemanticAnnotation)` with `DISTINCT` + deleted-guard. Projected to `AnnotationSummaryIO(appId, propertyName, valueName, propertyIRI, valueIRI)`. 3 new tests: empty-list, projection-correctness, DAO-not-called-when-flag-false. No schema change; no migration; no operator action required. | **ZERO** | **Three new `/v2/` endpoints.** One new Neo4j label `:ImportPlan` (OGM-registered in `NeoConnector`). No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. No new config keys. `V55` migration is a NOOP comment documenting that no schema change is required. | `V55__NOOP_ImportPlan_additive.cypher` — no-op (additive new label; nodes created on first POST). Test obligation: testcontainer-level integration (real Neo4j fingerprint query, real plan persistence) deferred alongside V35–V54 obligations. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the endpoints become available immediately. To smoke-test: `POST /v2/import/validate` with `{"collectionAppId":"<any-appId>","dataObjects":[{"localRef":"r1","name":"Test"}]}` using a user who has Write on the collection — expect 200 with a `commitId` in the response. The semantic graph endpoint: `GET /v2/import/context?collectionAppId=<appId>&includeSemanticGraph=true` now returns live annotations. |

| **IMP2 — import execute leg (`POST /v2/import/jobs`)** | New `POST /v2/import/jobs` endpoint that consumes the `commitId` issued by `POST /v2/import/validate` and executes the stored manifest. Pre-flight checks in order: (1) plan not found → 404; (2) plan `status=USED` → 409 (commitId is one-shot); (3) plan `status=EXPIRED` → 410; (4) plan `status=INVALIDATED` → 422; (5) `manifestJson = null` (pre-IMP2 plan) → 410; (6) re-check `expiresAt < now` → 410; (7) Write permission on target Collection → 403/404; (8) re-verify collection fingerprint → 409 if changed; (9) import lock conflict → 409. Execution: two-pass DataObject creation (Pass 1 creates without links; Pass 2 wires parent + predecessorIds via `updateDataObject` with `successorIds = null`), followed by container creation (`FILE` / `TIMESERIES` / `STRUCTURED_DATA`). Returns 201 `{jobAppId, planCommitId, status="COMPLETED", dataObjects, containers, errors}` or 207 for `PARTIAL_FAILURE`. **IMP2 limitation:** reference wiring (container → DataObject attachment via Reference nodes) is NOT performed — `ManifestReferenceIO` carries only localRefs, not payload OIDs. A warning is added to `errors` for any declared reference rows. Deferred to IMP3. New classes: `ImportJobsV2Rest` (`POST /v2/import/jobs`), `ImportExecutionService` (two-pass execute logic), `ImportJobRequestIO` (request record), and the previously described `ImportJobResultIO` (response record). `ImportPlan` gains a `manifestJson` field (stored by `ImportValidationService` from this PR onward). Cypher migration `V83__ImportPlan_manifestJson.cypher` adds `CREATE INDEX import_plan_commit_id IF NOT EXISTS FOR (p:ImportPlan) ON (p.commitId)`. Rollback: `V83_R__ImportPlan_manifestJson.cypher`. 12 unit tests in `ImportJobsV2RestTest`. | **ZERO** | **One new `/v2/import/jobs` endpoint.** One new optional property `manifestJson` on `:ImportPlan` nodes (plans validated before IMP2 deployment have `manifestJson = null`; they are treated as 410 by the execute endpoint). One new Neo4j index on `:ImportPlan(commitId)`. No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. No new config keys. UI stub for job status deferred. | `V83__ImportPlan_manifestJson.cypher` — idempotent `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS`. Rollback: `V83_R__ImportPlan_manifestJson.cypher` (drops the index). Pre-IMP2 plan nodes: `manifestJson = null` — they are rejected with 410 on first execute attempt; operators should re-validate to get a new plan if needed. Test obligation: testcontainer-level integration (real Neo4j + real plan persistence + execute) deferred alongside other IMP obligations. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V83 applies on first start. Plans validated after restart carry `manifestJson` and are executable. To smoke-test: (1) `POST /v2/import/validate` → note `commitId`; (2) `POST /v2/import/jobs {"commitId": "<id>"}` → expect 201 with `status=COMPLETED`. Pre-IMP2 plans (null `manifestJson`) return 410 — re-validate to get a new plan. |

| **REF-1 — enriched DataObject detail (`DataObjectDetailV2IO`)** | `GET /v2/collections/{cid}/data-objects/{did}` now returns `DataObjectDetailV2IO` (extends `DataObjectIO`) instead of the base `DataObjectIO`. The richer payload adds: `containers.{timeseries,files,structuredData}` — typed per-kind lists of `ContainerRefIO` (containerAppId, containerName, referenceId, referenceAppId); `predecessorSummaries`, `successorSummaries`, `childSummaries` — compact `DataObjectSummaryIO` (appId, id, name, status) for directly related DataObjects; `parentSummary` — same for the parent, null if top-level. All legacy fields from `DataObjectIO` are preserved. | **ZERO** | **Single `/v2/` endpoint enriched, not changed in shape.** `DataObjectDetailV2IO extends DataObjectIO` — any client treating the response as the subset `DataObjectIO` keeps working; JSON fields are strictly additive. No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. No migration, no config key. | None required. | **No action required.** Existing callers of the endpoint receive richer JSON; unused fields are ignored by well-behaved clients. |
| **API1 — typed reference appId arrays on `DataObjectDetailV2IO`** (2026-05-26) | `GET /v2/collections/{cid}/data-objects/{did}` response gains three new nullable array fields: `timeseriesReferenceAppIds`, `fileReferenceAppIds`, `structuredDataReferenceAppIds` (each `List<String>` of UUID v7 reference-node appIds, `@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)` so absent when empty). The existing `containers.{timeseries,files,structuredData}[].referenceAppId` field is unchanged. The `get_data_object` MCP tool description is updated to clarify that containerAppId ≠ referenceAppId and to add explicit navigation guidance. Root cause fix: an AI agent was passing reference-node appIds to `list_channels` (which expects a containerAppId) and receiving 404s. The frozen v1 `DataObjectIO` shape and all `/shepard/api/...` endpoints are **untouched**. | **ZERO** | **Strictly additive.** Three new nullable JSON fields on an existing `/v2/` response. Any client ignoring unknown fields is unaffected. No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. No migration, no config key. | None required. | **No action required.** Fields are null (absent from JSON) for DataObjects with no references. When references exist, each array is populated by `DataObjectV2Rest.buildContainersFromCypher()`. Two new unit tests verify population and backward-compat. |
| **ANC-1 — DataObject ancestry sub-endpoints** | Five new read-only `/v2/` endpoints under `/v2/collections/{cid}/data-objects/{did}`: `GET /predecessors` (direct), `GET /successors` (direct), `GET /children` (direct), `GET /predecessor-chain?depth=N` (variable-depth Cypher traversal, default 10, max 50), `GET /successor-chain?depth=N`. All return `DataObjectSummaryIO[]`. Auth: inherited Read on the parent Collection. Chain queries use string-interpolated Cypher depth bounds (Neo4j OGM limitation). 8 new unit tests in `DataObjectV2RestTest`. **Updated fire-322 (APISIMP-DO-RELATIONSHIP-LISTS-UNBOUND):** `GET /predecessors`, `GET /successors`, and `GET /children` now return `PagedResponseIO<DataObjectSummaryIO>` (envelope with `items`, `total`, `page`, `pageSize`) instead of bare `DataObjectSummaryIO[]`. Both endpoints accept `?page=0&pageSize=50` query parameters (page ≥ 0, pageSize 1–200, defaults 0/50). **Callers built against the ANC-1 bare-list response must update to read `body.items` instead of the bare array.** | **AWARE** | **Wire-shape change on three `/v2/` endpoints** (`/predecessors`, `/successors`, `/children`): response body changed from bare array to `PagedResponseIO` envelope. `/predecessor-chain` and `/successor-chain` are unchanged (still bare array). No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. No migration, no config key. | None required. | **Client update required for `/predecessors`, `/successors`, `/children`:** replace bare-array reads with `response.items` and check `response.total` for the full count. Default page=0/pageSize=50 means callers that previously received all items will continue to receive the first 50 items — collections with more than 50 direct predecessors/successors/children will now require explicit pagination via `?page=N`. |
| **QA-1 — quantified semantic annotations (`numericValue` + `unitIRI`)** | `SemanticAnnotation` Neo4j entity and `SemanticAnnotationIO` gain two optional fields: `numericValue` (Double) and `unitIRI` (String, intended for QUDT/other unit ontology IRIs). Both nullable; existing annotations without these properties are unaffected. `equals`/`hashCode` in `SemanticAnnotation` updated to include the new fields. `SemanticAnnotationIO` constructor copies both from the entity. `V56__NOOP_SemanticAnnotation_quantified.cypher` documents no schema migration is required. | **ZERO** | **Additive optional properties on `:SemanticAnnotation` nodes.** No endpoint changed. Existing annotations keep working. `SemanticAnnotationIO` JSON output gains two nullable fields — well-behaved clients ignore unknown fields. No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. | `V56__NOOP_SemanticAnnotation_quantified.cypher` — no-op (schema-free Neo4j properties; no DDL needed). Test obligation: service/DAO integration test for write+read of quantified annotations — deferred. | **No action required.** Fields are absent on existing annotations until explicitly set via the annotation update endpoint. |
| **FAIR-1 — FAIR metadata fields on DataObjects (`license` + `accessRights`)** | `AbstractDataObject` Neo4j entity and `AbstractDataObjectIO` gain two optional fields: `license` (String, SPDX expression, e.g. `CC-BY-4.0`) and `accessRights` (String, COAR Access Rights vocabulary). Both `@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)` — absent from JSON responses when null. `equals`/`hashCode` in `AbstractDataObjectIO` updated to include the new fields. `V57__NOOP_AbstractDataObject_fair_fields.cypher` documents no schema migration is required. | **ZERO** | **Additive optional properties on `:DataObject` (and subclass) nodes.** No endpoint changed. Existing DataObjects without these properties read as null. JSON output gains two nullable fields. No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. | `V57__NOOP_AbstractDataObject_fair_fields.cypher` — no-op (schema-free Neo4j properties; no DDL needed). Test obligation: end-to-end test for PATCH of `license`/`accessRights` and verify round-trip — deferred. | **No action required.** Fields are absent on existing DataObjects until explicitly set via PATCH. |
| **LIC1 — FAIR-1 completion: tightened semantics + UI + tests** | Extends FAIR-1: `accessRights` JavaDoc + `@Schema.enumeration` pinned to `OPEN \| RESTRICTED \| CLOSED \| EMBARGOED`. Fields land in the UI: `LicenseChip` / `AccessRightsChip` / `LicenseInput` / `AccessRightsInput` components; Collection list table gains an "Access" column; both detail pages (Collection + DataObject) show a FAIR metadata strip; all four create/edit dialogs wire the fields. 14 backend Jackson tests + 13 Vitest cases. Closes the deferred FAIR-1 test obligation. | **ZERO** | **No new endpoints.** Additive UI + test coverage over FAIR-1 fields already in the schema. No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. No migration, no config keys. | None (V57 NOOP already in place). | **No action required.** Fields were already readable/writable via PATCH; this PR adds display + input components. |

| **FAIR2+FAIR3 — `createdByOrcid` stamp + `embargoEndDate` + `PublishService` enforcement** | 2026-05-26. **FAIR2:** `AbstractDataObject.createdByOrcid` (nullable String) stamped server-side at DataObject creation time from `User.orcid`. Survives user-account deletion; provenance is durable. Read-only in all IO shapes — never accepted as user input. `AbstractDataObjectIO.createdByOrcid` carries the field read-only (absent from wire when null). DataObject detail page shows a linked ORCID badge. **FAIR3 (remainder):** `AbstractDataObject.embargoEndDate` (nullable ISO-8601 date String, e.g. `"2027-12-31"`) — user-provided, surfaces in `AbstractDataObjectIO`, `CreateDataObjectV2IO` inherits it via the parent class. DataObject detail page shows an editable chip (Write users) or read-only chip (Read users) when set; "Set embargo date" affordance appears when `accessRights=EMBARGOED` but no date is set. `PublishService.publish(force=false)` now rejects entities with `accessRights=RESTRICTED` or `accessRights=EMBARGOED` with `IllegalStateException`; `force=true` overrides. **Migration:** `V84__FAIR2_FAIR3_fields.cypher` adds an index on `embargoEndDate` for future range queries; rollback `V84_R__FAIR2_FAIR3_fields.cypher` drops it. No data-backfill needed (both fields nullable). **Tests:** 4 Mockito unit tests (FAIR2) + 4 Mockito unit tests (FAIR3) + 10 Vitest unit tests (frontend). | **ZERO** | **Additive optional properties on `:DataObject` nodes.** No endpoint changed. Existing DataObjects without `createdByOrcid` / `embargoEndDate` return null / absent from wire (NON_NULL omission). The `PublishService` enforcement is new behaviour on the publish path — operators who call publish on RESTRICTED/EMBARGOED entities (previously allowed) will now receive a 500-mapped `IllegalStateException` unless they pass `force=true`. No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. | `V84__FAIR2_FAIR3_fields.cypher` — additive index on `embargoEndDate`. Idempotent. `V84_R__FAIR2_FAIR3_fields.cypher` drops it. Test obligation: 8 backend Mockito tests + 10 Vitest tests landed in this PR. | **No action required for upgrade** (additive fields). Operators who have automated publish pipelines against RESTRICTED/EMBARGOED entities must add `force=true` to the publish call or change `accessRights` to `OPEN` first. |

| **FAIR7 — DMP snippet generator endpoint** (`GET /v2/collections/{appId}/dmp-snippet`) | 2026-05-26. New additive read-only endpoint under `/v2/`. Returns a copy-paste-ready Markdown DMP block pre-filled with all FAIR-relevant fields from the Collection (`license`, `accessRights`, `description`) and its DataObjects (`createdByOrcid`, `embargoEndDate`, reference payload kinds, DataObject count). Content negotiation: `Accept: text/markdown` (default) returns the Markdown body; `Accept: application/json` returns a `DmpSnippetIO` JSON wrapper carrying the same Markdown in `snippet` plus a `missingFields` array listing null/blank FAIR fields (`license`, `accessRights`, `orcid`, `description`). Auth: Read on the Collection; 401/403/404 grid unchanged. No new entities, no migrations. Files: `de.dlr.shepard.v2.fair.resources.DmpSnippetV2Rest`, `.services.DmpSnippetService`, `.io.DmpSnippetIO`. 8 Mockito unit tests. | **ZERO** | **Additive `/v2/` endpoint only.** No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. No new Neo4j nodes or properties. No config keys. Upstream v5.2.0 clients are unaffected. | None (no schema migration required). | **No action required.** Endpoint is opt-in — callers use it when building DMP forms. |

| **MCP-1 — `shepard-plugin-mcp` Python FastMCP sidecar** *(retired 2026-05-21 — replaced by MCP-2)* | Original Python sidecar at `/mcp/sse` (container `shepard-mcp:8811`). Superseded by the native Quarkus MCP server (MCP-2) at `/v2/mcp/sse`. Source tree (`plugins/shepard-plugin-mcp/`), Caddy `/mcp/*` route, compose service `shepard-mcp`, backend `McpSidecarPluginManifest` and the SPI registration have all been removed in the sunset commit. | **AWARE** | **Breaking for clients pinned to `/mcp/sse`** — point them at `/v2/mcp/sse` (same wire shape). The legacy URL now 404s. | — (no schema migration; just URL change). | **Operator action:** none if the operator never opted into the sidecar. If they did, they update connector configurations from `https://<host>/mcp/sse` to `https://<host>/v2/mcp/sse`. Auth model is unchanged (Bearer OIDC or Bearer API key). |

| **MCP-2 — native Quarkus MCP server (`/v2/mcp/sse`) + `JwtTokenAuthService` + `McpAuthFilter` + `McpContextBridge` + 8-tool surface** | Phase 1 of replacing the Python FastMCP sidecar with a JVM-native MCP server. Adds `quarkus-mcp-server-http:1.9.1`. **Eight MCP tools** in three classes calling the service layer directly — no HTTP hop, no token relay: `CollectionMcpTools` (`list_collections` / `list_data_objects` / `get_data_object`), `TimeseriesMcpTools` (`list_channels` / `get_channel_data` with LTTB downsampling), `ContentMcpTools` (`list_files` / `list_structured_data` / `list_annotations`). MCP endpoints land at `/v2/mcp/sse` (Quarkus-native), parallel with the legacy Python sidecar at `/mcp/sse`. Auth bridge: extracted `JwtTokenAuthService` (`@ApplicationScoped`) shares OIDC Bearer token validation between `JWTFilter` (JAX-RS) and the new `McpAuthFilter` (Vert.x `@Observes Filters`, priority 100) — no duplicated CVE-patch surface. 401 on missing / invalid Bearer. `McpContextBridge` propagates the validated principal from the routing-context stash into the fresh per-tool-invocation CDI request scope. `DataObjectDAO.findByShepardIdAtDepth(id, 2)` lets `get_data_object` populate `containers.{timeseries,files,structuredData}` (the default depth-1 load left the container hop null). Frontend: new "MCP" entry on `/me` profile sidebar (`McpPane.vue`) showing SSE + Streamable HTTP endpoint URLs ready to copy. 72 unit tests across the MCP surface (Java) + 4 unit tests for endpoint URL helpers (Vitest). Verified end-to-end against LUMEN TR-004: `get_channel_data` returns the seeded 12.358 g vibration peak at t≈8s; `list_annotations` returns the 9 annotations including "Anomaly Type = Vibration Anomaly". | **AWARE** | One new dependency in `backend/pom.xml`; one new config key `quarkus.mcp.server.http.root-path=/v2/mcp`. MCP coexists with the Python sidecar via distinct paths (`/v2/mcp/sse` vs `/mcp/sse`) — no operator collision. **`quarkus.http.auth.permission.*` is intentionally NOT used** for these routes (shepard's auth uses `JwtTokenAuthService`, not the Quarkus OIDC security provider). | — (no migration; no schema changes). | **To enable:** restart shepard; the SSE endpoint comes up at `/v2/mcp/sse` after restart. Verify with a Bearer token: `curl -N -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" https://shepard.nuclide.systems/v2/mcp/sse`. Without a token: `curl -i https://shepard.nuclide.systems/v2/mcp/sse` → expect 401 with `AuthenticationException` body. Users discover the endpoint via `/me#mcp`. Phase 2 (predecessor/successor chain tools, file/structured-data download, Python sidecar retirement) is a separate ledger entry. |

| **AI-SPI — `LlmProvider` SPI + `AiCapability` enum in backend core** | New SPI package `de.dlr.shepard.spi.ai.*` in the backend module: `LlmProvider` (interface), `LlmRequest` (immutable builder), `LlmResponse` (immutable builder), `AiCapability` (enum: TEXT / FAST_TEXT / IMAGE_GEN / VISION / EMBEDDING / STRUCTURED), `LlmException` (unchecked), `RequiresAiCapability` (repeatable annotation). These types are the compile-target for all AI-augmented plugins. `V58` uniqueness constraint on `:AiCapabilityConfig(appId)` — idempotent (`IF NOT EXISTS`). | **ADDITIVE** | Strictly additive — no new endpoints, no config keys, no operator-visible behaviour change. The SPI types are in the backend JAR (classpath additions only). Plugins that don't depend on this SPI are unaffected. | `V58__AiCapabilityConfig_constraint.cypher` — `CREATE CONSTRAINT AiCapabilityConfig_appId_unique IF NOT EXISTS FOR (n:AiCapabilityConfig) REQUIRE n.appId IS UNIQUE`. Rollback: `DROP CONSTRAINT AiCapabilityConfig_appId_unique IF EXISTS`. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V58 applies on first start. Nothing is visible unless `shepard-plugin-ai` is also deployed. |
| **AI1a — `shepard-plugin-ai` LLM provider plugin** | New plugin (`plugins/ai/`) implementing `LlmProvider` for OpenAI-compatible endpoints. Adds: `:AiCapabilityConfig` Neo4j entity (one node per `AiCapability` slot, seeded on first access); `:AiActivity` provenance node (capability, model, promptHash, token counts) written on every call; `GET /v2/admin/ai/capabilities` (list all slots); `GET /v2/admin/ai/capabilities/{capability}` (one slot); `PATCH /v2/admin/ai/capabilities/{capability}` (RFC 7396 merge-patch). API key masked as `***` in GET responses. Prompt injection defence (trusted system role + delimiter-wrapped untrusted user content). 28 unit tests. Default-off (`shepard.plugins.ai.enabled=false`). | **AWARE** | New optional plugin — opt-in by setting `SHEPARD_PLUGINS_AI_ENABLED=true`. No changes to any existing endpoint or entity. The `/v2/admin/ai/` surface is new (admin-gated). Existing operator workflow: unaffected until the plugin is enabled. **All capability slots default to `enabled=false`** — connecting an endpoint requires a subsequent `PATCH /v2/admin/ai/capabilities/TEXT` (or setting `SHEPARD_AI_TEXT_ENABLED=true`). API key is stored plain-text in v0 — encrypt Neo4j volume at rest. | — (no data-mutating migration; V58 additive constraint only). | **To enable:** set `SHEPARD_PLUGINS_AI_ENABLED=true` and configure the TEXT slot via PATCH or env vars (see `plugins/ai/docs/install.md`). To verify: `GET /v2/admin/ai/capabilities` returns a list of 6 slots all `enabled: false`. Activate TEXT slot and point `shepard-plugin-wiki-writer` at it for the first AI-augmented operation. |
| **WW1 — `shepard-plugin-wiki-writer` lab journal authoring assist** | New plugin (`plugins/wiki-writer/`) that generates Markdown lab journal entries for DataObjects using the LlmProvider TEXT capability. Adds one endpoint: `POST /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects/{dataObjectAppId}/wiki-write`. Reads the DataObject's attributes, description, Collection siblings, predecessors, and successors; assembles a layered prompt; calls the TEXT capability; writes a `LabJournalEntry` on the DataObject. Returns `{labJournalEntryId, generatedSummary, activityAppId, inputTokens, outputTokens}`. Soft-depends on `shepard-plugin-ai` (returns 503 when absent or TEXT unconfigured). Default-off (`shepard.plugins.wiki-writer.enabled=false`). 10 unit tests. | **AWARE** | New optional plugin — opt-in. No changes to existing endpoints or entities. No migration. The new `/v2/` endpoint appears only when the plugin is enabled. | — (no migration — writes `LabJournalEntry` via existing `LabJournalEntryService`; no new Neo4j labels or constraints). | **To enable:** `SHEPARD_PLUGINS_WIKI_WRITER_ENABLED=true`. Requires `shepard-plugin-ai` with TEXT capability configured. Smoke-test: `POST /v2/collections/<id>/data-objects/<id>/wiki-write` → expect 200 with `generatedSummary` if the AI endpoint is reachable; 503 if not. |
| **SHACL-1 — Jena SHACL validator + `POST /v2/shapes/validate` + HMAC audit chain** *(foundation slice of the SHACL changeover, brief 2026-05-22, PRs 1+3+4 shipped, PRs 2/5/6/7/8/9 deferred)* | Foundation for the SHACL changeover (non-TS scope). Adds: (1) Jena `jena-shacl:5.4.0` dependency (Apache-2.0); (2) `JenaShaclValidator` (`@ApplicationScoped`) — in-process SHACL validator taking `{dataTurtle, shapeTurtle}` as Jena Models, returning a structured validation report; (3) `POST /v2/shapes/validate` — validate-only contract for MCP / UI form-builder / plugin authors (catalog collapses into Template `kind=view` per aidocs/101 v2); (4) `:InstanceConfig` Neo4j singleton + `InstanceConfigService` (A3b/N1c2/UH1a pattern) carrying the per-instance HMAC signing key with `secretVersion` rotation; (5) `HmacChainService` — best-effort HMAC-SHA256(instance_secret_v_n, prevHmac ‖ activityCanonical) chain stamped onto every `Activity` write; (6) new `auditHmac` / `auditPrevHmac` / `secretVersion` fields on `:Activity`. **PR-2 (n10s→Jena substrate), PR-5 (predicate lift — V60 slot reserved), PR-6–8 (NCR/AFP/welding shape integration), PR-9 (`shepard-plugin-mffd-domain` move) DEFERRED.** 29 new unit tests, 1 existing test extended (ProvenanceServiceTest). | **ADDITIVE** | Strictly additive. New `/v2/shapes/validate` endpoint; new `:InstanceConfig` node (auto-seeded on startup); three new nullable properties on `:Activity`. No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. **Operator knob:** `SHEPARD_INSTANCE_SECRET` (env) / `shepard.audit.instance-secret` (config). When absent on first start, a fresh `SecureRandom`-derived 256-bit key is minted and a WARN is logged advising the operator to wire in a stable value before the next restart. Two pre-existing test files (`CollectionMcpToolsTest`, `ContentMcpToolsTest`) were `@Disabled` in this PR because they were already out of sync with their main classes — fixed in a follow-up that rewrote both against the post-MCP-2 surface (real `McpToolSupport` with injected `entityIdResolver` + `objectMapper`, `resolveOfType`/`resolveWithLabels` stubs, `McpException` `-32602` assertions, mirroring `TimeseriesMcpToolsTest`). 18 tests passing (9+9, up from 16 disabled). | `V59__InstanceConfig_constraint.cypher` — appId uniqueness, idempotent. `V60__NOOP_SHACL_predicates_placeholder.cypher` — reserved slot for the PR-5 predicate lift, NO-OP today. Both safe to re-run. Test obligation: regression test for chain verification across a rotation event — deferred to the rotation REST/CLI PR. | **No action required for upgrade.** Chain auto-seeds on first start. Set `SHEPARD_INSTANCE_SECRET` to a stable, archived value before the next restart for reproducible audit verification. Verify after restart: `MATCH (n:InstanceConfig) RETURN count(n)` returns 1; first new write produces an `Activity` with non-null `auditHmac`. |
| **TS-ID substrate (PR-1 of the 5-tuple → shepardId migration)** | Adds a `shepard_id UUID NOT NULL UNIQUE` column to the existing Postgres `timeseries` table via Flyway `V1.11.0__add_shepard_id_to_timeseries.sql`. Existing rows backfilled with `gen_random_uuid()` (one-shot). New `TsChannelResolver` JDBC service (`de.dlr.shepard.data.timeseries.repositories.TsChannelResolver`) resolves a `shepardId` UUID to the 5-tuple `(measurement, device, location, symbolicName, field)` and vice-versa. Supersedes `aidocs/platform/87-timeseries-appid-migration.md §3 TS-IDa` which assumed a `:Timeseries` Neo4j node — that node never existed; the channel is a Postgres row. **No wire change in this PR** — wire renames land in PR-2 onwards. v1 `/shepard/api/...` continues to emit `appId` byte-for-byte (proven by `V1WireFidelityTest`); `shepardId` is a NEW alias on the v2 surface only. | **ADDITIVE** | One new Postgres column on `timeseries` (additive, idempotent via `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`). One new injectable service in the backend JAR. No new endpoints, no config keys, no operator-visible behaviour change. Existing v1 and v2 callers receive byte-identical responses to before. | `V1.11.0__add_shepard_id_to_timeseries.sql` — `ALTER TABLE timeseries ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS shepard_id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL; CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_timeseries_shepard_id ON timeseries(shepard_id);`. Rollback `V1.11.0_R__drop_shepard_id.sql` provided. Idempotent and fail-fast (constraint violation aborts startup if backfill collision occurs — should never happen). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; Flyway applies V1.11.0 on first start. Each existing channel row receives a fresh UUID v4. The new `shepardId` is queryable via `SELECT shepard_id FROM timeseries WHERE ...` for ops; it surfaces on the wire in later PRs. |
| **TS-ID PR-3 (TS-IDc) — `GET /v2/timeseries-containers/{containerId}/channels/{shepardId}/data` single-key data fetch** | 2026-05-26. New data-point fetch endpoint that accepts a `shepardId` UUID instead of the legacy 5-tuple. Resolves `shepardId → (measurement, device, location, symbolicName, field)` via `TsChannelResolver.resolveTuple(uuid)` (already in place from PR-1) and delegates to the existing `TimeseriesService.getDataPointsByTimeseries()` — no new query logic. Query params: `start` + `end` (nanoseconds, required); optional `?downsample=lttb&max_points=N` (LTTB downsampling, same 5000-point hard cap as v1). Returns `TimeseriesWithDataPoints` (same wire shape as `GET /shepard/api/timeseriesContainers/{id}/timeseries` — compatible JSON). 404 when `shepardId` is unknown in this container. Container permission still checked via `TimeseriesContainerService.getContainer(containerId)`. 5 new unit tests (`TimeseriesChannelDataRestTest`) covering 404 branch, 200 branch, entity type guard, LTTB null-safety, and LTTB bypass when param absent. Demo dataset: `examples/ts-id-demo/seed.py` seeds a Collection "TS-IDc demo — AFP TCP thermal trail" (1 DO + 1 TS container, 4 channels, 1000 pts/channel). `--demo` flag calls both endpoints and prints a before/after comparison of 5-tuple vs shepardId addressing. Visible in the Shepard UI under Collections. v1 surface untouched. **Beta acceptance criteria**: (1) endpoint returns 200 with data points for a known shepardId; (2) unknown shepardId returns 404; (3) `?downsample=lttb&max_points=200` returns ≤200 points; (4) `V1WireFidelityTest` passes; (5) demo dataset seeded and visible on shepard.nuclide.systems. | **ZERO** | **One new `/v2/` endpoint** — no `/shepard/api/...` surface touched, no migration, no config keys, no new Neo4j label. The permission gate reuses the existing container service. `TsChannelResolver` (PR-1 substrate) is a read-only lookup; no new Postgres DDL. | None required (V1.11.0 from PR-1 in place). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the endpoint becomes available. Smoke-test: `curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" "https://shepard.nuclide.systems/v2/timeseries-containers/{id}/channels/{shepardId}/data?start=0&end=9999999999999999999"` → 200 with `{timeseries: {measurement, …}, points: […]}`. |
| **TS-ID PR-3b (TS-IDc / task #58) — live-window endpoint accepts `?shepardId=<uuid>`** | 2026-05-29 (`9b88c1d66`). Extends `GET /v2/timeseries-containers/{containerAppId}/channels/live-window` to accept an optional `shepardId` query parameter in addition to the legacy 5-tuple (`measurement`, `device`, `location`, `symbolicName`, `field`). When both forms are supplied on the same request, **shepardId wins** and the 5-tuple is ignored — documented in the OpenAPI summary. Backed by new `TsChannelResolver.findByContainerAndShepardId(containerId, shepardId)` which wraps `findByShepardId` with an in-memory container filter (cheap; no second SQL pass; deliberate cross-container leak guard — unknown / wrong-container shepardIds both surface as 404). Plan-time evidence on MFFD container 1772 (113 channels): shepardId path = 4.36 ms planning + 0.17 ms execution via `idx_timeseries_shepard_id` (UNIQUE B-tree); legacy 5-tuple = 2.18 ms planning + 0.07 ms execution via seq scan on `channel_metadata`. shepardId wins on cost-growth shape (constant vs. O(channels-per-container)); both are sub-millisecond at small container sizes. 6 new unit tests (`TimeseriesLiveWindowShepardIdTest`) covering known/unknown/wrong-container/both-keys/legacy-fallthrough/permission-check branches; 3 new resolver tests covering null short-circuit + container-filter projection. Frontend wiring (one caller as proof): `useFetchChannelPreview` composable accepts optional `channelShepardId` and prefers the v2 path-param endpoint when set; `ChannelPreviewChart.vue` + `TimeseriesMeasurementsTable.vue` pass the resolved shepardId through. 3 new Vitest cases cover the shepardId path, the legacy fallthrough, and the in-flight dedup. v1 surface untouched. | **ZERO** | **Zero new endpoints** — same path as before, one new optional query param. No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. No new config keys. No migration (the substrate index `idx_timeseries_shepard_id` already exists from V1.11.0). The TS-IDc field name on the wire is `shepardId` (not `timeseriesAppId` — see the 87 CHANGELOG); deep Java-internal rename to `shepardId` stays deferred to task #123. Existing 5-tuple callers see byte-identical responses. | None required (V1.11.0 from PR-1 in place). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the new query param becomes available. Smoke-test: `curl -H "X-API-Key: <key>" "https://shepard.nuclide.systems/v2/timeseries-containers/<containerAppId>/channels/live-window?shepardId=<uuid>&windowSeconds=60"` → 200 with `{windowStart, windowEnd, points: [...]}`. To confirm precedence: hit the same URL with `shepardId=<known>&measurement=wrong-value` — still returns 200 (the wrong 5-tuple was ignored). See `docs/admin/runbooks/11-ts-shepardid-lookup-migration.md` for the full operator verification. |

The IO carries `shepardId` (the new single-field identity) **plus** the legacy 5-tuple (`measurement`, `device`, `location`, `symbolicName`, `field`), the legacy numeric `id`, the `containerId`, and the `valueType`. This is the first /v2/ surface where `shepardId` appears as a JSON property — the end-to-end demonstration of the PR-1 substrate. The v1 endpoint `GET /shepard/api/timeseriesContainers/{id}/timeseries` is **untouched and continues to emit `TimeseriesIO` byte-for-byte** (locked by `V1WireFidelityTest`). 4 new unit tests (`TimeseriesChannelV2IOTest`) covering the projection helper + JSON shape + a structural guard that the v2 IO does NOT extend the v1 IO. | **ZERO** | **One new `/v2/` endpoint.** No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched.
