Smoke-test: `curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" https://shepard.nuclide.systems/v2/timeseries-containers/{id}/channels` → 200 with `[{"shepardId": "<uuid>", "id": <n>, "containerId": <n>, "measurement": ..., …}, …]`. |
| **IMP1a — `shepard-plugin-importer` scaffold (PR-1 of 7)** | New plugin module `plugins/importer/` producing `shepard-plugin-importer-${revision}.jar`. Carries only the `PluginManifest` implementation + `META-INF/services/de.dlr.shepard.plugin.PluginManifest` SPI hook + 4 unit tests + the docs trio (`reference.md`/`install.md`/`quickstart.md`). Wired into `backend/pom.xml`'s default-active `with-plugins` profile as a `<dependency>`; the build-time circular reference is broken by adding `<exclusion>shepard-plugin-importer</exclusion>` to every sibling plugin pom's backend `provided` dep (11 plugin poms touched: `aas`, `file-s3`, `git`, `hdf5`, `kip`, `minter-{datacite,epic,local}`, `spatial`, `unhide`, `video`). PR-1 ships **no REST surface, no DAO, no source adapter, no Cypher / SQL migration, no config keys, no CLI subcommand**. PR-2 lands the `importer_run` Postgres table + Flyway migration + `JobService`-shaped DAO/service (first concrete instance of `aidocs/platform/32`'s broader JobService design); PR-3 extracts `DLRv5Source` from `examples/mffd-showcase/scripts/mffd-dropbox-import.py` v12's `ShepardClient` lines 239-1170 (scope: MFFD-use-case methods); PR-4 the REST surface (`/v2/imports/...`); PR-5 the `shepard-admin importer …` CLI; PR-6 the frontend with 2s polling (decision #7); PR-7 the end-to-end integration test against a mock v5 source. Partial-plugin sequence (PR-1..PR-5 backend-only) explicitly authorised by user — `aidocs/44` IMP1 row stays 🚧 until PR-6 lands. | **AWARE** | **Zero new endpoints, zero new schema constraints, zero new admin REST, zero new config keys, zero new CLI subcommands.** One new entry in `GET /v2/admin/plugins` (the manifest registration): `importer | 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT | ENABLED | true | Apache-2.0 | https://github.com/noheton/shepard`. PM1e's persistent runtime-override path means an operator can disable the plugin at runtime via `shepard-admin plugins disable importer` and the override survives restart. `/shepard/api/...` surface unchanged. Image-size impact: ~5 KB (manifest class + the docs trio). | — (no migration in PR-1). PR-2 will ship a Flyway SQL migration `V1.11.1__add_importer_run_table.sql` creating the `importer_run` table; the rollback path is documented at the top of the migration file. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the PluginRegistry logs `IMP1a: importer plugin v1.0.0-SNAPSHOT active via PluginManifest SPI (id=importer, compat=>=6.0.0-SNAPSHOT,<7)`. The plugin is inert — visible in admin REST + CLI plugin lists, but exposes no user-callable surface until PR-4 lands the REST endpoints. To disable the entry: `shepard-admin plugins disable importer` (persisted via PM1e, survives restart). Test obligation: 4 plugin-unit tests landed in this PR; testcontainer-level integration regression (boot Quarkus + observe `IMP1a` startup log line + verify `GET /v2/admin/plugins` lists `importer`) is **deferred** alongside the broader QuarkusTest scaffolding — same posture as UH1a / MCP-1 precedent. |
| **IMP1b — `importer_run` Postgres table + service (PR-2 of 7)** | New Postgres table `importer_run` shipped via the plugin's Flyway migration `V1.11.1__add_importer_run_table.sql` (carried in `plugins/importer/src/main/resources/db/migration/`, picked up by Quarkus Flyway's classpath scan of `db/migration`). The column set is the **first concrete instance** of the generic JobService design proposed in `aidocs/platform/32 §3` — names deliberately mirror that kernel (`status`, `created_at`, `started_at`, `last_progress_at`, `finished_at`, `progress_total`, `progress_done`, `progress_message`, `error_class`, `error_message`, `result_url`, `result_metadata`, `request_payload`, `cancel_requested`). Importer-specific extras live alongside (`source_kind`, `source_config`, `target_collection_app_id`) — they don't belong in the generic kernel. Status enum: `PENDING / RUNNING / SUCCEEDED / FAILED / CANCELLED` (CHECK constraint + Hibernate `EnumType.STRING`). UUID v7 ids minted at submit time via `UuidCreator.getTimeOrderedEpoch()` (same as `AppIdGenerator`) so the caller knows the id before the row commits. Four compound indexes: `(principal, status)` for "my runs by status"; partial index on `(status, last_progress_at) WHERE status='RUNNING'` for the reaper; partial index on `(status, finished_at) WHERE status IN ('SUCCEEDED','FAILED','CANCELLED')` for the GC sweep; partial index on `(target_collection_app_id) WHERE target_collection_app_id IS NOT NULL` for the per-Collection import-history lookup PR-6's frontend uses. Three new classes in `de.dlr.shepard.plugins.importer.runs`: `ImporterRun` (JPA `@Entity`), `ImporterRunRepository` (Panache repo with `findOne` / `listByPrincipal` / `listPending` / `listStale` / `listTerminalOlderThan`), `ImporterRunService` (the `@ApplicationScoped` write-surface mirroring `aidocs/platform/32 §4`'s `JobService` interface method-for-method: `submit` / `markStarted` / `recordProgress` / `markSucceeded` / `markFailed` / `markCancelled` / `requestCancellation` / `getRun` / `getRunForAdmin` / `listMyRuns`). Permission boundary enforced at service level: only the submitting principal can `getRun` (a different principal sees `Optional.empty()`; row existence is not leaked). `getRunForAdmin` bypasses the check — the REST resource in PR-4 will gate it on `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`. State-machine invariants enforced via `ImporterRunStatus.isTerminal()`: a terminal row never transitions again, missing-row paths silently no-op rather than throw. PR-4 of the IMP1 series wires the `@Scheduled` worker + REST surface + heartbeat reaper + GC sweep. | **AWARE** | **One new Postgres table** (`importer_run`) shipped via plugin-supplied Flyway migration. **Zero new endpoints, zero new admin REST, zero new RFC 7807 problem types** (PR-4 will add `/v2/imports/...` + the problem types). **Zero new admin-runtime config keys** in this PR — the JobService knobs (`shepard.importer.{poll-interval,max-concurrent,stale-after,retention-days}`) land in PR-4 when the scheduler is actually wired. **One new deploy-time config key** in this PR: `shepard.plugins.importer.enabled=false` (default off; matches the PM1a default-opt-in policy every other plugin uses). One new `quarkus.index-dependency.shepard-plugin-importer.*` pair in `application.properties` so Quarkus's build-time JPA + CDI scanners pick the plugin's `@Entity` and `@ApplicationScoped` beans. `/shepard/api/...` surface unchanged. | `V1.11.1__add_importer_run_table.sql` — idempotent (`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` + `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS`) + fail-fast (any error aborts Flyway which aborts Quarkus startup — the standard CLAUDE.md migration rule). **No rollback file** — additive new table on a fresh schema; one-line `DROP TABLE IF EXISTS importer_run` reverses it. Migration regression test deferred to PR-7 (real Postgres testcontainer + submit + status transitions + reaper + GC). | **No action required for upgrade.** First restart post-IMP1b applies V1.11.1 against the existing application schema (no data touched; table is fresh). Operators consuming the published image find an empty `importer_run` table after restart; the plugin is inert until PR-4 lands the REST surface. To inspect from `psql`: `SELECT id, source_kind, principal, status, created_at FROM importer_run ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 10;`. To revert (very unusual — the table is harmless if empty): `DROP TABLE importer_run;` then redeploy the previous backend image. Test obligation: 28 new unit tests (3 status + 2 entity + 23 service) landed in this PR; PR-7's testcontainer-level integration test will exercise the full submit → claim → progress → succeed / fail / cancel cycle against a real Postgres + a mock v5 source server. |
| **IMP-LOCK — persistent import-in-progress lock with heartbeat + REST status** | New in-core feature adding a `:ImportLock` Neo4j entity that persists across backend restarts. When a client importer (e.g. `mffd-import-v15.py`) acquires the lock before a long import, a restarted backend can surface "an import was in progress" rather than losing state silently. Six new `/v2/` endpoints: `GET /v2/import/lock` (current status; 204 when none), `POST /v2/import/lock` (acquire — 409 on fresh conflict; auto-abandons stale locks), `POST /v2/import/lock/{lockId}/heartbeat` (extend heartbeat; call every ~30 s), `POST /v2/import/lock/{lockId}/release` (→ COMPLETED on success), `POST /v2/import/lock/{lockId}/abandon` (→ FAILED on error, with errorMessage), `DELETE /v2/import/lock/{lockId}` (admin cancel → CANCELLED; `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`). Stale threshold: heartbeat older than 5 minutes. Lock history is retained in Neo4j (COMPLETED / FAILED / CANCELLED / ABANDONED nodes are not auto-deleted) for audit. 17 unit tests in `ImportLockServiceTest` covering all lifecycle transitions + idempotency + stale-eviction. | **ZERO** | **Six new `/v2/import/lock` endpoints.** One new Neo4j label `:ImportLock`. No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. No new config keys. Migration `V65__Add_ImportLock_node.cypher` adds two uniqueness constraints (`lockId`, `appId`) — idempotent `IF NOT EXISTS`. No data-mutating migration. UI stub pending (import lock status is backend-only in this PR; frontend visibility deferred). | `V65__Add_ImportLock_node.cypher` — idempotent (`CREATE CONSTRAINT ... IF NOT EXISTS`). Safe to re-run. Rollback: `DROP CONSTRAINT ImportLock_lockId_unique IF EXISTS; DROP CONSTRAINT ImportLock_appId_unique IF EXISTS; MATCH (l:ImportLock) DETACH DELETE l;`. Testcontainer integration test deferred; unit-test obligation met (17 tests). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V65 applies on first start. Smoke-test: `GET /v2/import/lock` returns 204 (no lock). To acquire: `POST /v2/import/lock {"targetCollectionAppId":"<appId>"}` → 201 with lockId. Client importers should call `POST /v2/import/lock/{lockId}/heartbeat` every 30 s during long imports and `POST /v2/import/lock/{lockId}/release` or `abandon` on completion. |
| **IMP-DIAG — structured import diagnostic ring buffer + REST query surface (#193)** | New in-core feature adding `ImportDiagnosticsLog` (`@ApplicationScoped`, `ConcurrentHashMap<String, Deque<ImportDiagnosticEvent>>`, 10,000-event ring buffer per run, 24h eviction). `ImportDiagnosticEvent` is a plain Java record (not a Neo4j node) carrying `runId`, `timestamp`, `level` (INFO/WARN/ERROR), `phase` (WARMUP/DO_CREATE/REF_ATTACH/FILE_UPLOAD/COMPLETE), `entityAppId`, `message`, `attributes`. `runId` is the `lockId` from `ImportLock`. Four new `/v2/` endpoints: `GET /v2/import/diagnostics/{runId}` (full event list; optional `?level=` and `?phase=` filters), `GET /v2/import/runs` (list recent run IDs with start/last-event timestamps and most-severe level seen), `POST /v2/import/diagnostics/{runId}/events` (ingest a single event from the external Python importer), `POST /v2/import/diagnostics/{runId}/events/batch` (ingest multiple events in one call). `ImportLockService` is wired to emit WARMUP/INFO on `acquire()`, COMPLETE/INFO on `release()`, and COMPLETE/ERROR on `abandon()`. 35 passing unit tests: 18 in `ImportDiagnosticsLogTest` (ring-buffer overflow, concurrent writes, level/phase filtering, cleanup eviction, multi-run isolation) + 17 updated in `ImportLockServiceTest` (diagnostic event emission verified on acquire/release/abandon). **Note:** the DO_CREATE / REF_ATTACH / FILE_UPLOAD phases only appear in the ring buffer if the Python importer pushes them via the POST ingress endpoints — the Java service only captures lock-lifecycle boundaries directly. | **ZERO** | **Four new `/v2/import/` endpoints.** No new Neo4j nodes. No new Postgres tables. No migration. Events are in-memory only (lost on restart; 24h TTL). The POST ingress (`/v2/import/diagnostics/{runId}/events[/batch]`) lets the Python importer push phase events — this is the complement to the Python-side DiagSink landed in PROV-V15.11. | None — in-memory only, no migration needed. Test obligation: 35 unit tests land in this PR; testcontainer integration test deferred. | **No action required for upgrade.** The ring buffer is passive until a lock is acquired. To use: acquire a lock (`POST /v2/import/lock`), then `GET /v2/import/diagnostics/{lockId}` during or after the import. To filter errors only: `GET /v2/import/diagnostics/{lockId}?level=ERROR`. The Python importer can push per-DO events via `POST /v2/import/diagnostics/{lockId}/events` after each successful DO create. |
| **V1COMPAT.0 — `shepard-plugin-v1-compat` marker plugin (Phase 1: control plane + admin knob + deprecation observability)** | New plugin module `plugins/v1-compat/` ships the byte-stability control plane for the upstream-frozen `/shepard/api/...` surface. Phase 1 moves **zero code** — the 29 v1 REST classes and IOs stay where they are today, in core. What ships: (a) `:LegacyV1Config` Neo4j singleton with one field, `enabled: boolean` (A3b A3c pattern; mirror of `:UnhideConfig` / `:SemanticConfig`); (b) `V63__Bootstrap_legacy_v1_config.cypher` idempotent migration seeding the singleton with `enabled=true`; (c) `LegacyV1ConfigService` with a 5-second in-process read-through cache so the hot-path filter never blocks on Neo4j; (d) admin REST `/v2/admin/legacy/v1/config` (GET + RFC 7396 merge-patch) and `/v2/admin/legacy/v1/stats` (GET, in-memory hit counters per endpoint and per principal) — both gated `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`; (e) `LegacyV1GateFilter` returning HTTP 410 Gone + RFC 7807 `application/problem+json` (`v1-disabled`) when `:LegacyV1Config.enabled=false`; (f) `LegacyV1DeprecationFilter` adding three additive response headers on every `/shepard/api/...` flowing through (`Deprecation: true` + `Link: </v2/>; rel="successor-version"` per RFC 8594 + `X-Shepard-Legacy: true` fork marker the frontend banner keys on) and bumping in-memory counters; (g) frontend deprecation banner watching the `X-Shepard-Legacy` header; (h) deploy-time install default `shepard.legacy.v1.enabled=true` seeds the singleton on first start (the runtime row wins thereafter per the A3b precedence rule). Phase 2 (`aidocs/platform/103`) moves the v1 REST classes themselves into this JAR; Phase 1 is the operator-visible control plane that ships first. | **ZERO** | **Two new `/v2/admin/legacy/v1/*` endpoints; one new Neo4j label `:LegacyV1Config`; three additive RFC-standard response headers on every `/shepard/api/...` response** (`Deprecation`, `Link`, `X-Shepard-Legacy` — all additive, no client should break). No new required config keys. No `/shepard/api/...` response-body shape change. With default singleton state (`enabled=true`) every byte of every `/shepard/api/...` body and status code remains byte-identical to upstream shepard 5.2.0. Behaviour change is observable only when the operator deliberately flips the singleton via `PATCH /v2/admin/legacy/v1/config`. | `V63__Bootstrap_legacy_v1_config.cypher` — idempotent (`MERGE (c:LegacyV1Config) ON CREATE SET ...`) + `CREATE CONSTRAINT IF NOT EXISTS` on `:LegacyV1Config.appId`. Safe to re-run; never overwrites a runtime-mutated value. Test obligation: a real-Neo4j testcontainer integration that asserts the migration creates the singleton with `enabled=true` and that a second run leaves the row untouched — deferred to PR-2's integration suite. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V63 seeds the singleton with `enabled=true`. Operators choosing to flip the v1 surface off: `curl -X PATCH -H "X-API-KEY: ..." -H "Content-Type: application/merge-patch+json" -d '{"enabled":false}' https://<host>/v2/admin/legacy/v1/config` (requires `instance-admin` role). Operators monitoring v1 use: `GET /v2/admin/legacy/v1/stats`. The runtime knob is purely a singleton flip — runtime row wins; deploy-time `shepard.legacy.v1.enabled` only seeds the first time. To restore: `PATCH .../config` with `{"enabled":true}`. |

| **PROV-V15.11 — MFFD import v15.11 (structured diagnostic instrumentation)** | Follow-up to PROV-V15.9 (v15.10 was scoped in `052145cd` but never shipped as code — v15.11 supersedes). Script-only (`examples/mffd-showcase/scripts/mffd-import-v15.py` + new `tests/test_diag.py`); no backend Java, no migration. Adds the `DiagSink` class — a one-JSON-line-per-event structured emitter to stderr (atomic `os.write(2, ...)` under module-level lock, ≤4096 bytes = Linux PIPE_BUF), mirrored into the existing `Telemetry` channel (best-effort, never breaks emit). New `--diag-mode={quiet,normal,verbose,http-trace}` CLI flag (default `normal`; env `MFFD_DIAG_MODE`). 18 event kinds covering the full operational surface: startup, warmup_step, source_user_resolved, iter_start, iter_end, do_start, do_skip, do_done, do_error, http_request, http_response, worker_pool, jwt_status, manifest_poll, state_save, telemetry_flush, summary, shutdown. Pure-function `DiagSink.classify_error(http_status, error_body)` returns one of 9 stable hint codes (`jwt-expired-or-rotated`, `permission-denied`, `not-found`, `conflict`, `rate-limited`, `bean-validation`, `client-error`, `server-side`, `timeout-or-network`) with a human-readable action — emitted on `do_error` + non-2xx `http_response`. Per-DO correlation id (`<step>:<src_do_id>`) ties every related event together. **Non-2xx http responses ALWAYS emit regardless of mode** — the failure signal cannot be filtered. Credential masking (`DiagSink.mask_credential` → `sha256:<first-12-hex>`) with explicit plaintext-leak guard in the 36-case test suite. Shutdown event carries the final 60s rolling-window summary so `tmux capture-pane -p \| tail -100` after a clean exit shows the run rollup immediately. Closes the "2 AM debugging requires code spelunking" failure mode: any of 10 named failure classes (wire-shape, 401/403/404/409/429, rate limits, worker deadlock, JWT expiry, lock contention, state-file inconsistency, source-side missing content, Quarkus violations) is now diagnosable from `tmux capture-pane -t mffd -p \| tail -200` alone. 36 new pytest cases. Pattern card: `aidocs/integrations/95 §Pattern 19`. | **ZERO** | **No new endpoints.** **No new admin REST.** **No new backend config keys.** **No migration.** All changes are inside the import script — operators not running v15.11 see zero behaviour change. The deployed shepard binary is unchanged from v15.9. New diagnostic events flow into the SAME `structuredDataContainer/593753` that v15.4 IMPORT-T1 already writes to (Pattern 2) — additive event volume only, no new container, no new wire path. Stderr emission is local to the operator's host. The `--diag-mode` flag is opt-in; default `normal` is documented to produce ~5 events/sec steady state. | None — script-only follow-up. Sibling backlog row `LOG-AS-PROV-ARTEFACT` covers the dest-side consumption (lifting structured diagnostic events into PROV-O `:Activity` records). | **No action required for upgrade.** v15.11 is script-only; the deployed shepard binary is unchanged from v15.9. The running v15.9 script self-updates to v15.11 at the next graceful boundary (60s manifest poll) once the v15.11 manifest is published in `fileContainer/473932`. To use the new diagnostics: nothing — they activate automatically at default verbosity. To investigate a specific failure: `tmux capture-pane -t mffd -p \| grep -E 'kind":"(do_error\|http_response)' \| tail -20` returns the failure tail with `diagnostic_hint` per error. To increase verbosity for a single run: `--diag-mode=verbose` (adds worker_pool ticks + manifest_poll) or `--diag-mode=http-trace` (adds 2xx http response — debug only). Deferred work: LOG-AS-PROV-ARTEFACT (dest-side consumption); future v15.12+ additions to the kind taxonomy must bump the pattern doc version. |
| **PROV-V15.9 — MFFD import v15.9 (source-side user identity capture)** | Follow-up to PROV-V15.8 (worker fan-out + lazy enrichment). Script-only (`examples/mffd-showcase/scripts/mffd-import-v15.py` + new `test_user_capture.py`); no backend Java, no migration. Adds three new pure helpers on `ShepardClient` (`_decode_jwt_sub`, `_ascii_safe_header`, `resolve_self`, `apply_source_user_headers`) and a module-level `SOURCE_USER_INFO` cache populated once at startup from `GET /shepard/api/users/{jwt-sub}` on the SOURCE instance. Three forwarding channels into dest: (a) every step DataObject created on dest gets `source_user_username` + `source_user_displayName` + `source_user_email` + `source_user_instance` in its `attributes` dict; (b) `X-Source-User-*` headers injected into the dest requests.Session default headers, applied to EVERY POST/PUT/PATCH automatically (ProvenanceCaptureFilter PROV1a consumes — no per-call-site threading); (c) telemetry event `source_user_resolved` + counter (0/1 gauge) into runlog. Graceful degradation: any lookup failure (404, 403, malformed JWT, network blip, missing fields) drops the script back to v15.8 semantics, never aborts. The originally-specced dest-side `:User` mirror channel was carved out as backlog row `PROV-USER-MIRROR-ENDPOINT` after field probing on 2026-05-23 found nuclide dest exposes neither `POST /v2/admin/users` (404) nor `POST /shepard/api/users` (405) — user creation flows through OIDC/IDM, not a public REST surface. 19 new pytest cases in `test_user_capture.py` covering JWT-sub decode (UUID + username + malformed), ASCII header coercion (umlauts), and the resolve_self fallback chain (JWT-sub-404 → /users-no-path → upstream-list bail → 403 → network-blip). Pattern card: `aidocs/integrations/95 §Pattern 17`. | **ZERO** | **No new endpoints.** **No new admin REST.** **No new backend config keys.** **No migration.** All changes are inside the import script — operators not running v15.9 see zero behaviour change. The new HTTP traffic against the source is one extra `GET /shepard/api/users/{sub}` (or fallback `GET /shepard/api/users`) per import startup — normal API traffic against existing v5 endpoints. The new `X-Source-User-*` headers landing on dest writes are additive HTTP headers; if the dest's `ProvenanceCaptureFilter` doesn't yet know to consume them (PROV-USER-ENRICH backlog row not yet shipped), they sit in the request envelope unread but don't break anything. | None — script-only follow-up. | **No action required for upgrade.** v15.9 is script-only; the deployed shepard binary is unchanged from v15.8. The running v15.8 script self-updates to v15.9 at the next graceful boundary once the v15.9 manifest is published in `fileContainer/473932`. To use the new enrichment: nothing — it activates automatically when source + dest auth resolve. To verify: after an import, look for `source_user_username` in any imported DO's `attributes` map; or inspect the runlog `structuredDataContainer/593753` for `source_user_resolved` events. Deferred work (sibling backlog rows): PROV-USER-ENRICH (dest-side ProvenanceCaptureFilter consumes the headers → typed PROV-O triples on `:Activity`); PROV-USER-MIRROR-ENDPOINT (dest exposes a user-mint REST endpoint so the third attribution channel can land). |
| **PROV-V15.8 — MFFD import v15.8 (real worker fan-out + lazy enrichment)** | Follow-up to PROV-V15.7. Script-only (`examples/mffd-showcase/scripts/mffd-import-v15.py`); no backend Java, no migration. Fixes two anti-patterns in v15.1..v15.7: (PERF1) `--workers N` was a footgun — the wrapper spawned a `ThreadPoolExecutor`, ran no-op probes, then called `run_source_mode` sequentially inside; v15.8 moves the per-DO body into a callable submitted to the pool via `as_completed` drain+refill (one future per source DO, bounded at N in-flight); (PERF2) `iter_data_objects` eagerly fetched 3 cube3 GETs per source DO BEFORE the state-skip check; v15.8 yields stubs and lazy-loads refs only after the per-DO state-skip declines to short-circuit. Together: zero cube3 round-trips for fully-done DOs on resume (was 3); ~Nx real speedup on the cross-instance bottleneck. `ImportState` is now thread-safe (RLock); `existing_names` hoisted to per-step. See `aidocs/integrations/95 §Pattern 16`. | **ZERO** | **No operator-visible change.** Same script API, same wire calls, same response shapes. Speedup is observable in the existing telemetry (`dos_processed`/`heartbeat_s` gauge climbs ~Nx faster). | None — script-only follow-up. | **No action required for upgrade.** Running v15.7 self-updates to v15.8 at the next graceful boundary. To use real concurrency: set `--workers 4` (was a no-op in v15.1..v15.7; now actually parallel). |
| **PROV-V15.2 — MFFD import v15.2 (smart warmup with fail-fast diagnostics)** | Follow-up to PROV-V15.1. Script-only (`examples/mffd-showcase/scripts/_smart_warmup.py` + `mffd-import-v15.py`); no backend Java, no migration. Adds two new CLI flags (`--smart-warmup` default on, `--legacy-warmup` opt-out) and a smart warmup phase that probes auth + reads + source/dest writes + Garage before the main loop. Throwaway probe DataObjects are deleted on every exit path (success or abort). Wire-shape responses are diffed against `backend/src/test/resources/fixtures/v5/openapi-5.4.0.json` (282 KB, 90 paths) — the canonical wire-contract source for v5 source-side probes. On any failure: structured diagnostic (`verb`/`url`/`expected`/`got`/`where_to_look`/`what_to_try`/`exit_code`) and a per-failure-class exit code (auth=2, source-unreachable=3, garage-down=4, operator-interrupt=5, wire-shape-drift=6, write-permission-denied=7). 22 new stdlib unittest cases (total now 140). Closes `aidocs/16` IMPORT-W1/W2/W3. See `aidocs/agent-findings/v15.2-implementation.md`. | **ZERO** | **No operator-visible change.** Importer-only enhancement. The deployed shepard binary is unchanged from v15.1; the warmup runs entirely on the operator's host before any mutating call. The new `/v2/users/me` + `/shepard/api/users/{username}` + `/shepard/api/collections` reads + the throwaway POST/DELETE pair are normal API traffic against existing endpoints — they show up in `:Activity` provenance the same as any other call from the operator's principal. No new admin REST, no new schema, no new config keys. | None — script-only follow-up. | **No action required for upgrade.** To use the new warmup: refresh the `examples/mffd-showcase/scripts/*` checkout. The script picks up the new module automatically (`from _smart_warmup import ...`); operators who want to skip source-side write probes (e.g. when source has write disabled) can pass `--legacy-warmup` to fall back to v15.1 behaviour. The structured diagnostic block produced on failure is the one operator artefact — copy-paste it verbatim into the bug report (the script's design includes literal credentials per `feedback_no_redactions.md`, so no scrubbing needed). |
| **PROV-V15.1 — MFFD import v15.1 (snapshot brackets + per-DO AI mode + license + verifier + workers wiring)** | Follow-up to v15 (PROV-V15). v15.1 is **script-only** (`examples/mffd-showcase/scripts/mffd-import-v15.py`) — no backend Java changes, no migration. Adds five additive CLI flags (`--default-license`, `--default-access-rights`, `--name-mapping`, `--workers`, `--verify-imported`) and seven script-side capabilities closing gaps surfaced by the four persona reviews of v15 (`aidocs/agent-findings/v15-review-{data-ontologist,rdm,ime-aqe,reluctant-senior}.md`). What ships: (G1) pre-import snapshot labelled `v15-import-pre-<session>@<coll>` before the worker pool starts; (G4) post-import snapshot label changed to include `as-imported` substring (`v15-as-imported-<session>@<coll>`) so RO-Crate / regulatory pack consumers can find the canonical baseline by string match; (G5 partial) Collection-anchored `prov:wasInformedBy` + `prov:generated` annotations referencing snapshot URNs (full G5 — snapshot entity directly typed as `prov:Entity` — awaits a backend SnapshotService extension since no public turtle-ingest endpoint exists); (G2 + G8) per-step-DO F(AI)²R triples (`fair2r:modeOfProduction "ai"`, `fair2r:wasAcceptedAs "auto-applied"`, `fair2r:wasGeneratedByAi agent:claude-opus-4-7`) closing the EU AI Act Art. 50 per-artefact-visibility gap (deadline 2026-08-02); (G7) typed predecessor edges via `prov:wasInformedBy` annotation per predecessor (mirrors Neo4j `:PREDECESSOR_OF` into SHACL graph); FAIR R1 license + accessRights hydration on Collection + DOs with fail-fast gate for SOURCE/LOCAL paths (bootstrap + dry-run + verify-imported are exempt); operator name preservation via `--name-mapping <csv>` with `dcterms:alternative` annotation; minimum-viable independent verifier (`--verify-imported` walks dest, counts DOs by pattern, file refs by `fileSize>0`, TS refs with non-empty `timeseries[]`, DAG spot-check); worker-pool wiring (`--workers N`, default 1 = sequential pass-through, N>1 instantiates `ThreadPoolExecutor(N)` with bounded `Queue(256)` per aidocs/93 §5). 47 new unit tests across 7 files (`test_{snapshot_brackets,per_do_mode,license_population,typed_predecessors,name_mapping,verify_imported,worker_pool}.py`) — total now 118 stdlib unittest cases passing. | **ZERO** | **No new endpoints.** **No new admin REST.** **No new backend config keys.** **No migration.** All changes are inside the import script — operators not running the v15.1 script see zero behaviour change. The new CLI flags are additive (existing v15 CLI surface preserved byte-for-byte). When the script is invoked, the additional REST traffic against the dest is: 1 extra `POST /v2/collections/{appId}/snapshots` (pre-import), 1 extra `POST /shepard/api/collections/{c}/dataObjects/{do}/semanticAnnotations` per predecessor edge, 3 extra per step DO (modeOfProduction + wasAcceptedAs + wasGeneratedByAi), and 1 extra `POST /shepard/api/collections/{c}/semanticAnnotations` per snapshot (pre + post). For an MFFD import with 2 step DOs and 2 snapshots: ~10 additional semanticAnnotations + 1 extra snapshot — trivially observable as `:SemanticAnnotation` nodes in Neo4j. | None — script-only follow-up. Backend SnapshotService extension (auto-emit `prov:Entity` typing for new snapshots) is the planned PROV-V16 row. | **No action required for upgrade.** v15.1 is script-only; the deployed shepard binary is unchanged from v15. To use the v15.1 surface: refresh the `examples/mffd-showcase/scripts/mffd-import-v15.py` checkout, ensure the dest backend has V61 applied (carried from PROV-V15), set `--default-license=<SPDX>` + `--default-access-rights=<rights>` on the next import (the script refuses to start without them for SOURCE/LOCAL paths). Verify after import: collection carries snapshots with labels matching `v15-import-pre-*` and `v15-as-imported-*`; step DOs carry the three F(AI)²R semantic annotations (`MATCH (do:DataObject)-[r:HAS_SEMANTIC_ANNOTATION]->(a) WHERE a.propertyIRI CONTAINS 'fair2r' RETURN do.name, a.propertyIRI LIMIT 20`). To produce a verifier report: `python3 mffd-import-v15.py --verify-imported` against the same collection. Deferred work (v15.2 backlog): TPL17 Bloxberg anchoring, backend `X-AI-Agent` header consumption, mid-import snapshots, predecessor typing heuristics (rework vs normal), full G5 (auto-emit `prov:Entity` on SnapshotService.create), real-concurrency convergence tests for `--workers N>1`. |

| **INFRA-MONGO-PIN — mongo:8.0 → 8.0.4 pin (proxmox post-outage version-compat fix)** | Commit `327c7d08` pins the mongo Docker image tag in `infrastructure/docker-compose.yml` from the floating `8.0` to the explicit `8.0.4`. The proxmox outage caused the floating tag to drift to a later patch level the rest of the stack hadn't validated against, producing a boot-time version-incompatibility. Pinning to 8.0.4 stabilises the stack until the next planned mongo bump follows the documented incremental-upgrade guide (`https://gitlab.com/dlr-shepard/shepard/-/merge_requests/306`). | **ZERO** | **Operator-visible only at deploy time.** When operators do `docker compose pull`, they now get exactly mongo `8.0.4` instead of whatever `8.0`-floating points at on that day. No application code, no schema, no wire-shape change. The mongo data files are forward + backward compatible across 8.0.x patch releases. | None — image-tag pin only; no data migration. | **No action required for upgrade.** On next `docker compose pull` + restart: mongo runs at the pinned `8.0.4` patch. Operators with their own deploy infrastructure can adopt the same pin or stay on their own pinning discipline. The next mongo major bump (8.x → 9.x) will get its own row when the team validates it. |
| **INFRA-GARAGE-HEALTHCHECK — replace wget-based healthcheck with garage CLI** | Commit `997b5227` swaps the docker healthcheck for the `garage` service in `infrastructure/docker-compose.override.yml` from a wget probe to `garage status` (the only executable in the distroless `dxflrs/garage:v1.0.1` image). The container was functionally healthy throughout (backend reached Garage fine at `http://garage:3900`) but flagged `unhealthy` because the wget binary doesn't exist in the upstream image. | **ZERO** | **Cosmetic / observability-only.** `docker compose ps` now shows the Garage container as `healthy` within ~30s of start instead of perpetually `unhealthy`. No application change; no wire-shape change; no schema change. | None. | **No action required for upgrade.** Operators following the Garage activation runbook get the corrected healthcheck on next pull of `infrastructure/docker-compose.override.yml`. Verify after restart: `docker inspect shepard-garage --format '{{.State.Health.Status}}'` returns `healthy`. |

Policy `stage: feature-defined` → `stage: deployed` with edits to §3 (backfill ALLOWED with transparency markers, NOT auto-fire), §8 (added §8.1 marker template for retroactive Issues + Milestones), and §15 (new anti-pattern #7 — "Filing backfilled Issues without a BACKFILL disclosure marker — silent forgery of the audit trail"). Synthesis: `aidocs/agent-findings/gh-pm-adoption-synthesis-2026-05-23.md`. Backfill dry-run plan (no `gh issue create` fires until operator approves): `aidocs/agent-findings/gh-pm-backfill-plan-2026-05-23.md`. Enhancement backlog (24 element-anchored rows from the 5 personas): new `### GH-PM-ENH — 2026-05-23` section in `aidocs/16`. Per `feedback_no_synthetic_provenance.md` in agent memory: retroactive artefacts ALWAYS carry a per-artefact transparency marker. | **ZERO** | **Policy / process documentation only.** No application change; no wire-shape change; no schema change.
