Per `feedback_three_audience_docs.md`, the scattered admin documentation (`docs/admin.md` single file + `docs/deploy.md` + `docs/system-requirements.md` + `docs/ops/*.md` + `docs/install/*.md`) was consolidated into a structured `docs/admin/` subtree: `index.md`, `system-requirements.md`, `install.md`, `config.md`, `upgrade.md`, `backup.md`, `storage.md`, `auth.md`, `observability.md`, `security.md` — 10 pages, each carrying `audience: admin` + `stage: deployed` front-matter. Maintainer-audience pages (`docs/ops/cut-a-release.md`, `docs/ops/github-projects-board-setup.md`) deliberately kept at `docs/ops/` since their audience is the fork-maintainer cutting releases, not the operator running an instance. The two storage runbooks (`docs/ops/garage-activation-runbook.md` 17 KB + `docs/ops/migrate-gridfs-to-s3.md` 14 KB) kept where they are and linked from `docs/admin/storage.md` (inlining would violate the 200-line page rule). `docs/install/superset.md` kept where it is (integrator audience, linked from `docs/admin/install.md §Integrations`). Backlog row `DOCS-3A1` flipped from `queued` to `done` in `aidocs/16`. | **ZERO** | **Pages-only reshape.** No application change. Operator-facing URL changes: `/admin` → `/admin/`, `/deploy` → `/admin/install/`, `/system-requirements` → `/admin/system-requirements/`. **Old URLs continue to work** — redirect stubs at `docs/admin.md`, `docs/deploy.md`, `docs/system-requirements.md` carry both `<meta http-equiv="refresh">` and a JavaScript `window.location.replace` for belt-and-braces redirect. Top nav + footer + `docs/index.md` updated to point at the new URLs. Old paths flagged for removal in v6.1. | None. | **No action required for upgrade.** Operators bookmarking the old `/admin` URL get redirected automatically; the bookmark continues to work until v6.1, after which they should update to `/admin/`. |
| **PROV-RESOLVER-PATHWALK + PROV-V1-NUMERIC-LOOKUP — provenance capture stamps the correct target** | 2026-05-24. Closes RDM-2026-05-24-004 buckets B + C. The capture filter's target-resolver was rewritten in two layers: (1) a pure `PathTargetParser` that walks the request path **right-to-left** for the deepest known `(kind-plural, id)` pair (UUID **or** pure-numeric); (2) a CDI-bean `TargetEntityResolver` + new `EntityAppIdLookup` helper that exchanges a numeric `shepardId` for the entity's `appId` via one parameterised Cypher over the static label allow-list (`MATCH (n:<Label>) WHERE n.shepardId=$id ...`). Two structural wins: subresource creates (`POST /v2/collections/<C>/data-objects/<D>`) now attribute to the **DataObject**, not the **Collection** with kind=DataObject; v1 numeric writes (`PATCH /shepard/api/collections/42/dataObjects/45/...`) resolve to the correct appId via the lookup helper. The old title-case fallback for unknown plural segments is gone — verb-shaped segments like `payload` / `diff` / `detect-anomalies` can no longer pollute `targetKind`. **`aidocs/16` `PROV-BACKFILL-MIGRATION`** is filed separately for the optional retro-resolution of the ~280K orphaned historic `:Activity` rows (deferred so this PR stays shippable; the resolver fix alone prevents the bug recurring on every new write). | **AWARE** | **Zero new endpoints.** **Zero new config keys.** The wire shape of `GET /v2/provenance/activities` is byte-unchanged — same `ActivityIO` row shape, same JSON keys. The difference is **content**: rows captured post-deploy now carry `targetKind` + `targetAppId` filled correctly across both shelves (`/v2/` UUIDs **and** `/shepard/api/` numeric IDs). No frontend changes needed; `DataObjectProvLog.vue` was already filtering by `targetAppId` — the panel now actually finds matching rows. **No new dependencies.** The label allow-list in `EntityAppIdLookup.ALLOWED_LABELS` is kept in sync with `PathTargetParser.PLURAL_TO_KIND` via a unit-test invariant (`EntityAppIdLookupTest#allowListCoversEveryKindFromParser`) so drift is detected pre-merge. The Cypher label interpolation is defended by a static `Pattern` regex + the allow-list (no user input ever reaches the label slot). | None — substrate-level behaviour change. The optional Cypher retro-backfill of 280K orphaned `:Activity` rows is queued separately as `aidocs/16 PROV-BACKFILL-MIGRATION` so this PR doesn't block on a large-row migration. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the next 2xx mutation on any path will land an `:Activity` row with `targetKind` + `targetAppId` correctly stamped. Historic orphaned rows stay `NULL` until the optional backfill ships. To verify on a fresh deploy: `curl -X PATCH .../shepard/api/collections/<id>/dataObjects/<id> ...` (or any v2 write) → `MATCH (a:Activity) WHERE a.startedAtMillis > <recentMs> RETURN a.targetKind, a.targetAppId LIMIT 5;` should show non-NULL fields. To audit the impact on the orphan count: `MATCH (a:Activity) WHERE a.targetKind IS NULL RETURN count(a);` — pre-fix counts ~280 K on the live deploy; post-fix the count should stop growing and (after the optional backfill) drop materially. Test obligation: 30+ new unit tests (parser + resolver + capture filter + lookup) landed in this PR; live e2e at `e2e/tests/rdm-004b-provenance-resolves-targets.spec.ts` verifies an end-to-end PATCH → Provenance-panel-row walk against `BASE_URL=https://shepard.nuclide.systems`. |
| **OPS-MIGRATION-HEALTHCHECK — readiness gate on Neo4j migration-chain integrity** | 2026-05-24. New SmallRye readiness check `neo4j-migration-chain-readiness` (`de.dlr.shepard.common.healthz.MigrationChainReadinessCheck`) asserts on every probe that every classpath `V**` migration is APPLIED with a matching checksum on the live Neo4j. Closes a hidden production risk surfaced via UI-020 (2026-05-24): V61 (`v15_prov_predicates`, commit `0c6ead4b` from 2026-05-22) was on `main` for two days but had never been applied to the live deploy; the existing readiness probe reported green throughout because it only ping-checked Neo4j connectivity. Implementation delegates to the migrations library's own `Migrations.validate()` + `Migrations.info(ChainBuilderMode.COMPARE)` rather than reimplementing the CRC32 — same code paths that gate `migrations.apply()`. Result cached for `shepard.health.readiness.max-staleness` (existing key, default 30 s). Inspector logic is unit-tested with 6 scenarios; the live extractor path is exercised by an `@Tag("integration")` testcontainer test (`MigrationChainInspectorIT`) that applies a 2-step chain, then drops a `V99__never_applied.cypher` into the same dir and asserts the readiness flips DOWN with `pendingVersions: ["99"]`. Operator runbook `docs/admin/runbooks/migration-chain-integrity.md` covers diagnosis + the four `Outcome` shapes (`INCOMPLETE_MIGRATIONS`, `DIFFERENT_CONTENT`, `INCOMPLETE_DATABASE`, `CHECK_FAILED`) + the UI-020-style worked example for manually splicing V61 into the chain (including the reverse-engineered `CRC32` algorithm reference). | **ZERO** | **One new readiness check in `/shepard/api/healthz/ready`.** The check shows up in the `checks[]` array as `neo4j-migration-chain-readiness`; aggregate `status` flips to DOWN if the chain is broken (joining `neo4j-readiness`, `mongodb-readiness`, `timescaledb-readiness` already in that array). **No new endpoints, no new config keys** (reuses existing `shepard.health.readiness.max-staleness`). **No schema migration** — read-only against the existing `__Neo4jMigration` chain. **Behaviour change**: an operator whose live Neo4j has somehow skipped a migration will now get a 503 from `/healthz/ready` instead of silently serving the wrong schema; this is the desired end-state but means an existing operator with a drift discovers it on next probe. | No new migrations. | **No action required for the readiness check itself** — it's automatic on backend restart. **Action MAY be required for the chain it reveals**: re-poll `/shepard/api/healthz/ready` after upgrade; if `neo4j-migration-chain-readiness` is `DOWN`, follow `docs/admin/runbooks/migration-chain-integrity.md` (most often: just restart the backend, which lets `MigrationsRunner.apply()` catch up; rarely: manually splice a migration node per the runbook's worked example). The existing `MigrationsRunner` abort-on-error guard already prevents *new* drift on every clean restart; the readiness check catches the historical drift that accumulated before the gate was tight. |
| **UI-020 — bulk lab-journal-entries endpoint replaces a per-DataObject N+1 fan-out** | 2026-05-24. New `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/lab-journal-entries` endpoint (`CollectionLabJournalEntriesRest` under `de.dlr.shepard.v2.labjournal.resources`): single-query Cypher walk `Collection → DataObject → LabJournalEntry` returning every non-deleted entry in one round-trip, sorted by `createdAt DESC`, each carrying `dataObjectId` so the frontend can group client-side. Closes a production-affecting hot bug: opening the MFFD-Dropbox collection (~8500 DataObjects) previously fired ~8514 concurrent `GET /shepard/api/labJournalEntries?dataObjectId=N` requests from `CollectionLabJournalEntryList.vue`'s `Promise.all` fan-out, exhausting the browser socket pool and producing ~8311 console errors. Post-fix: **1 lab-journal request, 2 console errors** (both unrelated to lab-journal) — verified live on both MFFD-Dropbox and LUMEN. Auth: Read on the Collection (same `EntityIdResolver.resolveLong(appId)` + `PermissionsService.isAccessTypeAllowedForUser` pattern as `CollectionContainersRest`). 401/404/403 ordering matches the pattern. Response items reuse the existing `LabJournalEntryIO` shape (no new IO class). Backend-client adds `CollectionLabJournalEntriesApi.ts` (manual edit per the established backend-client workflow). Frontend adds `useFetchCollectionLabJournalEntries.ts` composable + updates `CollectionLabJournalEntryList.vue` to use it and pass-through `collectionAppId` from the parent collection detail page. Tests: 6/6 JUnit (`CollectionLabJournalEntriesRestTest` — auth ordering + happy path + empty case + perm check), 9/9 Vitest (`useFetchCollectionLabJournalEntries.test.ts` — single-call invariant, null appId, error fallback, refetch on change, plus `groupByDataObjectId` helper), 1/1 Playwright (`ui-020-labjournal-no-n+1.spec.ts` against `BASE_URL=https://shepard.nuclide.systems` — asserts ≤5 lab-journal requests + ≤50 console errors on MFFD-Dropbox; observed 1 + 2). Also bundles a pre-existing TS compile fix in `useV1DeprecationMiddleware.ts` (Middleware.post signature stricter after backend-client regen — `Promise<Response \| void> \| void` → `async ... Promise<Response \| void>`) that was blocking the frontend build. | **ZERO** | **One new `/v2/` endpoint**: `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/lab-journal-entries`. **No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path** — the legacy `GET /shepard/api/labJournalEntries?dataObjectId=N` endpoint stays byte-frozen for upstream-client compat. **No new config keys.** **No Neo4j migration** — queries the existing `Collection → DataObject → LabJournalEntry` graph. **No new node labels.** Frontend image rebuild required to pick up the composable + component changes; backend image rebuild required to expose the new endpoint. | No new migrations. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard (backend + frontend); the new endpoint appears in the OpenAPI spec at `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/lab-journal-entries`. The frontend automatically uses it on the collection detail page's Lab Journal expansion panel — operators see no UI change beyond MFFD-scale pages now loading without the browser-stalling socket exhaustion. Operators who never open a >100-DO collection see no functional change. To test directly: `curl /v2/collections/<collectionAppId>/lab-journal-entries -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>"` returns the full entry array in one call. |
| **RDM-005 — Metadata Completeness Score widget on Collection landing** | 2026-05-24. New frontend-only card on the Collection landing page (`pages/collections/[collectionId]/index.vue`, slotted between the RDM-001 Cite-this card and the Semantic Annotations section) that renders a 0–100 metadata completeness score with a red/amber/green chip (red < 50, amber 50–79, green ≥ 80) and a collapsible per-check list of 9 FAIR checks: name + description-≥-50-chars + license (LIC1) + accessRights (LIC1) + creator-ORCID (RDM-002) + ≥1 semantic annotation + ≥1 lab journal entry + heroImage + ≥1 DataObject. Each check carries a "Why this matters" tooltip mapping to the DataCite §N + FAIR sub-principle, and missing checks expose a tonal action button that scrolls smoothly to the relevant on-page edit affordance (description / license-chip-strip / annotations heading / lab-journal panel / hero-image area / DataObjects panel) with a 1.6s primary-glow pulse — the ORCID check routes to `/me` instead. Component tree: pure scorer `frontend/utils/metadataCompleteness.ts` (no I/O, 36 Vitest cases covering invariants + per-check pass/fail + band classification + boundary tests), widget `frontend/components/context/collection/MetadataCompletenessCard.vue` (fetches `SemanticAnnotationApi.getAllCollectionAnnotations` + `useFetchCollectionLabJournalEntries` + `UserApi.getUser({username: createdBy}).orcid` for the three off-shape inputs; auth-settle race fixed via watch on `useAuth().data.accessToken`), plus 5 Playwright e2e cases on live (`e2e/tests/rdm-005-metadata-completeness.spec.ts`). Closes top-5 #5 from `aidocs/agent-findings/rdm-scrutinizer-2026-05-24.md`. Live LUMEN + MFFD-Dropbox both score 40/100 (red) at deploy — name + description + dataObjects pass; license, accessRights, creatorOrcid, semanticAnnotation, labJournal, heroImage fail. | **ZERO** | **No backend change.** No new endpoint, no new config key, no migration. Reads only fields/endpoints already present post-LIC1 + RDM-002. Frontend image rebuild required to surface the new card. Operators on a previous frontend image continue to see the Collection landing without the score chip — no breakage. The widget plants six anchor ids on the parent page (`#metadata-description-section`, `#metadata-license-edit`, `#metadata-annotations-section`, `#metadata-labjournal-section`, `#metadata-heroimage-edit`, `#metadata-dataobjects-section`) — fully additive, no removed surface. | None — frontend-only. Test obligation: **closed in same PR** (36 Vitest + 5 Playwright e2e cases). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart the frontend container (`make redeploy-frontend`); the Metadata Completeness card appears on every Collection landing. Operators see real per-Collection FAIR completeness scores from first paint, with one-click jumps to each editor for the missing fields. The total-points invariant is pinned at 100 by a unit test, so any future check addition surfaces in code review. Follow-ups tracked as `RDM-005a` in `aidocs/16`: server-side completeness endpoint for harvest pipelines, child-DO annotation aggregation, embargo-date + funder-reference checks, "show only failing" filter. |
| **RDM-001 — "Cite this dataset" card on Collection landing** | 2026-05-24. New frontend-only card on the Collection landing page (`pages/collections/[collectionId]/index.vue`, slotted directly below the Description) that renders a copy-paste citation in four standards-track formats: APA-flavoured plain text, BibTeX (`@dataset{...}` per biblatex), RIS (`TY  - DATA` per the RIS spec), and CSL JSON (`type: dataset` per citationstyles.org). Inputs are read from the existing Collection wire shape — `name` for title, `createdBy` for the single-author slot, `createdAt` year for the publication year, `license` (post-LIC1) when set, and the canonical landing URL. License-absent path **omits the license line entirely** (no "unlicensed" / "no license" leak). Component tree: `frontend/utils/citation.ts` (pure formatter, no I/O), `frontend/components/context/collection/CiteThisCard.vue` (Vuetify `v-tabs` + per-tab `<pre>`-rendered citation + Copy button using existing `@vueuse/core` `useClipboard`), plus 46 Vitest cases (4 formats × license-present/absent × single/multi-author = 16 matrix + 30 edge-case probes for BibTeX escaping, RIS CRLF, CSL date-parts, Anonymous fallback) and a Playwright e2e (`e2e/tests/rdm-001-cite-this-dataset.spec.ts`) verifying live render on `/collections/42` (LUMEN) + `/collections/661923` (MFFD-Dropbox, exercises null-license branch when the collection has no license set). Closes the highest per-day-of-effort finding from the RDM Scrutinizer (`aidocs/agent-findings/rdm-scrutinizer-2026-05-24.md §Top-5 #1`). | **ZERO** | **No backend change.** No new endpoint. No new config key. No migration. No change to any wire shape. The card consumes fields the post-LIC1 wire shape already carries (`name`, `createdBy`, `createdAt`, `license`, `accessRights`). Frontend image rebuild required to surface the new card. Operators on a previous frontend image continue to see the collection landing without the citation block — no breakage. | None — frontend-only. Test obligation: **closed in same PR** (46 Vitest + 5 Playwright e2e cases). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart the frontend container (`make redeploy-frontend`); the "Cite this dataset" card appears on every Collection landing. Operators who pre-LIC1 left `license` unset see the citation without the license line — once they populate `license` via the Edit dialog (LIC1), the next render includes it. The single-author rendering uses `createdBy` (a username); a future richer-author surface (`contributors[]` edge, `authors` attribute) plugs into the same `formatCitation(authors: string[], ...)` signature without UI changes. Citation-key for BibTeX is deterministic (`shepard-<collectionId>-<year>`) so re-running the citation produces stable references. |
| **BUG-LJ-V1-COLL-ID — bulk lab-journal-entries endpoint NPEs on collections with entries** | 2026-05-24. Follow-up to UI-020 (row above). The endpoint shipped 2026-05-24 morning silently broke on any collection that actually had entries — MFFD-Dropbox returned 200 [] because it has no lab journal entries, hiding the bug; LUMEN (4 seeded entries) returned HTTP 500. Surfaced by the RDM-005 widget agent's browser-console capture. **Root cause** (NOT the brief's `v1-numeric-id` hypothesis — that was empirically wrong; LUMEN's Collection node has `appId` populated and the resolver succeeds): `CollectionLabJournalEntriesDAO`'s `RETURN DISTINCT lje` Cypher projection did not hydrate the INCOMING `has_labjournalentry` edge back to DataObject, so `LabJournalEntry.getDataObject()` came back null on every entry and `LabJournalEntryIO`'s constructor NPEd on `dataObject.getShepardId()`. **Fix**: DAO Cypher now projects a depth-1 neighbourhood path (`MATCH path=(lje)-[*0..1]-(n) WHERE n.deleted=false OR n.deleted IS NULL RETURN lje, nodes(path), relationships(path)`) — the canonical `CypherQueryHelper.getReturnPart` pattern — so the OGM hydrates both directions. Resource adds a defensive orphan-skip + WARN log as belt-and-braces (any LJE that somehow still lacks its back-reference is skipped rather than 500ing the whole bulk response). 1 new JUnit test pins the orphan-skip safety net (`list_skipsOrphanEntryWithNullDataObjectInsteadOf500`); 1 new Playwright e2e (`e2e/tests/bug-lj-v1-coll-id.spec.ts`) drives the regression from the UI side on `BASE_URL=https://shepard.nuclide.systems`. Filed `aidocs/16 OGM-HYDRATION-AUDIT` follow-up to sweep sibling v2 bulk endpoints for the same class of bug. | **ZERO** | **No wire change.** Same endpoint, same response shape, same `LabJournalEntryIO` — content is now correctly populated on every entry instead of returning HTTP 500. No new endpoint, no new config key, no migration, no new node label. Backend image rebuild required to pick up the DAO + resource changes. Operators on the pre-fix UI-020 image continue to see 500s on Lab Journal panels of seeded LUMEN-shaped collections (with entries) — the fix is the unblock. | None — substrate-level Cypher fix. Test obligation: **closed in same PR** (7 JUnit cases total in `CollectionLabJournalEntriesRestTest` + 1 Playwright e2e). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard (`make redeploy-backend`); the next `GET /v2/collections/{appId}/lab-journal-entries` against a collection with entries returns the full bulk payload instead of HTTP 500. To verify post-deploy: `curl -fsS -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" https://shepard.nuclide.systems/v2/collections/019e30b0-99a2-79e7-b7d8-c15396095b42/lab-journal-entries` should return a JSON array with at least 4 entries (the LUMEN seed: TR-004, Anomaly Investigation, TR-006, Publications) — each entry's `dataObjectId` field is populated. WARN log entry `Skipping orphan LabJournalEntry (appId=…)` only appears if a node with a missing back-reference exists; on a clean seed there are none. |
| **LIC1 — License + accessRights UI + tests (completes FAIR-1)** | Builds on FAIR-1 (above): the backend already shipped the `license` + `accessRights` fields on `AbstractDataObject` / `AbstractDataObjectIO` via V57. This slice (a) tightens the JavaDoc on both layers to match `aidocs/semantics/98 §4.1` (accessRights is the uppercase enum `OPEN \| RESTRICTED \| CLOSED \| EMBARGOED`, license is an SPDX identifier expression or `"PROPRIETARY"`); (b) adds an `enumeration` hint to the OpenAPI `@Schema` on `accessRights` so generated clients see the enum; (c) lands 14 Jackson serialisation tests pinning NON_NULL omission + round-trip on both Collection and DataObject IOs (the prior FAIR-1 row noted this test obligation as deferred — now closed); (d) ships the frontend: new `LicenseChip` / `AccessRightsChip` / `LicenseInput` / `AccessRightsInput` components, FAIR metadata strip on both detail pages, both fields wired into all four create/edit dialogs, plus 13 Vitest cases on the curated SPDX_LICENSES + accessRights enum. | **ZERO** | **Additive everywhere.** No new endpoint, no new migration (V57 already exists). The wire shape is unchanged for v5.2.0 clients: both fields stay NON_NULL-omitted on the wire when unset, so freshly-created entities serialise byte-identical to upstream (verified by the v5 fixture corpus). Setting either field via PATCH produces a `/shepard/api/` response that includes the new key — upstream clients ignore unknown JSON keys, so this is also a no-op for them. | None — V57 covers it. Test obligation from FAIR-1: **closed** (14 backend serialisation tests + 13 frontend Vitest cases land in this PR). Wire-fidelity regression: `DataObjectV5WireFidelityIT` / `CollectionV5WireFidelityIT` pass without fixture changes. | **No action required.** Existing entities continue to return without these fields. Researchers populate them via the new Edit dialog (visible in basic + advanced mode) or via PATCH. The `accessRights` enum is enforced client-side; the server accepts any String for forward-compat. Deferred: `embargo_until` date field, PID strategy, full SHACL substrate integration (`aidocs/semantics/98 §4.1`). |

| **CADDY-API-PASSTHROUGH — main hostname now serves the REST + MCP surfaces** | 2026-05-24. Closes Digital Native persona §0 (`aidocs/agent-findings/persona-digital-native-2026-05-24.md`). The Caddyfile (`infrastructure/proxy/Caddyfile`) gained three `handle` blocks routing `/v2/*` + `/shepard/api/*` + `/shepard/doc/*` directly to `backend:8080`. Before this change, programmatic callers with `X-API-KEY` / `Bearer` arriving at `https://shepard.nuclide.systems/v2/...` hit the Nuxt frontend which 302'd to NextAuth signin — the API surface was effectively undiscoverable at the public hostname unless the operator knew about the separate `shepard-api.nuclide.systems` vhost (which is documented only in compose-file comments). Post-fix the main hostname returns `HTTP/2 401` with `content-type: application/json` (backend's own auth challenge) for unauthenticated calls; authenticated calls land at the backend's resource. The Nuxt frontend's own API calls go through `NUXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_API_URL=https://shepard-api.nuclide.systems/shepard/api` so there's no SSR-side routing collision; `/api/auth/*` (NextAuth, same-origin in Nuxt) is preserved as the catch-all still routes there. The more-specific `handle /v2/mcp*` block keeps its SSE-friendly 24h timeouts. New user-facing doc `docs/help/api-access.md` (`stage: deployed`) covers API-key minting, OpenAPI client generation, MCP from Claude, common failure modes. **Caddy bind-mount inode-drift gotcha** filed as `LIM-DOCKER-BIND-MOUNT-INODE` in `aidocs/16` — single-file bind mounts in Docker pin the inode at container-start, so editor tools that atomic-replace the file (Write/`mv -f`) don't propagate until container restart; `caddy reload` against the file path won't help. Operator-facing implication: any future Caddyfile edit needs `docker compose restart caddy` (brief ~3s gateway blink), not `caddy reload`. | **ZERO** | **Additive routing change at the gateway only.** No backend change, no frontend change, no new endpoint, no new config key, no migration. The Caddyfile is the operator-visible artefact — operators who diff their config against upstream will see four new `handle` blocks. **Compat surface stays byte-identical**: the `shepard-api.nuclide.systems` vhost (Zoraxy → backend directly) is unchanged; existing clients hitting that hostname continue to work. **New affordance** for clients hitting the main hostname with an API key / Bearer: they get the real 401/200/etc. from the backend instead of an HTML signin page. The web UI behaviour is unchanged — browser users still get the SPA at `/` and every other non-API path. **MCP path stays canonical**: `/v2/mcp/sse` continues to route through the SSE-tuned handle (the more-specific block matches first in Caddy semantics). | None — gateway-config change. | **No application restart needed**, but a `docker compose restart caddy` IS needed because of the bind-mount inode gotcha above (`caddy reload` is NOT sufficient on this mount shape). Diff your `infrastructure/proxy/Caddyfile` against upstream; if you have your own custom Caddyfile, splice the three new `handle` blocks in front of the catch-all (order matters — Caddy `handle` matches longest-prefix-first, so the existing catch-all stays last). Verify with `curl -fsI https://shepard.nuclide.systems/v2/collections` (expect HTTP/2 401 with `content-type: application/json` and `via: 1.1 Caddy`) and `curl -fsI https://shepard.nuclide.systems/shepard/doc/openapi/v2.json` (expect HTTP/2 200 with the OpenAPI spec body). If those don't pass, your reverse proxy is still routing the API surface through the frontend; restart Caddy and re-verify. |
| **NEO-002-BACKFILL — ShepardFile.providerId NULL → 'gridfs' (substrate-only)** | 2026-05-24. One-shot Cypher backfill `MATCH (sf:ShepardFile) WHERE sf.providerId IS NULL SET sf.providerId = 'gridfs'`. Pre-state on live: 12 253 NULL + 71 gridfs + 33 s3. Post-state: 12 327 gridfs + 33 s3 + **0 NULL** (12 256 rows updated; +3 drift attributable to MFFD ingest during the backfill itself). The Cypher is idempotent — re-running it sets nothing if every row is already non-NULL. The V34 backfill that originally introduced this field only covered the 71 pre-existing rows; every subsequent `:ShepardFile` written before today's backfill landed with `providerId IS NULL`, and the read-side code path (`FileContainerService`) NULL-coalesces to `gridfs` so the substrate gap was invisible from the API. Closes the substrate side of NEO-AUDIT-2026-05-24-002. The formal migration carrier (V79) + write-path guard landed 2026-05-26 — see NEO-002-V79 row below. | **AWARE** | **One Cypher write to live Neo4j; zero schema change, zero migration node landed.** `:ShepardFile.providerId` keeps the same set of allowed values (`{gridfs, s3}`); the change is purely data. **No wire shape change.** `FileContainerService` continues to NULL-coalesce on the read path. **No new endpoint, no new config key.** | `V79__backfill_shepardfile_providerid.cypher` is the formal migration carrier (see NEO-002-V79 row). | **For operators who applied the out-of-band Cypher manually**: V79 is idempotent and will find 0 NULL rows; the migration runner records it as applied and moves on cleanly. |
| **NEO-002-V79 — V79 formal migration + write-path guard (NEO-AUDIT-002 full close)** | 2026-05-26. Completes NEO-AUDIT-2026-05-24-002 (see NEO-002-BACKFILL row above for the substrate-only 2026-05-24 fix). Three pieces: (1) `V79__backfill_shepardfile_providerid.cypher` — same `MATCH (sf:ShepardFile) WHERE sf.providerId IS NULL SET sf.providerId = 'gridfs'` Cypher that was applied out-of-band on 2026-05-24, now the formal migration carrier so a fresh upstream-fork upgrade runs it via the standard Flyway-style runner on first start. Fully idempotent (0 rows touched if already clean). (2) `V79_R__backfill_shepardfile_providerid.cypher` — operator-runnable rollback using `REMOVE sf.providerId` (mirrors V34_R pattern). (3) Write-path guard: `Objects.requireNonNull(file.getProviderId(), "ShepardFile.providerId must be set before persistence (NEO-AUDIT-002)")` added immediately before both `fileContainerDAO.createOrUpdate(...)` calls in `FileContainerService.createFile()` and `commitUpload()`. Belt-and-braces: the `effectiveProviderId()` NULL-coalesce is **kept in place** to handle any test fixtures or race-window rows transiently arriving NULL. 2 new unit tests (`getFile_nullProviderIdRowFallsBackToGridfs`, `getFile_blankProviderIdRowFallsBackToGridfs`) lock in the coalesce behaviour. Note: task spec named the file `V79__backfill_shepherdfile_providerid.cypher` (extra 'h') — corrected to `shepardfile` to match the consistent codebase spelling. | **ZERO** | **Additive only.** No new endpoint, no new config key, no new node label, no new relationship type. V79 Cypher is data-only (sets `providerId` on NULL rows); any instance already fixed by the out-of-band Cypher sees no change. The `Objects.requireNonNull` guard is an in-process assertion — only reachable if a future refactor breaks the stamp-before-persist contract; under the current call path it is dead code. Wire shape unchanged. | `V79__backfill_shepardfile_providerid.cypher` (idempotent, < 100 ms on 12k rows). Rollback: `V79_R__backfill_shepardfile_providerid.cypher`. Tests: `FileContainerServiceTest.getFile_nullProviderIdRowFallsBackToGridfs` + `getFile_blankProviderIdRowFallsBackToGridfs`. | **No action required for upgrade.** V79 runs automatically on first start. To verify: `MATCH (sf:ShepardFile) WHERE sf.providerId IS NULL RETURN count(sf);` — must return 0 after restart. |

| **SHACL-PR5 — `predicate_vocabulary` Postgres table + `GET /v2/shapes/predicates`** | 2026-05-26. Flyway migration `V1.16.0__Add_predicate_vocabulary.sql` creates a `predicate_vocabulary` table in TimescaleDB/Postgres mapping 51 `shepard:` predicate URIs to their authoritative substrate (neo4j 41 / postgres 8 / garage 2). Table has `CHECK` constraints on `substrate IN ('neo4j','timescaledb','postgres','garage')` and `cardinality IN ('one','many')`. Seed covers predicates from `shepard-core-shapes.ttl`, `mini-shapes.ttl`, `ledger-anchor-shapes.ttl`, `pipeline-shapes.ttl`, `rep-shapes.ttl`, `dqr-shapes.ttl`, and V61 prov predicates. New REST endpoint `GET /v2/shapes/predicates` (`ShapesPredicatesRest`) returns the full table as JSON; optional `?substrate=neo4j\|timescaledb\|postgres\|garage` filter. `PredicateVocabularyRepository` (`AgroalDataSource` JDBC, read-only, `@ApplicationScoped`) provides `findAll()`, `findByPredicate(uri)`, `findBySubstrate(substrate)`. 13 unit tests (`PredicateVocabularyRepositoryTest` 8 + `ShapesPredicatesRestTest` 5). | **ZERO** | **One new `/v2/` endpoint**: `GET /v2/shapes/predicates`. **One new Postgres table**: `predicate_vocabulary`. **No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path.** **No new config key.** **No Neo4j migration.** Gated `@RolesAllowed("authenticated")`. UI stub: N/A — this is a developer/agent-facing vocabulary endpoint with no direct user surface. | `backend/src/main/resources/db/migration/V1.16.0__Add_predicate_vocabulary.sql` — idempotent (`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` + `INSERT … ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`). Rollback: `DROP TABLE IF EXISTS predicate_vocabulary;`. Tests: `PredicateVocabularyRepositoryTest` + `ShapesPredicatesRestTest`. Testcontainer integration test (full migration + seed count assertion) deferred. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart the backend; V1.16.0 applies on first start. To verify: `curl -s "https://shepard.nuclide.systems/v2/shapes/predicates" -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \| jq 'group_by(.substrate) \| map({substrate: .[0].substrate, count: length})'` — should show `neo4j: 41`, `postgres: 8`, `garage: 2`. |

| **TPL2b — `POST /v2/shapes/render` (VIEW_RECIPE stateless projection)** | 2026-05-26. New stateless endpoint `POST /v2/shapes/render` (`ShapesRenderRest` under `de.dlr.shepard.v2.shapes.resources`). Sibling to the existing `POST /v2/shapes/validate`. Takes `{templateAppId, focusShepardId, mediaType?}`; looks up the `ShepardTemplate` by appId via `ShepardTemplateDAO.findByAppId`; validates `templateKind == "VIEW_RECIPE"` (422 otherwise); parses the template body JSON for `renderer` hint and `channelBindings[]` array; returns `{templateAppId, focusShepardId, renderer, channelBindings[{role, channelSelector, unit, required, status="DECLARED", resolved=null}]}`. Beta cut (TPL2b): all bindings carry `status="DECLARED"` — live channel resolution against the focus DataObject's TS references is deferred to TPL2c until `aidocs/platform/87` TS-ID migration provides a stable single-key channel identity. Non-VIEW_RECIPE `templateKind` values return 422 with a message pointing to `GET /v2/templates?kind=view`. Wire companion: `ShapesRenderRequestIO`, `ShapesRenderResponseIO` (with nested `ChannelBindingProjectionIO` + `ResolvedChannelIO` records). Frontend: `shapes-render` placeholder entry added to `frontend/components/common/placeholder/placeholderRegistry.ts` (`surface: "route"`, `backend: "shipped"`). 9 JUnit unit tests (`ShapesRenderRestTest`): null body, missing templateAppId, missing focusShepardId, template-not-found, non-VIEW_RECIPE kind, declared bindings with unit, empty bindings, null renderer. | **ZERO** | **One new `/v2/` endpoint**: `POST /v2/shapes/render`. **No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path.** **No new config key.** **No Neo4j migration** — read-only call to the existing `ShepardTemplate` node via `findByAppId`. **No new node labels.** The endpoint is gated `@RolesAllowed("authenticated")` — the same auth posture as sibling `POST /v2/shapes/validate`. `@Tag(name = "Shapes (v2)")` so it appears in the correct OpenAPI tag group. Backend image rebuild required to expose the new endpoint. | None. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart the backend; `POST /v2/shapes/render` appears in the OpenAPI spec under the "Shapes (v2)" tag. To verify: `curl -X POST https://shepard.nuclide.systems/v2/shapes/render -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"templateAppId":"<view-recipe-appid>","focusShepardId":"<dataobject-appid>"}' | jq .` — should return 200 with `channelBindings[]` each carrying `"status":"DECLARED"`. A non-VIEW_RECIPE templateAppId returns 422 with a human-readable message. |

| **TPL2-M1 — VIEW_RECIPE frontend renderer dispatch + MFFD LBR demonstrator (#83)** | 2026-05-26. M1 milestone closing the loop between `POST /v2/shapes/render` (TPL2b) and the frontend. Two changes: (1) `frontend/pages/shapes/render.vue` now branches on the `renderer` field — `"trace-3d"` or `"tresjs"` dispatches to `<Trace3DView>` (flat-array adapter + colour-bar legend); `"table"` dispatches to an inline `<v-table>`; unknown renderer hints show a `<v-alert>` listing supported values. (2) `examples/mffd-showcase/shapes.py` — standalone Python script that POSTs a VIEW_RECIPE ShepardTemplate with renderer=tresjs and 4 LBR iiwa bindings: force_x_N/force_y_N/force_z_N as x/y/z roles, torque_z_Nm as colour. M1 tracker: `aidocs/platform/83-tpl1-tpl2-shapes-templates-views.md`. | **ZERO** | **Frontend-only change.** No new endpoint, no backend config key, no migration. | None. | **No action required for upgrade.** Rebuild frontend image. Demo: `python3 examples/mffd-showcase/shapes.py --url https://shepard.nuclide.systems --api-key <token> --smoke`. |

| **VIS-T1 Phase 1 — `shepard-plugin-vis-trace3d` capability declaration + Trace3DViewShape SHACL** | 2026-05-28. New plugin module `plugins/vis-trace3d/` with manifest-only Phase-1 scope (mirrors VID1b phase 1 shape). Surfaces the `vis-trace3d` capability in `GET /v2/admin/plugins` with the `shepard.plugins.vis-trace3d.enabled` runtime toggle. Ships the **Trace3DViewShape SHACL** classpath resource at `/shapes/trace-3d-view.shacl.ttl` — extends the upstream `shepard-ui:ViewRecipeShape` meta-shape (`backend/src/main/resources/shapes/view-recipe-meta.shacl.ttl`) with the trace3d-specific predicates `trace3d:colorMap` (viridis/plasma/inferno/magma/cividis/turbo/rdbu/jet/heat/cool), `trace3d:interpolation` (none/linear/catmull-rom), `trace3d:alignment` (nearest/linear-resample/snap-to-x), `trace3d:sampleRate`, `trace3d:lineWidth`, `trace3d:valueRange`, `trace3d:timeRange`, `trace3d:frameKey`, `trace3d:rendererUrl`. Required channel-binding count tightened from `≥1` (meta-shape) to `3 ≤ bindings ≤ 4` (x/y/z required + optional colour). The plugin has **no CDI beans, no `@Path` resources, no sidecars** in Phase 1 — no `quarkus.index-dependency.*` entry needed. Three-audience plugin docs trio (`reference.md` / `quickstart.md` / `install.md`). `VisTrace3DPluginManifestTest extends AbstractPluginManifestTest` — 11 assertions (id pin, title non-blank, description mentions trace, licence Apache-2.0, repo URL present, compat targets v6, sidecars empty, classpath resource reachable, resource declares the right predicates, resource path constant pinned, lifecycle hooks safe with null context). The Three.js renderer (`Trace3DView.vue`, `Trace3DCanvas.vue`, `Trace3DChannelPicker.vue`, `Trace3DEditChannelsDialog.vue`) and the `POST /v2/shapes/render` dispatcher (`ShapesRenderRest`) **remain in-tree** — they shipped before this plugin lands (commits `643d271dc`, `70b133d28`, `f63f82624`, `6e970e659`). Phase 2 extraction is gated on **VIS-S1** (the `ViewRecipeRenderer` SPI dispatcher), queued in `aidocs/16`. | **ZERO** | **One new plugin in `GET /v2/admin/plugins`** with id `vis-trace3d`. One new runtime toggle: `shepard.plugins.vis-trace3d.enabled` (default `true`). **No new REST endpoints.** **No new Neo4j or Postgres migrations.** **No new node labels.** **No `/shepard/api/...` change.** The `POST /v2/shapes/validate` endpoint can now resolve the `Trace3DViewShape` SHACL definition from the plugin's classpath when the plugin is enabled. Operators who disable the plugin keep the in-tree `Trace3DView.vue` component on the frontend (Phase 1 limitation — full hide is Phase 2). | None. | **No action required for upgrade.** Pull and restart; the plugin JAR is bundled in the default image's `with-plugins` Maven profile. Verify: `curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" https://your.shepard/v2/admin/plugins \| jq '.[] \| select(.id == "vis-trace3d")'` returns a non-empty object with `state: "ACTIVE"`. To disable: set `shepard.plugins.vis-trace3d.enabled=false` in `application.properties` and restart. Phase 2 (frontend component extraction + `TraceFrameResolver` shipping the SPI implementation) lands once VIS-S1 ships. |

| **TPL9 — f(ai)²r AI provenance capture (inbound MCP AI-agent transparency)** | 2026-05-26. New `AiActivityType` enum (6 values: `ANNOTATION_SUGGESTION`, `IMPORT_MANIFEST_GENERATION`, `SPARQL_GENERATION`, `ANOMALY_DETECTION`, `SEMANTIC_ENRICHMENT`, `CHAT_RESPONSE`) + `AiProvenanceCapture` `@ApplicationScoped` CDI bean under `de.dlr.shepard.v2.ai`. `McpToolSupport.run()` gains TPL9 wiring: after every successful tool body, `captureAiProvenance(toolName)` inspects the routing context for `X-AI-Agent` header and, when present, delegates to `AiProvenanceCapture.record(...)` → `ProvenanceService.record(actionKind="AI_ACTION", ...)`. `Fair2rPredicates` gains `AI_ACTION_TYPE = "aiActionType"` constant. Design doc: `aidocs/platform/89-tpl9-fair2r-ai-provenance.md`. 7 unit tests in `AiProvenanceCaptureTest`. EU AI Act Article 50 transparency hook for inbound AI-agent MCP calls. Capture is best-effort — a provenance write failure never blocks the tool call. | **ZERO** | **No new REST endpoints.** **No new config keys.** **No Neo4j migration** — `actionKind = "AI_ACTION"` is a new string on the existing `String actionKind` property of `:Activity`; existing queries filtering by known `actionKind` values are unaffected. No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. New MCP behaviour: tool calls that include `X-AI-Agent` header now produce a supplementary `:Activity` node with `actionKind = "AI_ACTION"` and f(ai)²r metadata in the `summary` field (`aiActionType=<type> usedModel=<model> invokedBy=<agent> promptHash=<hash>`). Operators querying `GET /v2/provenance/activities` will see these rows — filter by `actionKind=AI_ACTION`. MCP clients that don't send `X-AI-Agent` see no change. | None. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; AI-aware MCP clients can begin setting `X-AI-Agent: <agent-name>` on tool calls. Verify: make an MCP tool call with `X-AI-Agent: test-agent` → `MATCH (a:Activity) WHERE a.actionKind = 'AI_ACTION' RETURN a.summary, a.startedAtMillis ORDER BY a.startedAtMillis DESC LIMIT 5;` should show the captured row. PROMPT1 (`aidocs/semantics/99`) will extend this with full prompt-body storage and `:PromptRun` substrate. |

| **NEO-AUDIT-001 — wire PROV-O edges on `:Activity` + V77 backfill** | 2026-05-26. Three PROV-O relationship types were documented on `:Activity` but never wired. 284,530 existing Activity nodes had zero graph edges, making provenance graph queries require a full label scan (569,069 dbHits). Fix is two-part: (1) forward-fix: `ActivityDAO.wireEdges()` new method issues three idempotent Cypher MERGE calls immediately after each `createOrUpdate` in `ProvenanceService.record()` (best-effort, swallowed on failure per observability contract); (2) backfill: `V77__backfill_activity_prov_edges.cypher` adds `Activity_targetAppId_idx` + `Activity_agentUsername_idx` indexes, then three batched `CALL { } IN TRANSACTIONS OF 1000 ROWS` blocks for WAS_ASSOCIATED_WITH, GENERATED, and USED. Javadoc on `Activity.java` corrected to PROV-O canonical OUTGOING direction. Rollback file `V77_R__backfill_activity_prov_edges.cypher` ships. 5 new unit tests in `ProvenanceServiceTest`. | **AWARE** | **No new REST endpoints.** **No new config keys.** `(:Activity)-[:WAS_ASSOCIATED_WITH]->(:User)`, `(:Activity)-[:GENERATED]->(:BasicEntity)`, `(:Activity)-[:USED]->(:BasicEntity)` are new relationship types in the Neo4j graph — any Cypher query that was already looking for these will now find results. The `GET /v2/provenance/activities` JSON response shape is unchanged. | `V77__backfill_activity_prov_edges.cypher` (idempotent, batched CALL blocks, online index build). Rollback: `V77_R__backfill_activity_prov_edges.cypher`. Tests: 5 new cases in `ProvenanceServiceTest` verifying `wireEdges` is called with correct args. Integration test (Testcontainer pre/post edge-count assertion) deferred — tracked `aidocs/16 NEO-AUDIT-001`. | **No action required for upgrade.** V77 runs on first start — index build is online (no downtime in Neo4j 5.x). Expect ~6 s total on a 284k-row dataset. To verify: `MATCH ()-[r:WAS_ASSOCIATED_WITH]->() RETURN count(r);` — should be non-zero after restart. To verify forward-wiring: make a new 2xx mutation then `MATCH (a:Activity) WHERE a.startedAtMillis > <recentMs> RETURN (a)-[:WAS_ASSOCIATED_WITH]->() LIMIT 1` should return a non-null relationship. Rollback: run `V77_R__backfill_activity_prov_edges.cypher` via `cypher-shell` then revert `ActivityDAO` + `ProvenanceService` (stops forward-wiring). |

| **TPL4 — attributes → annotations dual-write + Cypher backfill** | 2026-05-26. Bridge between the legacy `attributes` freetext map and the SHACL/ontology layer. Two parts: (a) **service-layer dual-write** — `AttributeAnnotationDualWriteService` (`@ApplicationScoped`) writes synthetic `:SemanticAnnotation` nodes whenever a `DataObject` is created or updated with a non-empty `attributes` map, gated on `shepard.tpl4.dual-write.enabled=false` (deploy-time-only opt-in, default off); (b) **one-shot backfill** — `V69__TPL4_attributes_to_annotations_backfill.cypher` runs on startup and creates the equivalent nodes for every pre-existing `DataObject` (and `Collection`) with attribute properties. Mapping: `propertyIRI = "urn:shepard:attribute:<key>"` (via `Constants.TPL4_ATTRIBUTE_PREDICATE_PREFIX`), `propertyName = "<key>"`, `valueName = <value>`, `source = "attributes-backfill"`. The `source` field is a new additive property on `:SemanticAnnotation` (Neo4j schema-free; existing nodes without it read as `null`, the "normal annotation" sentinel). Rollback: `V69_R__TPL4_attributes_to_annotations_backfill.cypher` deletes all `source='attributes-backfill'` nodes. New constants `Constants.ANNOTATION_SOURCE_ATTRIBUTES_BACKFILL` + `Constants.TPL4_ATTRIBUTE_PREDICATE_PREFIX` (value `"urn:shepard:attribute:"`). 6 unit tests in `AttributeAnnotationDualWriteServiceTest`. | **AWARE** | **New `source` property on `:SemanticAnnotation`** nodes (additive, schema-free, nullable). `hashCode`/`equals` on `SemanticAnnotation` include `source` — no behavioural change for existing rows (field reads as null). **No new endpoint, no new node label, no new relationship type.** V69 Cypher runs on first start; on an instance with no DataObject attributes it is a safe no-op. **New config key** (deploy-time-only, default off): `shepard.tpl4.dual-write.enabled=false`. | `V69__TPL4_attributes_to_annotations_backfill.cypher` — idempotent (`NOT EXISTS {MATCH...}` guard prevents duplicates on re-run). Rollback: `V69_R__TPL4_attributes_to_annotations_backfill.cypher`. Tests: `AttributeAnnotationDualWriteServiceTest` (6 cases: toggle-off, first-save, second-save idempotency, update path, null attrs, empty attrs). Integration regression test (Testcontainer + pre/post migration count assertion) deferred — tracked in `aidocs/16` as TPL4-INTEGRATION-TEST. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart the backend; V69 runs on first start and creates backfill annotations for any DataObjects with attributes. To verify: `MATCH (sa:SemanticAnnotation {source:'attributes-backfill'}) RETURN count(sa)` — will be 0 on a fresh install with no attributes, or the count of your legacy attribute entries. To enable the ongoing dual-write for future DataObject creates/updates: set `shepard.tpl4.dual-write.enabled=true` (env `SHEPARD_TPL4_DUAL_WRITE_ENABLED=true`) and restart. The toggle is intentionally deploy-time-only — the V69 one-shot migration is the path for existing data, and the toggle governs only new writes. Rollback: run the `V69_R__...` Cypher via `cypher-shell` then restart with the toggle off. |

| **SEMA-V6-011 — full SEMA-V6 shape + import-key filter in attribute dual-write** | 2026-05-26. Upgrades the TPL4 dual-write path (`AttributeAnnotationDualWriteService`) to populate the complete SEMA-V6 annotation shape introduced by SEMA-V6-001. Every synthetic `:SemanticAnnotation` created during dual-write now also carries: `subjectKind="DataObject"`, `subjectAppId=<dataObject.appId>`, `sourceMode="human"`, and explicit `null` for `vocabularyId`, `sourceActivityAppId`, `validFromMillis`, `validUntilMillis`, `confidence`. Additionally, transient ingest-marker attribute keys are **filtered out** of the dual-write path: keys starting with `source_`, `v15_`, `v16_`, `import_`, or `_import_` are skipped (a TRACE log is emitted per filtered key). No new Neo4j migration is required — V72 already seeds the Shepard internal vocabulary (`https://shepard.dlr.de/vocab/`). 3 new tests added (9 total pass): `semaV6011_v6FieldsPopulatedOnDualWrite`, `semaV6011_importMarkerKeysNotDualWritten`, `semaV6011_isImportMarkerKey_filterPatterns`. No change to the `shepard.tpl4.dual-write.enabled` toggle behaviour. | **AWARE** | **Additive enrichment of existing `:SemanticAnnotation{source:'attributes-backfill'}` nodes.** New nodes created after this change carry `subjectKind`, `subjectAppId`, `sourceMode` — this is pure additive Neo4j property addition; no existing field removed or renamed. **Import-marker keys are now silently skipped** — if `shepard.tpl4.dual-write.enabled=true` is set and DataObjects have `source_*` / `v16_*` / `import_*` attributes, those keys will no longer produce annotation nodes. This is a correctness fix (ingest markers should not be semantic annotations). **No endpoint change. No config change. No migration.** | None — no new migration file. Tests: `AttributeAnnotationDualWriteServiceTest` (3 new + 6 existing = 9 total). | **No action required for upgrade.** Existing `source='attributes-backfill'` annotations (created before this change) will have `null` for the new SEMA-V6 fields — this is expected and harmless. Only annotations created after this PR ships carry the full shape. To backfill `subjectKind`/`subjectAppId`/`sourceMode` onto the pre-existing synthetic annotations: `MATCH (sa:SemanticAnnotation {source:'attributes-backfill'})<-[:has_annotation]-(do:DataObject) SET sa.subjectKind='DataObject', sa.subjectAppId=do.appId, sa.sourceMode='human';` — safe to run online via `cypher-shell`. Import-marker keys already stored as annotations (e.g. `attributes||v16_pass1` on 8507 existing nodes per NEO-AUDIT-005) are NOT retroactively deleted by this change — that cleanup is SEMA-V6-012 (Phase 3). |

| **TPL11 — independence proof (`POST /v2/quality/independence-proof`)** | 2026-05-26. New stateless endpoint and UI badge for checking whether two DataObject sets are mutually independent — i.e. they share no common provenance ancestors (within a 10-hop window over the `has_successor` DAG) and no annotation key-value pair appears in both sets. Java classes: `IndependenceProofQuery` (Cypher constants), `IndependenceProofRequestIO` + `IndependenceProofResultIO` + `SharedAncestorIO` + `SharedAnnotationIO` (IO records), `IndependenceProofService` (`@ApplicationScoped`, session-injected, pure query logic, no new Neo4j entity), `IndependenceProofRest` (`@Path("/v2/quality/independence-proof")`, `@Authenticated`, `@POST`). Response: `independent` (boolean), `sharedAncestors[]` (ancestor appId + reachableFromA + reachableFromB), `sharedAnnotations[]` (key + value + fromA + fromB), `checkedAt` (ISO-8601). Frontend: `frontend/components/quality/IndependenceBadge.vue` (spinner / green "Independent ✓" / red "Not independent ✗" / grey error states) + `backend-client/src/apis/IndependenceProofApi.ts`. 6 JUnit unit tests (`IndependenceProofServiceTest`): independent when no overlap, not-independent on shared ancestor, not-independent on shared annotation, both fields populated, multi-member reachable lists, blank values excluded. | **ZERO** | **One new `/v2/` endpoint**: `POST /v2/quality/independence-proof`. **No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path.** **No new config key.** **No Neo4j migration** — read-only Cypher queries; no new node labels or relationships. **No Postgres/Mongo/Garage changes.** Auth: `@Authenticated` (any logged-in user). Best-effort: ancestor walk capped at 10 hops; annotation check covers string-valued `attributes` properties only. | None. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart the backend; `POST /v2/quality/independence-proof` appears in the OpenAPI spec under the "Data quality (v2)" tag. To verify: `curl -X POST https://shepard.nuclide.systems/v2/quality/independence-proof -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"setA":["<appId1>"],"setB":["<appId2>"]}' \| jq .` — returns 200 with `independent`, `sharedAncestors[]`, `sharedAnnotations[]`, `checkedAt`. |

| **PROV-USER-MIRROR-ENDPOINT — `POST /v2/admin/users/mirror` (`:MirroredUser` shadow node for cross-instance PROV-O attribution, #229)** | 2026-05-26. New `MirroredUser extends AbstractEntity` Neo4j node label (fields: `sourceInstance`, `sourceUsername`, `sourceDisplayName?`, `sourceEmail?`, `derivedFrom?` self-pointer). Admin-only idempotent endpoint `POST /v2/admin/users/mirror`: 201 on first create, 200 on re-call (same `appId` returned). MERGE semantics: `MirroredUserDAO.createOrUpdateBySourceKey` looks up by `(sourceInstance, sourceUsername)`, updates mutable fields if found, creates new node otherwise. `V74` migration adds `appId` uniqueness constraint + composite index on `(sourceInstance, sourceUsername)`. PROV1a `ProvenanceCaptureFilter` auto-captures the POST as an `:Activity` row. 9 unit tests in `MirroredUserRestTest`. | **ZERO** | **One new `/v2/` endpoint**: `POST /v2/admin/users/mirror`. **One new Neo4j node label**: `:MirroredUser`. **No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path.** **No new config key.** Auth: `instance-admin` role only. UI stub: N/A — admin-only data-management endpoint, no direct user surface. | `V74__Add_MirroredUser_appId_constraint.cypher` — idempotent (`IF NOT EXISTS` guards on both constraint and index). Rollback: `DROP CONSTRAINT MirroredUser_appId_unique IF EXISTS; DROP INDEX MirroredUser_sourceInstance_username_idx IF EXISTS; MATCH (n:MirroredUser) DETACH DELETE n;`. Tests: `MirroredUserRestTest` (9 unit tests). Testcontainer integration test (full V74 migration + smoke POST) deferred — tracked in `aidocs/16` as PROV-USER-MIRROR-INTEGRATION-TEST. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart the backend; V74 applies on first start. To verify: `curl -X POST https://<host>/v2/admin/users/mirror -H "X-API-KEY: <admin-key>" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"sourceInstance":"https://cube3.dlr.de","sourceUsername":"fkrebs"}' \| jq .` — should return 201 with `appId`, `sourceInstance`, `sourceUsername`. Second identical call returns 200 with the same `appId`. |

| **MFFD-BATCH-01 — `POST /v2/data-objects/batch` (HTTP 207 bulk create)** | New additive endpoint on the `/v2/` development surface. Accepts a JSON array of 1–500 `DataObjectBatchCreateItemIO` items; returns HTTP 207 Multi-Status with a per-item outcome array (`"created"` with minted `appId`, or `"error"` with machine-readable `errorCode`). Items are processed sequentially (no parallel Neo4j sessions). Per-item permission check (Write on target Collection) is memoised within the batch loop so N items targeting the same Collection hit the permissions service once, not N times. Error codes: `COLLECTION_NOT_FOUND`, `FORBIDDEN`, `PARENT_NOT_FOUND`, `INVALID_INPUT`, `INTERNAL_ERROR`. HTTP 400 on empty array or batch > 500 items; HTTP 207 always returned for valid-sized batches even when all items fail. New classes: `DataObjectBatchV2Rest` (`@Path("/v2/data-objects/batch")`), `DataObjectBatchCreateItemIO`, `DataObjectBatchResultItemIO`, `DataObjectBatchResponseIO`. 15 unit tests in `DataObjectBatchV2RestTest`. Provenance: `ProvenanceCaptureFilter` fires once per batch request (HTTP 207 ∈ 2xx); per-item provenance is deferred to a future PROV2 slice. | **ZERO** | **One new `/v2/` endpoint**: `POST /v2/data-objects/batch`. **No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path.** No new Neo4j labels, no new config keys, no new migrations. The single-create endpoint `POST /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects` is unchanged. | None required — purely additive endpoint, no schema change. Test obligation: 15 unit tests land in this commit; testcontainer-level integration test deferred (tracked in `aidocs/16` as BATCH-01-INTEGRATION-TEST). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the endpoint is immediately available. Smoke-test: `curl -X POST -H "X-API-KEY: ..." -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '[{"collectionAppId":"<appId>","name":"TR-001"}]' https://<host>/v2/data-objects/batch` → 207 with `{"created":1,"failed":0,"results":[{"index":0,"status":"created","appId":"<uuid>"}]}`. |

| **TPL3a-lite — upper-ontology alignment registry (`GET /v2/semantic/ontology/alignment`)** | 2026-05-26. Seeds 12 `(:OntologyAlignment)` nodes recording how core Shepard concepts map onto upper-ontology classes (BFO 2020 ISO/IEC 21838-2, IAO, PROV-O, IOF Core). New Java entity `OntologyAlignment` + `OntologyAlignmentDAO` + response IO `OntologyAlignmentIO` + read-only REST endpoint `GET /v2/semantic/ontology/alignment` (`@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`). Frontend: `ontology-alignment` placeholder tile added to admin panel (fragment `#ontology-alignment`), backed by `PlaceholderRestDump` showing the raw registry. 12 alignment rows: Resource→BFO:Continuant, Collection→IAO:DataSet, DataObject→IAO:DataItem, SemanticAnnotation→IAO:is_about, FileBundle→IAO:Document, Container→BFO:GenericallyDependentContinuant, Activity→PROV-O:Activity, Activity→BFO:Occurrent (transitive), Agent→PROV-O:Agent, User→IOF Core:Person, Shape→IAO:Plan, Template→IAO:Plan (equivalentClass). Mapping authority: `aidocs/semantics/96-upper-ontology-alignment.md`. 5 unit tests (`OntologyAlignmentTest`, `OntologyAlignmentRestTest`). Full TTL-loaded axiomatic layer queued as TPL3b. | **ADDITIVE** | **One new `/v2/` endpoint**: `GET /v2/semantic/ontology/alignment`. **No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path.** **No new admin REST knob, no config key.** **One new Neo4j node label**: `:OntologyAlignment`. Migration `V70__TPL3_upper_ontology_alignment.cypher` creates a composite node-key constraint `(shepardConcept, upperOntologyUri)` (idempotent `IF NOT EXISTS`) and MERGEs 12 alignment rows — no data mutation to existing nodes. | `V70__TPL3_upper_ontology_alignment.cypher` — idempotent. Rollback: `V70_R__TPL3_upper_ontology_alignment.cypher` (DETACH DELETE + DROP CONSTRAINT). Tests: `OntologyAlignmentTest` + `OntologyAlignmentRestTest` (5 unit tests). Testcontainer integration test deferred — tracked in `aidocs/16` as TPL3-INTEGRATION-TEST. Operator runbook: `aidocs/semantics/97-tpl3-upper-ontology-bootstrap.md`. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart the backend; V70 applies on first start and seeds the 12 alignment rows. Smoke-test: `GET /v2/semantic/ontology/alignment` (as instance-admin) returns a JSON array of 12 objects. Via Cypher: `MATCH (n:OntologyAlignment) RETURN n.shepardConcept, n.upperOntologyUri, n.confidence ORDER BY n.shepardConcept;`. UI: Admin → "Ontology Alignment" → raw REST dump in advanced mode. |

| **SEMA-V6-014 — Personal vocabulary minting + `personalVocabulariesEnabled` admin toggle** | 2026-05-26. Adds operator-gated personal vocabulary namespaces. Backend: `Vocabulary` entity gains two new nullable `@Property` fields: `type` (string; `"PERSONAL"` for user-minted, `null` for system/operator vocabularies) and `ownedByUserAppId` (string; owner's UUID v7). `SemanticConfig` entity gains `personalVocabulariesEnabled` (boolean, default `false`). `VocabularyDAO` gains two new query methods: `findByUri(String)` (pre-check before save, prevents V72 constraint violation from surfacing as a 500) and `listPersonalByOwner(String userAppId)`. `PatchSemanticConfigIO`, `SemanticConfigIO`, and `SemanticConfigRest.patchConfig()` updated to expose and apply the new toggle. Two new `/v2/` endpoints: `POST /v2/vocabularies/personal` (403 when feature disabled; validates name against `[a-z0-9][a-z0-9_-]{0,63}`; 409 on duplicate; mints URI `urn:shepard:personal:<userAppId>:<name>`; returns 201 + `VocabularyIO`) and `GET /v2/vocabularies/personal` (returns caller's personal vocabularies). New IO classes: `PersonalVocabularyRequestIO`, `VocabularyIO`. 10 unit tests in `PersonalVocabularyRestTest`. Frontend: `SemanticConfigPane.vue` admin pane with `personalVocabulariesEnabled` v-switch + `SemanticConfig` fragment wired into `/admin`. | **ADDITIVE** | **Two new nullable properties** on `:Vocabulary` nodes (`type`, `ownedByUserAppId`) — schemaless, null on all existing nodes, safe default. **One new nullable boolean property** `personalVocabulariesEnabled` on the `:SemanticConfig` singleton (null = `false` on existing instances). **Two new `/v2/` endpoints** under `/v2/vocabularies/personal`. **No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path.** No new Neo4j labels. No new migrations. | None — all new fields are nullable Neo4j properties. No data-mutating migration needed. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; `personalVocabulariesEnabled` defaults to `false` — the new endpoints exist but `POST /v2/vocabularies/personal` returns 403 until an operator flips the toggle. To enable: `PATCH /v2/admin/semantic/config` with `{"personalVocabulariesEnabled": true}` (or via the new "Semantic Config" tile in `/admin`). Smoke-test: `GET /v2/admin/semantic/config` → response includes `"personalVocabulariesEnabled": false`. |

| **SEMA-V6-013 — Annotation delete-policy operator knob** | 2026-05-26. Extends the `:SemanticConfig` singleton with a new field `annotationDeletePolicy` (nullable string; null = default `'author-or-manager'`). Three valid policy values: `'author-or-manager'` — the annotation author OR any collection manager may delete; `'author-only'` — only the author; `'manager-only'` — only collection managers. The policy is evaluated at DELETE time in `SemanticAnnotationV2Rest`: the endpoint now loads the singleton via `OntologyConfigService.loadSingleton()`, computes `isAuthor = caller.equals(annotation.agentUsername)` and `isManager = checkAccessForSubject(..., Manage, ...)`, then gates on the policy. To support author detection, `SemanticAnnotation` gains a new `@Property("agentUsername")` field populated at create time from the authenticated principal. `SemanticConfigIO` (read projection), `PatchSemanticConfigIO` (merge-patch body), and `SemanticConfigRest.patchConfig()` (validation whitelist + apply) are updated in the same slice. 3 new unit tests in `SemanticAnnotationV2RestTest` (`deleteAnnotation_byAuthor_allowedUnderDefaultPolicy`, `deleteAnnotation_byNonAuthorNonManager_blockedUnderDefaultPolicy`, `deleteAnnotation_withAuthorOnlyPolicy_blocksManager`). No new Neo4j migration needed — all new fields are nullable; existing annotations get `agentUsername=null` (treated as "unknown author", falls back to manager-only path under `'author-or-manager'` default). | **ADDITIVE** | **One new nullable property** `annotationDeletePolicy` on the `:SemanticConfig` singleton node (schemaless — null on existing instances; null = safe default). **One new nullable property** `agentUsername` on every new `:SemanticAnnotation` node going forward (null on existing annotations — handled gracefully by the policy gate). **No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path.** `PATCH /v2/admin/semantic/config` accepts the new field. No new Neo4j labels. No new migrations. | None — all new fields are nullable Neo4j properties with safe defaults. No data-mutating migration needed. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the policy defaults to `null` (= `'author-or-manager'`) — existing behaviour is `isManager` check only (since existing annotations have `agentUsername=null`, `isAuthor` is always false until new annotations are created). To change the policy: `PATCH /v2/admin/semantic/config` with `{"annotationDeletePolicy": "author-only"}` (or via CLI when SEMA-V6-013 CLI parity lands). Smoke-test: `GET /v2/admin/semantic/config` → response includes `"annotationDeletePolicy": null` (or the value you set). |

| **SEMA-V6-004 — Polymorphic `/v2/annotations/*` REST surface** | 2026-05-26. New additive endpoint family on the `/v2/` development surface. Six endpoints: `GET /v2/annotations` (list with filters: `subjectAppId`, `subjectKind`, `predicateIri`, `vocabId`, plus `page`/`pageSize` pagination), `GET /v2/annotations/find` (text search over value/predicate names), `GET /v2/annotations/{appId}` (get one), `POST /v2/annotations` (create), `PUT /v2/annotations/{appId}` (RFC 7396 merge-patch update), `DELETE /v2/annotations/{appId}` (hard delete), `GET /v2/annotations/{appId}/export/turtle` (OA-framed Turtle export). New Java: `SemanticAnnotationV2Rest.java`, `SemanticAnnotationV2DAO.java`, `AnnotationIO.java`, `CreateAnnotationIO.java`, `UpdateAnnotationIO.java` in `de.dlr.shepard.v2.annotations.*`. Polymorphic permission gate: `Collection` subjects resolved via `EntityIdResolver` + `isAccessTypeAllowedForUser`; all other subjects resolved via `isAccessAllowedForDataObjectAppId`. 32 unit tests in `SemanticAnnotationV2RestTest`. No new migrations (uses existing `:SemanticAnnotation` nodes with SEMA-V6-001 v6 fields). No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path. | **ADDITIVE** | **Seven new `/v2/` endpoints** under `/v2/annotations/*`. **No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path.** No new Neo4j labels. No new migrations. No new config keys. | None. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart the backend; the endpoints are immediately available. Smoke-test: `GET /v2/annotations?subjectKind=DataObject&pageSize=5` (authenticated) → JSON array (may be empty on a fresh install). `GET /v2/annotations/{knownAppId}/export/turtle` → `Content-Type: text/turtle` with OA-framed triples. |

| **F9 — `POST /v2/annotations` now correctly gates on Reference subjects** | 2026-06-20. Bug fix in `SemanticAnnotationV2Rest.checkAccessForSubject()`. Previously, annotating any Reference entity (FileReference, TimeseriesReference, URIReference, etc.) returned 403 because the permission gate passed the Reference's appId directly to `isAccessAllowedForDataObjectAppId()`, which queries `MATCH (c:Collection)-[:HAS_DATAOBJECT]->(d:DataObject {appId:…})` — a Reference appId never matches a DataObject node. Fix: when the resolved Neo4j labels contain a label ending in "Reference", the gate now calls `ReferencesV2Service.resolveByAppId()` to walk Reference → DataObject → Collection before deciding. Surfaced by MFFD bridge import (2026-06-17): ~4963 reference-annotation writes silently denied. No new endpoints, no schema changes, no config keys. 44 unit tests pass. Also fixes test NPE from commit `f9ecbd8f` (`resolveWithLabels` stub); supersedes PR #2017. | **ZERO** | **No endpoints added or moved. No new config keys. No migration.** Behavior fix: `POST /v2/annotations` with `subjectKind=<any-Reference-kind>` now returns 201 instead of 403 for callers with Write permission on the parent DataObject. Callers who were sending `subjectKind=DataObject` are unaffected. | — | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; Reference-subject annotations now flow correctly. Verify: `POST /v2/annotations {"subjectKind":"FileReference","subjectAppId":"<ref-appid>","predicateIri":"...","objectLiteral":"..."}` → 201. |

| **SEMA-V6-009 — `SemanticVocabularyProvider` SPI + `SemanticVocabularyRegistry` CDI registry + `GeoTimeVocabularyProvider` (spatial plugin)** | 2026-05-26. New in-tree SPI seam for pluggable vocabulary fragments. Three new Java types in `de.dlr.shepard.spi.semantic`: `SemanticVocabularyProvider` (CDI SPI interface; methods: `vocabularyUri()`, `vocabularyLabel()`, `predicates()`), `VocabularyPredicateDefinition` (immutable record: `iri`, `label`, `description`, `expectedValueType`), `SemanticVocabularyRegistry` (`@ApplicationScoped` CDI bean; resolves all `Instance<SemanticVocabularyProvider>` on `StartupEvent`, indexes by vocabulary URI and predicate IRI, logs a startup summary line, public API: `getProvider()`, `getPredicate()`, `allProviders()`, `allPredicates()`). Reference implementation: `GeoTimeVocabularyProvider` (`@ApplicationScoped`) in `shepard-plugin-spatial` contributes 5 GeoSPARQL + OWL-Time predicates (`geosparql:hasGeometry` IRI, `geosparql:asWKT` LITERAL, `geosparql:sfWithin` IRI, `time:hasBeginning` IRI, `time:hasEnd` IRI). Discovery is CDI-only (no `META-INF/services`). Tests: 10 unit tests in `SemanticVocabularyRegistryTest` (core); 9 unit tests in `GeoTimeVocabularyProviderTest` (plugin). Docs: `plugins/spatial/docs/reference.md` updated with vocabulary section. No REST endpoint yet (planned in a future SEMA-V6 slice). | **ADDITIVE** | **No new REST endpoints.** **No new Neo4j node labels, no migrations, no config keys.** New types `SemanticVocabularyProvider`, `VocabularyPredicateDefinition`, `SemanticVocabularyRegistry` appear on the classpath. `GeoTimeVocabularyProvider` is auto-discovered by CDI when `shepard-plugin-spatial` is on the classpath — logged at startup: `SemanticVocabularyRegistry: discovered 1 vocabulary provider(s): [http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#]; 5 total predicate(s)`. | None. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart the backend; the SPI is purely additive. The spatial plugin's 5 GeoSPARQL+OWL-Time predicates are available in the in-memory registry on startup (no Neo4j write). Custom vocabulary providers from other plugins are discovered automatically via CDI — no configuration needed. |

| **SEMA-V6-001/002/003 — SemanticAnnotation v6 columns + Vocabulary/Predicate entities + SemanticConfig v6 fields** | 2026-05-26. Three migration files: (V71) two Neo4j indexes on `:SemanticAnnotation` (`subjectAppId`, `vocabularyId`) for the 8 new v6 fields added to `SemanticAnnotation.java`; (V72) uniqueness constraints on `:Vocabulary.appId`, `:Vocabulary.uri`, `:Predicate.appId`, `:Predicate.uri` + 10 MERGE-seeded `:Vocabulary` bootstrap rows (Dublin Core, PROV-O, schema.org, DataCite, CHAMEO, Material OWL, Shepard internal, m4i, SKOS, GeoSPARQL); (V73) idempotent `coalesce` pass on the `:SemanticConfig` singleton to set `annotationMode = "PERMISSIVE"` and `suggestionEnabled = false` defaults on existing rows. Java changes: `SemanticAnnotation.java` gains 8 new nullable `@Property` fields (`subjectKind`, `subjectAppId`, `vocabularyId`, `sourceMode`, `sourceActivityAppId`, `validFromMillis`, `validUntilMillis`, `confidence`) with `hashCode`/`equals` extended; new `Vocabulary.java` + `Predicate.java` entities + `VocabularyDAO` + `PredicateDAO`; `SemanticConfig.java` gains 4 new fields (`defaultVocabularyAppId`, `annotationMode`, `suggestionEnabled`, `suggestionModelId`); new `SemanticConfigRest.java` at `GET`+`PATCH /v2/admin/semantic/config`; `OntologyConfigService.patchConfig()` added. Tests: 30 new tests across 4 test classes. | **ADDITIVE** | **8 new nullable properties** added to every `:SemanticAnnotation` node (schemaless — null on existing rows; no backfill). **2 new Neo4j node labels**: `:Vocabulary` and `:Predicate`. **One new `/v2/` admin endpoint**: `GET /v2/admin/semantic/config` (read) + `PATCH /v2/admin/semantic/config` (merge-patch). **No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path.** All new properties nullable — no existing query patterns break. | V71 (indexes, IF NOT EXISTS — idempotent), V72 (constraints + 10-vocab seed, MERGE-keyed on uri — idempotent), V73 (coalesce SET — idempotent). Rollback for V72: `DROP CONSTRAINT Vocabulary_appId_unique IF EXISTS; DROP CONSTRAINT Vocabulary_uri_unique IF EXISTS; DROP CONSTRAINT Predicate_appId_unique IF EXISTS; DROP CONSTRAINT Predicate_uri_unique IF EXISTS; MATCH (n:Vocabulary) WHERE n.source = 'V72-bootstrap' DETACH DELETE n;`. Tests: `SemanticAnnotationV6ColumnsTest` (6), `VocabularyDAOTest` (7), `PredicateDAOTest` (8), `SemanticConfigRestTest` (9). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V71/V72/V73 apply on first start. Smoke-test: `MATCH (v:Vocabulary) RETURN v.prefix, v.label ORDER BY v.label;` → 10 rows. `GET /v2/admin/semantic/config` (as instance-admin) → returns `{"preseedEnabled": true, "annotationMode": "PERMISSIVE", "suggestionEnabled": false, ...}`. |
