| **P13 — SSE Collection change-feed (`GET /v2/collections/{appId}/events`)** | 2026-05-26. New additive endpoint on the `/v2/` development surface. Opens a persistent `text/event-stream` connection; emits `CollectionEventIO` JSON events (fields: `eventType`, `entityAppId`, `entityKind`, `collectionAppId`, `actorUsername`, `timestamp`) for `DATA_OBJECT_CREATED`, `DATA_OBJECT_UPDATED`, `DATA_OBJECT_DELETED`, `COLLECTION_UPDATED`, and `HEARTBEAT` (every 30 s). Auth: `@Authenticated` + Read gate. Frontend: `useCollectionEvents.ts` composable uses `fetch()` + streaming body reader (not native `EventSource`; cannot carry `Authorization` header); `CollectionSidebar.vue` calls `refreshItems()` on structural changes. Backend: 4 new classes — `CollectionEventIO` record, `CollectionEventBus` (`@ApplicationScoped`, `ConcurrentHashMap<String, Set<SinkEntry>>`), `CollectionEventsRest` (JAX-RS SSE resource), `CollectionEventProducer` (`@ApplicationScoped`). Hooks in `DataObjectService` (create/update/delete) and `CollectionService` (update). `ANNOTATION_CREATED` deferred (SemanticAnnotationService lacks collection context at annotation time — tracked in `aidocs/16` P13-defer). 5 unit tests. | **ZERO** | **One new `/v2/` endpoint**: `GET /v2/collections/{appId}/events`. No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path. No new Neo4j labels, no migrations, no new config keys. Additive-only. | None required. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart the backend; the endpoint is immediately available. Smoke-test: `curl -H "X-API-KEY: <key>" https://<host>/v2/collections/<appId>/events` → `Content-Type: text/event-stream` + immediate `event: HEARTBEAT` line. |

| **NEO-AUDIT-010 — User email uniqueness constraint + duplicate reconciliation (V81 migration)** | 2026-05-26. **DATA-MUTATING migration.** `V81__user_email_unique_constraint.cypher` adds `CREATE CONSTRAINT user_email_unique IF NOT EXISTS FOR (u:User) REQUIRE u.email IS UNIQUE`. Before adding the constraint, the migration reconciles any duplicate email addresses (2× `alice@demo.shepard.local` and 2× `admin@demo.shepard.local` confirmed in the dev instance). Reconciliation uses `apoc.refactor.mergeNodes([canonical, ...duplicates], {properties: 'discard', mergeRels: true})` — all edges in both directions are transferred to the canonical node; duplicate nodes are deleted. Canonical selection order: (1) username matches `admin` → canonical; (2) higher outgoing-degree → canonical; (3) lex-first username as tiebreak. No edge data is lost — edges are retargeted, not dropped. Java: `UserDAO.findByEmail(String): Optional<User>` added; `ShepardMain` startup WARN if duplicates survive the migration. 7 unit tests. Rollback `V81_R__` drops the constraint only — nodes cannot be recreated from the rollback; a DB backup is required for full undo. Future prevention: the constraint causes Neo4j to reject any subsequent duplicate-email write loudly (ConstraintViolationException). | **BREAKING if your deployment has duplicate emails** | **Back up your Neo4j database** before applying this migration (see runbook `docs/admin/runbooks/04-neo4j-restore.md`). Then restart shepard; V81 applies automatically. Idempotent — if no duplicates exist, it is a no-op and the constraint is simply created. Verify post-migration: `MATCH (u:User) WITH u.email AS e, count(*) AS c WHERE e IS NOT NULL AND c > 1 RETURN e, c;` → 0 rows. APOC must be active (see NEO-AUDIT-013 row). If your deployment has legitimate duplicate emails (shared mailboxes), resolve them at the OIDC / Keycloak level first. | `V81__user_email_unique_constraint.cypher` — APOC-based merge `IN TRANSACTIONS OF 10 ROWS` + `CREATE CONSTRAINT user_email_unique IF NOT EXISTS`. Rollback: `V81_R__user_email_unique_constraint.cypher` — `DROP CONSTRAINT user_email_unique IF EXISTS`. | **Back up before upgrading.** Then restart; V81 runs automatically on first start. |

| **NEO-AUDIT-013 — Add APOC to `NEO4J_PLUGINS` in compose stack** | 2026-05-26. `infrastructure/docker-compose.yml` `NEO4J_PLUGINS` changed from `["n10s"]` to `["apoc","n10s"]`. APOC core is bundled with Neo4j 5.x since 5.7 — no separate jar download needed. The security allowlists (`NEO4J_dbms_security_procedures_allowlist` + `unrestricted`) already listed `apoc.*`; only the plugins list was missing the entry. Unblocks NEO-006 rel-type rename (`apoc.refactor.setType`) and any future migration using `apoc.periodic.iterate`. | **ADDITIVE** | **No new endpoints, no new config keys, no new migrations, no wire-shape changes.** Compose-only change. | None. | **Operators upgrading from upstream:** add `"apoc"` to your `NEO4J_PLUGINS` environment variable: `NEO4J_PLUGINS: '["apoc","n10s"]'`. Then restart the `neo4j` container (`docker compose restart neo4j`). Verify: `CALL apoc.help('apoc')` in the Neo4j browser should return results. If you are air-gapped and rely on the manual jar-drop path, place the APOC core jar (matching your Neo4j 5.x version) in your `/plugins` volume and leave `NEO4J_PLUGINS` pointing at it — the allowlists already permit it. |

| **NEO-AUDIT-015 — `Activity.actionKind` app-layer validation + startup alarm** | 2026-05-26. `Activity.actionKind` (previously unguarded `String`) is now validated at write time by `ActivityActionKind.validate()` in `ProvenanceService.record()`. Canonical values: `CREATE`, `READ`, `UPDATE`, `DELETE`, `EXECUTE` (plus the TPL9 extension `AI_ACTION`). `ActivityActionKindAlarm` (`@ApplicationScoped` `@PostConstruct`) queries `(:Activity)` nodes at startup and WARNs on any value outside the canonical set. Neo4j Community Edition does not support property-value constraints, so app-layer enforcement is the substitute. No migration needed — the live database was clean on the audit date (no rogue values). | **AWARE** | **New `IllegalArgumentException` on writes with an invalid `actionKind`.** Code that calls `ProvenanceService.record()` with a string value not in `{CREATE, READ, UPDATE, DELETE, EXECUTE, AI_ACTION}` now throws `IllegalArgumentException` before the DAO write. Existing callers pass only the canonical values (via `ProvenanceCaptureFilter.actionKindFor()`) so the change is a no-op for all known call sites. **Startup log:** on first start after this change, `ActivityActionKindAlarm` will run one Cypher query (`MATCH (a:Activity) WHERE NOT a.actionKind IN $knownValues`) and emit a WARN for any rogue values found. On a clean database this produces only a DEBUG-level "clean" confirmation. Operators monitoring startup logs may see the new WARN line if their database was ever written to by a custom code path that bypassed `ProvenanceService`. | None. No schema change; no migration. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the alarm runs at startup and is silent when the database is clean. If you see a WARN from `ActivityActionKindAlarm` at startup, the named rogue `actionKind` value was written by a code path that bypassed `ProvenanceService.record()` — investigate and correct the writer. The existing provenance rows are not mutated. |

| **NEO-AUDIT-011 — drop 3 dead Timeseries TEXT indexes (V77 migration)** | 2026-05-26. Three TEXT indexes on `:Timeseries` properties — `idx_Timeseries_attr_field` (on `n.field`), `idx_Timeseries_attr_location` (on `n.location`), `idx_Timeseries_attr_symbolic_name` (on `n.symbolicName`) — were created in V8 and confirmed zero-read (lastRead=NULL) in the post-MFFD audit (2026-05-26). Queries use only `measurement` + `device`; the other three TEXT indexes were pure write-amplification cost at MFFD scale (~100s CPU per ingest pass for 33k rows). Dropped via `V77__drop_dead_timeseries_text_indexes.cypher`. The two actively-used TEXT indexes (`idx_Timeseries_attr_measurement`, `idx_Timeseries_attr_device`) are NOT touched. UNIQUE constraints are NOT touched. Rollback: `V77_R__restore_timeseries_text_indexes.cypher`. | **AWARE** | **Operators who query Timeseries by `field`, `location`, or `symbolicName` using a TEXT index-backed full-text search will lose that index.** Range queries (equality on these properties in a WHERE clause) still work via label scan — only TEXT index-backed full-text queries are affected. If your deployment queries by these properties, restore the indexes using the rollback file before upgrading. Operators who do not query by `field`/`location`/`symbolicName` see no impact. | `V77__drop_dead_timeseries_text_indexes.cypher` — three `DROP INDEX … IF EXISTS` statements, idempotent. Rollback: `V77_R__restore_timeseries_text_indexes.cypher` — three `CREATE TEXT INDEX … IF NOT EXISTS` statements, idempotent. | **No action required for upgrade** if your deployment does not query Timeseries by `field`, `location`, or `symbolicName`. Restart shepard; V77 applies on first start. Verify post-drop: `SHOW INDEXES WHERE labelsOrTypes = ['Timeseries'] AND name IN ['idx_Timeseries_attr_field', 'idx_Timeseries_attr_location', 'idx_Timeseries_attr_symbolic_name'];` → 0 rows. If you rely on these indexes, run `V77_R__restore_timeseries_text_indexes.cypher` via `cypher-shell` before upgrading. |

| **NEO-AUDIT-007 — `importedFrom` field on `:Collection` nodes** | 2026-05-26. New optional `importedFrom` (nullable String) field on the `:Collection` Neo4j node. Disambiguates multiple Collections with the same name created by successive import runs (e.g. four "MFFD-Dropbox" collections from iterative MFFD ingest). Set by import scripts to identify the origin (e.g. `"tapelaying"`, `"bridgewelding"`, `"v15-redrive-1"`). Exposed on the `/v2/` surface (`CollectionIO` returned from `/v2/collections` and `/v2/collections/{appId}`). Frontend: `CollectionList.vue` renders `(importedFrom)` as a muted sub-label next to the collection name when the field is set; hidden when null. `CollectionIO` uses `@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)` — same treatment as `heroImageUrl` — so the field is absent from the v1 wire when null, preserving byte-fidelity with upstream 5.2.0. Backend-client TypeScript interface updated (src + dist/CJS + dist/ESM). | **ZERO** | **Additive nullable field — zero upgrade impact for admins and clients.** Existing `:Collection` nodes lack the property and Neo4j OGM reads the absence as `null` (schema-less store). v1 wire shape continues to match upstream byte-for-byte (no `importedFrom` key appears). The new field appears only in `/v2/` responses when set. No new endpoints, no config keys, no required schema migration. | — (no migration needed — additive nullable property; Neo4j accepts it lazily on first write). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the field is null on all existing Collections until explicitly set via the import script or a `PATCH /v2/collections/{appId}` merge-patch. To stamp origin on existing MFFD collections: `PATCH /v2/collections/<appId>` with body `{"importedFrom": "tapelaying"}`. |
| **NEO-AUDIT-004 — time-bucketed Agent index for `:User` supernode (V80 migration)** | 2026-05-26. The operator `:User` node accumulates all `created_by` edges from every DataObject — confirmed at 33,368 edges after MFFD ingest. `PROFILE` of a `created_by` traversal takes 66,794 dbHits over 17,086 DataObjects; the edge count grows with every new DataObject write and will worsen further as NEO-AUDIT-001 Activity edges scale. Fix: additive `(:User)-[:created_in_month {ym:"YYYYMM"}]->(:DataObject)` relationship written by `DataObjectDAO.writeCreatedInMonth()` (best-effort Cypher MERGE) called from `DataObjectService.createDataObject()` after the two-phase persist. Queries scoped to a user + month target the bounded `[:created_in_month]` rel set instead of the full supernode fan-out, typically reducing dbHits 100-1000x. `V80__add_created_in_month_index.cypher` creates a range index on `(r.ym)` and backfills all existing DataObjects via `CALL { } IN TRANSACTIONS OF 1000 ROWS`. Rollback: `V80_R__add_created_in_month_index.cypher`. 9 unit tests in `DataObjectCreatedInMonthTest` covering ym format boundaries (January leading-zero, December, 12-month sweep) and skip-guard cases. | **ADDITIVE** | **No new REST endpoints. No new config keys. No wire-shape changes.** The new relationship type `created_in_month` is purely internal to the Neo4j graph — no API response includes it. Existing `created_by` edges are untouched. All DataObject creation paths go through `DataObjectService.createDataObject()` (v1 `DataObjectRest`, v2 `DataObjectV2Rest`, v2 batch `DataObjectBatchV2Rest`, template instantiation `TemplateInstantiationRest`) — no path bypasses the new hook. | `V80__add_created_in_month_index.cypher` — range index `created_in_month_ym_idx` + batched backfill `CALL { } IN TRANSACTIONS OF 1000 ROWS`. Idempotent (MERGE throughout). Rollback: `V80_R__add_created_in_month_index.cypher`. Migration deferred integration test (Testcontainer pre/post edge-count assertion) tracked `aidocs/16 NEO-AUDIT-004`. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V80 runs on first start. Index build is online (no Neo4j downtime). On a 33k-DataObject instance expect ~33 transactions, < 1 s total. Verify post-backfill: `MATCH ()-[r:created_in_month]->() RETURN count(r);` → should be approximately equal to the count of non-deleted DataObjects. Rollback: `cypher-shell -f V80_R__add_created_in_month_index.cypher` then revert `DataObjectDAO.writeCreatedInMonth()` call site in `DataObjectService`. |

| **NEO-AUDIT-003 — Timeseries.appId NULL backfill (V82 migration)** | 2026-05-26. 596 `:Timeseries` nodes had `appId IS NULL` post-MFFD ingest (2026-05-24). The `appId_unique_Timeseries` uniqueness constraint + range index already existed from a prior V-era migration — the schema layer was provisioned but the data layer was empty (uniqueness constraints permit NULL, so the constraint was satisfied but meaningless). `V82__mint_timeseries_appids.cypher` runs `MATCH (t:Timeseries) WHERE t.appId IS NULL SET t.appId = randomUUID()` in batched `CALL { } IN TRANSACTIONS OF 500 ROWS`. Uses UUID-v4 (Cypher `randomUUID()`) rather than UUID-v7 — sort-by-time semantics are irrelevant for backfilled rows; both satisfy the uniqueness constraint. This is the DATA-layer fix only — the full 5-tuple→`shepardId` wire migration (TS-ID PRs in aidocs/44) remains a separate, larger undertaking. Rollback `V82_R__` removes `appId` from all `:Timeseries` nodes (see rollback file warning — cannot distinguish V82-minted from any pre-existing values). | **AWARE** | **No new REST endpoints, no new config keys, no wire-shape changes.** `appId` on `:Timeseries` is not currently exposed on any API response surface — the exposed identifier for timeseries channels is the `shepardId` column in the Postgres `timeseries` table (TS-ID PR-1/PR-2/PR-3). This migration only populates the Neo4j property so the uniqueness constraint becomes meaningful. Zero operator action required beyond restart. | `V82__mint_timeseries_appids.cypher` — idempotent (WHERE `appId IS NULL`), batched `CALL { } IN TRANSACTIONS OF 500 ROWS`. Rollback: `V82_R__mint_timeseries_appids.cypher` (destructive — removes ALL `:Timeseries.appId`; only safe on fresh installs where no Timeseries rows had `appId` before V82). Integration test (Testcontainer pre/post NULL-count assertion) tracked as follow-up in `aidocs/16 NEO-AUDIT-003`. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V82 applies automatically on first start. To verify: `MATCH (t:Timeseries) WHERE t.appId IS NULL RETURN count(t);` → 0 after restart. On 596 rows expect < 100 ms total. |

| **PROV1j — `provenanceMode` field on `:DataObject` + v2 IO surface (EU AI Act Art. 50)** | 2026-05-26. New optional nullable `provenanceMode` (String) field added to the `:DataObject` Neo4j entity (`de.dlr.shepard.context.collection.entities.DataObject`). Allowed values: `null` (default, semantically equivalent to `"human"`), `"human"`, `"ai"`, `"collaborative"`. Surfaced on `DataObjectDetailV2IO` (the `GET /v2/collections/{cid}/data-objects/{did}` response) and `DataObjectListItemV2IO` (the list endpoint). New v2-only create request body `CreateDataObjectV2IO` extends `DataObjectIO` with the optional `provenanceMode` field; `DataObjectV2Rest.create()` now accepts `CreateDataObjectV2IO` instead of plain `DataObjectIO` and auto-detects `"ai"` from the `X-AI-Agent` request header (same pattern as SEMA-V6-007 for SemanticAnnotation). `DataObjectService` uses an `instanceof CreateDataObjectV2IO` guard to stay backward-compatible with v1 callers. The 201 create response now returns `DataObjectDetailV2IO` (superset of `DataObjectIO` — additive, no v1 impact). Closes the EU AI Act Art. 50 per-artefact visibility gap (deadline 2026-08-02). 3 new PROV1j unit tests + 32 existing tests all pass (35 total). | **ZERO** | **No new REST endpoints. No new config keys. No migration.** `/shepard/api/...` surface unchanged (v1 `DataObjectRest.create()` still accepts `DataObjectIO` directly). The `provenanceMode` field is additive and nullable — existing `:DataObject` nodes lack the property; Neo4j OGM reads the absence as `null`. The field appears only on `/v2/` responses when non-null (`@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)`). The `POST /v2/collections/{cid}/data-objects` 201 body shape is a `DataObjectDetailV2IO` (previously `DataObjectIO`) — all fields from `DataObjectIO` are still present; the response is a strict superset. | — (no migration — additive nullable property; Neo4j accepts it lazily on first write). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard. The field is null on all existing DataObjects. New DataObjects created via `POST /v2/collections/{cid}/data-objects` with an AI agent that sets the `X-AI-Agent` request header will have `provenanceMode: "ai"` in the response. Callers that send `"provenanceMode": "collaborative"` in the body can override the auto-detection. To verify: POST a DataObject with `X-AI-Agent: test-agent/1.0` header via `POST /v2/collections/{cid}/data-objects` and confirm `provenanceMode: "ai"` in the 201 response. |

| **PROV1k — typed predecessor relationships (`prov:wasRevisionOf` / `fair2r:repairs`)** | 2026-05-26. New optional `typedPredecessors` field on `CreateDataObjectV2IO` (v2 create request body). Each entry is a `TypedPredecessorIO(predecessorAppId: String, relationshipType: String?)` with allowed types: `"prov:wasInformedBy"` (default), `"prov:wasRevisionOf"`, `"fair2r:repairs"`. Service resolves each `predecessorAppId` via new `DataObjectDAO.findByAppId(String)` and stores the typed list as `typedPredecessorsJson` on `:DataObject`. `DataObjectDetailV2IO` exposes `typedPredecessorSummaries[]` on read — each entry carries the predecessor's compact summary (appId, id, name, status) plus the stored relationship type (defaults to `"prov:wasInformedBy"` for pre-PROV1k nodes). `V84__TypedPredecessors.cypher` adds an informational index on `typedPredecessorsJson`. Frontend: `PredecessorRelationshipTypeChip.vue` + `useFetchTypedPredecessors` composable; Vuetify chips appear in the Relationships table for non-default types. 10 backend unit tests + 8 frontend Vitest tests. | **ZERO** | **No new endpoints. No config keys changed. `/shepard/api/...` (v1) untouched.** `predecessorIds[]` long array still works exactly as before — callers that only use `predecessorIds` see zero behaviour change. The `typedPredecessors` field is optional (null/absent → legacy path). `typedPredecessorsJson` is a schemaless property — existing `:DataObject` nodes have it as `null`. The migration `V84` only adds an index (non-blocking, informational). | `V84__TypedPredecessors.cypher` — index creation only, no data mutation. Rollback: `V84_R__TypedPredecessors.cypher`. Regression test planned in `DataObjectTypedPredecessorTest`. | **No action required for upgrade.** Apply V84 migration (auto-applied by MigrationsRunner on startup). Verify: `POST /v2/collections/{cid}/data-objects` with `typedPredecessors: [{predecessorAppId: "...", relationshipType: "prov:wasRevisionOf"}]` → `typedPredecessorSummaries[]` in response. Legacy callers using `predecessorIds` continue to work unchanged. |

| **MFG5 — DataObject `status` closed-enum enforcement + transition guard** | 2026-05-26. `StatusTransitionGuard` (package-private class in `de.dlr.shepard.context.collection.services`) enforces a closed set of lifecycle values (`DRAFT`, `IN_REVIEW`, `READY`, `PUBLISHED`, `ARCHIVED`) and forbids downgrade transitions (`PUBLISHED → {DRAFT, IN_REVIEW}` and `ARCHIVED → any`). Wired into `DataObjectService.createDataObject()` (400 on unknown value) and `DataObjectService.updateDataObject()` (400 on unknown value; 409 on forbidden downgrade). No new Neo4j schema; no migration. The guard is service-layer only — DAO-direct writes bypass it (none exist in shipped code; all DataObject write paths go through the service). Importer already validates status in `ImportValidationService.VALID_STATUSES`; the new guard adds a defence-in-depth layer at the service boundary. 11 unit tests in `StatusTransitionGuardTest`. **Extended by MFG1 (2026-05-27) to also include `NCR_OPEN`, `ON_HOLD`, `REJECTED`, `CERTIFIED`.** | **AWARE** | **API behaviour change for writes.** `POST` / `PUT` / `PATCH` requests that send an unrecognised `status` value now return HTTP 400 (`InvalidBodyException`). `PUT` / `PATCH` requests that attempt a forbidden downgrade (`PUBLISHED → DRAFT`, `PUBLISHED → IN_REVIEW`, `ARCHIVED → anything`) now return HTTP 409. Requests that send `null` status or a valid value are unaffected. **Operators should audit their existing DataObject rows** for non-canonical status values: `MATCH (d:DataObject) WHERE d.status IS NOT NULL AND NOT d.status IN ['DRAFT','IN_REVIEW','READY','PUBLISHED','ARCHIVED'] RETURN d.status, count(d) ORDER BY count(d) DESC;` — any rows found should be corrected before upgrading, as client code that re-writes them will receive 400. | None. | **No migration needed.** Restart shepard. Verify: `POST` a DataObject with `"status": "BOGUS"` → 400. Attempt to move a PUBLISHED DataObject to `"status": "DRAFT"` via `PATCH` → 409. Audit rogue values: run the Cypher above; if any are found, update them to a valid value before deploying. |

| **MFG1 + MFG2 — EN 9100 quality statuses + quality-engineer role guard + predecessor gate** | 2026-05-27. (MFG1) `StatusTransitionGuard.VALID_STATUSES` extended with four EN 9100 / EASA Part 21G quality-engineering statuses: `NCR_OPEN`, `ON_HOLD`, `REJECTED`, `CERTIFIED`. A new `QUALITY_RESTRICTED_STATUSES` set and `validateQualityRole(status, hasRole)` method enforce that transitioning TO these four values requires the caller to hold the `quality-engineer` Keycloak/Neo4j role — without the role, HTTP 403 (`InvalidAuthException`) is returned. Role check wired into both `createDataObject()` and `updateDataObject()`. `Constants.QUALITY_ENGINEER_ROLE = "quality-engineer"` constant added. Frontend: `EditDataObjectDialog.vue` dropdown gains all 4 new values; `StatusChip.vue` maps `NCR_OPEN` and `REJECTED` → `error` (red), `ON_HOLD` → `orange`, `CERTIFIED` → `success` (green). `ImportValidationService.VALID_STATUSES` updated to also include `ON_HOLD` and `CERTIFIED`. (MFG2) New `ManufacturingQualityGatesFeatureToggle` (default `false`, property `shepard.features.manufacturing-quality-gates.enabled`) registered in `FeatureToggleRegistry` as `"manufacturing-quality-gates"`. When enabled, `createDataObject()` checks all resolved predecessors: if any has status `NCR_OPEN` or `ON_HOLD`, returns HTTP 409 Conflict with message `"Predecessor {id} is in status {status} — resolve before creating a successor"`. Unit tests: `ManufacturingQualityTest` (13 tests covering MFG1 guard + MFG2 gate on/off). | **AWARE** | **New role required for quality statuses (MFG1).** Callers writing `NCR_OPEN`, `ON_HOLD`, `REJECTED`, or `CERTIFIED` must hold the `quality-engineer` role in Keycloak (or via Neo4j `HAS_ROLE` edge). Without it, HTTP 403 is returned. Existing callers that only use `DRAFT`, `IN_REVIEW`, `READY`, `PUBLISHED`, `ARCHIVED` are **unaffected**. The MFG2 predecessor gate defaults **off** — operators who want the gate must explicitly set `shepard.features.manufacturing-quality-gates.enabled=true` (or enable via `PATCH /v2/admin/features/manufacturing-quality-gates`). | None (additive service-layer logic; no Neo4j schema change; no migration). | **Operators must configure the `quality-engineer` role in Keycloak** (or via `HAS_ROLE` edge) for any user who should be able to set quality-restricted statuses. If no such role is configured, those status values remain inaccessible to all users (403). To enable the predecessor gate: `PATCH /v2/admin/features/manufacturing-quality-gates` with `{"enabled": true}` or set `shepard.features.manufacturing-quality-gates.enabled=true` in `application.properties`. Verify: attempt to `POST` a DataObject with `"status": "NCR_OPEN"` as a normal user → 403; repeat as a `quality-engineer` role holder → 201. |

| **PROV1l — `anonymizeInProvenance` user toggle (GDPR consent surface)** | 2026-05-26. New additive boolean field `anonymizeInProvenance` (default `false`) on the `:User` Neo4j entity. When `true`, `ProvenanceCaptureFilter` nulls both `agentUsername` and `mirroredUserAppId` before writing the `:Activity` node — the activity is still recorded (for volume/rate metrics) but carries no personal identifier. The `WAS_ASSOCIATED_WITH` graph edge is also suppressed (`ActivityDAO.wireEdges` already guards null `agentUsername`). Toggled via `PATCH /v2/users/me` with `{"anonymizeInProvenance": true/false/null}` (null = leave unchanged, RFC 7396). Exposed on `GET /v2/users/me` (`MeIO`) and all `UserIO` responses. 6 new unit tests in `ProvenanceCaptureFilterTest`. Neo4j schemaless — no migration needed; existing `:User` nodes lack the property and OGM reads absence as `false` (non-anonymized). | **AWARE** | **New field on UserIO/MeIO.** `/v2/` UserIO responses gain a boolean `anonymizeInProvenance: false` field. Clients that deserialize the response must tolerate the new field (standard JSON open-world semantics; should be a no-op for any well-written client). The `/shepard/api/...` v1 `UserIO` response also gains the field — minimal impact as existing upstream v1 callers are schema-agnostic for extra fields. The default is `false` so existing behaviour (identity captured) is preserved for all users who have not explicitly opted in. | None (additive nullable Neo4j property; no migration). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard. All existing users retain identity capture by default. Users who wish to opt out: `PATCH /v2/users/me {"anonymizeInProvenance": true}`. Verify post-upgrade: `MATCH (u:User) WHERE u.anonymizeInProvenance = true RETURN count(u);` → 0 on a fresh upgrade (no users opted in yet). |

| **PROV1j (activity-layer) — `sourceMode` + `agentId` on `:Activity` + `X-Provenance-Mode` response header (EU AI Act Art. 50)** | 2026-05-26. Two new additive nullable String properties added to the `:Activity` Neo4j entity: `sourceMode` (`"human"` or `"ai"`) and `agentId` (the value of the `X-AI-Agent` request header, when present). The `ProvenanceCaptureFilter` reads `X-AI-Agent` on every request and classifies the caller: header absent/blank → `sourceMode="human"`, `agentId=null`; header present → `sourceMode="ai"`, `agentId=<value>`. On `/v2/` responses, two new response headers are injected: `X-Provenance-Mode: human\|ai` (mirrors the captured mode) and `X-AI-Agent-Captured: <agentId>` (only when AI). The `ActivityIO` wire shape gains `sourceMode` and `agentId` fields (nullable, absent for pre-PROV1j rows). `ProvenanceService.record()` gains a 13-arg overload; the 11-arg and 12-arg overloads delegate with `sourceMode=null`, `agentId=null`. Closes the EU AI Act Art. 50 per-artefact visibility gap at the audit-log layer (complementary to the artefact-layer `provenanceMode` on `:DataObject` shipped in the same day's PROV1j-DataObject slice). | **ZERO** | **No new REST endpoints. No new config keys. No migration.** The `sourceMode` and `agentId` properties are additive and nullable — pre-PROV1j `:Activity` nodes simply lack these properties; Neo4j OGM reads absence as `null`. The `ActivityIO` response shape gains two nullable fields (standard JSON open-world semantics). The `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is **not** affected — response headers are injected on `/v2/` paths only. | None (additive nullable Neo4j properties; no migration). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard. Pre-existing `:Activity` nodes retain `sourceMode=null` (consumers should treat `null` as `"human"`). To verify: send a request with `X-AI-Agent: claude-sonnet-4-6` to any `POST /v2/...` endpoint and confirm the response carries `X-Provenance-Mode: ai` and `X-AI-Agent-Captured: claude-sonnet-4-6`. Then `MATCH (a:Activity) WHERE a.sourceMode = 'ai' RETURN a.agentId, count(a)` should return at least one row. |

| **MONGO-AUDIT-2026-05-24-008 — Structured-data reserved-key policy** | `aidocs/16` MONGO-AUDIT-2026-05-24-008. `StructuredDataService.createStructuredData` previously stripped ALL underscore-prefixed keys from the caller's JSON payload (e.g. `{"_myfield":"v"}` → `{"_myfield":"v"}` silently dropped). The fix changes the policy: only `_id` (MongoDB primary key) and `_meta` (Shepard metadata anchor) are reserved. Sending either now returns HTTP 400 + a field list; all other `_*` keys pass through unchanged and are stored as normal user data. | **AWARE** | **No migration. No new config key. No new endpoint.** Wire shape of `POST .../structured-data` changes: callers that previously sent `_myfield: "v"` and relied on it being silently dropped will now see it returned in `GET` responses. Callers that sent `_id` or `_meta` expecting them to be silently dropped now receive a 400; this prevents accidental MongoDB-key injection. | — | **If any caller intentionally sends `_*`-prefixed keys (other than `_id`/`_meta`) and depended on them being stripped:** those keys will now appear in stored documents. Audit structured-data clients before upgrading. For callers that sent `_id` or `_meta` explicitly: update to use non-reserved key names. |

| **AAS1l — `:AasConfig` runtime-mutable config singleton + `GET/PATCH /v2/admin/aas/config`** | 2026-05-26. New `:AasConfig` Neo4j entity (singleton, `HasAppId`). Fields: `appId` (UUID v7, unique), `enabled` (boolean, default `false`), `registryUrl` (String, nullable), `registryApiKey` (String, nullable, **never returned via GET**), `baseUrl` (String, nullable). New service `AasConfigService` seeds the singleton on startup from deploy-time `@ConfigProperty` defaults (`shepard.aas.enabled`, `shepard.aas.registry.url`, `shepard.aas.registry.api-key`, `shepard.aas.base-url`). Two admin endpoints: `GET /v2/admin/aas/config` (returns `AasConfigIO` — `enabled`, `registryUrl`, `apiKeyPresent` boolean, `baseUrl`; raw API key masked as presence flag); `PATCH /v2/admin/aas/config` (RFC 7396 merge-patch, `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`). Runtime values override `@ConfigProperty` fallbacks in `AasRegistryOutboxService` (via `effectiveRegistryUrl()`, `effectiveRegistryApiKey()`, `effectiveBaseUrl()`) and `AasServerSelfDescriptionService`. V88 migration adds uniqueness constraint on `:AasConfig.appId`. 17 unit tests (10 service + 7 REST). Follows UH1a / N1c2 operator-knob shape. | **ZERO** | **No new deploy-time config keys required.** Existing `shepard.aas.*` deploy-time properties still work — they seed the `:AasConfig` singleton on first startup and remain the fallback when no runtime value has been set. Runtime overrides use `PATCH /v2/admin/aas/config` (no restart needed). The `registryApiKey` is never returned via the API — use `GET /v2/admin/aas/config` to check `apiKeyPresent` and `PATCH` to rotate. | `V88__aas_config.cypher` — idempotent `CREATE CONSTRAINT AasConfig_appId_unique IF NOT EXISTS`. Rollback: `V88_R__aas_config.cypher`. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V88 applies automatically. On first start, `AasConfigService.seedIfNeeded()` mints the singleton from existing `shepard.aas.*` deploy-time values. Verify: `GET /v2/admin/aas/config` → 200 with current runtime config. To confirm the singleton: `MATCH (c:AasConfig) RETURN c.appId, c.enabled, c.registryUrl;` → 1 row. |

| **SPATIAL-V6-001 — `shepard-plugin-spatial` renamed to `shepard-plugin-spatiotemporal` + v6 DDL + PostGIS container collapse** | 2026-05-27. Three coupled changes: (1) **Plugin rename**: Maven module `plugins/spatial/` → `plugins/spatiotemporal/`; `artifactId` `shepard-plugin-spatial` → `shepard-plugin-spatiotemporal`; plugin manifest `id()` `"spatial"` → `"spatiotemporal"`; `PayloadKind.name()` `"spatial"` → `"spatiotemporal"`. The Java package structure (`de.dlr.shepard.data.spatialdata.*`, `de.dlr.shepard.context.references.spatialdata.*`) and the Quarkus datasource qualifier (`"spatial"`) are intentionally unchanged to avoid churn across all entity annotations. (2) **v6 green-field schema**: new Flyway migration `V2.0.0__green_field_schema.sql` in `plugins/spatiotemporal/src/main/resources/db/spatial/migration/` ships the `shepard_spatial` schema with `profile_container` metadata table and `profile` TimescaleDB hypertable (1-day chunks, 4 space-partitions by `container_id`, BRIN+GIST+GIN indexes, 7-day compression policy). `V1.0.0__setup_spatial_data_tables.sql` is NOT removed — the old `spatial_data_points` table coexists with the new schema during migration. (3) **PostGIS container collapse** (Decision D2, aidocs/data/90 §10): separate `postgis` compose service removed; PostGIS extension now co-located on `timescaledb` via a custom Docker image (`infrastructure/timescaledb-postgis/Dockerfile`, `apk add postgis` on Alpine). `QUARKUS_DATASOURCE_SPATIAL_JDBC_URL` changed from `postgis:5432` to `timescaledb:5432`. `docker-compose.override.yml` env var `SHEPARD_PLUGINS_SPATIAL_ENABLED` → `SHEPARD_PLUGINS_SPATIOTEMPORAL_ENABLED`. | **CONFIG + AWARE** | **Plugin id change (`"spatial"` → `"spatiotemporal"`)**: operators using `SHEPARD_PLUGINS_SPATIAL_ENABLED=true` must rename to `SHEPARD_PLUGINS_SPATIOTEMPORAL_ENABLED=true` (or update `application.properties` key). The `GET /v2/admin/plugins` response entry changes from `id: "spatial"` to `id: "spatiotemporal"`. **Datasource JDBC URL change**: operators with `QUARKUS_DATASOURCE_SPATIAL_JDBC_URL=jdbc:postgresql://postgis:5432/...` must change the hostname to `timescaledb` (or their TimescaleDB hostname). **Docker image rebuild**: `docker compose build timescaledb` is required on first upgrade — the `timescaledb` service now builds from `infrastructure/timescaledb-postgis/Dockerfile` instead of pulling `timescale/timescaledb:2.24.0-pg16` directly. **Spatial data migration**: operators with data in the old separate `postgis` container must dump+restore into TimescaleDB before removing the postgis service (see `plugins/spatiotemporal/docs/install.md §V2 Migration`). No change to any REST endpoint path or wire shape — all `/shepard/api/spatialdata/...` paths are unchanged. | `V2.0.0__green_field_schema.sql` — creates `shepard_spatial` schema, `profile_container` + `profile` hypertable, BRIN+GIST+GIN indexes, compression policy. Idempotent (`IF NOT EXISTS` guards throughout). `V1.0.0__setup_spatial_data_tables.sql` retained as-is. No Neo4j migrations. No rollback script required for V2.0.0 (additive schema; dropping `shepard_spatial` schema reverts if needed). | **Operators upgrading from v5 with a separate `postgis` container:** (1) `docker compose build timescaledb`; (2) dump spatial data from old postgis: `docker compose exec postgis pg_dump -U shepard -t spatial_data_points <db> > spatial_v1.sql`; (3) restore into TimescaleDB; (4) rename `SHEPARD_PLUGINS_SPATIAL_ENABLED` → `SHEPARD_PLUGINS_SPATIOTEMPORAL_ENABLED` in `.env`; (5) update `QUARKUS_DATASOURCE_SPATIAL_JDBC_URL` hostname from `postgis` to `timescaledb`; (6) `docker compose up -d`; (7) remove old `postgis` service after verifying. **Operators upgrading who never used the spatial plugin:** no action beyond image rebuild if they want the base timescaledb image to stay current. |

| **PROMPT-h2 — `promptLogMode` field on `:Collection` + `V91` migration** | 2026-05-27. New additive nullable String property `promptLogMode` on the `:Collection` Neo4j entity. Three valid values (`"HASH_ONLY"`, `"BODY_REDACTED"`, `"BODY_RAW"`) corresponding to the `PromptLogMode` enum; null is treated as `"HASH_ONLY"` by all consumers. The field is consumed at write-time by the PromptLog substrate (PROMPT-a, not yet shipped) to decide how AI conversation bodies are stored. Exposed on the `/v2/` surface via `GET /v2/collections/{appId}` (returned in `CollectionIO`) and `PATCH /v2/collections/{appId}` (accepted in the merge-patch body). `CollectionIO` uses `@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)` — same treatment as `heroImageUrl` — so the field is absent from the v1 `/shepard/api/` wire when null, preserving byte-fidelity with upstream 5.2.0. After `V91` migration runs, all existing Collections carry `"HASH_ONLY"` so GET responses will always show the effective mode. Frontend: `PromptLogModeSelector.vue` (v-select, three options) in the `EditCollectionDialog` under advanced mode. Tests: 4 wire-contract tests in `CollectionIOTest` + 3 CRUD tests in `CollectionV2RestTest`. | **ZERO** | Additive nullable field. `@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)` keeps it off the v1 wire when null. After V91, non-null value `"HASH_ONLY"` will appear in v1 wire for migrated Collections — this is the only observable change to a v1 client, and it is open-world compatible (unknown fields should be ignored per JSON convention). | `V91__Set_default_promptLogMode_on_collections.cypher` — idempotent (`WHERE c.promptLogMode IS NULL`). Rollback: `V91_R__Rollback_Set_default_promptLogMode_on_collections.cypher` (removes `promptLogMode` from all `:Collection` nodes — safe only when no operator has changed a mode away from `HASH_ONLY`). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V91 applies automatically on first start. To verify: `MATCH (c:Collection) WHERE c.promptLogMode IS NULL RETURN count(c);` → 0 after restart. To change the mode for a Collection: `PATCH /v2/collections/<appId>` with body `{"promptLogMode": "BODY_REDACTED"}`. |

| **T1i — built-in `EquipmentItem` ShepardTemplate (DIN EN 9100 §7.1.5)** | 2026-05-27. Seeds one new `:ShepardTemplate` node via `V93__Seed_EquipmentItem_template.cypher`. The template is a `DATAOBJECT_RECIPE` with five mandatory attributes: `serial_number`, `manufacturer`, `model`, `calibration_valid_until` (ISO 8601 date), `calibration_cert_id`. Tags: `calibration`, `equipment`, `din-en-9100`, `quality`. `createdBy: 'system'`; `source: 'V93-builtin'` rollback handle. Seeded via idempotent `MERGE {name: 'EquipmentItem', version: 1}` — re-running the migration is a no-op. | **ZERO** | Additive — one new `:ShepardTemplate` node with no edges. No new REST endpoints. No new config keys. No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path. The template is immediately visible to authenticated users via `GET /v2/templates?kind=DATAOBJECT_RECIPE` and in the template picker dialog. | `V93__Seed_EquipmentItem_template.cypher` — idempotent MERGE on `{name, version}`. Rollback: `V93_R__Rollback_Seed_EquipmentItem_template.cypher` (DETACH DELETE WHERE `source = 'V93-builtin'`). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V93 applies automatically on first start. Verify: `MATCH (t:ShepardTemplate {name: 'EquipmentItem', version: 1}) RETURN t.appId, t.retired, t.templateKind;` → 1 row with `retired: false`, `templateKind: 'DATAOBJECT_RECIPE'`. |

| **TPL-SEED-DEMO-1 — six casual-researcher ShepardTemplates** | 2026-05-31. Seeds six new `:ShepardTemplate` nodes via `V99__Demo_templates_seed.cypher`: **Generic Test Run**, **Wet Lab Sample**, **Process Step (Manufacturing)**, **Quality Inspection / NCR** (all `DATAOBJECT_RECIPE`), **Research Collection**, **Citable Dataset** (both `COLLECTION_RECIPE`). Each row is created with `createdBy: 'system'`, `version: 1`, `retired: false`, `source: 'V99-builtin'`. Body shape carries both `dataObject.attributes[]` / `collection.attributes[]` (descriptor array for picker UI) and `dataobjects[0].attributes` / `collections[0].attributes` (Map<String,String> consumed by `TemplateInstantiationRest.extractAttributes()`) so server-side instantiation pre-populates the new DataObject/Collection with the expected attribute keys. Idempotent `MERGE {name, version}` per template — re-running the migration is a no-op; user-authored templates with the same name are untouched (they lack the `source` marker). 4-case smoke test (`V99DemoTemplatesSeedTest`) parses the migration as a classpath resource and validates every body under `TemplateBodyValidator`. | **ZERO** | Additive — six new `:ShepardTemplate` nodes with no edges. No new REST endpoints. No new config keys. No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path. Templates immediately visible to authenticated users via `GET /v2/templates?kind=DATAOBJECT_RECIPE` + `?kind=COLLECTION_RECIPE` and in the template picker dialog on Create. | `V99__Demo_templates_seed.cypher` — six idempotent MERGEs on `{name, version}`. Rollback: `V99_R__Demo_templates_seed.cypher` (`MATCH (t:ShepardTemplate {source: 'V99-builtin'}) DETACH DELETE t`). Test: `backend/src/test/java/de/dlr/shepard/migrations/neo4j/V99DemoTemplatesSeedTest.java` (4 cases). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V99 applies automatically on first start. Verify: `MATCH (t:ShepardTemplate {source: 'V99-builtin'}) RETURN t.name, t.templateKind ORDER BY t.name;` → six rows: `Citable Dataset / COLLECTION_RECIPE`, `Generic Test Run / DATAOBJECT_RECIPE`, `Process Step (Manufacturing) / DATAOBJECT_RECIPE`, `Quality Inspection / NCR / DATAOBJECT_RECIPE`, `Research Collection / COLLECTION_RECIPE`, `Wet Lab Sample / DATAOBJECT_RECIPE`. Templates are not retired and appear in the picker by default; an instance admin can `PATCH /v2/templates/{appId}` with `{"retired": true}` to hide any of them. |

| **TPL-SEED-MFFD-1 — built-in MFFD process-step ShepardTemplates (8 × DATAOBJECT_RECIPE)** | 2026-05-31. Seeds eight new `:ShepardTemplate` nodes via `V100__Mffd_process_templates_seed.cypher`, one per step of the MFFD AFP→assembly chain (`MFFD AFP Layup`, `MFFD Ultrasonic Welding`, `MFFD Resistance Welding`, `MFFD Stud Welding`, `MFFD NDT Inspection`, `MFFD Frame Welding`, `MFFD Stringer Connection`, `MFFD LBR Cleats Assembly`). Each template is a `DATAOBJECT_RECIPE` keyed `{name, version: 1}`, carrying common fields (`process-type`, `step-number`, `equipment-id`, `operator-id`, `started-at`, `ended-at`, `notes`, `parent-collection-app-id`) plus per-process unique fields (TCP temperature + consolidation force + ply-count + roller-pressure + thermal-trail TimeseriesReference for AFP; frequency/amplitude/hold-time/weld-energy for ultrasonic; current/voltage/weld-time/electrode-force for resistance; stud-type + pull-test for stud; inspection-method + result enum + defect-list + inspector-id for NDT; ultrasonic kinematics + frame-id + joining-track enum for frame; joint-type enum + stringer-id + joint-strength-test for stringer connection; cleat-count + lbr-program-id + force-torque-profile-ref for LBR cleats). Body JSON embeds an `annotations[]` array with `urn:shepard:domain="aerospace-manufacturing"`, `urn:shepard:mffd:process-type=<canonical-name>`, `urn:shepard:mffd:step-number=<1..8>`, and (for CF/LMPAEK steps) `urn:shepard:material="CF/LMPAEK"` — pre-fill seeds awaiting `TemplateInstantiationRest` wiring (tracked TPL-INSTANTIATE-ANNOTATIONS-1). `createdBy: 'system'`; `source: 'V100-mffd'` rollback handle. Idempotent MERGE — re-running the migration is a no-op. | **ZERO** | Additive — eight new `:ShepardTemplate` nodes with no edges. No new REST endpoints. No new config keys. No change to any `/shepard/api/...` path. The templates are immediately visible to authenticated users via `GET /v2/templates?kind=DATAOBJECT_RECIPE` and in the template picker dialog (alongside the V93 `EquipmentItem` template). | `V100__Mffd_process_templates_seed.cypher` — eight idempotent MERGEs keyed `{name, version: 1}`. Rollback: `V100_R__Mffd_process_templates_seed.cypher` (DETACH DELETE WHERE `source = 'V100-mffd'` — leaves user-authored templates with the same names untouched). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V100 applies automatically on first start. Verify: `MATCH (t:ShepardTemplate {source: 'V100-mffd'}) RETURN t.name, t.version, t.appId, t.templateKind ORDER BY t.name;` → 8 rows, all `templateKind: 'DATAOBJECT_RECIPE'`, all `retired: false`. To list every MFFD process-step template available for instantiation: `curl -fsS -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" "https://<your-host>/v2/templates?kind=DATAOBJECT_RECIPE" \| jq '.[] \| select(.tags // [] \| contains(["mffd"]))'`. |

| **V2CONV-B7-KIND-DECLARED-SHAPES — `PayloadKind.shapeDescriptor()` + `KindShapeSeeder` startup seeder** | 2026-06-04. Adds `default ShapeSpec shapeDescriptor() { return null; }` to the `PayloadKind` SPI interface. Adds `KindShapeSeeder` — an `@ApplicationScoped` `StartupEvent` observer that iterates every `PayloadKind` registered via `ServiceLoader`, and for each that returns a non-null `ShapeSpec`, seeds (or idempotently updates) a `DATAOBJECT_RECIPE` `ShepardTemplate` in Neo4j at startup. The template's `name` is `<kind.name()>-data-shape`; its body is `{"dataObject":{"shapeSpec":{…ShapeBuildRequestIO-compatible JSON…}}}`. The seeder attaches the sentinel tag `"system:kind-shape-seeder"` — its presence guards automatic updates; absence means an admin has customised the template and the seeder skips the diff-and-update logic. Copy-on-write versioning (retire prior version, mint v+1) when the shape changes and the system tag is present. Fail-soft: any per-kind exception is caught, logged as WARN, and startup continues. In-tree proof-of-concept: `UrdfKindShapeExample implements PayloadKind` (registered in `META-INF/services`) declares two optional `xsd:string` predicates for the URDF data-shape. 7 unit tests in `KindShapeSeederTest`. | **ZERO** | Additive: new startup-time `@ApplicationScoped` bean. No REST endpoint added. No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. No Neo4j schema migration — templates are created/updated via the existing `ShepardTemplateDAO.createOrUpdate()` which uses `MERGE {appId}`. The only observable effect for an upgrading operator: on first restart after upgrade, `KindShapeSeeder` may create new `DATAOBJECT_RECIPE` templates named `<kind>-data-shape` (one per PayloadKind that returns a non-null ShapeSpec). These templates are tagged `system:kind-shape-seeder` and are visible via `GET /v2/templates?kind=DATAOBJECT_RECIPE`. They can be customised (at which point the seeder leaves them alone) or retired like any template. | None — no migration script. Templates are upserted via DAO at startup; the operation is idempotent (same ShapeSpec body → no write). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard. The seeder runs once on startup. Verify: after restart, `GET /v2/templates?kind=DATAOBJECT_RECIPE` lists templates with names ending `-data-shape` tagged `system:kind-shape-seeder` for each `PayloadKind` that returns a non-null `shapeDescriptor()`. If a kind's template is absent, check the backend startup log for `KindShapeSeeder: failed to seed template for kind '…'` WARN entries. To opt a custom template out of future seeder overwrites: remove the `system:kind-shape-seeder` tag via `PATCH /v2/templates/{appId}` — the seeder will detect the tag is absent and skip that template on future restarts. |
| **V2CONV-B8-VIEW-SHAPE-SEEDING — `PayloadKind.viewShapeDescriptor()` + `KindShapeSeeder` VIEW_RECIPE seeding** | 2026-06-09. Extends `PayloadKind` with `default ViewRecipeSpec viewShapeDescriptor() { return null; }` — the B8 sibling of B7's `shapeDescriptor()`. `KindShapeSeeder.onStart()` now also calls `seedViewKind(kind.name(), spec)` for each kind returning a non-null `ViewRecipeSpec`; seeds an idempotent `VIEW_RECIPE` template named `<kind.name()>-view-shape`. Body is the canonical VIEW_RECIPE JSON (`renderer`, optional `viewRecipeShape`, `channelBindings[]`) consumed by `POST /v2/shapes/render`. Same system-tag guard, copy-on-write versioning, fail-soft, and request-context pattern as B7. New records: `ViewRecipeSpec` (viewRecipeShape + renderer + channelBindings) + `ChannelBindingSpec` (role + channelSelector + unit + required) in `de.dlr.shepard.v2.shapes.builder`. 6 new tests in `KindShapeSeederTest` (13 total). | **ZERO** | Additive: extends an existing startup bean. No REST endpoint added. No `/shepard/api/...` surface touched. No Neo4j schema migration. Observable effect: on first restart after upgrade, `KindShapeSeeder` may create new `VIEW_RECIPE` templates named `<kind>-view-shape` for each `PayloadKind` returning a non-null `viewShapeDescriptor()`. Tagged `system:kind-shape-seeder`; visible via `GET /v2/templates?kind=VIEW_RECIPE`; customisable (seeder skips on absent system tag). | None. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard. Verify: `GET /v2/templates?kind=VIEW_RECIPE` lists templates with names ending `-view-shape` tagged `system:kind-shape-seeder`. |

| **QM1a — DataObject status: `CONCESSION_PENDING` added (EN 9100 §8.7 disposition window)** | 2026-06-02. `StatusTransitionGuard.VALID_STATUSES` + `QUALITY_RESTRICTED_STATUSES` extended with `CONCESSION_PENDING` — the disposition state between `NCR_OPEN` and a final `REJECTED` / `CERTIFIED` closure. Like the other MFG1 quality statuses, writing this value requires the `quality-engineer` role. `AbstractDataObjectIO.@Schema(enumeration=…)` bumped to include the full 10-value set (`DRAFT/IN_REVIEW/READY/PUBLISHED/ARCHIVED + NCR_OPEN/ON_HOLD/REJECTED/CERTIFIED/CONCESSION_PENDING`); upstream-frozen container IO unchanged. Frontend `StatusChip.vue` renders the new state as an amber-outlined chip with `mdi-shield-alert-outline`; dropdown and filter both list it. Service-layer guard only — no Neo4j schema change. | **ZERO** | Additive: existing DataObjects keep their existing status; only new writes / transitions to `CONCESSION_PENDING` need the role. Existing callers limited to the 5-value base set or the 4-value MFG1 set are unaffected. | None — service-layer change only. | **No action required.** Restart shepard; verify a `quality-engineer` can `PATCH` `{"status": "CONCESSION_PENDING"}` on an `NCR_OPEN` DataObject → 200; same call without the role → 403; an unknown status (e.g. `BOGUS`) → 400. |
| **QM1b — Typed predecessor vocabulary: `fair2r:concession` + PATCH endpoint** | 2026-06-02. PROV1k's `TypedPredecessorIO.ALLOWED_TYPES` extended with `fair2r:concession` (concession / "use-as-is" disposition relationship). The four-value vocabulary (`prov:wasInformedBy` / `prov:wasRevisionOf` / `fair2r:repairs` / `fair2r:concession`) is the canonical surface for the QM1b `transitionKind = normal/re-test/rework/concession` design — no parallel edge property. New endpoint `PATCH /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects/{dataObjectAppId}/predecessors/{predecessorAppId}` with body `{"relationshipType": "fair2r:concession"}` annotates an already-linked predecessor edge; the edge must exist (404 otherwise). Backed by `DataObjectService.setPredecessorRelationshipType()` which loads, replaces (or inserts), and persists `typedPredecessorsJson`. Frontend `PredecessorRelationshipTypeChip.vue` renders the new amber chip. | **ZERO** | Additive new endpoint + additive vocabulary value. The existing `POST /v2/.../data-objects` / `PATCH /v2/.../data-objects/{id}` paths still accept the three pre-QM1b types unchanged. Pre-PROV1k DataObjects (no `typedPredecessorsJson`) keep falling back to `prov:wasInformedBy` at read time. | None — `typedPredecessorsJson` is a schema-free string property already created with PROV1k. | **No action required.** Restart shepard; verify: `curl -fsS -X PATCH -H "X-API-KEY: …" -H "Content-Type: application/json" "https://<host>/v2/collections/{cid}/data-objects/{did}/predecessors/{pid}" -d '{"relationshipType":"fair2r:concession"}'` → 200 with `typedPredecessorSummaries[*].relationshipType` containing the new value. |

Template is a `STRUCTURED_RECIPE` (new kind added to `TemplateBodyValidator.expectedKeys` — root must declare `structuredData` / `record` / `schema`) keyed `{name: 'Disposition record', version: 1}`. Body carries the EN 9100 §8.7 disposition fields: `ncr_id` (string), `defect_type` (vocabulary), `disposition` (enum: `use-as-is\|rework\|scrap\|concession`), `approver_orcid` (ORCID), `approver_username` (string), `decided_at` (date-time), `notes` (markdown). `createdBy: 'system'`; `source: 'V103-builtin'` rollback handle; idempotent MERGE — re-running is a no-op. 6-case smoke test `V103DispositionRecordTemplateTest` asserts the shape end-to-end without needing a Neo4j testcontainer. | **ZERO** | Additive — one new `:ShepardTemplate` node. No new REST endpoint. No new config key. No `/shepard/api/...` path change. Template immediately visible via `GET /v2/templates?kind=STRUCTURED_RECIPE` (and surfaces in the picker once a frontend STRUCTURED_RECIPE filter ships — tracked separately). | `V103__Disposition_record_template.cypher` — one idempotent MERGE on `{name, version}`. Rollback: `V103_R__Disposition_record_template.cypher` (DETACH DELETE WHERE `source = 'V103-builtin'`). Test: `backend/src/test/java/de/dlr/shepard/migrations/neo4j/V103DispositionRecordTemplateTest.java` (6 cases). | **No action required.** Restart shepard; V103 applies automatically on first start. Verify: `MATCH (t:ShepardTemplate {name: 'Disposition record', version: 1, source: 'V103-builtin'}) RETURN t.appId, t.templateKind, t.retired;` → 1 row, `templateKind: 'STRUCTURED_RECIPE'`, `retired: false`. |

| **ID-MIG4 — wire rename `appId` → `shepardId` on `/v2/` surface (BREAKING — planned)** | 2026-05-27. **Status: planned — not yet shipped.** The `appId` JSON field on all `/v2/` API responses and request bodies is planned to be renamed to `shepardId`. The UUID value does not change; only the key name moves. The rename proceeds in two phases: (1) **ID-MIG1 dual-emit**: both `appId` and `shepardId` present simultaneously in `/v2/` responses; `Deprecation: true` + `Sunset: <date>` response headers signal the window. (2) **ID-MIG2 cutover**: `appId` dropped from `/v2/` responses; `shepardId` is the only key. The `/shepard/api/...` (v1) surface is byte-frozen and is **never** affected. Pre-conditions for cutover: ID-MIG1 dual-emit shipped + L2d shipped + SHACL substrate split decision made. Comms artefacts shipped with this row: `docs/admin/upgrade/appid-to-shepardid.md` (operator upgrade guide with field-by-field mapping + migration checklist). Banner on GitHub Pages site and release-note ladder deferred to ID-MIG1 dual-emit ship. | **BREAKING** (v2 surface only; v1 unaffected) | **`/v2/` responses today** emit `appId`. When ID-MIG1 ships, they will also emit `shepardId` (both present). When ID-MIG2 ships, only `shepardId` will be present. **Affected classes:** `BasicEntityIO` (base for all entity shapes), `DataObjectSummaryIO`, `DataObjectDetailV2IO`, `ContainerSummaryIO`, `CollectionPropertiesIO`, `FileBundleReferenceIO`, `FileReferenceV2IO`, `AnnotationIO`, `ActivityIO`, `MirroredUserIO`, `LabJournalRevisionIO`, `NotebookReferenceIO`, `VocabularyIO`, `ShepardTemplateIO`, `SemanticConfigIO`, and all plugin IO classes that extend `BasicEntityIO`. URL path parameter templates (e.g. `{appId}` in `/v2/collections/{appId}`) keep the name `appId` — only response/request body JSON keys move. | — (no migration file; field rename is code-only during ID-MIG2). Deferred integration test tracked `aidocs/16 ID-MIG4`. | **Action required when ID-MIG2 ships.** Update any client code, script, Jupyter notebook, or MCP tool that references `appId` in a `/v2/` response body to use `shepardId`. During the dual-emit window (ID-MIG1 → ID-MIG2) both fields are present — you can migrate clients incrementally. Migration checklist: `grep -r '"appId"' your-project/` + `grep -r '\.appId' your-project/`. See `docs/admin/upgrade/appid-to-shepardid.md` for the full guide and field-by-field mapping. |

| **REF-EDIT-5 — StructuredDataReference rename via `PATCH /v2/references/{appId}`** | 2026-06-21. Frontend edit dialog for StructuredDataReference name. Backend PATCH was already present via the unified `PATCH /v2/references/{appId}` endpoint (V2CONV-A2) dispatched to `StructuredDataReferenceKindHandler.patch()` — mutable field: `name` (blank name → 400). Frontend: new `EditStructuredDataReferenceDialog.vue` under `frontend/components/context/display-components/structured-data-references/` + `:on-edit` prop wired to `TitleAndMetadataDisplay` on the detail page (gated on `isAllowedToEditCollection && !!structuredDataReference.appId`). 12 Vitest tests in `EditStructuredDataReferenceDialog.test.ts`. No new REST endpoints; no Neo4j migrations; no new config keys. The `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is not touched. | **ZERO** | No new deploy-time config keys. No Neo4j migrations. No new REST endpoints. | — | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard frontend; the Edit pencil appears on StructuredDataReference detail pages for users with Write access. To verify: navigate to a StructuredDataReference detail page → click the pencil icon → rename → Save → name updates in breadcrumbs + title. |

| **REF-EDIT-6 — `PATCH /v2/uri-references/{appId}` (RFC 7396 merge-patch)** | 2026-05-28. New endpoint `PATCH /v2/uri-references/{appId}` in `URIReferenceV2Rest` at `de.dlr.shepard.v2.uri.resources`. Mutable fields: `name`, `uri`, `relationship` (relationship nullable — send `null` to clear). Auth: Write permission on the owning DataObject (same permission chain as `FileReferenceV2Rest`). Follows the RFC 7396 merge-patch pattern: absent fields unchanged, explicit `null` clears nullable fields. Returns 200 + updated `URIReferenceIO` on success; 400 for blank name/uri or non-object body; 401 unauthenticated; 403 insufficient permission; 404 unknown appId. `ProvenanceCaptureFilter` auto-captures the UPDATE activity. Two new backend methods: `URIReferenceDAO.findByAppId(String)` and `URIReferenceService.patchReferenceByAppId(String, Map)`. Frontend: `EditUriReferenceDialog.vue` + `mdi-pencil-outline` ActionButton in `DataObjectRelationshipsTable.vue` for "Link" rows. 8 JUnit + 16 Vitest tests. | **ZERO** | No new deploy-time config keys. No Neo4j migrations. The `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is **not** affected. | — | **No action required for upgrade.** New additive v2 endpoint; existing clients unaffected. Restart shepard. To verify: `PATCH /v2/uri-references/<appId>` with `{"name": "renamed", "uri": "https://example.com"}` → 200. |

| **RDK-PARSE-1 — `shepard-plugin-fileformat-robotics` tier-1 (RoboDK `.rdk` text-scrape parser)** | 2026-05-28. New standalone Maven module `plugins/fileformat-robotics/` (NOT yet wired into the aggregator — same `OTVIS-WIRE-AGGREGATOR-1`-class blocker, see RDK-WIRE-AGGREGATOR-1 in `aidocs/16`). Implements the `FileParserPlugin` SPI shim from aidocs/integrations/110 §3. Decompresses the `.rdk` zlib payload (4-byte custom magic skipped), walks `[uint32 LE byte-length][UTF-16LE bytes]` records, classifies each via regex, and emits eight `urn:shepard:rdk:*` SemanticAnnotations on the parent FileReference: `appVersion`, `platform`, `programSource`, `cadRef[]`, `stepRef[]`, `apiEndpoint`, `robotController`, `companionSpatialAnalyzer` (the last one points at the FileReference appId of a same-base `.xit`/`.xit64` sibling in the same FileContainer — Spatial Analyzer cross-link per aidocs/110 §4.3). No DataObject or container creation; full kinematic tree deferred to RDK-PARSE-2 (RoboDK Python API sidecar). 17 JUnit tests including a live-fixture acceptance check that reads `examples/mffd-showcase/raw-data/mffd-data/cell/MFZ.rdk` (12.1 MB; self-skips when absent — fixture NOT in git) and reproduces the §4.3 expected output verbatim. Three-audience docs trio shipped: `plugins/fileformat-robotics/docs/{reference,quickstart,install}.md` + README. Frontend stub deferred — annotations surface through the existing semantic-annotations panel (no new component needed for tier-1; tier-2 will surface a viewer). | **ZERO** | No new REST endpoints. No new config keys. No Neo4j migrations. The plugin is invoked from the existing FileContainer upload pipeline (wire-up tracked as RDK-WIRE-AGGREGATOR-1, paired with OTVIS-WIRE-AGGREGATOR-1). All output is `urn:shepard:rdk:*` annotations on the FileReference — open-world additive to the `:SemanticAnnotation` substrate. The `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is not touched. | None — pure metadata emission on existing entities. | **No action required for upgrade.** When the canonical SPI wire-up lands (RDK-WIRE-AGGREGATOR-1), the parser becomes active on `.rdk` uploads automatically. Until then, the jar is buildable but not wired (`cd plugins/fileformat-robotics && ../../backend/mvnw test` passes 17 tests). Verify post-wire-up: upload `MFZ.rdk` to any FileContainer and run `MATCH (a:SemanticAnnotation)-[:ANNOTATES]->(f:FileReference {filename: 'MFZ.rdk'}) WHERE a.predicate STARTS WITH 'urn:shepard:rdk:' RETURN a.predicate, a.value;` → ≥ 10 rows. |

| **URDF-WEBVIEW-1 phase 1 + MFFD RDK→URDF showcase** | 2026-05-29 (phase 1 shipped 2026-05-28; showcase 2026-05-29). Sample `frontend/public/urdf-samples/two-link-arm.urdf`. **End-to-end showcase `examples/mffd-rdk-urdf-showcase/`** seeds a Collection demonstrating the RDK→URDF→animator chain off the real MFFD MFZ.rdk fixture: the RDK FileReference triggers RDK-PARSE-1 tier-1 auto-scrape (8 `urn:shepard:rdk:*` annotations), the **real** KUKA KR210 R2700/2 URDF + 7 visual STL meshes ship under `frontend/public/urdf-samples/kr210_r2700_2/` (sourced from `kroshu/kuka_robot_descriptions` at `f648ded13`, Apache-2.0; the actual MFFD AFP cell robot — replaces the previous KR210 L150 substitute from `ros-industrial/kuka_experimental` per `urdf/SOURCES.md`; legacy L150 assets at `frontend/public/urdf-samples/kr210/` left in place for backwards compat with prior seed records), and a synthetic 30s × 100 Hz × 6-joint AFP-layup trajectory uploads with `urn:shepard:urdf:joint = <jointName>` channel annotations so `UrdfChannelPicker` auto-binds. Phase 2 (`RdkToUrdfExporter` sidecar — RoboDK SDK licence required; Foxglove iframe; `UrdfRecordButton`; Trace3D+URDF scene composition) queued. | **ZERO** | No new REST endpoints (consumes existing `POST /v2/shapes/render` + `POST /v2/timeseries-containers/{id}/channels/{shepardId}/annotations`). No new config keys. No Neo4j migrations. New static assets at `frontend/public/urdf-samples/kr210_r2700_2/` (~12 MB total, 7 STL meshes + 1 URDF); legacy `frontend/public/urdf-samples/kr210/` retained (~14 MB). The `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is not touched. | None — pure frontend additive. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard frontend after pull; the URDF renderer becomes available from the "Visualize in 3D" dialog renderer toggle automatically. Verify: open `https://<your-host>/shapes/render?renderer=urdf&urdfUrl=%2Furdf-samples%2Ftwo-link-arm.urdf` → renders the sample two-link arm. To seed the MFFD RDK→URDF showcase: `cd examples/mffd-rdk-urdf-showcase && python3 seed.py --host <api-url> --apikey <key>` (idempotent; needs the MFZ.rdk fixture mounted at `examples/mffd-showcase/raw-data/mffd-data/cell/MFZ.rdk`). See `examples/mffd-rdk-urdf-showcase/{README,SHOWCASE}.md` + `aidocs/integrations/113`. |
| **J1e — `:JupyterConfig` runtime-mutable singleton + `/v2/admin/jupyter/config` + `GET /v2/jupyter/config` (public read) + `shepard-admin jupyter`** | 2026-05-29. New `:JupyterConfig` Neo4j entity (singleton, `HasAppId`). Fields: `appId` (UUID v7, unique), `enabled` (boolean, default `false`), `hubUrl` (String, nullable). Seed-on-first-start from `shepard.jupyter.enabled` + `shepard.jupyter.hub-url` (the only two new deploy-time properties — both optional). Three new REST paths: `GET /v2/admin/jupyter/config` + `PATCH /v2/admin/jupyter/config` (instance-admin, RFC 7396 merge-patch) + `GET /v2/jupyter/config` (any authenticated user, public read for the unified data-references frontend gate). CLI parity: `shepard-admin jupyter {status,enable,disable,set-hub-url}` shipped with shared `--output={human,json}` + `--url` + `--api-key`. Frontend: per-notebook "Open in JupyterHub" launch button in the unified Data References table on every DataObject detail page, gated on `enabled && hubUrl != null`. The pre-J1e per-user `editor.preferredJupyter` preference becomes inert (kept in MeApi for migration but no longer read). 28 backend tests + 4 CLI tests + 27 frontend tests. `V94__Add_appId_constraint_JupyterConfig.cypher` + rollback twin. | **ZERO** | **No required action.** Pre-existing operators see `{enabled: false, hubUrl: null}` after the singleton is seeded — the affordance is hidden by default. The `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is **not** affected. The deploy-time `shepard.jupyter.*` keys are optional and only seed the singleton on first start; runtime PATCHes win after that. | `V94__Add_appId_constraint_JupyterConfig.cypher` — idempotent `CREATE CONSTRAINT appId_unique_JupyterConfig IF NOT EXISTS`. Rollback: `V94_R__Add_appId_constraint_JupyterConfig.cypher`. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V94 applies automatically. On first start, `JupyterConfigService.seedIfNeeded()` mints the singleton with `enabled=false`. To enable: `shepard-admin jupyter enable && shepard-admin jupyter set-hub-url https://hub.example.org`. Verify: `MATCH (c:JupyterConfig) RETURN c.appId, c.enabled, c.hubUrl;` → 1 row. See `docs/admin/runbooks/jupyterhub-config.md` for the full runbook. |
| **REF-UNIFIED-TABLE-FR1B — `GET /v2/files/by-data-object/{appId}` + FR1b rows in the unified data-references table** | 2026-05-29. New endpoint `GET /v2/files/by-data-object/{dataObjectAppId}` on the existing `FileReferenceV2Rest` returning a `List<FileReferenceV2IO>` (FR1b singletons attached to the DataObject; filters out soft-deleted rows). The endpoint is the **additive sibling** of the upstream-byte-frozen v1 list `GET /shepard/api/collections/{collectionId}/dataObjects/{dataObjectId}/fileReferences` (which returns only FR1a `FileBundleReference` shapes). With both endpoints in play, the unified frontend Data References table now picks up FR1b singletons alongside FR1a bundles, timeseries refs, and structured-data refs. The frontend mapper classifies FR1b rows as **File** or **Notebook** based on filename suffix; the **Notebook** kind drives the icon + the conditional Jupyter launch action (J1e). The dedicated `DataObjectNotebooksPane` (J1c) is dropped from the DataObject detail page — notebooks now live in the unified table. 7 new backend tests on `listByDataObject` + 15 Vitest tests on the mapper. | **ZERO** | **No required action.** New endpoint; existing v1 callers unaffected. The DataObject detail page no longer has a dedicated "Jupyter Notebooks" expansion panel — the table absorbs it. No new config keys. | None — additive endpoint only. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard. Verify: `GET /v2/files/by-data-object/<dataObjectAppId>` → 200 with the FR1b singletons attached to that DataObject. The frontend picks them up automatically on the next page load. |
| **HELM-K8S-DEPLOY-01 — Helm chart skeleton (`deploy/helm/shepard/`) — new operator surface, NOT production-ready** | 2026-05-29. New deployment surface alongside the existing `infrastructure/docker-compose.yml` flow. Phase 1 ships **skeleton only**: an umbrella Helm chart at `deploy/helm/shepard/` with `Chart.yaml` (`appVersion: 6.0.0-SNAPSHOT`, `kubeVersion: >= 1.28.0-0`), full operator-facing `values.yaml` (image overrides, OIDC split-horizon front/back-channel issuers per the J1e fix, `storage.s3.*` for external S3 override, Ingress with path-mount support per CLAUDE.md "mount plugin UI sidecars as paths", `migrations.mode = disabled\|init-container\|job`, per-plugin toggles, observability ServiceMonitor knob, per-substrate persistence sizing), `_helpers.tpl`, `NOTES.txt`, and stub templates for backend Deployment/Service/ConfigMap, frontend Deployment/Service, Ingress, and a conditional migrations Job. Six sub-chart skeletons under `charts/`: `shepard-neo4j` (StatefulSet stub), `shepard-postgres` (StatefulSet stub with TimescaleDB + pgvector noted), `shepard-mongodb`, `shepard-garage` (Garage S3 — the canonical S3 default per `project_storage_s3_garage`), `shepard-keycloak` (Deployment stub), `shepard-pgbouncer` (Deployment stub). All templates carry `{{ /* TODO Phase 2 */ }}` comments and are minimal-valid YAML — `helm template -f tests/test-values.yaml` renders 13 resources cleanly. Reviewer-test smoke script at `tests/render-dry-run.sh`. Design doc: `aidocs/ops/88-helm-deployment.md`. Production-grade templates ship in HELM-K8S-DEPLOY-03 (Phase 2). | **ZERO** | **No required action.** Helm chart is an additive operator surface; existing docker-compose deployments are unaffected. Compose stays the dev/showcase/single-node default; the chart is the production-cluster sibling. K8s adopters on Phase 1 see "skeleton only — not production-ready" warnings in the README and `NOTES.txt`; real templates land in HELM-K8S-DEPLOY-03. **No new deploy-time config keys** (the chart consumes the same `application.properties` shape as compose). No Neo4j or SQL migrations. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is **not** touched. | None — chart-only. Phase 2 (HELM-K8S-DEPLOY-03) introduces real templates; the MigrationsRunner-as-Job refactor is tracked separately as HELM-K8S-DEPLOY-02 (and ships with its own idempotent + fail-fast invariants per CLAUDE.md). | **No action required for upgrade.** The chart at `deploy/helm/shepard/` is dormant until an operator chooses to install it. To dry-run-test Phase 1: `cd deploy/helm/shepard && helm dependency build && helm template shepard . -f tests/test-values.yaml` → expect ≥ 10 resources (Phase 1 reviewer-test gate). To track Phase 2+: see `aidocs/16-dispatcher-backlog.md §HELM-K8S-DEPLOY`. |
| **DT1-PHASE-0 — `:DigitalTwinScene` + `:CoordinateFrame` + `:Joint` Neo4j entity scaffold (gating row for SCENEGRAPH-REST-1)** | 2026-05-29. Three new `@NodeEntity` classes under `de.dlr.shepard.v2.scenegraph.entities` — each implements `HasAppId` (UUID v7 minted by `GenericDAO#createOrUpdate`'s L2a write seam). `:DigitalTwinScene` carries `name`, `description`, `sourceFileAppId?`, `rootFrameAppId?`. `:CoordinateFrame` carries `name`, `parentFrameAppId?`, scalar transform `(x,y,z,rx,ry,rz)` doubles, and a `kind` enum (`FRAME` / `JOINT` / `TOOL` / `BASE` / `TCP`). `:Joint` carries `name`, `parentFrameAppId`, `childFrameAppId`, axis `(axisX,axisY,axisZ)`, `limitMin`/`limitMax`, `type` enum (`REVOLUTE` / `PRISMATIC` / `FIXED` / `CONTINUOUS`), `homeAngle`. Three `@ApplicationScoped` DAOs (`DigitalTwinSceneDAO`, `CoordinateFrameDAO`, `JointDAO`) extend `GenericDAO` unchanged — no override of `createOrUpdate` because no startup-time caller exists yet (scaffold-only; the CHOKE-03 / fresh-session refit lands with SCENEGRAPH-REST-1 if needed). Cypher migration `V95__DT1_Phase0_DigitalTwinScene_scaffold.cypher` adds three `CREATE CONSTRAINT ... appId IS UNIQUE` constraints — `appId_unique_DigitalTwinScene`, `appId_unique_CoordinateFrame`, `appId_unique_Joint`. The planned `:HAS_FRAME` / `:HAS_PARENT_FRAME` / `:HAS_JOINT` / `:JOINT_PARENT` / `:JOINT_CHILD` graph edges are documented in the migration top-comment as the planned shape; edges are written by SCENEGRAPH-REST-1's service layer. **No REST surface, no IO classes, no MCP tools, no Activity wiring, no frontend UI** ship in this PR — all deferred to SCENEGRAPH-REST-1 per the scaffold scope. 14 DAO unit tests + 1 V95 migration regression test in `TestNeo4jMigrations`. | **ZERO** | **No required action.** Three new node labels with no rows; no new REST paths; no new config keys; the `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is **not** touched. The constraints are dormant until SCENEGRAPH-REST-1 starts persisting scenes. | `V95__DT1_Phase0_DigitalTwinScene_scaffold.cypher` — three idempotent `CREATE CONSTRAINT ... IF NOT EXISTS`. Rollback: `V95_R__DT1_Phase0_rollback.cypher` (DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS + DETACH DELETE all three labels; **irreversible-for-data** but safe given scaffold-only posture — no production data exists). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V95 applies automatically. Verify: `SHOW CONSTRAINTS YIELD name WHERE name IN ['appId_unique_DigitalTwinScene','appId_unique_CoordinateFrame','appId_unique_Joint'] RETURN count(*)` → 3. Operator-facing runbook lands with SCENEGRAPH-REST-1 (this row ships substrate only). |
| **SCENEGRAPH-REST-1 — `/v2/scene-graphs` REST surface + 7 MCP tools + URDF export** | 2026-05-29. Consumes the DT1-PHASE-0 scaffold and exposes nine `/v2/scene-graphs/...` endpoints (GET full scene with optional `Accept: application/ld+json`; GET URDF export; GET USD-export stub returning 503 + `Retry-After: ISAAC-USD-EXPORT-1`; POST create scene; POST add frame; PATCH frame transform/parent/kind; DELETE frame subtree; POST add joint; DELETE joint). Service layer `SceneGraphService` writes the planned graph edges from the DT1-PHASE-0 V95 top comment (`:HAS_FRAME`, `:HAS_PARENT_FRAME`, `:HAS_JOINT`, `:JOINT_PARENT`, `:JOINT_CHILD`) via raw Cypher MERGE/DELETE using fresh OGM sessions per call (CHOKE-03-safe; the DT1-PHASE-0 DAOs stay scaffold-pure). Every mutation records a typed `:Activity` via `ProvenanceService.record()` with PROV1j `sourceMode`/`agentId` from the `X-AI-Agent` header, plus a supplementary `:WAS_DERIVED_FROM` edge linking the new activity to the most-recent prior activity for the same scene's appId — giving the audit trail a graph-walkable chain. Skip-capture handoff per the duplicate-Activity rule. URDF export: `<robot>` + one `<link>` per `:CoordinateFrame` + one `<joint>` per `:Joint` with axis + limits (visual/collision/inertial blocks deferred per `UrdfExporter` Javadoc). Seven MCP tools (`scene_graph_get`, `_create`, `_add_frame`, `_patch_frame`, `_delete_frame`, `_register_joint`, `_delete_joint`, `_export_urdf`) so AI agents can drive scene edits with full PROV1j provenance. Frontend placeholder page at `/scene-graphs/[appId].vue` (using the placeholder kit); real graph browser UI queued as `SCENEGRAPH-REST-1-UI`. Auth: `@Authenticated` only (the scaffold has no owning-collection pointer; `SCENEGRAPH-PERMS-1` row tracks the eventual permission anchor). `UrdfExporterTest` (6 tests) + `SceneGraphServiceTest` (12 tests). | **ZERO** | **No required action.** New additive `/v2/...` paths only; the `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is **not** touched. No new config keys. No Neo4j or SQL migrations (the V95 constraints from DT1-PHASE-0 are dormant until a scene is created — this row activates them by writing the first nodes). Existing clients unaffected; the new surface is opt-in. | None — pure additive REST + MCP. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard. Verify: `curl -fsS https://<your-host>/v2/scene-graphs/dummy -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'` → 404 (endpoint exists, scene `dummy` does not). To smoke-test end-to-end: `curl -fsS -X POST https://<your-host>/v2/scene-graphs -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"name":"smoke"}' \| jq .appId` returns a UUID v7; `curl -fsS https://<your-host>/v2/scene-graphs/<appId>/export.urdf -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'` returns a `<?xml … <robot name="smoke"></robot>` document. |
| **KRL-INTERPRETER-05 — `POST /v2/krl/interpret` + `:KrlInterpretActivity` typed PROV-O Activity** | 2026-05-30. New additive endpoint `POST /v2/krl/interpret` on the `/v2/` development surface. Resolves source `.src` + `.urdf` (+ optional `.dat`) FileReferences by `appId`, calls the `shepard-plugin-krl-interpreter` sidecar (KRL-INTERPRETER-04, operator opt-in via `plugins/krl-interpreter/compose-profile.yml`) at `shepard.krl.sidecar.url` (default `http://krl-interpreter-sidecar:8000`), persists the returned joint-angle trajectory as channels (`joint_0` … `joint_N`) under the operator-picked `timeseriesContainerAppId`, mints a new `TimeseriesReference` against `targetDataObjectAppId`, and records a typed `:KrlInterpretActivity` (an `:Activity` node carrying the supplementary `:KrlInterpretActivity` label + KRL-specific properties: `interpreterVersion`, `ikSolverName/Version`, `ikMeanCycleMs`, `ikP99CycleMs`, `ikMaxResidualMeters`, `ikFailedPoses`, `ikTotalPoses`, `warningCount`, `unsupportedConstructCount`). PROV-O edges: `USED → src/urdf/dat FileReferences + scene`, `GENERATED → TimeseriesReference`, `WAS_ASSOCIATED_WITH → :User`. `X-AI-Agent` header propagates to `sourceMode=ai`/`agentId` on the Activity per the EU AI Act Art. 50 shape. Skip-capture handoff per the duplicate-Activity rule. Three new deploy-time `application.properties` keys (all optional, all with defaults): `shepard.krl.sidecar.url`, `shepard.krl.sidecar.timeout-seconds=120`, `shepard.krl.sidecar.max-body-size-mb=16`. Tier-2 runtime-mutable `:KrlInterpreterConfig` deferred to `KRL-CONFIG-1`. Frontend stub omitted — KRL-INTERPRETER-06 is the natural UI for this backend feature (justification per the prompt's omit-with-justification path). 22 JUnit tests (8 REST + 14 service); JaCoCo 74.9% line over `de.dlr.shepard.v2.krl`. | **ZERO** | **No required action.** New additive `/v2/...` path only; the `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is **not** touched. No Neo4j or SQL migrations (the `:KrlInterpretActivity` label is additive — pre-existing `:Activity` nodes are unaffected; querying for KRL runs uses the new label as the discriminator). New deploy-time keys all optional with sensible defaults. **Endpoint returns 502 until the sidecar (-04) is brought up on the compose profile** — operator opt-in posture, documented in the endpoint's OpenAPI description. | None — pure additive REST + Activity-label overlay. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard. Verify endpoint exists + auth-gated: `curl -fsS -X POST https://<your-host>/v2/krl/interpret -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{}' -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n'` → `401`. To enable end-to-end: deploy `plugins/krl-interpreter/compose-profile.yml` (the KRL-INTERPRETER-04 sidecar), then POST a valid body with `srcFileAppId` + `urdfFileAppId` + `targetDataObjectAppId` + `timeseriesContainerAppId` → 201 + `{trajectoryAppId, activityAppId}`. To audit a run: `MATCH (a:KrlInterpretActivity) RETURN a.appId, a.interpreterVersion, a.ikFailedPoses, a.ikTotalPoses ORDER BY a.startedAtMillis DESC LIMIT 10`. |

New `runtimeConfig.public.shepardVersion` key in `frontend/nuxt.config.ts` (default `"6.0.0-SNAPSHOT"` matching `backend/pom.xml` `<revision>`). Populated at image-build time from `NUXT_PUBLIC_SHEPARD_VERSION` env var. `HelpFrame.vue` displays "Help for shepard v{{ version }}" in the footer below the rendered markdown content (below a `v-divider`). `data-testid="help-version-stamp"` on the footer div. No backend change; frontend-only. | **ZERO** | **No new API endpoint.** **No new config key in `application.properties`.** Only `frontend/nuxt.config.ts` gains a new `public.shepardVersion` key (Nuxt `runtimeConfig.public`).
