The footer is purely informational — no functional change. Operators who want to pin the displayed version: add `NUXT_PUBLIC_SHEPARD_VERSION=<your-version>` to the frontend container's env in `.env` or `docker-compose.override.yml`. |
| **SPATIAL-V6-GATE-CLEANUP — drop the legacy v5 `SHEPARD_SPATIAL_DATA_ENABLED` env; default the v6 spatiotemporal plugin ON** | 2026-05-28. Cleanup follow-on to SPATIAL-V6-001 (2026-05-27). The legacy v5 plugin (`shepard-plugin-spatial`) had its activation gated by the env var `SHEPARD_SPATIAL_DATA_ENABLED`. SPATIAL-V6-001 replaced that plugin with `shepard-plugin-spatiotemporal` and added a new gate `shepard.plugins.spatiotemporal.enabled` — but **both gates were left in place** for one release cycle's worth of compat. This row removes the legacy env entirely: (1) `infrastructure/.env` line removed; (2) `infrastructure/docker-compose.yml` env passthrough removed; (3) `infrastructure/docker-compose.override.yml` redundant `SHEPARD_PLUGINS_SPATIOTEMPORAL_ENABLED: "true"` override removed; (4) `SpatiotemporalPluginManifest.java` sidecar env Map entry removed; (5) `shepard.plugins.spatiotemporal.enabled` default flipped `false → true` in `application.properties` (the v6 flagship payload kind ships on default-on posture, same shape as `kip` / `minter-local` / `git`). Substrate consequence: after the next backend redeploy, the plugin's Flyway runner fires, `CREATE EXTENSION postgis` + `CREATE SCHEMA shepard_spatial` + `V2.0.0__green_field_schema.sql` execute on first start. | **CONFIG** | **Upstream operators who had `SHEPARD_SPATIAL_DATA_ENABLED` set explicitly should delete that line from their `.env`** — it is now ignored by both the backend and the manifest sidecar declaration. If an upstream operator wants to opt out of v6 spatial entirely, the only switch is `shepard.plugins.spatiotemporal.enabled=false` (in `application.properties`, env-overridable via `SHEPARD_PLUGINS_SPATIOTEMPORAL_ENABLED=false`). The `QUARKUS_DATASOURCE_SPATIAL_*` env vars are unchanged. No `/shepard/api/` wire change. `GET /v2/admin/plugins` will show `id: "spatiotemporal", enabled: true` on instances that did not opt out — change from pre-cleanup default which was `enabled: false`. | — | **Action required when upgrading from SPATIAL-V6-001 → SPATIAL-V6-GATE-CLEANUP:** (1) Delete the `SHEPARD_SPATIAL_DATA_ENABLED=...` line from `.env` (no longer consumed by anyone). (2) Optionally delete a manually-pinned `SHEPARD_PLUGINS_SPATIOTEMPORAL_ENABLED=true` from `.env` or your override — now the default. (3) Restart shepard. Verify on first start: backend log shows `SPATIAL-V6-001: spatiotemporal plugin v... active via PluginManifest SPI`; `psql -U postgres -d postgres -c "\dx"` shows `postgis` in the extension list; `\dn` shows the `shepard_spatial` schema. **No action required for operators who never used SPATIAL-V6** beyond the standard restart, unless they want to explicitly opt out (`shepard.plugins.spatiotemporal.enabled=false`). |

Both paths are active during the deprecation window; the shim will be removed once J1e-PR-02 completes the full backend relocation to the plugin module. No semantic change — both paths are byte-identical in request/response shape. No new config keys. No new Neo4j migrations. No `/shepard/api/` surface touched. | **AWARE** | **Two paths serve the same endpoint during the deprecation window.** `GET /v2/admin/jupyter/config` and `PATCH /v2/admin/jupyter/config` continue to work exactly as before, emitting a WARN log per call. `GET /v2/admin/plugins/jupyter/config` and `PATCH /v2/admin/plugins/jupyter/config` are the canonical paths going forward. Operators who have scripts or monitoring integrations calling the old path should update them to the new path. The old path will be removed when the J1e plugin relocation (J1e-PR-02 through J1e-PR-09) is complete. | — (no migration — path-only change in the REST layer; `:JupyterConfig` Neo4j entity and V94 constraint are unchanged). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; both paths are immediately available. | **IOT1 — W3C iot-lite IoT Device Ontology bundle added to the N1c2 preseed manifest** | 2026-05-30. New `iot-lite.ttl` stub (8 397 bytes, SHA-256 `38ea224bda8701533190eb7366861723d4fa107b446a50b609f6dec46c2501c2`) added to `backend/src/main/resources/ontologies/` and registered as the nineteenth entry in `ontologies-manifest.json`. IRI prefix `http://purl.org/iot/ontology/iot-lite#`. The manifest entry carries `fetchUrl: "http://purl.org/iot/ontology/iot-lite"` and `fetchFormat: "Turtle"` so the N1c admin CLI (`shepard-admin semantic refresh-ontologies --bundles=iot-lite`) fetches the full canonical TTL at runtime and replaces the in-bundle stub. The stub includes 8 core classes (`iotlite:Device` as `sosa:Platform` subclass, `iotlite:Service` and `iotlite:Attribute` as `sosa:ObservableProperty` subclasses, `iotlite:TagPoint` as `sosa:Sensor` subclass, `iotlite:Platform`, `iotlite:IoTLiteAPI`, `iotlite:Coverage`, `iotlite:Metadata`) and 4 key properties (`hasAttribute`, `isAssociatedWith`, `hasMetadata`, `hasCoverage`). SOSA/SSN (`ssn-sosa` bundle, N1k 2026-05-27) and QUDT (`qudt` bundle, N1b) were already in the manifest; only iot-lite remained from the IOT1 acceptance criterion. Added per IOT1 (2026-05-30); completes the three-ontology IoT anchor (SOSA/SSN + QUDT + iot-lite). | **ZERO** | **Additive ontology bundle — zero upgrade impact for admins and clients.** The manifest count moves from eighteen to nineteen. On first restart post-IOT1, the seed service imports the iot-lite stub via `n10s.rdf.import.inline` (same idempotent path as N1b/N1k); no new config keys; no Neo4j schema change; no REST endpoint change; no `/shepard/api/` surface touched. The bundle is `required: false` — operators who want to skip it can set `shepard.semantic.internal.preseed-ontologies.skip-bundles=iot-lite`. | — (no migration — content-level idempotent seed; the n10s `n10s_unique_uri` constraint absorbs re-runs). Rollback: set `skip-bundles=iot-lite` to exclude on next restart. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the seed service will log `OntologySeedService: bundle 'iot-lite' imported`. To verify: `MATCH (r:Resource) WHERE r.uri STARTS WITH 'http://purl.org/iot/ontology/iot-lite#' RETURN count(r);` → ≥ 1. To fetch the full canonical TTL: `shepard-admin semantic refresh-ontologies --bundles=iot-lite`. Used by the Shepard IoT-bridge plugin (IOT3, future) for MQTT/OPC-UA channel-to-ontology mapping and the home-showcase MQTT collector topology (`examples/home-showcase/collector.py`). |
| **SCENEGRAPH-CREATE-FROM-URDF-1 — `POST /v2/scene-graphs/from-urdf/{fileReferenceAppId}` one-call URDF mint** | 2026-05-30. New endpoint mints a `:DigitalTwinScene` from a singleton FR1b `:FileReference` carrying URDF XML in one call. Ports `examples/mffd-rdk-urdf-showcase/scenegraph/build_mffd_scene.py`'s `parse_urdf` logic to Java using `javax.xml.parsers.DocumentBuilder` (java stdlib — no new dependency). `ScenegraphFromUrdfService` orchestrates: resolve singleton via `SingletonFileReferenceService.getByAppId` → permission walk via `PermissionsService.isAccessAllowedForDataObjectAppId(parentDoAppId, Write, caller)` → idempotency check for existing `urn:shepard:scenegraph:scene-appId` semantic annotation → stream + parse URDF → mint scene + frames + joints via `SceneGraphService` (per-mutation `:Activity` capture preserved) → write back-annotation on the FileReference → record one umbrella `:Activity` for the parse-and-build. Error shapes: 400 for multi-file `FileBundleReference` appIds (singleton-FR1b contract; the operator-surfaced kr210 case) and for invalid URDF XML / missing `<robot>` root; 403 for missing Write on parent Collection; 404 for unknown appId or orphaned FileReference; 409 with `existingSceneAppId` for the idempotent re-call branch. XML parser configured with OWASP secure defaults (no DTD, no external entities — XXE-safe). 18 JUnit tests covering happy path, idempotency, error branches, parser correctness, XXE refusal; coverage ~91% line / ~75% branch on the new code (above the 70% new-code floor). Frontend wiring (the `OpenInSceneGraphButton.vue` modal's confirm button) deferred as SCENEGRAPH-CREATE-FROM-URDF-2-FE. | **ZERO** | **No required action.** Additive `/v2/...` path only; the `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is **not** touched. No new config keys. No Neo4j or SQL migrations (uses existing `:DigitalTwinScene` + `:CoordinateFrame` + `:Joint` from DT1-PHASE-0 + V95, and existing `:SemanticAnnotation` from SEMA-V6). Existing clients unaffected; the endpoint is opt-in. | None — pure additive REST + service. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard. Verify: `curl -fsS -X POST https://<your-host>/v2/scene-graphs/from-urdf/<urdf-fileref-appId> -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{}' \| jq .appId` returns the new scene's UUID v7; a second call returns HTTP 409 with `{"existingSceneAppId":"..."}` carrying the same appId (idempotency). For the canonical kr210 showcase: `019e789f-f45f-7f59-889b-8bb5a403c180` is the URDF FileReference (see `examples/mffd-rdk-urdf-showcase/`). Cross-ref: SCENEGRAPH-NAV-02 (the FE modal waiting on this), `build_mffd_scene.py` (the python script this replaces — the shell-driven bootstrap stays callable for ops use cases). |
| **SCENEGRAPH-LIST-1 + C3 SPARQL-internal alias — `GET /v2/scene-graphs` paginated list + reserved `internal` repoAppId** | 2026-05-30. Three navigation defects from the 2026-05-30 push, landed as one upgrade-tracker row: **C1 / SCENEGRAPH-LIST-1** adds the missing paginated list endpoint `GET /v2/scene-graphs?page=N&size=M` (response envelope `{ items[], total, page, size }`; order `updatedAt DESC, appId ASC`; per-row frame and joint counts aggregated in one Cypher round-trip; `DigitalTwinScene` entity gains nullable `@Property` fields `createdAt` and `updatedAt` — additive per the schema-changes-are-additive-and-nullable rule, so pre-feature rows return `null` and no migration is needed). No per-scene permission filter — matches the existing scaffold posture in `SceneGraphRest` (the SCENEGRAPH-PERMS-1 backlog tracks the eventual owning-Collection permission walk). **C2 / referenced-containers regression test** (no code change to `CollectionContainersRest` — a live runtime probe confirmed the endpoint returns 401 at the canonical path; the task #241 404 report was traced to a stale deployed image or cached generated client at the reporter's site, not a backend regression. A new `CollectionContainersRestRouteTest` pins the `@Path` value so future renames trip CI before the frontend breaks). **C3 / SPARQL "internal" alias** intercepts the literal `"internal"` string in the `GET /v2/semantic/{repoAppId}/sparql` path-param (case-insensitive `INTERNAL_ALIAS` constant) and resolves it to the bootstrapped `:SemanticRepository {type: INTERNAL}` row from V49 via a new `SemanticRepositoryDAO.findInternal()` method. The frontend playground defaults its repo selector to this alias so users don't have to memorise the V49-minted UUID v7. Explicit UUID v7 paths still resolve via `findByAppId()`; unknown explicit appIds still 404. 14 new JUnit tests across the three defects (7 for C1 in `SceneGraphServiceTest` + `SceneGraphRestListTest`, 1 route-pin for C2 in `CollectionContainersRestRouteTest`, 5 for C3 in `SemanticSparqlRestTest`, 2 DAO tests for `findInternal` in `SemanticRepositoryDAOTest`). | **ZERO** | **No required action.** All three changes are additive on the `/v2/` development surface; the `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is **not** touched. No new config keys. No Neo4j or SQL migrations (the C1 `createdAt`/`updatedAt` properties are added to the OGM entity only; Neo4j is schemaless on properties so existing rows return `null` and `saveScene` writes them on next save). C3 changes no public shape — explicit UUID v7 repoAppIds still work the same; the `"internal"` alias is a new affordance that simply stops 404-ing. | None — pure additive (REST surface + nullable Neo4j properties + new DAO method). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard. Verify C1: `curl -fsS https://<your-host>/v2/scene-graphs -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'` → 200 + `{items: [...], total: N, page: 0, size: 50}`. Verify C3: `curl -fsS 'https://<your-host>/v2/semantic/internal/sparql?query=ASK%20%7B%20%3Fs%20%3Fp%20%3Fo%20%7D' -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'` → 200 + W3C SPARQL Results JSON (no longer 404). The `:DigitalTwinScene` rows minted before this change still load via `GET /v2/scene-graphs/{appId}` — their `createdAt`/`updatedAt` are `null` until the next mutation refreshes `updatedAt`. |
| **SCENEGRAPH-PERMS-1 — per-scene permission gate on `/v2/scene-graphs/*`** | 2026-05-31. Replaces the SCENEGRAPH-REST-1 `@Authenticated`-only auth posture (every authenticated user saw every scene) with a real walk: `scene.sourceFileAppId → FileReference → DataObject → Collection`. The walk reuses the existing `PermissionsService.isAccessAllowedForDataObjectAppId(...)` helper (no new permission primitive). Read endpoints (`GET /v2/scene-graphs/{appId}`, `GET …/export.urdf`) require Read; write endpoints (`POST/PATCH/DELETE …/frames…/joints…`) require Write. List endpoint (`GET /v2/scene-graphs`) post-filters the page to the readable subset — page may return fewer rows than `size`; the envelope `total` field still reports the unfiltered total so an operator looking at the count vs. row-count gap can infer "you can't see N of these". Create gate: when the POST body carries `sourceFileAppId`, the caller must hold Write on its parent Collection; without `sourceFileAppId` (hand-built scene), only the `instance-admin` role can create. Hand-built scenes (no `sourceFileAppId`) fail closed on read + write for non-admins per the C3 default-deny rule. New service: `de.dlr.shepard.v2.scenegraph.services.SceneGraphPermissionService`. 25 JUnit tests (11 service + 5 list + 9 REST gates). | **AWARE** | **Behavioural change for `/v2/scene-graphs/*`.** Pre-feature: every authenticated user saw every scene and could mutate any scene. Post-feature: scenes are gated on their source URDF FileReference's parent Collection's `:Permissions` node — the same node that gates the DataObject the FileReference belongs to. Hand-built scenes (created via `POST /v2/scene-graphs` with no `sourceFileAppId`) become reachable only by the `instance-admin` role; **operators with hand-built demo scenes from before this change must either (a) re-mint them via `POST /v2/scene-graphs/from-urdf/{fileReferenceAppId}` so they get a real Collection anchor, or (b) have an instance-admin operator manage them via that role.** `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is **not** touched. No new config keys. The MCP layer (`SceneGraphMcpTools`) still bypasses this gate — tracked under `SCENEGRAPH-PERMS-1-MCP` follow-up. | None — pure behavioural change in the REST layer; no Neo4j or SQL migration (Collection-permission nodes the walk consults already exist from the C3 backfill). Rollback: revert this commit (no schema state to undo). | **Action required when upgrading: review hand-built `:DigitalTwinScene` rows.** Cypher: `MATCH (s:DigitalTwinScene) WHERE s.sourceFileAppId IS NULL RETURN s.appId, s.name, s.createdAt`. Each row returned will be reachable only by `instance-admin` after restart; re-mint via `POST /v2/scene-graphs/from-urdf/...` to anchor on a real Collection, or accept admin-only access. Restart shepard. Verify gate: `curl -fsS https://<your-host>/v2/scene-graphs/<appId> -H 'Authorization: Bearer <non-admin-token>'` → 200 when caller has Read on the parent Collection of the URDF FileReference; 403 otherwise; 404 when no scene exists. Verify list filtering: `curl -fsS https://<your-host>/v2/scene-graphs -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'` returns `items[]` scoped to readable scenes (compare `items.length` vs. `total` for the unfiltered-vs-scoped count). |
| **SNAPSHOT-LIST-1-REST — cross-Collection paginated snapshot list at `GET /v2/snapshots`** | 2026-05-31. New endpoint on the `/v2/` development surface returning every `:Snapshot` the caller can Read across all Collections (or scoped to one Collection via the optional `collectionAppId` query param). Response envelope `{ items: [{appId, name, createdAt, collectionAppId, collectionName}], total, page, size }`; page is post-filtered to the readable subset, `total` reports the unfiltered total (short-page acceptable; same shape as SCENEGRAPH-PERMS-1). Default `page=0`, `size=50` (clamped into `[1, 200]`). New `SnapshotListRest` resource at `/v2/snapshots`; SnapshotDAO gains `findAll(page, size)` + `countAll()` + `countByCollectionAppId(appId)`. JAX-RS template-specificity routing keeps existing `/v2/snapshots/{snapshotAppId}` resource (`SnapshotRest`) intact — `GET /v2/snapshots` dispatches to the new list resource, `GET /v2/snapshots/<appId>` to the existing one. 9 JUnit tests in `SnapshotListRestTest`. | **ZERO** | **No required action.** Additive `/v2/...` path only; the `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is **not** touched. No new config keys. No Neo4j or SQL migrations. Existing per-Collection list endpoint (`GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/snapshots`, V2b) is preserved byte-identically. Permission gate is identical to the existing snapshot read endpoint — each snapshot's reachability checks Read on its parent Collection. | None — pure additive (REST surface + new DAO methods). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard. Verify: `curl -fsS https://<your-host>/v2/snapshots -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'` → 200 + `{items:[…], total: N, page: 0, size: 50}` (items scoped to the caller's readable subset; total unfiltered). Scope to one Collection: `curl -fsS 'https://<your-host>/v2/snapshots?collectionAppId=<coll-appId>' -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'`. The endpoint is the backend dependency for the `SNAPSHOT-LIST-1-FE` `/snapshots/diff` picker (queued frontend follow-up). |
| **ADM-USR-GIT-BACKEND-1 — admin git-credential read + dedicated-rotate endpoints + `lastRotatedAt` field** | 2026-05-31. Two new admin endpoints under the existing admin path. **`GET /v2/admin/users/{username}/git-credentials`** returns `{ items: [{appId, host, username, displayName, lastRotatedAt}], total, page, pageSize }` (migrated from bare `{ items }` wrapper to `PagedResponseIO` envelope by APISIMP-GIT-CRED-LIST-ENVELOPE, fire-301; additive — clients reading `json.items` are unaffected) so an admin can see which credentials a user already owns; the PAT is type-omitted (the IO record literally has no `pat` field — type-level guarantee, not a hand-rolled filter). **`POST /v2/admin/users/{username}/git-credentials/{appId}/rotate`** accepts `{newPat}`, re-encrypts via the existing AES-256-GCM helper, and stamps `lastRotatedAt = now`. The existing idempotent `POST /v2/admin/users/{username}/git-credentials` continues to work byte-identically; internally its replace-by-host branch now routes through `rotateByUserAndAppId` so it also bumps the timestamp. New nullable `@DateLong` field `lastRotatedAt` on `:GitCredential` matches the existing `createdAt` shape; pre-feature rows return `null` per the additive-schema rule until they are next rotated. 13 JUnit tests in `AdminUserGitCredentialRestTest` cover list (empty / populated / PAT-omission / legacy null / 404 missing user), rotate (happy / 400 missing newPat / 404 missing user / 404 missing appId / 503 missing encryption key), and POST backwards-compat (create stamps; replace routes through rotate; replace with username change updates both). All `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")` like the existing POST. | **ZERO** | **No required action.** Additive `/v2/...` paths only; the `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is **not** touched. No new config keys. No Neo4j migration — `lastRotatedAt` is a nullable Neo4j property (Neo4j is schemaless on properties). The existing POST contract is preserved byte-identically. PAT is never returned on the wire by any endpoint (write-only credential rule). | None — pure additive (REST surface + nullable Neo4j property + new DAO method `rotateByUserAndAppId`). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard. Verify: `curl -fsS https://<your-host>/v2/admin/users/<username>/git-credentials -H 'Authorization: Bearer <admin-token>'` → 200 + `{ items: [...] }` (PAT field absent); `curl -fsS -X POST https://<your-host>/v2/admin/users/<username>/git-credentials/<credAppId>/rotate -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"newPat":"new-PAT-here"}'` → 204. To audit: `MATCH (c:GitCredential) RETURN c.appId, c.host, c.lastRotatedAt ORDER BY c.lastRotatedAt DESC` (legacy rows have `lastRotatedAt = null` until next rotate). |

| **#27-ARCHIVED — ARCHIVED publication state for Collection + Container + write-gate** | 2026-05-31. Extends the existing ARCHIVED status from DataObject (shipped 2026-05-26, COMP-NCR-STATUS / `5565e2beb`) to `Collection` and `Container` kinds. Collection already inherited `status` from `AbstractDataObject` — no code change needed there. `BasicContainer` gains a new `status` String field that propagates to every container subclass (`FileContainer`, `TimeseriesContainer`, `StructuredDataContainer`, `HdfContainer`, plus any future plugin subclass). `BasicContainerIO` exposes it with `@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)` so the legacy `/shepard/api/` wire stays byte-identical to upstream 5.2.0 (the field is absent until set). Two new REST paths on the `/v2/` shelf: `PATCH /v2/collections/{appId}/publication-state` and `PATCH /v2/containers/{appId}/publication-state` (single resource serves every container kind, polymorphic Cypher match on label). Both gated on Manage permission OR `instance-admin` role — stricter than the generic `/v2/collections/{appId}` merge-patch (Write permission) so a contributor cannot accidentally archive. New `ArchiveStateGuard` service raises HTTP 409 with body `"Collection/Container is ARCHIVED — writes to its children are blocked"` when a write is attempted against an archived parent's children — wired into `DataObjectService.createDataObject`/`updateDataObject` and into the shared `AbstractContainerService.assertIsAllowedToEditContainer` (so every container kind, including plugin-provided ones, inherits the gate automatically). Reads are unaffected. Cascading rules out of scope for v1 (children retain their own status when the parent is archived) — see `#27-ARCHIVED-01/02/03` in `aidocs/16-dispatcher-backlog.md` for the open `ARCHIVE-CASCADE-1` follow-up. Frontend: `CollectionArchiveControl.vue` + `ContainerArchiveControl.vue` render an "Archived" chip plus owner-gated Archive/Unarchive buttons with confirmation dialog; wired into `pages/collections/[collectionId]/index.vue`. JUnit (`ArchiveStateGuardTest`, `PublicationStateRestTest`) + Vitest (`archiveControl.test.ts`) cover guard/REST decision logic. | **ZERO** | **No required action.** All endpoints are additive on `/v2/`; `/shepard/api/...` is untouched. Status defaults to `null` on existing containers — the `ArchiveStateGuard` treats null as effectively READY so pre-feature payloads remain writable. The new field appears on the wire only when explicitly set (`@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)`), so v1 clients that have never used the field see no wire change. Operators wanting to archive a collection or container call the new `PATCH /v2/.../publication-state` endpoint with body `{"state":"ARCHIVED"}`. | `V97__NOOP_BasicContainer_status_additive.cypher` — Neo4j is schema-less, so the additive nullable property needs no DDL; the migration file documents the additive change + the inspection / unarchive Cypher commands. Rollback: `V97_R__NOOP_BasicContainer_status_additive.cypher` — strips `status` from every container node (data-only rollback, safe to re-run). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V97 is a NOOP (RETURN 1). To list archived containers post-upgrade: `MATCH (c) WHERE (c:FileContainer OR c:TimeseriesContainer OR c:StructuredDataContainer OR c:HdfContainer) AND c.status = 'ARCHIVED' RETURN labels(c)[0] AS kind, c.appId, c.name LIMIT 50;`. To archive a Collection: `curl -fsS -X PATCH https://<your-host>/v2/collections/<appId>/publication-state -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"state":"ARCHIVED"}'` → 200 with `{"state":"ARCHIVED","archived":true}`. To unarchive: same endpoint with `{"state":"READY"}`. A subsequent attempt to add a DataObject to an archived collection returns HTTP 409 with the body cited above. |
| **ROLE-GRANT-STALE-SESSION-02 — per-token rejection of stale-role JWTs** | 2026-05-31. Closes the operator-visible gap where granting (or revoking) `instance-admin` via `POST /v2/admin/instance-admins` (or direct Cypher) silently failed for users with an active session — the existing JWT still carried the pre-grant role set until manual sign-out + sign-back-in. New nullable `@DateLong` `roleChangedAt` property on `:User`, stamped by `InstanceAdminService.grantInstanceAdmin` / `.revokeInstanceAdmin` on every successful Neo4j-side mutation. `JwtTokenAuthService` reads the stamp on every OIDC Bearer authentication; when the JWT's `iat * 1000 < roleChangedAt`, throws `RoleChangedSinceTokenIssuedException` which `JWTFilter` translates to HTTP 401 + body `{"exception":"role_changed", "message":"Your session was issued before a role change. Sign out and back in to get the new role."}` plus `WWW-Authenticate: Bearer error="role_changed"`. Pre-feature `:User` rows have null `roleChangedAt` → gate is a pass-through (existing sessions keep working). API-key path (`X-API-Key`) bypasses the gate entirely — long-lived service tokens have their own revocation surface. DAO-read failures on the gate-side fail open with a WARN. Bootstrap grant skipped (no JWT exists yet at bootstrap time). Frontend: `UnauthorizedView` gains a `stale-session-reason="role-changed"` prop that upgrades the -03 speculative hint to a definitive "Your role just changed" message; admin pages (`/admin`, `/admin/instance-registry`, `/admin/provenance`) forward the prop via the new `useStaleRoleSession()` composable; `useAuthRefreshMiddleware` peeks the 401 body and flips the shared flag. Six new JUnit tests + two filter-level cases + three InstanceAdminService cases + 10 Vitest cases. | **ZERO** | **No required action.** Additive Neo4j property (schemaless) + additive 401 response shape on the existing `/shepard/api/...` + `/v2/...` surfaces. Pre-feature behaviour preserved for every existing session: rows with no `roleChangedAt` stamp pass through the gate unchanged. The new structured 401 body only fires AFTER an operator grants or revokes a role mid-session via the standard endpoints. Clients that don't parse the new `role_changed` error code see a generic 401 (same status, same shape minus the specific code). The `/shepard/api/...` v1 wire stays byte-identical (the gate sits in `JWTFilter` which serves both surfaces; the response is RFC 7235 conformant). | `V98__Role_changed_at_user.cypher` — NOOP (Neo4j is schemaless on properties), documents the additive change + inspection Cypher. Rollback: `V98_R__Role_changed_at_user.cypher` — strips the property from every `:User` node where set (idempotent). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V98 is a NOOP (RETURN 1). To inspect users whose role set has been mutated since the feature shipped: `MATCH (u:User) WHERE u.roleChangedAt IS NOT NULL RETURN u.username AS sub, u.email, datetime({epochMillis: u.roleChangedAt}) AS lastRoleChange ORDER BY u.roleChangedAt DESC LIMIT 50;`. To verify the gate from a stale token (after an admin grant): `curl -fsS -H "Authorization: Bearer <pre-grant-JWT>" https://<your-host>/v2/admin/features` → expects 401 + body `{"exception":"role_changed", ...}` and `WWW-Authenticate` header containing `error="role_changed"`. After the affected user signs out + back in, the fresh JWT's `iat` exceeds `roleChangedAt` and the request succeeds. To rollback: shutdown backend, run `V98_R` Cypher, restart on prior version — all `:User.roleChangedAt` properties are stripped and the gate is restored to its pre-feature pass-through behaviour. |
| **MFFD-AF-TRACK-MAPPING — V105 `has_successor.transitionKind` index + `POST /v2/admin/mffd/process-chain-mapping` YAML loader + admin UI + runbook** | 2026-06-02. Closes GAP-4 from `aidocs/agent-findings/mffd-feature-gaps-2026-06-02.md`. Ships the cross-process Predecessor edge infrastructure that turns the four disconnected MFFD sub-graphs (tapelaying → bridgewelding → ndt-thermography → cleats) into a coherent chain. **V105** adds a range index on the `has_successor.transitionKind` property (no data; the data lives in YAML uploaded at deploy time per the design). **REST**: `POST /v2/admin/mffd/process-chain-mapping` (`instance-admin`, consumes `text/yaml`/`application/yaml`/`text/plain`) parses the YAML, AND-matches source + target DataObjects via their `urn:shepard:mffd:*` SemanticAnnotation predicates (seeded by V100 templates), and idempotent-MERGEs `has_successor` edges with the entry's `transitionKind` (one of `normal | rework | re-test | concession` — same vocabulary as AAA2's QM1b, so the two PRs merge safely in either order). Response is `{schemaVersion, entries, matched, unmatched, edgesCreated, unresolved[], warnings[]}`. Records a single richer `:Activity` (`EXECUTE` / `MffdProcessChainMapping`) carrying the counters in the summary; uses `PROP_SKIP_CAPTURE` so `ProvenanceCaptureFilter` does not double-capture. **Frontend**: placeholder admin page at `/admin/mffd-process-chain` (file-upload + textarea + counters + unresolved checklist + warnings), linked from `/admin` tile grid. **Author tooling**: `scripts/validate-mffd-process-chain-mapping.py` (offline + live modes, 17 pytest cases), worked example `scripts/mffd-process-chain-mapping.example.yaml` (15 entries covering all 4 `transitionKind` values). **Runbook**: `docs/admin/runbooks/mffd-process-chain-mapping.md`. Design: `aidocs/integrations/118`. 11 service JUnit + 4 REST JUnit + 11 page-contract Vitest tests. | **ZERO** | **No required action.** Additive `/v2/...` endpoint + additive Neo4j relationship-property index; the `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is **not** touched. No new deploy-time config keys. The endpoint is opt-in — operators with no MFFD use case never need to call it. The edge property `has_successor.transitionKind` is OGM-unconstrained, so AAA2's QM1b (which uses the same property for rework-loop disposition) can land in either merge order without conflict. The MERGE is idempotent: re-running the same YAML preserves the edge set; updating an entry's `transitionKind` refreshes the existing edge. The loader **never deletes edges** — operators who need to drop an edge use the standard Predecessor edit UI or a one-off Cypher delete. | `V105__Mffd_process_chain_mapping_edge_index.cypher` — idempotent `CREATE INDEX rel_has_successor_transitionKind IF NOT EXISTS FOR ()-[r:has_successor]-() ON (r.transitionKind)`. Rollback: `V105_R__Mffd_process_chain_mapping_edge_index.cypher` (`DROP INDEX … IF EXISTS`; edges + their `transitionKind` properties survive). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V105 applies automatically. Verify: `SHOW INDEXES YIELD name, type, entityType WHERE name = 'rel_has_successor_transitionKind';` → 1 RANGE/RELATIONSHIP row. To apply a mapping (typical MFFD operator workflow at wave W4 in `aidocs/integrations/113`): `python3 scripts/validate-mffd-process-chain-mapping.py --url https://<host>/v2 --api-key <key> mapping.yaml` (read-only validator first), then `curl -fsS -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/yaml' -H 'X-API-Key: <key>' --data-binary @mapping.yaml https://<host>/v2/admin/mffd/process-chain-mapping`. To inspect the materialised edges: `MATCH ()-[r:has_successor {createdBySource: 'mffd-process-chain-mapping'}]-() RETURN r.transitionKind, count(r) AS n GROUP BY r.transitionKind`. |
| **NTF1-BACKEND — `:NotificationTransport` list entity + admin CRUD + SMTP/Matrix senders + per-transport test dispatch** | 2026-05-31. New `:NotificationTransport` Neo4j entity (list-shaped, `HasAppId`) — N transports addressable by appId, NOT a singleton like `:JupyterConfig`/`:UnhideConfig`. Fields: `appId`, `kind` (`SMTP`/`MATRIX`/`INAPP`), `name`, `enabled`, `lastTestResult/At/Detail`, plus nullable kind-specific fields. `smtpPassword` + `matrixAccessToken` are write-only on the REST surface — the read-side IO (`NotificationTransportReadIO`, a Java record) literally omits them at the type level (compile-time guarantee, with a regression test that serialises a populated entity and asserts the JSON contains neither value nor key-name "password"/"accessToken"). Four new REST paths under `/v2/admin/notifications/transports`: `GET` (list), `POST` (create with kind+name validation → RFC 7807 problem+json), `PATCH /{appId}` (RFC 7396 merge-patch via touched-flags on `NotificationTransportWriteIO`), `DELETE /{appId}` (204/404, no cascade — historical `:Activity` rows referencing the deleted transport's appId survive per the audit-trail-is-a-graph rule). Existing `POST /v2/admin/notifications/test` gains two optional fields: `transportId` (route via that specific transport instead of in-app default; 404 unknown, 503 no-sender, 200 OK, 502 fail/throw) + `recipientAddress` (per-transport routing override). Two `NotificationSender` SPI implementations: `SmtpNotificationSender` (Eclipse Angus `jakarta.mail` 2.0.3, per-send Session, STARTTLS toggle, protected `doSend` test seam) + `MatrixNotificationSender` (JDK `HttpClient`, Matrix client-server API v3 `PUT /_matrix/client/v3/rooms/{roomId}/send/m.room.message/{txnId}` with Bearer auth, package-private `doSend` test seam). All gated `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`. 71 JUnit tests total (entity, DAO, registry, two IO shapes, three REST resources, two senders). New dep: `org.eclipse.angus:jakarta.mail:2.0.3` (Eclipse Angus is the Jakarta Mail RI — EPL-2.0/EDL-1.0 dual-licensed). | **ZERO** | **No required action.** All endpoints are additive on the `/v2/` development surface; the `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is **not** touched. The default state is zero transports configured — pre-NTF1-BACKEND installs see an empty list and the existing in-app `POST /v2/admin/notifications/test` continues to work unchanged. To configure: `POST /v2/admin/notifications/transports` with `{kind, name, ...}` per the openapi schema; secrets travel on POST/PATCH and are never returned by GET. | `V96__Ntf1_NotificationTransport_scaffold.cypher` — idempotent `CREATE CONSTRAINT appId_unique_NotificationTransport IF NOT EXISTS`. Rollback: `V96_R__Ntf1_NotificationTransport_scaffold.cypher` (drops constraint; preserves any rows). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V96 applies automatically. Verify: `MATCH (n:NotificationTransport) RETURN count(n);` (expect 0 on a fresh upgrade; admins create transports explicitly). To add an SMTP transport: `curl -fsS -X POST https://<your-host>/v2/admin/notifications/transports -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"kind":"SMTP","name":"primary","enabled":true,"smtpHost":"smtp.example.org","smtpPort":587,"smtpUsername":"noreply","smtpPassword":"…","smtpFrom":"noreply@example.org","smtpTls":true}'`. To smoke-test: `curl -fsS -X POST https://<your-host>/v2/admin/notifications/test -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"transportId":"<minted-appId>","recipientAddress":"you@example.org","title":"Test","body":"hello"}'` → 200 "delivered via SMTP". The admin pane (`AdminNotificationsPane.vue`) upgrade to consume these endpoints is tracked as `NTF1-UI-TRANSPORT-CRUD-FOLLOWUP` in `aidocs/16`. Operator runbook deferred (tracked as `NTF1-RUNBOOK` — see `aidocs/integrations/40-notification-system.md` for the canonical design). |

| **MFFD-PLUGIN-SVDX-1 — `shepard-plugin-fileformat-svdx` tier-1 (Beckhoff TwinCAT Scope manifest extractor)** | 2026-06-02. Closes GAP-1 in `aidocs/agent-findings/mffd-feature-gaps-2026-06-02.md` partially. New standalone Maven module `plugins/fileformat-svdx/` (NOT yet wired into the aggregator — same `OTVIS-WIRE-AGGREGATOR-1`-class Jandex blocker, see `MFFD-PLUGIN-SVDX-WIRE-1` in `aidocs/16`). Implements a local `FileParserPlugin` SPI shim (verbatim shape of `fileformat-robotics` / `fileformat-thermography`). Decodes the 16-byte SVDX envelope header (uint64 LE pointer to BOM-prefixed trailing XML manifest + uint64 LE format-version stamp; magic markers `0x96 0x0c 0x00` at offsets 9..11 verified across 21 real DLR ZLP MFFD files), then StAX-streams the `<ScopeProject>` XML manifest and emits 15+ `urn:shepard:svdx:*` SemanticAnnotations on the parent FileReference: `formatVersion`, `projectGuid`, `projectName`, `dataPoolGuid`, `mainServer`, `recordTimeNs`, `autoSaveMode`, `assemblyName`, `channelCount`, `acquisitionCount`, deduplicated `amsNetId`/`port`/`dataType` lists, every `channelName` (rendered chart channel), every `symbolName` (`GVL_IO_US_Endeffektor.*` ADS data source), and `companionCsv` (sibling `<basename>.csv` or `<basename>_parsed.csv` FileReference appId, when present — TwinCAT Scope Export Tool output). Proprietary binary sample blocks deliberately not parsed (Beckhoff publishes no spec; community-standard `pytcs` punts on binary). 22 JUnit tests including a real-fixture acceptance check that reads the 7.2 MB smallest campaign file when present at `/mnt/pve/unas/dump/dataset/Punktschweißungen/` (self-skips otherwise — fixture NOT in git). Four-audience docs trio shipped: `plugins/fileformat-svdx/docs/{reference,quickstart,install,byte-layout-notes}.md` + mirrored in `frontend/public/docs/plugins/fileformat-svdx/`. Five concrete follow-up rows filed (`MFFD-PLUGIN-SVDX-{BINARY-PARSER,CSV-INGEST,SEMANTIC,BULK,WIRE}-1`). | **ZERO** | **No required action.** No new REST endpoints. No new config keys. No Neo4j migrations. The plugin is buildable but not invoked until the canonical SPI wire-up (`MFFD-PLUGIN-SVDX-WIRE-1`); the `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is not touched. All output is `urn:shepard:svdx:*` annotations on the FileReference — open-world additive to the `:SemanticAnnotation` substrate. | None — pure metadata emission on existing entities. | **No action required for upgrade.** When the canonical SPI wire-up lands (`MFFD-PLUGIN-SVDX-WIRE-1`), the parser becomes active on `.svdx` uploads automatically. Until then, the jar is buildable but not wired (`cd plugins/fileformat-svdx && ../../backend/mvnw test` passes 22 tests). Verify post-wire-up: upload a Beckhoff TwinCAT Scope `.svdx` to any FileContainer and run `MATCH (a:SemanticAnnotation)-[:ANNOTATES]->(f:FileReference) WHERE a.predicate STARTS WITH 'urn:shepard:svdx:' RETURN a.predicate, count(*) GROUP BY a.predicate ORDER BY a.predicate;` → ≥ 15 distinct predicates including `formatVersion`, `projectGuid`, `channelCount`, `symbolName`. |
| **MFFD-NDT-QUALITY-1 + MFFD-NDT-HEATMAP-2-UI — Thermography NDT quality-score backend + plate-heatmap pane** | 2026-06-02. Closes GAP-7 in `aidocs/agent-findings/mffd-feature-gaps-2026-06-02.md`. New REST surface `POST /v2/thermography/analyze` (request body `{imageBundleAppId, thresholdC?, gridWidth?, gridHeight?}`) and `GET /v2/thermography/{imageBundleAppId}/plate-heatmap`. Streams each TIFF in a `FileBundleReference` through the TwelveMonkeys ImageIO reader, computes per-frame `peakDeltaC = max(pixel) − median(pixel)` + `meanDeltaC` + hot-spot pixel coordinates, accumulates a row-major `float[gridH][gridW]` plate-heatmap (default 64×64, configurable), and writes seven derived `urn:shepard:ndt:*` SemanticAnnotations on the bundle (`peak-delta-c`, `mean-delta-c`, `hotspot-centroid-x`, `hotspot-centroid-y`, `threshold-c`, `frame-count`, `plate-heatmap-json`) plus a DataObject-level `quality-score = 1 − clip(maxPeakDeltaC / thresholdC, 0, 1)` aggregated `min` across multiple thermography bundles. All derived annotations carry `sourceMode="ai"` + `confidence=1.0` per the `ChannelUnitInferenceService` convention (system-generated, deterministic). Idempotent — re-running the analysis wipes existing `urn:shepard:ndt:*` rows on the bundle before re-writing. Permissions: Read on parent DataObject to fetch the cached heatmap; Write to run the analysis (mirrors the AnomalyDetectionRest pattern). Frontend: new `DataObjectThermographyPane.vue` (~270 LoC) mounts conditionally in the DataObject detail expansion-panel grid when a thermography-shaped `FileBundleReference` (name match: `thermo` / `ndt` / `.tif`) is attached; renders a Canvas-backed inferno-colormap heatmap with hover-tooltip + a Vuetify quality chip (green/amber/red bands) + a Re-analyze CTA (Write-gated). Pure helpers in `~/utils/thermographyHeatmap.ts` (11 Vitest cases all green). Backend: 26 JUnit tests (pure metric math + TIFF decoder happy/garbage/empty paths + heatmap codec round-trip). Honest scope: lives in-tree at `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/thermography/` because the existing `shepard-plugin-fileformat-thermography` is currently standalone (Jandex hang gates aggregator wire-up — see `OTVIS-WIRE-AGGREGATOR-1`); the relocation into the plugin is tracked as `MFFD-THERMO-MOVE-TO-PLUGIN-1`. AI-vision tier-2 defect classification deferred to `MFFD-NDT-AI-CLASSIFY-1`. | **ZERO** | **No required action.** Endpoints are additive on `/v2/`; `/shepard/api/...` is untouched. No new required config keys — three optional deploy keys (`shepard.v2.thermography.threshold-c`=80.0, `.grid-width`=64, `.grid-height`=64) ship with sensible defaults; per-call overrides via the `AnalyzeRequestIO` body let an operator tune without a redeploy. New backend dep `com.twelvemonkeys.imageio:imageio-tiff:3.10.1` (Apache-2.0); no transitive CVEs ≥ 7.0 as of 2026-06-02. The frontend pane mounts conditionally — DataObjects without a thermography-named FileBundleReference render no extra panel and pay no cost. | None — pure metric computation + annotation writes via the existing `:SemanticAnnotation` substrate; no Neo4j schema migration. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard. To run analysis on a bundle: `curl -fsS -X POST https://<host>/v2/thermography/analyze -H 'X-API-Key: <key>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"imageBundleAppId":"<bundle-appId>"}'` → 200 + `{framesAnalyzed, maxPeakDeltaC, qualityScore, …}`. To fetch the cached heatmap: `curl -fsS https://<host>/v2/thermography/<bundle-appId>/plate-heatmap -H 'X-API-Key: <key>'` → 200 + `{width, height, cells, minTemp, maxTemp, thresholdTemp, frameCount}`, 404 when not yet analyzed. To verify the derived annotations: `MATCH (a:SemanticAnnotation) WHERE a.propertyIRI STARTS WITH 'urn:shepard:ndt:' RETURN a.propertyIRI, count(*) AS n ORDER BY a.propertyIRI;`. Threshold override: `'{"imageBundleAppId":"…","thresholdC":60.0}'` (cooler thermoplastic processes flip the chip amber earlier). Per-deployment defaults: set `shepard.v2.thermography.threshold-c=60.0` in `application.properties` or a Quarkus profile override. |
| **COLL-SCENE-1 — Collection ↔ DigitalTwinScene hero link at `/v2/collections/{appId}/scene-graph`** | 2026-06-02. Adds a scalar `sceneGraphAppId` property on `:Collection` (additive nullable; pre-feature rows return `null`) plus a dedicated `/v2/collections/{appId}/scene-graph` REST surface (GET resolve linked scene identity, PUT link/replace, DELETE unlink). The link surfaces a hero scene-graph viewer on the Collection landing page (MFFD `MFZ.rdk` URDF, LUMEN bench scene, …) — see GAP-6 in `aidocs/agent-findings/mffd-feature-gaps-2026-06-02.md`. Permission gate is two-sided: GET requires Read on Collection; PUT requires Write on Collection **AND** Read on the target scene (per the scene-side `SceneGraphPermissionService` walk — prevents a writer on Collection A from blind-linking a private scene B they cannot themselves read); DELETE requires Write on Collection. **The Collection→Scene link does NOT widen scene readability** — `SceneGraphPermissionService` (scene → FileReference → DataObject → Collection) stays the source-of-truth for the scene side; this link only surfaces a render affordance on the Collection. Provenance is filter-driven via `ProvenanceCaptureFilter` (no domain-specific Activity enrichment justifies bypassing the filter). `CollectionIO` exposes `sceneGraphAppId` with `@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)` so v1 byte-fidelity is preserved (the field is absent until set). 18 JUnit tests on `CollectionSceneGraphRestTest`. Frontend: `CollectionSceneGraphHeader.vue` band at the top of the Collection landing page renders the URDF via `UrdfCanvas` (full-bleed, ~360px) when a scene is linked; writers see a "Link scene-graph" CTA otherwise; dangling pointer renders `EntityNotFound` for the band. | **ZERO** | **No required action.** All endpoints are additive on `/v2/`; `/shepard/api/...` is untouched. The new field appears on the wire only when explicitly set (`@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)`), so v1 clients that have never used the field see no wire change. Operators wanting to link a hero scene to a Collection call `PUT /v2/collections/{appId}/scene-graph` with body `{"sceneGraphAppId":"<scene-appId>"}`. Pre-feature Collections render no scene-graph band; the field stays null. | `V104__Collection_scene_graph_link.cypher` — NOOP (Neo4j is schemaless on properties), documents the additive change + inspection / bulk-unlink Cypher. Rollback: `V104_R__Collection_scene_graph_link.cypher` (REMOVE the property from every `:Collection` node where set; idempotent). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V104 is a NOOP (RETURN 1). To inspect Collections carrying a hero scene link: `MATCH (c:Collection) WHERE c.sceneGraphAppId IS NOT NULL RETURN c.appId, c.name, c.sceneGraphAppId ORDER BY c.name LIMIT 50;`. To link a scene from the CLI: `curl -fsS -X PUT https://<your-host>/v2/collections/<coll-appId>/scene-graph -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"sceneGraphAppId":"<scene-appId>"}'` → 200 + `{sceneGraphAppId, name, description, frameCount, jointCount, …}`. To unlink: `curl -fsS -X DELETE https://<your-host>/v2/collections/<coll-appId>/scene-graph -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'` → 204 (does NOT delete the `:DigitalTwinScene` itself — that stays addressable via `/v2/scene-graphs/{appId}`). To rollback: shutdown backend, run `V104_R` Cypher, restart on prior version — all `:Collection.sceneGraphAppId` properties are stripped; pre-feature behaviour is fully restored. |

| **MFFD-RENDER-AFP-THERMO-OVERLAY — `shepard-plugin-vis-afp-thermo-overlay` + `AfpThermoOverlayCanvas.vue`** | 2026-06-14. New standalone plugin module `plugins/vis-afp-thermo-overlay/` that registers an `AfpThermoOverlayTransformExecutor` via ServiceLoader (SPI IRI `urn:shepard:vis:transform/AfpThermoOverlayShape`). Consumed via the existing generic `POST /v2/mappings/{templateAppId}/materialize` endpoint (V2CONV-B3 surface, already shipped). The executor reads AFP + NDT DataObject annotations (`urn:shepard:mffd:*`, `urn:shepard:afp:*`, `urn:shepard:thermography:*`), computes tile-match verdict (MATCHED / MISMATCHED / UNVERIFIED based on section+module co-location), and emits a typed VIEW envelope JSON consumed by `AfpThermoOverlayCanvas.vue` (new `frontend/components/mffd/AfpThermoOverlayCanvas.vue`). Frontend slice adds `frontend/utils/afpThermoOverlay.ts` (7 pure helpers) + 23 Vitest test cases. Zero new REST endpoints (the generic materialize path already exists). Zero new Neo4j migrations. Zero new config keys. Plugin is wired in `plugins/vis-afp-thermo-overlay/` and loaded via ServiceLoader on classpath — no aggregator POM change required (standalone module, same pattern as `fileformat-thermography`). | **ZERO** | **No required action.** The plugin is additive — it extends the existing MAPPING_RECIPE path, does not introduce a new REST prefix, does not mutate the v1 surface. No new config keys. No migrations. Zero deploy-time friction for operators who don't use the AFP/NDT overlay use case. | None — pure annotation reads + VIEW envelope emission. | **No action required for upgrade.** If the `vis-afp-thermo-overlay` jar is on the classpath: the executor is discoverable at startup (verify: `GET /v2/mappings/executors` returns `urn:shepard:vis:transform/AfpThermoOverlayShape`). To use: create a MAPPING_RECIPE `ShepardTemplate` with `mappingRecipeShape = "urn:shepard:vis:transform/AfpThermoOverlayShape"`, call `POST /v2/mappings/{templateAppId}/materialize` with `{"inputs":[{"name":"afpDataObjectAppId","appId":"<uuid>"},{"name":"ndtDataObjectAppId","appId":"<uuid>"}]}` → 200 VIEW envelope JSON consumed by `AfpThermoOverlayCanvas.vue`. |

## Reporting an upgrade-path gap

If you find a change on `main` that breaks an upstream upgrade and
**isn't documented here**, that's the bug. File an issue on
`noheton/shepard` with the breaking commit hash and the upstream
behaviour you expected; the fix is either to add a row above or to
revert the change. The standing rule in `CLAUDE.md` makes the
tracker update part of the change — a missing row is a process gap,
not a documentation backlog item.
| **MFFD-SPATIAL — spatial pointcloud + trajectory promotion (W7) + frameAppId handshake** | 2026-06-02. New Python plugin module `plugins/spatial-importer/` (CLI only — no Java code; consumes the existing `shepard-plugin-spatiotemporal` REST surface). Promotes opaque `:FileReference` rows carrying ASCII pointclouds (`TPS 3D pointclouds.*` and `FSD course 3D pointclouds`) into typed `:SpatialDataContainer` rows on the existing `shepard_spatial.profile` hypertable. Idempotent on a SHA256 of the source bytes (stored as `urn:shepard:spatial:source-sha256` annotation). Demotes the original FileReference to `status=ARCHIVED` + `urn:shepard:spatial:promoted-to=<containerAppId>` annotation as a fire-and-forget secondary write. Backend change co-shipped: `SpatialDataContainer` gains a nullable `frameAppId` String property pointing at the CST1 `:CoordinateFrame.appId` (mirrors the PostGIS `coord_frame_app_id` column so a Cypher traversal can reach the frame without a substrate hop); V106 NOOP Cypher migration documents the additive property. Frontend: `SpatialPointsCanvas.vue` Three.js renderer with `pointcloud` (Points scatter) and `trajectory` (Line strip, time-as-colour) modes; voxel-grid downsample cap at 500k points; `DataObjectSpatialContainersPane.vue` lists per-DO spatial refs grouped by kind with "Open in 3D viewer" buttons; spatial container detail page wires the viewer in place of the "in development" placeholder. 4-page plugin docs bundle + 19 pytest cases against real Track 66 / Track 67 fixture bytes + 13 Vitest cases. Closes GAP-5 from `aidocs/agent-findings/mffd-feature-gaps-2026-06-02.md` and the W7 wave of `aidocs/integrations/113-mffd-real-data-import-plan.md`. | **ZERO** | **No required action.** Plugin is Python-only and consumes existing endpoints — no new REST routes added. The `frameAppId` is wire-omitted when null so legacy `/shepard/api/spatialDataContainers` callers see byte-identical responses. No new deploy-time config keys. The Python CLI is opt-in (admin runs it once per slice of W2-ingested Tracks). | `V106__NOOP_SpatialDataContainer_frameAppId_additive.cypher` (intentionally empty — Neo4j-schemaless additive property; just records the change in the migration ledger). Rollback: `V106_R__NOOP_SpatialDataContainer_frameAppId_additive.cypher` (also NOOP; documents the `REMOVE c.frameAppId` operator-cypher one-liner). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V106 runs (no-op). Verify: `MATCH (c:SpatialDataContainer) WHERE c.frameAppId IS NOT NULL RETURN count(c);` → `0` (no rows yet). To run the promotion pass on a Collection of W2-ingested Tracks: see `plugins/spatial-importer/docs/install.md`. The pass is idempotent (SHA256 keyed) — safe to re-run. The 3D viewer is reachable from any DataObject detail page's "Spatial data" expansion panel after the pass populates the containers. |
| **TS-CROSS-DO-VIEW-1 / TPL-VIEW-CROSS-PLY-TCP-1 — `POST /v2/timeseries/cross-data-object-bulk-data` + V102 view-recipe template** | 2026-06-02. New additive endpoint on the `/v2/` development surface: `POST /v2/timeseries/cross-data-object-bulk-data`. Resolves up to 100 DataObject appIds + one channel-predicate IRI to one LTTB-downsampled series per DO, returning `List<CrossDoSeriesIO>` (one entry per resolved DO, empty `points` when no channel matches the predicate). Reuses the existing TS-OPT3 CAgg routing via `TimeseriesService.getDataPointsLttbOptimised`. Per-DO permission gate: callers without Read on a given DO are silently dropped (never 403 the whole request); deterministic pick on multi-match (first channel by `symbolicName` ASC). LTTB target defaults to 500 rows / series (hard cap 5000). Sibling of TS-OPT2 (`POST /v2/timeseries-containers/{id}/channels/data/bulk`) — not an extension — because the grain differs (many DOs × one channel-predicate vs. many channels of one container). The new Cypher resolver `CrossDoChannelResolver` walks `(:DataObject {appId})-[:has_reference]->(:TimeseriesReference)-[:has_payload]->(:Timeseries)` and joins the shared appId to `(:AnnotatableTimeseries)-[:has_annotation]->(:SemanticAnnotation {propertyIRI = $piri})`. Companion Cypher migration `V102__Cross_ply_tcp_temperature_view_recipe_seed.cypher` seeds one `:ShepardTemplate` of kind `VIEW_RECIPE` ("Cross-ply TCP temperature", v1, `source: 'V102-builtin'`); body uses the validator's `view` + `renderer` outer keys with inner `channelPredicate=urn:shepard:afp:tcp-temperature-c`, `axisGrouping=urn:shepard:mffd:ply-number`, `downsampleTo=500`. Frontend: `CollectionCrossTrackViewPane.vue` mounted as a new "Cross-track view" `ExpansionPanelItem` on the Collection detail page; renders a 4-column small-multiples ECharts grid with shared x/y range, hover-sync, and click-through to DO detail. 11 JUnit tests + 13 Vitest tests. | **ZERO** | **No required action.** Additive `/v2/...` path only; the `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is **not** touched. No new config keys. The V102 migration is purely additive (one new `:ShepardTemplate` row, idempotent MERGE on `{name, version}`; user-authored templates with colliding names are untouched). Existing clients unaffected; the endpoint is opt-in. | `V102__Cross_ply_tcp_temperature_view_recipe_seed.cypher` — idempotent MERGE on `{name, version}`. Rollback: `V102_R__Cross_ply_tcp_temperature_view_recipe_seed.cypher` (DETACH DELETE WHERE `source = 'V102-builtin'`). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V102 applies automatically. Verify the migration: `MATCH (t:ShepardTemplate {source: 'V102-builtin'}) RETURN t.name, t.templateKind, t.retired;` → 1 row, `templateKind: 'VIEW_RECIPE'`, `retired: false`. Verify the endpoint exists + auth-gated: `curl -fsS -X POST https://<your-host>/v2/timeseries/cross-data-object-bulk-data -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{}' -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n'` → `401`. To smoke-test end-to-end, pick a Collection with AFP-layup DataObjects whose channels carry `urn:shepard:afp:tcp-temperature-c` annotations: `curl -fsS -X POST https://<your-host>/v2/timeseries/cross-data-object-bulk-data -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"dataObjectAppIds":["<appId-a>","<appId-b>"],"channelPredicate":"urn:shepard:afp:tcp-temperature-c","start":0,"end":3154000000000000,"downsampleTo":500}' \| jq '.[] \| {do: .dataObjectName, channel: .channelSymbolicName, points: (.points \| length)}'`. The frontend pane is reachable via the Collection detail page → "Cross-track view" expansion panel. |
| **COLL-TIMELINE-1 — `GET /v2/collections/{appId}/timeline` (process-chain swimlane chronograph)** | 2026-06-02. New additive endpoint on the `/v2/` development surface: `GET /v2/collections/{appId}/timeline?binSizeDays=1\|7\|30\|90\|365`. Returns a `CollectionTimelineIO` envelope — `binSizeDays` (actual, post-coarsening), `rangeStart`/`rangeEnd` (ISO-8601 UTC), `totalDataObjects`, `lanes[]` where each lane carries `key` (URL-safe slug), `label`, and `bins[]` (`day` ISO date, `count`, `ncrCount`, `rejectCount`). Lanes derived from distinct values of the `urn:shepard:mffd:process-type` SemanticAnnotation (V100 MFFD_PROCESS_TEMPLATES seed); DataObjects without that annotation collect into an `unclassified` lane so non-MFFD Collections still render. Adaptive auto-coarsening: when span / requested bin > 730 bins-per-lane, the ladder (1 → 7 → 30 → 90 → 365) snaps upward; response echoes the actually-used `binSizeDays`. Bin counts and status sums computed in a single Cypher round-trip (`datetime().truncate('day')` on `createdAt`, OPTIONAL MATCH against `:SemanticAnnotation` indexed by `subjectAppId`). Perf target: ≤ 2 s for an MFFD-scale Collection (≈ 8.2k DOs × 5 lanes). Read on Collection. `Cache-Control: max-age=300, must-revalidate` (matches the existing v2 convention). Frontend: lazy-mounted Timeline `ExpansionPanelItem` on `/collections/{appId}` between Cross-track view and Watched containers, renders one ECharts stacked-bar swimlane per lane (green=OK, amber=NCR, red=REJECTED), Day/Week/Month bin-size toggle, hover tooltip with the brief's "Lane, day — N DOs (n NCR, m REJ)" payload, click drill-down to `/collections/{appId}?tab=data-objects&process-type=…&date=…` (drill-down filter follow-up tracked separately). **COLL-TIMELINE-ANNOTATE-1 extension (2026-06-20):** bar click now opens a two-option action menu — "View DataObjects" (existing drill-down) or "Annotate this period" (opens `AddAnnotationDialog` scoped to the Collection, pre-filled with `urn:shepard:quality` predicate). No backend change, no migration. `binAnnotateMenuTitle()` helper added to `frontend/utils/collectionTimeline.ts`. 24 JUnit tests + 22 Vitest tests (18 original + 4 `binAnnotateMenuTitle`). See GAP-8 in `aidocs/agent-findings/mffd-feature-gaps-2026-06-02.md`. | **ZERO** | **No required action.** Additive `/v2/...` path only; the `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is **not** touched. No new config keys. No schema change (no migration). The endpoint is opt-in. Existing clients unaffected. Note: empty / non-MFFD Collections without `urn:shepard:mffd:process-type` annotations render a single `unclassified` lane with day-binned counts only — informative, not blank. | **None.** No migration ships with COLL-TIMELINE-1; the endpoint reads existing `:Collection`, `:DataObject`, and `:SemanticAnnotation` properties. The V100 seed (already present from MFFD_PROCESS_TEMPLATES) is the dependency for non-`unclassified` lanes; operators who haven't applied V100 still get the `unclassified` fall-through. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the new endpoint is reachable immediately. Verify auth-gated: `curl -fsS https://<your-host>/v2/collections/<coll-appId>/timeline -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n'` → `401` without a token; `200` with a Bearer token + Read permission. Smoke-test for an MFFD-shaped Collection: `curl -fsS https://<your-host>/v2/collections/<coll-appId>/timeline?binSizeDays=7 -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' \| jq '{binSizeDays, totalDataObjects, laneCount: (.lanes \| length), firstLane: (.lanes[0].key)}'`. Operators wanting to pre-populate non-MFFD lanes for a Collection seed `urn:shepard:mffd:process-type` annotations on the DataObjects (via the AnnotationDialog, the `/v2/semantic-annotations` REST, or `shepard-admin annotations add`). |
| **PROJ-1 — Project entity + sub-Collection registry + `/v2/projects/{appId}` namespace** | 2026-06-02. Adds the Project-as-registry pattern (per `aidocs/integrations/121-project-and-subcollections.md`). Three new semantic predicates (`urn:shepard:project`, `urn:shepard:partOf`, `urn:shepard:programme`) seeded into the controlled-vocab registry by `V108__add_project_predicates.cypher`. New dedicated REST namespace at `/v2/projects/{appId}` with four reads: `GET /v2/projects` (list every Project appId), `GET /v2/projects/{appId}` (envelope with programmes + aggregate counts), `GET /v2/projects/{appId}/sub-collections` (children + programme strip + `alsoMemberOf` chips), and `GET /v2/projects/{appId}/by-annotation/{predicate}/{value}` (cross-Collection roll-up). Any request whose path appId is not a Project returns uniform 404. New runtime SHACL gate `ProjectAnnotationConstraints` plugs into `SemanticAnnotationV2Rest.create()` — only the three Project predicates trigger work (O(1) for any other annotation); violations return 422 RFC 7807 with the structured constraint message (project-value-not-literal-true, partOf-target-not-a-Project, partOf-self-reference, programme-on-non-Project). Four MCP tools (`list_projects`, `get_project`, `get_project_sub_collections`, `query_project_by_annotation`) expose the same surface for agent clients. Frontend: top-nav `/projects` route (between Collections and Containers, both desktop + mobile drawer), `CollectionSubCollectionsPanel.vue` mounted unconditionally on every Collection-detail page (hides itself when the Collection isn't a Project), `CollectionList.vue` row-chip badge driven by a shared `useProjectMembership()` set lookup so per-row resolution is O(1). 32 backend JUnit tests + 7 frontend Vitest tests. One-shot `examples/mffd-showcase/scripts/seed-mffd-collections.py` idempotent bootstrap creates the `MFFD Upper Shell — Project` umbrella + the 5 step Collections (`mffd-afp-tapelaying`, `mffd-bridge-welding`, `mffd-spot-welding`, `mffd-ndt-thermography`, `mffd-cell`) + the 4 MFFD UserGroups + the `Clean Aviation JU` and `DLR Project Line 4` programme labels. | **ZERO** | **No required action.** All endpoints additive on `/v2/`; `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface untouched. No deploy-time config keys added; the three new predicates appear in the admin vocabulary picker after restart. The SHACL gate is bypassable from the v1 path (`/shepard/api/...` annotations are not gated — pre-feature behaviour), so legacy clients that wrote bare `urn:shepard:project` keys before the gate landed are not retroactively broken. The CollectionSubCollectionsPanel is invisible on every Collection that isn't a Project — there is no UX surface change for non-Project Collections. | `V108__add_project_predicates.cypher` — idempotent MERGE on the three `:Predicate` registry nodes. Rollback: `V108_R__add_project_predicates.cypher` (`DETACH DELETE WHERE source = 'V108-project-predicates'`; existing `:SemanticAnnotation` rows that carry these IRIs as their `propertyIRI` are *not* touched — the data they encode is independent of the registry node). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V108 runs (idempotent MERGE). Verify: `MATCH (p:Predicate {source: 'V107-project-predicates'}) RETURN p.iri, p.label ORDER BY p.iri;` → 3 rows. Verify the endpoint exists + auth-gated: `curl -fsS https://<your-host>/v2/projects -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n'` → `401` without a token; `200` (empty array `[]`) with a Bearer token on a fresh instance. Promote a Collection to a Project: see `docs/admin/runbooks/projects.md` for the curl recipes. To seed the MFFD Project + 5 step Collections in one shot: `SHEPARD_HOST=https://<your-host> SHEPARD_API_KEY=<key> python3 examples/mffd-showcase/scripts/seed-mffd-collections.py --dry-run` (verify the plan), then re-run without `--dry-run`. The script is idempotent — re-runs SKIP already-present Collections + annotations + UserGroups. To rollback: shutdown backend, run `V108_R` Cypher, restart on prior version — `:Predicate` registry nodes drop; annotation rows persist; the `/v2/projects/` endpoint returns 404 for everything (no Cypher hit on missing predicates). |
| **MFFD-SPATIAL-LINESCAN-IMPORTER-1 — TPS raw-data line-scan extension (W7b)** | 2026-06-02. Extends the `plugins/spatial-importer/` Python CLI with a new `--linescan-pass` step. Decodes the `Track_NN__Run_NN_/files/TPS raw data.N` 1292×964 8-bit grayscale PNG chunks (37 chunks per Track observed) into the existing `SpatialDataContainer` substrate: one container per chunk + one `SpatialDataPoint` per PNG row carrying the full intensity vector in `measurements.intensities` (annotated `urn:shepard:spatial:kind=brush-trace`). Row-as-time when no source-side timestamp is available (container annotated `urn:shepard:spatial:t-axis=row-index`). Same SHA256-keyed idempotency + FileReference demotion shape as the AAC1 pointcloud pass. Frontend: `expandBrushTraceRows()` helper in `utils/brushTrace.ts` (pure TS, vitest-friendly) + `brush-trace` render mode in `SpatialPointsCanvas.vue` re-using the voxel-grid decimation pass (964 × 1292 ≈ 1.25 M points per chunk capped at 500k). New 64×128 byte-stable PNG test fixture (`plugins/spatial-importer/tests/fixtures/tps_raw_data_chunk18_64x128.png`, sha256 `874039f6…`) cropped from real `Track_66__Run_23133_/files/TPS raw data.18`. 28 pytest + 6 backend JUnit (`BrushTraceIOTest`) + 6 Vitest cases. Closes W7b in `aidocs/integrations/113`; cross-links `MFFD-SPATIAL-RAW-DATA-INVESTIGATE` (resolved) + `MFFD-SPATIAL-BRUSH-TRACE-FROM-RAW` (merged). | **ZERO** | **No required action.** Python-only plugin extension; no new backend REST routes, no new config keys, no schema migration. The line-scan pass MERGES on the existing `(DO, kind, source-sha256)` tuple so re-runs are safe. The 16-bit / RGB rejection is fail-loud — operator gets a typed `LineScanDecodeError` if the source format ever drifts (tracked as `MFFD-SPATIAL-LINESCAN-FORMAT-DRIFT-1`). | **None.** No migration ships with W7b. The existing v5 `spatial_data_points` table accepts the JSONB `measurements.intensities` field with no schema change. Promotion of brush-trace to a first-class `profile_kind` on the SPATIAL-V6 hypertable is deferred — tracked as `MFFD-SPATIAL-BRUSHTRACE-SCHEMA-1`. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; no new state. To run the line-scan pass against a Collection of W2-ingested Tracks: `SHEPARD_API_KEY=… SHEPARD_URL=https://<your-host> python3 -m cli.main --linescan-pass --collection-app-id <UUID> --source /path/to/mffd-export/ts-export/tapelaying/ --workers 4`. Use `--dry-run` first to enumerate chunks per Track. Companion follow-ups: per-pixel calibration loader (`MFFD-SPATIAL-LINESCAN-CALIB-1`); multi-resolution streaming pyramid (`MFFD-SPATIAL-LINESCAN-PYRAMID-1`); AI defect detection (`MFFD-SPATIAL-LINESCAN-AI-1`). |
| **TEMPLATE-ICONS-1 — ShepardTemplate gains nullable `iconKey` (MDI name) + V107 backfill** | 2026-06-02. New nullable `iconKey: String?` property on `:ShepardTemplate`. `ShepardTemplateIO` / `CreateShepardTemplateIO` / `PatchShepardTemplateIO` each gain the field (`@Schema required=false, nullable=true`). The COW path (`ShepardTemplateDAO.nextVersionOf()`) carries `iconKey` through unchanged so an admin editing a description never silently strips the icon. `ShepardTemplateRest.create` + `.patch` write the field through; the PATCH treats the empty string as a clear request (sets to null, UI then falls back to per-kind default). The frontend `useTemplateIcon` composable resolves to `template.iconKey` when set, else a per-kind default (`mdi-folder-multiple` for Collection, `mdi-circle-medium` for DataObject, …). Wired into the MyTemplatesPane catalogue, the AdminTemplatesPane table, the AdminTemplateDialog (admin-editable Icon field + live preview), the TemplateAutocomplete dropdown, and the TemplatePickerDialog cards. Backend-client TS shims updated. Subsumes the long-pending task #22 (admin-configurable type icons). Design: `aidocs/integrations/122`. 31 backend JUnit tests + 12 Vitest tests on the composable + 2 new Vitest tests on `formatOption` (iconKey passthrough). | **ZERO** | **No required action.** Schema change is additive nullable — pre-feature `:ShepardTemplate` rows simply have no `iconKey` property (Neo4j is schemaless). The new wire field is null on pre-feature rows; v1 clients (which never read it) see no change. No new deploy-time config keys. No new endpoints. The existing `POST /v2/templates` and `PATCH /v2/templates/{appId}` carry the new field naturally — pre-feature operators ignore it; new operators can flip a template's icon at runtime via `PATCH /v2/templates/{appId}` with `{"iconKey":"mdi-layers"}`. | `V107__Template_icon_keys.cypher` — `MATCH (t:ShepardTemplate {name: $name}) SET t.iconKey = coalesce(t.iconKey, $newIcon)` for the 8 V100 MFFD process templates + 8 other shipped templates (V93 EquipmentItem, V101 demo templates, V102 cross-ply view recipe, V103 disposition record) + 10 future CamelCase names anticipated by `aidocs/integrations/122 §4` (no-op until those seeds land). Idempotent (`coalesce` skips already-set icons), admin-override-safe (admin-set icons via PATCH are never overwritten by re-running V107). Rollback `V107_R__Template_icon_keys.cypher` — `MATCH (t:ShepardTemplate) WHERE t.name IN [...] REMOVE t.iconKey` for the same 26-name list. **Note:** the spec doc proposed V104; that number was already taken by `COLL-SCENE-1` (Collection ↔ DigitalTwinScene link), so the migration ships as V107 (next free). | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V107 applies automatically and idempotently. Verify: `MATCH (t:ShepardTemplate) WHERE t.iconKey IS NOT NULL RETURN t.name, t.iconKey ORDER BY t.name;` — expect 16 rows for the actually-shipped templates (V93/V100/V101/V102/V103); future MFFD CamelCase templates pick up their icons when their seeds land. To override an icon at runtime: `curl -fsS -X PATCH https://<your-host>/v2/templates/<appId> -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"iconKey":"mdi-precision-manufacturing"}'`. To clear an icon (UI then falls back to per-kind default): PATCH with `{"iconKey":""}`. To rollback: shut down backend, run `V107_R` Cypher, restart on prior version — icon properties are stripped from the shipped 26-name set; admin-set icons on other templates remain. |
| **PROJ-SEMA-DUAL-OWNERSHIP-1 — partOf write requires Write on BOTH child + parent** | 2026-06-03. Tightens the PROJ-SEMA-WRITE-GATE-1 from `PROJ-1` to enforce the §2 "Owner-write only" rule from `aidocs/integrations/121`. Mutating a `urn:shepard:partOf` annotation now requires the `instance-admin` role OR Write permission on BOTH the child Collection (subject — already gated) AND the parent Project (target — new). Implemented as a new `checkParentWritePermission()` method on `ProjectAnnotationConstraints`, invoked from `SemanticAnnotationV2Rest.create()` AND `.update()` (PATCH can mutate `objectLiteral`, which flips the parent Project, so the gate runs there too). `urn:shepard:project` and `urn:shepard:programme` writes are no-op for this new check — those predicates only act on the subject (the Collection itself IS the Project) and have no separate parent target. Returns 403 with a structured deny message naming the parent appId on miss. 9 new unit tests on top of the existing 14 in `ProjectAnnotationConstraintsTest`. | **ZERO** | **No required action.** No new endpoints, no schema change, no config keys. The new gate runs only on `urn:shepard:partOf` writes — every other annotation predicate is unaffected. The MFFD seed script (`examples/mffd-showcase/scripts/seed-mffd-collections.py`) runs as instance-admin so it bypasses the new check; existing partOf annotations created by instance-admins before this row continue to exist. Standard operators using the `docs/admin/runbooks/projects.md` curl recipes who lack Write on the parent Project will now get a 403 instead of a successful write — this is the intended behaviour per `121 §2`. v1 (`/shepard/api/...`) annotation writes remain ungated (pre-feature behaviour). | **None.** No migration ships with this row. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart the backend; the gate is live immediately. Verify: as a non-admin user with Write on collection `<child>` but NOT on `<parent>`, `curl -fsS -X POST https://<your-host>/v2/annotations -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"subjectAppId":"<child>","subjectKind":"Collection","predicateIri":"urn:shepard:partOf","objectLiteral":"<parent>"}' -w '\n%{http_code}\n'` → `403` with the body's `detail` field naming the parent appId. As instance-admin, the same call returns `201` regardless of explicit Write grants. To audit existing partOf annotations whose author lacks Write on the parent (would now be rejected on re-create): `MATCH (a:SemanticAnnotation {propertyIRI: "urn:shepard:partOf"}) RETURN a.subjectAppId, a.valueName, a.agentUsername ORDER BY a.agentUsername;` then cross-check against `:Permissions` per (Collection, username, Write). |
| **MFFD-LAYER-RECIPE — `MFFDLayerOverview` VIEW_RECIPE template seed** | 2026-06-03. Seeds one `:ShepardTemplate` row of `templateKind = 'VIEW_RECIPE'`, name `MFFDLayerOverview`, version 1, source `V109-builtin`, iconKey `mdi-view-dashboard-variant`. The recipe body declares 5 panels — `run-summary` (run count + re-run badge breakdown per AAA2), `temperature` (cross-track TS over `urn:shepard:afp:tcp-temperature-c` per AAA3), `tps-line-scan` (stitched layer-surface PNGs in cell coords), `spatial-overlay` (3D viewer combining TPS + FSD pointclouds + NDT frames per AAC1 + Trace3D #142), `ndt-frames` (OTvis gallery per AAC2) — each pointing at the generic `GET /v2/projects/{appId}/by-annotation/urn:shepard:mffd:layer/{N}` endpoint (PROJ-REST-2, shipped as part of PROJ-1) as its data source. MFFD content artefact only — no new endpoint, no new code, no MFFD-specific UI. Other Projects (PLUTO mission-phase, LUMEN test-bench, …) get the same affordance by shipping their own VIEW_RECIPE with their own join key. The recipe renders via the already-shipped `POST /v2/shapes/render` route (TPL2, shipped 2026-05-26). | **ZERO** | **No required action.** Pure content addition; no schema change, no new endpoint, no new config key. Existing `:ShepardTemplate` consumers (`GET /v2/templates`, the admin template list, the TemplatePickerDialog) pick it up automatically after V109 runs. Pre-feature operators ignore it; the recipe is opt-in (referenced explicitly when calling `POST /v2/shapes/render`). | `V109__Mffd_layer_overview_view_recipe.cypher` — idempotent MERGE on `{name, version}` (re-runs are no-ops); user-authored templates with a colliding name are intentionally untouched (they lack the `source` marker). Rollback: `V109_R__Mffd_layer_overview_view_recipe.cypher` deletes only rows with `source = 'V109-builtin'`. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V109 applies automatically. Verify: `MATCH (t:ShepardTemplate {source: 'V109-builtin'}) RETURN t.name, t.templateKind, t.retired, t.iconKey;` → 1 row, `templateKind: 'VIEW_RECIPE'`, `retired: false`, `iconKey: 'mdi-view-dashboard-variant'`. Verify reachability via REST: `curl -fsS 'https://<your-host>/v2/templates?templateKind=VIEW_RECIPE' -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' \| jq '.[] \| select(.name == "MFFDLayerOverview") \| {name, version, templateKind, iconKey, source}'` → 1 result. To exercise the recipe end-to-end against a Layer DO (only meaningful once W2 ingest has populated `mffd-afp-tapelaying` Layer DOs): `curl -fsS -X POST https://<your-host>/v2/shapes/render -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"templateAppId":"<MFFDLayerOverview-appId>","subjectAppId":"<layer-DO-appId>"}'`. To rollback: shutdown backend, run `V109_R` Cypher, restart on prior version — the template row drops; existing `:ShepardTemplate` consumers stop returning it. Other VIEW_RECIPE templates (V102 Cross-ply TCP temperature) are untouched. |
| **SINGLETON-FILE-MIGRATION-WAVE — audit + UI default + SHACL guard + PROV wrapper for V23 (FR1b)** | 2026-06-03. Five sub-rows shipped under aidocs/16 §SINGLETON-FILE-MIGRATION. Closes the technical-debt loop on single-file `:FileBundleReference` rows surfaced 2026-05-30 (the kr210-r2700-urdf showcase — URDF + 7 meshes + .src uploaded as 9 single-file FileBundleReferences couldn't be resolved by `POST /v2/krl/interpret`). (1) **SINGLETON-FILE-01-AUDIT** — `scripts/audit-single-file-bundles.py` scans both legacy `fileOids[]` shape and post-V21 group/payload shape, writes a per-collection + per-extension matrix to `aidocs/agent-findings/`. (2) **SINGLETON-FILE-02-SEED-MIGRATION** — `examples/mffd-rdk-urdf-showcase/seed.py` now uses `upload_singleton_file_reference()` (POST /v2/files) for every one-file shape; idempotent via the existing `GET /v2/files/by-data-object/{appId}` list check. (3) **SINGLETON-FILE-03-BACKFILL-SCRIPT** — `scripts/backfill-single-file-bundles-to-singletons.py` reconciles with the in-tree V23 migration (V23 is the canonical transform; it preserves appId per the "appId in, content out" contract). Script audits, prompts for V23 toggle + restart, then emits a PROV-O `:Activity {kind: 'SINGLETON_FILE_MIGRATION'}` per converted row (queryable in the activity feed). (4) **SINGLETON-FILE-04-UI-DEFAULT** — `DataObjectFileUploadDialog.vue` defaults to **singleton-per-file**; "Bundle as one Reference" is an explicit toggle. Singleton path uses POST /v2/files; per-file name auto-derived. 15 new Vitest cases. (5) **SINGLETON-FILE-05-SHACL-GUARD** — `backend/src/main/resources/shapes/single-file-bundle-warn.shape.ttl` (sh:Warning severity) auto-loaded by the existing ShapeRegistry. | **ZERO** | **No required action.** All five sub-rows are additive on top of existing FR1b infrastructure (the V23 migration + the `/v2/files` shelf, both shipped 2025-10 / 2026-02 per the FR1b row at line 169). No new REST routes, no new config keys (V23's existing `shepard.migration.split-singletons.enabled` toggle is the only operator knob). The UI dialog change is opt-out — operators who prefer the legacy bundle-by-default flow flip the mode toggle once per upload. The new SHACL shape contributes warnings only when a single-file bundle exists; clean instances see no warning. | **None.** The data transformation lives in the already-shipped V23 migration; this wave adds tooling around it. The two new Python scripts and the new TTL file ship via the normal release packaging. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard (or pull the new frontend + scripts). To run the audit: `uv run scripts/audit-single-file-bundles.py` → writes `aidocs/agent-findings/singleton-file-audit-<date>.md`. To execute the backfill: see `docs/admin/runbooks/single-file-singletons.md` (snapshot → flip V23 toggle → restart → run `scripts/backfill-single-file-bundles-to-singletons.py --commit` for PROV-O activities). To verify the SHACL warning: `POST /v2/shapes/validate {"shapeId":"shepard:SingleFileBundleWarnShape","subjectAppId":"<collection-appId>"}` returns 0 warnings on a fully migrated instance. |
| **V2CONV-B2 — SHACL shape validation at template instantiation (422 on violation)** | 2026-06-03. Closes the dormant seam identified in `aidocs/platform/191 §3`. `TemplateInstantiationRest` now runs SHACL validation between `inheritanceResolver.flattenBody()` and `dataObjectService.createDataObject()`: (1) extract the top-level `shapeGraph` Turtle string from the flattened body (field set by `TemplateInheritanceResolver.deepMerge()`, which concatenates parent + child Turtle in `sh:and` order); (2) build a minimal data Turtle with the candidate DataObject's attributes projected as `<urn:shepard:instance:candidate> <urn:shepard:attribute:{key}> "{value}"` triples; (3) pass both to the existing `JenaShaclValidator`; (4) return HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity with the violation list if `!report.conforms()`. Fail-soft policy: a malformed `shapeGraph` or a Jena engine error is logged at WARN and skipped — a broken shape does not block DataObject creation. No `shapeGraph` field → validation is bypassed entirely (pre-feature and no-shape templates are unaffected). New helpers `extractShapeGraph(String)` and `buildDataTurtle(Map<String,String>)` are package-private for test access.
