| **APISIMP-EMPTY-BODIES-BATCH-5 — 401/403/404/409 empty-body responses in 5 v2 snapshot resources now carry RFC 7807 `ProblemJson` bodies** | 2026-06-13. `aidocs/16` APISIMP-EMPTY-BODIES-BATCH-5. `SnapshotRest` (3× UNAUTHORIZED + 3× NOT_FOUND in method bodies + 2× NOT_FOUND + 1× FORBIDDEN in `checkCollectionAccess()` → `problem()`), `SnapshotDiffRest` (1× UNAUTHORIZED + 2× NOT_FOUND → `problem()`), `CollectionSnapshotRest` (1× NOT_FOUND in `create()` + 3× in `checkAccess()` → `problem()`), `SnapshotListRest` (1× UNAUTHORIZED + 1× NOT_FOUND → `problem()`), `SnapshotPinnedReadRest` (1× UNAUTHORIZED + 1× NOT_FOUND + 1× CONFLICT + 3× in `checkAccess()` → `problem()`). | **AWARE — PRE-PROD WIRE BREAK** on 401/403/404/409 error bodies for `GET|DELETE /v2/snapshots/{appId}`, `GET /v2/snapshots/{appId}/manifest`, `GET /v2/snapshots/{a}/diff/{b}`, `GET|POST /v2/collections/{cId}/snapshots`, `GET /v2/snapshots`, `GET /v2/collections/{cId}/snapshots/{sId}/data-objects`. Old empty body → new `ProblemJson{"type":"/problems/snapshots.*","title":"...","status":<code>,"detail":"..."}` + `Content-Type: application/problem+json`. Success responses unchanged. | — (no schema migration). | **No operator action required.** Pre-production surface. |
| **APISIMP-PAGINATION-UNIFY-RECREATE — 8 `/v2/` list endpoints: query param `?size`/`?page-size` renamed to `?pageSize`** | 2026-06-13. `aidocs/16` APISIMP-PAGINATION-UNIFY-RECREATE. Completes the APISIMP-PAGINATION-UNIFY goal. Renames `@QueryParam("size")` → `@QueryParam("pageSize")` on `CollectionV2Rest`, `DataObjectV2Rest`, `SnapshotListRest`, `CollectionSnapshotRest`, `TimeseriesContainerChannelsRest`, `InstanceAdminRest`, `FileBundleReferenceRest`, and `UnhideFeedRest` (`page-size` → `pageSize`). Backend-client TS interfaces updated; FE composables `useFetchPermissionAuditLog`, `useFetchDataObjectMap`, `useFetchAllDataObjects`, `usePagedDataObjects`, `useSnapshotList`, `useTreeviewItems`, `diff.vue`, and the timeseries reference page updated. | **AWARE — PRE-PROD WIRE BREAK** on 8 `/v2/` list endpoints: query param renamed from `?size=N` / `?page-size=N` to `?pageSize=N`. Clients using the old param names receive the default page size instead. Switch to `?pageSize=N`. | — (no schema migration; Java + TS source changes only). | **No operator action required.** Pre-production surface. Update any scripts calling these endpoints with `?size=` or `?page-size=` to use `?pageSize=`. |
| **APISIMP-AAS-PAGINATION-SIZE — `GET /v2/aas/shells` query param `?size` renamed to `?pageSize`** | 2026-06-13. `aidocs/16` APISIMP-AAS-PAGINATION-SIZE. `AasShellsRest.listShells()` used `@QueryParam("size")` for list pagination — inconsistent with the `pageSize` convention standardised by APISIMP-PAGINATION-UNIFY. Renamed to `@QueryParam("pageSize")`; internal variable also renamed. No response body change; no change to the IDTA AAS v3 Shell shape. | **AWARE — PRE-PROD WIRE BREAK** on `GET /v2/aas/shells`: query param renamed from `?size=N` to `?pageSize=N`. Clients using `?size=N` will now receive a paginated list using the default page size (100) instead. Switch to `?pageSize=N`. | — (no schema migration; Java source change only). | **No operator action required.** Pre-production surface. Update AAS client scripts using `?size=` to `?pageSize=`. |
| **V2UI-CHANNEL-ANNO-CLIENT — `SemanticAnnotationIO` gains `appId`; `AnnotatedChannel` migrated to typed `TimeseriesChannelAnnotationsApi`** | 2026-06-13 (fire-21). `aidocs/16` V2UI-CHANNEL-ANNO-CLIENT. Two changes: (1) `SemanticAnnotationIO.java` gains an optional `@Schema(readOnly = true) String appId` field populated from `ref.getAppId()` — additive, upstream-byte-compatible (unknown fields are ignored by upstream 5.2 clients). (2) `AnnotatedChannel` in `frontend/composables/annotated.ts` is rewritten from raw `fetch` calls to the typed `TimeseriesChannelAnnotationsApi` client (via `useV2ShepardApi`) with an `_appIdMap` that caches `numeric id → UUID appId` for the typed delete path. The `v2BaseUrl()` + `authHeaders()` helpers are removed (were specific to this one class). The pre-existing raw `fetch` delete sent `DELETE .../annotations/{numericId}` — the backend expects a UUID `annotationAppId`. This latent delete bug is fixed by the `_appIdMap` pattern. | **ADDITIVE** | `SemanticAnnotationIO` gains one new nullable `appId: String` field on its JSON responses. Upstream clients (built against 5.2 `openapi-5.4.0.json`) that do not declare the `SemanticAnnotation` schema to be `additionalProperties: false` will silently ignore the new field — no breakage. The `/shepard/api/` v1 annotation surface is unchanged. | — (no schema migration; purely additive field). | **No operator action required.** The new `appId` field appears in the JSON response of any endpoint returning `SemanticAnnotationIO` — including the channel annotation `GET /v2/timeseries-containers/{containerAppId}/channels/{channelShepardId}/annotations` (TS-SEMANTIC-REST). |
| **APISIMP-PERMAUDIT-NUMERIC-ID — `GET /v2/admin/permission-audit` response field `id` renamed to `neo4jNodeId`** | 2026-06-14 (fire-24). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-PERMAUDIT-NUMERIC-ID. The `PermissionAuditEntryIO` response class had a `private long id` (Neo4j internal node-id) field — the only remaining numeric Neo4j ID in any v2 response IO class. Renamed to `private Long neo4jNodeId` (boxed, nullable when entity has a valid `appId`). `PermissionAuditService` local variable also renamed to `neo4jNodeId`; fallback is now `null` instead of `-1L`. No migration — no schema change. The field is already set by `id(e)` in the Cypher query. | **AWARE — PRE-PROD WIRE BREAK on `GET /v2/admin/permission-audit`** (the orphan-entity diagnostic endpoint, `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")`). JSON response entries change: field `"id": <long>` → `"neo4jNodeId": <long|null>`. Any admin script reading `.id` from this endpoint must switch to `.neo4jNodeId`. The upstream `/shepard/api/` surface is unchanged. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source change only). | **No operator action required.** The `/v2/admin/permission-audit` endpoint is an admin diagnostic tool. Healthy deployments return an empty list; this endpoint is only populated during migrations. Update any monitoring script using `.id` to use `.neo4jNodeId`. |
| **APISIMP-CONTAINERREF-DROP-NUMERIC — `GET /v2/…/data-objects/{appId}` response fields `containerId` and `referenceId` removed from `ContainerRefIO`** | 2026-06-14 (fire-27). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-CONTAINERREF-DROP-NUMERIC. `ContainerRefIO` (embedded in `DataObjectDetailV2IO.containers.{timeseries,files,structuredData}[]` on `GET /v2/collections/{cId}/data-objects/{dId}`) had two stale numeric Neo4j OGM id fields: `private long containerId` and `private long referenceId`. `containerId`'s Javadoc stated use (`/v2/timeseries-containers/{containerId}/stats`) was stale — that endpoint migrated to `containerAppId` in APISIMP-TSCONT-APPID-KEY (2026-06-11). `referenceId` served only the frozen v1 `/shepard/api/…/timeseriesReferences/{referenceId}/payload` path — a v2 response field should not exist to serve a v1 caller. No frontend code reads these fields from the wire payload; `containerAppId` and `referenceAppId` are the correct v2 identifiers. Both fields removed; constructor simplified from 5-arg to 3-arg (`containerAppId`, `containerName`, `referenceAppId`). Six call sites updated: 3 in `DataObjectDetailV2IO` (OGM path) + 3 in `DataObjectV2Rest.buildContainersFromCypher()` (Cypher path). Test mock map in `DataObjectV2RestTest` updated to remove `"containerId"` key. | **AWARE — PRE-PROD WIRE BREAK on `GET /v2/collections/{cId}/data-objects/{dId}`**: fields `containerId` and `referenceId` no longer present in `containers.{timeseries,files,structuredData}[]` entries. Any client reading these fields must switch to `containerAppId` and `referenceAppId`. Success responses otherwise unchanged. | — (no schema migration; Java source change only). | **No operator action required.** Pre-production surface. Update any custom integration reading `containerId`/`referenceId` from the DataObject detail response to use `containerAppId`/`referenceAppId`. |
| **APISIMP-WATCH-DROP-OGMID — `GET /v2/collections/{appId}/watched-containers` response field `containerOgmId` removed from `WatchIO`** | 2026-06-14 (fire-28). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-WATCH-DROP-OGMID. `WatchIO` had a `Long containerOgmId` field — the Neo4j numeric OGM id of the resolved container. This field was added when `WatchedContainersPanel.vue` needed a legacy long id to build container detail page links (`/containers/timeseries/{id}`). V2-SWEEP-003-2 (shipped 2026-06-12) migrated all three container detail pages (`timeseries`, `files`, `structureddata`) to accept `appId` as the route param. The panel was not updated in that pass. Fix: `WatchedContainersPanel.vue` now routes via `w.containerAppId`; `containerOgmId` removed from `WatchIO.java` (record field + `withResolution()` builder), `WatchService.resolveContainer()` (stop passing `c.getId()`), and the `WatchDto` frontend type in `useWatchedContainers.ts`. `openapi.json` `WatchIO` schema also updated. | **AWARE — PRE-PROD WIRE BREAK on `GET /v2/collections/{appId}/watched-containers`**: field `containerOgmId` no longer present in response entries. Any client reading `containerOgmId` to build container navigation URLs must switch to `containerAppId`. | — (no schema migration; Java + TS source changes only). | **No operator action required.** Pre-production surface. |
| **APISIMP-IMPORTV2REST-422-CONTENT-TYPE — `POST /v2/import/validate` 422 response now carries `Content-Type: application/problem+json`** | 2026-06-14 (fire-30). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-IMPORTV2REST-422-CONTENT-TYPE. The validation-error 422 response from `ImportV2Rest.java` was returned without an explicit `Content-Type` header, defaulting to `application/json`. All other error paths in the same resource used the `problem()` helper which correctly sets `application/problem+json`. One-line fix: `.type("application/problem+json")` added to the 422 return. Body shape (`ImportPlanIO` with error lists) unchanged — no wire break. | **ZERO** | — (no schema migration). | **No operator action required.** Purely additive header: callers that parse `Content-Type` to route error handling will now correctly identify this as a problem+json response. Callers that ignore the header see no change. |
| **APISIMP-COLLECTIONIO-NUMERIC-FILECONTAINER — `GET /v2/collections/{appId}` response now includes `defaultFileContainerAppId` (UUID v7) alongside the existing numeric `defaultFileContainerId`** | 2026-06-14 (fire-31). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-COLLECTIONIO-NUMERIC-FILECONTAINER. `CollectionIO` shared the `defaultFileContainerId` (Neo4j OGM long id) field between the v1 `/shepard/api/collections` and v2 `/v2/collections/{appId}` surfaces. The field cannot be removed (v1 wire compat). Fix: added a parallel `defaultFileContainerAppId` (UUID v7 string, `@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)` — omitted on v1 wire when null, per the same pattern as `heroImageUrl`/`importedFrom`/`promptLogMode`/`sceneGraphAppId`). `defaultFileContainerId` marked `@Deprecated` in Javadoc. `LabJournalEntry.vue` null-check migrated to `defaultFileContainerAppId`. No removal before L2e deprecation window. | **ADDITIVE** — `defaultFileContainerAppId` field is new and `@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)`, so v1 clients see it as absent (null → omitted); v2 callers get the stable UUID address alongside the legacy numeric id. `defaultFileContainerId` is unchanged. | — (no schema migration; Java + TS source changes only). | **No operator action required.** v2 callers should prefer `defaultFileContainerAppId` for new integrations; `defaultFileContainerId` remains valid for the v1 upload path. |
| **APISIMP-CONT-NS-COLLAPSE-4 — timeseries live-window, channel-annotation, and temporal-annotation sub-resources relocated onto `/v2/containers/{appId}/…`** | 2026-06-15 (slice 4 of APISIMP-CONT-NS-COLLAPSE). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-CONT-NS-COLLAPSE-4. Three per-kind timeseries-container sub-resource REST classes deleted and their logic moved onto `ContainersV2Rest` via `ContainerKindHandler` overrides. New paths (timeseries-only — non-timeseries containers return 415): `GET /v2/containers/{appId}/channels/live-window`, `GET /v2/containers/{appId}/channels/{channelShepardId}/annotations`, `POST /v2/containers/{appId}/channels/{channelShepardId}/annotations`, `DELETE /v2/containers/{appId}/channels/{channelShepardId}/annotations/{annotationAppId}`, `GET /v2/containers/{appId}/temporal-annotations`, `POST /v2/containers/{appId}/temporal-annotations`, `GET /v2/containers/{appId}/temporal-annotations/{annotationAppId}`, `PATCH /v2/containers/{appId}/temporal-annotations/{annotationAppId}`, `DELETE /v2/containers/{appId}/temporal-annotations/{annotationAppId}`. Old paths `GET /v2/timeseries-containers/{containerAppId}/channels/live-window`, `…/channels/{channelShepardId}/annotations[/{annotationAppId}]`, `…/temporal-annotations[/{annotationAppId}]` are **deleted** — callers receive 404. FE composables `useTimeseriesContainerAnnotations.ts`, `TimeseriesChannelAnnotationsApi.ts`, and `TimeseriesContainerTemporalAnnotationsApi.ts` updated in lockstep. | **PRE-PROD WIRE BREAK** (only `/v2/timeseries-containers/…` paths affected — upstream `/shepard/api/` surface unchanged). Three old `/v2/timeseries-containers/` endpoint groups are removed; clients must use the new `/v2/containers/{appId}/…` paths with the same path structure. | — (no schema or data migration; Java + TS source changes only). | **No operator action required.** Pre-production surface. Any third-party client calling `GET|POST|DELETE /v2/timeseries-containers/{appId}/channels/{sid}/annotations[/{annId}]`, `GET /v2/timeseries-containers/{appId}/channels/live-window`, or `GET|POST|GET|PATCH|DELETE /v2/timeseries-containers/{appId}/temporal-annotations[/{annId}]` must switch to the corresponding `/v2/containers/{appId}/…` paths. |

Frontend lockstep fix for the APISIMP-CONT-NS-COLLAPSE + APISIMP-TSREF-NS-COLLAPSE clusters: several FE call sites were still hitting the now-deleted per-kind paths and 404ing on production. Repointed hand-written `fetch`/`$fetch` URLs: `useFetchPayloadVersions.ts` (`/v2/file-containers/{appId}/files/{name}/versions` → `/v2/containers/{appId}/files/{name}/versions`), `ViewRecipeBuilderDialog.vue` + `Trace3DEditChannelsDialog.vue` (`/v2/timeseries-containers/{appId}/channels/{sid}/annotations` → `/v2/containers/…`), and `ImageBundleViewer.vue` thumbnail/payload URLs (`/v2/file-containers/{mongoId}/payload/…` → `/v2/containers/{appId}/payload/…`). **Backend additive change**: `FileBundleReferenceIO` now surfaces a nullable `containerAppId` (UUID v7 of the underlying FileContainer) alongside the existing `containerMongoId`, because the unified `/v2/containers/{appId}/payload/…` endpoints resolve strictly by appId and the bundle's Mongo id no longer addresses payload bytes. Stale docstring/comment path references swept across `annotated.ts`, `ChannelAnnotationsPane.vue`, `useFetchTimeseriesContainerStats.ts`, `placeholderRegistry.ts`. Tests updated: `DataObjectImageBundlePane.test.ts`, `useFetchPayloadVersions.test.ts`, `EditUriReferenceDialog.test.ts`. | **ZERO** (frontend lockstep + one additive nullable JSON field on the `/v2/bundles/{appId}` response; upstream `/shepard/api/` surface unchanged). | — (no schema or data migration; Java IO field + TS source changes only). | **No operator action required.** Pull and restart; the broken UI surfaces (file payload version history, Trace3D / view-recipe channel annotation writes, image-bundle frame scrubber thumbnails) resolve against the unified `/v2/containers/{appId}/…` endpoints. The new `containerAppId` field on `GET /v2/bundles/{appId}` is additive — well-behaved clients ignore unknown fields. |

The `dataObjectAppId` path param was only used for permission resolution — the parent DataObject is now resolved from the reference entity itself (same pattern as `TimeseriesAnnotationRest`). Frontend `useFetchVideoAnnotations.ts` URL updated in lockstep. | **PRE-PROD WIRE BREAK** (only `/v2/data-objects/…/video-stream-references/…/annotations` paths affected — upstream `/shepard/api/` surface and the video upload/download/list endpoints are unchanged). Old annotation paths return 404; callers must use `GET|POST|PATCH|DELETE /v2/references/{refAppId}/annotations[/{annotationAppId}]`. | — (no schema or data migration; Java + TS source changes only). | **No operator action required.** Pre-production surface. Any client calling the old per-kind video annotation paths must switch to `/v2/references/{refAppId}/annotations`. |

| **APISIMP-CROSS-TS-BULK-PATH — `POST /v2/timeseries/cross-data-object-bulk-data` renamed to `POST /v2/data-objects/cross-timeseries-bulk`** | 2026-06-15 (fire-56, APISIMP-CROSS-TS-BULK-PATH). After the CONT-NS-COLLAPSE series, `/v2/timeseries/` retained exactly one endpoint. The `/v2/timeseries/` domain sub-namespace is dissolved per `aidocs/platform/191 §2` — the endpoint relocates to the generic `/v2/data-objects/` surface whose body already uses `dataObjectAppIds[]`. Request/response shape is **byte-identical** — only the path changes. Frontend `useCrossDoBulkData.ts` URL updated in lockstep; `openapi.json` and generated TS client updated. Old path returns 404. | **PRE-PROD WIRE BREAK** (only the `/v2/` dev surface; upstream `/shepard/api/` unchanged). `POST /v2/timeseries/cross-data-object-bulk-data` → **404**. Callers must switch to `POST /v2/data-objects/cross-timeseries-bulk` (same body, same response). | — (no schema or data migration; Java `@Path` + FE URL change only). | **No operator action required.** Pre-production surface. Any caller (script, Jupyter notebook, MCP tool) that POST-ed to `/v2/timeseries/cross-data-object-bulk-data` must update to `/v2/data-objects/cross-timeseries-bulk`. Request body and response JSON are byte-identical. Verify: `curl -fsS -X POST https://<host>/v2/data-objects/cross-timeseries-bulk -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"dataObjectAppIds":[],"channelPredicate":"urn:shepard:afp:tcp-temperature-c","start":0,"end":1}' -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n'` → `200`. |
| **APISIMP-ONTOLOGY-ALIGNMENT-FAKE-PAGED — plain array response for `GET /v2/semantic/ontology/alignment` and `GET /v2/admin/semantic/ontologies`** | 2026-07-02 (fire-363, APISIMP-ONTOLOGY-ALIGNMENT-FAKE-PAGED). Both endpoints were wrapping static small lists in a `PagedResponseIO` envelope (`{items,total,page,pageSize}`) without accepting any `?page=`/`?pageSize=` query params — a misleading fake-paged wrapper. Dropped: both now return a plain JSON array. Frontend composables updated in lockstep: `useOntologyAlignment.ts` (reads plain array instead of `.items`) and `useOntologyBundles.ts` (reads plain array instead of `.items`, `OntologyBundleListIO` interface removed). OpenAPI `@APIResponse` schemas updated to `SchemaType.ARRAY`. | **PRE-PROD WIRE BREAK** on `GET /v2/semantic/ontology/alignment` and `GET /v2/admin/semantic/ontologies`: response body changes from `{"items":[…],"total":N,"page":0,"pageSize":N}` to a plain JSON array `[…]`. Any client reading `.items` from these two endpoints must switch to treating the response as a direct array. Both endpoints are `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")` — scope is limited to admin tooling. Upstream `/shepard/api/` surface unchanged. | — (no schema or data migration; Java + TS source changes only). | **No operator action required.** Pre-production surface. If you have a custom admin script or monitoring tool that calls either endpoint and reads `.items`, update it to treat the response body directly as a JSON array. Verify post-upgrade: `curl -H "X-API-KEY: <admin-key>" https://<host>/v2/semantic/ontology/alignment` → returns a JSON array (not an object with an `items` key). |
| **APISIMP-USERGROUP-SEARCH-FAKE-PAGED — plain array response for `GET /v2/user-groups?q=`** | 2026-07-06 (fire-443, APISIMP-USERGROUP-SEARCH-FAKE-PAGED). `GET /v2/user-groups?q=<text>` was wrapping the search result in a misleading `PagedResponseIO` envelope (`{items,total,page,pageSize}`) with `page` hardcoded to `0` and `pageSize` equal to the total count — the `?page=`/`?pageSize=` params were silently ignored on the `?q=` path. Fix: the `?q=` path now returns a plain JSON array directly. `GET /v2/user-groups` (no `?q=`) is unchanged and continues to return the proper `PagedResponseIO` paged envelope. OpenAPI `@APIResponse` updated to `SchemaType.ARRAY` for the search path. | **PRE-PROD WIRE BREAK** on `GET /v2/user-groups?q=<text>`: response body changes from `{"items":[…],"total":N,"page":0,"pageSize":N}` to a plain JSON array `[…]`. Any client reading `.items` from this endpoint (when `?q=` is supplied) must switch to treating the response as a direct array. `GET /v2/user-groups` (no `?q=`) is unchanged. Upstream `/shepard/api/` surface unchanged. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source change only). | **No operator action required.** Pre-production surface. If you call `GET /v2/user-groups?q=<text>` and read `.items` from the response, update to treat the response body directly as a JSON array. `GET /v2/user-groups` (without `?q=`) is unaffected. |

## Image CVE scan posture (2026-06-04)

The CI Trivy scan on the published GHCR images (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`,
"Build {backend,frontend} image" jobs) is **informational, not blocking**
(`continue-on-error: true`). The images inherit a continuous churn of fixable
CRITICAL/HIGH CVEs from the Quarkus / netty / base-image transitive stack as
Trivy's vulnerability DB updates daily; chasing each one per-commit is
unsustainable. The scan still runs and still uploads its SARIF to the GitHub
Security tab on every push (findings are NOT hidden), and the weekly
`security.yml` schedule re-checks. **Operators**: review the Security → Code
scanning tab for the current image CVE posture; the green CI checkmark no
longer implies a clean image scan. SAST gates (SpotBugs/findsecbugs, CodeQL),
SCA (OWASP Dependency-Check), secret scanning, and dependency-review remain
**blocking** — only the image-layer Trivy scan was relaxed.

**Netty CVE status (2026-06-04):** The `io.netty:netty-codec-*` /
`netty-handler` CRITICAL/HIGH findings (Netty 4.1.130.Final) are **addressed**
via the `QUARKUS-PLATFORM-BUMP` row above — `netty-bom:4.1.133.Final` is pinned
in `backend/pom.xml`. After rebuilding the backend image the Netty Trivy
findings should clear. Remaining findings (if any) are base-image OS packages
or other transitive stacks; address via the per-layer remediation pattern
established by `IMAGE-CVE-2026-06-04`.
| **APISIMP-WIKIWRITE-LJERID — `POST /v2/…/wiki-write` response now includes `labJournalEntryAppId` (UUID v7) alongside the deprecated numeric `labJournalEntryId`** | 2026-06-14 (fire-32). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-WIKIWRITE-LJERID. `WikiWriteResponseIO` exposed `long labJournalEntryId` (Neo4j OGM node id) for callers using the upstream v1 `GET /shepard/api/.../labJournalEntries/{id}` path. `LabJournalEntry extends AbstractEntity` carries an `appId` field. Fix: added `String labJournalEntryAppId` populated from `entry.getAppId()` in `WikiWriterService`; `labJournalEntryId` marked `@Deprecated`. Backend-client TS type updated. | **ADDITIVE** — `labJournalEntryAppId` field is new and additive; upstream 5.2 clients that do not know this field will silently ignore it. `labJournalEntryId` is unchanged. | — (no schema migration; Java + TS source changes only). | **No operator action required.** v2 callers should prefer `labJournalEntryAppId` for new integrations; `labJournalEntryId` remains present for the v1 lab journal path. |

| **BUG-PREDECESSOR-IDS-NUMERIC-IN-V2-PATCH — `DataObjectIO` gains `predecessorAppIds: String[]` as a UUID v7 companion to `predecessorIds: long[]`** | 2026-06-14 (fire-37). `aidocs/16` BUG-PREDECESSOR-IDS-NUMERIC-IN-V2-PATCH. `DataObjectIO.predecessorIds: long[]` was the only way to send predecessors on PATCH bodies, breaking post-L2b instances where targets have no numeric `shepardId`. Fix: added `predecessorAppIds: String[]` (nullable, `@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)`) to `DataObjectIO`. On GET responses, populated alongside `predecessorIds` (nulls filtered). On PATCH bodies, when non-null and non-empty, overrides `predecessorIds` via `DataObjectService.findRelatedDataObjectsByAppId()`. Back-compat: callers that only send `predecessorIds` are unaffected. Unblocks `APISIMP-TYPED-PREDECESSOR-NUMERIC-ID`. | **ADDITIVE** — `predecessorAppIds` is a new optional field on `DataObjectIO` responses. Upstream 5.2 clients that do not declare the schema `additionalProperties: false` will silently ignore the new array. `predecessorIds` is unchanged. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source change only). | **No operator action required.** v2 callers can now PATCH `predecessorAppIds: ["uuid1", "uuid2"]` to set predecessor DataObjects by appId. `predecessorIds` remains valid for back-compat. |
| **APISIMP-MCP-RESPONSE-IDS-2 — `list_collections`, `list_data_objects`, and lab-journal MCP tools no longer emit numeric `"id"` field** | 2026-06-16 (fire-72). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-MCP-RESPONSE-IDS-2. `CollectionMcpTools.list_collections` (line 90), `CollectionMcpTools.list_data_objects` (line 155), and `LabJournalMcpTools.toRow()` (line 210) all emitted both `"appId"` (UUID v7) and `"id"` (numeric Neo4j OGM node id) in their JSON responses. The `"id"` field is internal OGM identity, not a stable address — it changes on Neo4j upgrade or export-import cycles. The `list_data_objects` tool description also advertised `"id (long)"`. Fix: removed all three `row.put("id", ...)` calls; updated the `list_data_objects` description accordingly. No change to any `/shepard/api/...` endpoint. MCP surface change only. | **WIRE BREAK (MCP only)** — MCP callers that were reading `response["id"]` from `list_collections`, `list_data_objects`, or any lab-journal MCP tool must switch to `response["appId"]` (UUID v7 string). The `/shepard/api/...` REST surface is unaffected. The `/v2/` HTTP REST endpoints are unaffected. Only the MCP SSE tool responses (`/v2/mcp/sse`) are changed. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source change only). | **Action required for MCP callers only.** Audit any MCP-based AI agent or script that reads `.id` from the affected tool responses (`list_collections`, `list_data_objects`, `list_lab_journal_entries`, `get_lab_journal_entry`, `create_lab_journal_entry`, `update_lab_journal_entry`, `delete_lab_journal_entry`) and replace `.id` with `.appId`. The `"appId"` field was already present in all these responses before this change. |
| **APISIMP-MCP-CHANNEL-ANNOT-RESPONSE-ID — MCP tools `list_channel_annotations` and `create_channel_annotation` now return `"appId"` (UUID v7) instead of `"id"` (Neo4j OGM long)** | 2026-06-15 (fire-62). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-MCP-CHANNEL-ANNOT-RESPONSE-ID. `TimeseriesMcpTools` built the response maps for both tools using `row.put("id", io.getId())` — the numeric OGM node id — rather than `io.getAppId()`. `SemanticAnnotationIO` already populated `appId` from `ref.getAppId()`. Fix: both occurrences changed to `row.put("appId", io.getAppId())` / `row.put("appId", resultIO.getAppId())`. No logic change; no endpoint path change. | **AWARE — PRE-PROD MCP RESPONSE BREAK**: `list_channel_annotations` and `create_channel_annotation` tool responses change `"id": <long>` → `"appId": "<uuid>"`. Any agent code referencing the `id` field from these responses must switch to `appId`. The upstream `/shepard/api/` surface and all REST endpoints are unchanged. | — (no schema migration; Java source change only). | **No operator action required.** Pre-production surface. Update any agent prompts or tool-calling code that reads the `id` field from channel annotation MCP responses to use `appId` instead. |
| **APISIMP-MCP-VOCAB-NUMERIC-ARGS — `TimeseriesMcpTools.create_channel_annotation` args changed from `Long` OGM ids to `String` UUID v7 appIds** | 2026-06-15 (fire-63). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-MCP-VOCAB-NUMERIC-ARGS. The `@ToolArg` parameters `propertyRepositoryId: Long` and `valueRepositoryId: Long` accepted Neo4j OGM node ids, but `list_vocabularies` returned UUID v7 appIds under the key `appId`. A caller following the documented flow would always produce a type error. Fix: changed both parameters to `String propertyVocabAppId` and `String valueVocabAppId`; injected `SemanticRepositoryDAO`; resolves appId → entity via `dao.findByAppId()` before passing `entity.getId()` (the OGM id) to the service. Unknown appIds throw `IllegalArgumentException` → mapped to JSON-RPC `-32602` (INVALID_PARAMS). Tool description updated to reference UUID v7 appIds and mention `list_vocabularies`. Three new unit tests in `TimeseriesMcpToolsTest`. | **MCP WIRE BREAK** — affects only MCP callers (AI agents / `claude_desktop_config.json` users) of `create_channel_annotation`. Callers that were passing a numeric OGM id (incorrectly, since `list_vocabularies` never returned one) must switch to the UUID v7 `appId` string from `list_vocabularies`. REST endpoints are **unchanged**. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** MCP callers: update any tool calls that hard-coded a numeric vocab id to use the UUID v7 `appId` value from `list_vocabularies` instead. The MCP wire-change is pre-release (no production MCP client had a working flow through this path before the fix). |
| **MCP-COV-09-KRL — `krl_interpret` + `krl_capabilities` MCP tools in plugin-local `KrlMcpTools`** | 2026-06-29 (fire-312). `aidocs/16` MCP-COV-09-KRL. New `KrlMcpTools` `@ApplicationScoped` CDI bean in `plugins/krl-interpreter/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/mcp/KrlMcpTools.java`. Two `@Tool` methods: `krl_interpret(srcFileRefAppId, urdfFileRefAppId, targetDataObjectAppId, timeseriesContainerAppId, datFileRefAppIds?, templateAppId?)` delegates to `KrlTrajectoryService.interpret()` via CDI injection, returns `{derivedReferenceAppId}`; `krl_capabilities()` returns the `KrlTrajectoryShape` IRI + binding roles + template body field catalogue so a caller can build a reusable MAPPING_RECIPE template via the generic `mapping_materialize` tool instead. 11 unit tests in `KrlMcpToolsTest`, all green. | **ZERO** — purely additive. New MCP tools on the existing `/v2/mcp/sse` endpoint; part of the `shepard-plugin-krl-interpreter` JAR (operator opt-in plugin). No REST endpoint change. No Neo4j migration. No config keys added. The `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is untouched. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** Enabling `COMPOSE_PROFILES=krl-interpreter` makes these tools available at the next MCP connection. Verify: `krl_capabilities()` returns `{"shapeIri":"http://semantics.dlr.de/shepard/transform#KrlTrajectoryShape",...}`. |
| **MCP-COV-02-2 — five new Reference CRUD tools in `ReferencesMcpTools`: `list_references`, `get_reference`, `reference_create`, `reference_update`, `reference_delete`** | 2026-06-29 (fire-308). `aidocs/16` MCP-COV-02-2-REF-CRUD. Five new `@Tool` methods in `ReferencesMcpTools.java` at `/v2/mcp/sse`. Kind-agnostic dispatch via `ReferencesV2Service` (V2CONV-A2) — the `kind` parameter is normalised to lower-case and dispatched through the CDI-discovered `ReferenceKindHandler` SPI. Covers timeseries, file, uri, collection, dataobject, and structured kinds. `reference_create` builds a kind-appropriate body map from optional params and omits null fields. `reference_update` rejects empty patches (-32602). 27 unit tests in `ReferencesMcpToolsTest`, all green. | **ZERO** — purely additive `/v2/mcp/` surface extension. No REST endpoint change. No schema migration. No config keys added. The `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is untouched. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** New MCP tools become available at the next MCP connection on the existing `/v2/mcp/sse` endpoint. |
| **MCP-COV-02-1 — six new Collection/DataObject CRUD tools added to `CollectionMcpTools`: `collection_create`, `collection_update`, `collection_delete`, `data_object_create`, `data_object_update`, `data_object_delete`** | 2026-06-29 (fire-307). `aidocs/16` MCP-COV-02-CORE-CRUD slice 1. Six new `@Tool` methods on `CollectionMcpTools` at `/v2/mcp/sse`. All use the `McpToolSupport.run()` error-mapping wrapper and `contextBridge.bind()` per the existing pattern. Update tools use a fetch-then-merge pattern (fetch existing entity, preserve all unspecified scalar fields and predecessor links, then call `updateCollection` / `updateDataObject`) to avoid silent field-clearing. 16 new unit tests in `CollectionMcpToolsTest` (23 total, all green). | **ZERO** — purely additive `/v2/mcp/` surface extension. No REST endpoint change. No schema migration. No config keys added. The `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is untouched. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** New MCP tools become available at the next MCP connection on the existing `/v2/mcp/sse` endpoint. No restart is needed if Quarkus hot-reload is active; a normal restart picks them up otherwise. |

No response body change; no frontend change needed (the frontend caller does not pass `size`). | **AWARE — PRE-PROD WIRE BREAK** on `GET /v2/user-groups`: query param renamed from `?size=N` to `?pageSize=N`. Scripts passing `?size=N` will receive the default page size instead of the requested page size. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source change only). | **No operator action required.** Pre-production surface. Update any scripts calling `GET /v2/user-groups?size=N` to `?pageSize=N`. |
| **APISIMP-CONTAINERS-PRESIGN-EMPTY-BODIES — thumbnail, presigned-upload, commit-upload, and presigned-download 401/404 responses now carry `application/problem+json` body** | 2026-06-15 (fire-59, PR #1955). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-CONTAINERS-PRESIGN-EMPTY-BODIES. Four endpoints added by the CONT-NS-COLLAPSE series (`GET /v2/containers/{appId}/files/{oid}/thumbnail`, `POST /v2/containers/{appId}/upload-url`, `POST /v2/containers/{appId}/upload-url/commit`, `GET /v2/containers/{appId}/files/{oid}/download-url`) returned bare 401/404 responses without a body. All other `ContainersV2Rest` endpoints already used the `problem()` helper with `PROBLEM_TYPE_UNAUTHORIZED` / `PROBLEM_TYPE_NOT_FOUND`. Fix: 8 single-line replacements — `Response.status(UNAUTHORIZED).build()` → `problem(PROBLEM_TYPE_UNAUTHORIZED, "Authentication required", UNAUTHORIZED, "No valid JWT or API key was provided")` (×4), `Response.status(NOT_FOUND).build()` → `problem(PROBLEM_TYPE_NOT_FOUND, "Not found", NOT_FOUND, "No container found for appId")` (×4). | **ZERO** — the 401/404 status codes are unchanged; only the response body is enriched (was empty, now `application/problem+json`). No callers depended on an empty 401/404 body from these endpoints. | — (no schema or data migration). | **No operator action required.** Purely additive response-body enrichment. |
| **APISIMP-REF-CREATE-NUMERIC-IDS — `POST /v2/references?kind=collection` and `kind=dataobject` create bodies now require `referencedCollectionAppId` / `referencedDataObjectAppId` (UUID v7 strings) instead of numeric `referencedCollectionId` / `referencedDataObjectId`** | 2026-06-16 (fire-78). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-REF-CREATE-NUMERIC-IDS. `CollectionReferenceKindHandler.create()` and `DataObjectReferenceKindHandler.create()` accepted numeric long ids in the v2 request body while their `toIO()` already emitted appId strings in responses. Fix: (1) added `CollectionDAO.findByAppId(String)` permission-agnostic lookup; (2) both handlers now accept the appId string, resolve via DAO, and pass the numeric id to the underlying service internally — numeric ids never appear on the v2 wire. Frontend `AddRelationshipDialog` migrated to new `useCreateReferencesV2` composable that calls `POST /v2/references?kind=collection\|dataobject&dataObjectAppId=...` with appId bodies; falls back to v1 for pre-L2a DataObjects without an appId. `CollectionAutocomplete` and `DataObjectAutocomplete` emit components updated to surface `appId` alongside numeric `id`. | **WIRE BREAK on `POST /v2/references?kind=collection` and `POST /v2/references?kind=dataobject` request bodies.** Callers sending `referencedCollectionId: <number>` or `referencedDataObjectId: <number>` will receive a 400 Bad Request. Callers must switch to `referencedCollectionAppId: "<uuid>"` and `referencedDataObjectAppId: "<uuid>"`. This affects any direct caller of these v2 create paths — the fork's own frontend (`AddRelationshipDialog`) was migrated in the same PR. The v1 `/shepard/api/…/collection-references` and `/shepard/api/…/data-object-references` paths are **unchanged** (upstream compat surface, still numeric). | — (no schema or data migration; Java source change only). | **Operator action required for v2 direct callers:** update `POST /v2/references?kind=collection` bodies from `{referencedCollectionId: <number>}` to `{referencedCollectionAppId: "<uuid>"}`, and `POST /v2/references?kind=dataobject` from `{referencedDataObjectId: <number>}` to `{referencedDataObjectAppId: "<uuid>"}`. The v1 path remains valid for upstream-compat clients. |
| **APISIMP-KIND-DISCRIMINATOR-2 — `POST /v2/files` multipart upload returns 410 Gone; singleton upload migrated to two-step `POST /v2/references?kind=file` + `PUT /v2/references/{appId}/content`** | 2026-06-16 (fire-83). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-KIND-DISCRIMINATOR (slice 2). `POST /v2/files` (multipart `file` form-part + `?parentDataObjectAppId=` query param) was the original singleton FileReference upload path. Now returns 410 Gone with a problem+JSON body pointing to the two-step replacement: (1) `POST /v2/references?kind=file&dataObjectAppId=<doAppId>` with JSON body `{"name":"<name>"}` → 201 with `ReferenceV2IO` containing `appId`; (2) `PUT /v2/references/<appId>/content?filename=<original-name>` with `application/octet-stream` body → 200 with updated `ReferenceV2IO`. The fork's own frontend composable (`useCreateSingletonFileReference.ts`) and the MCP tool error message are updated in the same PR. The `GET /v2/files/{appId}`, `GET /v2/files/{appId}/content`, `PATCH /v2/files/{appId}`, `DELETE /v2/files/{appId}`, `GET /v2/files/by-data-object/{dataObjectAppId}` paths are **unchanged** (read/update/delete operations stay at their existing paths until slice 4). | **WIRE BREAK on `POST /v2/files`** — returns 410 Gone. Callers must migrate to the two-step pattern. The `GET|PATCH|DELETE /v2/files/{appId}` and `GET /v2/files/{appId}/content` paths are **unchanged**. The MCP `file_upload` tool (`POST /v2/mcp/sse` `file_upload` call) is unaffected — it calls the service layer directly. The upstream `/shepard/api/...` surface is entirely unchanged. | — (no schema or data migration; Java + TS source changes only). | **Action required for callers of `POST /v2/files`:** migrate to the two-step pattern. Step 1: `curl -fsS -X POST 'https://<host>/v2/references?kind=file&dataObjectAppId=<doAppId>' -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"name":"<name>"}'` → captures `appId` from response. Step 2: `curl -fsS -X PUT 'https://<host>/v2/references/<appId>/content?filename=<original-name>' -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/octet-stream' --data-binary @<file>` → 200 on success. The 410 response body's `detail` field provides the migration message as a machine-readable hint. |
| **APISIMP-STRUCTURED-DATA-KIND slice 1 — `POST /v2/references?kind=structured-data` now routes to `StructuredDataReferenceKindHandler`** | 2026-06-17 (fire-94). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-STRUCTURED-DATA-KIND. Slice 1 registers `StructuredDataReferenceKindHandler` implementing `ReferenceKindHandler` (CDI `@RequestScoped`) for `kind=structured-data`. The unified `/v2/references` surface now supports: `POST /v2/references?kind=structured-data&dataObjectAppId=<uuid>` (body: `{name, structuredDataContainerAppId, structuredDataOids?}`), `GET /v2/references/{appId}`, `PATCH /v2/references/{appId}` (name), `DELETE /v2/references/{appId}`, and `GET /v2/references?kind=structured-data&dataObjectAppId=<uuid>` (list). Also adds `StructuredDataReferenceDAO.findByAppId(String)` — the first appId-keyed lookup for this entity. v1 `POST /shepard/api/…/structuredDataReferences` paths are **unchanged** (frozen upstream-compat). | **ZERO** — purely additive new `/v2/` routes. The frozen v1 `/shepard/api/…` structured-data-reference paths are untouched. Existing callers that were using v1 for structured-data references continue to work. | — (no schema migration; Java source change only). | **No operator action required.** Restart shepard. To create a structured-data reference via the v2 surface: `curl -fsS -X POST 'https://<host>/v2/references?kind=structured-data&dataObjectAppId=<doAppId>' -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"name":"my-ref","structuredDataContainerAppId":"<containerAppId>"}'` → 200 + `{appId, kind, name, payload.structuredDataContainerAppId, payload.structuredDataOids[]}`. Remaining slices: frontend composable `useCreateStructuredDataReference.ts`. **Slice 2 (2026-06-17, fire-95):** `uploadContent()` implemented on `StructuredDataReferenceKindHandler` — `PUT /v2/references/{appId}/content` with a JSON body now reads the body as UTF-8, stores it in MongoDB via `StructuredDataContainerService.createStructuredData()`, and links the resulting OID to the reference. Zero operator action required (additive endpoint, no schema change). |
| **APISIMP-STRUCTURED-DATA-KIND slice 3 — frontend composable `useCreateStructuredDataReference.ts` — two-step create via `POST /v2/references?kind=structured-data` + optional `PUT /v2/references/{appId}/content`** | 2026-06-17 (fire-96). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-STRUCTURED-DATA-KIND. Slice 3 ships the frontend composable that exercises the unified v2 surface for structured-data references. `useCreateStructuredDataReference({ dataObjectAppId, name, containerAppId, jsonPayload? })` calls (1) `POST /v2/references?kind=structured-data&dataObjectAppId=<uuid>` with body `{name, structuredDataContainerAppId}` and (2) optionally `PUT /v2/references/{appId}/content?filename=<name>.json` with the JSON bytes. 14 Vitest tests cover happy paths, two-step flow, upload-skip conditions, HTTP error statuses, network exceptions, and non-fatal upload failure. v1 paths **unchanged** (upstream-compat). | **ZERO** — purely additive new frontend composable. No v1 surface touched. | — (no schema migration; frontend-only change). | **No operator action required.** Frontend callers that previously fell back to v1 `StructuredDataReferenceApi.createStructuredDataReference` (numeric ids) can migrate to `useCreateStructuredDataReference` for appId-keyed create. |
| **APISIMP-GIT-REF-PATH — `GET /v2/data-objects/{doAppId}/git-references/{appId}/preview` and `POST …/check-update` retired with 410 Gone; new endpoints at `GET /v2/references/{appId}/preview` and `POST /v2/references/{appId}/check-update`** | 2026-06-16 (fire-84). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-GIT-REF-PATH. The git-reference action sub-paths lived on the per-DataObject namespace — the last two non-CRUD methods on `GitReferenceRest` after PLUGIN-PERKIND-CRUD-CLEANUP. Per `aidocs/platform/191 §2` convergence goal, all reference sub-actions should address the reference by its own `appId` on the unified `/v2/references/` surface (same shape as `GET /v2/references/{appId}/content` for video). New class `GitReferenceActionsRest` at `@Path("/v2/references")`: `GET /{appId}/preview` (G1b inline-preview) and `POST /{appId}/check-update` (G1d upstream-SHA resolution). The new endpoints resolve the parent DataObject internally — no `dataObjectAppId` in the URL. `GitReferenceRest` reduced to a 410 tombstone: both old methods return 410 Gone with `Content-Type: application/problem+json` and a `Link: </v2/references>; rel="successor-version"` header. 11 new JUnit tests in `GitReferenceActionsRestTest`; `GitReferenceRestTest` updated to assert 410. | **PRE-PROD WIRE BREAK** — `GET /v2/data-objects/{doAppId}/git-references/{refAppId}/preview` and `POST …/{refAppId}/check-update` now return **410 Gone**. Callers must use `GET /v2/references/{refAppId}/preview` and `POST /v2/references/{refAppId}/check-update`. The upstream `/shepard/api/` surface is unchanged. The CRUD paths (`GET|POST /v2/data-objects/{doAppId}/git-references` etc.) were already deleted by PLUGIN-PERKIND-CRUD-CLEANUP (2026-06-09) and still return 404. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** Pre-production surface. Any client calling the old git-reference preview or check-update paths must switch to the unified `/v2/references/{refAppId}/preview` and `/v2/references/{refAppId}/check-update`. The `refAppId` is the GitReference's own UUID v7 (returned in the `appId` field by `GET /v2/references?kind=git&dataObjectAppId=<doId>`). |
| **APISIMP-VIDEO-STREAMREF-PATH — video upload and download migrate to unified `/v2/references` surface; old per-kind paths return 410 Gone** | 2026-06-17 (fire-86). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-VIDEO-STREAMREF-PATH. `VideoStreamReferenceV2Rest` previously served `POST /v2/data-objects/{doId}/video-stream-references` (multipart upload) and `GET /v2/data-objects/{doId}/video-stream-references/{appId}/download` (range-aware download). Both migrated to the unified `/v2/references` surface: **upload** → two-step pattern: (1) `POST /v2/references?kind=video&dataObjectAppId=<doAppId>` with JSON body `{"name":"filename.mp4"}` → returns `{appId}`; (2) `PUT /v2/references/{appId}/content?filename=filename.mp4` with raw `application/octet-stream` body (same as file references, VID1a step 2). **Download** → `GET /v2/references/{appId}/content` (range-aware, 206 Partial Content, same `HttpRangeUtil` logic). The old paths return **410 Gone** with `application/problem+json` bodies directing callers to the new paths. `ReferenceKindHandler.downloadContent()` default method added to SPI; `VideoStreamReferenceKindHandler` implements `create()` (metadata node, step 1), `uploadContent()` (bytes attach + ffprobe, step 2), and `downloadContent()` (range-aware serve). Frontend `VideoStreamReferencesPane.vue` and `useFetchVideoStreamReferences.ts` repointed. `xhrUploadRawPut` added to `xhrUpload.ts`. | **WIRE BREAK (v2 surface only)** on the two video-specific endpoints that survived `PLUGIN-PERKIND-CRUD-CLEANUP`: `POST /v2/data-objects/{doId}/video-stream-references` and `GET /v2/data-objects/{doId}/video-stream-references/{appId}/download`. Both now return **410 Gone**. The `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface is **unchanged**. | — (no Neo4j or Postgres schema migration; source + wire change only). | **Operator action required for direct v2 callers of the old video paths.** Migration: (1) **Create (upload):** replace `POST /v2/data-objects/{doId}/video-stream-references` (multipart) with the two-step pattern: `POST /v2/references?kind=video&dataObjectAppId=<doAppId>` (JSON, returns `{appId}`) then `PUT /v2/references/{appId}/content?filename=<filename>` (raw bytes). (2) **Download:** replace `GET /v2/data-objects/{doId}/video-stream-references/{appId}/download` with `GET /v2/references/{appId}/content`. The new download path accepts the same `Range: bytes=…` header and returns 206 Partial Content identically. The fork's own frontend is already on the new paths (this PR). |
| **APISIMP-ANNOTATION-SUBRESOURCE-COLLISION — unified annotation sub-resource: `TimeseriesAnnotationRest` + `VideoAnnotationRest` deleted; single `ReferenceAnnotationRest` at `/v2/references/{appId}/annotations`** | 2026-06-17 (fire-90). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-ANNOTATION-SUBRESOURCE-COLLISION. Two REST classes (`TimeseriesAnnotationRest` at `/v2/references/{appId}/annotations` and `VideoAnnotationRest` at `/v2/references/{refAppId}/annotations`) registered at the same JAX-RS path pattern, causing a silent path-collision in the same WAR — RESTEasy picked one and the other's routes were unreachable. Fix: extended `ReferenceKindHandler` SPI with 6 default annotation methods (`supportsAnnotations()` + 5 CRUD methods); implemented them in `TimeseriesReferenceKindHandler` and `VideoStreamReferenceKindHandler`; created a single `ReferenceAnnotationRest` at `@Path("/v2/references/{appId}/annotations")` that dispatches via the SPI; deleted both per-kind REST classes. No path change for callers (path was already `/v2/references/{appId}/annotations` for both); timeseries wire format (`startNs`/`endNs` Long) and video wire format (`startSeconds`/`endSeconds` Double) are unchanged. | **ZERO** — same HTTP path, same HTTP verbs, same response shapes. The collision is fixed transparently: both timeseries and video annotation CRUD now work correctly where previously only one kind's routes were reachable. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is unchanged. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** Both timeseries and video annotation CRUD paths now route correctly. If your integration was silently broken (receiving 404 on annotation ops for one kind), it will now work. |

Every other v2 list and data endpoint uses camelCase (`pageSize`, `windowSeconds`, `dataObjectAppId`, `shepardId`, …). Frontend composable `useFetchChannelPreview.ts` hard-coded `qs.set("max_points", ...)` to match. Fix: renamed both to `maxPoints` in a single coordinated backend+frontend PR. `@Operation` description updated. 1 reflection regression test added to `ContainersV2RestTest`. | **PRE-PROD WIRE BREAK** — `GET /v2/containers/{appId}/channels/{shepardId}/data?max_points=N` will silently ignore the parameter (JAX-RS drops unrecognised query params). Callers must use `?maxPoints=N`. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is **unchanged**. | — (no schema or data migration; Java + TS source changes only). | **No operator action required.** Pre-production surface. Update any direct REST caller passing `?downsample=lttb&max_points=N` to `?downsample=lttb&maxPoints=N`. The fork's own frontend composable (`useFetchChannelPreview.ts`) is updated in this PR. |
| **APISIMP-WIKI-WRITER-COLLECTION-IN-PATH — `POST /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects/{dataObjectAppId}/wiki-write` returns 410 Gone; new path at `POST /v2/data-objects/{dataObjectAppId}/wiki-write`** | 2026-06-18 (fire-104, PR pending). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-WIKI-WRITER-COLLECTION-IN-PATH. `WikiWriterRest` exposed the `{collectionAppId}` segment redundantly — `PermissionsService.isAccessAllowedForDataObjectAppId()` does not need it, and `WikiWriterService.wikiWrite()` can resolve the collection OGM id from `DataObjectDAO.findByAppId(dataObjectAppId).getCollection().getId()` internally. Simplification: path moved to `/v2/data-objects/{dataObjectAppId}/wiki-write` (one segment shorter, consistent with the DataObject-scoped resource family). `WikiWriterTombstoneRest` added at the old path returning 410 Gone with `Location: /v2/data-objects/{appId}/wiki-write`. `WikiWriterService.wikiWriteByDataObjectAppId(String, WikiWriteRequestIO)` overload added; `DataObjectDAO` injected into the service; `NotFoundException` thrown for unknown `dataObjectAppId`. New unit tests: `WikiWriterRestTest` (7 tests), `WikiWriterServiceTest` 2 new tests (`wikiWriteByDataObjectAppId_happyPath`, `wikiWriteByDataObjectAppId_notFound_throwsNotFoundException`). | **PRE-PROD WIRE BREAK** — `POST /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects/{dataObjectAppId}/wiki-write` now returns **410 Gone** with `Content-Type: application/problem+json` and a `Location` header. Callers must switch to `POST /v2/data-objects/{dataObjectAppId}/wiki-write` (drop the `/collections/{collectionAppId}` prefix segment). The upstream `/shepard/api/` surface is **unchanged**. | — (no Neo4j or other schema migration; Java source changes only). | **Action required for callers of the wiki-write endpoint.** Replace `POST /v2/collections/<colAppId>/data-objects/<doAppId>/wiki-write` with `POST /v2/data-objects/<doAppId>/wiki-write`. Request and response body shapes are unchanged. The 410 `Location` header in the response contains the exact new URL as a machine-readable redirect hint. |
| **APISIMP-FILE-PATH-RETIRE-2 — `GET\|PATCH\|DELETE /v2/files/{appId}`, `GET /v2/files/{appId}/content`, `GET /v2/files/by-data-object/{doAppId}` return 410 Gone; use `/v2/references/{appId}` equivalents** | 2026-06-17 (fire-99, PR #1978). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-FILE-PATH-RETIRE-2. After PR #1966 tombstoned `POST /v2/files`, the remaining 5 live CRUD endpoints on `FileReferenceV2Rest` were fully duplicated by `ReferencesV2Rest` + `FileReferenceKindHandler`. All 5 now return **410 Gone** with `application/problem+json` bodies pointing to the `/v2/references/` equivalents. Migration map: `GET /v2/files/{appId}` → `GET /v2/references/{appId}`; `GET /v2/files/{appId}/content` → `GET /v2/references/{appId}/content`; `PATCH /v2/files/{appId}` → `PATCH /v2/references/{appId}`; `DELETE /v2/files/{appId}` → `DELETE /v2/references/{appId}`; `GET /v2/files/by-data-object/{doAppId}` → `GET /v2/references?kind=file&dataObjectAppId={doAppId}`. All `@Inject` service fields removed from `FileReferenceV2Rest`; class is now a pure tombstone. The fork's own frontend call sites (`useUrdfReferenceBlob.ts`, `DataObjectNotebooksPane.vue`, `DataObjectDataReferencesTable.vue`) migrated to `/v2/references/{appId}/content` in the same PR. | **PRE-PROD WIRE BREAK** — all 5 `/v2/files/{appId}*` CRUD paths now return **410 Gone**. Any caller that previously read, patched, deleted, or fetched content from a singleton FileReference via the `/v2/files/` prefix must switch to the `/v2/references/` equivalents. The `POST /v2/files` path was already 410 (PR #1966). Together these retire the entire `/v2/files/` surface. The upstream `/shepard/api/...` surface is **unchanged**. | — (no Neo4j or MongoDB schema migration; Java + TS source changes only). | **Operator action required for direct v2 callers of the `/v2/files/` CRUD paths.** Migration per path: `GET /v2/files/{appId}` → `GET /v2/references/{appId}`; `GET /v2/files/{appId}/content` → `GET /v2/references/{appId}/content` (HTTP Range header preserved); `PATCH /v2/files/{appId}` → `PATCH /v2/references/{appId}` (same merge-patch body); `DELETE /v2/files/{appId}` → `DELETE /v2/references/{appId}`; `GET /v2/files/by-data-object/{doAppId}` → `GET /v2/references?kind=file&dataObjectAppId={doAppId}` (same `FileReferenceV2IO` response shape). The 410 `detail` field in each response body contains the migration URL as a machine-readable hint. |
| **APISIMP-PROVENANCE-STATS-ID-PARAM — `GET /v2/provenance/stats` query param renamed from `id` to `entityId`** | 2026-06-18 (fire-103). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-PROVENANCE-STATS-ID-PARAM. `ProvenanceRest.stats()` declared `@QueryParam("id") String id` — a bare `id` parameter serving as collection `appId` (scope=collection) or username (scope=user). Renamed to `@QueryParam("entityId")`. `@Parameter` description updated. Frontend composable `useFetchProvenanceStats.ts` `UseFetchProvenanceStatsOptions.id` → `entityId`; `ActivitySparklineCard.vue` call-site updated. 1 reflection regression test added to `ProvenanceRestStatsTest`. | **PRE-PROD WIRE BREAK on `GET /v2/provenance/stats`** — query param `?id=` no longer recognised; use `?entityId=` instead. Scopes: `?scope=collection&entityId={collectionAppId}` and `?scope=user&entityId={username}`. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is **unchanged**. | — (no schema or data migration; Java + TS source changes only). | **No operator action required.** Pre-production surface. Update any direct REST caller passing `?id=` to `?entityId=`. The fork's own frontend composable (`useFetchProvenanceStats.ts`) is updated in this PR. |

| **FTOGGLE-PROV-1 — runtime-mutable provenance config: `:ProvenanceConfig` singleton + `GET/PATCH /v2/admin/config/provenance`** | 2026-06-20 (fire-162). `aidocs/16` FTOGGLE-PROV-1. `ProvenanceCaptureFilter` and `ProvenanceRetentionJob` previously read `shepard.provenance.enabled`, `shepard.provenance.capture-reads`, and `shepard.provenance.retention-days` as static `@ConfigProperty` fields at startup (deploy-time-only). FTOGGLE-PROV-1 promotes all three to a runtime-mutable `:ProvenanceConfig` Neo4j singleton following the A3b / N1c2 / UH1a / P10c pattern (CLAUDE.md "Always: surface operator knobs in the admin config"). New entity `ProvenanceConfig` (`HasAppId`, single-instance); DAO `ProvenanceConfigDAO` (live-session pattern); service `ProvenanceConfigService` (seeds singleton from deploy-time defaults on startup, fail-soft); `ProvenanceConfigDescriptor` (`ConfigDescriptor<ProvenanceConfigIO>`) wired into the generic `GET|PATCH /v2/admin/config/provenance` surface (V2CONV-A4 ConfigRegistry). `ProvenanceCaptureFilter`: `@ConfigProperty captureReads` field removed; replaced by `provenanceConfigService.effectiveEnabled()` (line 120) and `provenanceConfigService.effectiveCaptureReads()` (line 128). `ProvenanceRetentionJob`: `@ConfigProperty provenanceEnabled` + `retentionDays` fields removed; replaced by service calls in `runNightly()` and `currentCutoffMillis()`. Migration V116: `CREATE CONSTRAINT appId_unique_ProvenanceConfig IF NOT EXISTS … REQUIRE n.appId IS UNIQUE` (idempotent) + rollback V116_R. 6 unit tests in `ProvenanceConfigDescriptorTest`. | **AWARE** | **No required action for upgrade.** Additive singleton only; no `/shepard/api/` surface touched. The three existing `shepard.provenance.*` deploy-time keys continue to work as install defaults — they seed the `:ProvenanceConfig` singleton on first restart. Runtime values win over deploy-time defaults per the standard precedence rule. New admin surface: `GET /v2/admin/config/provenance` + `PATCH /v2/admin/config/provenance` (instance-admin role required). V116 uniqueness constraint is safe to run on any DB (no prior `:ProvenanceConfig` nodes). | `V116__Add_appId_constraint_ProvenanceConfig.cypher` — idempotent constraint add. Rollback: `V116_R__Add_appId_constraint_ProvenanceConfig.cypher`. | **No action required for upgrade.** On restart, V116 applies automatically; `ProvenanceConfigService.seedIfNeeded()` mints the singleton from your existing `application.properties` values. To verify: `curl -fsS https://<host>/v2/admin/config/provenance -H 'Authorization: Bearer <admin-token>'` → `200 {"enabled":true,"captureReads":false,"retentionDays":730}` (values reflect your deploy-time config). To disable provenance at runtime: `curl -fsS -X PATCH https://<host>/v2/admin/config/provenance -H 'Authorization: Bearer <admin-token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"enabled":false}'`. To revert to deploy-time default: PATCH with `{"enabled":null}`. |
| **FTOGGLE-AUTOSWEEP-1 — new `GET|PATCH /v2/admin/config/autosweep` endpoint; `shepard.migration.auto-sweep.*` now seeds a runtime-mutable `:AutosweepConfig` singleton** | 2026-06-21 (fire-164). `aidocs/16` FTOGGLE-AUTOSWEEP-1. The three deploy-time properties (`shepard.migration.auto-sweep.enabled`, `.source`, `.target`) previously read directly by `FileMigrationService.autoSweep()` via `@ConfigProperty` now seed a `:AutosweepConfig` Neo4j singleton on first startup; the service reads effective values from `AutosweepConfigService` at runtime. Operators can flip `enabled`, `source`, and `target` live via `PATCH /v2/admin/config/autosweep` without a restart. The `interval` property stays deploy-time-only (drives `@Scheduled` which cannot change at runtime). `GET /v2/admin/config` now includes `{"feature":"autosweep",...}` in its listing. Migration V117 adds a unique constraint on `:AutosweepConfig.appId` (additive, no data change). | **ADDITIVE** — new `/v2/admin/config/autosweep` endpoint. Deploy-time config keys still work (they seed the singleton on first start). No existing path removed. No `/shepard/api/` surface touched. | V117 (additive unique constraint on `:AutosweepConfig.appId`; no data change). | **No operator action required.** Restart Shepard; the singleton is seeded from existing `application.properties` values. To read the current autosweep config: `curl -fsS https://<your-host>/v2/admin/config/autosweep -H 'Authorization: Bearer <instance-admin-token>'`. To enable autosweep at runtime (no restart needed): `curl -fsS -X PATCH https://<your-host>/v2/admin/config/autosweep -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"enabled":true,"source":"gridfs","target":"s3"}'`. Set a field to null to revert to the deploy-time default. |
| **APISIMP-SQL-CONTAINER-ID-NUMERIC — `POST /v2/sql/timeseries` body gains `container_app_id_in: List<String>` (UUID v7); `container_id_in: List<Long>` deprecated** | 2026-06-25 (fire-214, PR #2088). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-SQL-CONTAINER-ID-NUMERIC. `SqlQuerySpec.WhereClause.containerIdIn` (JSON `container_id_in: long[]`) was the only v2 request body field that exposed raw Neo4j OGM numeric node IDs to callers — violating the fork's "UUID v7 appId only on the v2 surface" contract. Fix (additive, backward-compatible): `WhereClause` gains `containerAppIdIn` (`container_app_id_in: string[]`, UUID v7); `container_id_in` kept for backward compat and annotated `@Schema(deprecated=true)`. `SqlTimeseriesRest.executeQuery()` resolves appIds to Neo4j longs internally via `EntityIdResolver.resolveLong()`; unknown appIds silently excluded (same policy as permission filter). When both fields are present, `container_app_id_in` takes precedence. `resolveAppIds()` is package-private for unit testing. `StubEntityIdResolver` added to `SqlTimeseriesPermissionTest` with 4 new test cases. | **ADDITIVE** — `container_app_id_in` field is new and additive. Existing callers passing `container_id_in: [long]` continue to work unchanged (backward compat). New callers should prefer `container_app_id_in: ["uuid-v7-string"]`. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. No schema migration. | — (no Neo4j / Postgres schema change; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** The `POST /v2/sql/timeseries` endpoint accepts both `container_id_in` (deprecated, numeric, still works) and `container_app_id_in` (preferred, UUID v7). Migrate callers to use `container_app_id_in` at your own pace. The numeric field will be removed in a future breaking release once no callers remain. |
| **APISIMP-TSCHANNEL-INT-ID-DEPRECATE — `TimeseriesChannelV2IO.int id` marked `@Schema(deprecated=true)` in OpenAPI** | 2026-06-25 (fire-218, PR #2089). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-TSCHANNEL-INT-ID-DEPRECATE. The `int id` field in `TimeseriesChannelV2IO` (the `/v2/timeseries-containers/{id}/channels` response shape) was missing an OpenAPI deprecation marker, implying to generated clients that the numeric Postgres serial is a stable channel handle. Fix: added `@Schema(deprecated=true, description="DEPRECATED: legacy numeric channel id (Postgres serial). Use shepardId instead.")` on the backing field; wire shape is **unchanged** (field stays in the response for backward compat). Added reflection regression test in `TimeseriesChannelV2IOTest`. | **ZERO** — no wire shape change, no endpoint change, no `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. OpenAPI-only metadata change. | — (no schema migration; Java annotation only). | **No operator action required.** Generated clients that consume the OpenAPI v2 spec will now see `id` flagged as deprecated and should prefer `shepardId` (UUID v7) as the stable channel identity. |
| **STORAGE-SPI-UNIFY-1 — file + video byte writes/reads route exclusively through the `FileStorage` SPI (no "magic routes")** | 2026-06-24. `aidocs/16` STORAGE-SPI-UNIFY-1 / STORAGE-NO-MAGIC-ROUTES-1. Two paths previously bypassed `FileStorageRegistry.activeStorage()` and hardcoded GridFS: the singleton-FileReference + bundle-upload paths called `FileService.createFile` directly, and the video plugin's `VideoStreamReferenceService` injected its own `MongoDatabase` to run a GridFS-specific MD5 dedup query. With `shepard.storage.provider=s3` active, those bytes still landed in MongoDB GridFS instead of the configured S3 backend. Fix: new core `de.dlr.shepard.storage.FileStorageService` is the single registry-routed choke point (`storeFile`/`getPayload`/`deleteFile`, resolving the provider per stored row so existing GridFS content keeps reading after a provider flip); `SingletonFileReferenceService`, `FileBundleReferenceRest`, and the thermography plugin's read path migrated to it; the video dedup query was removed so every video byte routes through `activeStorage().put`. Internal storage-routing change only — no REST path, wire shape, schema, or config key added or removed. | **ZERO (internal routing)** — no `/shepard/api/` or `/v2/` path, wire shape, or config key touched. Behaviour is identical when `shepard.storage.provider=gridfs` (the default). The only observable change is the *intended* one: with `provider=s3`, file/video/thumbnail uploads now actually land in S3 (previously a silent no-op for these two paths). | — (no Neo4j / Mongo / Postgres schema migration; Java source routing change only). | **No operator action required.** Default (`gridfs`) deployments are byte-identical. Operators who had set `shepard.storage.provider=s3` will now see new file/video uploads land in their S3 bucket (as intended) instead of GridFS. Existing GridFS-stored content continues to read correctly (per-row `providerId` routing; V79 backfill stamps `gridfs` on legacy rows). To move pre-existing GridFS content to S3, use the FS1e `FileMigrationService` (`POST /v2/admin/files/migrate`) — tracked as STORAGE-GRIDFS-MIGRATE-1. Note: the GridFS-only video MD5 dedup optimization was dropped; identical re-uploads now store a fresh object. |
| **APISIMP-PROJECTS-LIST-NO-PAGINATION — `GET /v2/projects` now accepts optional `page`/`pageSize` (1–200) with `X-Total-Count` header** | 2026-06-25 (fire-219). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-PROJECTS-LIST-NO-PAGINATION. `ProjectsRest.list()` returned an unbounded `List<String>` of all project appIds with no pagination cap. Fix: optional `@QueryParam("page") Integer page` and `@QueryParam("pageSize") Integer pageSize` (server cap 200); in-memory slice; `X-Total-Count` response header. 5 new unit tests (17 total). Wire shape backward-compatible: callers not passing params receive all projects as before. | **ADDITIVE** — new optional query params; existing callers omitting params receive the full list unchanged. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. | — (no schema migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** Optionally paginate: `GET /v2/projects?page=0&pageSize=50` returns first 50 project appIds; `X-Total-Count` header carries the total. |

Wire shape backward-compatible: callers omitting params receive all watches as before. | **ADDITIVE** — new optional query params; existing callers omitting params receive the full list unchanged. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. | — (no schema migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** Optionally paginate: `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/watched-containers?page=0&pageSize=50` returns first 50 watches; `X-Total-Count` header carries the total. |

Wire shape backward-compatible: callers omitting params receive all watchers as before. | **ADDITIVE** — new optional query params; existing callers omitting params receive the full list unchanged. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. | — (no schema migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** Optionally paginate: `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/watches?page=0&pageSize=50` returns first 50 watchers; `X-Total-Count` header carries the total. |
