| **APISIMP-PAGINATION-LIST-GIT-SOURCES — `GET /v2/admin/semantic/git-sources` now accepts optional `page`/`pageSize` (1–200) with `X-Total-Count` header** | 2026-06-25 (fire-224). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-PAGINATION-LIST-GIT-SOURCES. PR #2094. `OntologyGitSourceRest.list()` returned the full `List<OntologyGitSourceIO>` with no pagination cap — the last unbounded admin list endpoint in the APISIMP sweep. Fix: optional `@QueryParam("page") Integer page` and `@QueryParam("pageSize") Integer pageSize` (server cap 200); in-memory slice applied at REST layer (preserving `listAll()` for the nightly ingest scheduler); `X-Total-Count` response header. 5 new unit tests in `OntologyGitSourceRestTest`. Wire shape backward-compatible: callers omitting params receive all sources as before. | **ADDITIVE** — new optional query params; existing callers omitting params receive the full list unchanged. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. | — (no schema migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** Optionally paginate: `GET /v2/admin/semantic/git-sources?page=0&pageSize=20` returns first 20 git sources; `X-Total-Count` header carries the total. |
| **APISIMP-PUBLICATIONS-KIND-PATH-SEGMENT — new `GET /v2/publications?entityAppId={appId}` flat alias; `GET /v2/{kind}/{appId}/publications` deprecated in OpenAPI** | 2026-06-25 (fire-225). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-PUBLICATIONS-KIND-PATH-SEGMENT. `PublicationsListRest` was mounted at `/v2/{kind}/{appId}/publications` requiring callers to know the entity-kind URL segment, breaking discoverability from an `appId` alone. Fix: new `FlatPublicationsRest` class at `GET /v2/publications?entityAppId={appId}` resolves entity kind internally via the kind-agnostic `EntityIdResolver.resolveLong(appId)` — same permission semantics (Read access on entity). Existing `/v2/{kind}/{appId}/publications` kept for backward compat; marked `deprecated = true` in `@Operation`. 9 new unit tests in `FlatPublicationsRestTest`. | **ADDITIVE** — new endpoint; existing `/v2/{kind}/{appId}/publications` untouched at wire level. OpenAPI `deprecated=true` is informational only. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. | — (no schema migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** Update API clients to use `GET /v2/publications?entityAppId=<appId>` — no kind segment needed. The kind-parameterised path continues to work. |
| **MISSING-V2-APPID-IN-SEARCH — new `GET /v2/search?q={query}` + frontend migration to v2** | 2026-06-25 (fire-226 backend; fire-227 frontend). `aidocs/16` MISSING-V2-APPID-IN-SEARCH. Backend slice 1 (fire-226, PR #2096): `SearchV2Rest` at `GET /v2/search`; `SearchV2ItemIO` (appId, name, kind, parentCollectionAppId). Fire-227 adds `parentCollectionAppId` field to `SearchV2ItemIO` (resolves owning collection appId for dataobject results so frontend needs no secondary GET); updates `useGlobalSearch.ts` to call `GET /v2/search` via `useV2ShepardApi(SearchV2Api)` for collections + dataobjects; `HeaderBar.vue` drops `useCollectionAppIdResolver` from the search navigation path; `SearchV2Api` + models added to `backend-client`. Container search remains v1 (V1-EXCEPTION: no `/v2/containers/search` endpoint yet). | **ADDITIVE** — new endpoint + new frontend composable wiring; existing `POST /shepard/api/search` untouched. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. | — (no schema migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** |
| **APISIMP-PAGINATION-PARAM-STYLE — all remaining v2 list `page`/`pageSize` params converted to validated non-null primitives** | 2026-06-26 (PR #2101 → fdf32556). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-PAGINATION-PARAM-STYLE. `ContainersV2Rest.list()` and `CollectionLabJournalEntriesRest.list()` used nullable `Integer page`/`Integer pageSize` with no `@DefaultValue` or validation — silently accepting negative values. Converted to `@DefaultValue("0") @PositiveOrZero int page` / `@DefaultValue("50") @Min(1) @Max(200) int pageSize` on both endpoints; service layer updated to accept non-null primitives. | **ADDITIVE** — validation only; callers supplying `?page=-1` or `?pageSize=0` now receive HTTP 400 instead of a miscomputed result. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. | — (Java source change only). | **No operator action required.** Update scripts passing `?page=-1` or `?pageSize=0`. |
| **APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE-1 — all shape-B v2 list endpoints now return `{items, total, page, pageSize}` envelope; `X-Total-Count` header kept in parallel** | 2026-06-26 (PR #2102 → dd051adc). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE. Three pagination response shapes coexisted across v2 list endpoints. This PR standardises shape-B endpoints (8 REST resources) onto a new shared `PagedResponseIO<T>` record (`de.dlr.shepard.v2.common.io.PagedResponseIO`) containing `{items: List<T>, total: long, page: int, pageSize: int}`. Affected endpoints: `GET /v2/containers` (ContainersV2Rest), `GET /v2/notifications` (NotificationRest), `GET /v2/projects` (ProjectsRest), `GET /v2/collections/{id}/watched-containers` (CollectionWatchesRest), `GET /v2/collections/{id}/watches` (CollectionWatchersRest), `GET /v2/collections/{id}/dqr` (CollectionDQRRest), `GET /v2/admin/semantic/git-sources` (OntologyGitSourceRest), `GET /v2/collections/{id}/lab-journal` (CollectionLabJournalEntriesRest). The `X-Total-Count` response header is **kept in parallel** during the deprecation window — existing clients reading the header continue to work. Shape-C endpoints (plain array, no total) tracked in APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE-2. | **WIRE-SHAPE CHANGE** — response body changes from a JSON array to a JSON object `{items:[…], total:N, page:0, pageSize:50}` for the 8 listed endpoints. `X-Total-Count` header remains. Clients that deserialise the body as a raw array **will break**; clients that read only the header remain unaffected. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. | — (no Neo4j / Postgres / Mongo schema migration; Java source changes only). | **Action required for clients reading the v2 list body as an array.** Update to read `response.body.items` (the array) and optionally `response.body.total`. Clients reading only the `X-Total-Count` header need no change during the deprecation window. New shared record: `de.dlr.shepard.v2.common.io.PagedResponseIO`. |
| **APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE-2-1 — `GET /v2/collections` now returns `{items, total, page, pageSize}` envelope; `X-Total-Count` header kept in parallel** | 2026-06-26 (branch APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE-2-1). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE-2. `CollectionV2Rest.list()` was a shape-C endpoint (plain array, no total). Migrated to `PagedResponseIO<CollectionV2IO>` using in-memory pagination (load all user-visible collections, slice by `page`/`pageSize`). `X-Total-Count` header kept during deprecation window. Annotation list endpoints (`SemanticAnnotationV2Rest.list()`, `ReferenceAnnotationRest.list()`) + frontend composable migration in APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE-2-2. | **WIRE-SHAPE CHANGE** — `GET /v2/collections` response body changes from a JSON array to `{items:[…], total:N, page:0, pageSize:50}`. `X-Total-Count` header remains. Clients deserialising the body as a raw array must update to `response.body.items`. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. | — (no schema migration). | **Action required for clients reading `/v2/collections` body as an array.** Update to `response.body.items`. |
| **APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE-2-2 — `GET /v2/annotations` and `GET /v2/annotations/find` now return `{items, total, page, pageSize}` envelope** | 2026-06-26 (PR #2104 → 43cc4d8d, fire-236). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE-2-2. `SemanticAnnotationV2Rest.list()` and `find()` were shape-C endpoints (plain array, no total). Migrated to `PagedResponseIO<AnnotationIO>`. New DAO methods `countFiltered(...)` and `countTextSearch(...)` added to `SemanticAnnotationV2DAO` using `session.query(...).queryResults()` pattern. `backend-client` updated with `PagedResponseAnnotationV2` model; `frontend/composables/annotated.ts` updated to read `page.items`. | **WIRE-SHAPE CHANGE** — `GET /v2/annotations` and `GET /v2/annotations/find` response body changes from a JSON array to `{items:[…], total:N, page:0, pageSize:50}`. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. | — (no schema migration). | **Action required for clients reading annotation list endpoints as raw arrays.** Update to `response.body.items`. |
| **APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE-2-3 — `GET /v2/references/{appId}/annotations` now returns `{items, total, page, pageSize}` envelope; FE composables updated** | 2026-06-26 (PR #2105 → 715748eb4, fire-236). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE-2-3. `ReferenceAnnotationRest.list()` was a shape-C endpoint (plain array, no total). Wrapped at REST layer in `PagedResponseIO<Map<String,Object>>(rows, rows.size(), 0, rows.size())` — no SPI change, `ReferenceKindHandler.listAnnotations()` still returns `List<Map<String,Object>>`. `useTimeseriesReferenceAnnotations.ts` and `useFetchVideoAnnotations.ts` updated to read `page.items ?? page` defensively (fallback for old-backend compatibility). | **WIRE-SHAPE CHANGE** — `GET /v2/references/{appId}/annotations` response body changes from a JSON array to `{items:[…], total:N, page:0, pageSize:N}`. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. | — (no schema migration; no SPI change). | **Action required for clients reading reference annotation list as a raw array.** Update to `response.body.items`. |
| **P21-V2-METADATA-EDIT — `PUT /v2/containers/{appId}` + `PUT /v2/references/{appId}` — full-replace editable metadata + frontend rename dialog** | 2026-06-26 (PR #2113 slice 1 merged; slice 2 in PR). `aidocs/16` P21-V2-METADATA-EDIT. Adds two new endpoints on the unified v2 surfaces: `PUT /v2/containers/{appId}` — full-replace of all mutable container metadata (`name` required; `status` applied from body, null/cleared when absent); `PUT /v2/references/{appId}` — full-replace of all mutable reference metadata (`name` required; kind-specific mutable fields delegated to handler's `put()` default method). Both endpoints are distinct from: (containers) the existing `PATCH /v2/containers/{appId}` (RFC 7396 merge-patch, absent fields unchanged); (references) the existing `PUT /v2/references/{appId}/content` (binary content upload, different path). `ReferenceKindHandler` SPI gains a `default put()` method delegating to `patch()` — zero existing handler changes required. `ContainersV2Service` gains `putByAppId()` that normalises the body to always carry `name` + `status` before delegating to the handler's `patch()` — so absent `status` is cleared. `ReferencesV2Service` gains `putByAppId()`. The frozen `/shepard/api/...` v1 surface (FileRest, StructuredDataRest, TimeseriesRest, all `*ReferenceRest`) is **unchanged** — they remain create-only-then-delete per upstream byte-compat policy. | **ADDITIVE** — two new `/v2/` endpoints; no existing endpoint changes; no v1 surface touched. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** Restart shepard; the endpoints are live immediately. To rename a container: `curl -fsS -X PUT https://<host>/v2/containers/<appId> -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"name":"new-name","status":"READY"}'` → 200 `ContainerV2IO`. To clear a container's status: omit `status` from the body — `curl … -d '{"name":"current-name"}'` — the PUT resets `status` to null. To rename a reference: `curl -fsS -X PUT https://<host>/v2/references/<appId> -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"name":"new-name"}'` → 200 `ReferenceV2IO`. |
| **APISIMP-UNBOUNDED-FILEGROUPS — `GET /v2/bundles/{appId}/groups` response wrapped in `PagedResponseIO` envelope; `?page`/`?pageSize` (1–200, default 50) added; `FileGroupDAO` gains `findByBundleAppId(appId, page, pageSize)` + `countByBundleAppId(appId)` overloads** | 2026-06-28 (fire-279, PR #2145). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-UNBOUNDED-FILEGROUPS. `FileBundleReferenceRest.listGroups()` returned an unbounded `List<FileGroupIO>` with no count or pagination. Fix: new `countByBundleAppId(String bundleAppId): long` DAO method using inline Cypher `count(g)`; new `findByBundleAppId(String bundleAppId, int page, int pageSize)` DAO overload (SKIP/LIMIT); `FileGroupService` gains matching `listGroups(bundleAppId, page, pageSize)` and `countGroups(bundleAppId)` delegates; REST layer adds `@DefaultValue`/`@Min`/`@Max` validated `page` and `pageSize` query params and wraps response in `PagedResponseIO<FileGroupIO>`. 4 JUnit tests updated (paged-envelope assertions, empty-page case, 404 and 403 branches). | **WIRE-SHAPE CHANGE** — `GET /v2/bundles/{appId}/groups` response body changes from a JSON array `[{…},…]` to a JSON object `{items:[{…},…], total:N, page:0, pageSize:50}`. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is **unchanged**. Clients that deserialise the body as a raw array **will break**. | — (no Neo4j schema migration; Java source changes only). The new `countByBundleAppId()` Cypher uses `count(g)` on the existing `HAS_GROUP` relationship — read-only, no new indexes needed. | **Action required for clients reading `GET /v2/bundles/{appId}/groups` body as a raw array.** Update to read `response.body.items` (the `FileGroupIO[]` array) and optionally `response.body.total`, `response.body.page`, `response.body.pageSize`. Pass `?pageSize=200` to retrieve all groups in one call for bundles with fewer than 200 groups. |
| **APISIMP-TEMPLATES-LIST-UNBOUNDED — `GET /v2/templates` response wrapped in `PagedResponseIO` envelope; `?page`/`?pageSize` (1–200, default 50) added; `ShepardTemplateDAO` gains `count()` + paginated 4-arg `list()` overload** | 2026-06-28 (fire-277, PR #2143). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-TEMPLATES-LIST-UNBOUNDED. `ShepardTemplateRest.list()` returned an unbounded `Array<ShepardTemplateIO>` with no count or pagination. Fix: new `count(String kind, boolean includeRetired)` DAO method using inline Cypher; new 4-arg `list(String kind, boolean includeRetired, int page, int pageSize)` DAO overload (SKIP/LIMIT); REST layer adds `@DefaultValue`/`@Min`/`@Max` validated `page` and `pageSize` query params; response body changed from plain array to `PagedResponseIO<ShepardTemplateIO>`. `backend-client` updated: `PagedResponseShepardTemplate` model added; `TemplatesApi.listTemplates()` return type changed to `Promise<PagedResponseShepardTemplate>`. 5 frontend callers migrated to `page.items`: `useFetchTemplates.ts`, `CreateCollectionDialog.vue`, `MyTemplatesPane.vue`, `TemplateAutocomplete.vue`, `TemplateShapeEditor.vue`. Raw-fetch callers use `data?.items ?? data` defensive fallback. 6 new JUnit tests (`listPageEnvelopeFieldsAreCorrect`, `listPage1CallsCorrectDaoParams`, `listKindFilterPassedToDao`; existing 4 tests updated to 5-arg signature + `PagedResponseIO` assertions). The existing 2-arg `list(String, boolean)` overload kept for 3 non-paginated internal callers. | **WIRE-SHAPE CHANGE** — `GET /v2/templates` response body changes from a JSON array `[{…},…]` to a JSON object `{items:[{…},…], total:N, page:0, pageSize:50}`. The `?kind=` and `?includeRetired=` query params are unchanged. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is **unchanged**. Clients that deserialise the body as a raw array **will break**. | — (no Neo4j schema migration; Java source changes only). The `ShepardTemplateDAO.count()` Cypher uses `MATCH (t:ShepardTemplate) WHERE … RETURN count(t)` — read-only, no new indexes needed. | **Action required for clients reading `GET /v2/templates` body as a raw array.** Update to read `response.body.items` (the `ShepardTemplate[]` array) and optionally `response.body.total`, `response.body.page`, `response.body.pageSize`. Clients using the `pageSize=200` shortcut (the fork's own FE composables) will get all templates back as a single page — no functional change, just a body-shape change. |
| **TS-CONFLICT-POLICY-1 — timeseries point-write defaults from silent last-wins to reject-on-duplicate; explicit `overwrite=true` in the service layer restores the old behaviour** | 2026-06-27 (fire-250, PR pending). `aidocs/16` TS-CONFLICT-POLICY-1. New `ConflictException` (HTTP 409) added. `TimeseriesDataPointRepository.insertManyDataPoints(…, boolean overwrite)`: when `overwrite=false` uses `ON CONFLICT (timeseries_id, time) DO NOTHING` and throws `ConflictException` when `affected < submitted`; when `overwrite=true` (legacy path) retains the existing `ON CONFLICT … DO UPDATE SET` behaviour. Old 3-arg `TimeseriesService.saveDataPoints(containerId, ts, points)` delegates to `overwrite=true` — **v1 `/shepard/api/…/payload` behaviour is byte-identical to upstream 5.2.0**. The new 4-arg overload `saveDataPoints(…, overwrite)` is the point at which v2 ingest callers can plumb the flag. 3 new `@QuarkusTest` cases in `TimeseriesServiceQuarkusTest`. No schema migration — the PostgreSQL conflict clause is a query-level change only. | **ZERO for the v1 surface** (byte-identical to upstream for all `/shepard/api/…/payload` writes). Any v2 caller invoking `saveDataPoints(…, overwrite=false)` directly will get 409 on duplicate `(channel, timestamp)` pairs. | — (no schema or data migration; SQL conflict clause is a string constant in `buildInsertQueryObject`; no Flyway script needed). | **No action required for v1 callers.** V1 `POST /shepard/api/timeseriesContainers/{id}/payload` behaviour is unchanged — `saveDataPoints(containerId, ts, points)` (3-arg) delegates to `overwrite=true`. V2 feature callers (e.g. KRL interpret, SVDX CSV ingest) that invoke the new 4-arg overload with `overwrite=false` will receive a `ConflictException` (HTTP 409) on duplicate rows; pass `overwrite=true` to restore the old silent-overwrite semantics. |
