| **APISIMP-AAS-SHELLS-LIST-ENVELOPE + APISIMP-AAS-SUBMODELS-UNBOUNDED — `GET /v2/aas/shells` and `GET /v2/aas/shells/{aasId}/submodels` wrapped in `PagedResponseIO` envelope; count queries added; submodels endpoint gains `?page`/`?pageSize`** | 2026-06-28 (fire-281, branch APISIMP-AAS-SHELLS-PAGINATION). `aidocs/16` rows 3826–3827. `AasShellsRest.listShells()` returned a plain `List<AasShellIO>` despite accepting `?page`/`?pageSize` params — no `total`, no page echo. `AasShellsRest.listSubmodels()` was unbounded (no pagination params, no cap). Fixes: (1) `CollectionDAO.countAllCollectionsByShepardId(String username)` — new Cypher `COUNT(c)` query mirroring `findAllCollectionsByShepardId`'s WHERE clause; (2) `DataObjectDAO.countTopLevelByCollectionAppId(String appId)` — new count overload; (3) `DataObjectDAO.findTopLevelByCollectionAppId(String appId, int page, int pageSize)` — new SKIP/LIMIT paginated overload; (4) `listShells()` signature changed from nullable `Integer page` to primitive `int page` with `@DefaultValue("0")`; response wrapped in `PagedResponseIO<AasShellIO>`; (5) `listSubmodels()` gains `@DefaultValue("0") int page` + `@DefaultValue("50") int pageSize`; response wrapped in `PagedResponseIO<AasReferenceIO>`. Runtime clamping: `safeSize = Math.min(Math.max(pageSize, 1), 200)`. 7 new/updated tests in `AasShellsRestTest`. | **WIRE-SHAPE CHANGE** — `GET /v2/aas/shells` response body changes from a JSON array `[{…},…]` to `{items:[{…},…], total:N, page:0, pageSize:50}`. `GET /v2/aas/shells/{aasId}/submodels` response body changes from a JSON array to the same envelope shape. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is **unchanged**. | — (no Neo4j schema migration; Java source changes only). The new `countAllCollectionsByShepardId` and `countTopLevelByCollectionAppId` Cypher queries use `COUNT(c)` / `COUNT(d)` — read-only, no new indexes needed. | **Action required for AAS clients reading `GET /v2/aas/shells` or `GET /v2/aas/shells/{aasId}/submodels` body as a raw array.** Update to read `response.body.items`. Pass `?pageSize=200` to retrieve up to 200 shells/submodels in one call. |

| **APISIMP-ANNOTATION-MERGE-PATCH-HTTP — `PATCH /v2/annotations/{appId}` (was `PUT`); method-level `@Consumes({"application/merge-patch+json", …})` added** | 2026-06-30 (fire-315, branch `APISIMP-ANNOTATION-MERGE-PATCH-HTTP`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-ANNOTATION-MERGE-PATCH-HTTP. `SemanticAnnotationV2Rest.update()` was annotated `@PUT` despite implementing RFC 7396 merge-patch semantics (null-guarded field assignments, explicit `// Apply merge-patch` comment). Changed to `@PATCH` (RFC 5789); method-level `@Consumes({"application/merge-patch+json", MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON})` added so OpenAPI correctly signals the merge-patch media type. Internal activity-recording string updated from `"PUT /v2/annotations/…"` to `"PATCH /v2/annotations/…"`. 2 new reflection tests verify `@PATCH` present / `@PUT` absent / `@Consumes` carries `"application/merge-patch+json"`. The `/shepard/api/` v1 annotation surface is **unchanged**. | **AWARE — PRE-PROD HTTP-METHOD BREAK on `PUT /v2/annotations/{appId}`** — `PUT /v2/annotations/{appId}` now returns `405 Method Not Allowed`; callers must switch to `PATCH`. The wire body shape (`UpdateAnnotationIO`) and the merge-patch field semantics are **unchanged** — only the HTTP method and `Content-Type` hint change. The `/shepard/api/` v1 annotation surface is not touched. | — (no Neo4j or Postgres schema migration; Java source changes only). | **Migrate callers of `PUT /v2/annotations/{appId}` to `PATCH /v2/annotations/{appId}`.** The request body (`UpdateAnnotationIO`) and response shape are identical. Optionally send `Content-Type: application/merge-patch+json` (the API also accepts `application/json` for untyped clients). To verify: `curl -fsS -X PATCH https://<host>/v2/annotations/<appId> -H 'Content-Type: application/merge-patch+json' -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -d '{"validUntilMillis":null}'` → 200. A `PUT` to the same path → 405. |

| **VID-FFMPEG-TRANSCODE-2026-06-29 (PR-2a) — `TranscodingProvider` SPI + `LocalFfmpegTranscodingProvider` (default) + on-upload h.264 proxy + `proxyOid`/`proxyAvailable`/`proxyStatus` on the video reference wire IO** | 2026-06-29. `aidocs/16` VID-FFMPEG-TRANSCODE-2026-06-29. New SPI package `de.dlr.shepard.spi.transcode` in backend (`TranscodingProvider`, `TranscodeRequest`, `TranscodeResult`, `TranscodingProviderRegistry`). Plugin-video ships `LocalFfmpegTranscodingProvider` (`id="ffmpeg-local"`) — shells out to the ffmpeg binary already baked into the backend image (Dockerfile lines 45-65, BtbN static build). The sidecar shape mooted in the original backlog row is NOT shipped: ffmpeg is in-container per the existing `VideoProbeService` precedent, so the SPI's local/remote split satisfies the architectural intent without the extra service. New CDI singleton `VideoTranscodeOrchestrator` submits a fire-and-forget job to a daemon thread pool (default 2 workers) after `VideoStreamReferenceService.attachBytes()` / `.create()` commits the bytes to FileStorage — produces a `<name>_proxy.mp4` h.264/yuv420p/+faststart proxy in the SAME container as the source via `FileStorage.put()` (no-magic-routes). Two additive nullable Neo4j properties on `:VideoStreamReference`: `proxyStorageLocator` (opaque `"providerId:locator"` for the proxy bytes) and `proxyStatus` (`PENDING`/`READY`/`FAILED`/null). v2 ref wire IO surfaces three new fields: `proxyOid` (the storage-locator string), `proxyAvailable` (boolean — true iff status is READY and locator non-null), `proxyStatus` (the lifecycle string). Secondary-writes-fire-and-forget posture: a missing provider / missing storage / non-zero ffmpeg exit / timeout all log WARN and leave the upload unaffected. Six new `shepard.plugins.video.transcode.*` config properties (deploy-time defaults). 4 new test classes (registry, provider command-builder + helpers, orchestrator gating + filename helpers, status enum) + extended kind-handler test for the new IO fields + existing service test updated to mock the orchestrator. | **ZERO** — additive only. Pre-feature `:VideoStreamReference` rows have null `proxyStorageLocator` and `proxyStatus` and are reported as `proxyAvailable=false` by the wire IO — the read path treats this identically to a freshly-uploaded ref that hasn't finished encoding yet. The v1 `VideoStreamReferenceIO` wire shape is unchanged. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is unchanged. New deploy-time keys (`shepard.plugins.video.transcode.enabled=true`, `.transcoder=ffmpeg-local`, `.workers=2`, `.bitrate-kbps=3000`, `.max-height=1080`, `.timeout-seconds=3600`, `shepard.plugins.video.ffmpeg.binary=ffmpeg`) are all opt-out (set `.enabled=false` to suppress). | — (no Neo4j or Postgres schema migration — additive nullable properties on `:VideoStreamReference` per the additive-nullable rule; pre-feature rows transparently carry null). | **No required action for upgrade.** Pull and restart. New uploads automatically transcode; existing video references retain their original bytes and report `proxyAvailable=false` until they are re-uploaded (the backfill admin endpoint that re-runs transcoding on existing refs is queued as a PR-2c follow-up — see `aidocs/16` VID-FFMPEG-TRANSCODE-BACKFILL). To disable on a deployment that can't afford the encode CPU: `shepard.plugins.video.transcode.enabled=false`. To raise concurrency: `shepard.plugins.video.transcode.workers=N`. To swap encoder profiles (e.g. NVENC on a GPU host): drop a `ffmpeg-nvenc` binary into `/usr/local/bin/`, set `shepard.plugins.video.ffmpeg.binary=ffmpeg-nvenc`. **PR-2b (the `VideoPlaybackShape` view-recipe + frontend `VideoPlayer` switch to `proxyOid`) is sliced and queued separately** — PR-2a alone is harmless: the frontend keeps reading the original bytes and ignores the new fields. |

| **APISIMP-PROV-ISO8601-TIMESTAMPS — `since`/`until` params on `/v2/provenance/*` now accept ISO 8601 instants in addition to epoch-ms** | 2026-06-30 (fire-313, branch `APISIMP-PROV-ISO8601-TIMESTAMPS-1`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-PROV-ISO8601-TIMESTAMPS. All 9 `ProvenanceRest` endpoints (`listActivities`, `listActivitiesProvJson`, `listActivitiesJsonLd`, `listEntityActivities`, `listEntityActivitiesProvJson`, `listEntityActivitiesJsonLd`, `countActivities`, `countActivitiesJsonLd`, `stats`) previously declared `@QueryParam("since") Long` / `@QueryParam("until") Long` forcing callers to pass epoch-ms. These now accept `@QueryParam("since") String` / `@QueryParam("until") String` and resolve via a new static `parseTimestamp(String)` helper: values containing `T` → `Instant.parse()` (ISO 8601 instant); purely numeric values → `Long.parseLong()` (epoch-ms); null/blank → null (no filter); anything else → HTTP 400 `application/problem+json`. `@Parameter` descriptions updated to document both formats. 11-test `ProvenanceRestTimestampTest` regression suite added. | **ADDITIVE** — epoch-ms callers are unchanged (no `T` in a pure integer string). New ISO 8601 format available immediately after upgrade. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is untouched. | — (no schema migration; source-only change). | **No operator action required.** Existing callers passing `?since=1767225600000` continue to work unchanged. Callers may now also pass `?since=2026-01-01T00:00:00Z`. Malformed values now return 400 instead of the previous runtime exception; callers that expected a 5xx on bad input must handle 400. |

| **APISIMP-COLLECTION-CREATE-LONG-INPUT — `POST /v2/collections` and `PATCH /v2/collections/{appId}` input schemas no longer expose `defaultFileContainerId: Long`; `defaultFileContainerAppId: String` is now the writable v2 input field** | 2026-07-01 (fire-346, branch `APISIMP-COLLECTION-CREATE-LONG-INPUT`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-COLLECTION-CREATE-LONG-INPUT. `CollectionIO` (the shared v1/v2 IO class) exposes `defaultFileContainerId: Long` as an accepted input field — a raw Neo4j node id visible in the generated v2 OpenAPI input schema. `defaultFileContainerAppId: String` existed on `CollectionIO` but was marked `readOnly = true` (documentation-only hint) so v2 callers had no official path to set the FileContainer via appId. Fix: new `CreateCollectionV2IO` class (v2 POST input body — no `defaultFileContainerId` field; `defaultFileContainerAppId` writable) and `UpdateCollectionV2IO` class (v2 PATCH documentation target). `CollectionV2Rest.create()` now accepts `CreateCollectionV2IO`, translates it to `CollectionIO`, and delegates to `CollectionService`. `CollectionV2Rest`'s PATCH `@RequestBody` schema updated to `UpdateCollectionV2IO`. `CollectionService.createCollection()` and `updateCollectionByShepardId()` resolve the FileContainer via `FileContainerService.getContainerByAppId(String)` when `defaultFileContainerAppId` is non-null (with `defaultFileContainerId` as backward-compat fallback for v1 callers). The `CollectionIO` class itself is NOT modified — v1 `/shepard/api/collections` surface is byte-identical. New test `createWithDefaultFileContainerAppId_translatesFieldToCollectionIO` verifies the translation. | **INPUT-SCHEMA CHANGE on `POST /v2/collections`** — the OpenAPI request schema now uses `CreateCollectionV2IO` which does not include `defaultFileContainerId: Long`. Callers that were passing `defaultFileContainerId` on the v2 POST endpoint must migrate to `defaultFileContainerAppId: String`. The service layer retains a backward-compat fallback (checks appId first, then Long id), so existing deployments sending the Long field will continue to function at runtime; only the documented input schema changes. The `/shepard/api/collections` v1 surface is **unchanged**. The GET response shape is unchanged. | — (no Neo4j or Postgres schema migration; Java source changes only). | **Action required for v2 callers passing `defaultFileContainerId` on `POST /v2/collections` or `PATCH /v2/collections/{appId}`.** Migrate to `defaultFileContainerAppId` (UUID v7 string). Look up the FileContainer's `appId` from `GET /v2/file-containers/{id}` or `GET /v2/collections/{collectionId}/file-container` if you only have a numeric id. The runtime fallback will keep existing callers working through the transition window. |

| **SEMANTIC-ANNOTATE-BULK-REST-1 — `POST /v2/annotations/bulk` — bulk-create up to 100 semantic annotations in one round-trip** | 2026-07-01 (fire-350, branch `SEMANTIC-ANNOTATE-BULK-REST-1-1`). `aidocs/16` SEMANTIC-ANNOTATE-BULK-REST-1. New additive endpoint `POST /v2/annotations/bulk` on the `/v2/` development surface. Accepts `List<CreateAnnotationIO>` (same wire shape as `POST /v2/annotations`); max 100 rows per call; best-effort per row — failed rows are recorded with an error message and do not abort the rest of the batch. Returns `BulkAnnotationResultIO { requested, succeeded, failed, results[] }` where each `BulkAnnotationItemResultIO` carries `{ ok, appId, subjectAppId, error }`. `X-AI-Agent` header sets `sourceMode=ai` on all rows in the batch. Single fire-and-forget summary `:Activity` with `PROP_SKIP_CAPTURE` set (no duplicate Activity from `ProvenanceCaptureFilter`). Frontend stub: `BulkAnnotatePane.vue`. Follow-up SEMANTIC-ANNOTATE-BULK-UI-1 queued for the multi-select dialog. 8 JUnit tests. | **ZERO** — additive only. New `/v2/` endpoint; the `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is untouched. The `POST /v2/annotations` single-create endpoint is unchanged. | — (no Neo4j or Postgres schema migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** Pull and restart. New endpoint available at `POST /v2/annotations/bulk`. |

| **APISIMP-INSTANCE-REGISTRY-BESPOKE — public instance registry GET moved from `/v2/admin/instances` to `/v2/instance/registry`** | 2026-07-02 (fire-355, branch `APISIMP-INSTANCE-REGISTRY-BESPOKE`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-INSTANCE-REGISTRY-BESPOKE. `InstanceRegistryRest` had an asymmetric auth split: `GET /v2/admin/instances` was `@PermitAll` (public) while `PATCH /v2/admin/instances` was `@RolesAllowed(INSTANCE_ADMIN_ROLE)`. A public GET under `/v2/admin/` is misleading — `/admin/` conventionally implies operator-only access. Fix: new `InstanceRegistryPublicRest` at `GET /v2/instance/registry` (same `@PermitAll` posture, consistent with `GET /v2/instance/capabilities` and `GET /v2/instance/identity`). The `GET` method removed from `InstanceRegistryRest` — that class now exposes only `PATCH /v2/admin/instances` (operator-only). `PublicEndpointRegistry.PUBLIC_PATHS` updated to register `/v2/instance/registry` instead of `/v2/admin/instances`. Frontend composables `useInstanceRegistry.ts` and `useInstanceRegistryAdmin.ts` updated to read from the new GET URL; PATCH URL unchanged. 3 new `PublicEndpointRegistryTest` cases. | **AWARE — PATH CHANGE on `GET /v2/admin/instances`** | The public read endpoint has moved from `GET /v2/admin/instances` to `GET /v2/instance/registry`. Any client or script polling `GET /v2/admin/instances` must update to `GET /v2/instance/registry`. The PATCH surface (`PATCH /v2/admin/instances`) is **unchanged** — no auth model change, no body schema change. The old GET path now has no handler (`@PermitAll GET` method removed) and returns `404 Not Found`. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is **unchanged**. | — (no Neo4j or Postgres schema migration; Java source changes only). | **Action required for any client polling `GET /v2/admin/instances` without a JWT (the public read).** Update the URL to `GET /v2/instance/registry`. The response body shape (`InstanceRegistryIO`) is identical. `PATCH /v2/admin/instances` callers require no change. The fork's own frontend composables are updated in this PR. |
| **APISIMP-PROV-CURSOR-INCONSISTENT — `?pageSize=` renamed `?limit=` on `GET /v2/provenance/activities` (all three content-type variants)** | 2026-07-02 (fire-357, branch `APISIMP-PROV-CURSOR-INCONSISTENT`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-PROV-CURSOR-INCONSISTENT. The three content-negotiation variants of `GET /v2/provenance/activities` (JSON, PROV-JSON `application/prov+json`, JSON-LD `application/ld+json`) accepted `?pageSize=` to cap the cursor window, which misleads callers who see `pageSize` and expect a `?page=` sibling for offset navigation. The endpoint uses time-cursor pagination (`?since=`/`?until=`); `pageSize` is a window-size cap, not a page selector. Renamed to `?limit=` to match cursor-pagination conventions (e.g. GraphQL connections, cursor APIs) and added a clear `@Parameter` description: "Cursor window size — use `?since=`/`?until=` for navigation, not `?page=`". Generated client `ListActivitiesRequest.pageSize` renamed to `ListActivitiesRequest.limit`; three frontend call sites (`useFetchAdminActivities.ts`, `DataObjectProvGraph.vue`, `DataObjectProvLog.vue`) and the Vitest test suite updated accordingly. | **PARAM RENAME on `GET /v2/provenance/activities`** — query parameter `?pageSize=` renamed to `?limit=`. Callers passing `?pageSize=N` will silently receive the default 100-row window instead of the requested limit. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is untouched. The per-entity `GET /v2/provenance/entity/{appId}` endpoints retain `?pageSize=` for now (separate follow-up). | — (no schema migration; Java source + generated client + frontend changes only). | **Action required for callers using `?pageSize=` on `GET /v2/provenance/activities`.** Rename the query parameter from `?pageSize=N` to `?limit=N`. If using the `@dlr-shepard/backend-client` generated client: rename `listActivities({ pageSize: N })` → `listActivities({ limit: N })`. All other query params (`agent`, `targetKind`, `targetAppId`, `since`, `until`) are unchanged. |
| **APISIMP-ENTITY-PROV-PAGESIZE — `?pageSize=` renamed `?limit=` on `GET /v2/provenance/entity/{appId}` (all three content-type variants)** | 2026-07-02 (fire-359, branch `APISIMP-ENTITY-PROV-PAGESIZE`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-ENTITY-PROV-PAGESIZE. Completes the cursor-parameter rename begun in APISIMP-PROV-CURSOR-INCONSISTENT (#2230): the three content-negotiation variants of `GET /v2/provenance/entity/{appId}` (JSON, PROV-JSON, JSON-LD) still accepted `?pageSize=`. Renamed to `?limit=` to match the `/activities` surface. Generated client `ListEntityActivitiesRequest.pageSize` renamed to `ListEntityActivitiesRequest.limit`. | **PARAM RENAME on `GET /v2/provenance/entity/{appId}`** — query parameter `?pageSize=` renamed to `?limit=`. Callers passing `?pageSize=N` will silently receive the default 100-row window. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is untouched. No frontend callers of this endpoint exist in this fork. | — (no schema migration). | **Action required for direct API callers using `?pageSize=` on `GET /v2/provenance/entity/{appId}`.** Rename to `?limit=N`. If using the generated client: rename `listEntityActivities({ pageSize: N })` → `listEntityActivities({ limit: N })`. |

| **APISIMP-SHAPES-PREDICATES-BARE-LIST — `GET /v2/shapes/predicates` now returns a `PagedResponseIO` envelope with a `?limit=` cap** | 2026-07-02 (fire-361, branch `APISIMP-SHAPES-PREDICATES-BARE-LIST-add-limit-envelope`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-SHAPES-PREDICATES-BARE-LIST. `GET /v2/shapes/predicates` previously returned a raw JSON array (`[{predicateUri,substrate,...}, ...]`) with no size guard. Fixed to wrap in the standard `PagedResponseIO` envelope (`{items, total, page, pageSize}`) and accept an optional `?limit=` query parameter (default 200, max 500). Frontend composable `useShapePalette.ts` updated to read from `.items`. | **WIRE BREAK on `GET /v2/shapes/predicates`** — response body shape changes from a raw JSON array to the `PagedResponseIO` envelope `{items:[], total:N, page:0, pageSize:N}`. Direct API callers that deserialize the response as an array must unwrap `.items`. Callers using the fork's own `useShapePalette.ts` composable require no change (already updated). The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is unchanged. | — (no schema migration; Java source + frontend changes only). | **Action required for direct callers of `GET /v2/shapes/predicates` that expect a bare array.** Change your deserialisation to read `.items` from the response envelope. To cap the result below the default 200, pass `?limit=N`. |
| **APISIMP-SHAPES-PREDICATES-FULL-PAGINATION — `GET /v2/shapes/predicates` `?limit=` renamed to `?pageSize=` and `?page=` added** | 2026-07-04 (fire-402, branch `APISIMP-SHAPES-PREDICATES-FULL-PAGINATION-add-page-param`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-SHAPES-PREDICATES-FULL-PAGINATION. APISIMP-SHAPES-PREDICATES-BARE-LIST (fire-361) added a `?limit=` cap but left `page` hardcoded at 0 in the `PagedResponseIO` envelope, making multi-page navigation impossible. Fixed: `?limit=` renamed to `?pageSize=` (same constraint: `@Min(1) @Max(500)`, default 200); new `?page=` query parameter added (`@Min(0)`, default 0). The slicing uses `subList(page * pageSize, min((page+1)*pageSize, total))`; a page past the end returns an empty `items` list with the correct `total`. 4 tests updated + 2 new tests added (`pageParam_returnsCorrectSlice`, `pageParam_beyondTotal_returnsEmptyItems`). Frontend `useShapePalette.ts` calls the endpoint without any `?limit=`/`?pageSize=` query param (loads page 0 / default pageSize), so requires no change. | **PARAM RENAME on `GET /v2/shapes/predicates`** — query parameter `?limit=` renamed to `?pageSize=`. Callers passing `?limit=N` will silently receive the default 200-entry window. | — (no schema migration; Java source + test changes only). | **Action required for direct callers of `GET /v2/shapes/predicates` that pass `?limit=N`.** Rename the query parameter from `?limit=N` to `?pageSize=N`. To paginate past page 0, add `?page=N`. |
| **APISIMP-VOCAB-BROWSE-FAKE-PAGED — `GET /v2/semantic/vocabularies` and `…/used-by/{appId}` drop fake `PagedResponseIO` envelope; `GET …/{vocabId}/predicates` gains real `?page=`/`?pageSize=` pagination** | 2026-07-02 (fire-364, branch `APISIMP-VOCAB-BROWSE-FAKE-PAGED-drop-fake-paged`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-VOCAB-BROWSE-FAKE-PAGED. Three endpoints on `VocabularyBrowseRest` fixed: (1) `GET /v2/semantic/vocabularies` previously wrapped a plain list in `PagedResponseIO(body, body.size(), 0, body.size())` with no `?page=`/`?pageSize=` params — now returns a plain `List<VocabularyIO>`. (2) `GET /v2/semantic/vocabularies/used-by/{entityAppId}` same fake-paged fix — now returns a plain `List<VocabularyIO>`. (3) `GET /v2/semantic/vocabularies/{vocabId}/predicates` previously returned an uncapped unwrapped list — now accepts `?page=` (default 0) / `?pageSize=` (default 50, max 200) with JSR-380 `@PositiveOrZero`/`@Min(1)`/`@Max(200)` constraints and returns a genuine `PagedResponseIO<PredicateIO>` slice. Frontend composable `useSemanticVocabularyPredicates.ts` updated to read `body.items` from the `PagedResponseIO` envelope; Vitest tests updated to match. `scope` `@QueryParam` on `listVocabulariesUsedBy` gains an `@Parameter` description (APISIMP-VOCAB-BROWSE-SCOPE-UNDOCUMENTED). | **WIRE BREAK on `GET /v2/semantic/vocabularies` and `GET /v2/semantic/vocabularies/used-by/{entityAppId}`** — response body shape changes from `{items:[], total:N, page:0, pageSize:N}` envelope to a plain JSON array `[{appId, uri, label, enabled}, ...]`. Direct API callers that deserialize the response as a `PagedResponseIO` object must change to deserialize as an array. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is unchanged. **`GET /v2/semantic/vocabularies/{vocabId}/predicates` is ADDITIVE** — the response remains `PagedResponseIO`-shaped; the new `?page=`/`?pageSize=` params are optional with backward-compatible defaults. | — (no schema migration; Java source + frontend changes only). | **Action required for direct callers of `GET /v2/semantic/vocabularies` or `GET /v2/semantic/vocabularies/used-by/{appId}` that deserialize the response as a `PagedResponseIO` envelope.** Change your deserialisation to read the response as a plain JSON array instead of unwrapping `.items`. The predicate list endpoint (`/{vocabId}/predicates`) is unchanged — it remains a `PagedResponseIO`; callers may now optionally pass `?page=N&pageSize=50` to paginate large ontologies. |
| **APISIMP-REF-ANNOTATION-LIST-NO-PAGINATION — `GET /v2/references/{appId}/annotations` now accepts `?page=` / `?limit=` and returns a sliced `PagedResponseIO` envelope** | 2026-07-02 (fire-362, branch `APISIMP-REF-ANNOTATION-LIST-NO-PAGINATION-add-pagination`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-REF-ANNOTATION-LIST-NO-PAGINATION. `GET /v2/references/{appId}/annotations` previously returned all annotation rows in one call (no pagination params, fake `PagedResponseIO(rows, rows.size(), 0, rows.size())`). Fixed: adds `?page=` (default 0) and `?limit=` (default 200, max 1000) query params; returns a genuinely sliced page. `total` always reflects the full annotation count; `page` / `pageSize` reflect the requested page. No frontend change needed — `useTimeseriesReferenceAnnotations.ts` already reads `.items`. | **ADDITIVE** — no wire break. The response envelope was already `PagedResponseIO`-shaped; the new params are optional with the same defaults. Callers that do not pass `?limit=` get the same envelope (now with actual pagination arithmetic applied). The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is unchanged. | — (no schema migration; Java source change only). | No action required for existing callers. To paginate a large annotation set, pass `?page=N&limit=200`. |
| **APISIMP-DQR-EVALUATE-BARE-LIST — `POST /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/dqr/evaluate` now returns a `DQRResultsIO` envelope with a `?limit=` cap (default 5 000, max 5 000)** | 2026-07-02 (fire-371, branch `APISIMP-DQR-EVALUATE-BARE-LIST`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-DQR-EVALUATE-BARE-LIST. `POST .../dqr/evaluate` previously returned a bare `List<DQRResultIO>` with no size guard. A collection with 10 000 DataObjects and 5 enabled DQRs could generate 50 000 rows in a single synchronous response. Fixed: new `DQRResultsIO` record (`results`, `truncated`, `total`) wraps the result; optional `?limit=` query param (JSR-380 `@Min(1) @Max(5000)`, default 5 000) caps the returned list. When `total > limit`, `truncated: true` is set and `total` carries the uncapped count so the caller can decide whether to raise the limit or add async evaluation. 5 new JUnit tests in `CollectionDQRRestTest` cover 401, empty envelope, within-limit, truncation, and annotation validation. | **WIRE-SHAPE CHANGE on `POST /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/dqr/evaluate`** — response body changes from a bare JSON array (`[{dqrAppId, dataObjectAppId, passed, message}, ...]`) to `{results:[...], truncated:false, total:N}`. Direct API callers that deserialize the response as an array must unwrap `.results`. Callers that relied on receiving all results unconditionally should check `truncated` and pass `?limit=` accordingly. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is untouched. | — (no schema migration; Java source change only; `DQRResultsIO` is a new record in `de.dlr.shepard.v2.quality.io`). | **Action required for direct callers of `POST .../dqr/evaluate` that deserialize the response as a bare array.** Change deserialisation to read `.results` from the `DQRResultsIO` envelope. Also check `.truncated`; if `true`, raise `?limit=` or implement async evaluation. |
| **SEARCH-V2-4-PRE — new `GET /v2/users?q=` endpoint; `GET /v2/user-groups` gains `?q=` text-search param** | 2026-07-03 (fire-393, branch `SEARCH-V2-4-PRE-user-search-endpoint`). `aidocs/16` SEARCH-V2-4-PRE. New `UserSearchV2Rest` at `@Path("/v2/users")` — `GET /v2/users?q=<text>` searches by username / firstName / lastName / email (OR-contains, case-insensitive). Returns a flat `List<UserIO>` (same shape as v1 `/shepard/api/search/users`). Query built via Jackson `ObjectMapper` (injection-safe). Requires non-blank `?q=`; returns 400 otherwise. `UserGroupSearchService.searchByText(String text)` added — returns `List<UserGroup>` entities (name-contains, Jackson-serialised query). `UserGroupV2Rest.listUserGroups()` gains a `?q=` param: when non-blank, delegates to `searchByText`, maps to `UserGroupV2IO`, wraps in `PagedResponseIO`. When `?q=` is absent, the existing pagination path (page/pageSize/orderBy/orderDesc) is unchanged. 6 new unit tests (4 in `UserSearchV2RestTest`, 2 in `UserGroupV2RestTest`). No numeric id exposed. Unblocks SEARCH-V2-4 (frontend migration of `useUserSearch`/`useGroupSearch` composables). | **ZERO** — purely additive `/v2/` endpoints. The upstream `/shepard/api/search/users` and `/shepard/api/search/userGroups` v1 paths are **unchanged**. `GET /v2/user-groups` without `?q=` behaves identically to before. No new config keys; no Neo4j migrations; no schema changes. | — (no schema migration; Java source changes only). | **No action required for upgrade.** After restart, `GET /v2/users?q=alice` returns a flat list of matching `UserIO` objects. `GET /v2/user-groups?q=rocket` returns a `{items, total, page, pageSize}` envelope filtered by name-contains. Both require an authenticated JWT (`@Authenticated`). |
| **SEARCH-V2-4 — `useMemberSearch` and `usePermissionUserSearch` migrated from v1 `SearchApi` to `GET /v2/users?q=` and `GET /v2/user-groups?q=`** | 2026-07-04 (fire-394, branch `SEARCH-V2-4-user-member-search-v2`). `aidocs/16` SEARCH-V2-4. Removes last `useShepardApi(SearchApi)` calls in the permissions subsystem. `useMemberSearch.ts` and `usePermissionUserSearch.ts` now use `useV2ShepardApi(UserApi)` / `useV2ShepardApi(UserGroupsApi)`. `backend-client/UserApi` extended with `searchUsersV2` method (`GET /v2/users?q=`); `ListUserGroupsRequest` extended with `q?`. Groups mapped from `UserGroupV2` to `UserGroup` shape with `id=0` sentinel; equality checks in `EditPermissionsDialog`, `MemberPermissionList` migrated from `member.id` to `member.appId`. `MemberAutocomplete` label updated (drops numeric id, shows last 8 chars of appId). 12 new Vitest tests covering both composables. | **ZERO — frontend-only change.** No new v2 endpoints, no v1 paths touched, no config keys, no Neo4j/DB schema changes. The `GET /v2/users?q=` and `GET /v2/user-groups?q=` endpoints were already shipped in SEARCH-V2-4-PRE (PR #2273). | — | **No action required for upgrade.** Permission dialogs now search users and groups via v2; results are identical to the v1 search. |
| **APISIMP-MAP-PERMISSIONS-V2 — `mapPermissions.ts` `fetchUser` and `fetchUserGroupByAppId` migrated from v1 `useShepardApi` / `useUserGroupsV2` composables to `useV2ShepardApi`** | 2026-07-04 (fire-394, branch `APISIMP-MAP-PERMISSIONS-V2`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-MAP-PERMISSIONS-V2. `mapPermissions.ts` is the internal helper that resolves `UpdatedPermissions` structs (usernames + group appIds) into `MemberPermissions[]` objects displayed in the permissions list. `fetchUser()` previously called `useShepardApi(UserApi).value.getUser()` (v1 base URL `/shepard/api`); now calls `useV2ShepardApi(UserApi).value.getUser()` (v2 base URL). `fetchUserGroupByAppId()` previously called `useUserGroupsV2().getUserGroup({id})` (v1 composable returning a `UserGroupV2` with `createdAt: string`); now calls `useV2ShepardApi(UserGroupsApi).value.getUserGroupV2({ appId })` (backend-client type with `createdAt: Date | undefined`). `v2ToUserGroup()` simplified accordingly. Both `useShepardApi` and `useUserGroupsV2` imports removed from the file. | **ZERO — frontend-only change.** No API endpoints changed; both `GET /v2/users/{username}` and `GET /v2/user-groups/{appId}` were already available. No v1 paths touched, no config keys, no Neo4j/DB schema changes. | — | **No action required for upgrade.** The permissions load-and-display path now calls the v2 endpoints exclusively. |
| **APISIMP-SNAPSHOT-PINNED-DO-UNCAPPED — `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/snapshots/{snapshotAppId}/data-objects` now paginates with `?page=`/`?pageSize=`; `SnapshotDataObjectsIO` gains `totalDataObjects`, `page`, `pageSize` fields** | 2026-07-02 (fire-372, branch `APISIMP-SNAPSHOT-PINNED-DO-UNCAPPED`, PR #2247). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-SNAPSHOT-PINNED-DO-UNCAPPED. `GET .../data-objects` previously embedded the full `List<String>` of DataObject appIds in `SnapshotDataObjectsIO` with no cap — a 50 000-DataObject snapshot serialised ~3 MB of UUID strings per call. Fixed: `SnapshotPinnedReadRest.getDataObjects()` now accepts `?page=` (0-based, `@PositiveOrZero`, default 0) and `?pageSize=` (`@Min(1) @Max(2000)`, default 500); applies `subList(from, to)` slicing; passes the sliced list + `totalDataObjects` + `page` + `pageSize` to `SnapshotDataObjectsIO`. The IO record gains three new fields: `totalDataObjects` (full count before slicing), `page`, `pageSize`. 3 new pagination JUnit tests: first page, second page, beyond-last-page → empty. | **WIRE-SHAPE CHANGE on `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/snapshots/{snapshotAppId}/data-objects`** — `SnapshotDataObjectsIO` gains 3 new fields (`totalDataObjects`, `page`, `pageSize`). The existing `dataObjectAppIds` field is now a sliced page window (not the full list) when `?pageSize=` is smaller than the total count. Callers that always fetched the full list without pagination will now receive at most 500 appIds per call and must paginate via `?page=` to retrieve all. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is untouched. | — (no schema migration; Java source change only). | **Action required for callers of `GET .../data-objects` that expected an unbounded `dataObjectAppIds` list.** Paginate by incrementing `?page=` until `dataObjectAppIds.size() < pageSize` (last page). Use `totalDataObjects` to allocate the full result buffer. Default `?pageSize=500` is safe for most snapshots; increase up to `?pageSize=2000` to reduce round trips on large snapshots. |

| **APISIMP-STATS-NUMERIC-ID — `GET /v2/file-containers/{id}/stats` and `GET /v2/structured-data-containers/{id}/stats` path param renamed from numeric `{containerId}` to UUID string `{containerAppId}`** | 2026-07-04 (fire-403, branch `APISIMP-STATS-NUMERIC-ID-appid-path-param`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-STATS-NUMERIC-ID. Both stats endpoints (added by UI21-SIZEBAR-DATA) previously exposed a Neo4j numeric id as `{containerId}` in the path (APISIMP Axis 4 violation). Fixed: path param renamed to `{containerAppId}` (UUID v7 string); `EntityIdResolver.resolveLong()` bridges to the OGM Long internally. `operationId` added to both `@Operation` annotations. `V2-EXCEPTION` Javadoc added explaining the standalone `@Path` (not yet merged into the `ContainersV2Rest` kind-dispatcher). Frontend caller `useContainerCardinality.ts` updated to accept `string | null | undefined` instead of `number`; `ContainerCardinalityCell.vue` gains `containerAppId` prop; `ContainerList.vue` passes `rowProps.item.appId`. | **PATH-PARAM CHANGE on two `/v2/` endpoints** — `GET /v2/file-containers/{containerId}/stats` → `GET /v2/file-containers/{containerAppId}/stats`; `GET /v2/structured-data-containers/{containerId}/stats` → `GET /v2/structured-data-containers/{containerAppId}/stats`. Callers passing a numeric Neo4j id in the path must update to pass the entity's `appId` (UUID v7 string). The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is untouched. | — (no schema migration; Java + TypeScript source changes only). | **Action required for any direct API caller of these two `/stats` endpoints that currently passes a numeric id.** Update the path to use the entity's `appId` string instead. The frontend `ContainerList` sizebar is updated in the same PR and requires no operator action after restart. |

| **APISIMP-DQR-LIST-IN-MEMORY-PAGING — `DataQualityRequirementService.list()` signature change; DAO gains paginated overload + count query** | 2026-07-06 (fire-439, branch `APISIMP-DQR-LIST-IN-MEMORY-PAGING-1`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-DQR-LIST-IN-MEMORY-PAGING. `CollectionDQRRest.list()` previously called `service.list(collectionAppId, caller)` → `dao.findByCollectionAppId(collectionAppId)` with no SKIP/LIMIT, then paged in-memory via `all.subList(from, to)`. Fixed: new `dao.findByCollectionAppId(String, int, int)` Cypher variant with `SKIP $skip LIMIT $limit`; new `dao.countByCollectionAppId(String)` COUNT query; `service.list()` signature changed to `(collectionAppId, caller, int page, int pageSize)` returning `PagedResponseIO<DQRIO>`; REST layer removes in-memory `subList` and delegates. **Service signature is a Java internal contract — affects in-tree unit tests only; no REST wire shape change.** | **ZERO for operators and REST clients** — The `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/dqr` JSON response shape is unchanged. The `page`/`pageSize` query params, the `PagedResponseIO` envelope, the `X-Total-Count` header all pre-existed; they now correctly reflect DB-side pagination instead of in-memory slicing. | — (no schema migration; Java source change only). | **No action required for upgrade.** The `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/dqr` wire shape is unchanged; `X-Total-Count` and `{total, items, page, pageSize}` semantics are preserved. |

| **APISIMP-TS-CONT-ANNOT-IN-MEMORY-PAGING — `AnnotatableTimeseriesDAO` and `TimeseriesAnnotationDAO` gain paginated Cypher overloads; `AnnotatableTimeseriesService` exposes counted/paged channel-annotation methods; `TimeseriesContainerKindHandler.listChannelAnnotations()` and `listTemporalAnnotations()` remove in-memory `subList`** | 2026-07-06 (fire-441, PR #2360, branch `APISIMP-TS-CONT-ANNOT-IN-MEMORY-PAGING-1`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-TS-CONT-ANNOT-IN-MEMORY-PAGING. Both annotation-listing methods previously loaded the full annotation set into JVM heap then paged via `List.subList(from, to)`. Fixed: `AnnotatableTimeseriesDAO.countAnnotationsByAppId(String)` + `findAnnotationsByAppId(String, long, int)` (Cypher SKIP/LIMIT on `has_annotation` relationship); `TimeseriesAnnotationDAO.countByContainerId(long)` + `findByContainerId(long, long, int)` (Cypher SKIP/LIMIT on `has_temporal_annotation` relationship); `AnnotatableTimeseriesService` adds `countAnnotationsByChannelShepardId` + paged `getAnnotationsByChannelShepardId`; unbounded variants marked `@Deprecated` (retained for MCP tools). Handler removes `subList` and delegates both endpoints to the new DAO/service methods. | **ZERO for operators and REST clients** — Wire shape of `GET /v2/timeseries-containers/{appId}/channels/{channelShepardId}/annotations` and `GET /v2/timeseries-containers/{appId}/temporal-annotations` is unchanged. `page`/`pageSize`/`X-Total-Count`/`PagedResponseIO` envelope pre-existed; paging is now DB-side. | — (no schema migration; Java source change only). | **No action required for upgrade.** Both endpoint wire shapes are unchanged; `X-Total-Count` and `PagedResponseIO` semantics are preserved. |

| **APISIMP-MINTER-CRED-CONFIG-UNIFY — `POST/DELETE /v2/admin/minters/datacite/credential` and `POST/DELETE /v2/admin/minters/epic/credential` tombstoned (410 Gone); credential now settable via `PATCH /v2/admin/config/minter-{type}` with a `password`/`credential` write-only field** | 2026-07-07 (fire-454, branch `APISIMP-MINTER-CRED-CONFIG-UNIFY-1`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-MINTER-CRED-CONFIG-UNIFY. Operators previously needed to discover two separate admin surfaces per minter: the generic config endpoint (`PATCH /v2/admin/config/minter-datacite`) for fields like `enabled`/`apiBaseUrl` and a bespoke sub-resource (`POST /v2/admin/minters/datacite/credential`) for credentials. Fixed: `DataciteConfigDescriptor.applyMergePatch()` now accepts a `"password"` field — non-blank string delegates to `DataciteMinterConfigService.setCredential()`; explicit `null` delegates to `clearCredential()`; absent means no credential change. `EpicConfigDescriptor` gains the same pattern with field name `"credential"`. Both `POST/DELETE .../credential` methods on `DataciteAdminRest`/`EpicAdminRest` are tombstoned (return 410 Gone with a message pointing to the PATCH path). `POST .../test-connection` is retained as a standalone diagnostic action. The credential plaintext is handled identically to before — AES-GCM encrypted at rest, SHA-256 fingerprint in read responses, never logged, never in `:Activity` nodes. 3 new tests per descriptor (set / null-clears / blank-rejects); credential tests in both `AdminRest*Test` classes updated to assert 410. | **BREAKING on the credential sub-resource paths (both `/v2/` dev surface, no `/shepard/api/` impact)** — `POST /v2/admin/minters/datacite/credential` and `DELETE /v2/admin/minters/datacite/credential` (and their Epic equivalents) now return 410 Gone. Callers must migrate to `PATCH /v2/admin/config/minter-datacite` with a `"password"` field. `POST .../test-connection` is **unchanged**. | — (no schema migration; Java source changes only). | **Action required for any script or IaC step using `POST/DELETE .../credential`.** Migrate to `PATCH /v2/admin/config/minter-datacite` with `"password"`. To set the DataCite credential: `curl -fsS -X PATCH https://<host>/v2/admin/config/minter-datacite -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"password":"<plaintext>"}'` → 200 with `passwordSet=true` and `passwordFingerprint=<8-hex-chars>`. To clear: same PATCH with `"password": null`. To set ePIC credential: same pattern at `/v2/admin/config/minter-epic` with `"credential"`. The `POST .../test-connection` endpoint is unchanged. |
| **APISIMP-SQL-TIMESERIES-NUMERIC-CONTAINER-ID-FALLBACK — `POST /v2/sql/timeseries` now returns HTTP 400 when `container_id_in` (deprecated numeric Neo4j id field) is non-empty; `container_app_id_in` (UUID v7) is the sole accepted container filter** | 2026-07-08 (fire-466, PR #2394). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-SQL-TIMESERIES-NUMERIC-CONTAINER-ID-FALLBACK. Row 736 of this table (fire-214, PR #2088) added `container_app_id_in` as an additive alternative and kept `container_id_in` as a deprecated backward-compat fallback. This fire removes that fallback: `SqlTimeseriesRest.executeQuery()` now returns `HTTP 400 application/problem+json` with migration guidance when `spec.where().containerIdIn()` is non-null and non-empty. Empty or absent `container_id_in` is not rejected (the field was nullable and some callers may serialise it as `[]`). The `requestedIds` resolution path was simplified to read exclusively from `containerAppIdIn`. `SqlQuerySpec.WhereClause.containerIdIn` `@Schema` description updated to state: "Removed — passing this field returns HTTP 400." Three new tests in `SqlTimeseriesPermissionTest`: `containerIdIn_returns400WithMigrationGuidance`, `containerIdIn_emptyList_noTombstone`, `containerIdIn_nonEmpty_withContainerAppIdIn_stillReturns400`; four existing permission tests updated to use `containerAppIdIn` exclusively. | **BREAKING — `POST /v2/sql/timeseries` with a non-empty `container_id_in` array now returns `HTTP 400`** (was: numeric ids resolved and used). Callers that were relying on the deprecated fallback (fire-214 backward compat) must migrate to `container_app_id_in` (UUID v7 string array). The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is **unchanged**. | — (no schema migration; Java source changes only). | **Action required for any caller still passing `container_id_in` on `POST /v2/sql/timeseries`.** Replace the `container_id_in` field (numeric Neo4j ids) with `container_app_id_in` (UUID v7 `appId` strings). Example: `{"where":{"time_between":{...}, "container_app_id_in":["01930a2b-fe4c-7e3c-9f1d-aaaaaaaaaaaa"]}}`. Look up a container's `appId` via `GET /v2/containers/{appId}` or `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/referenced-containers`. Empty or absent `container_id_in` (`[]` or omitted) is not rejected — only a non-empty array triggers 400. |

| **APISIMP-REFS-LIST-NO-XTOTALCOUNT — `GET /v2/references` response now includes `X-Total-Count` header** | 2026-07-08 (fire-473, branch `APISIMP-REFS-LIST-NO-XTOTALCOUNT`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-REFS-LIST-NO-XTOTALCOUNT. `ReferencesV2Rest.list()` (`GET /v2/references?kind=...&dataObjectAppId=...`) was the only paginated v2 list endpoint missing the `X-Total-Count` response header. The `PagedResponseIO` envelope body was already correct (fire-320, APISIMP-REFS-FAKE-PAGINATION) but the header was omitted. Fixed: added `.header("X-Total-Count", total)` to the `Response.ok(...)` chain; updated `@APIResponse` description to include the standard "Header X-Total-Count = total count before paging (kept during deprecation window, APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE)." clause; added `list_returnsXTotalCountHeader` unit test. | **ADDITIVE** — existing `PagedResponseIO` body and `page`/`pageSize` params are unchanged; only the header is added. Clients that already read the header from other list endpoints need no change. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is untouched. | — (no schema migration; Java source changes only). | **No action required for upgrade.** Clients may optionally read `X-Total-Count` from `GET /v2/references` responses for parity with all other paginated list endpoints. |

| **APISIMP-CONTAINERS-CHANNELS-LIST-BARE-ARRAY — `GET /v2/containers/{appId}/channels` response wrapped in `PagedResponseIO`; `ContainerKindHandler.listChannels` SPI return type changed** | 2026-07-08 (fire-467, branch `APISIMP-CONTAINERS-CHANNELS-LIST-BARE-ARRAY`, PR #2395). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-CONTAINERS-CHANNELS-LIST-BARE-ARRAY. `ContainersV2Rest.listChannels()` already accepted `?page=`/`?pageSize=` Bean Validation params but the `ContainerKindHandler` SPI returned `Optional<List<TimeseriesChannelV2IO>>` — a bare list with no total-count signal. Callers could not distinguish "exactly pageSize items" from "no more pages." Fixed: (1) `TsChannelResolver.countByContainerId(long)` added (Panache COUNT query). (2) `ContainerKindHandler.listChannels` interface return type changed from `Optional<List<TimeseriesChannelV2IO>>` to `Optional<PagedResponseIO<TimeseriesChannelV2IO>>`. (3) `TimeseriesContainerKindHandler.listChannels()` now calls `countByContainerId` and wraps results in `PagedResponseIO`. (4) `ContainersV2Rest` `@APIResponse` schema updated from `SchemaType.ARRAY` to `PagedResponseIO`. Handler unit tests updated to mock `countByContainerId` and assert `page.total()`, `page.pageSize()`. | **WIRE-SHAPE CHANGE on `GET /v2/containers/{appId}/channels`** — callers now receive `{items: [...], total: N, page: P, pageSize: S}` instead of a bare JSON array. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is untouched. | — (no schema migration; Java source changes only). | **Action required for any direct caller of `GET /v2/containers/{appId}/channels`.** Callers receiving a bare JSON array must be updated to unwrap `.items` from the `PagedResponseIO` envelope and use `.total` to determine whether to fetch additional pages. The query parameters `?page=` and `?pageSize=` are unchanged. |

| **APISIMP-XCOUNT-OPENAPI-HEADER — 38 v2 list endpoints now formally declare `X-Total-Count` in the OpenAPI `@APIResponse` spec** | 2026-07-08 (fire-479, PR #2411, branch `APISIMP-XCOUNT-OPENAPI-HEADER-1`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-XCOUNT-OPENAPI-HEADER. All v2 list endpoints already emitted `X-Total-Count` at runtime but 37 of 38 only mentioned it in the `description` string — no formal `headers = @Header(name="X-Total-Count", schema=@Schema(type=SchemaType.INTEGER))` block on `@APIResponse(responseCode="200")`. `AdminConfigRest` was the sole exception (PR #2409). Without the formal declaration, OpenAPI-generated clients never saw the header in the generated spec, making programmatic pagination invisible to tooling. Fix: batch-added the `@Header` annotation to all 38 REST endpoints (37 in `backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/` + `AdminConfigRest` already done). Two bonus fixes in `ContainersV2Rest` (`listChannelAnnotations`, `listTemporalAnnotations`) caught by cross-checking handler vs. REST class. | **ADDITIVE** — no wire shape change; only the OpenAPI spec gains formal `X-Total-Count` header documentation. All runtime behaviour is unchanged. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is untouched. Plugin files (`AasRegistrationAdminRest`, `AasShellsRest`) deferred to `APISIMP-XCOUNT-OPENAPI-HEADER-2` (next fire). | — (no schema migration; Java source changes only). | **No action required for upgrade.** OpenAPI-generated clients will now include `X-Total-Count` in the generated response model for all paginated list endpoints. |

| **APISIMP-PLUGIN-IO-SCHEMA-MISSING — class-level `@Schema(name, description)` added to 34 plugin wire-IO classes** | 2026-07-09 (fire-492, branch `APISIMP-PLUGIN-IO-SCHEMA-MISSING`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-PLUGIN-IO-SCHEMA-MISSING. 34 plugin wire-IO Java types (records, classes, enums across 10 plugin modules: aas, ai, git, hdf5, minter-datacite, minter-epic, spatiotemporal, unhide, v1-compat, video, wiki-writer) were missing class-level `@Schema(name = "…", description = "…")` annotations. Without the annotation MicroProfile OpenAPI cannot emit a human-readable description for the type in the generated `/v2.json` spec, making plugin endpoints appear in the spec with anonymous unnamed schemas. Fix: batch-added `import org.eclipse.microprofile.openapi.annotations.media.Schema;` and a class-level `@Schema` carrying the type's canonical name and a one-sentence endpoint-oriented description to all 34 types. CLI-only types (10 excluded) and types already annotated (22 excluded) were not modified. | **ADDITIVE** — no wire shape change, no endpoint change, no `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. OpenAPI-only metadata change: plugin response schemas now carry stable names and descriptions in the generated spec. | — (no schema migration; Java annotation only). | **No operator action required.** Generated API clients that consume `/v2.json` will now see named, described schemas for all 34 plugin wire types. |

| **APISIMP-CROSS-BULK-KIND-PATH — `POST /v2/data-objects/cross-timeseries-bulk` renamed to `POST /v2/data-objects/cross-bulk?kind=timeseries`** | 2026-07-10 (fire-512, branch `APISIMP-CROSS-BULK-KIND-PATH-1`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-CROSS-BULK-KIND-PATH. `CrossDoBulkDataRest` (`POST /v2/data-objects/cross-timeseries-bulk`) hard-coded the timeseries kind in the path segment. Per `aidocs/platform/191-v2-surface-convergence.md §2`, kind-specific routing belongs in a `?kind=` discriminator so future payload families (file-bulk, structured-bulk) share the same path. Fix: `@Path` changed to `/v2/data-objects/cross-bulk`; `@QueryParam("kind")` added (required; only `timeseries` accepted; 400 for null or unknown values); `CrossDoBulkTombstoneRest` tombstones the old path with 410 Gone + `Location: /v2/data-objects/cross-bulk?kind=timeseries`; frontend `useCrossDoBulkData.ts` URL updated. | **PATH RENAME on `/v2/` surface** — `POST /v2/data-objects/cross-timeseries-bulk` now returns 410 Gone; callers must switch to `POST /v2/data-objects/cross-bulk?kind=timeseries`. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is untouched. | — (no schema migration; Java source + frontend changes only). | **Action required for any direct caller of `POST /v2/data-objects/cross-timeseries-bulk`.** Rename the endpoint URL and add `?kind=timeseries`. The request body and response shape are unchanged. |

| **APISIMP-USERGROUP-NUMERIC-PERMS-BLOCK — `PATCH /v2/user-groups/{appId}/permissions` now returns HTTP 400 for numeric `readerGroupIds`/`writerGroupIds`; UUID-based `readerGroupAppIds`/`writerGroupAppIds` added** | 2026-07-10 (fire-510, branch `APISIMP-USERGROUP-NUMERIC-PERMS-BLOCK-1`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-USERGROUP-NUMERIC-PERMS-BLOCK. `UserGroupV2Rest.patchUserGroupPermissions()` previously called `Long.parseLong()` on entries in `readerGroupIds`/`writerGroupIds` and persisted them directly — silently corrupting data when numeric Neo4j ids changed across DB restores. `ContainersV2Rest` already blocked this pattern (fire-403). Fix: (1) `readerGroupIds` or `writerGroupIds` present in the PATCH body → HTTP 400 `application/problem+json` with migration guidance; (2) new `readerGroupAppIds`/`writerGroupAppIds` (UUID v7 string lists, max 100 each) accepted — entries are resolved to numeric Neo4j ids at call time via `UserGroupService.getUserGroupByAppId()`. `@Operation` description updated. 4 new unit tests: 2 asserting 400 on numeric fields, 2 asserting 200 on UUID fields. | **BREAKING — `PATCH /v2/user-groups/{appId}/permissions` with a non-empty `readerGroupIds` or `writerGroupIds` field now returns HTTP 400** (was: silently accepted and stored). Callers must replace numeric-id arrays with UUID v7 appId arrays. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is **unchanged**. | — (no schema migration; Java source changes only). | **Action required for any caller passing `readerGroupIds`/`writerGroupIds` on `PATCH /v2/user-groups/{appId}/permissions`.** Replace the numeric-id arrays with `readerGroupAppIds`/`writerGroupAppIds` (UUID v7 `appId` strings). Look up a group's `appId` via `GET /v2/user-groups/{appId}` or the user-groups list. Example: `{"readerGroupAppIds":["01900000-0000-7000-8000-000000000099"]}`. |

| **APISIMP-ANOMALY-5TUPLE-ADD-UUID — `POST /v2/references/{appId}/detect-anomalies` now accepts `channelAppId` as an alternative to the 5-tuple channel selector** | 2026-07-10 (fire-511, branch `APISIMP-ANOMALY-5TUPLE-ADD-UUID-1`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-ANOMALY-5TUPLE-ADD-UUID. `AnomalyDetectRequestIO` previously required callers to supply the full 5-tuple (`measurement`/`device`/`location`/`symbolicName`/`field`) to identify which channel to score when a `TimeseriesReference` holds multiple series. Clients that already hold a channel `appId` (e.g. from `GET /v2/containers/{appId}/timeseries-channels`) had to expand it back into 5 fields — a pointless round-trip. Fix: (1) new optional `channelAppId` String field on `AnomalyDetectRequestIO`; (2) `selectSeries()` in `AnomalyDetectionRest` resolves by matching `ReferencedTimeseriesNodeEntity.appId` within the reference's in-memory linked-series list; (3) providing both `channelAppId` and any 5-tuple field → 422; (4) `channelAppId` not found in ref → 400; (5) all existing behaviour (auto-select for single-series refs; 5-tuple filter) is preserved. 5 new unit tests cover the channelAppId paths. | **ADDITIVE** — new optional field on the request body of `POST /v2/references/{appId}/detect-anomalies`. Existing callers omitting `channelAppId` see no change. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is untouched. | — (no schema migration; Java source changes only). | **No action required for upgrade.** Callers can continue using the 5-tuple selector unchanged. To use the new selector: include `"channelAppId": "<uuid-v7>"` in the request body (and omit all 5-tuple fields). Providing both selectors returns 422. |
| **APISIMP-ANOMALY-ACTION-PATH — `POST /v2/references/{appId}/detect-anomalies` moved to `POST /v2/references/{appId}/actions?action=detect-anomalies`; old path returns 410 Gone** | 2026-07-10 (fire-513, branch `APISIMP-ANOMALY-ACTION-PATH-1`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-ANOMALY-ACTION-PATH. `AnomalyDetectionRest` registered `POST /v2/references/{appId}/detect-anomalies` — a kind-specific sub-path under the generic `/v2/references` hierarchy — but the resolver was timeseries-only, silently returning 404 for any non-timeseries kind. Per `aidocs/platform/191-v2-surface-convergence.md §2`, kind-specific operations belong under a kind-discriminated action param. Fix: (1) old `AnomalyDetectionRest` deleted; (2) new `ReferenceActionsRest` at `POST /v2/references/{appId}/actions?action=<name>` dispatches named actions; unknown action → 422; action on non-timeseries kind → 422 (via `ReferencesV2Service.resolveByAppId`); (3) `AnomalyDetectionTombstoneRest` at old path returns 410 Gone with `Location: /v2/references/{appId}/actions?action=detect-anomalies`; (4) frontend `useTimeseriesReferenceAnnotations.ts` URL updated. All request body fields and response shapes unchanged. | **PATH RENAME on `/v2/` surface** — `POST /v2/references/{appId}/detect-anomalies` now returns 410 Gone; callers must switch to `POST /v2/references/{appId}/actions?action=detect-anomalies`. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is untouched. | — (no schema migration; Java source + frontend changes only). | **Action required for any direct caller of `POST /v2/references/{appId}/detect-anomalies`.** Rename the endpoint URL to `POST /v2/references/{appId}/actions?action=detect-anomalies`. The request body (`AnomalyDetectRequestIO`) and response shape (`AnomalyDetectResultIO`) are unchanged. The old path now returns 410 Gone with a `Location` header pointing to the new path. |
| **URDF-FILEREF-PICKER-SEARCHABLE — new `GET /v2/references/urdf` lists accessible URDF singletons instance-wide for the searchable 3D-view picker** | 2026-07-10 (branch `URDF-FILEREF-PICKER-SEARCHABLE-impl`). `aidocs/16` URDF-FILEREF-PICKER-SEARCHABLE. The "Visualize in 3D → URDF" picker could only ever list URDFs on the current DataObject (`GET /v2/references?kind=file&fileKind=urdf&dataObjectAppId=…` requires `dataObjectAppId`), but the dialog opens from a timeseries whose DataObject has no URDF — forcing the user to paste a UUID (violates the "user never types an ID" rule). Adds a new **additive** endpoint `GET /v2/references/urdf?q=&page=&pageSize=` (`ReferencesV2Rest.listAccessibleUrdf` → `AccessibleUrdfService` → `SingletonFileReferenceDAO.findAllUrdfCandidates`) that lists every `.urdf` singleton FileReference (name ends `.urdf` case-insensitive OR `fileKind=urdf`) the caller may **Read**, across all collections, permission-filtered per parent Collection via `PermissionsService.filterAllowedDataObjectAppIds`. New `AccessibleUrdfIO` returns `{appId, name, dataObjectAppId, collectionAppId, collectionName}` so the picker renders a `"<name> — <collection>"` label. Paged (`pageSize` default 50, max 200), optional `q` substring name filter, fail-soft (empty page, never 5xx). Frontend `ViewRecipeBuilderDialog` URDF field is now a searchable `v-autocomplete` over this list (advanced paste-appId fallback kept one window). Tests: `AccessibleUrdfServiceTest` (8), `ReferencesV2RestTest` (+3), `useFetchReferenceOptions.test.ts` (+5). | **ZERO** — purely additive new `/v2/` route + new nullable IO. No `/shepard/api/…` v1 path touched. No Neo4j label/property added (reads existing `SingletonFileReference.name`/`fileKind` + `Collection.appId`/`name`). | — (no migration; read-only Cypher projection + Java/TS source only). | **No operator action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; the endpoint is live immediately and the URDF picker populates itself. To verify: `curl -fsS 'https://<host>/v2/references/urdf?q=kr210' -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'` → paged JSON of accessible URDF references. Note: only URDF singletons are listed — legacy single-file URDF *bundles* must first be migrated to singletons (see `SINGLETON-FILE-MIGRATION`). |
| **MP4-PROMOTE-VIDEO — `.mp4` (+ common video) singleton FileReferences recognized as `fileKind="video"` + rendered inline; V119 backfill migration** | 2026-07-10 (branch `MP4-PROMOTE-VIDEO-impl`). `aidocs/16` MP4-PROMOTE-VIDEO. No `FileKindDetector` claimed `.mp4`, so the ~178 existing MP4 `:SingletonFileReference` nodes had `fileKind = NULL` and rendered as opaque files. Fix (additive `fileKind=video` seam — NOT `VideoStreamReference` minting, which would be a huge/risky data migration): (1) new core `VideoFileKindDetector` (`de.dlr.shepard.spi.payload`) claims `mp4, mov, m4v, avi, mkv, webm, mpg, mpeg, wmv → "video"` (case-insensitive) — every future upload/ingest tags `fileKind=video` automatically via `SingletonFileReferenceService.detectFileKind`; (2) idempotent Neo4j backfill migration `V119__promote_video_filekind.cypher` sets `fileKind="video"` on `:SingletonFileReference` whose lowercased `.name` ends in a video extension AND `fileKind IS NULL` (~178 rows on the live box); paired rollback `V119_R__`; (3) frontend renders `fileKind=video` File refs as video (inline `VideoPlayer` on the file-reference detail page + video icon + clickable row in `DataObjectDataReferencesTable`). No bytes/containers moved — metadata tag only. JUnit (detector + migration static-file), Vitest (mapper + `isInlineVideoFileRow`). | **ZERO / ADDITIVE + REVERSIBLE** — Neo4j-schemaless additive nullable property; no `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched; no new config keys; no wire-shape change. Pre-feature rows without `fileKind` read as `null` exactly as before. | `V119__promote_video_filekind.cypher` — `MATCH (r:SingletonFileReference) WHERE r.fileKind IS NULL AND toLower(r.name) ENDS WITH '.<video-ext>' SET r.fileKind = 'video'`. Idempotent (`fileKind IS NULL` guard → re-run matches 0 rows), non-clobbering. Rollback: `V119_R__promote_video_filekind.cypher` (`REMOVE r.fileKind` scoped to video-named + `fileKind='video'`). Migration regression test (testcontainer pre/post assertion) tracked as `MP4-PROMOTE-VIDEO-MIGRATION-TESTS`; a static-file test (`V119MigrationFileTest`) ships now. | **No action required for upgrade.** Restart shepard; V119 runs automatically and tags existing MP4 references. Verify: `MATCH (r:SingletonFileReference) WHERE r.fileKind='video' RETURN count(r);` → ~178. New video uploads are auto-tagged. To roll back: run `V119_R__promote_video_filekind.cypher` via `cypher-shell` (safe on a freshly-migrated install; see the migration top-comment for the caveat on post-migration uploads). |
| **APISIMP-UNHIDE-FEED-PAGECAP — `GET /v2/unhide/feed.jsonld` `pageSize` cap lowered `@Max(500)` → `@Max(200)`; `@Operation` description corrected** | 2026-07-11 (fire-543, PR #2476, sha `d6b2a8ed`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-UNHIDE-FEED-PAGECAP. `UnhideFeedRest.java:138` declared `@Max(500)` (description "Range [1, 500]") and the `@Operation` description incorrectly stated "page-size capped at 1000". Lowered to `@Max(200)`, updated parameter description to "Range [1, 200]", fixed `@Operation` description to "capped at 200". The `UnhideFeedService.MAX_PAGE_SIZE = 1000` service-layer constant is unchanged (internal; unreachable via REST). | **AWARE** — callers of `GET /v2/unhide/feed.jsonld` requesting `?pageSize=N` where N > 200 now receive HTTP 400 (Bean Validation). The Helmholtz harvester uses the default (100) and is unaffected. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. | — (no schema migration; Java source changes only). | **Check for any scripts requesting `pageSize > 200` on the Unhide feed endpoint and lower them to ≤ 200.** The default (100) and response shape are unchanged. |
| **APISIMP-SNAPPINNED-PAGECAP — `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/snapshots/{appId}/data-objects` `pageSize` cap lowered `@Max(2000)` → `@Max(200)`, default lowered 500 → 50** | 2026-07-11 (fire-543, branch `APISIMP-SNAPPINNED-PAGECAP-1`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-SNAPPINNED-PAGECAP. `SnapshotPinnedReadRest.java:133` declared `@Max(2000)` with `@DefaultValue("500")` — both dramatically above the v2-standard cap. The default was also lowered from 500 to 50 to avoid a Bean Validation 400 for callers not passing `pageSize` (since 500 > 200). The `@Schema(maximum, defaultValue)` annotation and `@Operation` description text updated to match. 1 new reflection-based regression test `maxPageSize_isAtMost200` in `SnapshotPinnedReadRestTest` (asserts `@Max` value ≤ 200 via `getAnnotation(Max.class)`); existing `paginates_beyondLastPage_returnsEmptyList` test updated from `pageSize=500/skip=5000` to `pageSize=50/skip=500`. The `subList()` slicing logic in the method body and `SnapshotDataObjectsIO` record are unchanged. | **AWARE** — (a) callers requesting `?pageSize=N` where N > 200 now receive HTTP 400; (b) callers NOT passing `pageSize` now receive 50 results per page (was 500). The endpoint is paginated; check `X-Total-Count` header and page through if needed. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. | — (no schema migration; Java source changes only). | **Check scripts calling `GET /v2/collections/…/snapshots/…/data-objects` that rely on the old default of 500 items/page or request > 200 items/page. Add explicit `?pageSize=N` (N ≤ 200) or paginate using `X-Total-Count`.** |
| **APISIMP-XTOTALCOUNT-DUAL-EMIT-DROP — `X-Total-Count` response header removed from all v2 dual-emit list endpoints; `body.total` (in `PagedResponseIO`) is now the sole SSOT for the total count** | 2026-07-11 (fire-546, branch `APISIMP-XTOTALCOUNT-DUAL-EMIT-DROP`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-XTOTALCOUNT-DUAL-EMIT-DROP. Prior to this change, all v2 list endpoints emitted the total count in two places: `PagedResponseIO.total` in the body (canonical, added by `APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE`) AND `X-Total-Count` header (redundant, kept "during deprecation window" after `APISIMP-XTOTALCOUNT-BATCH`). This fire closes the deprecation window for all dual-emit endpoints (~35 REST source files, ~51 call sites), leaving `X-Total-Count` only on `DataObjectV2Rest.listDataObjects` (plain-array endpoint, excluded — see `APISIMP-XTOTALCOUNT-LIST-DO` queued). Also removed stale `headers = @Header(name="X-Total-Count", ...)` OpenAPI annotation blocks (docs must match reality) and now-unused `Header`/`SchemaType` imports. **Exclusion:** `DataObjectV2Rest.listDataObjects` still emits `X-Total-Count` + `Content-Range` (plain-array response, `usePagedDataObjects.ts` reads the header). | **AWARE** — callers of any v2 dual-emit list endpoint that read ONLY `X-Total-Count` header (and not `body.total`) will stop seeing the header. `DataObjectV2Rest.listDataObjects` is **unchanged** (still emits the header). No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. All `PagedResponseIO` body shapes (`{items, total, page, pageSize}`) are unchanged — `body.total` is the correct read target and was always present. | — (no schema migration; Java source changes only). | **Migrate any script or caller reading `X-Total-Count` from a `/v2/` list endpoint to instead read `body.total` from the `PagedResponseIO` JSON response body.** Example: `const total = response.body.total` instead of `response.headers.get("X-Total-Count")`. `DataObjectV2Rest.listDataObjects` (`GET /v2/data-objects?...`) is the one endpoint where the header still exists (will be removed in a future `APISIMP-XTOTALCOUNT-LIST-DO` fire). |
| **APISIMP-NAME-ALIAS-RETIRE — `?name=` query parameter removed from `GET /v2/containers`, `GET /v2/collections`, and `GET /v2/data-objects/{collectionAppId}/data-objects`** | 2026-07-11 (fire-547, branch `APISIMP-NAME-ALIAS-RETIRE`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-NAME-ALIAS-RETIRE. Three `/v2/` list endpoints carried a deprecated `@QueryParam("name")` backward-compat alias alongside the canonical `?q=` text-search param (introduced in the fire-510 range). While the alias was active, using `?name=` emitted a `Deprecation: true` response header; the deprecation window elapsed at fire-545. This fire removes the aliases entirely: `ContainersV2Rest.list()`, `CollectionV2Rest.list()`, and `DataObjectV2Rest.list()` no longer declare `@QueryParam("name")`. The `Deprecation` response header is no longer emitted. Three `list_nameParamIsGone` reflection tests guard against re-introduction. Note: JAX-RS silently ignores unknown query params, so a caller still passing `?name=` will NOT receive a 400 — they will receive an unfiltered result (the name filter is simply not applied). Callers relying on `?name=` for filtering will silently get wrong results. | **AWARE** — callers of `GET /v2/containers?name=`, `GET /v2/collections?name=`, or `GET /v2/data-objects/{collectionAppId}/data-objects?name=` for filtering will now receive unfiltered results (no 400, but the filter is silently dropped). The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is **unchanged**. | — (no schema migration; Java source + test changes only). | **Migrate callers of the three `/v2/` list endpoints that use `?name=` to use `?q=` instead.** The semantics (case-insensitive substring match on display name) are identical. This fork's own frontend already uses `?q=` exclusively; only external clients built against the deprecated param need updating. |
| **APISIMP-XTOTALCOUNT-LIST-DO — `X-Total-Count` response header removed from `DataObjectV2Rest.listDataObjects` and 5 sibling endpoints; `Content-Range` is now the sole pagination metadata header** | 2026-07-11 (fire-547, branch `APISIMP-XTOTALCOUNT-LIST-DO-2`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-XTOTALCOUNT-LIST-DO. `DataObjectV2Rest.listDataObjects` (`GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects`) was the last v2 endpoint still emitting `X-Total-Count` after `APISIMP-XTOTALCOUNT-DUAL-EMIT-DROP` (fire-546). The endpoint returns a plain JSON array (not `PagedResponseIO`) with `Content-Range` + `X-Total-Count` headers; `usePagedDataObjects.ts` previously read `X-Total-Count` as the fast-path. Fix: `usePagedDataObjects.ts` `parseTotalCount()` simplified to `Content-Range`-only (the same value, always present); `.header("X-Total-Count", ...)` removed from `list()` and its 3 early-return branches (`parentAppId`, `predecessorAppId`, `successorAppId` unknown-entity returns). Also cleaned the 5 sibling endpoints (`predecessors`, `successors`, `children`, `predecessorChain`, `successorChain`) which emitted `X-Total-Count` on top of the `PagedResponseIO` body total — redundant since `APISIMP-XTOTALCOUNT-DUAL-EMIT-DROP`. Removed stale `@Header` OpenAPI annotation blocks from all 6 methods. `grep -rn 'X-Total-Count' backend/src/main/java/de/dlr/shepard/v2/dataobject/` now returns empty. `DataObjectV2RestTest` updated to assert `assertNull("X-Total-Count")` in both count-header tests. `usePagedDataObjects.dbopt5.test.ts` mock switched from `X-Total-Count` to `Content-Range` header. | **AWARE** — callers of `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects` that read ONLY the `X-Total-Count` header for the total count will stop seeing it. Migrate to `Content-Range` (`/(\d+)\s*$` tail captures the total). `predecessors`, `successors`, `children`, `predecessorChain`, `successorChain` similarly no longer emit the header — use `body.total` from the `PagedResponseIO` envelope. The `/shepard/api/` v1 surface is **unchanged**. | — (no schema migration; Java source + TS composable changes only). | **Migrate callers reading `X-Total-Count` from `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects` to parse the `Content-Range` header instead.** Pattern: `/\/(\d+)\s*$/.exec(contentRange)?.[1]`. For the predecessor/successor/children/chain endpoints, read `body.total` from the `PagedResponseIO` JSON response body. |

Frontend `FileContainerAccessor.ts` three-step presigned upload migrated: destructure `{ uploadUrl, fileId }` and commit body key `fileId`. | **WIRE BREAK** — callers of the presigned-upload flow: (1) `POST /v2/containers/{appId}/upload-url` response: `.oid` → `.fileId`; (2) `POST /v2/containers/{appId}/upload-url/commit` request body: `{"oid": ...}` → `{"fileId": ...}`. | **APISIMP-NOTEBOOK-LIST-PAGINATE — `GET /v2/lab-journal/{appId}/notebooks` response shape changed from bare array to `PagedResponseIO` envelope** | 2026-07-12 (fire-554, branch `APISIMP-NOTEBOOK-LIST-PAGINATE`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-NOTEBOOK-LIST-PAGINATE. `NotebookRest.listNotebooks` previously returned a raw `Response.ok(List<NotebookReferenceIO>)` with no pagination params — the only v2 list endpoint still lacking `PagedResponseIO`. Fix: added `@QueryParam("page")` (default 0) and `@QueryParam("pageSize")` (default 50, max 200) params; response shape now `{"items":[…],"total":N,"page":0,"pageSize":50}`. The in-memory assembled list is sliced after building (both FR1b singleton + FR1a bundle sources), so no DAO change was needed. Frontend composable `useFetchNotebooks.ts` was already reading `.items` from a `PagedResponse` type in anticipation of this change. No v1 surface touched. | **AWARE — v2 wire-shape change** — `GET /v2/lab-journal/{appId}/notebooks` response changed from a JSON array to a `PagedResponseIO` envelope. Pass `?page=0&pageSize=50` (or omit for defaults). |

| **APISIMP-PROBLEM-GENERIC-THROWS — direct JAX-RS exception throws replaced with `problem()` in `FileMigrationRest` (6 sites) and `SqlTimeseriesRest` (2 sites)** | 2026-07-12 (fire-555, branch `APISIMP-PROBLEM-GENERIC-THROWS`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-PROBLEM-GENERIC-THROWS. `FileMigrationRest` threw `BadRequestException`, `NotFoundException`, and `WebApplicationException` directly; `SqlTimeseriesRest` threw `BadRequestException` at the MAX_CONTAINERS guard and the compiler-error catch. The `ShepardExceptionMapper` assigned only generic type URIs (`/problems/not_found_entity`, `/problems/bad_request`) to these responses, breaking RFC 7807 machine-readability. Fix: added `import static de.dlr.shepard.v2.common.ProblemResponse.problem;`; added 6 `PT_*` resource-specific type-URI constants to `FileMigrationRest` and 2 to `SqlTimeseriesRest`; replaced all 8 throw sites with `return problem(PT_*, title, status, detail)`; removed now-unused `BadRequestException`, `NotFoundException`, `WebApplicationException` imports. | **AWARE — error body enrichment on `/v2/admin/files/migrate` and `POST /v2/sql/timeseries` error paths.** HTTP status codes are **unchanged** (400 / 404 / 409 / 500). Error response `Content-Type` was already `application/problem+json` (via `ShepardExceptionMapper`); it remains so. The `type` URI in the error body changes from a generic `/problems/bad_request` or `/problems/not_found_entity` to a resource-specific URI (e.g. `/problems/file-migration.rollback.not-found`). Callers matching on the generic type URI must update their error handler. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** Callers checking the `type` field of 400/404/409/500 error responses from these endpoints should update to the new resource-specific type URIs. Callers checking only the HTTP status code are unaffected. |

Fix: changed `FileGroupIO` to extend `BasicEntityV2IO` (which carries `@JsonIgnoreProperties({"id"})`). All other v2 IO classes (`CollectionV2IO`, `DataObjectDetailV2IO`, `BasicContainerV2IO`, `BasicReferenceV2IO`) already suppressed `id`; this closes the last gap. No v1 surface touched. | **AWARE — v2 wire-shape change** — `GET/POST/PATCH /v2/bundles/{bundleAppId}/groups/…` responses no longer include an `"id"` top-level field. Only the `"appId"` UUID v7 field identifies a group. | — (no schema migration; one `extends` change). | **No action required** unless you have an external client reading `"id"` from FileGroup responses — use `"appId"` instead. |

The old long fields are kept with `@Deprecated @Schema(deprecated=true)` for one deprecation window. | **APISIMP-ONTOLOGY-ALIGNMENT-EPOCH-MS-TO-ISO — `OntologyAlignmentIO.createdAt` changed from epoch-ms `Long` to ISO 8601 `String`** | 2026-07-13 (fire-578, branch `APISIMP-ONTOLOGY-ALIGNMENT-EPOCH-MS-TO-ISO`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-ONTOLOGY-ALIGNMENT-EPOCH-MS-TO-ISO. `OntologyAlignmentIO.createdAt` was typed `Long` and described as "Epoch-millis timestamp when the row was first created by the migration." on the `GET /v2/semantic/ontology/alignment` response. Fix: changed field type to `String`; added `toIso()` private static helper using `DateTimeFormatter.ISO_INSTANT.format(Instant.ofEpochMilli(ms))` with null-guard; `OntologyAlignmentIO.from()` calls `toIso(entity.getCreatedAt())`; `@Schema` description updated to "ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the row was first created by the migration. Null for rows seeded before this field was added." Tests: `OntologyAlignmentRestTest` updated — `createdAt` assertions changed from raw `Long` values to ISO 8601 strings (`"1970-01-01T00:00:01Z"`, `"1970-01-01T00:00:00.999Z"`); null-case uses `assertNull()`. | **AWARE — v2 wire-shape change** — `GET /v2/semantic/ontology/alignment` `createdAt` field in each array element changes from a numeric millisecond value (e.g. `1751356800000`) to an ISO 8601 UTC string (e.g. `"2026-07-01T00:00:00Z"`). Null is still valid for rows seeded before the `createdAt` field was introduced. The endpoint is `@RolesAllowed("instance-admin")` so scope is limited to admin tooling and direct API callers. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. No Neo4j schema or data migration needed (the `:OntologyAlignment` entity stores `createdAt` as `Long` epoch-ms; conversion is at the IO layer only). | — (no schema migration; Java source and test changes only). | **No operator action required.** Callers reading `createdAt` as a number from this endpoint must update to parse an ISO 8601 string instead. Null-safety is unchanged. Verify after upgrade: `GET /v2/semantic/ontology/alignment` (as instance-admin) → each object's `createdAt` is a quoted ISO string or `null`. |

After the 10-fire stabilization window, the tombstone param and rejection branch are removed from `ProvenanceRest.stats()`. The `@APIResponse(400)` description is simplified to drop the entityId reference. | **AWARE — tombstone behavior removed on `GET /v2/provenance/stats`** — callers still passing `?entityId=` will no longer receive a 400 migration error; JAX-RS ignores the unknown param and the request proceeds on its remaining params.
