| **APISIMP-COLLECTION-EVENT-EPOCH-MS-TO-ISO — `CollectionEventIO.timestamp` changed from epoch-ms `long` to ISO 8601 `String` in the SSE collection change-feed** | 2026-07-13 (fire-579, branch `APISIMP-COLLECTION-EVENT-EPOCH-MS-TO-ISO`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-COLLECTION-EVENT-EPOCH-MS-TO-ISO. `CollectionEventIO.timestamp` was typed `long` (epoch-ms) on the SSE wire-format record for `GET /v2/collections/{appId}/events`. Fix: changed field type to `String`; added public `static String toIso(long epochMs)` helper using `DateTimeFormatter.ISO_INSTANT.format(Instant.ofEpochMilli(epochMs))`; updated all 6 call sites in `CollectionEventProducer` (4 event types) and `CollectionEventBus` (subscribe heartbeat + periodic heartbeat). `CollectionEventsRest` `@Operation` description updated to say "ISO 8601 UTC string". Frontend: `CollectionEventIO` TypeScript interface in `useCollectionEvents.ts` changed `timestamp: number` → `timestamp: string`; test fixtures updated. 7 new JUnit tests in `CollectionEventIOTest` covering epoch 0, round millis, sub-second, known timestamp, Z-suffix, field reflection, and heartbeat construction. | **AWARE — v2 SSE wire-shape change** — each SSE event delivered by `GET /v2/collections/{appId}/events` now carries `timestamp` as an ISO 8601 UTC string (e.g. `"2026-07-13T10:21:00Z"`) rather than a numeric epoch-ms value. Any client that parses the `data:` JSON payload and reads `timestamp` as a number must update to `new Date(event.timestamp)` (ISO string → `Date`) or `Date.parse(event.timestamp)`. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. No Neo4j schema or data migration needed (conversion is at the IO layer only). | — (no schema migration; Java source, test, and frontend TS changes only). | **Update SSE clients** reading `CollectionEventIO.timestamp` as a number: change to ISO string parsing (`new Date(ts)` or `Date.parse(ts)`). This fork's own frontend (`useCollectionEvents.ts`) is already updated. |
| **APISIMP-PROVENANCE-STATS-EPOCH-MS-TO-ISO — `ProvenanceStatsIO.sinceMillis`/`untilMillis` renamed `since`/`until` and changed from epoch-ms `long` to ISO 8601 `String`** | 2026-07-13 (fire-581, branch `APISIMP-PROVENANCE-STATS-EPOCH-MS-TO-ISO`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-PROVENANCE-STATS-EPOCH-MS-TO-ISO. `ProvenanceStatsIO` (`GET /v2/provenance/stats`) exposed `sinceMillis` and `untilMillis` as `long` epoch-ms window-echo fields. These are query-parameter echoes (effective window bounds after defaulting), not durations or sensor timestamps — inconsistent with the ISO 8601 campaign. `bucketMillis` is a *duration* (not a timestamp) and stays `long`. Fix: renamed fields to `since`/`until`; changed type to `String`; added `public static String toIso(long epochMs)` helper (`DateTimeFormatter.ISO_INSTANT.format(Instant.ofEpochMilli(epochMs))`); `ProvenanceStatsService.compute()` calls `ProvenanceStatsIO.toIso(sinceMillis)` and `toIso(untilMillis)` when constructing the response. New `ProvenanceStatsIOTest` (2 tests) guards the helper. | **AWARE — v2 wire-shape change** — `GET /v2/provenance/stats` response fields `sinceMillis`/`untilMillis` (numeric) are replaced by `since`/`until` (ISO 8601 UTC strings, e.g. `"2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"`). `bucketMillis`, `buckets`, `cumulative`, and all other fields unchanged. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. No Neo4j migration needed (conversion is at the IO layer). | — (no schema migration; Java source and test changes only). | **No operator action required.** Callers reading `sinceMillis`/`untilMillis` as numbers from `GET /v2/provenance/stats` must update to read `since`/`until` as ISO 8601 strings instead. Parse with `Instant.parse(since)` (Java) or `new Date(since)` (JS). `bucketMillis` remains numeric. |
| **APISIMP-LJE-ENTRY-V2-CRUD — `GET/PUT/DELETE /v2/lab-journal/{appId}` + `LabJournalEntryIO.id` suppressed via `@JsonIgnore`** | 2026-07-13 (fire-582, branch `APISIMP-LJE-ENTRY-V2-CRUD`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-LJE-ENTRY-V2-CRUD. New `LabJournalEntryRest` (`@Path("/v2/lab-journal")`) adds three appId-keyed operations: `GET /{appId}` (Read-permission gate), `PUT /{appId}` (Write-permission + `assertIsCreator` gate, body `UpdateLabJournalEntryIO.journalContent`), `DELETE /{appId}` (Write-permission + `assertIsCreator` gate, 204). Resolves entry via existing `LabJournalEntryDAO.findByAppId(String)`, delegates to `LabJournalEntryService` with numeric `entry.getId()` for the actual mutation. `UpdateLabJournalEntryIO.java` new IO class. `LabJournalEntryIO.id` annotated `@JsonIgnore` (was already `@Deprecated`) — suppresses the numeric Neo4j OGM id from all JSON serialization. Backend-client: `LabJournalEntryV2Api.ts` new TS client; `UpdateLabJournalEntryV2.ts` new model; `LabJournalEntry.ts` `id` field made optional (`number \| null`). Frontend: `LabJournalExistingEntry.vue` migrated from `useShepardApi(LabJournalEntryApi)` to `useV2ShepardApi(LabJournalEntryV2Api)`; all three operations (`cancelEditing`, `saveChanges`, `deleteEntry`) now call the appId-keyed v2 endpoints; `getDataObjectLink()` switched from numeric `dataObjectId` to `dataObjectAppId`; title line shows `appId` instead of numeric `id`. `CollectionLabJournalEntryList.vue` + `DataObjectLabJournalEntryList.vue`: `:key="entry.id"` → `:key="entry.appId ?? index"` (id now optional/null). `useShepardApi(FileContainerApi)` retained in `fetchImage()` — no v2 image endpoint yet (tracked FILE-CONTAINER-V2 in `aidocs/16`). | **BREAKING — `LabJournalEntryIO.id` field removed from ALL serialized JSON responses, including the frozen `/shepard/api/` v1 surface.** `@JsonIgnore` applies globally; any v1 client reading `labJournalEntry.id` as a numeric node id will now receive `null` / missing field. The numeric `id` was already `@Deprecated` (fire-568) but was still present on the wire. New `/v2/lab-journal/{appId}` surface is additive (`/v2/` shelf) and has no upstream equivalent. | — (no Neo4j schema migration; additive REST + IO + frontend TS changes only). | **BREAKING for v1 `id` consumers:** Any client reading `LabJournalEntry.id` as a number from `GET /shepard/api/labJournalEntries?dataObjectId=N` or any other v1 lab-journal endpoint must switch to `appId` (UUID v7 string). Verify after upgrade: a `GET /shepard/api/labJournalEntries?dataObjectId=1` response should no longer include a numeric `"id"` field. New v2 operations: use `GET/PUT/DELETE /v2/lab-journal/{appId}` for single-entry lifecycle. |

Renamed to `@Path("/{appId}")` and `@PathParam("appId") String appId` throughout the PATCH method body (8 sites). The convenience test-overload `patch(String id, PluginPatchIO)` keeps `id` as its Java param name (not a `@PathParam`; delegates unchanged). Zero wire-shape change — the URL path segment is anonymous in JAX-RS; callers send the plugin-id value, not the variable name. OpenAPI operationId stays `patchPlugin`; the generated path-param name in the spec changes from `id` to `appId`. | **ZERO** — no wire-shape change; the URL segment value is unchanged. The `{…}` brace name is a JAX-RS extraction variable only. Generated OpenAPI client stubs that reference the old `id` param name must regenerate. | — (no schema migration; pure Java variable rename). | **No action required.** If you generate client stubs from the OpenAPI spec, re-run codegen; the path-param name in the generated method signature changes from `id` to `appId`. | **APISIMP-LJE-NUMERIC-PARENT-ID — lab-journal REST resources use `isAccessAllowedForDataObjectAppId` (appId-keyed) instead of `isAccessTypeAllowedForUser` (numeric id)** | 2026-07-14 (fire-596, PR #2556, branch `APISIMP-LJE-NUMERIC-PARENT-ID-fire596`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-LJE-NUMERIC-PARENT-ID. Five call sites in `LabJournalEntryRest` (lines 94, 135, 171), `LabJournalHistoryRest` (line 128), and `LabJournalRenderRest` (line 117) passed `dataObject.getId()` (Neo4j numeric id) to `permissionsService.isAccessTypeAllowedForUser()`. Replaced all five with `permissionsService.isAccessAllowedForDataObjectAppId(dataObject.getAppId(), accessType, caller)` — the v2 permission check keyed on stable UUID v7 appId. Behaviour unchanged; no wire-shape change. | **ZERO** — internal permission-call routing only; no REST endpoint path, request body, or response shape changed. | — (no schema migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** Internal correctness fix. |
| **APISIMP-MCP-TS-NANOS-ISO — MCP tool `@ToolArg` time-window params changed from `Long` nanoseconds to ISO 8601 `String`** | 2026-07-14 (fire-597, PR #2557, branch `APISIMP-MCP-TS-NANOS-ISO-fire597`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-MCP-TS-NANOS-ISO. Eight `@ToolArg` slots across `TimeseriesMcpTools` (`get_channel_data`: `startNanos`→`startTime`, `endNanos`→`endTime`; `ts_query_multi`: same) and `ReferencesMcpTools` (`reference_create`: `Long start`→`String start`, `Long end`→`String end`; `reference_update`: same) changed from raw nanosecond `Long` to ISO 8601 UTC `String`. Conversion at the MCP boundary via a private `isoToNanos(String)` helper using `Instant.parse(iso.trim()).getEpochSecond() * 1_000_000_000L + getNano()`. Response `window` dicts in both tools also updated: keys `startNanos`/`endNanos` → `startTime`/`endTime` (ISO strings). Tool descriptions updated; "Divide by 1_000_000" unit-disambiguation note removed. | **AWARE — MCP tool wire break** — MCP callers (`claude_desktop_config.json` / AI agents) of `get_channel_data`, `ts_query_multi`, `reference_create`, and `reference_update` must now pass ISO 8601 UTC strings (e.g. `"2024-06-01T08:00:00Z"`) for time-window args. Nanosecond `Long` values no longer accepted. Response `window.startTime`/`window.endTime` are ISO strings (previously `window.startNanos`/`window.endNanos` numeric longs). No `/shepard/api/...` REST endpoint or `/v2/` HTTP endpoint changed — MCP SSE surface only. | — (no schema migration; Java source and test changes only). | **Update MCP tool callers.** Pass ISO 8601 UTC strings for all time-window arguments instead of nanosecond longs. Point timestamps inside `channels[].points[].timestamp` remain nanosecond longs unchanged. |
| **APISIMP-COLLECTION-PERMISSIONS-PUT-VS-PATCH — `PUT /v2/collections/{appId}/permissions` changed to `PATCH` with `Content-Type: application/merge-patch+json`** | 2026-07-14 (fire-597, branch `APISIMP-COLLECTION-PERMISSIONS-PUT-VS-PATCH-fire597`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-COLLECTION-PERMISSIONS-PUT-VS-PATCH. `CollectionPermissionsRest.editPermissions` was annotated `@PUT` with class-level `@Consumes(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)`. REST semantics require `PATCH` + RFC 7396 `Content-Type: application/merge-patch+json` for a partial-update operation; `PUT` implies idempotent full replacement and lacks Content-Type specificity. Fix: import changed `PUT → PATCH`; method annotation changed `@PUT → @PATCH`; method-level `@Consumes("application/merge-patch+json")` added (overrides class-level `application/json`); `@Operation` description and `@APIResponse` annotations updated. Generated TypeScript client `CollectionPermissionsApi.ts` updated: `method: 'PUT' → 'PATCH'`, `Content-Type: 'application/json' → 'application/merge-patch+json'`. 12 unit tests added in `CollectionPermissionsRestTest` (4 per endpoint: `getPermissions`, `editPermissions`, `getRoles`). | **AWARE — wire-breaking on `PATCH /v2/collections/{appId}/permissions`** — callers must switch HTTP verb from `PUT` to `PATCH` and `Content-Type` from `application/json` to `application/merge-patch+json`. Response shape (`PermissionsIO`) is unchanged. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source + TS client changes only). | **Update REST clients calling this endpoint:** change verb to `PATCH` and add `Content-Type: application/merge-patch+json`. This fork's own frontend (`CollectionAccessor.ts` via generated `CollectionPermissionsApi`) is already updated. |
| **APISIMP-SEARCH-IN-MEMORY-MERGE-PAGE (option-a cap) — `GET /v2/search` combined result set capped at 1 000 items** | 2026-07-14 (fire-598, branch `APISIMP-SEARCH-IN-MEMORY-MERGE-PAGE-fire598`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-SEARCH-IN-MEMORY-MERGE-PAGE. `SearchV2Rest.search()` previously fetched ALL matching Collections + ALL matching DataObjects into JVM heap, merged them, then sliced with `List.subList`. This is option-a of a two-phase fix: a `SEARCH_RESULT_CAP = 1000` constant truncates the combined list before paging; `total` reflects the capped count when exceeded. Cap documented in OpenAPI `description` and `@APIResponse(200)`. Option-b (DAO-level SKIP/LIMIT) queued as follow-up in the same backlog row. Two regression tests added. No change to endpoint path, request/response shapes, or `/shepard/api/` v1 surface. | **AWARE** — `GET /v2/search?q=<broad>` responses where the combined match count exceeded 1 000 will now return `total=1000` instead of the true match count, and items beyond position 999 are not returned. The previous behaviour of returning a page from beyond position 999 is no longer available until option-b ships. **Workaround:** narrow the query with `collectionAppId` to scope results within a single collection. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** Callers should use `collectionAppId` to narrow large result sets. |
| **APISIMP-PROVENANCE-STATS-BUCKET-ARRAY — `GET /v2/provenance/stats` `buckets`/`cumulative` fields changed from raw `long[]` arrays to typed `{t, count}` objects** | 2026-07-14 (fire-601, branch `APISIMP-PROVENANCE-STATS-BUCKET-ARRAY-fire601`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-PROVENANCE-STATS-BUCKET-ARRAY. `ProvenanceStatsIO` previously exposed `List<long[]> buckets` and `List<long[]> cumulative` where index 0 of each inner array was `bucketStartMillis` (epoch-ms long) and index 1 was a count — a positional contract OpenAPI cannot describe. Inconsistent with `since`/`until` (already ISO 8601 strings on the same IO). Fix: introduced inner `record BucketIO(String t, long count)` on `ProvenanceStatsIO`; `t` is ISO 8601 UTC bucket-start (via `ProvenanceStatsIO.toIso()`); `count` is the activity count. `buckets` and `cumulative` fields are now `List<BucketIO>`. DAO-level `ActivityDAO.StatsSnapshot.buckets` stays `List<long[]>` (internal contract); conversion happens in `ProvenanceStatsService.compute()`. `cumulativeIntegral` static helper updated accordingly. Tests updated from array-index access to record-accessor calls (`.t()`, `.count()`). | **AWARE — v2 wire-shape change** — `GET /v2/provenance/stats` response fields `buckets` and `cumulative` change element type from a 2-element JSON number array `[epochMs, count]` to a JSON object `{"t": "2026-07-14T00:00:00Z", "count": 42}`. Callers reading `buckets[i][0]` (timestamp) and `buckets[i][1]` (count) must update to `buckets[i].t` and `buckets[i].count`. Same for `cumulative`. No other fields changed. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. No Neo4j migration needed. | — (no schema migration; Java source and test changes only). | **No operator action required.** Update REST/JS callers of `GET /v2/provenance/stats` that destructure `buckets` or `cumulative` as arrays: replace `buckets[i][0]` with `buckets[i].t` (ISO 8601 string) and `buckets[i][1]` with `buckets[i].count` (number). |

Fix is purely additive: each file gains (1) `import org.eclipse.microprofile.openapi.annotations.headers.Header`, (2) `headers = @Header(name = "X-Total-Count", ...)` in the 200 `@APIResponse`, (3) `.header("X-Total-Count", total)` on the `Response` builder. **Additive-only — no wire value changes for existing callers; new header only.** Endpoints: `LabJournalHistoryRest.history()`, `CollectionLabJournalEntriesRest.list()`, `NotebookRest.list()`, `ReferenceAnnotationRest.list()`, `CollectionContainersRest.list()`, `CollectionV2Rest.list()`, `AdminUserGitCredentialRest.list()`, `SemanticAdminRest.listOntologies()`, `OntologyGitSourceRest.list()`, `ReferencesV2Rest.list()` + `listAccessibleUrdf()`. | **ADDITIVE — 10 `/v2/` endpoints now emit `X-Total-Count` in the response. Callers that prefer HTTP header pagination can now use the header on these endpoints. | — (no schema or data migration). | **No operator action required.** The header is now available on all standard v2 paged list endpoints. |

The `@APIResponse(responseCode="400")` annotation for the blank-appId guard was also missing. Fix: added `content = @Content(...)` to the 200 block and inserted a 400 block with `ProblemJson` schema. | **APISIMP-REFS-INMEM-PAGING — `countByDataObject` and paginated `listByDataObject(skip, limit)` for `FileReferenceKindHandler` and `UriReferenceKindHandler` pushed to Cypher; in-memory `subList` anti-pattern eliminated** | 2026-07-16 (fire-634, branch `APISIMP-REFS-INMEM-PAGING`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-REFS-INMEM-PAGING. `ReferenceKindHandler`'s default `countByDataObject` loaded all references then computed `size()`; the default `listByDataObject(appId, subKind, skip, limit)` loaded all then Java-`subList`-sliced. Fix: (1) `SingletonFileReferenceDAO` — new `countByDataObjectAppId(String, String subKind)` Cypher `count(r)` query (with optional `AND r.fileKind = $subKind` when non-null); new `findByDataObjectAppId(String, String subKind, int skip, int limit)` Cypher SKIP/LIMIT query with OPTIONAL MATCH `has_payload → ShepardFile` path-projection. (2) `URIReferenceDAO` — new `countByDataObjectAppId(String)` Cypher `count(r)` query; new `findByDataObjectAppId(String, int skip, int limit)` Cypher SKIP/LIMIT query returning `r, d, hr`. (3) `SingletonFileReferenceService` + `URIReferenceService` — new `countByDataObject`/`listByDataObject` service wrappers. (4) `FileReferenceKindHandler` + `UriReferenceKindHandler` — override both `countByDataObject(appId, subKind)` and `listByDataObject(appId, subKind, skip, limit)` calling the new service methods. `resolveParent()` still validates DataObject existence. 10 new unit tests across `SingletonFileReferenceDAOPagingTest` (new class) and `URIReferenceDAOTest`. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. | **ZERO** — no wire-shape change; no API surface change. The `/v2/references` response shape is identical. This is a backend-only performance fix: COUNT and SKIP/LIMIT are now computed by Neo4j rather than in the JVM. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source and test changes only). | **No operator action required.** |
| **APISIMP-CHANNEL-SHEPARDID-PREMATURE-RENAME — `channelShepardId`/`shepardIds` renamed to `channelAppId`/`channelAppIds` in v2 containers channel surface** | 2026-07-15 (fire-622, branch `APISIMP-CHANNEL-SHEPARDID-PREMATURE-RENAME`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-CHANNEL-SHEPARDID-PREMATURE-RENAME. Fire-APISIMP-CHANNEL-SHEPARDID-NORMALIZE (fire-558) had renamed `{shepardId}` → `{channelShepardId}` in `ContainersV2Rest`. This created an inconsistency with `ReferenceActionsRest` which already used `channelAppId` for the same concept, and with CLAUDE.md which prohibits ad-hoc `appId→shepardId` renames ahead of the coordinated task #123 pass. Fix: (1) `ContainersV2Rest`: all `@Path("/{appId}/channels/{channelShepardId}/…")` annotations → `{channelAppId}`; `@QueryParam("shepardId")` → `@QueryParam("channelAppId")` on the live-window endpoint (WIRE CHANGE); `@Parameter` descriptions updated. (2) `BulkChannelDataRequestIO`: record field `shepardIds` → `channelAppIds`; `@Schema(description …)` updated (WIRE CHANGE — JSON key in `POST /v2/containers/{appId}/channels/data/bulk` body). (3) `ContainerKindHandler` SPI: `@param channelShepardId` → `@param channelAppId` in Javadoc; method parameter names updated. (4) `TimeseriesContainerKindHandler`: all three overrides updated. (5) Generated TS client `ContainersApi.ts`, `BulkChannelDataRequest.ts`: all `channelShepardId`/`shepardIds` identifiers → `channelAppId`/`channelAppIds`. (6) Frontend: `AnnotatedChannel` class, `useFetchChannelPreview.ts`, `ChannelPreviewChart.vue`, `TimeseriesMeasurementsTable.vue`, `shapesRenderChannels.ts`, `ChannelAnnotationsPane.vue`, `placeholderRegistry.ts`, `[timeseriesReferenceId]/index.vue` updated; all tests updated. No v1 `/shepard/api/` surface touched. | **AWARE — two v2 wire-shape changes:** (1) `GET /v2/containers/{appId}/channels/live-window?channelAppId=…` — query param renamed from `?shepardId=` to `?channelAppId=`. (2) `POST /v2/containers/{appId}/channels/data/bulk` request body — JSON key `"shepardIds"` → `"channelAppIds"`. Path segment `{channelAppId}` in URL is JAX-RS extraction only — no HTTP change. `@PathParam` variable names are not exposed on the wire. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source, generated TS client, and frontend changes only). | **Update REST callers:** (1) `GET …/channels/live-window` — change query param from `?shepardId=<uuid>` to `?channelAppId=<uuid>`. (2) `POST …/channels/data/bulk` — change request body key from `"shepardIds"` to `"channelAppIds"`. This fork's own frontend and generated client are already updated. |
| **APISIMP-XCOUNT-WAVE4 — add `X-Total-Count` response header and formal `@Header` OpenAPI declaration to 34 v2 paged list endpoints across 22 files missed by WAVE1–3 sweeps** | 2026-07-16 (fire-623, branch `APISIMP-XCOUNT-WAVE4`, PR pending). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-XCOUNT-WAVE4. Remaining v2 list endpoints that still lacked the standard `X-Total-Count` response header and the `headers = @Header(...)` declaration in their `@APIResponse(responseCode="200")` block. Endpoints span: `ContainersV2Rest` (`list`, `getLinkedDataObjects`, `listChannels`), `DataObjectV2Rest` (`predecessors`, `successors`, `children`, `predecessorChain`, `successorChain`), `SemanticAnnotationV2Rest` (`list`, `find`), `PluginsAdminRest` (`list`), `InstanceAdminRest` (`listInstanceAdmins`, `permissionAudit`, `permissionAuditLog`), `BundleGroupsV2Rest` (`listGroups`, `listGroupFiles`), `CollectionWatchersRest` (`list`), `TimeseriesContainerKindHandler` (`listChannelAnnotations`, `listTemporalAnnotations`), `NotificationRest` (`list`), `NotificationTransportRest` (`list`), `ProjectsRest` (`list`), `ProvenanceRest` (`listActivities`, `listEntityActivities` — rb-pattern; header added as statement before `return rb.build()`), `ShapesPredicatesRest` (`predicates`), `CollectionSnapshotRest` (`list`), `SnapshotListRest` (`list`), `SnapshotRest` (`manifest` — int total cast to long), `CollectionTemplatesRest` (`listAllowed`, `listUsed`), `ShepardTemplateRest` (`list`, `tags`), `UserGroupV2Rest` (`listUserGroups` — two return paths), `UserSearchV2Rest` (`searchUsers` — uses `paged.total()`), `PersonalVocabularyRest` (`list` — two return paths, early-empty emits `0L`), `CollectionWatchesRest` (`list`). Fix pattern per file: (1) `import org.eclipse.microprofile.openapi.annotations.headers.Header` when missing; (2) `headers = @Header(name = "X-Total-Count", description = "Total count before paging.", schema = @Schema(implementation = Long.class))` in the `@APIResponse(responseCode = "200")` block; (3) `.header("X-Total-Count", total)` chained on `Response` builder, or `rb.header(...)` statement for cursor-pagination pattern. No v1 `/shepard/api/` surface touched. No data or schema migration. Purely additive. | **ADDITIVE — 34 v2 list endpoints now emit `X-Total-Count` in the response. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. No request body, response body, status code, or URL path changed.** Callers that already read `body.total` from `PagedResponseIO` are unaffected. Callers that prefer HTTP-header pagination can now use the header on all standard v2 paged list endpoints. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** The `X-Total-Count` response header is now available on all standard v2 paged list endpoints. Callers may optionally read the header; all existing pagination via `body.total` continues to work. |

| **APISIMP-CONT-LIST-INMEM-PAGING — all 3 container handlers (`file`, `timeseries`, `structured-data`) push `count()` + `list(skip, limit)` to Cypher; in-memory double full-scan eliminated** | 2026-07-16 (fire-635, branch `APISIMP-CONT-LIST-INMEM-PAGING`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-CONT-LIST-INMEM-PAGING. `ContainersV2Rest.list()` called `containersService.count(kind, q)` then `containersService.list(kind, q, skip, pageSize)`. Both paths fell through to `ContainerKindHandler` default methods that loaded all containers then computed `.size()` / `.subList()`. Two full table scans per paged `GET /v2/containers?kind=…` request. Fix: (1) `FileContainerDAO` + `TimeseriesContainerDAO` + `StructuredDataContainerDAO` — each gains a `count*Containers(params, username)` method executing a Cypher `count(c)` query with the same permission filter as the existing `findAll*` method. (2) `FileContainerKindHandler` + `TimeseriesContainerKindHandler` + `StructuredDataContainerKindHandler` — each overrides `count(nameFilter)` (delegates to new DAO count method) and `list(nameFilter, skip, limit)` (calls `service.getAllContainers(params.withPageAndSize(skip/limit, limit))` so the DAO appends `SKIP $offset LIMIT $size`). No v1 `/shepard/api/` surface touched. | **ZERO** — no wire-shape change; no API surface change. The `/v2/containers` response shape is identical. This is a backend-only performance fix: COUNT and SKIP/LIMIT are now computed by Neo4j rather than in the JVM. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** |
| **APISIMP-SDR-LIST-APPID-PATH — `StructuredDataReferenceKindHandler.listByDataObject` (non-paged MCP path) eliminates DataObject OGM load + numeric-id round-trip; uses new `StructuredDataReferenceDAO.findByDataObjectAppId(String)` directly** | 2026-07-16 (fire-639, branch `APISIMP-SDR-LIST-APPID-PATH`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-SDR-LIST-APPID-PATH. The non-paged SPI overload of `listByDataObject` called `resolveParent(appId)` (full OGM DataObject load) to get `parent.getId()` (numeric Neo4j id), then called `findByDataObjectNeo4jId(id)` which internally re-resolved the appId and post-filtered by numeric id in Java. Three Neo4j round-trips instead of one. The paged overload already used a direct appId-keyed DAO method. Fix: added `findByDataObjectAppId(String)` (unbound, non-deleted only, `ORDER BY createdAt ASC`) to `StructuredDataReferenceDAO`; replaced the non-paged `listByDataObject` body with a direct call to this new method. No v1 `/shepard/api/` surface touched. | **ZERO** — no wire-shape change; no API surface change. This is a backend-only performance fix: the non-paged path (used by MCP tools) now issues a single Cypher query instead of three. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** |
| **APISIMP-USERGROUP-SEARCH-PAGING — `GET /v2/user-groups?q=` search path gains real DB-backed pagination; fake `total=items.size()` and ignored `page`/`pageSize` eliminated** | 2026-07-16 (fire-637, branch `APISIMP-USERGROUP-SEARCH-PAGING-1`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-USERGROUP-SEARCH-PAGING. The stale row `APISIMP-USERGROUP-SEARCH-FAKE-PAGED` (aidocs/34 line ~688) described a "plain array" approach that was never applied to code. The actual code state had `searchByText(q)` loading all matching groups in memory and returning `total=items.size(), page=0, pageSize=items.size()` — ignoring the caller's `?page=` and `?pageSize=` params. Fix: (1) `SearchDAO` — new `countUserGroups(selectionQuery, var)` Cypher `RETURN COUNT(var)` method; new `findUserGroupsPaged(selectionQuery, var, pagination)` method using `ORDER BY name ASC SKIP offset LIMIT size` then path-projection. (2) `UserGroupSearchService` — new `countByText(text)` and `searchByTextPaged(text, page, pageSize)` methods using the new DAO methods. (3) `UserGroupV2Rest.listUserGroups()` search branch — replaced unbounded `searchByText(q)` with `countByText(q)` + `searchByTextPaged(q, page, pageSize)`; `PagedResponseIO` now carries the true DB count and honours the requested page. No v1 `/shepard/api/` surface touched. No Neo4j migration needed. | **AWARE — v2 wire-shape change on `GET /v2/user-groups?q=<text>`.** Response `total` now reflects the true DB count of matching groups (was `items.size()`, i.e. always equalled the returned array length). Response `page` and `pageSize` now reflect the requested page (were hardcoded to `0` and total-count respectively). Callers that requested a specific page via `?page=N` on the `?q=` path previously received all matching groups regardless of page; they now receive only the requested page slice. No URL, status code, or response field names changed. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** Pre-production surface. Callers that previously read `body.total` from a `GET /v2/user-groups?q=<text>` response will now see the true DB count (which equals `body.items.length` only on the last page or when fewer results exist than `pageSize`). Update pagination logic if it relied on `total == items.length` as a "single page" sentinel. |
| **APISIMP-GIT-CRED-INMEM-PAGING — `GET /v2/me/git-credentials` replaces in-memory `findAllByUser`+subList with DB-backed `countByUser` + `findByUser(skip, limit)`; adds missing `X-Total-Count` header** | 2026-07-16 (fire-638, branch `APISIMP-GIT-CRED-INMEM-PAGING-1`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-GIT-CRED-INMEM-PAGING. `MeCredentialsRest.list()` called `gitCredentialDAO.findAllByUser(caller)` (unbounded Cypher, loads all credentials for the user), mapped to a full `List<GitCredentialIO>`, then sliced with Java `subList`. `GitCredentialDAO` already had `countByUser(username)` and `findByUser(username, skip, limit)` (SKIP/LIMIT Cypher) — never called by the REST layer. Fix: replace `findAllByUser` with `countByUser` + `findByUser(caller, (long) page * pageSize, pageSize)`; remove Java subList; add `.header("X-Total-Count", total)` (was missing). 4 unit tests updated to mock paged DAO methods; `X-Total-Count` assertion added. All 22 git-plugin tests green. No v1 `/shepard/api/` surface touched. | **ADDITIVE — `X-Total-Count` header now present on `GET /v2/me/git-credentials` (was absent). No URL, status code, body shape, or `PagedResponseIO` field names changed.** Callers that already read `body.total` are unaffected; the header is a new convenience. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** |
| **APISIMP-CROSSDO-LTTB-PREPAGING — `POST /v2/data-objects/cross-bulk?kind=timeseries` pages the allowed-DO list before LTTB queries; `findNamesByAppIds` now called only for the page slice** | 2026-07-17 (fire-639, branch `APISIMP-CROSSDO-LTTB-PREPAGING-1`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-CROSSDO-LTTB-PREPAGING. `CrossDoBulkDataRest.getCrossDoBulkData()` previously iterated all allowed DataObjects, calling `crossDoChannelResolver.resolveChannelsByPredicate()` and `timeseriesService.getDataPointsLttbOptimised()` for every allowed DO, built the full result list in memory, and only then sliced with `out.subList(fromIndex, toIndex)`. For a 100-DO request with `pageSize=10`, 90 LTTB queries were executed and discarded. Fix: build `allowedRequestedIds` (ordered, permission-filtered) first; compute `total = allowedRequestedIds.size()`; slice to `pagedIds` before resolver/LTTB calls; call `findNamesByAppIds(new HashSet<>(pagedIds))` only for the page slice; iterate `pagedIds` for resolver+LTTB loop only. `X-Total-Count` header now correctly reflects `total`, not `out.size()` (these were identical before the fix). Added `import java.util.HashSet;`. No v1 `/shepard/api/` surface touched. | **ZERO** — no wire-shape change. Response fields (`items`, `total`, `page`, `pageSize`) and `X-Total-Count` carry semantically identical values to before; `total` continues to count permission-allowed DOs from the request. The only observable difference is that LTTB is no longer called for out-of-page DOs. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** |

| **APISIMP-CONTAINER-STATS-OGM-COUNT — `GET /v2/containers/{appId}/stats?kind=file` and `?kind=structured-data` replace OGM lazy-load item count with Cypher `COUNT` queries** | 2026-07-17 (fire-641, PR #2611, SHA `763e37f`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-CONTAINER-STATS-OGM-COUNT. `FileContainerKindHandler.getStats()` called `c.getFiles().size()` and `StructuredDataContainerKindHandler.getStats()` called `c.getStructuredDatas().size()` — both trigger OGM lazy relationship loads that materialise every attached entity into JVM heap just to return a single count integer. For MFFD image containers (5 000+ PNG frames) this hydrates thousands of full entity graphs. Fix: added `ShepardFileDAO.countByContainerAppId(String)` and `StructuredDataDAO.countByContainerAppId(String)` (each a single Cypher `RETURN count(f)` query); handlers now call the DAO method directly with no container OGM entity fetch. Pattern mirrors the existing `ShepardFileDAO.countByGroupAppId`. No v1 `/shepard/api/` surface touched. | **ZERO** — no wire-shape change; no API surface change. `GET /v2/containers/{appId}/stats` response fields and status codes are identical. This is a backend-only performance fix. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** |
| **APISIMP-BUNDLE-KIND-TOIO-OGM — `FileBundleReferenceKindHandler.toIO()` replaces OGM lazy-loads with Cypher COUNT queries for `groupCount` and `fileCount`** | 2026-07-17 (fire-641, branch `APISIMP-BUNDLE-KIND-TOIO-OGM`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-BUNDLE-KIND-TOIO-OGM. `toIO()` called `ref.getGroups().size()` and `ref.getFiles().size()` — OGM relationship traversals that hydrate the full `FileGroup` list (each with its `ShepardFile` payloads) and all directly-attached `ShepardFile` nodes into JVM heap. For bundles used in bulk-list contexts (`GET /v2/references?kind=bundle`), every reference in the page triggers two such traversals. Fix: (1) `ShepardFileDAO.countByBundleReferenceAppId(String)` — `MATCH (:FileBundleReference {appId: $bid})-[:has_payload]->(f:ShepardFile) RETURN count(f) AS total`. (2) `FileBundleReferenceKindHandler` injects `ShepardFileDAO` and the already-present `FileGroupDAO`; `toIO()` now calls `fileGroupDAO.countByBundleAppId(bid)` and `shepardFileDAO.countByBundleReferenceAppId(bid)`. Pattern mirrors `FileGroupDAO.countByBundleAppId` (already present) and `ShepardFileDAO.countByGroupAppId` (already present). 4 new unit tests added. No v1 `/shepard/api/` surface touched. | **ZERO** — no wire-shape change; no API surface change. `GET /v2/references/{appId}` and `GET /v2/references?kind=bundle` response fields are identical. Backend-only performance fix. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** |
| **APISIMP-MEREST-WATCHED-COUNT — `GET /v2/users/me` replaces `findByUsername().size()` OGM load with a Cypher `count(w)` query in `CollectionWatcherDAO`** | 2026-07-17 (fire-642, PR #2613, SHA `baff16c`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-MEREST-WATCHED-COUNT. `MeRest.getMe()` called `collectionWatcherDAO.findByUsername(username).size()` — a full `MATCH (w:CollectionWatcher) WHERE w.username = $username RETURN w` query that hydrates every `CollectionWatcher` node for the user just to return the integer count. Fix: added `countByUsername(String username)` to `CollectionWatcherDAO` (`MATCH (w:CollectionWatcher) WHERE w.username = $username RETURN count(w) AS cnt`), replaced the `.findByUsername().size()` call with `(int) countByUsername(...)`. Pattern mirrors the existing `countByCollectionAppId` on the same DAO. 2 new DAO tests, 3 updated `MeRestTest` stubs. No v1 `/shepard/api/` surface touched. | **ZERO** — no wire-shape change; no API surface change. `GET /v2/users/me` response fields are identical. Backend-only performance fix. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** |
| **APISIMP-SNAPSHOT-LIST-N+1 — `GET /v2/snapshots` permission loop pre-warms `EntityIdResolver` memo via batch `resolveLongs()` to eliminate up to 200 serial Neo4j round-trips** | 2026-07-17 (fire-643, PR #2614, SHA `0ef4735`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-SNAPSHOT-LIST-N+1. `SnapshotListRest.list()` called `entityIdResolver.resolveLong(collectionAppId)` for each snapshot in the page (up to `pageSize=200`); on a cold request, each call issued a separate single-node Cypher query. `EntityIdResolver.resolveLongs()` already existed to batch-resolve a collection of appIds in one `MATCH (e) WHERE e.appId IN $appIds` query and populate the memo. Fix: before the permission loop, collect distinct non-null collection appIds from the page and call `resolveLongs()` once; subsequent per-snapshot `resolveLong()` calls hit the memo (zero additional Cypher). 1 new test `list_batchResolvesCollectionIds_inSingleCall` verifies `resolveLongs()` is called once with all collection appIds. No v1 `/shepard/api/` surface touched. | **ZERO** — no wire-shape change; no API surface change. `GET /v2/snapshots` response fields, status codes, and permission semantics are identical. Backend-only performance fix. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** |
| **APISIMP-FILEGROUP-DELETE-GUARD — `FileGroupService.deleteGroup()` last-group guard replaces `findByBundleAppId().size()` OGM load with `countByBundleAppId()` Cypher COUNT** | 2026-07-17 (fire-643, PR #2615, SHA `4f2e195`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-FILEGROUP-DELETE-GUARD. `deleteGroup()` called `fileGroupDAO.findByBundleAppId(bundleAppId)` — a query that loads ALL `FileGroup` nodes under the bundle including their nested `ShepardFile` payloads via `OPTIONAL MATCH (g)-[:has_payload]->(f:ShepardFile)` — just to call `.size() <= 1` as a last-group guard. `FileGroupDAO.countByBundleAppId(bundleAppId)` (a pure Cypher `RETURN count(g)` already on the DAO) makes the guard a single-integer read with zero entity hydration. Fix: replaced 4-line sibling-load guard with a single inline condition `if (bundleAppId != null && fileGroupDAO.countByBundleAppId(bundleAppId) <= 1) { throw … }`. 3 existing delete tests updated to stub `countByBundleAppId`; 1 new test `deleteGroup_usesCountQueryNotFullLoad` verifies the count path. No v1 `/shepard/api/` surface touched. | **ZERO** — no wire-shape change; `DELETE /v2/bundles/{bundleAppId}/groups/{groupAppId}` semantics unchanged. Backend-only performance fix. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** |
| **APISIMP-URI-COLL-DOREF-NONPAGED-APPID — `UriReferenceKindHandler`, `CollectionReferenceKindHandler`, `DataObjectReferenceKindHandler` non-paged `listByDataObject()` replace OGM round-trip with direct appId-keyed DAO queries** | 2026-07-17 (fire-643, PR #2616, SHA `068489ea`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-URI-COLL-DOREF-NONPAGED-APPID. Fix: added unbound `findByDataObjectAppId(String)` to `URIReferenceDAO`, `CollectionReferenceDAO`, and `DataObjectReferenceDAO` (each a `MATCH (d:DataObject {appId: $aid})-[hr:has_reference]->(r:…Reference) … RETURN r, d, hr ORDER BY r.createdAt ASC` query rooted at the `appId` label index). Handlers now call the DAO method directly, bypassing the OGM parent fetch entirely. `UriReferenceKindHandler` gained an `@Inject URIReferenceDAO` field; the other two already had their DAOs injected. 3 existing list-delegate tests updated; 3 new blank-appId guard tests added (51 total). | **APISIMP-LABJOURNAL-NUMID-DATE — v2 lab-journal endpoints switch to `LabJournalEntryV2IO`, eliminating `dataObjectId` (numeric Neo4j id) and upgrading timestamps to ISO-8601 `Instant`** | 2026-07-17 (fire-644, branch `APISIMP-LABJOURNAL-NUMID-DATE`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-LABJOURNAL-NUMID-DATE. | **APISIMP-SEARCH-MCP-INMEM — MCP `search` tool consolidates N per-label Cypher queries into a single UNION ALL** | 2026-07-17 (fire-646, branch `APISIMP-SEARCH-MCP-INMEM`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-SEARCH-MCP-INMEM. `SearchMcpTools.searchLabel()` was called once per Neo4j label (up to 12 round-trips for an all-kinds search, each with its own `LIMIT 500`). Replaced by `searchUnion(List<String> labels, String query)` which builds one UNION ALL Cypher with a literal `kindOrd` integer per arm, `ORDER BY kindOrd ASC, name ASC, appId ASC`, and `LIMIT 2000`. Permission filter and in-memory pagination unchanged (SEARCH-MCP-PERMS-1 contract preserved). `StubSearchTools` in tests overrides `searchUnion` instead of the removed `searchLabel`; two new tests assert single-call behaviour. No v1 `/shepard/api/` surface touched; MCP JSON-RPC wire shape unchanged. | **ZERO** — internal performance change to the MCP `search` tool; no MCP protocol change, no REST endpoint change, no v1 surface touched. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source changes only). | **No operator action required.** |
| **APISIMP-SEMADMIN-ONTOL-INMEM-PAGE — `SemanticAdminRest.listOntologies()` eliminates in-memory full-list materialisation before `subList` page slice** | 2026-07-17 (fire-649, branch `APISIMP-SEMADMIN-ONTOL-INMEM-PAGE`). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-SEMADMIN-ONTOL-INMEM-PAGE. `SemanticAdminRest.listOntologies()` called `configService.listMerged(manifest)` which returned every built-in and every user-uploaded `OntologyBundle` into a single `ArrayList<BundleView>`, then converted all entries to `OntologyBundleIO` before `subList`-slicing to the requested page. Fix: added `OntologyConfigService.countMerged(builtin)` (returns `builtin.size() + userBundleDAO.countAll()`, where `countAll()` is a single Cypher `MATCH (n:UserOntologyBundle) RETURN count(n)` query) and a sliced `listMerged(builtin, skip, limit)` overload that iterates the built-in list and then the user-bundle DAO with skip/limit, allocating O(pageSize) entries. `SemanticAdminRest.listOntologies()` now calls `countMerged` for total and the sliced `listMerged` for the page. `UserOntologyBundleDAO.countAll()` added (O(1) Cypher count). No v1 `/shepard/api/` surface touched. 9 new unit tests across `OntologyConfigServiceTest` (4) and `SemanticAdminRestTest` (5 stubs updated + 1 new verify test). | **ZERO** — no wire-shape change; no API surface change. `GET /v2/admin/semantic/ontologies` response envelope, field set, and status codes are identical. Backend-only performance fix: `total` is now computed with a single Cypher count; the page is built with O(pageSize) allocations instead of O(total). | — (no schema or data migration; Java source and test changes only). | **No operator action required.** |
| **APISIMP-DO-CHAIN-ENVELOPE — `PagedResponseIO` replaced with `BoundedListIO<T>` on depth-bounded chain endpoints** | 2026-07-17 (fire-651, branch `APISIMP-DO-CHAIN-ENVELOPE`, PR #2623). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-DO-CHAIN-ENVELOPE. `DataObjectV2Rest.predecessorChain()` and `DataObjectV2Rest.successorChain()` returned `PagedResponseIO(result, result.size(), 0, result.size())` — `page` always 0, `pageSize` always equal to `items.size()`, implying false paginability on endpoints that are depth-bounded (not paginated). New `BoundedListIO<T>` record (`items`, `appliedDepth`) introduced in `de.dlr.shepard.v2.common.io`; both chain endpoints now return `BoundedListIO<DataObjectSummaryIO>`. `appliedDepth` echoes the `?depth=` value actually applied server-side (capped at 50). `X-Total-Count` header preserved. No v1 `/shepard/api/` surface touched. Two existing chain tests in `DataObjectV2RestTest` updated; two additional assertions added (`appliedDepth` + `X-Total-Count` header check). | **AWARE — pre-prod wire-shape change** — `GET /v2/collections/{cid}/data-objects/{did}/predecessor-chain` and `/successor-chain` response body changes from `{"items":[…],"total":N,"page":0,"pageSize":N}` to `{"items":[…],"appliedDepth":N}`. Callers reading `total`, `page`, or `pageSize` must switch to `items.length` (item count) and `appliedDepth`. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. No Neo4j migration needed. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source and test changes only). | **No operator action required.** Update callers of `GET .../predecessor-chain` or `.../successor-chain` that read `total`/`page`/`pageSize` from the response body: switch to `items.length` for the count and `appliedDepth` for the server-side depth cap that was applied. |
| **APISIMP-PROV-CURSOR-ENVELOPE — `PagedResponseIO` replaced with `CursorPageIO<T>` on cursor-based provenance endpoints** | 2026-07-17 (fire-650, branch `APISIMP-PROV-CURSOR-ENVELOPE`, PR #2622). `aidocs/16` APISIMP-PROV-CURSOR-ENVELOPE. `ProvenanceRest.listActivities()` and `ProvenanceRest.listEntityActivities()` (two cursor-paginated endpoints that use `?since=`/`?until=` time windows, not offset pages) were returning `PagedResponseIO<ActivityIO>` with `total = rows.size()` and `page = 0` — semantically misleading for a cursor API where there is no DB total count. New `CursorPageIO<T>` record (`items`, `pageSize`, `hasMore`, `nextCursor`) introduced in `de.dlr.shepard.v2.common.io`; both endpoints now return `CursorPageIO<ActivityIO>`. `pageSize` in the body now mirrors the *requested* limit (was `rows.size()`). `hasMore = rows.size() >= limit`; `nextCursor = hasMore ? Instant.parse(rows.last().startedAt).toEpochMilli() : null`. `X-Has-More` and `X-Next-Cursor` headers still emitted. `ProvenanceRestCursorPagedTest` (8 tests) guards `pageSize`/`hasMore`/`nextCursor` semantics. No v1 `/shepard/api/` surface touched. | **AWARE — pre-prod wire-shape change** — `GET /v2/provenance/activities` and `GET /v2/provenance/entity/{appId}/activities` response body changes from `{"items":[…],"total":N,"page":0,"pageSize":N}` to `{"items":[…],"pageSize":N,"hasMore":true/false,"nextCursor":epochMs|null}`. Callers reading `total` or `page` must switch to `hasMore`/`nextCursor`. No `/shepard/api/` v1 surface touched. No Neo4j migration needed. | — (no schema or data migration; Java source and test changes only). | **No operator action required.** Update callers of `GET /v2/provenance/activities` or `GET /v2/provenance/entity/{appId}/activities` that read `total`/`page` from the response body: switch to `hasMore` (boolean) and `nextCursor` (epoch-ms long, or null when hasMore=false). Pass `nextCursor` as `?until=` on the next request to walk backward through the activity stream. |
